short_description: Users cannot select Monospace 700 when booking orders with local material GB_NEB_C_INT and GB_FRB_KW_INT
introduction: User cannot select Monospace 700 when booking orders with local material GB_NEB_C_INT and GB_FRB_KW_INT.
instructions: The value Monospace 700 has not been defined for the Characteristic Technical Platform. Hence the user is unable to select the value NF (Monospace 700). The values maintained in the table KC_NEB_DEL_CLASS were Sales org, Technical platform, Delivery class, Supply line. The value NF was added to the characteristic KCO_RUSH_TECH_PLATFORM in transaction CT04. Now we were able to select the value but it had some inconsistency. The new value NF was not added to the delivery class. We added the value to the characteristic KCO_DELIVERY_CLASS in CT04. We maintained the values in the table KC_NEB_DEL_CLASS using tcode CU60. Also the values were maintained in the table KC_NEB_LEAD_TIME. The values maintained in the table KC_NEB_DEL_CLASS were Sales org, Technical platform, Delivery class, Supply line. The values maintained in the table KC_NEB_LEAD_TIME were Sales org, Delivery class, activity times. Please check attachment for more details.

short_description: The Order Confirmation Sent was not being received by the recipient for an order
introduction: The Order Confirmation Sent was not being received by the recipient for an order
instructions: Issue Description : The Order Confirmation Sent was not being received by the recipient for an order. Order confirmation failed to be received by the user in the form of Email are 811646520 1.1 Analysis: This was happening for Output Type- YMAO in Z04, It was green from SAP side but the mails were not being received by the Contact Person whose ID was maintained in the recipient list. When we checked from SAP end everything is fine and was working well but we assumed that the batch job was not picking the OC for some orders so we took the help of the ABAP team to find out the BATCH JOB that was responsible. We checked the time at which the output was processed from the Order and after knowing the time of delivery we crossed checked it in the job- YMAI_PURCHASING_YMAI So we checked the Job on 9.04.2015 and the job did pick this particular output but was not delivered the Email to the recipient So we took some random orders from the list and checked with the Recipient whether the mail was received or not. For Example: Order: 811628809 Recipient : Salla Alexei The output was sent successfully and the mail was received by the user. So everything was working fine even for the order which was reported, so we started our further analysis by checking the Partners in the Sales Order Header and the Output screen. For the order: 811628809 The Contact person in the header was-401923(Alexei Salla) This was same even in the output window But for the Reported order they were different It was 612181 in the output window but the email was being sent to 855220 (Sales Order Header) 1.2Resolution: After cross checking with some of the other orders and getting confirmation from the recipients we came to a conclusion that this was happening due to the CP (Contact Person ) which is varied from Sales order header partner functions and YMAO output condition Partner . So instructed the user to make sure to have the CP in both areas (Sales order header partner functions and YMAO output condition Partner) and we waited for the JOB to trigger and it worked. (we need to wait for the job YMAI_PURCHASING_YMAI to run since this job runs on hourly basis ) Soon after the job runs it successfully triggered the Emails to the correct recipients after changing the CP so that it is similar in both areas (sales order and Output) And the results are turned positive which can be revealed by the below screen shot. 1.3Testing: Validated with the sales order 811646520 in Z04 1.4Production: Validated with the sales order 811646520 in Z04 Attachment added.....

short_description: Failed IDOC’s in T code Z_EP_MIDOCS
introduction: ISSUE : Automatic GR is not triggered for few PO and when Z_EP_MIDOCS ( Report which Creates Invoice) is run, these fail because GR is not done. Failed IDOCs in Transaction code Z_EP_MIDOCS-Idoc selection report , where the automated GR did not work. POSSIBLE CAUSE : Shipping Point (DE01) missing for Inbound Delivery of failure case.
instructions: Every day twice a background Job ( 220_AUTOMATIC_GR_GSS_AFTERNOON ) is scheduled which creates Automatic GR for all open Inbound deliveries. This background job actually triggers program ( Z_SD_R_GOODRECPT) in which the code checks for mandate parameter (Shipping Point) in inbound delivery . If shipping point ( Receiving Point ) is empty automatic GR is not created. For all our failure cases shipping point DE01 is missing and hence GR was not created. SOLUTION : In the below path SPRO-> Logistics Execution >Shipping> Basic Shipping Functions >Shipping point & goods receiving point determination>Assign goods receiving point for inbound deliveries . Map the missing storage locations with the shipping points here. we do not have entries for SLOC 2223 for plant 220 (FAILURE CASE) and we have entry for SLOC 5310 for plant 220 (SUCCESS CASE)

short_description: MBM Plan issue
introduction: To analyze and find reason as to how the service order generated for equipment 40067622 in the month of February had only one operation whereas the MBM plan in ZI05 transaction had two operations scheduled for the same month. The MBM plan for equipment 40067622 in ZI05 shows two operations for February , GKB, BM: Basis Modul and GEO, KM: Kamm Modul
instructions: MBM Plan issue Issue : To analyze and find reason as to how the service order generated for equipment 40067622 in the month of February had only one operation whereas the MBM plan in ZI05 transaction had two operations scheduled for the same month. The MBM plan for equipment 40067622 in ZI05 shows two operations for February , GKB, BM: Basis Modul and GEO, KM: Kamm Modul : Whereas in the service order (604705095) generated that month has only one operation BM: Basis Modul On analysis it has been found that the profile assigned to the equipment had been changed from M100018902 to DEE6000912. Hence the modules planned for each month will also differ which led to the incorrect number of operations in the order. Analyzing the groups and task list in service order generated for the months before and after February : Service order ‘604305759’ for December’2012 has group M1000189 : Service order for February,2013 ‘604705095’ also has group M1000189 : The task list of this group indicates GEO, KM: Kamm Modul is not planned for February : Whereas after Feb the service order generated for April ‘605149536’ has different group DEE60009 with different task list and planned modules : Hence as the profiles and groups changed in February this led to incorrect operations in service order.

short_description: Relinking sales order to an equipment
introduction: Relinking sales order to an equipment
instructions: Relinking sales order to an equipment: Go to the equipment (IE03) and note down the serial number. Now go to the edit mode and change the serial number. Here we need to give the serial number of the equipment to which we are trying to relink But as the serial number is already linked to another equipment , system will not allow us to use that serial number So for time being we can change it to last serial number (+1). But to complete the process, we need to change the sales order, only after this changes will be reflected in YPMDS. Now go to the other equipment and change the serial number (give the serial number of the first equipment-which was changed before). Now come back to the 1 st equipment and change the serial number (from last serial number to serial number of the second equipment). Every time after changing the serial number , we need to change the technical object in the sales order (just change and rechange) For this go to YPMDS-> change sales order ->extras-> technical objects, just change and re-change the serial number (just for the data base to store). Now save the order. Sales orders will be successfully re-linked to the equipments

short_description: Variation order creation and cancellation v.E WI27
introduction: Variation order creation and cancellation v.E WI27 Process.
instructions: Contents 1 PURPOSE . 2 2 TARGET GROUP . 2 3 SCOPE . 2 4 RESPONSIBILITIES . 2 5 HANDOVER TO OM TEAM .. 2 6 CHECKING IF VERSION HAS BEEN ALREADY CREATED .. 3 7 VERSION CREATION: BUDJET VERSION -2 . 4 8 VARIATION ORDER CREATION . 5 9 ADDING COSTS TO NETWORK . 9 10 VERSION CREATION: BUDJET VERSION -1 . 10 11 HOW TO SEE ISSUED INVOICE FOR VARIATION ROW .. 10 12 CANCELLATION OF VARIATION ORDER ROW .. 11 REVISION HISTORY . 13 VARIATION ORDER CREATION AND CANCELLATION 1 PURPOSE This document instructs how to create and cancel variation orders to any existing sales order in SAP. Variation order means an agreed change to existing sales order with an additional item/row including increase/decrease of sales price and/or costs. 2target group This procedure is targeted to Order Management Specialists (OMS) in KDI Order Management team (OM team). 3Scope The document applies to processing of any existing sales orders in SAP Z04 within KDI in KONE Elevators Ltd. 4Responsibilities Order Management team is responsible to implement and maintain this procedure and instructions 5HANDOVER TO OM TEAM If variation order is related to additional material and tendering/ordering is processed via Technical Sales Support team (TSS team), then TSS team sends to OM team an email with needed details which enables OMS to create commercial input in SAP. The mail shall include the following details: Existing order and KONE Equipment number (KEN) referred to Order reference Description and quantity of goods per item KDI sales price and currency per item Price from supplier per item = KDI costs CMII percentage Dispatch point(s) -> if more than one detailed specification Delivery terms Terms of payment if special terms agreed upon Additional information - such as to be delivered with some other order etc. If variation order is related to other reasons where tendering/ordering is not via TSS team, then handover to OM team has to be done by Area Manager in charge of that particular country. 6checking IF VERsion has been already created Before creating the version, check if the version has already been created to the sales order. Go to YPMDS to project management view. Go to cost/billing and cost control Check that you have the correct SO number and correct SO value before pressing EXECUTE. If version -2 is empty, the version creation has not been done. Proceed to point nro 7. 7Version creation: Budjet version -2 Before issuance of variation order, you need to create an initial budjet version -02 manually. Go via YPMDS to project management view. Go to Cost/Billing and Create Budjet version Add project number, reference version to 0 and Target version to -2 (= originally estimated). DELETE tick in Process control / Test run EXECUTE You get a report of production run Repeat the steps from instructions point nro 6 to check that column for Version -2 is completed with values. Those can be compared with revised values including future variation order to be shown in column Version -1. 8variation ORDER CREATION Enter existing sales order (SO) via Z750 in change mode VARIATION MATERIAL Add variation material to 1 st free row. Use the correct variation material: FI_VARIATION ELE -> with any elevator order FI_VARIATION_ESC -> with any escalator order FI_VARIATION_FRB -> with any FRB order FI_VARIATION_MOD -> with any TRB order When issuing variation order because of SOC storage costs, please use the variation material A or B depending on the actual material. All 1 for order quantity Add High level item (HL itm) = item/row to which you want to link your variation order (row 5 under). Variation order is always linked with existing item/row – it cannot be created as independent item. Before pressing enter, write down the exchange rate IF the sales order is in USD. Costs are always added in EUR into configuration. ENTER Choose Type of variation: B. Linked to billing plan Select origin of variation as OTHERS Add Unit description: contents of variation Add costs to appropriate row: either material or labour or subcontracting Remember to indicate values always in EUR. If sales order is in USD, please use the exchange rate of the sales order. Add zero if no costs for some row BACK WITH GREEN ARROW SALES ORDER Double click the newly created variation row Select sheet Billing Plan A selection criteria of Milestones for billin plan is displayed, confirm WBS element (=the same as for main item) by ENTER Biling plan of the main item is displayed. Select row for Final invoice to which variation billing will be linked and click Copy . Pass the info screen by ENTER Billing plan for variation row will be displayed with the same details as for main row. GO BACK WITH GREEN ARROW ITEM TEXTS FOR SOC STORAGE Add item text into variation order by double clicking the variation order row, choosing TEXTS sheet and writing the description on ITEM NOTE. Text will appear on the invoice when issuing ADVANCE INVOICE for the variation order/storage. SALES PRICE Add sales price by double clicking item row and going to worksheet Conditions Add PB00 to 1 st free row and click ENTER PB00 row will move upwards. Add sales price for Variation order row and click ENTER . Icon in the beginning of BP00 row has turned from red circle into green square. GO BACK WITH GREEN ARROW Sales price for Variation order row is displayed at the end of item row And total sales price has been updated in field Net value SAVE 9aDDING COSTS TO NETWORK Costs need to be added also in network for the main item. If variation row is created for SOC storage, no need to ad costs to network. Costs are automatically added to the system when PR has been updated with the new material price with storage cost. Enter into network in Change mode. Enter network via green box “Activity overview”. Add costs into appropriate row and SAVE You can check proper registration of costs also in report via transaction S_ALR_87013543: 10version creation: BUDJET VERSION -1 Repeat steps 7 A-D but set target version for -1 . And you will get Version -1 shown with changed values for comparison with originally planned values in column Verrsion -2. 11HOW TO SEE ISSUED INVOICE FOR VARIATION ROW Separate advance invoice for variation row is not show in Z750 view as normally. To see the issued invoice and its number, go via YPMDS to the order and double click BILLING/PAYMENT ANALYSIS You are led to follow-up of sales orders (billing, payments and partial payments) view where you can see billing information for elevator and variation rows. Elevator or variation row can be identified from the report by its material. Below is the view for variation row where it can been seen that advance invoice for 100% has been issued on 3 rd February and the invoice number is shown in the BILL. DOC part. Invoice can be also seen by clicking the SO number which will take you to WBS element overview 12CANCELLATION OF VARIATION ORDER ROW In case variation order row has to be cancelled, follow instructions Order Cancellation in SAP, appropriate fields starting from 7.1. Check billing and payment status undo possible allocations cancel invoices Delete planned costs in network Go to network via transaction CN22, enter into network via green box Delete planned costs related ONLY to variation order – NO other costs to be deleted (related to main order) SAVE Cancel row in sales order Go to SO in change mode + add CANC in Description row Go to worksheet Reason for rejection and choose reason for rejection ENTER + validate info screen by another ENTER Variation row becomes “grey” SAVE REVISION HISTORY Issue Date Description of Change Compiled by Approved by A 25.9.2015 New document E. short_description: KCC user found wrong business place in billing document.
introduction: KCC user found wrong business place in billing document.
instructions: KCC business place determination rule: Business place is the financial report dimension to differentiate branch companies in KCC, thus should be posted correctly. When billing, the billing header business place is determined in following sequence: get BP from SO item VC get BP from add-on table zfi_kcc_ma_bp. get BP from standard configuration table idvkbur_bupla This logic is coded in billing copy control rule 902. The root cause for the wrong BP in this ticket is because in YFSO/YFLO/YV3B, the rule was 001 instead of 902.

short_description: Ask IT - Business partner with GUID 5250A44DDF6A0F00E10080000A30B81F does not exist
introduction: What is the reason for error Business partner with GUID 5250A44DDF6A0F00E10080000A30B81F does not exist
instructions: Jobs for program FDM_COLL_SEND_ITEMS appeared to be ending correctly as SM37 gave status Finished: Jobs FSCM and FSCM_DTATS are not relevant => could even be stopped. But in practice if we look in detail the log of these jobs, we can see that they actually were not fully processed and were stopped due to critical error. Can be seen in the spool (but difficult because over 100.000 messages per job) or via transaction SLG1 with object FIDM, subobject FIDM_SEND and a date restriction. Can be seen that all the jobs (history only kept for 1 month) with External ID = Update Run: Periodic Data Transfer (which correspond to the jobs Z04_FSCM_1D_1 and Z04_FSCM_INDIA_1D_1) actually ended with a ‘Very important error: ð The reason for these jobs to be terminated was amazingly caused by 1 single Customer for which due to an unknown reason data was only create partially for the Business partner of the customer ð The partial data was removed => and run prog FDM_COLL_SEND_ITEMS successfully ð The scheduled job at 22:00:00 also ran successfully (errors is ok, because not all customers are mapped to BP; succesfull as job does not end with STOP anymore) now: To be sure this solved the problem, simulated worklist creation for some of the cases mentioned in the mails like 10684176 from 220 and 12187628/12181132 from 282 ð Before the change, simulation said these would come in worklist (as simulation was still on old data…) ð After change and thus after successful FDM_COLL_SEND_ITEMS, simulation said they would not come in worklist anymore.

short_description: Ask IT - SD document 340601290 is not in the database or has been archived
introduction: SD document 340601290 is not in the database or has been archived
instructions: 1.1 Issue Description: When we try to view or edit the sale order nos. SD NO- 340605002 / 340601291 / 340601524 /340602663/340601290 SD document 340601290 is not in the database or has been archived The orders were created but when trying to view in change/display mode, it is showing the error. 1.2 Analysis: Repair orders were created by the users and the sales document number was generated for it. When trying to open the order, the error message was displayed. When checked in Sm13, the following error was found. The order is not saved in the database due to the error message “Cost center assignment to the main work center has not been maintained” It is because, the Cost Center assignment to the Work center was not maintained. In the data which you provided, the Equipment is 40190607. It had the Work Center as 282AKD01. Cost Center 282AKDS is maintained to the Work center 282AKD01. When checked in Z03 system, the Validity End date was only until 31.01.2016. So after the end date, the cost center assignment is not valid. Because of this, error is obtained when updating the order to the database. I can see that in Z04 system, the work center is last changed on 16.02.2016. 1.3 Solution: Extend the validity End date in the Work center – Costing tab. Test done in Z03 system: Work center: 282AKD01 The Validity End date is on 31.01.2016 The validity is ended. So the Cost Center assignment to the Work center is no longer valid. Test case: I have created a Sales Lead notification: 302322968 for the equipment 40190607. The Equipment has the Main Work center has 282AKD01 For this sales lead, I have created YTRS SEB Repair order Repair Order No: 340489517 When trying to display this order, we are getting the error message The Updation of the Order to the database is terminated, because of the following error “Update was terminated Cost center assignment to main work center has not been maintained” Reason: The Cost Center assignment to the Work center validity has expired. Now I have extended the Validity end date Sales Lead Notification created: 302322941 Repair Order created: 340489519 The Repair Order is saved to the database and we can view it. In both the cases, the data which I maintained is the same. The difference is in the previous case, the Validity is expired and I have extended the validity of Cost center assignment to Work center.

short_description: Sold to (SP) 10701369 is on the split billing table but it is still combining invoices
introduction: Sold to (SP) 10701369 is on the split billing table but it is still combining invoices
instructions: 1.1 Issue Description: Sold to (SP) 10701369 is on the split billing table but it is still combining invoices. 1.2 Analysis: The sold to party 10701369 has been maintained in the Split Billing Table. Table: ZCS_UCT07 So when the sold to party is maintained in this table, SAP should split invoices when we are trying to do billing in VF01 using multiple contracts. But it is not happening and collective billing is happening for two contracts 40980016 and 40980012. 1.3 Testing: For the split billing to take place, the sold to party should also be maintained in another table ZWW_UCT08 in sm30 transaction. The sold to party was not maintained in this table. Hence the invoices was not splitting. The entry was maintained in this table in Z04 system Here, if we provide X to the item level field, then the individual invoice will be created for each line item in the contracts. 1.4 Solution: Now the invoices are splittin after maintaining entry in another table ZWW_UCT08

short_description: How to provide ESS/PLT access
introduction: How to provide ESS/PLT access? Definitions ESS: Employee Self Service is a Web enabled tool used by the employee himself to create, change or display (depending on the info type) their own HR-related data in the Enterprise Portal., as well as to report leaves or book holidays. PLT: People Leadership Tool is a Web Tool used for documenting Performance Discussions. List of countries in which PLT tool is live. CNE Finland , UK, NEM (Poland, Czech Republic, Slovakia, Slovenia, Hungary) WSE France , Italy, KME (Oman, Qatar, Bahrain, UAE) EAP China , HK & Taiwan, SEA (Thailand, Malaysia), India ENA USA (one region) KSU SOF (Finland), SOI (Italy), TCU (Czech), ELK & ESK (China), India, MCU (Mexico) Other Major Projects, GD, Global functions, Bratislava SSC
instructions: Requests for ESS/PLT access should be raised or approved by the local PLT Key Users. 1. If the requested user id is a new user, we need to create the user in Central system of CUA (SM4.055) as explained in Article: KBA174 2. If the requested user id is an existing user in Central system (SM4.055), log into SM4/055, start transaction SU01 and check the account Validity Period as well as the Accounting Number that cannot display “Inactive” as field value. Also make it sure the account is not locked. 3. Open the “Roles” tab and assign the authorization roles ZALLESSPLT and ZHRPORTAL in H04/009 4. Log into H04/009, start transaction SU01 4.a. Check the account Validity Period as well as the Accounting Number that cannot display “Inactive” as field value. Also make it sure the account is not locked. 4.b. Deactivate the password but if the user is assigned roles other than ESS and/or MSS and/or PLT roles. 5. Log into the KoneWay portal http://kwportal.kone.com/irj/portal using your NT userid and password 6. Select the “User Administration” tab. 7. Type the userid and hit the “GO” button 8. Click on the left cell to highlight the row 9. User’s details will appear; select the “Assigned Groups” tab. 10. Hit the “Modify” button 11. Type *SELF* in the search field and hit the “GO” button. 12. Select the “EP_Employee_Self_Service” group and hit the “Add” button. Caution: the “EP_Employee_Self_Servide_PLT” group must NOT be assigned. ESS and ESS PLT functions are merged into the ESS group; what the user will be able to use in the portal is determined by the authorization roles he has in H04/009 13. On the right side of the screen you see the ESS group has been added; hit the “Save” button. 14. The user is added to the “ESS PLT” group. 15. You can leave the portal now.

instructions: Steps to correct the issue: As requested by PST Team every week we are performing the below steps in order to eliminate the incorrect lost/won records. 1) Executing “ZG_RUSH_SC_CHECK_GDRULC02¬” query to collect all the double won and lost records for each individual Sales organization which are listed below: Source System Sales Org ZE-AM MKOF CKE CKQ 227 223 ZE-EU 207 299 220 221 225 234 237 258 259 281 260 262 277 323 282 SFA BEL KHM KED KAI KNE KOU 205 232 299 261 275 279 280 ZE-AP 201 202 SIN 230 238 239 240 244 KCC HKG1 246 254 2) Impacted double W/L record contracts will be corrected through DTP GDRULC02->GDRULC02 (REM DOUBLE LOST). 3) Hence after the daily loads in CPM Production system service contract items with monetary value will report correctly in the RUSH SC reports.

short_description: SS:Creation of Metadata in Streamserve
introduction: How to create Metadata in Streamserve
instructions: For creation of the Metadata, we can have 2 scenarios: Adding metadata field to New metadata group Adding metadata field to existing metadata group Steps for adding metadata field in both the cases: Open IXML message in the source Right click on XMLIN Event, select and click open. To create field in the XMLIN Event, Go to Message panel --> Field folder, Right click and then select New --> Field(Ctrl+Shift+F). Enter name of the Field/ID, Match criteria and Variable name in the properties.Save and close the XMLIN window. Open XMLOUT process in IXML window. To add Metadata, go to Process --> Message --> root -->data. Right click on “data”, select “Add” --> “Element”(Ctrl+Shift+E) within the process tree in the process panel. Now, enter the name of the Element according to the provided specification. for mapping the field, select the field(from the "Event" Panel) which is to be added to the Element in the process and right click, select “Add field”(Ctrl+Shift+F) After adding field to the metadata save and close the XMLOUT process. Note: Name of the Element should be the Metadata name and field/variable name can be anything as per the mapped value from XML. Open “TENDER_LETTER” message. Open the Event of that message. To create field in the XMLIN event. Go to Message panel-->Field folder, Right click select New and Field(Ctrl+Shift+F). Enter name of the Field/ID, Match and Variable name in the properties. Save and close the XMLIN event window. Note: Don’t give the same Variable name as given to IXML XMLIN Variable. It is a conflict leads to fatal error. Add the Log for the metadata to trace the Metadata. Right click on XMLIN doc of TENDER_LETTER and open Script. Add a Log entry inside the Script. Open the Resource Set. To Add a Metadata group (if required)--->Right click on the metadata group set--> New -- >Metadata Group. Give the name to the Metadata group as per the provided Specification. Double click the Metadata group to open. Click on PLUS(+) icon to add the Metadata. Give the Metadata name and Type of Metadata. Click OK. Repeat the Step 16 for adding more Metadata one by one. Click on Share button to Share the Metadata among Document types. Select the Document types to which the metadata has to be shared and click OK. Save and Close the Metadata Group Window. Open the Document type from the resource set. Select the required Metadata group. In right window, Select Metadata Variable and provide the variable name for the metadata as per the provided specification. Select the Message and check the Metadata added recently. Save and close the Document type. Save-All the Project changes. Export and deploy the project in the control center to use these metadata in the application.

short_description: Clone Copy request for an existing user (without SOD Check)
introduction: How to provide clone access for an existing user (without SOD Check)?
instructions: Since the user is already existing in the system, so please confirm with the user if the access is needed in addition or replacement of the existing access and then proceed accordingly. A. Clone copy in addition to user’s existing access : Steps: 1. Login to SAP CUA system. 2. Spool the clone’s access/roles by executing SUIM -> User -> Cross-System Information -> Users by Roles , in CUA system itself. 3. After executing step 2, following screen will appear. Give the clone ID (KEINKAR in this case) and the target system (LOGZ04C007 in this case), select “valid frm current to 31.12.9999” and click on execute. 4. Once we click on execute, we will get the list of roles assigned to the clone currently. 5. Copy the full list in an excel sheet, so that the old category/restricted roles can be excluded/filtered out from that list. 6. Repeat the above mentioned process to copy User’s existing access into excel sheet. 7. Exclude the roles from assignment which (user’s roles) are matching with clone’s access. Note : If the clone is assigned with any base role, that needs to be replaced with the country specific versions. If the country specific version does not exist, it needs to be created after taking the country specific Role owner’s approval. ZKON*/ZSUP*/ZWOR* are recognized as the Old category roles and needs to be excluded from the list. ZS* and ZS037_RSTR roles are restricted for assignments to business users in production environment. Exceptions: Users from ENA Units (US, Canada, Mexico) can be assigned with the old roles. ZWORFLSLSB and ZWORSLCASB are assigned as a part of QFB access and are not restricted. ZS024, ZS036, ZS037, ZS042, ZS046 and ZS*_USR are allowed and can be assigned on Role owner’s approval. 8. After filtering out the roles to be assigned, the same needs to be sent to the country specific Role Owner’s approval via e-mail. Please refer to the country Vs Person Responsible sheet for the appropriate country specific Role Owners. 9. Once the approval is received, please proceed for the access provisioning for the user by executing the transaction SU01 -> Roles tab. After copying all the roles, click SAVE. Please note that the access being provided in Z04 system should also be replicated to Z02 and Z03 system as well. B . Clone copy in replacement of user’s existing access : 1. Step 1 to Step 5 – Follow the process stated for Clone copy in addition to user’s existing access. Note : If the clone is assigned with any base role, that needs to be replaced with the country specific versions. If the country specific version does not exist, it needs to be created after taking the country specific Role owner’s approval. ZKON*/ZSUP*/ZWOR* are recognized as the Old category roles and needs to be excluded from the list. ZS* and ZS037_RSTR roles are restricted for assignments to business users in production environment. Exceptions: Users from ENA Units (US, Canada, Mexico) can be assigned with the old roles. ZWORFLSLSB and ZWORSLCASB are assigned as a part of QFB access and are not restricted. ZS024, ZS036, ZS037, ZS042, ZS046 and ZS*_USR are allowed and can be assigned on Role owner’s approval. 6. After filtering out the roles to be assigned, the same needs to be sent to the country specific Role Owner’s approval via e-mail. Please refer to the country Vs Person Responsible sheet for the appropriate country specific Role Owners. 7. Once the approval is received, Delimit the user’s existing access in CUA (SM4/055) (in this case LOGZ04C007). Change the ‘Valid to’ to one day less from present date (In this case present date is 21.09.2016 so ‘Valid to’ changed to 20.09.2016). If changes are done in LOGZ04C007system, do the same changes in LOGZ02C007 & LOGZ03C007 also. After delimiting the existing access of user, provide newly requested access by executing the transaction SU01 -> Roles tab. After copying all the roles, click SAVE. Please note that the access being provided in Z04 system should also be replicated to Z02 and Z03 system as well.

short_description: SRM Access Provisioning
introduction: How to process a SRM request?
instructions: SRM- Supplier Relationship management Definition: SAP supplier relationship management ( SRM ) is used with enterprise resource planning (ERP), product lifecycle management (PLM) and supply chain management (SCM) applications to ensure an effective implementation of cross-application business processes . 1. Login to SAP CUA system. 2.If user does not exists in SAP, please refer to article KBA174 for creation of user. 3.If user exists in SAP, please follow the below steps to provide SRM access. 3.1 Pull the list of roles ( In Validity) assigned to Clone. Check if we need to take any approval before access provisioning of requested roles 3.2 If the request is for any other role than ZKONSRMCOMP1 the approval from Julie Wilkin is required Type of SRM user Role SRM normal user ZKONSRMCOMP1 SRM super user – always request approval from Julie Wilkin ZKONSRMCOMP5 Additionally required to assign for all users ZS001 3.3.1 If the request contains only ZKONSRMCOMP1 role then please proceed with access provisioning of role (ZKONSRMCOMP1) in SAP R03 and R04 systems using SU01 -> Roles tab. After assigning the roles click on save. Note: Role: ZKONSRMCOMP1 contains below single roles, so if the request comes for either of below roles then also no need to take explicit approval. Security team can proceed with access provisioning. 3.3.2 If the request is for any other roles than ZKONSRMCOMP1 then approval from Julie Wilkin is required. 3.3.2.1. After receiving approval please proceed with access provisioning using SUIM->Roles tab and then save. 4. In order to access SRM application, along with SAP access provisiong users should be added into org structure in R03 and R04 Systems using transaction code: USERS_GEN. Note: Since Security team does not have access to transaction USERS_GEN in SAP R04 and R03 systems, Security team needs to contact SRM team in order to add users into org structure in R04 and R03 SAP systems.

short_description: Ask IT - Global locking/unlocking of users in SAP
introduction: How to lock/unlock the users globally in all SAP systems?
instructions: Global locking of users: In the below scenarios we need to lock the users globally in all SAP systems. When users left the organization/user’s validity got expired in SAP IDM. After conversion of Contractor to Permanent employee/Permanent to Contractor employee . In this document we will see the process of locking/unlocking of users globally when users left the organization or user’s validity got expired in SAP IDM. Users left the organization/user’s validity got expired in SAP IDM . If users left the organization then those users will get disabled in IDM (Identity Management) then an automatic mail will trigger to Security team with the Disabled users list from IDM team on daily basis. Copy all the users and execute SU10 transaction code with the mentioned users to check whether those users are existing in SAP or not. 3. Filter the users who are existing in the SAP system and follow the below steps in order to do the necessary changes while locking the users globally. 3.1 List the filtered users in SU10 transaction code and click on Change Icon as highlighted below. 3.2 After clicking on change option below screen will appear, there keep user group as Terminated, Accounting number to: INACTIVE and valid through: keep previous day date (suppose if you are doing this changes on 20.10.2016 then keep valid through as 19.10.2016) and then save. 3.3 After saving it will trigger some option like these changes will affect all the listed users there click on tick mark. 3.4 After selecting the tick mark it will go back to the SU10 transaction code with the listed users, lock the users globally in all SAP systems. 3.4.1 After choosing the below lock option pop-up will trigger that do you want lock all the listed users there select tick mark. Global unlocking of users: For unlocking of the users globally in all SAP systems Security team will follow the below process. Check the user’s validity in SAP IDM (KW portalidentity management tab) Note: KW portal link: http://kwportal.kone.com/irj/portal 2. After that select the Manage tab. 3. Search the user with either user id/email/user Name after that select the user then user details will arise, check whether user is having validity or not/account is enabled or disabled. Note: If user is not having validity in IDM then Account is Disabled option will be checked or else on top account is disabled is mentioned as true. 4. If user is having validity in IDM then follow the below process for unlocking of users in SAP. 4.1 In SAP go to SU01 -enter the user id-Change 4.2 Change the user group from TERMINATED to other group depends on the user’s country, validity through to the date same as IDM and remove INACTIVE/LEAVER in Accounting Number then save. 4.3 Select the lock/unlock option in Initial screen of SU01 transaction code. 4.4 Select the global unlocking of users in all SAP systems. 5. If user is disabled/not having validity in IDM then Security team will not unlock the user in SAP systems.

short_description: Global HR LTR Access Provisioning
introduction: How to provide Global HR LTR access?
instructions: 1. Login to SAP CUA system. 2. If the requested user is a new user, we need to create the user in Central system of CUA (SM4.055) as explained in Article: KBA174. 3. If user exists in SAP, please follow the below steps to provide Global HR LTR access. Note : Requests for Global HR LTR access should be raised or approved by the Key Users if approver is not there in the request form please refer to HR Approvers excel sheet for seeking local HR approval. short_description: USK FL Branch inventory set up, execution & support
introduction: How to set up the front line inventory runs for both the service vans and the main branch offices.
instructions: This document will show you how to set up the frontline inventory runs for both the service vans and the main branch offices. These are run based on an Excel schedule provided to GD by the business. The reason GD is involved is the fact that in order to set up the variants to run by the SAP program, you have to have access to SE38. step 1 – Excel Schedule There are two specific individuals that control the inventory process for the frontline in the USA. They are KEISMEE and KEISRAJ3 based out of ISC in India. These user ID’s will be used so remember them when setting up the batch jobs. Both of these individuals or Jennifer/Tom will send you an Excel sheet for the schedule. The Excel sheet will give you a list of branches and vans that will run each week for the inventory process. You use the information to set up the jobs then according to their schedule. The “planned count date” is always on a = Monday except specific requests. The “actual batch job run date” is always the = Sunday (right before this specific Monday) except specific requests. We always start the first batch job to run at 11 am, then each successive batch job is set up to run 5 minutes apart from each other. (i.e., so on a Sunday they would start at 11:00:00am, then 11:05:00, then 11:10:00, and so on and so forth). Step 2 – using SE38 Use SAP transaction SE38 to set up the program variants each week. The variant contains specific dates, so you need to set up each individual branch and van to run in their own variant. In order to get access to SE38, you must use FIREFIGHTER in Z04. So, for GD this is a normal way to access SE38. Then you run the transaction, you will use program = ZUSMR016. Then you can start to set up the details within the variant based on the information that you get from the Excel schedule you received from the business. I have used in the past a standard way of naming the variant. This is how it works. If the name of the variant is for example = BPI VAN 4243. Then this means = Branch Physical Inventory Van 4243. If the name of the variant is for example = BPI BR 240. Then this means = Branch Physical Inventory Branch 240 (or for Los Angeles). Also for some Branches there are sub branches, in that case do not forget to include the Main Branch number along with the numbers for all the subs, these can be either defined as a range or independent values in the definition of variant. Save each variant with this naming convention since you will use this later when you actually set up the batch job using SM36. A sample variant is shown on next page: Step 3 – using sm36 After you have completed setting up the specific variants for that week, you can now set up the batch job which will use those specific program variants to run the specific branch and vans for that week according to the Excel schedule received by the business. In SM36 you will start at this screen below --- this is where you put in the consistent naming convention for the job name. You then select = icon on toolbar of SM36. You will get this screen then where you will enter the user ID of the person who controls the inventory document. It will either be KEISMEE or KEISRAJ3 for BRANCHES and VANS. By putting in their user ID it lets them process the output of the batch job which is a job they will then execute in SM35 in order to get their inventory documents. As you can see above, the USER field holds the user ID of KEISMEE or KEISRAJ3. I put in the physical inventory program name = ZUSMR016. I put in the specifc name of the program variant that I wanted to run inventory for which is = BPI BR 0230. Which means = BRANCH PHYSICAL INVENTORY for BRANCH 0230. I then select this icon in the dialog pop up box screen = I can then set the print program settings to what is shown below for all VANS and all BRANCHES exactly the same each time. The output of the job processing goes out as a pdf attachment in the mail to the relevant user who can download the same and print at his place. I then select the green check mark to go to the next step. I then select the icon to save my settings in this specific variant for VAN 3102. After I select the SAVE icon, it will then show me this screen below --- I just select again right away the and then I see the next screen below where I can then set up the date & time for the batch job to run. On the screen above you can then select the icon where you can then set up the date and time for the batch job. You will then see this pop up dialog box below --- You can then select the icon in this pop up dialog box. It will then allow you to set specific date and time details as show below. As I said before, we always set up the batch job date for a SUNDAY prior to the Excel sheet schedule which is always showing the “planned count date” for the inventory. So, in this case, my Excel sheet said --- So, my SUNDAY date prior to this MONDAY date is = May 31, 2015. The first job will start at the hour of 11am on that SUNDAY. So, as you can see, this time is = 11:00:00. We use military time for all batch job set up. We then select for our job to be saved for that inventory run. Then select immediately again for the final job to be saved and released into the schedule. You will get the “Job Released” message at the bottom of the SAP screen which lets you know you were successful in setting up the batch job. Job BPI BR 0230 saved with status: Released I then always to into SM37 and pull up all of the batch jobs set up that day and verify the total quantity set up with the total quantity in the Excel sheet to make sure they match. You can run SM37 to find your batch jobs on the SUNDAY you have specified as shown below. Once you have run SM37 with these settings you will then get this result show below. You will get the “Job Released” message at the bottom of the SAP screen which lets you know you were successful in setting up the batch job. Job BPI BR 0230 saved with status: Released I then always to into SM37 and pull up all of the batch jobs set up that day and verify the total quantity set up with the total quantity in the Excel sheet to make sure they match. You can run SM37 to find your batch jobs on the SUNDAY you have specified as shown below. Once you have run SM37 with these settings you will then get this result show below. Step 6 – Support Level 1 Support Action: In the event that something occurs that stops the process, then the 1 st level of support would be for the ISC team to contact Manish Kashyap ( manish.kashyap@kone.com ) or call +91-8125264169 located in Hyderabad, India with the WIPRO support team. Level 2 Support Action: If the 1 st level of supports fails, then please contact Ramprasad Rachakonda ( Ramprasad.rachakonda@kone.com ) in the Brussels, Belgium time zone or call +32 494165145. Remember, this is Europe time zone. Level 3 Support Action: If all else fails, please contact Tom Berens ( tom.berens@kone.com or call 1-309-314-0340 in the USA time zone. Remember the difference in time between India and the USA is normally 10.5 hours. short_description: Link and instruction to SAP GRC Password reset tool and by sending an email to sapgrp@grp.kone.com
introduction: 1> How to reset SAP system password using Password Self-Service (PSS) functionality of SAP GRC? 2> How to reset SAP system password by sending an email to sapgrp@grp.kone.com?
instructions: Objective: This KB Article explain the steps to follow for resetting your SAP user account passwords using SAP GRC (Governance, Risk & Compliance) Password Self-Service(PSS) functionality. End user portal link : SAP Password Reset to reset your SAP system password Notes: 1> SAP GRC Password Self Service(PSS) functionality is enabled for below SAP systems connected to GRC : SAP ERP systems i.e. SAP ERP Development (Z01), SAP ERP Acceptance (Z02), SAP ERP Pre-Production(Z03), SAP ERP Production (Z04) and SAP ERP Sandbox (Z00), SAP BW Systems i.e. BI HANA Development (BW1), BI HANA Pre-Production (BW3) and BI HANA Production (BW4) SAP BH Systems i.e. Beacon Development (BH1), Beacon Test System (BH3), and Beacon Production (BH4) SAP SOLMAN Systems i.e. SOLMAN Development (SA1), SOLMAN Production (SA4) SAP GRC Systems i.e. GRC Development (GRD), GRC Acceptance (GRQ) and GRC Production (GRP) SAP HR systems i.e. HR Development (H01), HR Acceptance (H03) and HR Production (H04) 2> If you have already changed the password once today, whether manually or through GRC PSS (and forgotten it), SAP GRC system will not be able to reset the password for you today. You will be able to reset the password via PSS tomorrow only. 3> In case of any errors during any of the below mentioned steps for password reset, please drop a mail to commonplatforms.servicedesk@kone.com with all relevant details and error screenshots where available - CPSD will create a ticket and help to resolve the same. Important Note: While logging into Password Self-Service tool, In case system shows user ID "GRC_EUIBB" already logged in by some other user then uncheck / untick the "Cancel all existing logons" option and click on continue button as shown in below screen shot. If "Cancel all existing logons" is not unchecked / unticked and if you have clicked on "Continue" then system will close all other user's sessions who are already actively using the tool. After password reset is completed, please click on "Logoff " option as shown in the below screen shot instead of closing the browser window directly. So that your session will be closed (technically) successfully. We have two options to reset SAP Systems Password. Option 1> Using SAP GRC password self service functionality available in End user portal Option 2> By sending an email to sapgrp@grp.kone.com following corresponding instructions Option 1: Using SAP GRC password self service feature in End user portal STEP-BY-STEP INSTRUCTIONS step1. Clink e nd user portal link : SAP Password Reset to reset your SAP system password Once clicked on above link, system will open below log on screen step2 . Enter your Active Directory User ID (the one you use for logging on to Windows / KONE NET ) in the ‘Userid’ field and your Windows password in the ‘Password’ field and click on the ‘Log On’ button / press Enter. step3. click on ‘Password Self-Service’ link in the next window as shown below. step4. After clicking on Password Self Service it will redirect you to the new page/tab, here you need to select the system(s) in which the password needs to be changed. step5 . You can select single / multiple system(s) at the same time by clicking on the checkbox for which password reset is required. step6. Once the desired system(s) have been selected, click on 'Reset Password' button at bottom-left side of systems pane. Then In the "Status" column you will get an update 'Success' or 'Failure' As shown in the above screen, when the message is 'Success' in the 'Status' column, just below the 'Reset password' button you will get a message stating "Email has been sent to your mail box containing the password details." at the bottom of the above screen as highlighted. step7. You will receive an email as shown below (normally within 2 to 10 minutes of the above 'Success' status) to the email id linked to you SAP user account in the respective SAP system(s) which will contain the initial reset password. step8. You will need to go to the logon screen of the relevant SAP System. In the above sample case, we have reset the password for BH3 (Beacon Test System) and BW1 (BI HANA Development). Copy the password. Enter your user account name in the ‘User’ field and your newly generated initial password in the ‘Password’ field (Hint: you can copy the password from the notification e-mail using Ctrl + C & Ctrl + V to avoid spelling mistakes and irrelevant spaces): step9. Click on or press Enter it will give a pop-up which would suggest you to change the password with 2 text boxes, one with New password and the other will be Repeat password. Once you give the new password, you will be logged into the system, and you have successfully changed the password using GRC Password Self Service. Option 2: By sending an email to sapgrp@grp.kone.com following corresponding instructions explained below. STEP-BY-STEP INSTRUCTIONS step1. Open logon screen of the relevant SAP System and then you can see the message with steps to be followed to reset password by sending an email to sapgrp@grp.kone.com in highlighted area of below screen. For example , below screen shot shows instructions/ information to follow for resetting the password for SAP ERP Production (Z04) system on log on screen. Similarly respective SAP systems contains the instructions/ information for resetting the password on the logon screen. Step2. open a new e-mail window in outlook, enter mail ID sapgrp@grp.kone.com in "TO" field, mention SAP Password Reset in "Subject" field and in the E-mail body content mention Z04 (i.e., the system ID which is mentioned after Email body content in information area of above screen). Similarly, the system ID should be mentioned in the Email body of the respective SAP System based on the instruction available on the logon screen. NOTE: In the Email body please do not put any other text apart from the respective system ID (in this demo case, its Z04). Once all the details are entered, please click on “send” button. Step3. You will receive an email as shown below (normally within 2 to 10 minutes after sending email as shown in the above screen shot) to the email id linked to you SAP user account in the respective SAP system which will contain the initial reset password. step4. You will need to open the logon screen of the respective SAP System. In the above sample case, we have reset the password for Z04 ECC (SAP ERP) Production system. Copy the password. Enter your user account name in the ‘User’ field and your newly generated initial password in the ‘Password’ field (Hint: you can copy the password from the notification e-mail using Ctrl + C & Ctrl + V to avoid spelling mistakes and irrelevant spaces): Step5. Click on or press Enter it will give a pop-up which would suggest you to change the password with 2 text boxes, one with New password and the other will be Repeat password. Once you give the new password, you will be logged into the system, and you have successfully changed the password using email sapgrp@grp.kone.com . In case of any errors during any of the above mentioned steps for password reset, please mail commonplatforms.servicedesk@kone.com with all relevant details - and error screenshots where available - CPSD will create a ticket and help resolve the same. Please refer to FAQ document for common issues faced while using SAP GRC PSS application.

short_description: Windchill - Access and Permissions
introduction: How to request access and permissions to Windchill?
instructions: Windchill accounts and access to the PDMLink license can be requested via MyAccess role " Windchill: PDMLink User ". Once the line manager approves the request, the account will be automatically added. Please note that it may take 2–4 hours for the access to be reflected. This will grant access to the "Products" section in Windchill (PDMLink). If you require access to the "Projects" section (ProjectLink), please request role " Windchill: ProjectLink User ". Access to Windchill product content is preferably managed via user groups. Please discuss with your manager or team leader, what group membership you require and request the appropriate role(s) from MyAccess , for example "Windchill: User Group: CAT_CRD_SIGN_DESIGNER_UG". You can also use the Container Search in Windchill to find the appropriate user group. See further instructions here . After content owner's approval, it might take up to one hour before the newly added permissions have been synced to Windchill. Access to Windchill project content is managed by the project manager. You can also send a request to the support team via AskIT portal . (Something doesn't work - Mechanical Design - Creo, Creo View Express, Windchill, Link-It). NOTE: Do not comment this article for access requests. They will be ignored.

short_description: Windchill - Service Requests
introduction: How to request Windchill related services?
instructions: For some frequent requests, you can use the "Windchill Service Request" form found from AskIT portal . It contains services like resetting life-cycle state, check-in help request and other pre-defined Windchill related services. In case you can't find the appropriate request from the list of services, use the "Generic Service Request (Non Standard Catalog)" form . This form allows you to freely describe your request. Please be as specific as possible. In case something is broken or otherwise is not working as expected, create an incident via "Something doesn't work" section using the appropriate "service", e.g. Windchill. Please mention - when this problem started to occur - what you did just before and after that - what was the expected result - what was the actual result (attach screenshot if possible) - if the issue is reproducible with another document, with another account being logged in and on another computer

short_description: Software installation by Ask IT
introduction: How to install Software by Ask IT?
instructions: Ask IT to collect and confirm the below required details - Operating System: Hostname/Computer name: Required applications: ALWAYS instruct end users to: Go to the Software Center (only works in KONE network) Browse application categories (or use search box), select the application icon and hit Install The application will be automatically installed on client pc To do the software installation through SCCM, proceed as follows: 1. Open RDP with EUHCIMSHOPS101 and login with T2 credentials. 2. From search, open configuration manager console. 3. To assign a software to a KONE device, click on device collection, search for required package. 4. Right click on the desired package and select Add Resources . 5. From the next window ( Add Resources to Collection ), type in the computer name to which the package is supposed to be assigned and click Search . The computer name will then appear under the search results. Click Add -> Ok . 6. To complete the entire process, right click again on that software package and select Update Membership to respectively update the list of devices to which that package has been assigned. (This might take some time) and check for the member count (should be increased by 1 after adding the device to collection). To check the deployment status from SCCM, proceed as follows: 1. After 2-3 hours, deployment status can be checked from SCCM console. Click on monitoring à Deployments, search for required application, click on view status. 2. Respective deployment state will be recorded. Check for respective device ID in each status tab.

User needs to fill the KPM2_CREATE_FORM_v03 template with ADID, Firstname, Lastname, EmailID, Usertype, and Salesofficecode as mandatory. 3. User needs to fill the actions column as "YES" for whichever action the user requires access to and send the template back to us so that account can be created.

short_description: ASKIT How to speed up your Laptop
introduction: How to speed up your Laptop?
instructions: a ) Remove unwanted programs. There are a number of unwanted and unused programs on every computer which slow down its performance. It is highly recommended to remove these programs to free valuable resources of your computer. All you need to do is to go to your control panel and use add remove program section to uninstall unwanted software b)Delete cookies and temporary Internet files. Cookies and temporary Internet files occupy computer memory which results is slow computer performance. You can go to Internet options under tools on your browser and delete cookies. You can also use my computer section of your computer and right click your C drive and perform disk clean up to remove unwanted files. This is quick way to boost your laptop / PC performance. c)Perform Disk Defragmentation. Over the time, files are scattered in different parts of the computer. Disk defragmentation is the solution to clean up all the mess and tidy up all files and folders on hard drive. Disk defragmentation option is available under programs and system tools section. It may take an hour or more to complete this process but the performance boost you gain is noticeable immediately. Note that many recent operating systems defragment automatically on a regular basis, so running disk defragmenter may be a waste. Also note that it won't boost the performance of a solid state drive (SSD) d) Other files which might have a negative impact on the computer´s performance are stored under the below directory: c:\windows\temp folder Files stored under that file location can be deleted, as such files are just temporary files.

short_description: ASKIT FF How to export .pst file data in Outlook 2010
introduction: How to export .pst file data in Outlook 2010?
instructions: If you want to back up only some of your Outlook data, you can create a new backup .pst file of only the data that you want to save. This is also known as exporting .pst file data. For example, you might want to use this section if you have important information in only some folders and you have other, less important items in much larger folders. You can export only the important folders or contacts and omit folders like Sent Mail. Follow these steps to export a specific folder: Click the File tab. In the Outlook Options window, click Advanced . Click Export . In the Import and Export Wizard , click Export to a file , and then click Next . Click Outlook Data File (.pst) , and then click Next . Select the folder to export, and then click Next . Click Browse , and then select the location where you want the new .pst file to be saved. In the File Name box, type the name that you want to use for the new .pst file, and then click OK . Click Finish.

short_description: ASKIT FF How to use the Outlook Inbox Repair tool to repair .pst and .ost files
introduction: How to use the Outlook Inbox Repair tool to repair .pst and .ost files?
instructions: USER'S DETAILS (if Trusted Caller): COMPUTER NAME or IP ADDRESS: OUTLOOK VERSION: DETAILED DESCRIPTION: WHEN DID THE PROBLEM START? IS THE ISSUE REPRODUCABLE ON ANOTHER PC? IN OWA? NUMBER OF USERS IMPACTED: TROUBLESHOOTING STEPS TAKEN BY GSD (eg, new profile created, archiving of email): NOTES: 1. When assigning the case to the Mail and Messaging Team, always use the CI: server-exchange (kone-fi) 2. Always ensure to include and attach any available error messages and screenshots, log files, etc., in the case Personal Folders file (.pst): Data file that stores your messages and other items on your computer. You can assign a .pst file to be the default delivery location for e-mail messages. You can use a .pst to organize and back up items for safekeeping. Offline Folder file (.ost): The file on your hard disk that contains offline folders. The offline folder file has an .ost extension. You can create it automatically when you set up Outlook or when you first make a folder available offline. If you can't open your Personal Folders file (.pst) , Offline Folder file (.ost) , or you suspect that your .pst or .ost data file is corrupt, you can use the Inbox Repair tool ( Scanpst.exe ) , to diagnose and repair errors in the file. Exit Microsoft Outlook . Double-click Scanpst.exe , located at drive :Program FilesMicrosoft OfficeOFFICEx (x as a version of Office, example 2003, 2010 or 12) folder. Tip: If Scanpst is not located in the Office folder try to search option to find the tool. In the Enter the name of the file you want to scan box, enter the name of the .pst or .ost file that you want to check or click Browse to look for the file. To specify scan log options, click Options and click the option(s) desired. Click Start . When the scanning is completed, and if errors were found, you will be prompted to start the repair process. To change the name or location of the backup file created during the repair process, in the Enter name of backup file box, enter a new name, or click Browse to look for the file. Click Repair . Start Outlook using the profile that contains the .pst file that you tried to repair. On the Go menu, click Folder List (Folder List: Displays the folders available in your mailbox. To view subfolders, click the plus sign (+) next to the folder. If the Folder List is not visible, on the Go menu, click Folder List. In the Folder List , you may see a Recovered Personal Folders folder containing default Outlook folders or a Lost and Found folder. The recovered folders are usually empty, because this is a rebuilt .pst file. The Lost and Found folder contains folders and items that the Inbox Repair tool recovered. Items that are missing from the Lost and Found folder cannot be repaired. If you see a Recovered Personal Folders folder, you can create a new .pst file, and then drag the items in the Lost and Found folder into the new .pst file. When you have finished moving all items, you can remove the Recovered Personal Folders (.pst) file, including the Lost and Found folder, from your profile. Notes If you are able to open the original .pst file, you may be able to recover additional items from your damaged .pst file. By default, the Inbox Repair tool creates a file called file name .bak, which is a copy of the original .pst file with a different extension. The .bak file is located in the same folder as your original .pst file. You may be able to recover items from the .bak file that the Inbox Repair tool could not recover. Make a copy of the .bak file, giving the file a new name with a .pst extension. Import the "bak.pst" file, and then move any additional recovered items to the new .pst file that you created. A copy of the log file is written to the same location as the .pst file.

short_description: ASKIT How to use room mailboxes
introduction: How to use room mailboxes?
instructions: A room mailbox is a resource mailbox that’s assigned to a physical location, such as a conference room, an auditorium, or a training room. After an administrator creates room mailboxes, users can easily reserve rooms by including room mailboxes in meeting requests. How Do I Add a Room Mailbox in Microsoft Outlook Open Microsoft Outlook Click File tab in the Toolbar Click Account Settings button, select Account Settings Select the E-Mail tab Highlight your mailbox, click the Change button Click the More Settings button Select the Advance tab Click the Add button Type the Shared E-Mail Address Click the Apply and Ok buttons Click Next , Finish , and Close buttons Rooms can be set up to handle reservation requests in one of two ways: Moderated – (Auto accept is not enabled) Requests to reserve the resource must be approved or denied by a person (delegate). Auto-Accept - Requests to reserve the resource are automatically accepted, based on availability. A room mailbox has its own calendar, which can be viewed and modified just like a person's calendar. When a request for a room or piece of equipment is accepted in Exchange, an event is added to the resource's calendar. Moderated If a room is set up as a moderated resource, requests to reserve the room or equipment must be accepted or declined manually. This provides you with oversight to the resource and allows you more control over who has access to the resource and at what times. A moderated resource must have at least one delegate who is responsible for accepting or declining reservation requests, and it can have many delegates associated with it if you desire. When a request to reserve a room or piece of equipment is made, an email is sent to the delegate(s) for the resource. A delegate can accept or decline requests to reserve the resource, just like a person accepts or declines meeting requests for his or her own calendar. Delegates will also need to approve or deny updates to the meeting that change date and/or time. Auto-Accept A room or piece of equipment which is configured to auto-accept reservation requests handles its own calendar automatically. No one is responsible for accepting or declining reservation requests on behalf of the resource. This provides you with an easy way to manage resources that are up for grabs in a first-come, first-serve way. Exchange will always accept reservation requests so long as the following criteria are true. If one or both of the criteria cannot be met, Exchange will automatically decline the request. The room or piece of equipment is available (i.e. someone else has not already booked the resource for the requested date and time). The request falls within the resource's booking window. Booking Window All room and equipment resources in Exchange have a booking window, which defines how far in the future you can reserve a resource. By default, the booking window is 180 days. As indicated above, if a room or piece of equipment is configured to auto-accept and a request is made to reserve the resource outside of the booking window, Exchange will decline the request. For example, if you try to reserve a room one year from now (i.e. 365 days from now) and that room is set up to auto-accept, your request will be denied because it falls outside the booking window (which is 180 days by default). However, if you try to reserve that same room three months from now (i.e. 90 days from now), Exchange will automatically accept your request because it falls within the booking window.

short_description: AskIT SAP Password Unlock/Reset
introduction: How to reset/unlock SAP Password?
instructions: SAP SYSTEM: USER ID: USER AUTHENTICATON : ACTION REQUESTED FROM ASK IT? (put x in appropriate box(es)) [ ] ACCOUNT UNLOCK [ ] PASSWORD RESET IS THE USER EXPERIENCING REPEAT LOCKOUTS? (YES/NO) DESCRIPTION OF ACTIONS TAKEN BY ASK IT: CAN THE USER NOW LOG IN SUCCESSFULLY? DOES THE USER CONFIRM CASE CLOSURE? For SAP Password reset/account unlock the same user authentication process applies as for NT/Windows account PW reset. If the user request a new password because the old one is not working, ALWAYS remember to try Unlocking him in the system (no authentication needed - no password comunicate to the user), only if unlocking is not working please proceed with the steps below. It needs to be checked if User ID is within validity - this can be checked in extensionAttribute2 in Active Directory. If User ID is expired, AskIT will ask end user to contact his manager for his details updation in Workday. If User is still within validity, proceed with the below steps. User Validation Questions/Password Reset procedure Agent will confirm three things from the end user : KONE User ID Manager Name Telephone number If any of the 2 things matches AskIT agent will go ahead and reset the password for the end user. Else, AskIT will not reset the password and will ask end user to contact his manager for his details updation. Change of password for End user requested by a Manager/SDM. SDM/Manager is allowed to call/email in and request for a change/reset of password on behalf of the end user– If this happens on call, 1. Inform the SDM/Manager to share the “password reset email” for the end user to the Ask IT mailbox for respective location. 2. Ask IT analyst should record that email in the ticket as a reference and give SDM/Manager the password of the end user without asking any security questions. 3. If this happens on email, please call back SDM/Manager and share password. Note : No password should be shared on the email. Colleague is not allowed to call in and change password for a user– If this happens, tell the user on call to let the end user know that the end user alone must call in and get the password changed. How to use the SAP tool for password reset/Account unlock. 1) Open the KONE VDI. 2) Login by using KONE email id/user id and Password and Click "OK" 3) Click on the SAP Logon icon. 4) SAP window will open and SAP logon 760 window will appear. 5) Scroll down and look for System name: Z04 Produ.. SID: Z04 or other system - as requested. 6) Double click on Z04 Production(SSO). (or other system, as requested). and click logon. Note: SAP is now enabled with SSO for Password less login or For password login for example Z03 (or other system, as requested) - A new login screen will open Asking for user and Password: - Here you should choose Client 007 (for Z04) (Clients for other systems are Z03 : 007, Z02: 007, Z01: 055, BW4: 004, BW3 : 004, BW1 : 004 , BH4 : 004, BH3 : 004, BH1 : 004 , GRP : 100, GRQ : 100, GRD : 100, SA4 : 100, SA1 : 100 ) - The user is your KONE user ID in CAPITAL (KXXXX or EXT_XXXX or CON_XXXX - as applicable) - Enter your password and hit Enter 7) A new window will open, scroll down and double click on “User Maintenance”. or 8) - A new window will open where you can search for the User by entering their KONE user ID. - Under “User Maintenance” you have 3 options to click: - “Display” gives you information about the user. - “Lock/Unlock” gives you the option to lock or unlock a user account. - "Change Password” Gives you the option to reset a users SAP password. A. Password Status - active: Please enter the affected KONE user name, then click on "Change Password". You will be able to reset password here for the system you have logged on to. After login a new window will open. a) Write the new password b) Confirm by clicking tick Also, AskIT can inform on the call to user that Password Self-Service is now available via KONE Way portal and send below details to the user via mail : ******************************************************************************************* Did you know that by using the new Password self-service portal you can speed up your SAP password reset requests. Please use the link http://kwportal.kone.com/irj/portal/ The instructions for using the tool are available in this Knowledge Base Article(KBA480) – https://koneprod.service-now.com/kb?id=kb_article_view&sysparm_article=KBA480 ******************************************************************************************* Please note that if the user is asking to change the password or it locked to the QFB system this meaning SAP Z04. B. Password Status - Deactivated: If user password is deactivated then request user to login using SSO option. If still user facing issue to login using SSO then route ticket to SAP Security Team.

short_description: IRMA - How to add holidays and leave types in IRMA?
introduction: Question: How to add holidays and leave types in IRMA?
instructions: Introduction National bank holidays for a specific working unit are normally added into IRMA as part of the FL rollout project for 5 years ahead. The FLs may however need to modify or add additional holidays, and eventually of course the holidays will need to renewed in the system. FL Key users are not able to this themselves, as it requires access to Workforce Planner System Maintenance. The centrally configured holidays show in the Workforce Planner application Employee planning form as below, scroll over to show the description. The different leave types (= the “explanations” to absence which exist in Employee planning section, such as Illness, Vacation, Training etc.) are also configured per working unit as part of the rollout project, but modifications may be requested by the FL from ongoing support. The leave types can be seen in the Workforce Planner application when assigning a leave to an resource in Employee planning form. Adding Holidays and Leave types Login to IRMA Workforce Planner System Maintenance. Go to Views - > Admin view: Working units, employees, roles A. Adding Leave Types 1. Go to Tab -> Leaves types in the Admin view: Working units, employees, roles. 2. Right Click and select New… 3. Following window will pop up. 4. Now add the required information and press Ok 5. Drag the working unit from the working unit maintenance tab below as shown in the image. 6. Once you have made all modifications, synchronize the view to reflect the changes in Workforce Planner. A. Creating National Holidays 1. Go to Tab -> Holidays 2. Right Click and select New… 3. Add the name and date of the holiday. 4. Drag the working unit from the working unit maintenance tab below as shown in the image. 5. Once you have made all modifications, synchronize the view to reflect the changes in Workforce Planner. short_description: IRMA : How to update Certificates for IRMA QJLauncher URL (IIS)
introduction: How to update Certificates for IRMA QJLauncher URL (IIS)
instructions: Step 1: Get the Certificates from Platform Owner and skip to Step 4. Steps to get CSR (Step 1a to Step 3 Knowledge purpose) Step 1a: Create a CSR request based on the below information Use the URL https://certificatetools.com/ to create the same. Note : Make sure you don't fill anything in Subject Alternative Names Step 2: After submitting, download the CSR and Key as separate files with extensions .csr and .key respectively Step 3: Create an AskIT request as per below instructions and attach the .csr file in the request Step 4: Get the certificates from the certificates team (Point of Contact : kishore.baskar@kone.com / Connectivity_CSC@KONE.COM) and save it in the new 207 server (EUHCIMSWEBIR207) along with .key file (Saved while creating CSR) to this certificates folder Note: Certificates need to be installed in .pfx format. Step 5: we will receive the certificates in .crt format we need to convert it to .pfx Click Start --> Search openssl --> Run as Admin Step 6: Run the following command pkcs12 -export -out D:\Certificate\irma04_konenet_com_1704117661282\irma04_konenet.pfx -inkey D:\Certificate\irma04_konenet_com_1704117661282\irma04_konenet.key -in D:\Certificate\irma04_konenet_com_1704117661282\CorporationServiceCompanyRSAOVSSLCA.crt -in D:\Certificate\irma04_konenet_com_1704117661282\irma04_konenet_com.crt Note : Replace the path and file name as per your new path and file name Step 7: When prompted for password, type "kone" and press enter. Again it will ask you to enter password one more time. re-enter "kone" again and press enter. Now it will generate .pfx file inside the directory you provided in the command. Step 8: Now copy this .pfx file to the server where IIS is hosting the website and open the IIS inside the server Step 9: Open the Server Certificates Step 10: Click on the import button select your .pfx file and enter password as "kone". Select Web Hosting in the drop down. Once clicked on OK, the new certificate will be visible in the list with new expiry date Step 11: Click on the Default Web Site and then Bindings Step 12: Select your Website URL and then edit Step 13: Select the new certificate and then click OK Step 14: Now reload the website again in browser and verify the expiry
