short_description: IRMA Automatically getting closed, IRMA, IRMA access issue, Automatically closing
introduction: Question: How to fix IRMA automatically getting closing issue for the user? Keywords : IRMA Automatically getting closed, IRMA, IRMA access issue, Automatically closing
instructions: Steps to follow: If a user reports that IRMA is automatically getting closed the following steps need to be followed. Step 1: Get the below details from the user. Ask any role change happed for him recently. Ask user for the last login date(Aprox) without any issue. Step 2: If any role change happed for the user, please check the latest role of the user whether it has been added in our Quintiq Role mapping. If not please add the role mapping by the following path below. Note: If the role is added in the KT table directly go to (step 4) Open IRMA WP system maintenance panel and go to Edit --> Edit Knowledge. Right click on Workforce planner WP definition and lock the KT table. Again, right click and click on edit tab then select Role Mapping. Note: WP_Defnition should be locked before clicking on edit. Right click the table and add the role by add row --> enter the values--> Apply --> OK. Step 3: After adding the roles delete the user in both system maintenance and user administrator and create new access once again. (As per the IRMA access Creation KBA) Then ask the user to check the access. Step 4: If the changed role of the user is already available in the role mapping, then delete the user in both system maintenance and user administrator. Step 5: After deleting the user from the application, open PAM server of IRMA Prod or Pre prod (i.e 101 or 201 server) and follow the below path for user settings and search for the user ID profile folder. Path for prod: E:\Quintiq\KoneIrma_WP_Prod_6110\var\Irma_ibr_toorma\_var\_Main\UserSettings Path for pre prod: D:\Quintiq\KoneIrma_WP_Test_6110\var\Irma_ibr_toorma\_var\_Main\UserSettings Step6: After opening the userID profile folder you could see the workforce planner folder into it. Create a new folder and name it as backup then just open the workforce planner folder and check the logs by sorting it by date. Step7: From the Workforce planner folder, with respect to the user given information of last successful login, cut the logs up to last successful login and paste it in backup folder created. Step 8: Again, re provide the access to the user for IRMA with respect to IRMA access creation KBA KB000A8703 and ask the user to check for the IRMA access.

short_description: How to Open new Integration incident ticket with Verizon
introduction: How to Open new Integration incident ticket with Verizon
instructions: Step1:- open service now(askit.kone.com/ koneprod.service-now.com)--> Goto Workspace--> Select Service operation Workspace. Step2:- On the List column scroll down to incident and open incident case --> Click new to create new incident. Step3:- Please fill the required information’s(highlighted one) and save the incident ticket. (sample screenshot provided below) Step4:- Once the Incident ticket is created, click create incident task, and fill the same description on the task ticket as well. Step5:- Near the configuration item(CI) Tab you need to mention “*( yoursiteID) ” For Example:- If your site is au009, then type “ *au009 ” in the Configuration item tab and you will see all the CI details for this site. Now you can choose either IP switch or IP router (for this site). Select the appropriate option accordingly. Step6:- Once the information is updated correctly, mark the queue name as KONE.Verizon.Integration ( mandatory ) and also fill the work notes (any additional information from user) and save the task ticket. if you have any screenshot and document for this issue from user, please attach it in the attachment tab. Once you save this ticket, Verizon ticket will be automatically created for this case Step7:- Verizon will start working on this case and status will be changed as work in progress . Once Verizon update any comment on the Task ticket, then the status will changes to pending . Pending status with sub-status details 1.Pending – Maintenance time (Verizon planned for maintenance window for testing) No action from AskIT 2.Pending – Monitoring (Verizon keep this ticket for monitoring) No action from AskIT 3.Pending – Update customer (Action from AskIT as well as Verizon) Note:- When the ticket is pending from Update customer, AskIT need to copy the Verizon information in Task ticket and share it with RSM & LCON as per KBA KB000A8884. 4.Pending – Customer time (pending for action from Kone) Note:- When the ticket is pending from customer time, AskIT need to copy the Verizon information in Task ticket and share it with RSM & LCON as per KBA KB000A8884. You can see the Verizon updates on Activity tab, and respond back to Verizon with updated comments in Activity tab and save the ticket.

short_description: Remote support solutions for Intune enrolled field devices
introduction: Question: How can field users be remote supported How to use TeamViewer to remote support Intune enrolled devices
instructions: Answers: Intune mobile device management tool does not provide capability to remote support Intune enrolled devices. If remote support capability is needed, Microsoft Teams screensharing is in most of the cases a sufficient solution for that. If screensharing is not sufficient, with an additional cost, TeamViewer solution can be used to remote control end user mobile devices. Due to the additional costs involved, TeamViewer license will be granted only for the persons needing it. Screensharing with Microsoft Teams is a sufficient remote support solution in most of the countries and there are no additional costs involved with Teams. With Teams, it is possible to screenshare work profile, personal profile and devices settings of an Intune enrolled device as needed. Start a Teams meeting with the person to be supported (invite a meeting or initiate a call from Team chat) Ask the person to share their mobile device screen. Even though the Teams app is installed in the work profile, the whole device, including personal profile and device settings, can be shared if so needed. Remote control with TeamViewer : guidelines for requesting and using the solution available here .

short_description: How to troubleshoot WebSSO
introduction: WebSSO, troubleshooting
instructions: KBA HOW TO TROUBLESHOOT WebSSO This document provides a basic troubleshooting to find the issue from Azure AD end or application end, when the application is using Azure active directory for WebSSO. Contents Basic checks for end users . 3 Troubleshooting steps in Azure AD (WEBSSO team only) : 11 Flow diagram Note: The errors below are common errors which are reported most frequently. Please follow below flow chart and find steps described below: Note: Modifications in Azure AD/Microsoft Entra ID enterprise/App registration should have app owner role or Application Admin privilege access. Many of the below steps require to verify some settings from the KONE SSO end from the Azure Active Directory. To do the setting verification, you should also know (or guess correctly) the name of the SSO configuration that leads to the application: 1. Go to https://aad.portal.azure.com/ 2. Type in your KONE credentials. Typically MFA is also required 3. Select from the left navigation “Enterprise applications” 4. Type the name of the application into the search bar stating “Search by application name or object ID”. The naming is typically “KONE SSO [Name of the application]”. Unfortunately, the search isn’t good and cannot find from the middle of the name. Thus, in practice you always have to type “KONE SSO “and then try to guess the next word or beginning of the next word If you cannot find the correct application, please contact SSO support to ask for the name of it. Sign-in logs page link: Basic checks for end users Do you need to renew the secret? Here's how to do that: NOTE: After renewing the secrets and certificates remember the remove the old secret and certificate from the Azure! Client Secrets (Yes/No) Application team should know if the secret is needed or not. If you need to renew the Client Secrets then follow steps below. -Go to Entra and find the application you are the owner. -Click on Certificates & secrets and then click +New client secret as in screenshot below: Note: Client secret can be customized for 2 years-but default valid period is 6 months. Once added, new secret value and secret ID will be created, copy immediately and keep it safe as the same needs to be integrated in application end for security purpose (As the value will be hidden after few minutes and cannot be retrieved if loss) Note as below in the example below Secret Value: XXXXXXXXXXX808Q~ELztGO3cPSpWc~zF5 Secret ID : XXXXXXXXX96dd-aa2d-4192-acbf-5b5 Now you have renewed the secret. Step - 1) Try accessing application in incognito mode via Edge browser and try clearing the cache Steps to clear cache: Open edge browser, click on “three dots” at the top right corner and select “settings”. Under settings, click on “privacy, search and services” select “clear browsing data” and “time range” as “All time” and “clear now” as per below screen shot: Step-2) Check if the KONE SSO is up and running for the other appli c ations (Single/ Multiple users) Login yourself to 1 or 2 other applications that use AAD SSO. You can test e.g. with these 2: kone-prod.adobecqms.net (This should be open for all users) Myaccess.kone.com (This should open for all users) Note: if the user hasn’t enrolled MFA for him/herself (yet or not at all), then MyAccess doesn’t work If the login worked to these other application(s), then we know that SSO as a whole is not down, but the problem is either in the individual application SSO (application – SSO connection), or in individual user settings. If all worked for the users, we know now, that the user’s account in general is ok in the Azure Active Directory end. We have now been able to narrow down the possible problem to be either in the individual application SSO (application – SSO connection), or in individual users’ rights concerning that one application. Note: If SSO service is down, reach out to Web SSO support to raise Microsoft ticket Reference link to check global outage details: Azure status Step-3) The problem is only for one/some of the users, some users can login with SSO to the application, some cannot. Check if the user who has problems is authorized to use the application from the SSO end. See below Error, AADSTS50105 AADSTS50105 Reference link: Error AADSTS50105 - The signed in user is not assigned to a role for the application. - Active Directory | Microsoft Learn Reason: The signed in user is not assigned to a role for the application. Resolution: a) Please navigate to Azure portal and search for application b) Under manage-->Properties(check application Assignment required(Yes/No)). If No, access is allowed for all users, and in this case the AADSTS50105 error should not have come to the user. Please contact SSO support to help with troubleshooting. c)If yes, User should be part of User/Groups d)If user is not part of User Groups, the needed access role should be requested from the My access portal. Role can be requested by user or App owner on behalf of users. Step-4. The problem is for all users of the application, none of the users can login with SSO Application team checks: Start browser in incognito mode. Initiate the application login with the incognito browser IF the SSO login UI doesn’t come up at all then the problem is somewhere in the application, application host server or network connectivity from the application to SSO service. Example screen shots for reference: Server Error: Network Connectivity error: If SSO sign-in logs are successful then Authentication from Kone is working as expected and should check where URL is landing, if the site is landing on Application page then issue is with Application end. App owner should check with app vendor team like what parameters application is looking for...(ex: token configuration, App role, attribute, group claims etc...) Step-5) Azure logs why SSO is failing checks: IF the login SSO UI is initiated for the user… Check application Audit logs with user who has owner/Admin rights Owner can from Azure AD, if something has been recently changed in the SSO end configuration for the application (this can only be done by SSO integration/Application owners, see “Owner” tab from the left, or by AAD administrator – please ask SSO support to check this) : Open the application SSO settings from AAD. Open “Audit logs” from the left-hand navigation (available only for “Owners”) The list shows all the changes that have been done to the SSO configuration during the past 30 days. If the list is empty, then you can be sure that nothing has changed in the SSO end for the application. In practice the error is somewhere else e.g. in connectivity from the application end, something has changed in the application end SSO configuration (very often inadvertently as part of an application change or application upgrade) Note: the change log is very precise. If individual users or individual groups have been added in the “Users tab”, it will show here. If any attribute in any of the SSO configuration has been changed (even those attributes that have no functional impact, e.g. “Notes” field”) If there ARE changes , then carefully examine the done changes, the dates of the changes etc. and see if there is correlation between when the changes were done, and when the problems started occurring (there should be extremely close connection) the experienced behavior, and the changes that have been done. Please contact SSO support and raise your observations to them. Check application sign-in logs with user who has owner/Admin rights Open the Enterprise applications blade from left navigation and search for application and under Activity click “sign -in logs” If SSO sign-in logs are successful then Authentication from Kone is working as expected and should check where url is landing, if the site is landing on Application page then issue is with Application end. User should check with app vendor team like what parameters application is looking for...(ex: token configuration, App role, attribute, group claims etc...) If sign in successful and failing at Conditional access, involve SSO support, they will involve AAD global admin for Conditional access policy modifications. For analyzing sign in logs, attaching reference link: Sign-in logs in Azure Active Directory - Microsoft Entra | Microsoft Learn 5-a) For SAML applications: Check if the certificate that needs to be exchanged between the application and the SSO service, has expired Open the application SSO settings from AAD. Open Single sign-on tab from the second layer navigation from the left If you can see something here: then this is SAML2.0 based on SSO integration and you can continue reading the fields on this page Check the (3) SAML Certificates. Does the “Expiration” show future value? If yes, then you know that the certificate is still valid As a side step, you can check if the “Notification Email” has the correct value i.e. some application side technical contact, who is responsible for the keeping the various application certificates up-to-date. If contact is not correct, then you should ask one of the people mentioned on the “Owners” tab to update the correct contact there. If you saw nothing of the above in “Single sign-on tab”, then the application is using OAuth as SSO integration protocol. 5-b) For Open Id/OAuth2.0 protocol-based applications Client secret check: navigate to App registration https://portal.azure.com/#view/Microsoft_AAD_IAM/ActiveDirectoryMenuBlade/~/RegisteredApps check… for application and navigate to certificate and secrets, check for expiry date 5-c) AADSTS50011(Reply/redirect url) Reason: The redirect URI/ Reply Url specified in the request does not match the redirect URIs configured for the application Resolution: Make sure the redirect URI sent in the request matches one added to your application in the Azure portal(Enterprise/App registration). Reference link: Error AADSTS50011 the redirect URI does not match the redirect URIs configured for the application - Active Directory | Microsoft Learn 5-d) AADSTS650056 (Misconfigured application) Reason: The Issuer attribute sent from the application to Azure AD in the SAML request doesn’t match the Identifier value configured for the application in Azure AD. Resolution Ensure that the Issuer attribute in the SAML request/Application end matches the Identifier/Entity Id value configured in Azure AD SSO set up. Verify that the value if Identifier in Azure SSO settings matches the value of identifier value displayed in the error. Reference link: Error AADSTS650056 - Misconfigured application - Active Directory | Microsoft Learn Troubleshooting steps in Azure AD (WEBSSO team only) : The first step for the SSO team to check, in case SSO team is involved into the trouble shooting: Try to login to the application yourself. Experience yourself the problem that the user reported. You can draw much better conclusions of the possible problem (when correlating with other trouble shooting steps) if you can see the full sign-in interaction yourself, not just some “individual screen shot”. Primary: Audit logs to be checked for Application/User if any files are modified Secondary: Need to check user sign-ins and application sign-ins to verify issue if the SSO authentication works for the user If SSO authentication says “success”  Compare the authentications between the users who succeed and who don’t succeed. Are there some commonalities in the log? Need to involved App Vendor team: If User request is not reaching Azure AD due to App host server issues(network/Connectivity) User Authentication is successful and No failure logs in Azure AD Basic Azure error codes: Azure AD authentication & authorization error codes - Microsoft Entra | Microsoft Learn

Choose the required Authentication protocol. Azure AD supports all three of the most common integration methods (SAML 2.0, OpenID Connect and OAuth). Choosing an SSO method depends on how the application is configured for authentication. Cloud applications can use federation-based options, such as OpenID Connect, OAuth, and SAML. Application Vendor team to help choose the required protocol as per the application dependency. Before proceeding with the integration, it is mandatory to register the application in CMDB (Which will help SSO service in notifying in case of any updates/modifications) Steps to register the application in CMDB Click on the link https://koneprod.service-now.com/ Search for applications /business applications and choose new. Complete the form and get the application registered in CMDB database, if you have any queries kindly contact the ASK IT portal (CMDB is not in SSO scope of support) Note: To integrating the application, it is mandatory to have either of the roles “Application Administrator” “Global Administrator” Since app owners will not have the above roles, owners should raise Service request to Web SSO Team for integrating Application/creating empty container. Instructions for raising Service request: For creating Service Request from AskIT – Click on https://koneprod.service-now.com/esc?id=kone_ec_dashboard Search with single sign-on and click on 'single sign-on with AAD' as per below screenshot, then fill the required details, attach the filled intake form and submit the Service Request. Note: below details to be filled for creating empty container: Application Name Application Owner contact details (KONE person who is configuring the app) Application CMDB ID (If available) Federation Authentication Protocol required (SAML/OAuth2/OpenID-Connect) Pre-integrated (Below link to be used for checking if application is pre-integrated: Tutorial: Azure Active Directory single sign-on (SSO) integration with Salesforce - Microsoft Entra | Microsoft Learn) Search for the application using “Filter by title” as highlighted below: Once, empty container is created, Web SSO Team will share below details with app owner who can proceed further and modify all the fields: Application Name: KONE SSO XXXX Application Id (Client Id): Launching the portal: Link to launch Azure portal: https://portal.azure.com/ Steps to be followed for integrating SAML based app: Enter credentials to login the portal, once in Click on View as below Click on Enterprise Applications blade under Manage Search with the shared name as below: Properties In properties section, you can edit name, assignment required and update the notes field Editing application name In case app owner wants to change App name in future, edit “Name” field and “Save” Assignment required: Usually, the User assignment is preferred to be set as NO, however if you need to restrict to groups/users follow next step. Access is allowed for All KONE Users, If the Assignment required is set to NO. To restrict application access to a specific set of users/groups, below highlighted field is set to YES Notes: Field is used to add CMDB ID and the format for filling is “Instance ID: INST_XXXX_X_X_X_XX_XX_XXXXXX” and vendor contact and click on “save” Users and groups: When the application is on-boarded to MyAccess (mandatory), then MyAccess automatically adds also an "[application]_All_Users" group, which includes all the roles and the role members of that application. Please use that group in assigning users of the application. Click on Users and groups followed by Add user/group and select the user/group as per requirements. Note: The application should be onboarded to my access, the group should be added already in Azure AD for the role to be available for assignment. Setting up Owners: For adding additional owners, Navigate to Owners blade and click on Add, search the user and click on Select and Save Note: Once added the Owner will have complete access to Modify the SSO integration of the application. Single Sign-on: Click on Single Sign-on blade followed by SAML as highlighted below. You can set up SAML SSO in two ways Uploading the Metadata file (shared by Vendor team, no manual steps are required) Manually required fields need to be added as marked below which are Entity ID (Mandatory) Reply URL (Mandatory) Sign on URL Relay State Logout URL Click on Edit to update the basic SAML configuration like Identifier, Reply URL as below and save Attributes and claims This can often be left untouched as in default. The only thing one should consider changing in the claims mapping is email. Reason: Email address changes fairly often in KONE i.e. it is not immutable. A quite common example when it changes: an external has initially no KONE email and then his email is john.doe@externalcompany.com. When later on it is identified that the user needs KONE O365 licenses (e.g. KONE Teams or like), then the user also gets kone email, in this example john.doe@kone.com. Thus, a strong recommendation is: Clear rule: if the application is not sending emails to users (notifications or similar), then never map email address to application’s email claim, instead use UPN. This avoids problems down the line, and is robust solution since UPN is very rarely changing If application does send emails to the users, then If the application side email address claim is in the application end the key value to the user, then consider mapping UserPrincipalName from KONE side to the application email field. This means that for some external users the email notifications don’t work but that might then be the cost to take to ensure that the user mapping ~always stays. Also, raise a feature request to the software vendor that email shouldn’t be the key value to the user, instead UPN/ UserID or similar should be used. As per Application Vendor team requirement, app owner can download and share required certificate format i.e Base64/Raw/PEM/ XML [LK1] To establish connectivity between the Identity Provider (Azure) and the application end, below IDP details needs to be configured in Application end. IDP Details - Application ID - Tenant ID - 2bb82c64-2eb1-43f7-8862-fdc1d2333b50 Login URL - https://login.microsoftonline.com/2bb82c64-2eb1-43f7-8862-fdc1d2333b50/saml2 Azure AD Identifier -https://sts.windows.net/2bb82c64-2eb1-43f7-8862-fdc1d2333b50/ Logout URL - https://login.microsoftonline.com/2bb82c64-2eb1-43f7-8862-fdc1d2333b50/saml2 App Federation Metadata Url Certificate details Conditional Access: Filter for applications Currently Conditional Access policies can be applied to all apps or to individual apps. Application filters for Conditional Access allow organizations to tag service principals with custom attributes. These custom attributes are then added to their Conditional Access policies. Filters for applications are evaluated at token issuance runtime, a common question is if apps are assigned at runtime or configuration time. Assign roles Assign Microsoft Entra roles to users - Microsoft Entra ID | Microsoft Learn Assign a custom security attribute to an application When you don't have a service principal listed in your tenant, it can't be targeted. The Office 365suite is an example of one such service principal. Sign in to the Microsoft Entra admin center (Microsoft Entra admin center) as at least a Conditional Access Administrator. Browse to Identity > Applications > Enterprise applications. Search the service principal you want to apply a custom security attribute For Ex: “KONE SSO SAML App”. Under Manage > Custom security attributes, Select Add assignment. Under Attribute set, select ConditionalAccessApp filter. Under Attribute name, select policyRequirement. Select the MFAfrequency: Once you have done the above step you can test the policy. Once all the details are added in Application end, app owner/User to test the application and validate SSO access This completes basic SAML application configuration. ___________________________________________________________________________________ Steps to be followed for integrating the application using OAuth2/OpenID-Connect: Launching Azure Portal: Link to launch portal: https://portal.azure.com/ Enter credentials to login to the portal, once in Click on View as below Click on App Registrations as shown below: Search for application: Select the application: Authentication: Click on Authentication and then Add a platform. Choose the type of Platform i.e. Web or alter options in case of vendor team’s requirement, since most of the applications are configured in Web platform. Then add the Redirect URls for eg in our case will be as below, you can also add more URIs by clicking on add URI Eg: https://userguideappreg.konenet.com Logout URL if available eg as below in our case https:// userguideappreg.konenet.com Click on save Client Secrets (Yes/No) Note that secret is not required, this is application team requirement. If you need to renew the Client Secrets then follow steps below. Go to Entra and find the application you are the owner. Click on Certificates & secrets and then click +New client secre t as in screenshot below: Note: Client secret can be customized for 2 years-but default valid period is 6 months. Once added, new secret value and secret ID will be created, copy immediately and keep it safe as the same needs to be integrated in application end for security purpose (As the value will be hidden after few minutes and cannot be retrieved if loss) Note as below in the example below Secret Value: XXXXXXXXXXX808Q~ELztGO3cPSpWc~zF5 Secret ID : XXXXXXXXX96dd-aa2d-4192-acbf-5b5 Now you have renewed the secret. Token Configuration- (If applicable) Below claims will be shared by vendor team, accordingly option to be selected. API Permission (Delegated/Application) Click on API permissions > Add a permission > Microsoft Graph Select the requirement as per Vendor team suggestions (Application/Delegated) Select the type of permission required and click Add Permissions. The API permissions by default will be as below which is: User.Read anything else needs to be added explicitly depending on your requirements. *API permissions to be requested to SSO team for delegated access and application level to the Global Admins *Admin consent to be requested to SSO team Owners Ensure that the owners are updated, for efficient management, at least 2 owners are required. To update owner, click Owners blade and then click Add owners, Select the owner by searching in the search bar, choose and update as below. The owners will be displayed as below Renaming application, Limiting application access and Notes: Click on Home, Click Enterprise Applications blade followed by name eg:“KONE SSO User Guide” Search for application and select: Properties: In properties section, you can edit name, assignment required and update the notes field Editing application name In case app owner wants to change App name in future, edit “Name” field and “Save” Assignment required: Usually, the User assignment is preferred to be set as NO, however if you need to restrict to groups/users follow next step. Access is allowed for All KONE Users, If the Assignment required is set to NO. To restrict application access to a specific set of users/groups, below highlighted field is set to YES Notes: Field is used to add CMDB ID, and vendor contact and click on “save” Users and Groups: When the application is on-boarded to MyAccess (mandatory), then MyAccess automatically adds also an "[application]_All_Users" group, which includes all the roles and the role members of that application. Please use that group in assigning users of the application. Click on Users and groups followed by Add user/group and select the user/group as per requirements. Note: The application should be onboarded to my access, the group should be added already in Azure AD for the role to be assigned. Now the application has been integrated successfully and configured, you can proceed to test the application if it works fine. Check application sign-in logs with user who is added as an owner of the application: Open the Enterprise applications blade from left navigation and search for application and under Activity click “sign -in logs” If SSO sign-in logs are successful then Authentication from KONE is working as expected and User now should check where error page is landing, if the error is landing on Application page then issue is with Application end. User should check with app vendor team like what parameters application is looking for...(ex: token configuration, App role, attribute, group claims etc...) If sign in successful and failing at Conditional access, involve SSO support, they will involve AAD global admin for Conditional access policy modifications. For analyzing sign in logs, attaching reference link: Sign-in logs in Azure Active Directory - Microsoft Entra | Microsoft Learn Troubleshooting Guide Who is "we"? short_description: Create and maintain Test Account in MyAccess
introduction: What is a test account? Who can create? How to create a Test account in MyAccess? How to update the Test account in MyAccess? How to view Test account in MyAccess? Warning Messages before disable of Test account. Disable/Enable/Delete Test account.
instructions: What is Test account? Software and testing teams often need to create test accounts to AD for manual testing, but the accounts are often "forgotten" after testing and they stay enabled in AD without owner information. These forgotten enabled accounts pose a security risk for KONE. Therefore, all test accounts are to be managed from MyAccess. Test account name will be auto generated with “TEST_”. Who can create? Test accounts for software testing can be created to AD and life cycle managed from MyAccess only by users who have a role “MyAccess: Test Account Manager” assigned in MyAccess. If user does not have above role, option for ‘Test Account’ will not be shown on “Create AD account” -page. You can raise a request in MyAccess for this by searching for role “MyAccess: Test Account Manager”. The request will come for review and approval of the Identity and Access Management team. How to create a Test account in MyAccess Login to MyAccess: https://myaccess.kone.com . On the Home page you need to select from top-left corner's drop down (icon with 3 lines) Manage Access > Manage Identity > Create AD Account On click of ‘Create AD account’, account type selection form is presented to user with option for ‘Test Account’ If user does not have role ‘MyAccess: Test Account Manager’, option for ‘Test Account’ will not be shown. In that case, you need to navigate to “Manage User Access” and request for yourself that role. If the user has the role, the user is presented with an account creation form with fields to be filled out such as – Full Account Name Please note that the full account name will be generated with “TEST_”+ the account name given in field. Account Name (required field) Max characters allowed 15. No Special characters allowed. Account Name should be unique. The Account Name doesn't accept _T1, _T2, _T3 or _ADM. System/Application Name (required field) Valid from (required field) refers to date since when account is created. Valid to (required field) refers to the date until when the account will be active. Test account will expire and get disabled after this date if no actions are taken by the account owners to extend the validity period. You can select a date maximum 180 days in future. Country (required field) refers to the region where Test Account belongs. This is requested merely for technical reasons. Description of account (required field) Please describe what is the account used for. Max characters allowed 50. Owner (required field) is the primary owner of the account who will receive the initial Test account password. You can optionally provide more than one owner for the test account. If you do so, at least one of the owners must be a KONE employee. In this case, the password will be delivered to all the owners. Deputy owner (required field) is the other owner of the account who will also be able to update the account during its life cycle. You can optionally provide more than one deputy owner for the test account. If you do so, at least one of the deputy owners must be a KONE employee. Allowed Logon Computers is a mandatory field. Please specify the allowed logon computer(s)(Servers) for the account. If the account is used only in the cloud environment, kindly update the Account Usage to Cloud and add "DummyComputer" as the allowed logon computer. If "DummyComputer" is added as allowed logon computer, then the account cannot login to any domain joined servers. If the account needs to login to domain joined server(s), please add those server(s) as allowed logon computers. On filling up the fields of form, user need to click ‘Next’ button on right corner. This will present a read only form with entered details for different fields. To make any changes to field values, user need to click ‘Back’ button on left corner. If entered details are correct, then user need to click ‘Submit’ button on right corner of UI page. On successful submission of the request, a message will be shown on the screen as below and the account will be created within 30 minutes time. Test Account will be created in Active Directory. Newly created test account will be memberOf AD group -TestAccountGroup. Password for created account is sent by email to account owner once the account is created. How to update test account in MyAccess Login to MyAccess https://myaccess.kone.com . On the Home page you need to select from top-left corner's drop down (icon with 3 lines) Manage Access > Manage Identity > Edit Test account. By clicking ‘Edit AD account’, account selection form is presented to user with dropdown list option to select the account which is having “Active Directory” account for edit. On click of ‘Edit’ button on right corner, Edit Test Account page opens with some of the fields available for update. Fields available for update are System/Application Name Valid To Description, Deputy Owner , Owner , Allowed Logon Computers On filling up the fields of form, user need to click ‘Confirm’ button on right corner. This will present a read only form with entered details for different fields. To make any changes to field values, user need to click ‘Back’ button on left corner. If entered details are correct, then user need to click ‘Submit’ button on right corner of UI page. This will submit the form for Test Account update. On successful submission of the request, a message will be shown on the screen as below and the account will be updated within 30 minutes time. NOTE: Warning message before account gets disabled: MyAccess will send multiple warnings to both Owner and Deputy Owner before account is disabled so that the owners will have enough time to go extend the validity if needed. The warnings are being sent 60, 30, 14, 7 and 1 day(s) before the validity of the account ends. If the account is not extended 30 days before expiration, in addition to the account owner and deputy owners, corresponding CMDB application owners also will receive the notification to ensure the accounts are extended on time. The notification will look as below.

short_description: Robot Account - create and Manage in MyAccess
introduction: Question What is Robot account? Who can create? How to create Robot account in MyAccess? How to update Robot account in MyAccess? How to view Robot account in MyAccess? Disable/Enable/Delete Robot account.
instructions: Question What is Robot account? Who can create? How to create Robot account in MyAccess? How to update Robot account in MyAccess? How to view Robot account in MyAccess? Disable/Enable/Delete Robot account. Answer What is Robot account? Robot Account represents a non-person identity that has an AD account that is by default in enabled state if the identity is active. The account is used by a robotic process automation (RPA) platform to execute human-like tasks in KONE environment. From MyAccess two types of robot account can be created – BluePrism and Other Script Robots. Robot account name will be auto generated as per robot type selected while creating. “AUT_”+”PXXXX”+”BXX” (For BluePrism type robots) and “SCR_AUT_”+”PXXXX”+”BXX” (For Other Script robot type) Who can create? Robot accounts can be created to AD and life cycle managed from MyAccess only by users who have a role “MyAccess: Robot Account Manager” assigned in MyAccess. If user does not have above role, option for ‘Robot Account’ will be not be shown on “Create AD account” -page. You can raise a request in MyAccess for this by searching for role “MyAccess: Robot Account Manager”. The request will come for review and approval of Identity and Access Management team. How to create Robot account in MyAccess Login to MyAccess: https://myaccess.kone.com . On the Home page you need to select from top-left corner's drop down (icon with 3 lines) Manage Access > Manage Identity > Create AD Account On click of ‘Create AD account’, account type selection form is presented to user with option for ‘Robot Account’ If user does not have role ‘MyAccess: Robot Account Manager’, option for ‘Robot Account’ will be not be shown. In that case, you need to navigate to “Manage User Access” and request for yourself that role. If user has the role, user is presented with account creation form with fields to be filled up such as – Robot type: Default radio button option selected - ‘BluePrism’. Robot Account Name Please note that the robot account name will be generated as per robot type selected “AUT_”+”PXXXX”+”BXX” (for BluePrism type) or “SCR_AUT_”+”PXXXX”+”BXX” (for Other Script robot type). This account name given in field. It should be unique across existing samaccountnames. Process Number (required field) Bot Number (required field) Display Name Please note that the robot account name will be generated with based on first name + last name First Name (required field) refers to Name for the robot given by the user. Second Name (optional Field) Last Name Auto generated field/attribute. Not shown in the creation/updation form. Always the same: “Robot” Valid from (required field) refers to date since when account is created. Valid to (required field) refers to the date until when the account will be active. Robot account will expire and get disabled after this date if no actions are taken by the account owners to extend the validity period. You can select a date maximum 13 months in future. Country (required field) refers to the region where Robot Account belongs. This is requested merely for technical reasons. Owner (required field) is the primary owner of the account who will receive the initial Robot account password. You can optionally provide more than one owner for the robot account. If you do so, at least one of the owners must be a KONE employee. In this case, the password will be delivered to all the owners. Deputy owner (required field) is the other owner of the account who will also be able to update the account during its life cycle. You can optionally provide more than one deputy owner for the robot account. If you do so, at least one of the deputy owners must be a KONE employee. On filling up the fields of form, user need to click ‘Next’ button on right corner. This will present a read only form with entered details for different fields. To make any changes to field values, user need to click ‘Back’ button on left corner. If entered details are correct, then user need to click ‘Submit’ button on right corner of UI page. Robot Account will be created in Active Directory with password set as never expire. Owner will receive a notification with the initial password. The initial password delivered by MyAccess has to be reset by the owner and then stored in the RPA password vault. Password must be complex (minimum 12 characters including upper and lower case characters, numbers and special characters) and shouldn’t be shared with anyone. P.S. You can find password complexity matrix here. How to update Robot account in MyAccess Login to MyAccess: https://myaccess.kone.com . On the Home page you need to select from top-left corner's drop down (icon with 3 lines) Manage Identity > Edit AD Account. On clicking ‘Edit AD account’, account selection form is presented to user. If user is the deputy owner or owner of the account which is having “Active Directory” account, user is presented with account edit form with fields to be filled up such as – Robot Account Name (Non-editable) Process Number (Non-editable) Bot Number (Non-editable) Display Name Please note that the robot account display name will be read only and generated based on first name + last name First Name (Editable and required field) refers to Name for the robot given by the user. Last Name Auto generated field/attribute. Not shown in the creation/updating form. Always the same: “Robot” Valid from (Non-editable) refers to date since when account is created. Valid to (required field and editable) refers to the date until when the account will be active. Robot account will expire and get disabled after this date if no actions are taken by the account owners to extend the validity period. You can select a date maximum 13 months in future. Country (required field and editable) refers to the region where Robot Account belongs. This is requested merely for technical reasons. Owner (required field and editable) is the primary owner of the account who will receive the initial Robot account password. Deputy owner (required field and editable) is the other owner of the account who will be able to also update the account during its life cycle. On filling up the fields of form, user need to click ‘Next’ button on right corner. This will present a read only form with entered details for different fields. To make any changes to field values, user need to click ‘Back’ button on left corner. If entered details are correct, then user need to click ‘Submit’ button on right corner of UI page. Robot Account changes will be updated in Active Directory. Disable/Enable/Delete Robot Account MyAccess will disable Robot account whenever it has reached its validity end date. Owner and Deputy Owner will receive notification when Robot account has been disabled including the instructions on how to re-enable the account again. Robot account can be re-enabled by updating the validity end date of the account within 30 days from disablement. After that, Robot account will be permanently deleted. Owners will receive automatic notification when Robot account has been deleted by the system. Warning Message before Robot Account is Disabled. MyAccess will send multiple warnings to both Owner and Deputy Owner before account is disabled so that the owners will have enough time to go extend the validity if needed. The warnings are being sent 60, 30, 14, 7 and 1 day(s) before the validity of the account ends. If the account is not extended 30 days before expiration, in addition to the account owner and deputy owners, corresponding CMDB application owners also will receive the notification to ensure the accounts are extended on time. The notification will look as below. Disabled Robot account cannot be re-activated. New Robot account must be created if previous account has not bee activated before disabling. Robot Account Inactive Owner Escalation If any of the Robot Account’s Owner/deputy Owner goes inactive Approval form is sent to owner to update deputy owner. If owner goes inactive/blank: If owner goes inactive/blank then its deputy Owner is set as its Owner and Warning email and Approval is sent to owner to update the deputy Owner. And reminder warning email will be sent until deputy owner is updated . If deputy owner goes inactive/blank: If deputy owner goes inactive/blank then a Warning email and Approval is sent to owner to update the deputy owner., And reminder warning email will be sent until deputy owner is updated. If owner and deputy owner goes inactive/blank: If both owner and deputy owner goes inactive/blank an Escalation email is sent to MyAccess support team update the accounts Manager and deputy Owner from Edit AD account Quicklink. If you experience issue please open a ticket to CUST.KONE.INT.SD.

short_description: DOS - PF - Error while creating the new project
introduction: If the users are facing the below error while creating new project in the Project folder site. Please follow the below steps. or
instructions: The error having the status code 401 is due to the user doesn't have the required permission to the site. Step 1 : We need to check the User access to the Project folder site. The user should be a Member of Project Folder Members AAD group/Security group. If the user was not added in AAD group/Security group please add the user in the respective AAD group/Security group. Note: Please refer the KBA (KB0011658) for the Knowledge on how to raise a request through MyAccess portal to add the user in the respective Members AAD group/Security group. Step 2 : If the user is already present in the AAD group , please check whether the respective AAD group/Security group is added in all the site collections or not . If not added in the site collection, we need to check with the configuration owner to add the missing AAD group/Security group to the respective site collection. Please follow the below steps to check the whether the respective Members AAD group is added in all the site collections or not Open the respective project folder site and click on the gear(settings) icon on the top right. And click on the Site Contents . please refer the below screenshot Navigate to the URL bar in the browser and add _1 after the projectfodlers_[countrycode] to the URL and click on enter and you will be redirected to the site collection. Example : Main site URL: https://kone.sharepoint.com/sites/ projectfolders_deu Site collection URL: https://kone.sharepoint.com/sites/ projectfolders_deu_1 /_layouts/15/viewlsts.aspx?view=14 Once you have redirected to the respective site collection , click on the gear(settings) icon on the top right and click on the Site Permissions and check whether the respective Members AAD group is added or not. Please refer the below screenshot. Repeat the 3 & 4 steps for all the Site Collections and check. The error having the status code 500 is due to some internal server error or might be due to network issue. Step 1 : If the user is already having the required access, we need to try creating a test project in the project folder site and check whether the same issue is occurring or not. Step 2 : If we didn't get any error while creating new test project, request the user to recheck and try creating new project again. Step 3 : If the same error is occurred, We need to enable the trace (inspect) and check why the creation process is failing. Step 4 : We need gather all the information (site name, error screenshot) and share with the development team/Configuration owner along with our analysis for further action.

short_description: Self-Help article to resolve Intelex Mobile App Issues - Kone Safety Solution, KSS - Intelex
introduction: This is a self-help article for end users to resolve below issues in Intelex Mobile Application. (Android & iOS) Login Issue Unable to report Incidents, Near Misses & Inspections Error in selecting Business Unit, Supervisor, Action owner, Equipment, Type of Inspection & Checklist. Finding a location to report Incident, Near Misses & Inspection.
instructions: Mobile Login Issue: Kindly try below steps to fix Intelex mobile app issue. (Android, iOS device) For accessing Intelex without any errors or issues, your Intelex Mobile App needs to be downloaded from your WORK PROFILE and it should be in latest version. Install app from play store/ Apple store (Uninstall and Reinstall the app from work profile). If you install Intelex App from your work profile, Intelex Production URL " https://global.intelex.com/Login3/Kone " is readily available to authenticate to your Intelex Account & also it auto upgrades the app whenever there will be a new version released. Enter your KONE credentials to authenticate. Set 4-digit code to login easily to Intelex without using password from next login time. NOTE: Clear your default browser Cache, Cookies, History (in All Time- Advanced). If above steps are followed and still facing the issue, please respond to the ticket with points. Are you facing same issue in Web Version (Laptop Browser)? Is this the first time you are logging in to Intelex Mobile App? Please confirm if other apps like Outlook, Teams is working fine with same email address which you trying to login Intelex? How did you try to install (Work Profile or Personal Profile) Which mobile device you are using for Intelex login? Kindly share the error screen shot and the screen shot of current version from Intelex Mobile. If possible, please share a recorded video of using Intelex Mobile App. Error while reporting Incidents/Near Misses & Inspections: Your Intelex Mobile App must be downloaded from your WORK PROFILE and it should be in latest version. Install app from your work profile play store/ Apple store (Uninstall and Reinstall the app from work profile).Verify if you are facing the same issue in Web browser also, respond to the support team with the error screen shot. Issue in selecting fields/providing details.: Try providing the details from web browser and see if the issue still exists. If there is no issue in web browser, follow step 2. If the issue exists in web browser also, follow step 3 below. Your Intelex mobile app must be updated with latest version. Update it from your play store/apple store (Uninstall and Reinstall the app from work profle). Respond to the support via the incident you reported to ASKIT with error screen shot. Location not visible in Mobile App: Open the location field and type the location name correctly and wait for few seconds to load the location data. If still the location is not loaded, try reporting the same from web browser and see if the location is visible for you. If the location is visible in web browser, follow step 3. If the location is not visible in web browser also, that clarifies you do not have visibility/ fall under different location structure in Intelex. follow step 4 below for getting access to the location. Your Intelex mobile app must be updated with latest version. Update it from your play store/apple store (Uninstall and Reinstall the app). Respond to the ASKIT incident with the location name that you are not able to see for reporting Incident, Near Miss & Inspection. Also mention the reason on why you want to see or report in that location. If your issue is not listed in this self-help article, kindly respond to the ASKIT ticket with exact details of the issue attaching error screen shot. If you are looking for Location Roles to Analyze, Investigate, Review & Approve Incidents & Near Misses in Intelex, kindly request the access from the Service Now portal KSS Intelex - Authorizations .

short_description: How to handle Verizon reactive ticket
introduction: Reactive ticket means responding to Office network service related issues that has already happened. It is a record of a problem or request raised by the End-users/suppliers that needs to be addressed.
instructions: How to address office network service issues? If any issue comes under keyword of Network, Wifi, LAN, WAN, Wireless, Switch, Internet they all belong to office network issue. Whenever reactive ticket raised from end-user, Please get below information before creating a new Incident ticket in Office network service and Task ticket to Verizon integration queue. General network template:- 1. Issue start Date and time ? (CET) 2. Were there or are there currently any Site power issues ? (has power been verified on the site?) 3. Any change/update to user’s devices (laptop, desktop) ? 4. Any other service/application not working? 5. Number of affected users ? 6. Best time to contact the user :- (with timezone) 7. Mode to contact the user? phone/email/chat - 8. Does user speak English? Y/N ? 9. Local IT availability at the site ? Y/N ? Network slowness issue:- If someone(end-user) raise issue related to network slowness please get the required information’s below. Check whether user are connected via home network or office network Check the users are connected via LAN or WIFI Check the speed test With enabling zscalar, Without enabling zscalar in (WIFI and LAN Network) Network slowness issue :- Ping test results (from end user machine) – ping google DNS server Machine IP Address – ipconfig/all(copy the full information) MAC Address – getmac Trace route – tracert “affected application” Error messages and applications impacted – Speed test- any browser type --> https://www.speedtest.net/ Notes:- When ever you face slowness in Office network and home network kindly Disconnect Zscalar and test again. Type of request from Zscalar Website blocking, url-not accessible, network slowness, Whitelisting these are the common issues comes under Zscalar. When ever a ticket is raised mentioning these Keywords under ZScalar assign the ticket to Kone.ITO.firewall queue. How to Open new Reactive Integration incident ticket with Verizon Step1:- open service now(askit.kone.com/ koneprod.service-now.com)--> Goto Workspace--> Select Service operation Workspace. Step2:- On the List column scroll down to incident and open incident case --> Click new to create new incident. Step3:- Please fill the required information’s(highlighted one) and save the incident ticket. (sample screenshot provided below) Step4:- Once the Incident ticket is created, click create incident task, and fill the same description on the task ticket as well. Step5:- Near the configuration item(CI) Tab you need to mention “*( yoursiteID) ” For Example:- If your site is au009, then type “ *au009 ” in the Configuration item tab and you will see all the CI details for this site. Now you can choose either IP switch or IP router (for this site). Select the appropriate option accordingly. Step6:- Once the information is updated correctly, mark the queue name as KONE.Verizon.Integration ( mandatory ) and also fill the work notes (any additional information from user) and save the task ticket. if you have any screenshot and document for this issue from user, please attach it in the attachment tab. Once you save this ticket, Verizon ticket will be automatically created for this case Step7:- Verizon will start working on this case and status will be changed as work in progress . Once Verizon update any comment on the Task ticket, then the status will changes to pending . Pending status with sub-status details 1.Pending – Maintenance time (Verizon planned for maintenance window for testing) No action from AskIT 2.Pending – Monitoring (Verizon keep this ticket for monitoring) No action from AskIT 3.Pending – Update customer (Action from AskIT as well as Verizon) Note:- When the ticket is pending from Update customer, AskIT need to copy the Verizon information in Task ticket and share it with RSM & LCON as per KBA KB000A8884. 4.Pending – Customer time (pending for action from Kone) Note:- When the ticket is pending from customer time, AskIT need to copy the Verizon information in Task ticket and share it with RSM & LCON as per KBA KB000A8884. You can see the Verizon updates on Activity tab, and respond back to Verizon with updated comments in Activity tab and save the ticket.

short_description: Verizon Proactive cases troubleshooting
introduction: What is Proactive ticket? Verizon is proactively monitoring all network components in KONE Office Network, and the Network Event Mgt. monitoring detects and automatically reports incidents - Verizon refers to this as ‘Proactive’. These incidents are sent as Proactive tickets also to KONE Service Now.
instructions: Step 1: When Verizon find any issues in Kone devices and requires information from Kone for further troubleshooting, Verizon will create a Proactive ticket and assign the ticket to “ASK IT India Queue”.. Please make a note that proactive ticket always starts with subject “ Proactive ”, but sometime, they will send ticket to Kone to get the site access hours, site contact etc with normal description”. You can also verify the tickets “ opened for” parameter filled by “Verizon Integration ” Ticket View’s in SNOW: Step 2: ASK IT agent will open proactive SNOW ticket (whenever the ticket is in Onhold & inprogress state)and go to the “Activity” section and verify the details updated by Verizon NOC in Activity section. Step 3: ASKIT agent view the Verizon updates under “Activity” and see if Verizon ask ASKIT team to collect any information from Kone end. See below example tickets and see the latest ask from Verizon….. Note:- Whenever Verizon need any update from Kone, Please verify the SITE ID, Device name are properly mentioned in the Description tab. If not, reply back to Verizon team to provide the proper information to investigate further. Step 4: Please copy the information from “Activity“section and send email to relevant stakeholders. If Site Tier type is Tier1 to Tier 3, First, second and third communication sends to Local POC, SDM , RSM. For Tier 4 to Tier 6 sites , first communication sends to Local POC, second and third communication to SDM and RSM. Make sure the subject will be standard for all the Proactive tickets. Subject: “2024031800292” Issue short description_Site ID(eg:-SiteID-EG002)_Snow ticket INC00025618 Message template from ASK IT to LCON: Hi Sir/Madam, As per our master site data, you are the local point of contact for the site “XXXXX”. We have received an incident ticket #xxxxxx from the Verizon network vendor, and below are the details of the incident. Kindly request that you check and respond back with details to be shared with Verizon for further troubleshooting. Message template from ASK IT to SDM : Hi SDM, We have received a network proactive alarm for the site “XXXXX” from the Verizon vendor and contacted LCON for the site, but we didn’t get a response. short_description: How to use Ricoh Secure printing
introduction: Secure printing: Protecting Your Documents and Data
instructions: Secure Printing is part of KONE’s global LRS VPSX printing solution and is currently available in Finland, Italy and in the Netherlands. The feature is available in selected printers which are marked as Secure Printing devices. Secure Printing also known as Follow Me printing is a feature that enables you to print confidential documents securely. When you send a request to the printer, the job will not be printed until you release it at the printer. This ensures that the documents are not picked up by someone else. To start using Secure Printing you need to register in the service and have KONE ID card or access key for authentication. Please follow the below steps: 1. Swipe your KONE ID card or access key at the printer to authenticate and enroll the card. 2. Register by typing your KONE email address and password into the printer's control panel. Then log off and log in again to complete the enrollment process To print and collect your job, please follow the below steps: To print select Secure Print Ricoh from your PC’s print queue selection. For more information on how to find the queue, please see AskIT self-help article KBA8184 . Collect your job from any Secure Printing device by swiping your ID card at the printer and then choose Secure Print option in the printer’s control panel. After you have completed printing then log off . In case you forget to log off, the printer will automatically sign you out in 60 seconds. Please note that if you have not registered in Secure Printing service you can still print your job as before. However, if you want to copy or scan with Secure Printing device, you need to choose Guest option in the printer’s control panel to proceed.

short_description: Ask IT - How to approve SAP Access Request as a Line Manager using SAP GRC?
introduction: How to approve or reject GRC request as line manager?
instructions: SAP Access Request Process for ECC Once an access request is submitted by the end user/key user, an email is triggered automatically to the line manager of the request for the 1st level approval. Approve/Reject SAP Access Request as a Line Manager directly from email: In the email you can see the user who is requesting access to SAP, the reason and the roles / user group that are included in the request. As a line manager your duty is to validate that the user needs SAP in his/her work and that correct accesses are requested; correct user group / correct Clone ID. To Approve / Reject the SAP Access Request, line manager needs to click on respective link. A new window will appear and there is a need to just click Send . Alternatively, you can directly reply to the email sent by GRC system by writing either Approve or Reject. Note : While approving / rejecting the access request, please do not change the subject line of the e-mail. If the subject line of e-mail is changed, system will not understand decision of manager whether request is approved / rejected. Do not use Chinese characters! After actions taken, workflow is routed to the respective role owners/user group approvers for next steps.

short_description: How to set delegation in SAP GRC system?

instructions: During the vacation/planned leave Line managers, Role owners and SoD approvers can set delegation in the GRC system so that requests can be processed in timely manner. NOTE! Existing approval requests are not routed to the delegate, only the ones created after delegation is successfully created in SAP GRC. Original approver will still get the email notifications during the leave but those can be left unnoted. HOW TO SET THE DELEGATION: Log on to the GRC via following link: https://knecigrp9800.konenet.com:44398/sap/bc/webdynpro/sap/grac_uibb_end_user_login?sap-client=100# Enter your Network User ID and Password. Following screen appears where you choose “Approver Delegation”: Use your Network ID together with password for GRC (GRP) to login: If you don’t have access to GRP system, please send email to KONE_SAP_Security_GRC@KONE.COM and request them to set up the delegation accordingly. Click on “Delegate” button Define the user who will act as your delegate during the leave. You can search the correct user based on different criteria. Define the correct validity time for the delegation and set the status as “Active”. Delegation can be set for SAP Access Request process (AC) and/or SAP Process Control testing / review. Click on “Save” and the delegation is saved successfully. If you are facing issues, please contact SAP Security team for support: KONE_SAP_Security_GRC@KONE.COM

short_description: GRC - Access Requests - Report
introduction: 1. How to pull a report on access requests submitted in GRC?
instructions: 1. How to pull a report on access requests submitted in GRC? Introduction: This KB article explains step by step procedure to pull a report on access requests submitted / created by requestor(s) in a given time period along with status of requests in SAP GRC. Step 1> Log in to SAP GRC production system and then execute transaction code "NWBC" Step 2> Upon executing NWBC t-code, system will open a NWBC (Netweaver Business Client) portal screen in browser. Then navigate to Work Center "Access Management" --> "Search Requests" under Access Request Administration Work Set. Step 3> Search request screen opens in a new browser session and select the filter criteria as below and then click on "Search" button. Select "Creation Date" filter from drop down, "Is between" in second column and then enter the "From Date" and "To Date" as per required period. Select "Created by User ID" as second filter, "IS" in second column and then enter the valid user ID (i.e. user ID of requestor who submitted access requests in GRC) in 3 rd. column of filter. If the report to be pulled for all requests submitted by different requestors, then add as many "Created by User ID" filters as required based on number of requestors by clicking on + (Plus) button at the end of the filter. Then enter different requestor's user ID in each filter. Step 4> The results of search criteria will be displayed in bottom pane of the browser screen with all "access requests" with different statuses (i.e. Approved, Rejected, Cancelled, Finished, Running, Aborted etc. as applicable) which are created / submitted during the time period selected in filter criteria. Step 5> The result of the report can be exported to a excel sheet following below two steps. Click on "Select All" option at the top left corner of "Results" (bottom) pane. Then click on Export --> Export to Microsoft Excel option. Step 6> Then Save / Download the report by clicking on "Save" or "Open" appears at bottom of browser after clicking on "Export to Microsoft Excel" option. Downloaded Excel Report Screen appears as below: Step 7> If this report has to be run periodically / frequently, then the filters selection criteria can be saved as variant. Enter a variant name (Ex. "Access Requests") then click on Save. When report has to be run from second time, by selecting the variant from "Saved Variants" option, all filter selection criteria will be loaded which is saved in variant. Then review and change the inputs for filters as required each time when report has to be run.

Step 11: If the value is wrong inform them to resend the IDOC again with the correct value by getting confirmation from the user who created the IDOC. It will insert as new record in the database. short_description: FSM- Resource absence creation process.
introduction: Resource absences indicate when a service resource is unavailable to work. In FSM you can create two type of resource absences; individual absences and crew absences . “Crew” type service resource absences are considered in scheduling and shown on the Gantt chart and they apply for all individual resources allocated to that specific service crews. Absences associated with individual crew members’ corresponding service resources aren’t considered. To create resource absences for any user, FSM country key user approval is mandatory. 1. HOW TO CREATE/ INSERT MULTIPLE UNPLANNED RESOURCE ABSENCES FOR MULTIPLE SERVICE RESOURCES USING SALESFORCE INSPECTOR: 1. create file with following details: service resource ID Start date End date 2. open data import on salesforce inspector and select insert action on ResourceAbsence object. There are two formats of file 1. excel 2. CSV. Choose excel format. 3. Copy the data and paste in the data section. 4. Do field mapping. Service resource Id to ResourceId Start time to Start End time to End 5. After checking all settings click on import. 2. HOW TO CREATE NEW RESOURCE ABSENCE FOR INDIVIDUALS: 1. Select “New Resource Absence” behind the Service Resource. 2. Select the Absence record type “Non Availability”. 3. Set the following values for the Absence record: a. Type: Absence b. Type of Absence: Planned c. Availability: d. Absent except for Emergency: Used for meetings, trainings e. Absent for all type of work: Used for vacations, holidays, etc. f. Approved: ”Yes” value selected, because otherwise the absence needs to separately still be approved 3. CLONING RESOURCE ABSENCES TO MULTIPLE TECHNICIAN : Create the first resource absence for a technician (see above instructions) Go to the created Resource absence, select Clone c. Change the name of technician (= Resource) and save the record for the technician 4. LONG TERM RESOURCE ABCENSES: In case of long-term absences of technicians, a planned Resource Absences needs to be created so that work is not scheduled when technician is absent. Long-term resource Absence should be created by the supervisor. Note! In case technician receives compensation for these absences, then a separate indirect timesheet entry for the absence needs to be created either by the technician or by the supervisor. Check with local HR how long-term absences are compensated! If technician is absent for a long time (several weeks or months), it is necessary to reassign the responsibilities of the absent technician. Supervisor can reallocate some work by adjusting Work Center Assignments (see other training material how to do that). Supervisor may also be able to have a temporary technician from another supervisor's team. If this happens, the following activities need to be done: a. Finish temporary technician's Service Territory membership (Primary) in the Service Territory where he/she usually works by setting there an end date 2.Add Service Territory membership (Primary) to the temporary technician in the Service Territory where he/she is planned to work in 3.Adjust Work Center Assignments found under the service resource for both the absent resource and the replacing resource. For the absent resource; edit the work center assignment and edit the date for the beginning of the absence. For the replacing resource, create a new work center assignment to the new work center assignment with the same starting date as the absent resource work center assignment end date. 5. HOW TO CREATE RESOURCE ABSENCE FOR MULTIPLE TECHNICIANS: Supervisor can also create resource absence for multiple technicians. Dispatcher is able to assist with this task if agreed with supervisor. In order to create a resource absence for multiple technicians, open a service resource and go to “New Multi-Resource Absence” from the downwards arrow icon on the upper right side of the screen. Follow the different windows and fill in the needed information, then save/finish the information.

short_description: How do I resolve KONE Apple Mac hardware issues?
introduction: This article offers a detailed walkthrough on logging a request with Apple Support for hardware issues.
instructions: Follow the below steps to log a case with Apple support for resolving hardware issues on KONE Mac devices Identifying Hardware Issues: Users are encouraged to promptly report any hardware issues to AskIT, Remote Support Team, and OSS team. Support teams must validate reported issues to determine if they are hardware-related. Checking Warranty Information: Verify the warranty information’s through the ITSM tool CMDB_Mac_Devices Devices within warranty (4 years for new devices, 3 years for devices purchased before September 2023) qualify for the warranty claim process. Logging a Case with Apple Enterprise Support Call the local Apple toll-free number and provide the organization code. Clearly communicate hardware issue details and device information to Apple support. Apple support will analyze the reported hardware issue and provide further instructions. The person logging the case should coordinate with the end user and Apple support until the issue is resolved or hardware is replaced. Out-of-Warranty and Repair Process -OSS/Remote Support If the device is not covered by warranty and is repairable, notify both the user and the IT SDM. Verify if it is acceptable to proceed with the repair cost before initiating the repair process Request a repair cost estimate from the service provider. The user must get approval for repair costs from the Line Manager or IT SDM. If approved, proceed with the repair process and hand over the repaired device to the user. Note: Maintain transparent communication with end users, Apple support, and relevant parties. Document all interactions and resolutions for future reference. Adhere to any additional organization-specific protocols or requirements during the hardware warranty claim process.

short_description: How to update Company AD Password on MacOS
introduction: This article presents a detailed, step-by-step guide on how to update company AD password on MacOS.
instructions: Follow the below steps to update your Company AD password on your macOS device: Access Password Reset Page: Go to https://password.kone.com from your web browser. Follow the instructions provided in the password reset Knowledge Base (KB) article - KB000A8113 Once the password reset is successfully completed, proceed further to connect to the VPN. Maintain the active VPN connection for a certain duration (refrain from disconnecting until the password is updated). During this VPN connection, the device can synchronize with the Active Directory using the new password After some time, use Lock Screen options (Control + Command + Q) to lock the device. Try Unlock it using the newly updated password. After the acceptance of the new password, proceed to restart your device. Update Keychain Password: After restarting, you might be prompted to update the Keychain password. Click on "Update Keychain password" and enter the new password to unlock the Mac device. Allow time for the Keychain to sync with the new password. Remember, it's essential to follow these steps in sequence for a successful update of your Company AD password on macOS.
