short_description: SAP Password Reset - Single Sign-On (SSO) Users
introduction: Here are instructions for accessing SAP systems using Single Sign-On (SSO) and resetting SAP system passwords: Accessing SAP systems with Single Sign-On (SSO) enabled. Resetting SAP system password with the assistance of the AskIT team. Instructions to Accessing SAP systems with Single Sign-On (SSO) enabled. Step 1 : Open SAP GUI log on pad in your laptop / desktop. Step 2 : Navigate / Scroll to respective SAP system with SSO enabled in above SAP GUI log on pad and then double click on it. Example: If you want to access Z04 production system with SSO then navigate to "Z04 Production (SSO)" and then double click on the entry as shown in below screenshot. Note 1 : To log in / access to SAP system with SSO enabled, you do not require to enter user ID and password. Note 2: In case you are prompted with below screen to reset / change your SAP password even if you are accessing / opening SAP system with SSO then you can delete your existing password by simply clicking on delete (trash can) button at bottom-right corner as shown in below screenshot. Note 3: In case, you are facing any issues while accessing SAP systems with Single Sign On (SSO) enabled then please reach out to AskIT (mail ID: AskIT@kone.com ) with details of issue. Instructions to delete password for SAP systems with Single Sign-On (SSO) enabled. Since SAP Systems are now SSO enabled, no login password is required to login to SAP systems. However, the password policies in SAP systems are still active and valid. If you have an active SAP Password, then SAP system will force you to change it once it reaches its end of lifecycle. In such case, you will be prompted with below screen to reset your SAP password even if you are using SAP SSO at the time of login. It is obvious that you will not remember the existing SAP password since you are using SSO, therefore you have option to delete your existing password by simply clicking delete (trashcan) button in the above screenshot. Once you click on delete button, you will no longer have password in SAP and hence this message will never be prompted again for you in future. Please note, you will have to use SSO enabled system entries in SAP GUI logon pad to access SAP system without having to enter User ID & password. If you are facing any challenges or issues related to SAP SSO then kindly reach to AskIT (mail ID: AskIT@kone.com ).

short_description: ePil - Enable the SQL job monitoring in EPIL
instructions:
Step1: Access your SQL Server Management Studio (SSMS) and connect to the Database Engine. Expand Management, and right-click Database Mail. In this menu, select Configure Database Mail. Step 2: When this appears on your screen, click Next. Step 3: Select ‘Set up Database Mail by performing the following tasks’ and click Next. Step 4: Fill in your Profile Name then click Add to input your SMTP account. Step 5: Fill in the fields with your details, then click OK. Step 6: Expand ‘SQL Server Agent’. Step 6: Right click ‘Operators’. Click ‘New Operator’. Step 7: Provide all the details and click ‘OK’. Step 8: Right click the job in which the monitored to be enabled. Click ‘Properties’. Go to ‘Steps’. Click ‘New’. Step 9: Provide the Step Name and Command. -- ========================================= -- Deleting temporary tables -- ========================================= SET NOCOUNT ON IF OBJECT_ID('Tempdb..#EPIL_Job') IS NOT NULL DROP TABLE #EPIL_Job; -- ======================================================================================== -- by default it will take the current server name, we can set the server name as well -- ======================================================================================== --DECLARE @sql varchar(4000) DECLARE @svrName varchar(40) SET @svrName = 'EPIL_QA_EUDVMMSSQL103' -- =========================== -- creating a temporary table -- =========================== CREATE TABLE #EPIL_Job ( Job_Name varchar (256), Time_Run varchar(30), Job_Status varchar(8), Job_Outcome varchar(9) ) -- =========================================================================== -- inserting SQL Agent Job status in to temporary table -- =========================================================================== INSERT INTO #EPIL_Job (Job_Name,Time_Run,Job_Status,Job_Outcome) SELECT Name AS Job_Name ,CONVERT(VARCHAR,DATEADD(S,(run_time/10000)*60*60 /* hours */ +((run_time -(run_time/10000)* 10000)/100)* 60 /* mins */ +(run_time -(run_time/100)* 100)/* secs */ ,CONVERT(DATETIME,RTRIM(run_date),113)),100)AS Time_Run ,CASE WHEN enabled=1 THEN 'Enabled' ELSE 'Disabled' END Job_Status ,CASE WHEN SJH.run_status=0 THEN 'Failed' WHEN SJH.run_status=1 THEN 'Succeeded' WHEN SJH.run_status=2 THEN 'Retry' WHEN SJH.run_status=3 THEN 'Cancelled' ELSE 'Unknown' END Job_Outcome FROM MSDB.dbo.sysjobhistory SJH JOIN MSDB.dbo.sysjobs SJ ON SJH.job_id=sj.job_id WHERE step_id=1 AND DATEADD(S,(run_time/10000)*60*60 /* hours */ +((run_time -(run_time/10000)* 10000)/100)* 60 /* mins */ +(run_time -(run_time/100)* 100)/* secs */, CONVERT(DATETIME,RTRIM(run_date),113))>=DATEADD(d,-1,GetDate()) AND Name LIKE '%ePil_ImportFromPackageSearchStaging%' ORDER BY Name,run_date,run_time -- ================================= -- HTML -- ================================= -- define CSS inside the HTML head section DECLARE @body varchar(max)= ' '; DECLARE @xml VARCHAR(MAX); --get core xml string SET @xml = CAST(( SELECT Job_Name AS 'td','',MAX(Time_Run) AS 'td','',Job_Status AS 'td','' ,CASE when Job_Outcome ='Failed' THEN 'zr'+ Job_Outcome when Job_Outcome ='Succeeded' THEN 'zg'+ Job_Outcome ELSE Job_Outcome END AS 'td' FROM #EPIL_Job GROUP BY Job_Name,Job_Status,Job_Outcome ORDER BY Job_Name FOR XML PATH('tr'), ELEMENTS ) AS NVARCHAR(MAX)); DECLARE @tableHTML NVARCHAR(MAX) ; SET @xml=replace(@xml, 'zg', ''); -- highlight in Green SET @xml=replace(@xml, 'zr', ''); -- highlight in Red SET @body +='

SQL Agent Job Status on: ' + @svrName + '

' + @xml+'
Job NameTime RunJob StatusJob Outcome
'; IF (SELECT count(*) FROM #EPIL_Job) > 0 BEGIN DECLARE @today VARCHAR(20) = convert(VARCHAR, getdate(), 110) DECLARE @subject NVARCHAR(max) DECLARE @sqlstr VARCHAR (400) DECLARE @recipients varchar(256) SET @sqlstr ='SQL Agent Job Status on: '+ @svrName +' - ' + @today SET @recipients= 'SupplyChain-ApplicationSupport@KONE.com;elena.cozzi@kone.com;saku.paananen@kone.com;' -- change to your own -- ========================================== -- Email server configuration -- ========================================== EXEC msdb.dbo.sp_send_dbmail @profile_name = 'EPIL Appln Support', -- Change Profile Name @from_address = 'donotreply@kone.com', @recipients = @recipients, -- Change recipients name @subject = @sqlstr, @body = @body, @body_format = 'HTML' ; END -- =================================== -- Removing temp tables -- =================================== DROP TABLE #EPIL_Job Step10: Provide the profile name and all the other details correctly in the command and click ‘Ok’.

short_description: 1PLAN - How to update the 1PLAN Access template
introduction: Updating 1PLAN Access template Whenever there is an update in Attribute hierarchy in 1PLAN application, the 1PLAN access template file should be also updated.
instructions: Step 1: 1PLAN Access template file is present in 10151 Share point, download the excel file. Step 2: Upload the file in the backup folder in 10151 share point, create a folder on today’s date and upload the file inside it. Step 3: Open the template in excel as shown below. Do not Move/Delete this file. Unhide the required hidden sheets (Master data, Hierarchy raw, Hierarchy1). Right click on any sheet and click unhide. You can CTRL click on all the three sheets together. Step 4: Open 1PLAN application, in the Navigation panel , click on Area , in selectable values, right click and click on hide empty categories , and sort the selectable values based on External ID. Click on any of the displayed External ID value and press CTRL+A & CTRL+C. Step 5: In the excel file, open the Master data sheet, select only the Area IDs (ignore Global/All) , delete the existing Area IDs, paste the copied Area IDs from the application. Go to the application, now click on any of the display values, and press CTRL + A & CTRL + C. Step 6: In the Master data sheet in the excel file, there is another Area column which has the description of the areas. Select only the area descriptions (ignore All) , delete them, and paste the descriptions that were copied from the application. Repeat the same steps for Frontline, Orglevel4, Orglevel5, Orglevel6, Orglevel7. Step 7: Go to 1PLAN application, export the Attributes list. On the top left corner you can find the export icon, click on it. Specify the path where the exported file should be stored, Check the Attributes check box, and click on ok . Step 8: Open the Attributes excel file, go to the AttributeValueOfAttributeValue sheet, change the heading of B and D column as Parent and ID , delete the entire A and C columns, the sheet should contain only Parent and ID column. Right click on the sheet name, click Move or Copy , copy this sheet to a new book , save the book as Attributes.csv in the same folder. In the Attributes.csv file, if both Parent and ID columns have the same value, delete them, save & close the file. Step 9: Download the python file from the share point to generate the hierarchy. All the required files should be in same folder, open the python file, change the path, path should point to the Attributes.csv file. .csv extension is not required in the file path. Step 10: After changing the file path, save the file and run it. After the successful run, a new file with _ python on its name will be generated in the same folder. Step 11: Open the Attributes_Python file, Select all the cells, and copy them. Go to the 1PLAN Access template file, open the Hierarchy Raw sheet, select all cells , and paste . Step 11: The Hierarchy1 sheet will be automatically updated when the above steps were done, open that sheet and verify if that has all the entities that 1PLAN has. You can also verify the hierarchy in the Template sheet, make sure you revert the changes that you did in this template sheet before you close the file.

short_description: How to Troubleshoot OneDrive Sync issues
introduction: Clear office cache: Navigate to C:\users\username\AppData\Local\Microsoft\Office\16.0\OfficeFileCache Delete all files/folders inside Reset OneDrive Press the Windows key + R to open "RUN" box where you can type the commands below: Enter the command: %localappdata%\Microsoft\OneDrive\onedrive.exe \reset Confirm > The OneDrive system tray icon should disappear and re-appear after two minutes. If the OneDrive icon doesn’t re-appear, run the following command: %localappdata%\Microsoft\OneDrive\onedrive.exe Go to Control Panel > User Accounts > Credential Manager > Click on "Windows Credentials" > Select the OneDrive credential > Remove Also, on the Control Panel > User Accounts > Credential Manager > Click on "Web Credentials" > Select the OneDrive credential > Remove Registry Editor>HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE>Software>Microsoft>Office>15/16>Export>Delete Unlink The OneDrive account Right-click the OneDrive icon in the taskbar>Go to Settings> Head over to the Account tab > Now, go to Unlink this PC to disconnect your OneDrive account from your Windows PC Uninstall OneDrive and install latest version - Try to completely uninstall OneDrive: Windows Key+X > Windows PowerShell (Admin). Copy the below line, paste into Powershell and Enter taskkill /f /im OneDrive.exe This will terminate any OneDrive process Copy the below, paste into Powershell and Enter if you’re using 32-bit Windows 10 %SystemRoot%\System32\OneDriveSetup.exe /uninstall if you’re using 64-bit Windows 10 %SystemRoot%\SysWOW64\OneDriveSetup.exe /uninstall (Note - It is recommended that you create a backup of your registry before modifying registry as suggested below so that if you commit some mistake, you will be able to restore - https://support.microsoft.com/en-us/help/322756 ) Now Windows Key+R > Type Regedit and Enter Go to this path HKEY_CURRENT_USER\Software\Microsoft\OneDrive You can remove all folders that include OneDrive numeric version (example - 18.172.0826.0010 folder) and from the Accounts folder, remove all the folders inside After that restart computer and install One Drive latest version from this link

short_description: Guidelines for manually canceling or completing work orders in the SAP system.
introduction: To manually cancel or complete work orders in the SAP system, follow these guidelines: 1. Cancellation of Orders: Rules for allowing Cancellation of Work Order in SAP: Work Order is Planned Order Type, i.e. MBM (YSM1, YSM2) or Repair (YSM6) In FSM the Service Appointment. Status should be : New, Scheduled, Cancelled, Rejected, Blocked Status should not be : On Route, Arrived, Start Work, Stop Work, Backreport, Signature, Completed No other SA is open for that Work Order, basically the same as in 2a and 2b. In FSM the Site Visit Work Order : Status should be : Rejected Status should not be : Finished, Accepted, Arrived, On Route, Backreport, Signature, Leave Site 2. Completion of Orders: Before marking an order as complete in the SAP system, ensure that both the work order and its related service appointments and site visits are completed in the FSM system.

short_description: Steps to reprocess KSA invoices Customer Invoicing Solutions (Exstream).
introduction: How to reprocess KSA invoices Customer Invoicing Solutions (Exstream). If we encounter any incident from KSA (Kingdom of Saudi Arabia) wherein the invoices aren't available in SAP/ECM. First check in tracking tool, Kibana, and in S3. Also check with SAP if the idoc xml is sent and accepted by Pagero. After all these checks, if you find the zip file of the invoice in the "input_pagero" folder in S3, meaning we have received inputs from Pagero and further generation and delivery of the invoice did not happen, we will have to reprocess these failed invoices. Below are the steps to reprocess such invoices. Note: Steps are same for all environments.
instructions: Steps: Locate and download the required zip file/s from the "input_pagero" folder. Check if we have tracking of these invoices in Tracking tool. If tracking present we need to add the required invoice numbers in the duplicateBypass.tbl file in S3. The detailed steps for this are mentioned in the KB00011732 . If there is not data related to these invoices we can reprocess directly no need to add the invoice number. Login to prod from powershell. Get pods and note the " of-operator flow "pod. ( Note: The pod name changes frequently make sure you get the name of the latest pod.) Cd into required folder. ( Note: path should not have space and number make changes accordingly The command should look like this kubectl cp <zip_file_name.zip> <pod name>:/app/from/operator/zatca/in for example kubectl cp Archive.0062033168_0000002750141576.zip.62033168.zip of-operatorflow-5df87fcf5c-rjfqz:/app/from/operator/zatca/in Now first check in Tracking tool for the success then check in s3 CyberDuck it takes up to 10 min to update the status of ECM wait and then check confirm.

short_description: Instructions to reset SAP user account password without Single Sign On (SSO)
introduction: Instructions to reset SAP user account password without Single Sign On (SSO) In case, you still need to access the respective SAP system without Single Sign On (SSO) / i.e., with user ID and password and you want to reset password then you can follow below options to reset your password for respective SAP system. Option 1> Reset SAP system password using Password Self-Service (PSS) functionality in SAP GRC end user portal link. Notes: 1> SAP GRC Password Self Service (PSS) functionality is enabled for below SAP systems connected to GRC: SAP ERP systems : SAP ERP Development (Z01) SAP ERP Acceptance (Z02) SAP ERP Pre-Production(Z03) SAP ERP Production (Z04) SAP ERP Sandbox (Z00) SAP BW Systems : BI HANA Development (BW1) BI HANA Pre-Production (BW3) BI HANA Production (BW4) SAP BH Systems : Beacon Development (BH1) Beacon Test System (BH3) Beacon Production (BH4) SAP SOLMAN Systems : SOLMAN Development (SA1) SOLMAN Production (SA4) SAP GRC Systems : GRC Development (GRD) GRC Acceptance (GRQ) GRC Production (GRP) SAP HR systems : HR Development (H01) HR Acceptance (H03) HR Production (H04) Notes: 2> If you have already changed the password once today, whether manually or through GRC PSS (and forgotten it), SAP GRC system will not be able to reset the password for you today. You will be able to reset the password via PSS tomorrow only. STEP-BY-STEP INSTRUCTIONS Step1 : Clink on e nd user portal link : SAP Password Reset to reset your SAP system password. Once clicked on above link, system will open below log on screen. Step2 : Enter the User ID (the one you use for logging on to Windows / KONE NET ) in the ‘Userid’ field and your Windows password in the ‘Password’ field and click on the ‘Log On’ button / press Enter. S tep3. Click on ‘Password Self-Service’ link in the next window as shown below. Step4. After clicking on Password Self Service, it will redirect you to the new page/tab, here you need to select the system(s) in which the password needs to be reset. Step5 . You can select single / multiple system(s) at the same time by clicking on the checkbox / tick box for which password reset is required as shown in below screenshot. Step6. Once the desired system(s) have been selected, click on 'Reset Password' button at bottom-left side shown in above screen. Then In the "Status" column you will get an update 'Success' or 'Failure' As shown in the above screen, when the message is 'Success' in the 'Status' column, just below the 'Reset password' button you will get a message stating "Email has been sent to your mail box containing the password details." at the bottom of the above screen as highlighted. Step7. You will receive an email as shown below (normally within 2 to 10 minutes of the above 'Success' status) to the email id linked to you SAP user account in the respective SAP system(s) which will contain the initial / generated password as shown in below screenshot. Step8. You will need to go to the logon screen of the relevant SAP System. In the above sample case, we have reset the password for BH3 (Beacon Test System) and BW1 (BI HANA Development). Copy the password. Enter your user account name in the ‘User’ field and your newly generated initial password in the ‘Password’ field. (Hint: you can copy the password from the notification e-mail using " Ctrl + C" & paste it to Password field in below screen using " Ctrl + V" to avoid spelling mistakes and irrelevant spaces): Step9. Click on or press Enter it will give a pop-up to change the password with 2 text boxes, one is New password and the other one is Repeat password. Once you fill in the New Password and Repeat Password in above pop-up window and clicked on Enter button, you will be logged into the system, and you have successfully changed the password using GRC Password Self Service. Option 2: Reset SAP system password by sending an email to sapgrp@grp.kone.com following corresponding instructions explained below. STEP-BY-STEP INSTRUCTIONS Step1. open a new e-mail window in outlook, enter mail ID sapgrp@grp.kone.com in "To" field, mention SAP Password Reset in "Subject" field and in the E-mail body content mention respective SAP system ID. ( Example: System ID of SAP ERP /ECC Production is Z04, System ID of Beacon Production is BH4, System ID of BI HANA / CPM Production is BW4 etc.). NOTE: In the Email body please do not put any other text apart from the respective system ID (in this demo case, it's Z04). Once all the details are entered, please click on “send” button. Step3. You will receive an email as shown below (normally within 2 to 10 minutes after sending email as shown in the above screen shot) to the email id linked to you SAP user account in the respective SAP system which will contain the initial reset password as shown in below screen shot. Step4. You will need to open the logon screen of the respective SAP System. In the above sample case, we have reset the password for Z04 ECC (SAP ERP) Production system. Enter your user ID in the ‘User’ field and your newly generated initial password in the ‘Password’ field. (Hint: you can copy and paste the password from the notification e-mail using Ctrl + C & Ctrl + V to avoid spelling mistakes and irrelevant spaces): Step5. Click on or press Enter it will give a pop-up to change the password with 2 text boxes, one is New password and the other one is Repeat password. Once you fill in the New Password and Repeat Password in above pop-up window and clicked on Enter button, you will be logged into the system, and you have successfully changed the password using GRC Password Self Service. short_description: KONE VR - GeForce Experience - How to update graphics card drivers
introduction: The graphics card (GPU) is the single-most important component inside the VR computer when it comes to running VR applications. KONE VR computers are running NVIDIA GeForce graphics cards which get frequent software updates in the form of display drivers. These drivers are distributed through GeForce's webpage as well as a dedicated application called GeForce Experience. This article explains how to update the drivers using GeForce Experience.
instructions: To check for, and update graphics card drivers Launch GeForce Experience, note that it may perform an automatic update to itself if a newer version is available. Enter your T2 admin credentials if prompted. Sign in using the account of your choice if prompted If the sign-in fails, please refer to support article KB0011185 Go to the Drivers tab. If an update is available, press "Download" Once the download has completed, select "Express Installation" to start the driver installation. You will be prompted to enter your T2 admin credentials if you have not signed into Windows using them Allow GeForce Experience to install the drivers Note that the monitor of the VR computer may flicker / go black during the driver installation Once the installation is complete, you will see a confirmation After closing the confirmation window, GeForce Experience may still show you the installation buttons Restart the computer If you want to verify the latest drivers were successfully installed, open the Drivers tab of GeForce Experience again after the restart. The page should confirm that you have the latest drivers.

short_description: KONE VR - How to install Windows updates (Windows 10 / Windows 11)
introduction: KONE IT manages Windows updates on all computers. To make sure critical updates install on the VR computer, make sure the computer is periodically switched on while being connected to the KONE network. Leave the computer on for several hours if possible to allow it download and install all pending Windows updates.
instructions: If you are experiencing unexplained issues with the VR computer / running VR modules, you may want to check that Windows is up-to-date. To check for and install Windows updates manually, Open the Start menu Type "Check for updates" and click on the search match to perform update check (OR navigate to the Windows Update menu through "Settings > Windows Update" on Win11 or "Settings > Update & Security > Windows Update on Win10) The Windows Update menu opens and shows any available updates If available, install updates by following the on-screen instructions. Note that you may need to restart the computer once updates have been installed.

short_description: Space permissions in Confluence Cloud - Add/View/Edit/Admin access to Confluence Cloud Spaces

If you are a space admin follow below The admins can be found by navigating to Space settings -> Users and check which user is having Admin rights. Then write the approval mail to the admin to get approval to provide view/edit/admin access. If there are 2 admins then write the approval mail to both of them to get approval to provide view/edit/admin access.. If there are more than 2 admins then write the approval mail to few admins to get approval to provide view/edit/admin access.. SPACE PERMISSSION By default every space in Confluence Cloud will have confluencecloud_user group added when the space is created, which will allow every user to access this space with basic access defined. You can check by navigating to Space settings -> Space access -> Groups and check if the group is added. Case 1: If this group confluencecloud_user is not added then it means that this Space is restricted from Viewing itself . In this case we have to get approval from the Space admins and give the required access(view, edit or Admin access). Case 2: If this group confluencecloud_user is already added and the user is having the basic access but the user need elevated access such as Edit access or Admin access , for this case also we have to get approval from the Space admins Kindly follow the below steps to restrict the user's permissions(Read or Edit Access or Admin) to the respective space. Step 1: Go to the respective Space(here for eg: Software Engineering(IT)), navigate to Space settings - > Space access -> Users -> Edit NOTE: Space admins can also add the Confluence Cloud users to the space and Please ensure the Pre-req are met. Step 2 : At the bottom, you will have option as Search for internal users . Search the user name, select the respective user and Click on Add . Then define the permissions as below for View , Edit and Admin access(as per user's request) and click Save . NOTE : Archive check box should not be selected. It is not for end users. View Access (Only check box corresponding to View should be selected) Edit Access (The checkboxes corresponding to View , Add , Delete and Export options should be selected) Admin Access (The checkboxes corresponding to View , Add , Delete , Export and Admin options should be selected) Click on Save button. To Remove the user from the Space permissions, unselect all check boxes and click on Save

short_description: How to restart Service need EMR applications when heartbeat alert is on
introduction: Introduction This runbook introduces how to monitor and restart the applications running on EMR cluster at AWS global. EMR is Amazon's managed cluster platform, especially suitable for running big data frameworks such as Apache Spark. Currently, there are four applications running on EMR: StreamingEngine (Engine, Fault Sequence Detector, FSD) WibeDoorStateDetection NoServiceStoppedBetweenFloors GroupUtilizationAggregator Currently, only StreamingEngine has been deployed to China, whereas all four have been deployed to global AWS. CloudWatch alarms EMR applications have four CloudWatch alarms: ApplicationErrorAlarm TriggerExecutionDurationAlarm ProcessedRowMetricsMissingRecoveryAlarm ProcessedRowsPerSecondAlarm Each alarm has the same prefix structure: "247-EMR_<cluster name>_<application name>_<alarm name> (for instance, the ApplicationErrorAlarm of FSD is named as 247-EMR_streaming-emr6_StreamingEngine_ApplicationErrorAlarm). Alarms have been configured to send a notification to Teams channel " 247 Analytics AWS DevOps PROD alarms " when in alarm and when recovered. Flowchart for EMR alarms What happens when an application stops If one or more of the applications running on EMR cluster dies, autorecovery configured to YARN settings will try to restart the application in total ten times . If YARN is unable to restart the application within 15 minutes, CloudWatch alarm called "EMR...ProcessedRowMetricsMissingRecoveryAlarm" will trigger alarm, and sends notification to Teams. When this happens, the L2 team should first try to restart the application. If this action does not successfully restart the application and remove the alarm, please contact 24/7 Safety Analytics team
instructions: How to restart an application (using AWS console) Log in to the AWS account (production account: LZ-IoT-GL-247-Analytics-prod) and open management console. Go to EMR, choose the cluster named as streaming-emr6 and proceed to the Steps tab. In the steps tab, filter the steps of the wanted application by using the name of the application. The name of the failed application can be retrieved from the alarm name. Choose the highest restart step and click Clone step . Note that choosing the highest step is crucial in order to restart the correct version. If in doubt of the correct version, see "How to see the version of application causing the alarm". For checking the version of the restarted application, see "Appendix: How to see which applications are currently running" instructions below. Keep the default values and click Add . Wait until the status changes from Pending to Completed. Fig. Steps tab of EMR on AWS management console. Red squares mark the "Clone step" button and the highest restart step. Note the filtering using the application name or part of it (here "Wibe"). How to see the version of application causing the alarm (using AWS console) Log in to the AWS account (production account: LZ-IoT-GL-247-Analytics-prod) and open management console. Go to DynamoDB and choose Tables/Explore items from the left pane. Choose streaming-emr6-EmrApplicationTable and click the correct application name. Version can be found from the lowest row. Appendix: How to see which applications are currently running YARN logs Log in to the AWS account (production account: LZ-IoT-GL-247-Analytics-prod) and open management console. Go to EMR and choose the cluster named as streaming-emr6 . Check the Master public DNS from the Summary tab. Then, go to Hardware tab and choose from "Cluster: streaming-emr6" Master node type and click the link in the ID column. Search the instance which Private DNS name corresponds with the Master public DNS name and click its link in the EC2 instance ID column. In the EC2 tab, select streaming-emr6 instance and click Connect (fig. 3). Fig. 3 EC2 tab on AWS management console. The streaming-emr6 instance and the Connect button are marked with red squares. Choose Session Manager tab and click Connect (fig. 4). Fig. 4 Session manager tab for connecting the EMR cluster. In the Session terminal, type sudo su su hadoop To see which applications are currently running, type yarn application -list To see certain application stages (options are: ALL,NEW,NEW_SAVING,SUBMITTED,ACCEPTED,RUNNING,FINISHED,FAILED,KILLED), type the following command and include the needed app stages. Example code is for listing all FINISHED,FAILED and KILLED stages. Use grep at the end to filter with application name (e.g., type "yarn application -list -appStates FINISHED,FAILED,KILLED | grep StreamingEngine" to include only streamingEngine). yarn application -list -appStates FINISHED,FAILED,KILLED To see which application version is currently running, first get the application ID by typing (the following example returns the application ID of the StreamingEngine) yarn application -list | grep StreamingEngine | cut -d ' ' -f1 Then, copy the application ID and replace <INSERT APPLICATION ID> with the copied ID. The code returns the current application version. yarn logs -applicationId <INSERT APPLICATION ID> -log_files stderr | grep application.version | head - 1 | sed 's/.*version=//' CloudWatch logs An alternative way to examine the YARN logs is to make queries to CloudWatch logs. Log in to the AWS account (production account: LZ-IoT-GL-247-Analytics-prod) and open management console. Go to CloudWatch and choose Logs Insights from the left panel. Select the following log groups : /emr/streaming-emr6/bootstrap-logs /emr/streaming-emr6/console-logs /emr/streaming-emr6/container-logs /emr/streaming-emr6/yarn-logs Then, write a suitable query to search correct rows from the logs. An example query to search the 20 most recent rows from the WibeDoorStateDetection application logs can be found below. To search the logs of another application, replace 'WibeDoorStateDetection' with either 'StreamingEngine', 'NoServiceStoppedBetweenFloors' or 'GroupUtilizationAggregator'. fields @timestamp , @message | sort @timestamp desc | filter @message like 'WibeDoorStateDetection' | limit 20 Check the timestamp of the log entries to see when they have been written. The name and version of the application can typically be found from the end of each log entry.

short_description: Page Restrictions in Confluence Cloud
introduction: This KBA will have the instructions to How to handle requests related to page restrictions in Confluence Cloud To be more specific How to handle requests stating "Need View/Edit/Admin access to pages in Confluence Cloud"
instructions: The page restrictions can be set by the Space admin or any user who is having Edit access to that space. Whoever sets the page restrictions will have the higher level of access to that page to otherwise the admin of that page PAGE RESTRICTIONS In spite of the Space Permission, the pages and sub pages can be restricted from Viewing and editing . Case 1 : If a user is having an Edit option(pen icon) in the Confluence page, this means that there are no restrictions applied to the page. Case 2 : If a user does not have an Edit option(pen icon) in the confluence page, this means that Edit restriction is applied to the page. NOTE: Owner of the page or user having edit access to the page can also provide the view/edit access to other Confluence Cloud users. To fix this issue, click on the Lock icon , check the user who is having higher level access(the user who is having Edit access) get approval from them and provide the edit access to the user by following the below steps Search for the user name and click on the user name. Click on Add button. You can see that user is added then click on Share button Case 3 : If a user is not able to view a page and gets below error but able to view the respective Space, this means that there is View restriction applied to the page. To fix this issue, click on the Lock icon , check the user who is having higher level access(the user who is having Edit access) get approval from them and provide the edit access to the user by following the below steps Search for the user name and click on the user name. Select the level of access View Only or Can edit and then Click on Add button. You can see that user is added then click on share button NOTE : Even by having Admin rights, the support person will not be able to view some of the restricted page, to find it, you can check by navigating to Space Settings -> under Content select Restricted and you can find the restricted pages and the creator.

short_description: How to Check/Where to check 24/7 Planner Site Survey info when it is not yet visible in Planner Tool after survey being conducted
introduction: How/Where to check 24/7 Planner Site Survey info when it is not yet visible in Planner Tool after survey being conducted Site surveys enabling 24/7 Planner are conducted by technicians using Data Collection Tool(Site Survey Tool). Planner Key Users and Salespersons can access survey information in Planner Tool, but the visibility of site survey data in Planner tool has 48 hours delay from site survey being conducted . This doc provides a work around (from using Planner Tool) to view the site survey data within this 48-hour period: In addition to 24/7 Planner Tool, Site survey data can be viewed also in KONE Trace portal, right after the survey is conducted.
instructions: Here are step by step instructions: Login to TRACE Portal: https://trace.kone.com/ and input the Equipment/Ken Number Navigate to the Documents Section and select the site survey option. You should be able to view the survey details under the latest survey ID Note: If the site survey data is still not visible after 48 hours in Planner tool, please raise an incident to 24/7 Planner support team.

short_description: How to translate Service Need descriptions, symptoms, and action recommendation
introduction: How to translate Service Need descriptions, symptoms, and action recommendation Service need translations includes 3 separate content that needs to be translated: Service need description is the name of the service need and visible in KFM first. Service need symptom is the short explanation how technician/customer might see the fault on site. Service need action recommendations are the instructions which is the most probably actions that will fix the issue on site.
instructions: In PDM, Select Other functionalities and tools -> Service needs -> Manage translations. Go to Export translations You would need to make below selections for the export: What do you want to translate? Service need translation object will be included in the file you want to download translations for: SN description SN symptom SN actions What language you want to translate? Is the language you want to translate From what language(s) you want to translate from? Includes possibility to select the language where you want to translate your translations from Include only objects with no existing translation will bring you only the ones that do not have the translations in the language you want to translate. NOTE! When you do the translations for the first time please do not tick the box since some of the items might include old NOT TRANSLATED ones that would not be visible if box is selected When all selections have been done you can press button Start to export After pressing Start you will see the Download translation template link under the selection list. Click it for PDM to save the excel file on your PC. File will include either all the translations or only the ones that are missing translations depending on the selection you made in the Include only objects without existing translations tick box. · Add translations to the excel file If you have downloaded all translations and not only those where it’s missing, remove the rows that should not be changed. Translate the texts and save the document. · Import the file After you have done all the translations in the excel. Go to PDM Service Need-> Manage Translations-> Import translations Choose File from your computer Click Start button If import is successful, you will get OK translations updated information, if import is not successful the system will tell the line where problem is. The result will be available for the service needs you have translated.

short_description: How to create NS Order in SAP Z03/Z04?
introduction: How to create NS Order(Non SAP) in SAP Z03/Z04?
instructions: STEPS TO CREATE NS ORDER IN SAP Open “ZXCAMOS” Transaction code in SAP. Click on “Get Variants Icon”. Remove “created by”. Click on “Excute” Icon. You may find different variants here. Select the variants according to your need based on the which frontline and which version you need (You can also check on the short description). For example: Let’s take the variant “CA3-232W-KCC1” were, CA3 – DB 232W – Version KCC1 – Frontline We also have variants related to same frontline like “CA3-232W-KCC1EB” & “CA3-232W-KCC1FR” were, CA3-232W-KCC1EB – For Ebuli products CA3-232W-KCC1FR – For FRB Products. Note: You can always check on the “Short description” for which variant need to be choose. Select the variant as per your request and click on the ok icon. Now you can see CAMOS Command lines are filled automatically and click on excute button to proceed further. Now you will get KTOC order creation page. Follow the same instruction as how we create the tender. Upload the NS order template. Now you will have unit ready and click on handshake button and perform handshake(solve the inconsistency before Handshake if you have any). Fill the Mandate values as per your request. Give the equipment number starts with 4 and contain 8-digit value eg: “45678912” and also give 3C date which is today or future dates. To perform handshake click on Handshake button. Now you have successfully created the NS order – kindly note the NS order number for future references.

short_description: Ask IT - Windows 11 Feature Update - 23H2 Troubleshooting / Windows Update Troubleshooting
introduction: Feature update not getting installed or showing update failed error in software center. Monthly updates not getting installed via settings app or software center
instructions: This error typically occurs when an update is stuck and cannot proceed. To address the issue, follow these Level 1 steps: When a feature update is initiated, a hidden folder is created in the root drive (C:). Enable the option to view hidden files. Locate and delete the hidden folder. Delete Software Distribution folder after stop "Windows Update Services" and BITS Services: Folder Location- C:\windows\softwareDistribution\ Stop Windows Update Services: Open Command Prompt as an administrator. You can do this by searching for “cmd” in the Start menu, right-clicking on Command Prompt, and selecting “Run as administrator”. Type the following commands and press Enter after each one: net stop wuauserv net stop bits Delete the Software Distribution Folder: Open File Explorer and navigate to C:\Windows\SoftwareDistribution Select Software Distribution Folder and perform Shift + delete Restart Windows Update Services: Go back to the Command Prompt and type the following commands, pressing Enter after each one: net start wuaserv net start bits This process will clear the contents of the Software Distribution folder, which can help resolve issues with Windows Updates / Feature updates Once these folders are deleted restart SMSAGENT Services and launch Software center to the initiate update from software center. After installation is completed restart the machine again. Generally, feature update takes 40-60 mins depending on network bandwidth and Monthly updates take up to 15 minutes, so this troubleshooting is applicable on both scenario i.e. Feature update failure/Monthly update failure (Intune/software center). In case of feature update troubleshooting the Size of feature update is approx. 3gb so make sure if user is trying over VPN, the network connection is stable.

short_description: How to Troubleshooting Missing emails
introduction: How to Troubleshooting Missing emails Only the respective owner of Outlook can perform this action. You cannot perform this action in others’ Outlook. Please check following troubleshooting options: Check your internet connection Outlook needs a working internet connection to reach its email servers. If your computer, phone, or tablet is not connected to the internet, Outlook can’t refresh and load your new emails in your inbox. So take a moment to check if your internet connection is working properly. The fastest way to do that is to open your web browser and try to load your favorite website. If nothing happens, your internet is down, which would explain why you’re not receiving emails at the moment Check the Junk Email folder Sometimes messages are mistakenly marked as junk by other email apps. Check your Junk Email folder and see if the message is there. If the message has been moved incorrectly to this folder, select the message and then select Not junk > Not junk . If you're using the Outlook for iOS or Outlook for Android apps, go to the Junk Email folder, select the message and select > Move to Inbox . If you're using a mobile browser, go the Junk Email folder, select the message and select > Mark as not junk . To avoid messages being moved to the Junk Email folder by another email app, use a Microsoft app such as Mail for Windows 10 or Outlook for Windows. Note: Messages are automatically removed from the Junk Email folder after 30 days and aren't recoverable. Check the Other tab If Focused Inbox is turned on for your account, the message might be in the Other tab. Select the Other tab and see if the message is there. If the message has been moved incorrectly to the Other tab, select the message and select Move to > Move to Focused inbox . If you're using the Outlook for iOS or Outlook for Android apps or a mobile browser, select the Other tab, select the message and select > Move to Focused inbox Check the Deleted Items folder Make sure the missing message isn't in your Deleted Items folder . If the message has been moved incorrectly to this folder, select the message and then select Restore . If you're using the Outlook for iOS or Outlook for Android apps or a mobile browser, go to the Deleted Items folder, select the message and select > Move to folder > Inbox . If you don't see the message in the Deleted Items folder, the message might have been removed but is still recoverable. In Deleted Items, above the message list, select Recover items deleted from this folder . If you find the message, select it and then select Restore . Notes: Messages removed from the Deleted Items folder are recoverable for 30 days. You can't recover messages removed the Deleted Items folder in Outlook for iOS or Outlook for Android apps or a mobile browser. To do this, please use a computer. Messages deleted from a child's account can't be recovered Check the Filter menu If you're filtering your message list, the message you're looking for might not appear. To change your filtering options, select Filter above the message list and select All . If you're using the Outlook for iOS or Outlook for Android apps or a mobile browser, the current filter selection appears above the message list. To clear the filter selection, select it above the message list. Check your rules You might have created an Inbox rule or a Sweep rule that's that's moving messages to another folder. Go to Rules settings and check your rules. If you find a rule that's incorrectly moving messages out of your inbox, select to edit the rule or to delete it. Note: You must use a computer to view and edit your rules. Check the Archive folder If you've archived a message, it won't appear in your inbox. Make sure the missing message isn't in your Archive folder . If you want to move an archived message back to your inbox, select the message and then select Move to > Inbox . If you're using the Outlook for iOS or Outlook for Android apps or a mobile browser, go to the Archive folder, select the message and select > Move to folder > Inbox . Check the recent sign-in activity for your account If you get an email about unusual activity on your Microsoft account, or if you’re worried that someone else might have used your account, go to the Recent activity page . You’ll see when your Microsoft account was signed in during the last 30 days, along with any device or app-specific info. Check your blocked users list Outlook lets you block a sender or a domain in just a few clicks. This is a great way to protect your inbox from spammers and cyberbullies. Once you’ve blocked someone, Microsoft automatically sends all their emails to your Junk Folder regardless of their content. So if you’re waiting for an email from someone, you may want to check if the sender is registered on your blocked users list. Note: To access your list of blocked users, sign in to your Outlook account. Then go to Settings > Junk Email > Blocked senders and domains . Is the email address of the sender listed here? If yes, make sure to remove it. You can even whitelist this address by adding it to your Safe senders and domains list . Check How’s your storage limit? Outlook accounts can have up to 100GB of storage per mailbox. Which is more than enough in most cases. But if you’ve never cleaned your inbox, then you might have maxed out your storage limit. If that’s the case, you won’t be able to receive emails as long as you don’t make room in your inbox. Try clearing your account from unwanted content. Alternatively, you can save your large attachments to OneDrive.

short_description: INSTRUCTIONS ON REQUESTING LOCATION DATA FOR A LOST MOBILE DEVICE
introduction: INSTRUCTIONS ON REQUESTING LOCATION DATA FOR A LOST MOBILE DEVICE
instructions: INSTRUCTIONS ON REQUESTING LOCATION DATA FOR A LOST MOBILE DEVICE BASICS • If a mobile device is enrolled in Intune as a corporate-owned, fully managed device, and if the device is reported as lost or stolen by the device user or another person on their behalf (with the device user’s written consent), KONE may request the location data of a device through the Intune admin portal. • The “locate a device” feature may only be used if the device user has given written consent allowing KONE to search for the device using the location data. • The “locate a device” feature is available for a limited number of global Intune admins of KONE and KONE’s service provider Infosys. The location data is available in the Intune admin portal, provided that the device user has enabled the location services on the device. The location data may only be used for locating a lost or stolen device as described herein. • When access to the device’s location data is requested, a notification from the Intune application will appear on the device, explaining that the device has been reported as lost or stolen and that the location of the device has been queried by the organization’s support team. A standard notification sound will be played unless notification sounds have been turned off. In case of any illegal or other suspicious activity, always contact security@kone.com ASK IT PROCESS FOR PROVIDING LOCATION DETAILS OF A LOST MOBILE DEVICE 1. The device user provides their written consent allowing KONE to search for the device using the location data of the device. The consent may be provided in free written form, i.e., by email or by using a consent form. 2. The device user, or another person on their behalf (with the device user’s written consent), contacts Ask IT to request the location of a lost mobile device that is enrolled in Intune as corporate-owned, fully managed device. The device user's written consent allowing KONE to search for the device using the location data is required. Ask IT ensures the written consent is available and archives it in the ticket log. a. If the device user has several devices enrolled in Intune as corporate-owned, fully managed devices, it must be clearly stated which device is lost to ensure which device’s location data is being requested. b.Once the written approval is logged into the Ask IT ticket log system, Ask IT transfers the ticket to the Infosys Mobile Device Management Level 2 support team (the “MDM team”). 3. The MDM team confirms whether the device is enrolled in Intune as corporate-owned, fully managed device, meaning that it is possible to request the device location data, and if so, requests the device location data through the Intune admin portal. 4. The MDM team contacts the requestor: a. If the device location data is available, the MDM team will provide the location data to the requestor. b. If the device location data is not available (e.g., because the device location services are turned off), or if the device is not enrolled in Intune as corporate-owned, fully managed device, the MDM team will inform the requestor that the location data of the device is not available.

short_description: How to configure Incident questionnaire in ServiceNow CMDB
introduction: This KBA will help on how to configure Incident questionnaire in ServiceNow CMDB: "Incident Questionnaire" - New field enabled in the ITSM tool for Service Offering in Service Offering Table. Optimizing Incident Resolution with Automated Questionnaires •Streamlined Process: Operations Managers can create and manage questionnaires or templates for applications •Predefined Questionnaires: When users submit an incident for a specific “Service Offering,” relevant questionnaires are automatically added to the incident description. •Efficiency Boost: This automation reduces investigation time for your support team, leading to faster resolutions. •Time Savings: AMS support teams or ASK IT can efficiently update templates for vendor-related cases, facilitating quicker incident resolution.
instructions: A dd Question for Service Offering : Step 1 : Login into ServiceNow -> All -> Service Offering -> Search for your application NOTE : If Incident Questionnaire is not visible in the List, Go to Personalize and add the Incident Questionnaire Step 2 : Double-click on the empty space in “Incident Questionnaire” against your Application name. In the pop-up dialog, you can enter template questions or any mandatory questions that will support your team in their investigations. Example: Assest ID, Windows ID, URL, Environment, How many impacted, etc. Step 3: Click “Tick” icon to save the questionnaire. NOTE : This feature is only for users who submit tickets through the “Self-service” portal. Operations Managers can update / edit anytime in the tool based on the requirements. You can verify by logging into the Employee Center page. Go to “Something doesn’t work,” select the application where the questionnaire was added, and you will find the questions listed in the description field.

short_description: How to Approve Service request and Change via Outlook
introduction: This KBA will help on how to approve Service Request and Change via Outlook
instructions: One-Click Approval via Outlook - Service Request 1. Submit Service request from Employee center 2. If Approval required 3. Notification sent for approval to the approvers. 4. Approvers can request additional information from Requestor via clicking the RITMXXXX URL 5. Approve the Request by providing approval comments and clicking on Submit 6. Reject the Request by providing rejection comments and clicking on Submit 7. Before Approving/Rejecting Approvers can click “View Details” for quick view of RITM 8. Approvers can also view the Approval Request and Approve/Reject from View Approval Request One-Click Approval via Outlook - Change Step 1: Submit Change Request & Request Approval Step 2: Approval Mail Sent to Approvers Step 3: Approvers can click on the “Link” to view the Change Request Step 4: Approve The Change Request By Providing Approval Comments Step 5: Reject The Change Request By Providing Rejection Comments Step 6: Approvers can also view the Approval Request and Approve/Reject from there

short_description: ePil - How to check if Outbound Delivery number is triggered to ePil
introduction: ePil - How to check if Outbound Delivery number is triggered to ePil
instructions: Login SAP and open t-code VL03N. Paste the Outbound Delivery Number and click on Enter. Now under Extras, click on Delivery Output and then click on Header. Check if there is YOIG and YOIP output triggered. If they are present, then the order will be trigger to ePil database. Now check in ePil if the order is there or not using the Outbound Delivery number.

short_description: Change the primary and secondary owners in the Product/Project in windchill
introduction: How to change the primary and secondary owners in the Product/Project in windchill?
instructions: Scenario 1: If the request to change the owners is from a Primary or Secondary owner, no approval is required. Scenario 2 : If the request is not from the "Current Owners" we need to seek approval from the Primary/Secondary owner and confirm with the destination owner. Scenario 3: If all approvers are no longer available, the Service Manager can approve the change with a valid business reason provided by the requestor.

short_description: Planview: How to change Work type from Light Project to KONE Way Roll-out?
introduction: The Work type can be changed. It is a complex task, so Planview users can request this to be done by Support. Before raising an AskIT ticket, please read the following instructions.
instructions: Information to be attached to the ticket List of projects: provide names and Work IDs of the projects, where the work type needs to be changed. NOTE: Projects in Done or Rejected statuses will not be converted, as they are historical info and do not benefit anymore from this activity Gate model: Tell to Support which Gate model the projects should have, once converted into Roll-out: Light (R0 and CC) or Full (R0, R1, R2, R3, CC). Extract current Schedule and Status information Work type change impacts the Schedule and Work Status information. The existing schedule will get removed and the Work Status will get reverted back to Funnel. (Other project information will not be impacted) If you wish to use the current Schedule or Status information as reference after the conversion activity, please make sure to extract it from Planview beforehand. Setting R-gate schedules after Work type change Once the Work type is changed into Roll-out, project will have the R-gates available in the schedule. User now needs to enter the milestone dates and do the needed gate approvals to get the project into correct status. How to raise ticket in AskIT 1. Open AskIT --> search Generic service request 2. Open Generic Service request pick the service as Planview and provide all the required details and submit the request

short_description: How to change work type from Light Project to Roll-out
introduction: How to change work type from Light Project to Roll-out ?
instructions: We may receive request from end users for changing the work type from light project to KONE Way Roll-out. In such cases please follow the below instructions. 1. Open Planview ( https://kone.pvcloud.com/ ) --> Go to Work tab --> Work Portfolios --> Select the correct portfolio (eg.Lean KONE- Work type change) --> Portfolio View. 2. Create a Work portfolio (Satu can help here), which contains the work items in question and use “KONE Way Roll-out” column-set (the Work type can only be changed from the column set view, that is why the portfolio is needed) 3. Change the Work type from Work portfolio, from e.g. Roll-out column set 4. Click three dots -->work view to open the project. 5. In the project, enter the correct gate model -> Save 6. Go to Work and Assignment, and if there is any dates or Percent Complete figures on the schedule, delete them. 7. Go to ”Lifecycle” tab > Find box ”Execute KONE – Paste WBS Template” - Click the pen icon in that box. From the pop-up, click ”Restart Lifecycle” > Confirm with ”OK” Click pen icon again --> Click ”Perform” Next check from ”Work and Assignments” that milestones have appeared Back in ”Lifecycle” tab, mark the two bottom boxes of the lifecycle as Complete through the pen icon --> ”Mark Complete" 8. Go to ”Roll-out Gate Approvals (ADMIN)” tab - Add a value ”NEW (Draft)” to the ”Next Gate” field > Save - Now the values should be like this: - Work status: Funnel, Current Gate: NEW (Draft), Next Gate: NEW (Confirmed) Once the above mentioned actions are done, inform users on the same to update the schedules.

short_description: How to handle Splunk Alert – Long Running Reports and Dashboards tickets by CRM AMS
introduction: We have an alert created for the reports and dashboards that are taking more than 10 minutes to load in Business org. Platform team has identified 60 odd reports and dashboards like these where loading time is more than 10 minutes. So if there are any reports or dashboards other than the ones that are identified by Platform team taking more than the 10 minutes limit then there will be an alert generated with name "Splunk Alert – Long Running Reports and Dashboards" . So whenever the CRM team gets this alert ticket, Below are the steps that needs to be followed.
instructions: Step 1: In the Splunk ticket, There will be an excel file attached with the report details which are taking more than 10 minutes for loading. Step 2: We need to verify this report in CRM with the help of Report id that is present in the Excel file Step 3: After opening that report in CRM, If that is taking more than 10 minutes, then we need to copy the Report id and fetch the information about the reports from the query editor (like Workbench, Salesforce inspector etc.). Step 4: We need to fetch the information like Name of report, Developer, who created the report, when the report was created, when it was modified and Last modified by. Query for the Report details: Select id, Developer Name, CreatedBy.name, CreatedBy.IsActive, FolderName, CreatedDate, LastRunDate, LastModifiedDate, LastModifiedBy.name From Report where id= 'report id' Step 5: After getting this information, we need to contact the user who created(if created user is Inactive) contact last Modified user that specific report and send him an email about the performance issue asking to adjust the filters. Step 6: If the created by name and Last Modified by name are different. Then we need to send an email to both the users regarding performance issue and need to attach the screenshot of the current filters that are present in the report and explain them which ones are affecting the loading times。 Step 7: Check with User to revise the filter criteria so as to make it run below 10 minutes. if its new report and if user doesn't agree for the filter changes or we don't get any response from the users then Platform team to be notified (Zuheb & Priyanka). If user agrees and made changes from their end then we can check once by running that report in CRM and attach the new filter screenshot in the ticket and close it.

short_description: How to find the GPS coordinates in SAP using Equipment number in IMT?
introduction: Whenever the supervisor changed the location(Site Coordinates) of a project/network in IMT3, the coordinates will be updated in the SAP table ZPS_IMT_EQUI_GPS.
instructions: Steps to find the updated coordinates, 1. Login with SAP Z03 or Z04 2. Type and use the T-code SE16N 3. Enter the table name as ZPS_IMT_EQUI_GPS and in selection criteria, please enter the equipment number(Eg: 42398168) in the Equipment field as like below, 4. After click execute, we can be able to see the updated coordinates. Note: Using the Maximum no. of hits to limit/reduce the co-ordinates list(Eg: 500)

introduction: This KBA will help the user on how to raise a ITSM Service Request form from Employee Center for Creation / Modification and Deletion of a Service Request form in ServiceNow. To Download the ITSM Service Request Template, use this link: ITSM Service Request Template
instructions: Step 1: Login into ServiceNow -> Employee Center View Use this link Kone Employee Center Homepage - Kone Employee center (service-now.com) Step 2: Search and o pen the ITSM Service Request form from Employee Center. Step 3: To create a new Service Request Form in ServiceNow Click Action -> Create Step 3.1: Download the Service Request template on opening the KBA. Step 3.2: Fill the Excel template and Attach the file Step 3.3: Submit Step 4: To modify an Existing Service Request Form in ServiceNow Click Action -> Modify Step 4.1: Download the Service Request template on opening the KBA. Step 4.2: Fill the Excel template and Attach the file Step 4.3: Submit Step 5: To delete an Existing Service Request Form in ServiceNow Click Action -> Delete Step 5.1: Select the Service Request Form name from the dropdown Step 5.2: Submit

short_description: ePil - How to check pick request (YOIG&YOIP) output type triggered in SAP ?
instructions: Step : 1 We have to check YOIG & YOIP message status using T- code VL03N in the SAP. Step : 2 Insert Outbound delivery and click “enter” Delivery number – 710860165 Step : 3 Then page will be display. Step : 4 Go to Extras ->Delivery output-> Header Step : 5 We can find the YOIG & YOIP message indicates in green color status box successfully. NOTE : Pick request (YOIG&YOIP) output will be created for the outbound delivery in SAP. Outbound delivery creators will send the 3X form along with delivery note to epil users. The epil user should fill the 3X form and return to requestor. once requestor received the 3X form and they will triggered the pick request (YOIG&YOIP) output in SAP.

instructions: 3) Go to Special-->More-->TaskConfigurator --> More -->Create TACO frontline settings. 4) Enter the Necessary Details in the appeared dialog box to Create a TACO frontline Settings. 5 ) Click start to perform the required action to create a frontline settings. short_description: TACO - How to release the TACO order manually in PDM
introduction: Error: Scenario's : 1) Error occurs when we try to release the Order in TACO (refer above error image) 2) Mismatching the state for the TACO Order in PDM as well as TACO
instructions: Steps: For Scenario 1 : 1) Go to the Configuration History for the particular Network in TACO UI 2) Try to Create a new revision for the Configuration by hit this option from TACO UI, then try to release the Newly created (draft state) TACO order Configuration revision in TACO UI. For Scenario 2 : If the First Scenario Fail's. Please proceed for the Second scenario. 1) Launch the Variclient, locate the TACO order and check for the configuration state mismatching. 2) Incase mismatched (we say it's release in TACO UI and Draft in PDM) missing Parameter may be the reason and so Collect the missing parameter for the TACO order from the TACO-Key User and manually add the missing configuration in PDM. TACO order --> Search for the Attribute parameters--> Manually enter the missing value's and click OK and save the changes. 3) After the missing parameters added manually execute the order action from Variclient. TACO order --> specials--> Digital Factory--> Execute order Actions. 4) Click start to perform the order action. Once after started, New Configuration revision created (which is in Draft state). 5) Now the Newly created configuration (which is is draft state) need to be released from PDM like below : TACO order -->specials-->TaskConfigurator--> Release plan. Once we hit the release plan functionality new Popup open's and click start to release the Newly created configuration in PDM. Once all these steps completed. Issue Fixed.

short_description: TACO - How to solve the issue while releasing an Task Configuration order Manually in PDM
introduction: Error : Steps to reproduce the Error :
instructions: 1) Business End user trying to Release the order 2) The below error thrown while try to Release the Plan during TACO order release in PDM. Reason for the Issue : If the Characteristics are missed from the Order. It might thrown these kind of Error's. Here, characteristics DIM_CAR_GUIDE_DISTANCE_CC and DIM_WALL_A_TO_GUIDE_AXIS are missing from the order -TACO6736031-15. Resolution : 1) Get the Necessary details from the TACO-Key User like TACO Order ID, Network ID, etc.., 2) Create a Bug in Jira and requesting Help from the Advanced level Support (Especially @tommi.syrjanen@kone.com) to find the Missing characteristics. 3) Reference Bug -- https://kone.atlassian.net/browse/ITPLM-410 4) Once we got the characteristics share with the TACO-Key User and close the AskIT ticket.

short_description: SAP Security Role Modification Process
introduction: Task Handled by L2 Team User Group Modification Master and Derived roles Creation & Modification Single Role Creation & modification
instructions: User Group Modification User will request for User Group (UG) modification for below cases, Standard t-code addition Custom t-code addition Standard t-code addition: L1.5 team will assign the ticket with L2 team for additional standard t-code assigned with User Group role, we will be searching for the relevant role using t-code SUIM and identify which role can provide access to requested Transaction code. If the standard t-code is not present in any of the existing single/Derived roles, we will be adding into any of the users existing roles depend on the functionality/Auth. Objects of the t-code. Identifying the single/Derived role includes the criteria of UG role or Company code which is already assigned to the user (IT/Business). Once the role is identified, will perform SoD Risk Analysis to check if adding role/t-code is causing any SOD conflict with User Group. Steps to check SoD risk analysis is provided in below document. Once our analysis is completed, we will be sending mail to User Group owner with below details along with SoD report. If no SOD is found – User group owner will approve upon his/her analysis. If SOD is found – We will inform user group owner and internal control team. Upon their decision the request will be approved/rejected Ticket no. User ID Username User GROUP Composite role Role Description T-code need access Role Description T-code in role SoD Conflict(yes/No) Above details will be Reviewed by User Group owner and below is the Link for User Group owner Approval Matrix, KONE Access Requests Approval Matrix.xlsx Once User Group Owner approved, Create CR, NC for role changes – Steps to create CR, NC are attached below. We will add the identified role to User Group Role in Z01 System in t-code PFCG. User Groups are named as YECCC:XXX_YYY_ZZZZ (YECCC = Composite roles in ECC, XXX = Team to which user belongs to, YYY= Job Description, ZZZZ= Operative unit) EX: YECCC:KBS_BRA_CN1 Add the User Group to TR and will be Transported to Pre-production for UAT. Create a test user id in Pre-production using same access which user have in Production along with the requested access and send credentials to user for UAT After receiving the confirmation from user that the requested access is successful, then the TR will be moved to Production Steps to create TR attached below Once changes moved to production Ticket can be closed with confirmation from user And If user group owner rejected due to SoD risk/access not needed, we will communicate same to user and Ticket can be closed. Custom t-code addition: L1.5 team will assign the ticket with L2 team for additional custom t-code assigned with User Group role, we will be searching for the relevant role using t-code SUIM and identify which role can provide access to requested Transaction code. If the custom t-code is present in any of the existing single/Derived roles, we will be following the same process as standard t-code addition. If the custom t-code is not present in any of the existing single/Derived roles, we will be adding into any of the users existing roles depend on the functionality/Auth. Objects of the t-code. Some custom t-codes doesn’t have Auth. Obj maintained in SU24. So, we must connect with user and create test user id’s and test role in Z03 (Pre-production) for that t-code and check if any authority check has been maintained or not. We have to analysis the errors from t-codes SU53, ST01 and STAUTHTRACE. Once we got all the required details, we need to add and maintain Auth. Obj related to that t-code as per requirement into SU24 as below, Checking SoD analysis and adding t-code is explained in steps 2.1.3 and 2.1.5 Once our SoD analysis is completed, If no SOD is found – t-code added into role. If SOD is found – We will inform SDO/CO and internal control team. Upon their decision the request will be approved/rejected Upon the approval t-code will be added into rol...

short_description: How to create SAP Access Request for ECC systems based on missing Authorization Object(SU53)
introduction: * How to handle Incident/Service Requests for Access to missing Authorization Object(SU53)? * How to assign missing role based on SU53 screenshot for missing t-code/object?
instructions: If existing user facing issue for missing authorization for transaction code/ object after executing the transaction, then end user have to take SU53 by following below steps or IT consultant can apply trace on users ID by using Transaction code: STAUTHTRACE in order to provide missing authorization. Steps to follow to capture the error/SU53: (DO NOT SKIP ANY STEP) • Login to the respective SAP system. • Execute the required transaction code • If you see the error message, save the error screenshot. • Paste the screenshot in a new word document • Execute /oSU53 in the transaction code text box. • Take that screenshot also, save it. • Paste this screenshot in the same word document and share/attach the document in the incident. Sample Example of SU53 screenshot: Once we got the SU53, will analyze the appropriate role using t-code: SUIM based on users existing SAP access. T-code : SUIM Role : If suitable role found as per User Existing Access, then we raise GRC for role assignment. Note: If Appropriate role not found for SU53, then we will send the Incident to Security AMS L2 support team if any new role creation/existing role modification required. Once users confirmed the access, will proceed for the closure of the ticket. Reference KBA: KBA1169 How to create SAP Access Request for ECC using SAP GRC? KBA7233: AskIT - How to raise GRC access request for end users based on Transaction Code(s)/ Role(s) for ERP(Z0*) systems.

short_description: How to Set Up Delegate Approvers in ServiceNow
introduction: In ServiceNow, delegate approvers can be set up to ensure that approval tasks are managed efficiently when the primary approver is unavailable. This Knowledge Article gives instructions to users on How to set up Delegate Approvers in ServiceNow.
instructions: Steps to Set Up Delegate Approvers: 1. Log in to ServiceNow Use your credentials to log in to your ServiceNow prod instance. Kone Employee Center Homepage - Kone Employee center (service-now.com) 2. Click on the profile Icon and select your profile option `3. Scroll down, you can see Delegate 4. Fill in the Delegation Form: User : Select the user who is the primary approver and is delegating their approval responsibilities. Delegate : Select the user who will act as the delegate (i.e., the person who will handle the approvals on behalf of the primary approver). From Date: Set the date from which the delegation should begin. To Date: Set the date until which the delegation will be active. If the delegation is ongoing, this can be left open-ended. Delegate Type : Choose the type of delegation (e.g. Approvals, Assignments, CC notification, Meeting invitation.) depending on the requirements. Create – Fill the form and click on create.
