short_description: How to extract SOD Report
introduction: How to extract SOD Report ?
instructions: Whenever a mail/ ticket for Segregation of Duties report extraction is received from Role/ SoD approver then analyze the SoD report and share it with the approvers. Below are the steps followed to extract SoD from the access requests that are raised. Step1> Login to GRC Production (GRP) system. Step2> Execute transaction code NWBC then go to path Access Management à Access Requests Administration à Search Requests and click on “Search Requests” hyper link. Once clicked on “Search Requests” hyper link below screen will be displayed then enter GRC access request number and click on “Search” button for which SoD report need to be extracted. Select the request and click on “open”. Step3> once request is opened, click on “Risk Violations” tab and scroll down to see Risk result. In the risk result section select format as “Detail” and click on “Export” (Export to Microsoft Excel) to get the risk result downloaded. Step4> open the downloaded excel sheet and remove the unnecessary columns (like Last Executed On, Execution Count, Control, Monitor, Org Rule ID, Mitigation Type) from the sheet. Apply filter on “Short description” and select only valid one and then highlight the roles under the valid with the same color (As roles requested in the request comes under the valid, to identify existing and newly requested roles highlighting with color) Add Access Risk ID and Function description in the sheet. Step5> Apply filter to the header row and select only “TCD” from the “Resource Extn” column. Now from the column “Access Risk ID” select one risk and then select one of the functions from “Function” column and copy roles of that function to next new sheet and repeat this for all functions under the risk as shown below. Step6> once function related roles are copied to the new sheet then remove the duplicate roles and repeat for other functions in the same risk. Step7> Repeat step5 and step6 for all the risks. Step8> once analysis is completed is for all the risks then send an email to the respective approver by keeping GRC team and Internal Controls manager (Kristiina Kantola) in Cc. Below is the sample email sent to approver after risk analysis is completed. Step9> If the approver replies for removal of existing list of roles, then raise a GRC access request for removal of roles and send an email to approver by sharing the GRC access request number.

short_description: AskIT Printer Support: Common Issues and Solutions (RICOH)
introduction: AskIT Printer Support: Common Issues and Solutions (RICOH)
instructions: AskIT is responsible to support: Basic printer issues Print Queue issues Printer connectivity issue or unable to print. Basic Printer troubleshooting: AskIT needs to verify that the physical printer is in a ready state, ensure the correct default printer is set, ping the printer IP provided by the user, and validate it in the CMDB. If a request times out, AskIT will gather the following information from the end-user and assign the ticket to the VPSX team: - Printer name or model -IP address -MAC address & Serial Number Print Queue issues: Print jobs get stuck in the queue when a device stops printing in the middle of a job. This can happen due to various reasons such as a sudden power failure, low ink, no paper, a paper jam etc. AskIT agent needs to identify the issue and try to resolve it. Restarting the print spooler service from services.msc, From the Cancel option on the printer or click on Cancel All Documents if you want to cancel all the printing jobs on queue. After performing the 1 st line troubleshooting, AskIT to get the below information from the user and assign the ticket to VPSX team. - Printer name or model -IP address -MAC address & Serial number Printer connectivity issue or unable to print / Offline: Corrupted, incompatible, or outdated drivers, wrong printer settings, Printer offline and non-working cables. Issue: Printer is connected to the network but still not printing. Possible cause: The printer may be offline, has a paper jam or low on toner. Solution : Check if the printer is online, remove paper jams or replace the toner. Get the printer IP from Printer Properties>Ports>Configure ports and check the ping result, if IP is not pinging, Need to check if the IP address is valid or it has been changed which can be validated from CMDB ,If IP needs to be modified it can be checked by Verizon team, if we are able to ping the IP however printer is not functioning, it can be assigned to VPSX. If any of users came for network printer support which is not available in the printer locator https://koneprinter.konenet.com , follow the article KB0011569 and assist user accordingly. User Initiated Helpdesk Calls – Only RICOH Device The steps are described below: The user contacts AskIT agent regarding a problem with a Ricoh printing device. The AskIT agent finds out whether the problem is toner related and if it is, asks the user to contact Ricoh directly. If it is not, the AskIT agent logs the problem in ServiceNow tool. The AskIT agent investigates the nature of the problem to separate network related problems from device related ones. If the problem is device-related, the AskIT agent will contact the local Ricoh helpdesk and share the device information via email in the country where the device is located. The local Ricoh helpdesk will provide Ricoh internal ticket number to the AskIT agent. The AskIT agent will update Ricoh ticket with the internal ticket number in SN. Ricoh will resolve problem by following the below steps. Attempt to resolve problem remotely by contacting the local end/key user. Send a field technician to resolve problem on site. If the technician discovers that the problem is not device related and should have been managed by the AskIT support, he/she records this in the call. If the problem is not Ricoh device related, the technician closes the call and sends an email to the AskIT with the ticket number, explaining the situation. Once the problem is solved, Ricoh will inform the AskIT agent about the resolution referring the AskIT ticket number. The AskIT agent will inform the end user about the problem resolution. The call is closed by the AskIT and Ricoh local helpdesk. Ricoh support contact details: The SDM’s or end users who wish to report an issue directly to a Ricoh technician for general queries can do so by providing the contact information listed below. By doing so, they can avoid having to contact AskIT first, which will then submit a ticket and contact Ricoh support, which will shorten the wait time for a resolution. short_description: How to replace Owner / Approver in SAP GRC
introduction: How to replace Owner / Approver in SAP GRC
instructions: When an existing approver in the GRC system left the KONE organization or his / her role is changed then the approver needs to be changed / updated according to the mappings in the GRC Production (GRP) system. In GRC system different types of approvers exists and below are the steps explained to change different approvers. NOTE: If the replacement approver ID is not present in GRC Production then create approver ID by assigning the user group role YGRCC:ACC_APR_GLB (GRC, Access Control, Access Approver, Global) Also maintain approver ID in the Access Control Owners (path: NWBC à Access Management à GRC Role Assignments à Access Control Owners) Once clicked on “Access Control Owners” hyper link below screen will be displayed then click on “create” butto n. Once clicked on “create” button below screen will be displayed then enter approver ID select type as “Role Owner” and for Firefighter owner and controller select as “Firefighter ID Owner” and “Firefighter ID Controller”. 1. Role Approver Step1> Login to GRC Production (GRP) system. Step2> Execute transaction code NWBC then go to path Access Management à Role Mass Maintenance à Role Update and once clicked on Role Update hyper link below screen will be displayed. Click on “Add” button as shown below. Step3> Once clicked on “Add” button then below screen will be displayed and select the criteria as per the ticket / email received and follow steps as shown in the below screen. Step4> Select Attributes as “Owners”, Action as “Update” and click on “Next” button. Step5> Enter “Old Value” and “New Value” by selecting “Assignment Approver” tick box as highlighted in the below screen and click on “Next” button. Step6> click on submit to get role approver updated. 2. SoD Owner / Approver Step1> Login to GRC Production (GRP) system. Step2> Execute tcode SM30 and enter table name “ZSOD_VALIDATOR” and click on Maintain as shown in the below screen. Step3> once clicked on “Maintain” below screen will be displayed then click on “New entries”. Step4> Enter “Unit” and “User Name” as highlighted in the below screen then click on save button. Step5> Once new entries are added then in the table then delete the old entries for which replacement is done. Select the entry which is to be deleted and click on “delete” button. Once clicked on “delete” then click on “save” button as shown in the below screen. 3. User Group Approver User group (composite role) approver replacement process is same as Role approver. Please follow: 1 Role Approver process. 4. User Group Owner Step1> Login to GRC Production (GRP) system. Step2> Execute tcode SM30 and enter table name “ZUSERGRP_SOD” and click on Maintain as shown in the below screen. Step3> once clicked on “Maintain” below screen will be displayed then click on “New entries”. Step4> Enter “User Group”, “User Name” and “Unit” as highlighted in the below screen then click on save button. Step5> Once new entries are added then in the table then delete the old entries for which replacement is done. Select the entry which is to be deleted and click on “delete” button. Once clicked on “delete” then click on “save” button as shown in the below screen. 5. Finance Approver (Beacon Team ID Approver) Step1> Replace approver same as Role approver. Step2> Login to GRC Production (GRP) system. Execute tcode SM30 and enter table name “TEAM_IDS_BH” and click on Maintain as shown in the below screen. Step3> once clicked on “Maintain” below screen will be displayed then update the approver name directly in the “Finance Approver” column and click on “save” button as shown in the below screen. 6. Firefighter Owner Step1> Login to GRC Production (GRP) system. Step2> Execute transaction code NWBC then go to path Access Management à Emergency Access Management à Owners and once clicked on Owners hyper link below screen will be displayed. Click on “Assign” button. Step3> Enter Owner ID and click on “Add” button. Step4> once clicked on “Add” button below screen will be displayed, then click on highlighted box to select the Firefighter ID. Step5> follow steps as mentioned in the below screen to select the Firefighter ID. Step6> Once Firefighter ID is selected then click on “save” button as shown in below screen. Step7> select the Firefighter Owner ID whose mapping to Firefighter IDs is to be deleted by applying filter and click on “Select All” as shown in the below screen. Click on “Delete” button to get the selected entries deleted from the Firefighter Owner list. 7. Firefighter Controller Step1> Login to GRC Production (GRP) system. Step2> Execute transaction code NWBC then go to path Access Management à Emergency Access Maintenance à Controllers and once clicked on Controllers hyper link below screen will be displayed. Click on “Assign” button. Step3> Enter Controller ID and click on “Add” button. Step4> once clicked on “Add” button below screen will be displayed, then click on highlighted box to select the Firefighter ID. Step5> follow steps as mentioned in the below screen to select the Firefighter ID. Step6> once Firefighter ID is selected then select Notification by as “Email”. NOTE: select notification type as “Email” for Development & Quality systems and select notification type as “Workflow” for Production systems. Step7> Once above step is completed then click on “save” button as shown in below screen. Step8> select the Firefighter Controller ID whose mapping to Firefighter IDs is to be deleted by applying filter and click on “Select All” as shown in the below screen. Click on “Delete” button to get the selected entries deleted from the Firefighter Controller list.

short_description: KIS_EXTREME How to use the BypassDuplicate functionality to re-process invoices.
introduction: We recognize the possibility that third-party issues, as well as unforeseen challenges, may emerge in the future. To preemptively prevent the creation of a Change Request (CR) to disable the duplicate check feature in the future, we have introduced a new functionality. This functionality allows for bypassing the duplicate check process for specific invoice numbers. It has been successfully deployed into production and is functioning as anticipated, thereby eliminating the necessity for the creation of an emergency CR in the future. Below are the steps to be followed for using the functionality.
instructions: Steps to use the duplicate check bypass functionality in Exstream. Download the bypass file: Login to production resource S3 server kis-k8s-prod.s3.amazonaws.com . Download the duplicateBypass.tbl file from /custom_resources/kis_prod/inner_custom/tables . Open the file : Open the downloaded file in any text editor. Add the invoice numbers you want to bypass the duplicate check, then a tab character (one or more) followed by word “BYPASS”. Invoice numbers must be entered exactly as they appear in BELNR idoc field (ie. With leading zeroes). Then save the file Lines starting with “//” are comment lines. Please leave them to the file. Please find the below example. Upload the file back to Production: Login to production S3, same location where you downloaded duplicateBypass.tbl. Upload the modified duplicateBypass.tbl to S3, replacing the old one. (If your FTP client doesn’t allow to replace the existing file, remove the old file first, and then upload) Wait for about one minute for Exstream pod to sync the files from S3. Or you can login to the Exstream pod with kubectl exec --stdin --tty prod-kis-app1-0 -- /bin/bash, and check from /opt/app/platform/custom/tables that the table has the invoices listed. Note : There is no need to restart the application after you have uploaded the file to production. It will sync to the database automatically in few minutes. Re-trigger the invoices : Trigger the invoices from SAP, or reprocess the invoices from input files from S3 – whichever method is appropriate. You can ask the user to re-trigger the invoice from SAP itself or post the input xml from postman to https://prod-exstream.konenet.com/invoice/kis-xml Find an example below. Clean the bypass file: Once you are done re-triggering confirm the same from Kibana logs and Tracking tool and then download the bypassDuplicate.tbl file again and erase the invoice no/nos you have added. Please do not remove the comment lines.Save these changes. 6. Upload the file again : Now upload the modified files back to S3 in the appropriate location.

short_description: EPIL - How to troubleshoot job failure in database
instructions: Step 1: Connect to the EPIL database Step 2: Go to 'SQL Server Agent'. Then to 'Jobs'. Step 3: Then right click on the job 'ePil_ImportFromPackageSearchStaging'. Step 4: Then click 'View History'. Step 5: Click on the error. The error will be shown as below. Executed as user: KONENET\EUDVMMSSQL103_SEKO58. ------------ [SQLSTATE 01000] (Message 0) Populating PACKAGES [SQLSTATE 01000] (Message 0) Package Insertdbo.sp_ImportFromPackageSearchStaging: Violation of PRIMARY KEY constraint 'PK_PACKAGES'. Cannot insert duplicate key in object 'dbo.PACKAGES'. The duplicate key value is (9017275105). (Error No. 2627) at line # 68 [SQLSTATE 01000] (Message 0) Populating PACKAGES_LIST [SQLSTATE 01000] (Message 0) UPDATE Content for changed EXDIV [SQLSTATE 01000] (Message 0) Warning: Null value is eliminated by an aggregate or other SET operation. [SQLSTATE 01003] (Message 8153) Removing Processed Data from Package Search Staging Tables [SQLSTATE 01000] (Message 0) Package Insertdbo.sp_ImportFromPackageSearchStaging: Violation of PRIMARY KEY constraint 'PK_PACKAGES'. Cannot insert duplicate key in object 'dbo.PACKAGES'. The duplicate key value is (9017275105). (Error No. 2627) at line # 68Data clean upsp_StagingPackageSearchDataCleanUp: Column name or number of supplied values does not match table definition. (Error No. 213) at line # 9 [SQLSTATE 01000] (Message 0) ------------ [SQLSTATE 01000] (Message 0) Update Missing Project Ids [SQLSTATE 01000] (Message 0) Transaction count after EXECUTE indicates a mismatching number of BEGIN and COMMIT statements. Previous count = 0, current count = 1. [SQLSTATE 25000] (Error 266). The step failed. Step 6: As per the above error message there is duplicate value in inserting to 'Packages' table. Step 7: Run the below query and get the duplicate values and delete it. select * from hu_content where exidv like '%9017275105%' Step 8: Deleting the duplicate value will make the job to run successfully.

short_description: Recreate corrupt baselines in AEM
introduction: How to recreate the corrupt baseline in AEM
instructions: The baseline script was able to identify corrupt baselines and add the prefix corrupt_ to them. These need to be manually recreated. Open the corrupted baseline in AEM. Click the three dots next to the baseline you will recreate and choose Manage labels . Add the label “keyuser_fix”, select “all references” and click Apply . AEM will notify when the label has been added. Click the + button to create a new baseline. Use the following options for baseline creation: Baseline name: give the baseline the temp name WIP_[original baseline name] Baseline option: use file version Select the version based on: label Select labels: keyuser_fix Direct references: use the latest version if no version exists for the selected label Indirect references: use the latest version if no version exists for the selected label Click Apply . AEM will notify when the baseline is created. Open the WIP_ baseline for editing. Make sure the column “label” is visible. Enable filters in the excel file by selecting the top row and clicking Filter in the Data tab. Filter the Label column with all labels that do not have “keyuser_fix”. You know have a filtered list of assets that you need to modify in the baseline. Go to the baseline in AEM. Find the assets without the label keyuser_fix. The label cannot be used in the search so use the list in excel to find the files. Choose the correct version for each of these assets based on the following logic: Check the release label date for the ditamap. Choose the date that is the closest released date before or on the date of release for the map. For example, if the ditamap has label “Released 2023-08-30”, the topic/image release date should have the same or closest earlier. When you have finished selecting the versions, save the baseline. Click the three dots next to the baseline you have recreated and choose Manage labels . Remove the “keyuser_fix” label. Click the three dots next to the baseline you have recreated and choose Rename . Remove WIP_ and _1 from the baseline name and save.

short_description: SPD - How to fix the Spend Actual for a Project
instructions: Step 1: Connect to the SPD production database. Step 2: Run the below query by passing the respective values. i.e. the errored data Unit, Commodity, Category, Material, Year-1 (If user is mentioning for 2024 then year will be passed as 2023), Quarter will be for each and every Quarter. select spend_pc,* from Cdty_KONE_reportMCCImpacts where unit='usk' and commodity='gasoline' and category='car fleet' and material='generic fuel' and year='2024' and quarter='Q1' select spendpc,* from Cdty_KONE_updateSpendToImpacts3 where unit='usk' and commodity='gasoline' and category='car fleet' and material='generic fuel' and year='2024' and forecast=1 and quarter='Q1' Step 3: Compare the Spend_PC and SpendPC from both the resultset. If the values are different then update 'spend_pc' of 'Cdty_KONE_reportMCCImpacts' table with the 'spendpc' of 'Cdty_KONE_updateSpendToImpacts3'.

short_description: KONE Corporate Linux -Microsoft Intune Enrollment (or) Login Failure for Non-Compliant Devices
introduction: KONE Corporate Linux -Microsoft Intune Enrollment (or) Login Failure for Non-Compliant Devices
instructions: KONE Corporate Linux -Microsoft Intune Enrollment (or) Login Failure for Non-Compliant Devices 1.0 Introduction Issue: Corporate Linux Ubuntu- Microsoft Intune Enrollment/Login Failure for Non-Compliant Devices For Non-compliant Linux Ubuntu devices, the users will encounter login failure and authentication difficulties on the Microsoft Intune Application, as shown below. Instructions : Prerequisites: 1). The End User has to raise a incident ticket with the KONE Linux Support team to remove any existing Linux device entries from the Intune and Azure portals. 2). Intune Linux Admin task: Login to Intune portal, verify that the device has been removed from the Intune and Azure portals, and delete any existing Linux device entries . 2.0 Resolution steps for Linux Intune Enrollment (or)Login issue Open the terminal window on Linux Ubuntu device and run the below resolution steps. Step 1: Stop Identity Service sudo systemctl stop microsoft-identity-device-broker y systemctl --user stop microsoft-identity-broker Step 2: Clean up state sudo systemctl clean --what=configuration --what=runtime --what=state microsoft-identity-device-broker systemctl --user clean --what=state --what=configuration --what=runtime microsoft-identity-broker rm -r ~/.config/intune Step 3: Uninstall the Intune package sudo apt remove intune-portal Step 4: Uninstall any versions of Edge that are installed sudo apt remove microsoft-edge-dev Step 5: Optional, but this can potentially free space by some larger dependencies that the auth broker require sudo apt autoremove Step 6: remove secrets stored sudo apt install libsecret-tools -y secret-tool search --all env 60a144fbac31dfcf32034c112a615303b0e55ecad3a7aa61b7982557838908dc secret-tool clear env 60a144fbac31dfcf32034c112a615303b0e55ecad3a7aa61b7982557838908dc secret-tool search --all name LinuxBrokerRegularUserSecretKey –unlock secret-tool search --all name LinuxBrokerSystemUserSecretKey –unlock secret-tool clear name LinuxBrokerRegularUserSecretKey secret-tool clear name LinuxBrokerSystemUserSecretKey Step 7: Please reboot the device. 3.0 Install Microsoft Intune app for Ubuntu Desktop Install Microsoft Intune application ( Enroll a Linux device in Intune | Microsoft Learn ) Run the following commands in a command line to manually install the Microsoft Intune app and its dependencies on your device. 1. Install Curl. sudo apt install curl gpg 2. Install the Microsoft package signing key For Ubuntu 22.04: curl https://packages.microsoft.com/keys/microsoft.asc | gpg --dearmor > microsoft.gpg sudo install -o root -g root -m 644 microsoft.gpg /usr/share/keyrings/ sudo sh -c 'echo "deb [arch=amd64 signed-by=/usr/share/keyrings/microsoft.gpg] https://packages.microsoft.com/ubuntu/22.04/prod jammy main" > /etc/apt/sources.list.d/microsoft-ubuntu-jammy-prod.list' sudo rm microsoft.gpg 3. Install the Intune app. sudo apt update sudo apt install intune-portal 4. Reboot your device. 4.0 Sign-in to Microsoft Intune application & Enroll the Device. Open Microsoft Intune application and sign in and ensure device status is compliant. Follow these steps to sign-in a Linux device on your organization's network. Open the Microsoft Intune app & sign in with your work or school account. Review the pre-enrollment screens. Then select Next to begin enrollment. Wait a few minutes while the Intune app enrolls your device. The device will require a few minutes to sync with KONE compliance Polices and become device compliant. Click refresh to sync the device If device is still non-Compliant contact KONE Linux Support Team “ kone.Linuxsupport@kone.com” **********************************************DONE*********************************************************

Login to KEO server and open the log file using the below path and check for the error. D:\TMA\_LOGS\PROD\EasyOrder\20241008.log Check whether Supplier mapped for the materials for Belgium unit is mapped for any other unit in MAT.ProductDetails table. [Supplier] values ( 'GSS' , 'FSL_023' , '0' , '0' ), ( 'Hilti' , '70038993' , '0' , '0' ) Update the other units using the below query. update [MAT] . [ProductDetails] set FK_Supplier = '4072' where PK_ProductDetails in ( '308179' , '308180' , '308181' , '308182' ) Try importing the material catalogue and import will be successful.

short_description: AEM - Guide for resolving common issues
introduction: How to troubleshoot the issues that arises in AEM?
instructions: This document addresses common issues in AEM/TIP. Some of the common issues are listed below: 1. PDF issues a) PDF generated with error The error 'file:/mnt/crx/author/crx-quickstart/profiles/ditaot--c71918cf-a8a4-9609-0b2e-cf6f8bf83512/DITA-OT/temp/temp20240521084215397/d35faf9df961a797d19a37a863d63bfc7bd87b1f.dita' does not exist or cannot be loaded may occur while generating PDF. This error might happen if there are improper header tags in the reltable. It requires validation within the XML editor. Solution Identify the reltable with the ID from the error log. Open the source view of the reltable with the error. Check for improper header tags, delete them, save the asset, and generate the PDF output. b) Table column issue The error may occur when table columns are not aligned properly. Solution Some column values were in "mm". c) PDF not generated (Red output) The error Expected scheme-specific part at index 5: x-wc may occur while generating PDF. This error might happen if there are any Windchill references available in the ditamap. Solution Identify the Windchill reference used in the topic. Remove the internal reference, save the asset, and generate the PDF output. Note: If any windchill reference still exists, we have a solution to fix it. d) PDF not generated (Red output) The error Index 1 out of bounds for length 1 may occur while generating the PDF. This error might be caused by a simple table issue. Solution The simple table has a header row. Remove the header row from the simple table and generate the PDF output. 2.TIP issues a) Topics Missing in TIP Some topics may be missing when published to TIP. This could be due to topics containing <shortdesc> and other old Windchill elements in their meta information. Solution: Remove the Windchill meta information and republish to TIP. This will make the missing topics visible. b) Image not visible in TIP output The error message in TIP will be svg invalid "dl10163003issuel1forrelease/1d7ea24e-db89-4a24-8aeb-7fadd4564388.svg": Start tag expected, '<' not found, line 1, column 1 (1d7ea24e-db89-4a24-8aeb-7fadd4564388.svg, line 1) . Solution: Republish the ditamap to TIP, which will make the missing images visible. c) Publishing to External Subcontractors TIP does not work We cannot see the published document in EXT-TIP. This issue is related to the filter. Solution: Log in through SSO and select the filter as "Last update" instead of "Relevance" so we can find the recently published document. d) TIP publication error Some topics may not have been published in TIP. This issue might be due to the column width value being set in px in the table. Solution: Remove the px value, replace it with *, and republish to TIP. Image Oversize The image inside the table is oversized for the column. Solution: Fix this by removing the height and width attributes of the image in the source view.

short_description: How To Upgrade from Windows 10 to Windows 11 OS Version
introduction: This Knowledge Base Article (KBA) provides a step-by-step guide for users for the mandatory upgrade from Windows 10 to Windows 11.
instructions: IT - How To Upgrade from Windows 10 to Windows 11 OS Version Introduction This Knowledge Base Article (KBA) provides a step-by-step guide for users for the mandatory upgrade from Windows 10 to Windows 11. Windows 11 has improved performance, enhanced security features and a smoother user experience. For experienced users, the advanced features offer exciting new possibilities. For those who are less experienced, the operating system is designed to be user-friendly, with readily available support to assist you. Advanced security features. Advanced interface and refreshed Start Menu. Customizable Widget board. Redesigned Settings App. Practical Snap Layouts and Snap Groups. Personalized Virtual Desktops. Productivity-Boosting Focus Sessions. Optimized performance. The attached guide outlines the process to ensure a smooth transition, covering prerequisites, installation steps, and post-installation checks. Instructions Please download the attached user guideline to get step-by-step instructions. Note : Windows 11 Operating System upgrade depends on the device hardware compatibility and that the KONE environment PC refresh lifecycle has been followed. Only eligible users receive an email notification when their device is ready to be updated to Windows 11. The upgrade process will take 1-2 hours. It’s important to save all work and start the upgrade process when prompted. The process may be deferred three times, but after the deadline, the device will automatically start the upgrade process. The deferral messages will be prompted every eight hours. The overall process may take 1 to 3 hours, depending on system configuration and performance, and it is expected that the PC will reboot during this time. The size of the download is approximately 10 GB, so it is important to maintain stable connectivity to complete the download. This Windows upgrade requires a minimum of 40GB of space in the C drive, so verifying the available space before beginning the activity is crucial. Open File Explorer and click on This PC to view the space available under OS Disk (C:). If the computer will be turned off during the update, it will interrupt the upgrade process once it has started, as this can lead to errors and potentially damage the system. If you have any questions or need assistance, our Device Management team at kone.sccmadmin@KONE.com for smooth upgrade. Attachments Win 10 to 11 – Step-by-step guide

short_description: Onboard application to MyAccess
introduction: This quick guide gives you application onboarding information, what you should choose and what are the provisioning methods.
instructions: KBA Application onboarding Purpose Application onboarding purpose is to centralize Applications Authorizations to MyAccess and Role based accesses can be designed per application requirements. Application On-boarding refers to having all the Roles (access levels) in MyAccess such that they are requested or dynamically assigned to users from MyAccess, and access is granted automatically or manually by respective team. If you have any issue with filling the application onboarding request in ServiceNow, please contact AskItyou. Note that AD based applications can are now onboarded automatically to MyAccess-which means in practise you can just create roles in MyAccess for your application. Benefit After application is onboarded to MyAccess, users can request access’s (roles) in MyAccess. Role request is then forwarded to named approver(s) which also can be same as owners of the role (pls see page 3) Role owners can create roles and schedule deletion of roles in MyAccess. CreateRoles Role Owners can run Role Reports, Role Details Report or Role Membership reports. Role Owner or group of owners is defined for each role. Owner details of a certain role can be seen by opening MyAccess and navigating from the top left corner to Manage Roles > View Role Details. That view is open for all users Role Owner, or application owner can conduct access reviews of the access that other users have, to verify whether it is correct and appropriate and makes any necessary corrections to revoke inappropriate access. All applications that are onboarded to MyAccess have automatically ApplicationName_All_Users which is email enabled so application owners are able to utilize that i.e email sending. This role is non-requestable and not visible to users. What are the provision methods? (choose only one method) AD group-based provisioning (granting of access) Automated provisioning done by MyAccess for applications whose access is granted through AD groups Automatic de-provisioning of access is done by MyAccess when user is leaving company or not entitled for a certain role. How does it work: AD access flow -- User will get to MyAccess, search for role name and raise a request. The request will flow to the approver level’s and once it’s get approved by the approvers Once there request is approver the user will be added to the mapped AD group and they will have the access to application(as that AD group will be further integrated with application) Ticket-based provisioning How does it work: Ticket -- Users will get to MyAccess, search for role name and raise a request. The request will flow to the approver level’s and once it’s get approved by the approvers. Once MyAccess request gets approved, then MyAccess will generate a ticket in the provided queue by the application team MyAccess checks the service request till it gets resolved, then only it marks MyAccess request as completed and notify the requester The requester will get access based on the ticket Manual Provisioning (Email based) How does it work: Email -- Users will get to MyAccess, search for role name and raise a request. The request will flow to the approver level’s and once it’s get approved by the approvers. Mail will be triggered to the provisioner’s with user details, and User will also get mail that his/her request got approver with the role completion text. Questions to ask: -Application Name -Application Description -URL(if any) -Does any person get access to this application? (It can be for end users, support team, developers, admin, key users, supervisors, or anyone else) -Who are the users who will access this application - Kone employees /Contractors or Contingent Workers/ Supplier Distributor/ External vendors who do not -have KONE ids. -Are there one or more levels of access for this applications (example: developer, admin, user, approver etc.) -How access is currently granted to any user for this application? -Who grants access to the application? -How and when is access from this application revoked? -Does the application need Login? -If access is given, is it on user's regular id (example: JWiley02) or on admin accounts -Does it need any installation for users to be able to use the application? -Is access granting and installation done together? If yes, how? HOW TO GET STARTED Always make sure your application is registered to ITSM-->CMDB. After that, you can go fill-out the ITSM request form and attach the excel which is mandatory before submitting the request. Submit your request and in case support have any questions they will contact you. Note that currently you are not able to create email enabled groups via MyAccess. Request approvals-what are the approval flow options? Approval Type Approval Flow No approval needed Requested Role will be provisioned without any approval. Manager An approval request is sent to the Manager of the person to whom the role is requested (requestee). Role will be assigned after Manager’s approval is received Named Approvers Approval request is sent to the approvers listed under “First Level Approval” Role will be assigned as soon as one of the approvers has approved Manager, Named Approvers Approval request is sent to Manager and after Manger’s approval to the approvers listed under “First Level Approval”. Role will be assigned as soon as Manager and one of the “First Level approvers” has approved. Named Approvers, Named Approvers Approval request is sent to the approvers listed under “First Level Approval”. As soon as some of the “First Level Approvers” has approved, request will be sent to the “Second Level Approvers” Role will be assigned as soon as one of the First level approvers and one of the Second level approvers have approved. Manager, Named Approvers, Named Approvers Approval request is sent first to Manager, after Manager’s approval to the First Level Approvers. As soon as some of the First Level Approvers has approved, request will be sent to the Second Level Approvers. Role will be assigned as soon as, Manager and one of the First and one of the Second level approvers have approved.

short_description: How to request access from "KONE Safety Solution (KSS), Intelex - Authorizations" Catalog
introduction: This KBA Article helps end users on how to request access for different request types in Kone Safety Solution, Intelex application. 1. Location Roles & Security Groups Access 2. Employee Location Change 3. Add/Remove Employee in Country Supervisor List 4. Manage Incidents, Near Misses & Inspections
instructions: KONE Safety Solution (KSS), Intelex – Authorizations Catalog Instructions: From Employee Center page, in the search field, search for “KONE Safety Solution” by typing any keyword like KSS or Intelex or Kone Safety Solution, and click on the option “KONE Safety Solution (KSS), Intelex - Authorizations” as shown in below screen shot. From this page, you can request access for below request types & select whether the request is for Self or other. Location roles will allow you to receive email notifications for Analyzing, Investigating, Review, Approve & Close Incidents & Near Misses. You can review the location role descriptions in the URL http://tinyurl.com/yauwuz52 and then request for location role from 1 st option “Location Roles & Security Groups Access”. Choose the request type and whether it is New, Update or Remove roles from below Choose the approver from below Approver column (Safety Manager or key user) and then select which role you want to be assigned to your profile, also for receiving email notification when a record a Incidents or Near miss is reported and due for your action. If you don’t want any location role and need only security group, please click on “No Location Role Needed check box”. Choose one security group from the drop-down list and fill the reason for access request and click on Order Now. If you don’t want security group and only need location roles, please check the box for No Security group needed and select location roles. Employee Location Change Request This request option is to request for location change in Intelex, if you have moved to a different location. Provide Location Name as per your current work location and business justification which are mandatory information required from requester and click on “Order Now” button. Add/Remove Employee in Country Supervisor List: For requesting to add any employee to the Country Supervisor List, please click on the request type “Add/Remove Employee in Country Supervisor List” and select whether the request is for Adding or Removing employee from supervisor list. Once you click on Add or Remove option, provide the country name from which the employee need to be added or removed. NOTE : Once you attach the document, click on “Order Now” button to submit the request. Manage Incidents, Near Misses & Inspections: For requesting to Edit, Delete or Re-Open any Incidents, Near Miss or Inspection, click on “Manage Incidents, Near Misses & Inspections” Click on Request type to select whether it is Modify or Delete or Re-Open record. If you are requesting to Modify any record, select record type whether it is Incident or Near Miss or Inspection and fill the Record Number field, Attach Approval and then provide the details of which field you want to change and what information you want to update in “Modify Record Details” field. Then click on “Order Now” button to submit the request. If you are requesting for multiple records modification, select “Yes” under Multiple record field and upload the list of record IDs and provide the reason for multiple records modification under Additional Details field. If you are requesting to Delete any record, select record type whether it is Incident or Near Miss or Inspection and fill the Record Number field, Attach Approval. Then click on “Order Now” button to submit the request. If you are requesting for multiple records modification, select “Yes” under Multiple record field and upload the list of record IDs and provide the reason for multiple records removal under Additional Details field. If you are requesting to Re-Open any record, select record type whether it is Incident or Near Miss or Inspection and fill the Record Number field, Attach Approval. Then click on “Order Now” button to submit the request. If you are requesting for multiple records re-open, select “Yes” under Multiple record field and upload the list of record IDs and provide the reason for multiple records re-open under Additional Details field. NOTE : The record will be re-opened and kept in initial workflow stage only like “Analysis”. No record will be kept under Investigation, Review or Approval stage.

short_description: ServiceNow VIP Users Request Automation
introduction: This process is fully automated, simplifying the management of VIP users in ServiceNow. The form is accessible only to users with the roles IT Managers and IT Service Delivery Managers . This is an automated process. Once a request is submitted, the user will be added or removed from the VIP user list. No further tracking is required.
instructions: 1. Accessing the VIP Users Catalog Open the ServiceNow VIP Users catalog. 2. Adding a User to the VIP List 2.1 Select Add User under the Action field. 2.2 In the Requested For field, search for and select the user to be marked as VIP. Note : The list will display only users who are not currently marked as VIP. 2.3 Once the user is selected, they will automatically be added to the VIP list. This process is automated. 2.4 After submission, the request will automatically move to the Completed state, and the user will be marked as a VIP. 2.5 The requestor will receive a feedback notification once the request is completed. 3. Note : The list will display only users who are currently marked as VIP. 3.3 Once the user is selected, they will automatically be removed from the VIP list. This process is automated. 3.4 After submission, the request will move to the Completed state, and the user will no longer be marked as a VIP. 3.5 The requestor will receive a feedback notification once the request is completed.

short_description: CVMS troubleshooting guide
introduction: The Application Security Service is offering free scanning services to improve the security of your applications. This troubleshooting document will help you in case you are experiencing any of the issues related to these area's: SAST: Coverity IaC: Checkov SCA: Black Duck 3rd party binary files are analyzed with Black Duck binary analysis Orca cloud security If the trouble persist, please open an incident: Correct assignment group is: Kone.AppSec.AppSecTools
instructions: 1. Why unable to see project in Coverity? There is no Coverity scan performed for this project (as no snapshot is present in Coverity for this project). Once you scan project and findings are pushed to Coverity, it will be visible in Coverity. 2. Why are findings not reflecting in CVMS? Check if the latest scan is run using master/main branch. Other branches results will not be pushed in CVMS Check for severity of the findings – only High and critical severity findings will be reflected in the CVMS and not medium, low severity Check if connectors for Blackduck, Coverity, etc are configured corrector or not Check for project repository name should be same as onboarded project name. Check for branch in security tool and CVMS configured is same Check if your cloud account IDs (not names) are configured in Settings - Project Metadata – Cloud Account ID. You can add multiple IDs and mix AWS, Azure and Alibaba IDs. If your workload runs in an IT shared account, it must also have the old CMDB (Correlation) ID value in the format INST_2021_1_20_1_44_12_1234567. 3. Blackduck scan pipeline failing Getting error code 11 Is it the first time running blackduck for that project if yes then It can be because it uses bom_compare_strict, which expects a project in the blackduck env But as the scan is rapid, it doesn't find it. Ask to run intelligent and after that finishes, try again with rapid mode. 4. Getting connector ID not found error, missing SRM configuration for project , couldn't connect to CVMS Try to delete project from CVMS and onboard it again with correct confirgurations and ask to re-run the pipeline 5. Please provide project onboarding process. Please refer to the following SharePoint links it includes all the information on how to use scanner templates , including onboarding process of the new projects. GitHub Actions integration DevSecOps at KONE Scanner Templates GitHub repo Onboarding Process 6. Not able to see the project in CVMS To get access to the projects, you will need to get access to following access groups through MyAccess portal: (access group will be the group which project is using ) MyAccess URL: https://myaccess.kone.com/identityiq 7. Facing issue in Blackduck report generation. Pipeline error - Licensing issue: 'Number of versions per project' is beyond its licensed limit (Actual value: 11 there's a limit of 10 project versions per project. So, if there are any project versions that don't need anymore, need to delete. (No. of versions means no. of branches) 8. Getting registry error while running Checkov scan pipeline error – ERROR: Job failed: failed to pull image "registry.gitlab.com/konecorporation/r-d/devops/security-templates/containers/checkov:" with specified policies [always]: invalid reference format (manager.go:235:0s) Use latest tags for Checkov -templates/containers/checkov: ... Authenticating with credentials from job payload (GitLab Registry) Pulling docker image… For this can you please use latest tag 9. How to setup Coverity locally as customer do not have CICD pipeline or customer needs to setup locally Need to get steps from developer for this 10. How to see the scanned history in Blackduck? There's no real way to see the scanning history for blackbuck intelligent scans (from the environment). If you want to see previous scans, you would need to fetch the scan artifacts from previous runs of the pipeline, that will work for both rapid and intelligent modes. 11. The default branch of the project is other than main master then that is, ok? The findings are aggregated to CVMS when they are coming from a scan against the default branch (master/main). Any other scan leaves artifacts in the pipeline results and pushes the findings to the backend server. so basically, default branch should be main/master 12. Getting "[FATAL] License authorization failure: MASKED" error while running pipeline Since our coverity_new image doesn’t use the latest tag and instead uses tags like build-latest, buildless-latest, or versioned tags such as build-X.Y.Z and buildless-X.Y.Z, please update the Kubernetes runner configuration to force a fresh pull of these specific images. To do this, configure the imagePullPolicy specifically for coverity_new images to Always. This ensures that the runner pulls the latest version of build-latest or buildless-latest tags as required, without affecting other images in your pipeline. The configuration should look like this: containers: - name: build image: registry.gitlab.com/konecorporation/r-d/devops/security-templates/containers/coverity_new:build-latest imagePullPolicy: Always - name: buildless image: registry.gitlab.com/konecorporation/r-d/devops/security-templates/containers/coverity_new:buildless-latest imagePullPolicy: Always Note: The exact configuration steps may vary depending on your Kubernetes environment. Please work with your DevOps team to apply this setting for coverity_new images without affecting other images in your pipelines. Applying imagePullPolicy: Always specifically to coverity_new images ensures that you’re always using the most recent version of build-latest or buildless-latest tags, without unnecessary pulls for other images. 13. Blackduck pipeline error “No git executable was found.",” "extracted": false, "status": "ATTEMPTED", "statusCode": "ATTEMPTED", "statusReason": "No git executable was found.", "extractedReason": "", You have not set the new variable to true Now if if you want to have an intelligent scan, you need to set BLACKDUCK_INTELLIGENT to True blackduck_intelligent: extends: .blackduck:intelligent stage: Security variables: BLACKDUCK_INTELLIGENT: "True" Note: If you look at the "detect.blackduck.scan.mode": "rapid" line, you see that the scan is running rapid mode. As we had the change very recently, that's how we quickly know what the issue can be. AskIt Correct assignment group: Kone.AppSec.AppSecTools

short_description: How to restore the soft-deleted mailbox and OneDrive
introduction: This KBA is published Only for M365 L2 Support Team on How to restore the soft-deleted mailbox and OneDrive. Process of Restoring a soft-deleted mailbox If any user is seeking an office data from older account to new account after 30 days of disabled state. The procedures in this section explain how to restore the mailbox for a soft-deleted user. For example, if the mailbox has been deleted after 30 days from the date of disable. The mailbox will be available in deleted users (soft-deleted) Connect to Exchange Online PowerShell Run the following cmdlet to identify the soft-deleted mailbox that you want to restore. PowerShell Script for ExchangeGUID of Soft-Deleted Mailbox Get-Mailbox -SoftDeletedMailbox -Identity <NameOrAliasOf SoftDeletedMailbox > | Format-List ExchangeGuid For the soft-deleted mailbox that you want to restore, note its GUID value (you use the value in Step 4). Use a target mailbox for the restoring soft-deleted mailbox, Run the following command to get the GUID value of the target mailbox that you'll need in the next step. PowerShell Script for ExchangeGUID of Target Mailbox Get-Mailbox -Identity <NameOrAliasOfNewTargetMailbox> | Format-List ExchangeGuid Replace <SoftDeletedMailbox ExchangeGuid > with the GUID value from Step 2, and <NewTargetMailbox ExchangeGuid > with the GUID value from Step 3, and run the following cmdlet to restore the mailbox: PowerShell Script for Mailbox Restore New-MailboxRestoreRequest -SourceMailbox <SoftDeletedMailbox ExchangeGuid > -TargetMailbox <NewTargetMailbox ExchangeGuid > -AllowLegacyDN To check the status of the restore request, Run the following command PowerShell Script to check the status of the restore request Get-MailboxRestoreRequest Note: Use only the ExchangeGuid to map the data from old account to new account. If archive data needs to be mapped to an existing mailbox, first ensure that the target mailbox has the required license for an archive mailbox. Additionally, verify that the Online Archive is enabled. If it is not already enabled, please enable it on-premises. PowerShell Script to Enable archive mailbox Enable-RemoteMailbox -Identity <user mailbox> -Archive Run the following cmdlet to find the ArchiveGuid of Soft-Deleted Mailbox PowerShell Script for ArchiveGuid of Soft-Deleted Mailbox Get-Mailbox -SoftDeletedMailbox -Identity <NameOrAliasOf SoftDeletedMailbox > | Format-List ArchiveGuid Use a target mailbox ArchiveGuid for the restoring soft-deleted mailbox Archive Data, Run the following command to get the ArchiveGUID value of the target mailbox that you'll need in the next step. PowerShell Script for ArchiveGUID of Target Mailbox Get-Mailbox -Identity <NameOrAliasOfNewTargetMailbox> | Format-List ArchiveGuid Replace <SoftDeletedMailbox ArchiveGuid > with the ArchiveGUID value from Step 6, and <NewTargetMailbox ExchangeGuid > with the ArchiveGUID value from Step 7, and run the following cmdlet to restore Archive data of the mailbox: PowerShell Script for Archive Mailbox Restore New-MailboxRestoreRequest -SourceMailbox <SoftDeletedMailbox ArchiveGuid > -SourceIsArchive -TargetMailbox <NewTargetMailbox ArchiveGuid > -TargetIsArchive -AllowLegacyDNMismatch Process of Restoring a deleted OneDrive If the user was deleted within 30 days, you can restore the user via PowerShell to restore the OneDrive. The procedures in this section explain how to restore the OneDrive . Connect to SharePoint as a SharePoint Administrator Connect-SPOService -Url https://kone-admin.sharepoint.com Determine if the OneDrive is available for restore. If you know the URL of the OneDrive, run the following command: PowerShell Script for Restore OneDrive if you know the URL Get-SPODeletedSite -Identity <URL> A user's OneDrive URL is based on their username. For example, https://contoso-my.sharepoint.com/personal/user1_contoso_com. You can find their username on the Active users (or Deleted users) page in the Microsoft 365 admin center. If you don't know the URL of the deleted OneDrive, run the following command: PowerShell Script for Restore OneDrive if you don't know the URL Get-SPODeletedSite -IncludeOnlyPersonalSite | FT url If the OneDrive appears in the results, it can be restored. Restore the OneDrive to an active state: PowerShell Script to Restore the OneDrive Restore-SPODeletedSite -Identi<URL>ty <URL> Assign an administrator to the OneDrive to access the needed data: PowerShell Script to delegate the OneDrive access to another account Set-SPOUser -Site <URL> -LoginName <UPNofDesiredAdmin> -IsSiteCollectionAdmin $True If the URL is not available in deleted list then check with below script and delegate the access to OneDrive PowerShell Script to check the SPO site Get-SPOSite -identity <URL> PowerShell Script to delegate the OneDrive access to another account Set-SPOUser -Site <URL> -LoginName <UPNofDesiredAdmin> -IsSiteCollectionAdmin $True After 15 days of delegation remove the access to OneDrive Account PowerShell Script to remove the OneDrive access to another account Remove-SPOUser -Site <URL> -LoginName <UPNofDesiredAdmin> Reference Microsoft Articles: Article for Restoring a soft-deleted mailbox : https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/exchange/recipients-in-exchange-online/delete-or-restore-mailboxes Article for Restoring a deleted OneDrive : https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/sharepoint/restore-deleted-onedrive Note: Please follow all the above steps mentioned to restore a soft-deleted mailbox and deleted OneDrive when the account is available in deleted users (soft-deleted)

short_description: How to Onboard a new country in MAP3 application.
introduction: Onboarding a New Country in MAP Overview: This document outlines the process for onboarding a new country in the MAP (Maintenance and Acquisition Planning) application. The access provider needs to have the necessary permissions to modify user access within the MAP application. Prerequisites: Before starting the onboarding process, ensure the following prerequisites are met: Admin Access : The Access provider must have admin access to the MAP application. Request Source : The request to onboard a new country will come from MAP Business or a key user for that country. SAP Files : The Access provider will receive 12 files from SAP, with filenames in the format “country_countrycode” .
instructions: Process for Onboarding a New Country: Step 1: Access the MAP Server The access provider must have access to either the MAP41 (PROD) or MAP31 (QA) server in PAM. The request to onboard a new country will be received via email from the client or Incident, Service Request ticket in SNOW. Step 2: Gather Information Obtain the necessary details for adding the new country: Country Name Country Code (as configured in SAP) Step 3: Database Access Ensure the access provider has permission to access the MAP database to add the new country. Step 4: Enable the Country in SAP Confirm that the country is enabled in SAP to ensure the system sends the required SAP files after onboarding. The following URL should be accessible via the internet: https://pam.konenet.com/privx/ Inserting Country Data in the MAP Database: Step 5: Update the Hierarchy Table Log in to SQL Management Studio on the MAP41 or MAP31 server. Insert the new country details into the Hierarchy table . The following fields must be populated: HierarchyID : (Unique ID for the new country) ParentID : 4012 SourceKey : Company code EntryTypeID : 1 Description : Country Name (e.g., “Belgium” or “India”) This step ensures that the new country is available in the MAP database. Step 6: SAP-MAP Integration Once the new country data is inserted into the database, the SAP-MAP integration job will create a folder in the SAPFiles directory with the name of the new country (e.g., 220, 221, BEL, KDL) on the server. Step 7: Finalizing the Onboarding After completing the steps above, the system will process the new country’s files data received from SAP. The integration job will pick up the files, process them, and ensure the new country is available in the MAP application. Conclusion: After successfully completing all the above steps, the new country will be onboarded and visible in the MAP application for further use. Note - This guide ensures that you follow the correct steps for adding a new country, from database updates to SAP integration, ensuring smooth processing within the MAP application..

short_description: How to Enable DMP Integration for a Country in MAP3.
introduction: How to Enable DMP Integration for a Country in MAP Overview This document outlines the process for enabling DMP (Dynamic maintenance planning) integration for a country in the MAP (Maintenance and Acquisition Planning) application. The access provider must have the necessary permissions to configure integration settings within the MAP application. Prerequisites Before proceeding with enabling the DMP integration, ensure the following prerequisites are met: Admin Access : The access provider must have admin access to the MAP application and the MAP servers ( MAP31 and MAP41 ). Request Source : The request must originate from the SAP Product Owner , MAP Business , or a Key User . Country Onboarding : The company to be DMP-enabled must already be onboarded in the MAP application. Required Details : All necessary details (Country, Country Code, Environment [QA or PROD]) must be provided..
instructions: Step-by-Step Process for Enabling Integration Step 1: Insert Country Code into Database Navigate to the database in the specified environment (either QA or PROD ). Insert the country's Country Code into the DmpEnabledCountries table. This action marks the country as eligible for DMP integration. Step 2: Create Folder for Country in dmp_files Directory Create a new folder named after the country (using the country’s full name) inside the dmp_files directory. Ensure the folder structure adheres to the naming convention and is placed correctly in the directory. Step 3: Update the CompanyCountry.json File Locate the CompanyCountry.json file in the SAP_MAP_importer folder. Insert the country’s details into the JSON file to ensure proper integration with the MAP application. Conclusion By following the above steps, the DMP integration for the specified country will be enabled. Once the DMP file is received, they will be processed within the dmp_files folder. Note : All DMP-enabled countries require a total of 13 files : 12 SAP files 1 DMP file This guide ensures that you follow the correct steps to enable DMP integration for a country, ensuring smooth processing of DMP files within the MAP application.

short_description: How to Provide Key User Access in MAP3
introduction: KBA: How to Provide Key User Access in MAP Overview This document outlines the process for providing Key User ( Superuser ) access to the MAP (Maintenance and Acquisition Planning) application. The access provider must have the necessary permissions to modify user roles within the application. Prerequisites Before proceeding with granting key user access, ensure the following prerequisites are met: Admin Access : The access provider must have admin access to the MAP application. Access Request : Ensure the user has raised an access request via ServiceNow . If the user has not raised the request, provide them with the following link for guidance: ServiceNow Access Request .
instructions: Step-by-Step Process for Providing Access Step 1: Receive the Access Request Service Request or ASKIT Incident ticket : Receive the access request through either a Service Request or an ASKIT Incident ticket. If received via an Incident ticket , cancel it and open a new Service Request ticket on behalf of the user, or share the link above so the user can create the request themselves. Step 2: Gather Required Details for Access- Collect the following information to proceed with access provisioning: Country Branch/Planner group Environment (QA/PROD) Role (Read, Analyze, or Key User) Step 3: Verify Missing Information If any user details are missing, request the information from the user before proceeding. Step 4: Obtain Approval from MAP Business Obtain formal approval for granting access from the MAP Business: Email Alessandro Mancuso (alessandro.mancuso@kone.com) and keep Pallavi Tiwari (pallavi.tiwari@kone.com) and the AMS team (raju.bitode@kone.com, rathin.roy@kone.com) in CC . Step 5: Grant Access Upon Approval Once approval is obtained, provide the necessary access to the user according to the requested role. Additional Notes If the Approver is Unavailable : If the approver is unavailable or out of office (OOO), await their response. Keep the service ticket in a pending state and document the exception reason if the ticket ages. Example of an Access-related Ticket : RITM00087158 (example of an access request ticket). By following the above steps, the access provider can successfully grant key user access in MAP, ensuring that the user has the necessary permissions to perform their tasks.

short_description: AskIT - How to handle access request/ install ESiteSurvey for KONE domain user
introduction: How to handle access request/ install ESiteSurvey for KONE domain user.
instructions: This article contains the procedure to install or access request for ESiteSurvey Application in user's machine. ESiteSurvey is a KONE software to perform technical calculations for KONE Elevators. To use ESiteSurvey, the user should request for ESiteSurvey role via MyAccess. This is only required for users who are requesting ESiteSurvey installation for the very first time. User can raise the request in KONE MyAccess - Home by searching ESiteSurvey. ESiteSurvey has 4 roles: ESiteSurvey: R&D User ESiteSurvey: Business User ESiteSurvey: Site Engineer Shared Drive: SEC_FF_ESiteSurvey_RW Note: User can raise any one of the role based on their requirement. After approval by their Line Manager, the AskIT ticket will be created. Once ticket is created, ticket is landed in EngCalc queue (KONE.SC.ENG.CALC) Create a task for L3 queue to provide database access to the user Once task is completed by L3 team, then assign it back to AskIT. Also add a comment to AskIT stating that "Once Pre-Req is pushed assign the ticket back to EngCalc queue (KONE.SC.ENG.CALC). AskIT should add the device to Collection. "collection name: KONE_ESiteSurvey_PreReq_EN_1.24.2.1" and then help user to install the Package from Software Center manually. After the Pre-Req is installed successfully, the desktop shortcut is created for ESiteSurvey application. Click on the Desktop shortcut and install the application by clicking launch tool. The installation process need to be done only by the user. This can be achieved in the below manner. 1. Reach out to the user via Teams, and connect to the user’s machine. 2. Ensure that the machine is connected to KONE network either directly or via KONE VPN. 3. Open ESiteSurvey by typing ESiteSurvey in the Search Box, and clicking on ESiteSurvey. Install ESiteSurvey Pre-Req - PC Refresh: Check if Pre-Req is show in Software Center. If not, check with SCCM team if User’s machine ID is added as Primary machine, User connected to KONE Network or VPN, Whether user have restarted the machine in last 24 hours. Once Pre-Req is shown in Software center, then help user to install the Package from Software Center manually. After the Pre-Req is installed successfully, the desktop shortcut is created for ESiteSurvey application. Click on the Desktop shortcut and install the application by clicking launch tool.

short_description: How to Raise Request for access of GSS China Users in KONE Parts
introduction: KBA Document: Incident Resolution and Solution Introduction This KBA document details the new features and changes related to the incident, providing an analysis of the issue and the solution that was implemented. This document is designed to assist support teams and users in understanding how the issue was addressed and resolved. About This KBA Incident Number : INC00271294 Description : Request for access in KCC_016, GSS China web, to enable spare parts order release. User : Rasheen Ibrahimkutty Application Site : parts.kone.com
instructions: Solution Provided Issue Summary : The user requested access to the CDC (China Distribution Center) via the KCC_016 platform to enable the release of spare parts orders. The user already had access to the EDC (European GSS access), which did not affect the request. Business Approval Requirement : Access to the CDC requires business approval. Therefore, the case was reviewed with Staengler Krisztina to obtain the necessary business approval. Business Response : The configuration for access is set at the customer level. While it can be configured, it would apply to all users of KQA(the customer). The business needed confirmation from the FL (Frontline - KONE country branch) to proceed with this configuration for all users. However, granting access solely for the specific user (Rasheen) is not possible because this setting is applied universally to all users at the customer level. After further discussions with the business, the visibility of the CDC was updated accordingly, and the access request was resolved, successfully closing the ticket. Resolution Summary The visibility of the CDC was updated as per the business requirements. The user now has the necessary access to the CDC, and the spare parts order release function is operational. The request was resolved with business approval and the configuration change at the customer level. Conclusion This document outlines the resolution of the access request for the CDC in the KCC_016 system, as well as the necessary business approval process. All involved parties have confirmed the solution, and the access issue has been successfully resolved.

short_description: How to Troubleshoot OneNote Sync issues
introduction: How to Troubleshoot OneNote Sync issues Sign into the Right OneNote Account The OneNote desktop app works with multiple accounts. For example, you can sign into the OneNote desktop app with a free Microsoft account for your personal notebooks and an Microsoft 365 work account for your business-related notes. Here’s how to determine which account is signed into OneNote: Open the OneNote desktop app. Go to the Settings and More menu (the three-dot icon). Select Settings. Select Accounts Turn on the Sync notebooks automatically toggle switch. Turn on the Sync down all files and images toggle switch Close the Options pane Troubleshoot Sync Problems When a Notebook Won’t Sync If a notebook will not sync, determine if the sync problem is with the OneNote desktop app or with the Microsoft server. Open the OneNote desktop app. Right-click the notebook and select Copy Link to Notebook. The link is copied to the Clipboard Open a web browser. Go to the Address bar, paste the link to the notebook, and press Enter. If prompted, sign into your Microsoft, work, or school account. If the Notebook Opens Successfully If the notebook opens successfully in OneNote Online, the problem may be with the OneNote desktop app. Usually closing and reopening the notebook will fix the sync problem. To close and reopen a OneNote notebook: In the OneNote desktop app, right-click the notebook and select Close This Notebook In OneNote Online, select Edit Notebook > Edit in Microsoft OneNote. Wait while the notebook syncs and opens in the OneNote desktop app. If the Notebook Does Not Open If the notebook does not open in OneNote Online, the problem may be with the server. Check the Microsoft Service Status page. If OneNote is good to go, check with your ISP to see if your service is working properly. Fix Sync Issues When a Section of a Notebook Won’t Sync If you’re working with others on a shared notebook and sections of the notebook are not updating, manually sync the notebook. If that doesn’t work, copy the section that won’t sync to a new section. Share and Collaborate in OneNote with 18 Tips and Tricks Manually Sync a Notebook To manually sync a notebook: Right-click the notebook that won’t sync. Select Sync. Select Sync This Notebook. Select the Page sync status icon. If the Section Does Not Sync If the section did not sync, create a new section and copy the pages from the section that does not sync to the new section. Then, press Shift+F9 to manually sync the notebook If the one note don’t sync properly or any issues in signing in with OneNote application , If the one note doesn’t sync properly or any issues in signing, please follow the below steps: Open One note application. Click File -> Info -> View Sync Status (Take a screenshot of below for your reference to re-map the notebooks again). Also ensure the below highlighted option is chosen “Sync automatically whenever there are changes” Now make a right click on their OneNote and choose “Close this Notebook”. Likewise, perform the same step for remaining notes which is mapped in the application as shown below. Once all mapped notebooks are removed and closed. Please click File -> Account -> Sign out from your One note application. Close all the Microsoft application which is opened from your Desktop. Once all Microsoft applications are closed, now open Word/Excel and try to sign in with your KONE email address. Once signed in, please open the OneNote application and check if you have logged in with correct credentials. Now click File -> Open OneDrive -> Search for the previous notebook name which was already mapped. When you choose the Notebook, it will be synced directly and perform the same for the rest of the notebooks which was mapped before. Once all notebooks are mapped all contents will be visible.

short_description: The new "Order Workstation form" in Employee Center portal

short_description: Mass Derived Role Creation & Maintenance
introduction: Description This accelerator solution aims at simplifying and further automating the process of derived role creation and maintenance. Using this tool we can create derived roles, maintain the org field values, and generate the profiles at same time. We can reduce up to 90% of effort and time that we normally spend on standard Profile Generator Transaction. This automation improves productivity by reducing time and effort and quality by avoiding human errors in org value maintenance. This tool also has an additional option to delete roles which can help during role clean-up activity. Automation Requirement Derived role creation and org. value maintenance is frequently performed task in SAP landscape and unique for individual roles. This task is repetitive, time consuming and there is a chance of human errors while manually updating org values. During implementation, redesign projects lots of manual hours are wasted in performing this task
instructions: Input / Selection screen Input screen provides below selection options. • Selection Criteria: Multiple Derived Role Creation or Multiple Roles Org. Value Updates (ADD, DELETE) or Multiple Derived Role Deletion. • CSV File Path: CSV File to be uploaded from local disk. Click on Allow Output screen shows summary of roles created. Click on Allow 2.Org. Values Update. -> Execute it *First make sure that add the master role under all derived roles . • Action: ▪ Download Input file format : To Download template for Org value updating process ▪ Add Org Values from file : Add Multiple Org. Values for Multiple roles from input file. ▪ Delete Org Values from file : Delete specified Org. Values for Multiple roles from input file. ▪ Delete All Org Values : Delete all Org. Values of Multiple Roles from input file. Output screen shows summary of roles Org. Values update. Key features and Benefits • Complete automation of Derived Role creation, deletion, and maintenance. • Need not be transported in production system. • Improves productivity by reducing turnaround time significantly. • Improves quality by reducing human errors

short_description: PAM Tool – Instructions for SAP Security Team
introduction: Where do we store System/Service/RFC User’s credentials for KONE SAP systems?
instructions: PAM (Privileged Access Management ) tool is used for secure storage of System/Service/RFC(communication) User Id Credentials along with many other functions. PrivX PAM access and permission models are based around role-based access controls (RBAC). A role is created and associated with users. Roles can be explicitly granted to specific users by PAM administrators. Alternatively, users can request specific roles using workflow requests. Based on the job role / Team of the PAM user, different roles are assigned. These PAM roles can be requested via Kone MyAccess Portal ( https://myaccess.kone.com/identityiq/home.jsf ). Users' roles determine what targets (be those hosts, applications, networks, and in our case secrets) they can access. Roles to be requested for SAP security Team are as follow: - While adding the new secret ( system/service/communication user’s credentials), you have to define users associated with specific role/s , who will be able to read the secret (Read Permission) and make changes to the existing secret (write Permission). Password must be defined in the JSON scripting format. - link and KBA details provided below to setup access for PAM tool. ========================================================================================================================================================================================================================================== Link: MFA Setup and reset for PAM - Knowledge Article# KB000A5577 https://koneprod.service-now.com/now/nav/ui/classic/params/target/kb_view.do%3Fsysparm_article%3DKB000A5577 There need to be 4 persons who will be owners for the System/Service/Communication User Id from each track or team - 2 should be from KONE side and 2 should be from Vendor side who is providing technical support for that system. From KONE side this is normally Config owners / System Owners etc. The contact details (email ids) of the 4 persons needs to be updated on the share point excel file available named “System IDs and Owners All Platforms” Folder Path – for Navigation without link: · Application Management Unit Documents Security and Compliance General – Cross Platform Repositories System User IDs Repository Note : “New user id creation/ update (password reset) existing user/Sharing the password to other teams” needs to be approved from Kone side owner of the user on email. Once user is created/updated in concerned SAP system, credentials need to be updated in PAM tool for the same. New user ID/secret creation on PAM tool Once new user is created in SAP system, we need to create a new entry called secret in PAM tool. Login to PAM tool and select “Secretes” Tab -> select “Add secrete” button Provide the below inputs: User name Scope: Global Add roles for Read and write access to the secret Credentials Other details like Owner and Purpose Note: If the JSON format for password is not correct, “Save” button will not be enabled and you will see the error to correct the format. JSON script password needs to be maintained in below format: { "data": "******** // SID1-Client1, Owner- XXXXX, Purpose - XXXX", "data2": "******** // SID1-client2, Owner- XXXXX, Purpose - XXXX", "data3": "******** // SID2-Client1, Owner- XXXXX, Purpose - XXXX " } Once Secrete is saved , you can see the overview: Secret can be viewed in below format, Example: To update the existing user, search the user and click on edit to update the password or to add credentials for some other system for same user id :

short_description: Kone parts - Delivery Option Visibility
introduction: KBA Document: Incident Resolution and Solution Introduction This KBA document details the new features and changes related to the incident, providing an analysis of the issue and the solution that was implemented. This document is designed to assist support teams and users in understanding how the issue was addressed and resolved. About This KBA Incident Number : INC00348896 Description : In my KONE parts account, i can see only delivery option to Europe , I want to see the delivery options to other parts example China and order the material to China address. Application Site : parts.kone.com
instructions: Solution Provided Issue Summary : In my KONE parts account, i can see only delivery option to Europe , I want to see the delivery options to other parts example China and order the material to China address. Name of the user nithil.natarajan@kone.com and his colleague’s is aapo.oksman@kone.com User shared screenshots also User saying for him it is showing only EDC Germany but for his colleague it is showing like before So, for user also want like his colleague. Scenarios: We will have mainly 2 scenarios related to this type of incident. Scenario 1: If the user and colleague belonging to same organization, and this user is not making orders. He need see the delivery dates from other offices. The partner number column in user table need to update.so set it same as is his colleague. Scenario 2 : If the users are from different org and, then these will be checked case by case and likely will be kept. If the customer code is correct for the users, then only option is to change the plant visibility configuration for the whole branch. The plant visibility is limited by the organization and customer, and we don't change those without good reason. It’s the organization (customer) level setting, some see al 3 delivery plants and some only one of any. So, by updating plant if the user is not from the same organization or the existing plant visibility need to increase. And we can add the delivery plans to the plant visibility. This needs business approval needed. Resolution Summary The visibility of the delivery plant needed for user, if the user and colleague belong to same organization then updated the Plant column in User table with the data that colleague. The visibility of the delivery plant needed for user, if the user and colleague not belong to same organization then the Plant visibility need to be added with the delivery Plants which need approval from Business. Conclusion This document outlines the resolution of the visibility of delivery plants. All involved parties have confirmed the solution, and the access issue has been successfully resolved.
