KBA for Raising access for PIP (Product Information Portal) in MyAccess
introduction: KBA for Raising access for PIP (Product Information Portal) in MyAccess Description: This KBA is intended for the users to raise access requests for PIP (Product Information Portal) access for Production and QA Environment. Prerequisites: Users: Users should be working in KONE environment as PIP is a web intranet application and uses user’s KONE ID to login. It uses Azure AD login method. VPN is not required to login.
instructions: Steps to Raise Access requests: MyAccess: Users must visit MyAccess portal for raising any PIP related access requests (https://myaccess.kone.com/identityiq/home.jsf) Roles: Access to application and permissions on pages and data are determined in Azure by the AD Groups allowed for the user. The list of AD Groups allowed for a user, received by the PIP Explorer during the user authentication through the Azure AD profile, are used by the application to determine the access and visibility on data, as described in the table below: PIP Application Role Description PIP Explorer <Env> KONE-PIP-VIEWER_QA KONE-PIP-VIEWER_PROD When present the user can access the application and perform searches and see part availability. (Without this role user cannot access PIP even if other roles are present). Eg. KONE-PIP-VIEWER_PROD MyAccess KONE-PIP-VIEWER_OFFERING_GSS When present the user can view materials of GSS Spare Unit. KONE-PIP-VIEWER_OFFERING_PDM When present the user can view materials of PDM Spare Unit. KONE-PIP-VIEWER_PRICE_TP_VIEW When present the user can view the Transfer Price of parts. KONE-PIP-VIEWER_PRICE_KRRP_VIEW When present the user can view the KONE Regional Reference Price (price with discount applied on the Global Reference Price for this region) of parts. KONE-PIP-VIEWER_PRICE_LLP_VIEW When present the user can view the Local List Price (price expressed in the local currency) of parts. KONE-PIP-VIEWER_PRICE_KGRP_VIEW When present the user can view the Global Reference Price (price for end customer) of parts. KONE-PIP-VIEWER_PRICE_PB00_VIEW When present the user can view the Local Purchasing Price (for locally supplied materials) of parts. KONE-PIP-VIEWER_PART_PDM_STRUCTURE When present user can view in part details a button to open in new browser window PDM and view documents linked to material (PDM access is required) KONE-PIP-VIEWER_PR_CONSUMABLE_TECHNICIAN When present user is able to open PIP from KFM or FSM and order consumable catalogue materials and place PR from PIP MyAccess KONE-PIP-VIEWER_PR_CONSUMABLE_SUPERVISOR When present user is able to open PIP and order consumable catalogue materials and select cost center and storage location address from user’s plant and place PR from PIP MyAccess KONE-PIP-VIEWER_ SALESFORCE_LEAD When present user is able to open PIP from KFM or FSM from Sales lead and add items from PIP basket back to the sales in KFM or FSM MyAccess NOTE: <Env> is the application environment (ex. DEV, QA, PROD). Next Steps: After Raising the Access Requests, it would take some time for the access to be Accepted. Requesting access Accesses are requested via MyAccess application. Each role will be approved after initial roll out by one above manager. Currently Role owners are approving. Removing access Accesses can be removed via MyAccess application without approval Contact Support: If the user requires further assistance, please contact your IT support team or raise a support ticket.

short_description: TMS – LS120 Status manual workaround instructions
introduction: TMS – LS120 Status manual workaround instructions
instructions: In TMS choose “KONE Shipper” client (1) Select “Transport list” (2) Select filter “All orders with open / in process / ready for dispatch / on hold” (3) Press “Download the order list” (4) “Transport list download successful” e-mail should follow Open the .zip file Open the TransportList.csv file Once the file is open, select “Developer” tab (1) Press “Macros” (2) Select “TMS_Status_Check” (3) Press “Run” (4) The file should now look like this: The file should now look like this: Add filters, and filter so that only “SOI - Supply Line Italy ESU” & “FRB - SSE Supply Finland (TRB)” are visible. Copy all the sales order numbers (Column C) Go to SAP transaction “ZV_MILEST_SL” Select variant “EXTRA_COSTS” Enter all the sales orders using multiple selection Untick the “Not confirmed” box, make sure the usage is 3X and Execute: Go through each sales order found in the table and find the corresponding outbound delivery in the Excel sheet and put a mark in the F column (this indicator is used later on to filter the final report). Repeat the step for all the rows found in the SAP report. Once completed, go back into the Excel, and filter out “SOI - Supply Line Italy ESU” & “FRB - SSE Supply Finland (TRB)” Copy all the outbound deliveries (Column A) Go to SAP transaction “SE16N” “LIPS” as the Table, Fetch field put “GRKOR” and press enter. De-select all from icon and select only “GRKOR”, “VBELN” & “POSNR” Enter all the outbound delivery numbers from multiple selection. Check the number of entries and adjust the “Maximum no. of hits” accordingly Execute Export the list as “Spreadsheet” and open The Excel file should look like this: Select all the data cells in Column A and convert to numbers (otherwise the Excel formulas won’t work) In the transport list put the following formula into the first visible cell in Column E (assuming the SAP report was saved as “EXPORT.XLS”) =VLOOKUP([@[Transport Order]],[EXPORT.XLSX]Sheet1!$A:$C,3,0) Copy down the formula Sort Column E in A to Z order Repeat the following steps for each unique DG number found in Column E filter, starting with 1. Once filtered, copy all the sales order numbers (Column C) Go to SAP transaction “ZV_MILEST_SL” Select variant “EXTRA_COSTS” Enter the sales order numbers using multiple selection Filter the milestone with corresponding DG number e.g. “3X.D1” and make sure only “Confirmed” is selected. NOTE: Milestones from 10 onwards use different logic, DG10 = 3X.DA, DG11 = 3X.DB, DG12 = 3X.DC etc. Execute . Again go through each sales order found in the table and find the corresponding outbound delivery in the Excel sheet and put a mark in the F column (this indicator is used later on to filter the final report). Once the step is individually repeated for all the DG numbers found in the Column E filter, filter the Column F with the “x” Note the IDOC contains information of the Outbound Delivery number, Sales order number, Status etc. The Status of the delivery number would be LS110 if the milestone 3X is not yet confirmed and the status should change to LS120 if the milestone 3X is confirmed and the IDOC containing this information should be sent to TMS from SAP. LS110 and LS120 are the status of the delivery number.

short_description: How to Clear the Print jobs from the Printer Queue
introduction: This Knowledge Base Article will guide you through the steps to clear print jobs from the printer queue.

short_description: How to raise access request in MyAccess Tool for IoT Provisioning Portal, IoT Device Management Portal, 24/7 Cloudview, KC3View & Site survey/Data collection tool
introduction: How to raise access requests for 24/7 Cloudview, KC3View & Site survey/Data collection tool Tools & Access levels: By having this role, it will provide access to the following applications Provisioning Portal Device management portal 24/7 Cloudview KC3View Site survey/Data collection tool User Management Portal ( for RnD personnel) In the new model of AD based roles, there are 10 roles available for requesting: IOT_READER : Read-only access to equipment based on company code restriction, No available device actions. IOT_ANALYST : Read-only access to equipment based on company code restriction, No available device actions. IOT_TECHNICIAN : Limited access to single equipment with company code restrictions. Available device actions: service need deactivation, deprovisioning. Individual KEN/address-based search function available IOT_TECHNICIAN_REMOTE : All features for single equipment with company code restrictions, with remote actions enabled. IOT_TROUBLESHOOTER : All features with company code restrictions. Similar to the old Operator role. IOT_TROBLESHOOTER_THD : All features with company code restrictions. Similar to the old Operator role with excel report enabled. IOT_CALLOUT_AGENT : New role. IOT_GLOBAL_ANALYST : View access rights to overall equipments no company code restrictions. IOT_GLOBAL_ADMIN : All features with no company code restrictions. IOT_GLOBAL_USER_ADMIN : RnD based role which approves/revokes access rights of other users. Does periodic audits and generates role reports on monthly basis. Authorization matrix is explained here: https://kone.atlassian.net/wiki/x/radUAw

Go to File > Options > General and look for "Enable Microsoft Copilot". Update Office Apps: Open Word or PowerPoint → File → Account → Update Options → Update Now Ensure Office version is Build 18324.20240 or later . Enable the Copilot Button (If hidden) In Word or PowerPoint, go to Options → Customize Ribbon → Ensure Copilot is checked. Repair or Reinstall Office package Note: There is currently much more demand for Copilot licenses than availability. Licenses will be released based on current usage, and those on the waitlist will receive licenses as they become available. The Platform team will use the number of requests to help estimate the demand for additional licenses in the future. In the meantime, the process will continue a first-come, first-served basis. Once the license is applied, the changes will follow accordingly.

short_description: Genesys Cloud CX & SalesForce Settings and Troubleshooting steps for FAQ
introduction: Genesys Cloud CX & SalesForce Settings and Troubleshooting steps for FAQ
instructions: File link : Troubleshoot & FAQ - Genesys.docx Troubleshoot Q guide! Genesys Cloud CX & SalesForce Recommended requirements SalesForce Genesys Cloud CX Test via: https://kone.my.salesforce.com/speedtest.jsp Test via: https://fast.com Octane score 30.000+ >25MB/s download & 5MB/s upload Network latency <150ms Dual-core processor Download speed >3MB/s Windows 10 or 11 >8GB RAM, with 3GB available for SF tabs >8GB RAM Browser: Google chrome Needed settings for Genesys & SalesForce: In Genesys we need to have certain settings set correctly. At minimum you need to allow: Microphone usage. Notifications. Pop-ups & re-directs. Headset access. Sound. You can remove/add these access restrictions via the settings of your browser. In Google Chrome (recommended) its easy via the settings pop-up in front of the URL bar. If not all settings are visible, please reset the permissions and add the permissions again. SalesForce: In SalesForce we must give permission to the same settings. At minimum you need to allow: Microphone usage. Notifications. Pop-ups & re-directs. Headset access. Sound. SalesForce Genesys Connector: The integration we use for Genesys in SalesForce we call the “Genesys connector”. This connector allows us to answer calls via SalesForce. Currently available in CSD/THD/FSM modules. The Genesys connector needs to be added to the agents SalesForce profile. This can be done via a CRM Key User or a ticket via AskIT. Please request to have one of the following to be assigned under “call center” on the agents SF profile depending on their role: Genesys Cloud for Salesforce Lightning (CS) Genesys Cloud for Salesforce Lightning (FSM) Genesys Cloud for Salesforce Lightning (THD) Genesys Connector Settings: Always make sure the small pop-up is open, this is the connection between Genesys Cloud CX and SalesForce. Without this pop-up, you will not be able to receive or answer calls. In the small pop-up, you can set the settings for your microphone and sound. In the Genesys Connector itself, it is recommended to also adjust some settings. Under “notifications” please tick the box “ Show system notification for alerting interactions” . Under “Queue” please tick the box “Remember the last selected queue ”. SalesForce pop-ups: SalesForce will recognize telephone numbers and will, based on the recognition, follow up with some actions. When the number is recognized as a SalesForce contact 2 pop-ups will open in SalesForce. The contact page & the account page linked to that contact. Here you can easily find all the cases, equipments, workorders linked to that contact or account. When a number is recognized but is not a SalesForce contact (manually entered in the case). With a singular case --> the case is opened in a pop-up. With multiple cases --> the search page is opened with the cases showing. When a number is not recognized SalesForce will open a pop-up, asking if you want to add a contact. Alarm pop-ups: Alarm pop-ups are created via Genesys Cloud CX ( http://apps.mypurecloud.de ) Therefore, this should always be opened in the background. To receive pop-ups, a IOT permission/role must be allocated to the user. This can be requested via MyAccess. Please assign the following role to the agents : UK: KoneIoTApplications : Operator - PROD - UK – OKB NL: KoneIoTApplications : Operator - PROD - Netherlands – 237 Troubleshooting FAQ: Please always start with checking the basics and identifying the issue correctly. Where is the issue originating from? Error Origine system Errors in SalesForce (profile, permissions, account) SalesForce Telephone issue Genesys Connector Genesys No pop-ups (one person) Genesys No pop-ups (entire team) KRM Server When creating a ticket, please provide as much information as possible. After creating a ticket, escalated via the AskIT telephone number to give the ticket a higher priority. I do not receive any alarm pop-ups. Please check if you have Genesys Cloud CX open. ( http://apps.mypurecloud.de ) This should always be open in the background , open your wallboard on your screen for example. If the webpage is already open, please check if there is a pop-up blocker active. Note: only open one page, having multiple Genesys pages open will result in multiple pop-ups. My pop-ups open after I already closed my call (slowness in pop-ups). Basically, the pop-ups are created too slow. First thing to do, is clear your internet cache and cookies. Since Genesys, SalesForce and the new pop-ups are all browser related a full internet cache can result in slowness of the browser. If that did not resolve the issue, please check if the same thing happens in another browser. It might be browser related. Does it happen to more agents? Please check the KRM server status. It is possible to reset that server as well via an AskIT ticket. In addition to the above, the agent can manually open the pop-up via the interactions menu in Genesys Cloud CX. I want to open the popup after I already closed my call The agent or supervisors can find the URL after wrapping up the call. For the agents from workspace , they can check their own interactions: then open the interaction > expand the participant data > ctrl+f > search for URL from there the agent can copy the URL and open it in a different tab. The supervisor can do the same for all the calls. My entire browser freezes First check if your laptop is reaching the minimum requirements for Genesys to work. Please do the tests from SalesForce & the speed test (see beginning of the document). If the requirements are not met, hardware needs to be changed. Another solution can be to clear the internet cache and cookies. My call is logged automatically to the wrong case This issue is known and a ticket is already created for this. The Genesys Connector is automatically linking calls to the last case that the agent clicked on. The agent can do 2 things. During the call, go to “interaction log” and unlink the call. (select “none”) If accidently linked anyway, you can find the correct case and link it to that one. SalesForce opens a “new contact” window when a lift alarm comes in. Unfortunately, SalesForce does not recognize the difference between our lift numbers. So yes, if the number is not in SalesForce, it will always open the new contact window. The calls are not being recorded. All inbound customer calls are being recorded in Genesys. Outbound calls are only recorded when the agents call “on behalf of a queue”. When not calling “on behalf of a queue” the calls are not being recorded. Internal calls (genesys to genesys) are also not recorded. Note: Agents can standard call on behalf of a queue. My agent is not able to sign in. First check if you are using the correct login method. Choose “more login options” and enter the organization ID (koneoyj-emea-prod). After that, sign in with your normal KONE credentials (laptop login and password). If that does not work, check the Genesys account from the user. Go to admin -> people -> search user Once in the agents account -> Check under “persons details” to see if the email adress is correct. If not, change it here. Login data (SSO) is linked to the correct KONE credentials. If no agent can be found. You need to request a Genesys Account via AskIT / Orange. My agent is not receiving calls, but is “on queue”. Check the assigned ACD skills & queues. Admin -> People -> search agent -> ACD skills / Queues Check here if all correct skills (and levels) are assigned. Check under queues if the agent is assigned to the correct queues. An agent left KONE, but his account is still there. Create a ticket to AskIT to remove the account from Genesys. A new agent is joining our team, how do I get a new account? In the future this will go via MyAccess. For now, use AskIT to create a ticket. Be specific, the better you supply the data, the less you have to adjust later on. Think abo...

short_description: LEAP - How to troubleshoot Common Microsoft Excel Issues (Basic problems, macro-related issues and other Excel-related errors)
introduction: How to troubleshoot Common Microsoft Excel Issues (Basic problems, macro-related issues and other Excel-related errors )
instructions: This KBA is published Only for Kone Way Users on How to troubleshoot Common Microsoft Excel Issues (Basic problems, macro-related issues and other Excel-related errors ) Troubleshooting Common Microsoft Excel Issues Introduction This guide provides end users with troubleshooting steps for common Microsoft Excel issues, including basic problems, macro-related issues, and other Excel-related errors. 1. Basic Excel Issues & Troubleshooting 1.1 Excel is Slow or Freezing Possible Causes: Large datasets Excessive formatting Add-ins causing conflicts Troubleshooting Steps: Restart Excel and try again. Disable Add-ins: Open Excel → Go to File > Options > Add-ins . Select COM Add-ins and click Go . Uncheck all add-ins and click OK . Restart Excel and enable add-ins one by one to identify the issue. Clear Excess Formatting: Use Ctrl + End to find the last used cell. If excessive formatting is present, go to Home > Editing > Clear > Clear Formats . Check Hardware Acceleration: Go to File > Options > Advanced . Under Display , check Disable hardware graphics acceleration . Restart Excel. 1.2 Excel Not Responding or Crashing on Open Possible Causes: Corrupted workbook Conflicting add-ins Troubleshooting Steps: Open Excel in Safe Mode: Press Win + R , type “excel /safe” and press Enter . Check for Updates: Open Excel → Go to File > Account > Update Options > Update Now . 1.3 Excel File Won’t Open Troubleshooting Steps: Unblock the File: Right-click the file > Properties > Check Unblock under Security. Disable Protected View: Go to File > Options > Trust Center > Trust Center Settings > Protected View . Uncheck Enable Protected View for files originating from the Internet . 2. Macro-Related Issues & Troubleshooting 2.1 Macros Not Running Possible Causes: Macros are disabled File is not in a trusted location Troubleshooting Steps: Enable Macros: Go to File > Options > Trust Center > Trust Center Settings > Macro Settings . Select Enable all macros (Use cautiously). Save File in a Trusted Location: Open Trust Center > Trusted Locations . Add the folder where the Excel file is saved. Check if Macros Are Corrupt: Open the Visual Basic for Applications (VBA) Editor (Alt + F11). Try running the macro manually. 2.2 Macro-Related Excel Crashes Possible Causes: Corrupt VBA code Incompatible Excel version Troubleshooting Steps: Run Macros in Safe Mode: Open Excel in Safe Mode (excel /safe). Check for Infinite Loops in VBA Code. Use Step Debugging (F8 in VBA Editor) to find problematic lines. Disable Add-ins and Try Again. 3. Other Excel Issues & Fixes 3.1 Excel Formulas Not Updating Troubleshooting Steps: Ensure Calculation Mode is Set to Automatic: Go to Formulas > Calculation Options and select Automatic . Force Recalculation: Press Ctrl + Alt + F9 to refresh all formulas. 3.2 Pivot Table Errors Troubleshooting Steps: Refresh the Pivot Table: Right-click the Pivot Table and select Refresh . Check Data Range: Go to PivotTable Tools > Change Data Source and ensure correct range selection. 3.3 Excel Cannot Save the File Troubleshooting Steps: Check if the File is Open Elsewhere. Try Saving as a New File: Use File > Save As and choose a different location.

short_description: LEAP - How to troubleshoot Microsoft Form issues
introduction: How to troubleshoot Microsoft Form issues
instructions: This KBA is published Only for Kone Way Users on How to troubleshoot Microsoft Form issues Microsoft Forms Troubleshooting Guide for End Users Microsoft Forms is a simple and powerful tool for creating surveys, quizzes, and polls. However, users may encounter issues while using the platform. This guide provides troubleshooting steps for common problems faced by end users. Common Issues and Solutions 1. Unable to Access Microsoft Forms Symptoms: Error message: "You don’t have permission to access this form." Microsoft Forms not loading or showing a blank screen. Troubleshooting Steps: ✅ Check Internet Connection – Ensure your device is connected to a stable internet network. ✅ Ensure you are signed in with the correct Microsoft 365 account (Sign Out and Sign In Again) ✅ Try opening Forms in an incognito/private browsing window. ✅ Clear your browser cache and cookies. 2. Microsoft Forms Not Loading or Freezing Symptoms: Forms page keeps loading indefinitely. The site becomes unresponsive. Troubleshooting Steps: ✅ Restart Browser & Device – Close and reopen your browser or restart computer. ✅ Disable Browser Extensions – Some extensions, such as ad blockers, may interfere with Forms. ✅ Try a Different Network – Switch to another Wi-Fi or mobile data connection to rule out network-related issues. ✅ Clear Browser Cache & Cookies – Go to browser settings and delete cache and cookies. ✅ Update Your Browser – Ensure you are using the latest version of your browser. 3. Microsoft Forms Not Saving Responses Symptoms: Responses disappear after submission. Users report not receiving confirmation after submitting. Troubleshooting Steps: ✅ Ensure that the form owner has not disabled response collection. ✅ Verify that the form is not set to "One response per person" if using a shared link. ✅ Try submitting the form using a different browser or device. ✅ Check the internet connection for stability. 3. Unable to Share or Collect Responses Symptoms: "This form is restricted to specific users." "You don't have permission to share this form." Troubleshooting Steps: ✅ Ensure that sharing settings allow responses from "Anyone with the link" (if needed). ✅ Try regenerating the form link by clicking "Copy link" again. ✅ If sharing via email, ensure recipients have permission to access the form. 4. Microsoft Forms Not Loading or Crashing Symptoms: Slow performance. Freezing or crashing while editing or responding. Troubleshooting Steps: ✅ Restart your browser and try again. ✅ Disable browser extensions, especially ad blockers or privacy tools. ✅ Switch to another browser (Microsoft Edge, Chrome, or Firefox). 5. Missing or Deleted Forms Symptoms: A form you created is missing. Accidentally deleted a form. Troubleshooting Steps: ✅ Check your Forms homepage to see if it was archived. ✅ Look in the Recycle Bin of OneDrive or SharePoint (if linked). ✅ If shared with a team, ask a co-owner if they have access. 6. Email Notifications Not Working Symptoms: No email confirmation after a response is submitted. No email notifications for new responses. Troubleshooting Steps: ✅ Ensure that "Email notification of each response" is enabled in the form settings. ✅ Check your email spam/junk folder. ✅ If using Power Automate for notifications, verify that the workflow is running correctly. ✅ Test with a different email address to confirm the issue. 7. Forms Integration Issues with Excel or Power Automate Symptoms: Responses are not showing in Excel. Power Automate is not triggering form responses. Troubleshooting Steps: ✅ Ensure that the Excel file is stored in OneDrive or SharePoint (not locally). ✅ Refresh the Excel sheet or re-download responses from "Open in Excel" . ✅ In Power Automate, check the flow history for errors and verify permissions. ✅ Reconnect Microsoft Forms in Power Automate and test the flow.

How to Clean Up Outlook / Delete an Email Permanently / Use the Browser Version of Outlook to Delete Emails
introduction: This KBA is published Only for Kone Way Users on How to Clean Up Outlook / Delete an Email Permanently/ Use the Browser Version of Outlook to Delete Emails
instructions: How to Clean Up Outlook When the Mailbox Size Limit is Reached Issue: If you receive a notification that your mailbox size limit has been reached, preventing you from sending or receiving emails. Resolution Follow these steps to clean up your Outlook mailbox: Option 1: Empty Deleted Items Folder: Go to Deleted Items in the left pane. Right-click and select Empty Folder. Delete Unused Sent Items: Navigate to the Sent Items folder. Sort by Date or Size, then delete unnecessary emails. Option 2: Log a ticket with ASKIT for enabling Archive and Retention Policy to archive old mails to Archive folder How to Delete an Email Permanently Issue: If you want to delete an email permanently so that it is not recoverable from the Deleted Items folder. Resolution: Delete Normally: Select the email and press Delete. The email moves to the Deleted Items folder. Permanently Delete from Deleted Items: Open the Deleted Items folder. Right-click on the email and select Delete Permanently. Alternatively, press Shift + Delete when selecting the email to bypass the Deleted Items folder. Empty Recoverable Items: Go to Deleted Items. Click Recover Deleted Items Select emails and click Permanently Delete. How to Use the Browser Version of Outlook to Delete Emails When You Can’t Do It in the App Issue: You cannot delete emails in the Outlook desktop app due to errors or syncing issues. Resolution Access Outlook Web App (OWA): Open a browser and go to https://outlook.office.com/ . Log in with your credentials. Delete Emails in OWA: Navigate to the folder where the email is located. Select the email(s) and click the Delete button. Empty Deleted Items: In the left pane, click Deleted Items. Click Empty Folder or select specific emails and delete them permanently. Clear Cache (If Emails Do Not Delete in the Browser): Press Ctrl + Shift + R to refresh the page. Try using an incognito window (Ctrl + Shift + N) and delete the emails again. By following these procedures, we ensure effective management of the mailbox and mitigate the risk of storage capacity issues.

short_description: How to Handle Large Emails issue in Outlook
introduction: This KBA is published Only for Kone Way Users on How to Handle Large Emails issue in Outlook
instructions: Issue If you are trying to send, receive, or manage large emails in Outlook, but you face issues such as: Email fails to send due to attachment size limits. Slow Outlook performance when handling large emails. Difficulty in opening or downloading large attachments. Solution 1. Check Attachment Size Limits Outlook itself has a 25MB default limit for sending attachments ✅ Workarounds: Use OneDrive or SharePoint: Upload large files to OneDrive, SharePoint, or Teams and share the link instead of attaching the file. Split large attachments: If possible, split the file into smaller parts before sending. 2. Managing Large Emails in Your Inbox ✅ Steps to Identify Large Emails: Launch Outlook and select the email folder you want to clean up. Go to the Search bar and type hasattachments:yes to filter emails with attachments. Select " All Outlook Items " from the drop-down menu next to the Search bar to ensure the search covers all items. Once done, find the attachments below that you want to delete or archive from the mailbox folder. After that, move to the cursor on the attachment you want to remove and right-click. Then, hit the Remove Attachment option to delete attachment from the email. If you need the attachment but don’t want it in Outlook, save it to a local drive and then remove it from Outlook email. 3. Sending Large Emails via OneDrive Link Upload your file to OneDrive. Compress the file: Use a ZIP tool (Right-click > Send to > Compressed (zipped) folder). Right-click the file and select Share. Copy the link and paste it into your email. Click Copy Link. This saves a direct link to the large file to your computer's clipboard. Paste the file into the email message in Outlook. Whether you're using Outlook.com on the web or the Outlook app on your computer, you can paste the URL you copied into the body of the message. Just right-click the location in Click send to send the email 4. Handling Large Emails on Outlook Web (OWA) If Outlook is slow due to large emails, try using Outlook Web App (OWA) at https://outlook.office.com . Large emails are easier to open and download on the web. You can save attachments directly to OneDrive from OWA. 5. Dealing with Stuck Large Emails in Outbox If an email with a large attachment is stuck in Outbox: Go to Outbox and delete the email. Restart Outlook. Resend using OneDrive or compress the file. By adhering to these guidelines, we can efficiently manage large emails in Outlook

short_description: Logging a Ticket for DP (Distribution Point) Requests – Workstations Only
introduction: This process applies only to workstation-related Distribution Points (DPs) managed under MECM (Microsoft Endpoint Configuration Manager). Note: Server-based DPs are not supported via this process.
instructions: Scope of Support This process applies only to workstation-related Distribution Points (DPs) managed under MECM (Microsoft Endpoint Configuration Manager). Note: Server-based DPs are not supported via this process. When to Use This Log a ticket when you encounter any of the following workstation DP issues: DP is offline or unreachable Deployment packages not reaching clients DP is not visible in MECM console Errors seen in DP status monitoring dashboard Steps to Log a Ticket Go to ServiceNow → Self-Service Employee Center Select “Something Doesn’t Work” You will be presented with a form titled "Report an Issue (Incident)" Alternatively, access the form using this link In the Service dropdown, select “DP Health Check” This will populate a set of predefined questions designed to gather all necessary details The SCCM team should: Log the incident ticket in the name of the Onsite Support lead (S Sundararajan) for all region/sites. Route the ticket to the RDS queue - KONE.GLOBAL.RDS Mention clearly that it is a DP availability issue requiring physical validation (RDS team will then route the ticket as per mentioned site details) The OSS team must acknowledge the ticket and respond within SLA timelines After physical checks and resolution: OSS should update the ticket with root cause and actions taken SCCM team must: Validate the DP status post-action If no further action is required, the OSS team can proceed to close the ticket. However, if additional action is needed, the SCCM team will take ownership of the ticket and continue working on it from their queue. Good to Know If the SCCM team fixes the issue remotely: Reassign the ticket (if logged under OSS) to the SCCM group and proceed to close it If fixed by OSS: OSS team can acknowledge SCCM’s confirmation and close it from their queue Escalation Path If no hardware issues are found by the OSS team: OSS may reassign the ticket back to SCCM SCCM will then investigate software/configuration-level issues further Out of Scope Server-based Distribution Points Non-MECM (e.g., 3rd-party or legacy) DPs Requests without supporting evidence or confirmation of the issue For DP Replacement Requests: Confirm and provide the device model and hardware configuration details for the replacement DP. When creating the replacement image, ensure the latest image is installed on the DP – do not include core applications typically used for end-user workstations. Specify the required hard disk capacity and any other essential hardware specifications that need to be considered or validated. Clearly mention if any special setup or configuration is required on the new DP hardware (e.g., BIOS settings, network configuration, etc.) Note: For hardware-related issues, please continue to follow the current process as it is being practiced now.

SMCR - How to troubleshoot SMCR authentication issue - Server returned HTTP response code: 403.
introduction: Ways to troubleshoot SMCR authentication issue - Server returned HTTP response code: 403
instructions: Step 1 : Verify Access to the SMCR Application Check if you can access the SMCR application using the following link. SMCR Production link - https://smcr.kone.com SMCR QA link - https://smcr-qa.kone.com Check if the link opens correctly and the application loads without any errors. Step 2: Try Accessing in Incognito Mode If the application does not load as expected, open a new incognito window and try accessing the SMCR application again using the same link. Step 3: Try Accessing using a different browser. Attempt to access the SMCR application using an alternative browser to rule out any browser-specific issues. Step 4: Check user’s roles and permissions to verify if they have the necessary access to SMCR. Check if the user’s name is listed under any of the following roles in azure AD: ROLE_GLB_SMCR_ADMIN ROLE_GLB_SMCR_SUPPORT ROLE_GLB_SMCR_USER If the user’s name appears in any of the specified groups that confirms they have the appropriate access rights to SMCR application. Step 5: Revoke and Re-request access. Revoke the existing access to SMCR, then submit a new MyAccess request to regain access. Once approved, verify if able to access the application successfully. Step 6: Check recent logs on the PAM server Log in to the PAM server and review the recent activity logs. Look for any entries related to the user’s access attempts or potential authentication issues. Step 7: Check for recent logs in AWS Login AWS - link: https://koneitcloud.awsapps.com/start#/ with your AWS credentials. Open Cloudwatch and click on Logs and then Log groups. Click on log streams under the selected log groups to find the logs. Step 8: Temporary Workaround : Use Legacy Authentication Mode As a temporary measure, switch to legacy authentication mode. This method bypasses Single Sign-On (SSO) and instead uses an internally stored, encrypted password for authentication. Use those credentials to log in to the SMCR application until the SSO functionality is fully restored. Step 9: Updated the underlying Spring Boot and Spring Cloud libraries to a new version and made the necessary code changes accordingly.

short_description: How to Restore a Deleted SharePoint Site from SharePoint Admin
introduction: This KBA is published Only for M365 L2 Support Team on How to Restore a Deleted SharePoint Site from SharePoint Admin. Purpose This article explains how to restore a deleted SharePoint site from the SharePoint Admin Center in Microsoft 365. This applies to both Communication sites and Team sites (including Microsoft Teams-connected sites). Prerequisites You must be a SharePoint Administrator or Global Administrator . The site must have been deleted within the past 93 days . Procedure Step 1: Log into Microsoft 365 Admin Center Go to https://admin.microsoft.com Log in with your Admin credentials . Step 2: Access SharePoint Admin Center In the left navigation pane, expand Admin centers . Click on SharePoint . Step 3: Navigate to Deleted Sites In the SharePoint Admin Center, select "Sites" > "Deleted sites" from the left menu. Step 4: Restore the Deleted Site From the list of deleted sites, select the site you want to restore. Click the "Restore" button at the top. Step 5: Confirm the Restoration A prompt will ask you to confirm. Click "Restore" . The site restoration will begin and may take a few minutes. Once completed, the site will reappear under "Active sites" . Notes Restored sites retain their original URLs. Any Microsoft Teams connection is automatically re-established if it was a Teams-connected site. If the site was permanently deleted (beyond 93 days), you will not be able to restore it. Microsoft Article: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/sharepoint/restore-deleted-site-collection

short_description: Unable to import opportunities after order received(won)
introduction: Unable to import opportunities after order received(won)
instructions: Issue: Users reported that, they are unable to import opportunities via My opportunities or Shared opportunities. Root Cause: If the opportunity stage was "Order received (won)" or "Lost" in CRM, user cannot those opportunities via My opportunities or Shared opportunities. Even though user change stage back to ‘Prospecting/Design Assist’, ‘Budget Price’, ‘RFQ’, ‘Tender/Proposal’, ‘Negotiation/Review’, ‘Order Agreed’ it will not work. In CRM user can change stage but once the stage is set to "Order received (won)" or "Lost" in KCC it will not accept. This is working behavior and not an error. Solution: When user working on opportunity make sure to look on stage in CRM.

short_description: How to Receive an Exception to Use USB for Critical Work Tasks
introduction: Writing to removable storage devices will be blocked. Users can still read files from USB and SD storage devices, but they won't be able to write or save new files to them. This change does not affect other USB devices like a mouse, keyboard, or audio equipment. Employees who have expressed concerns about a critical business impact will be excluded, after they have applied for an exception.
instructions: Implementation: Write access to removable media will be blocked for users who haven’t applied for an exception. The blocking of user groups will be rolled out in phases, starting late April 205 with those who have not used USB for a long time. Groups and teams who have expressed concerns about a critical business impact will be excluded, after they have applied for an exception. By the end of May 2025, everyone will be covered under the new policy except for those who have been granted the exception. Alternative Solutions: Instead of USB, KONE managed services such as SharePoint and OneDrive should be used to share and transfer data. If this isn’t possible, users can apply for an exception. Exception Criteria: The exception can be granted for business-critical work tasks, such as: Testing Upgrades Updating and developing software Transferring large files Configuring and troubleshooting Submitting tender documents in USB format as required by clients, especially in government and public sector projects R&D tasks Data collection Machine configurations Storing and transferring large legal documents and evidence for litigation and compliance purposes Training materials 3D printing Transferring documents to external printers Data backup Measurement instruments and specialized equipment Field and onsite support Media screens and event presentations Custom software Backup and recovery Note: The exception cannot be granted for transferring private documents. Application Process: The exception form will be open until the end of May 2025. If someone gets blocked, they can apply using the exception form and will be automatically exempted. Users do not need to apply again once granted the exception; it will remain in effect for the time being. Important Information: Please note the following steps to ensure consistent handling: Ensure the user submits the USB exception request form (Only if needed): https://forms.office.com/e/dGHVQFJ9Bu When users reach out to AskIT, verify if they are part of the exception group: KONENET-Workstations-DenyWriteAccessRemovableStorageExceptions If they are in the group, proceed with troubleshooting. If they are not, advise them to submit the exception form using the link above Key Points to Remember for Ask IT: The USB restrictions implemented are WRITE restrictions only. Users will receive an error message when attempting to write to a USB device if restrictions are in place. If a USB device does not appear at all, this issue is unrelated to the AccessRemovableStorageExceptions policy and requires standard troubleshooting by AskIT. AccessRemovableStorageExceptions" to clarify that this policy only affects write operations, not device detection. Actions to be taken – AskIT agents must perform proper troubleshooting for USB detection issues. This includes basic checks: Checking device manager for hardware recognition Verifying USB port functionality Testing with different USB devices Reviewing system logs for errors Checking driver status

short_description: Copying Files from Production
introduction: Copying Files from Production
instructions: 1. Purpose This document outlines the standard process for file copying from the production environment to ensure system stability and prevent data corruption. 2. Scope This SOP applies to all team members involved in rollout activities who require files from the production server. 3. Responsibility Rollout Team: Must follow the defined procedure to request file transfers. Support Team: Responsible for handling and verifying file transfers from production. 4. Procedure 4.1 Requesting Files from Production Rollout Team Member Identifies the Requirement: Determine the specific files needed for deployment or analysis. Document the request with justification and file details. Submitting a Request: Contact the Support Team via the designated channel (email, ticketing system, etc.). Provide necessary details, including file location, purpose, and urgency. 4.2 File Transfer by Support Team Verification: Review the request for accuracy and relevance. Confirm that file transfer is necessary. Execution: Copy files securely from the production server to the required location. Ensure data integrity is maintained during the process. 4.3 Post-Transfer Validation Confirm File Integrity: Check for any corruption or discrepancies. Validate that all required files are accessible. Communication: Notify the Rollout Team upon completion of the transfer. Document the process for record-keeping. 5. Compliance & Governance Unauthorized copying of files from production is strictly prohibited. Any deviations from this SOP must be approved by Platform owner/Manager.

short_description: How to proceed when the PDFs of successful invoices are not attached in SAP
introduction: In this article we will explore the reasons and options when the PDFs of the invoices are not attached in SAP, although the invoices are processed and delivered to customers.
instructions: Introduction: When the invoices are processed by vendor and the IDoc status becomes 41, then the PDF of the invoice should be attached in SAP once the layout is created. Sometimes, the invoices are processed and delivered to customer, but the PDF of the invoices are not attached in SAP. This creates trouble since we can't recheck and track the payment of the invoices. Reason of Failure: This situation can arise because of multiple reasons but the most common is batch failure from vendor while processing the invoices in baches and PDFs are not loaded on SFTP server. Since the PDFs are not loaded on SFTP server, the PDFs are not attached in SAP since the attachment of PDFs are automated by job runs and the PDF attachment step is missed on time. Way Forward: Once we identify the reason of failure of invoice PDF attachment, we can explore options to bring back the invoices' PDF's in SAP. The PDFs of the invoices should be handy with us since the PDFs are present on the invoicing portal but not in SAP. We need to get the PDFs from the vendor/invoicing portal. The idea is to download the PDF of the invoices and attach the PDFs manually in SAP. If there is only few invoices, this activity can be done by SAP SD team but if the number is huge as experienced in ASkIT ticket INC00354990: BILLTRUST Europe - 229 invoices in Error status 39 . The KBS team did the activity of attaching the PDFs of the invoices and Finance support team can avail the PDFs of the invoices. Steps to follow while manually attaching the PDFs of the invoices in SAP: 1. T Code: V03/VF02 2. Double click on KWGlobal Invoice Original—-> Select PDF from local system and attach and save 3. Post that we could see the attachment in the attachment list

short_description: 1PLAN - How to renew SSO certificate
introduction: Objective: Renew the SSO certificate for 1PLAN, ensuring seamless authentication and security.
instructions: Implementation of 1PLAN SSO Certificate Renewal Implementation Steps: Step 1: Preliminary Check in QServer: Verify that no existing SSO certificate is present. Step 2: Azure-side Activation: Activate the certificate with validity until 2028 within Azure to ensure proper functionality. Step 3: IDP Metadata Update: Export the IDP metadata URL from Azure. Import the metadata into the Quintiq User Administrator (QSAML service). Verify that the updated metadata now contains the latest certificate details, including Microsoft Federated XML and IDP information. Step 4: Validation & Testing: Conduct thorough testing within the 1PLAN environment. Ensure authentication and SSO functionality operates as expected. Confirm successful metadata import and certificate renewal. Conclusion: The new certificate is fully implemented and tested, ensuring smooth authentication via SSO for 1PLAN.

Instructions to be followed by ASKIT when users contact them related to Domains/URLs Blocked by Microsoft Defender Web Content Filtering
introduction: This article guides the Service Desk team on how to handle user requests when access to a newly registered domain is blocked by Microsoft Defender Web Content Filtering. Users will see an error message as "This content is blocked by your organization."
instructions: Step 1: Confirm User Issue and Capture Required Details Verify that the user is receiving the blocking message from Defender SmartScreen stating: "This content is blocked by your organization” Note: if the error message is from Zscaler or no error message must be checked with ITO firewall team or appropriate process has to be followed by AskIT and those incidents should not be followed with this KBA. Collect the following information from the user: Full URL/domain being blocked Screenshot of the block message Business justification for accessing the domain Urgency or business impact, if any Step 2: Create Incident and Acknowledge End-User If reported via email/chat/phone, create an incident in the ITSM portal. If an incident already exists (e.g., self-logged), review for completeness of the above details. Acknowledge the request to the user and let them know: "Your request has been forwarded to the Cyber Defense for review. short_description: How to Merge Duplicate Calendar and Remove an Extra Calendar in Outlook
introduction: This KBA is published Only for M365 Support Team on How to Merge Duplicate Calendar and Remove an Extra Calendar in Outlook Purpose: This article provides detailed steps to move all calendar entries from one calendar ("Duplicate") into another ("Original") and remove the extra calendar in Microsoft Outlook Desktop. Procedure Step 1: To Move all events from one calendar ("Duplicate") to another one ("Original") To move all events from one calendar to another, firstly, you need to change the view to list. 1. In Outlook 2010/2013/2016, click "View" > "Change View" > "List". (If you are in Outlook 2007, click "View" > "Current View" > "All Appointments".) 2. Then press "Ctrl + A" to select all events in the calendar you want to move events from and right click to display the context menu. 3. Click "Move" to go to the submenu and select one calendar you want to move to. Note: If the destination calendar is not listed in the submenu, you can select "Other Folder” and then specify the destination calendar in the "Move Items" dialog box. Also, you can directly drag the event items to the calendar you use. 4. Go back to Calendar view to confirm the events now appear in the main Calendar. Step 2: Remove the Extra Calendar (“Duplicate”) 1. In Calendar view, locate the “Duplicate” under Calendars. 2. Right-click on Duplicate. 3. Choose Delete Calendar (if it's a user-created calendar) or Remove from View (if it's a shared calendar). Note: You cannot delete the default Calendar or calendars owned by others.

short_description: WinShuttle: Access Steps and Support Contacts
introduction: WinShuttle , is a robust suite of tools designed to streamline and automate business processes within SAP environments. It enables users to interact with SAP data seamlessly through familiar interfaces like Microsoft Excel, reducing the need for complex coding or extensive IT involvement. Key functionalities include: Data Creation and Maintenance : Facilitates the creation and updating of master and transactional data in SAP systems. Data Migration : Supports the migration of data from legacy systems to SAP, aiding in transitions such as M&A activities or upgrades to SAP S/4HANA. Data Extraction : Allows for the extraction of data from SAP into Excel for reporting and analysis purposes. By leveraging WinShuttle, users can enhance data accuracy, improve efficiency, and reduce operational costs associated with SAP data management.
instructions: Accessing WinShuttle: Requesting Access : First-time users must request access through the MyAccess Portal . Search for the term " Winshuttle " within the portal. short_description: How to check the LENS transactions for message flow
introduction: How to check the LENS transactions for message flow
instructions: 1. Overview This document will give us a brief idea how to activate and deactivate and reset the password in Lens. 2. Access to Portal Application Name URL to Access Credentials Required Lens Portal https://lens.liaison.com/ Liaison shared UID and PWD 3. Lens Portal Details Credentials would be received via mail once access has been provided to the LENS portal. Once connected to LENS page enter your Username and password and click on Login. You will enter the Home/Landing page. PO transactions: From this we can provide the PO information directly to check that particular transaction. In this case, enter the required PO number in the search bar and enter. For PO 4558442094, it is visible that the ‘ORDERS’ type transaction has been successfully delivered with the date and timestamp. OBD transactions: OBD (Outbound delivery) can be checked for the transaction status Enter the OBD number in the search bar. For 1129045052, from this we can see that this order has been sent by KONE, and the receiver is DHL India (Despatch Centre) and the transaction type is IFTMIN, which is successfully delivered. Using the Filters option for checking: By using filters, we can search for the range with Sender name, Receiver name, Date range These filters can be used to find any message with particular transaction type These are the statuses that can be checked using lens if a message with a particular EDI type (ORDERS, DESADV, ORDRSP,…) is delivered or not. Now, for Example use case, apply the filter, Set ‘Date range’ from 1st June to 3rd June Enter the receiver’s name as ‘Rikun Manufacturing Private Limited’ Once this is done, we can see the message transaction from June 1 to June 3rd, the receiver being ‘Rikun Manufacturing Private Limited’, has 374 messages delivered successfully and the messages are of type ORDERS and DOCGEN Select the Date range filter from June 1st to June 3rd. Then the transactions happened from June 1st to June 3rd completely are displayed. The delivery status of those messages can be checked. Thus, the messages can be traced using IDs or with filter or required Supplier or message type.

short_description: Steps to follow when users are unable to access salesforce records
introduction: Access to records are based on Role hierarchy and Sharing settings. If user is unable to access records, Then the first step to analyze the issue is to check Role hierarchy of the user trying to access the record. Further Sharing settings setup needs to be checked to fix the issue with record access.
instructions: Understanding of Role Hierarchy : Users in a higher role automatically have access to the records owned by users below them in the hierarchy, Users having role in same level cannot access each other records and Users in a lower role does not have access to the records owned by users above them in the hierarchy. 1. If user is unable to access a record, Compare the role of user trying to access the record with role of record owner. 2. If the role of user trying to access the record is at higher level when compared to the role of record owner, Then the record will be accessible by the user. 3. If the role of user trying to access the record is at same level or in lower level when compared to the role of record owner, Then the record will not be accessible by the user. 4. To fix the record access issue, Role of user needs to be updated to higher level role(Only suggested if affected user is supposed to be in higher role as per their position in FL). 5. If Role cannot be updated due to their position in front line then Sharing settings needs to be checked for possibilities to fix the access issue. Understanding the setup for Sharing settings : Sharing rules are created to share country related records with their country related Groups, Role based on criteria or record owner. 1. Key users and few FL users have access to view the setup maintained for Sharing settings, To access Sharing settings navigate to setup-->Quick find box-->Sharing settings-->Switch between objects to view Sharing rules present for each object. 2. If user is unable to access a record, Role hierarchy needs to be checked as first step of analysis. In case Role cannot be updated to higher role then the sharing settings setup needs to be considered. 3. Under sharing settings we have sharing rules created for each object based on criteria or record owner, To view detailed settings for particular object select the object from the dropdown menu. 4. Once you are on the detail page of a object for example Account, search for sharing rules based on your country. 5. Sharing rules present will have Criteria, Share With and Access type. - Criteria - Conditions for the rule to pick up records to be shared with. - Shared With - Records matching the criteria will be shared with specified Group or Role or Role and Internal Subordinates or Portal Role - Access Type - Records picked up by rule will be either be shared with Read Only or Read/Write access. 6. Based on the rules present for your country, Add the affected user to public group or to the role as specified in the shared with section for the rule. Access type needs to considered to obtain Edit access to the records. 7. Once the user is added to public group or role, Records will be shared with affected user and access as specified on the rule. 8. If the criteria of existing rule does not match with your requirement, Then a service request needs to be raised by key user to create a new sharing rules based on your requirement. Note : Additionally if a user is facing issue with access to Account or Opportunity records of a specific user(record owner) then the user can be added to Default Account Team or Default Opportunity Team of record owner to obtain the access to records.

short_description: MES - Automatic inventory decrease for warehouse location
introduction: Automatic inventory decrease for warehouse location
instructions: Automatic inventory decrease for warehouse location 1. Open Delmia Apriso Client 2. Open Job Scheduler in desktop client. Filter the job ”K1X_Location_ClearInventory” and enable the job. 3. Open warehouse location 4. Select the location for which inventory needs to be decreased. 5. Link the characteristic “K1X_InventoryDecrease” to this location and save it, by going on the link tab à Unit characteristic. NOTE: Based on the above configuration – Job will fetch inventory records which is older than today’s date at where -> ‘K1X_InventoryDecrease’ characteristics is attached to Warehouse location to identify all location in this Job which be deleting the inventory of the location. After fetching inventory records job decrease inventory based on inputs passed from above point with transaction code ‘INVDEC’. This Job is running daily once and executing above steps accordingly. Empty Logistic Units are then deleted by another scheduled job K1X_ContainerRemove_KNEE. The job can handle around 2000 transactions at once. Exception : When starting to use this in locations where multiple records(45000+ materials) exists, in this case ITC will be doing the initial deletion.

short_description: MES - Automatic Default HU Creation
introduction: Automatic Default HU Creation
instructions: 1. Open Delmia Apriso Client 2. Open Work Center in desktop client, and go on Link tab. Attach the characteristic ”K1X_Enable_Auto_HU_Creation” and define Attribute ”Y”. 3. Open Advance Determination Value Editor 4. Select the determination ”K1X_DEFAULT_HU_CREATION”. Advanced Determination is built for Package Type + Product =Packaging Instruction. 5. Select Finished Product Code (Material) from Drop-down for which Default HU Should Create. 6. Select created packaging instruction or packaging Product 7. If Packaging Instruction is selected from “Whether Packaging Instruction OR Packaging Product” then select “Packaging Instruction Code” to create Default HU. In this case Packaging Product ID is not required. 8. If Material option is selected from “Whether Packaging Instruction OR Packaging Product” then select “Packaging Product ID” to create Default HU. In this case Packaging Instruction Code is not required. Note: - For one Finished product there should only be one data (Packaging instruction or Packing product). The packing material inside the Packaging Instruction should be different than the packing material of other packaging instructions.

short_description: 1PLAN - How to extract Orderbook and Rates data from 1PLAN.
introduction: To extract Orderbook and Rates data from 1PLAN, make sure that latest WD6 orderbook data is received from BW and latest WD8 Rates data is received from Beacon.
instructions: Orderbook and Beacon Rates data extract from 1PLAN Step 1: Make sure the WD6 orderbook data is available in the RDS and not loaded into 1PLAN production application yet. Step 4: Right click on the orderbook data table (orderbook_qdl) and right click on it and select ‘ Export Data ’ In the Export target section, select the option ‘CSV’ and click Next. Step 5: Click Next until you go to the Output section, select the path where the file needs to be exported and click on Proceed. Step 6: Rename the exported orderbook data .csv file as ‘ 1planorderbook ’ Step 7: Login to the 1PLAN AWS production environment, open the S3 bucket (one-plan-data-prod) and find the WD8 Rates file in the Beacon Archive folder. Step 8: Download the latest WD8 Rates .csv file and rename it as ‘ BeaconRates ’ Step 9: Close the 1PLAN production AWS environment and login to the AWD DDP account. Step 10: Open the S3 bucket and upload the files in the mentioned path as shown below.

short_description: Support Process For SDI Related Issues
introduction: What is the Support Process involved for SDI related issue?
instructions: Contents 1. Purpose 2. Scope 3. Overview 4. KONE IT Support Model 5. Instructions for SCMS Support 6. Liaison Support 7. Instructions for PDM Support 8. Service Request for Material Table Update 9. Material Table Content 1. Purpose The purpose of this document is for the understanding of the support process involved for SDI related issues. 2. Scope This document is prepared for KONE – SCMS Team and provides the instructions and processes, the SCMS, Liaison and PDM team follows for SDI related issues. 3. Overview The SDI project provides enhancements to EDI communication and Supplier Web content. There are no new applications introduced. 4. KONE IT Support Model The KONE IT Support Model is as follows: SCMS Support Team is the primary contact point for all EDI/Supplier Web issues. KONE Key Users contact SCMS support team to resolve Supplier Web related issues. OpenText Service team sends automatic emails to SCMS Support in specific error conditions. Firstly, SCMS support team contacts Liaison support team and reports the related issues. Secondly, SCMS support team contacts PDM support team, when it is clear that the issue is related to PDM. The Material Table Updates will be handled as Service Requests. 5. Instructions for SCMS Support Use cases: Key User reports that Supplier has not received Order Information. Key User reports that Supplier cannot find order specific document package from Supplier Web. Liaison Service sends automatic error notifications ( see 6. Liaison Support ). SCMS Support checking: SCMS Support logins to Supplier Web first to check the following - Does order exist in Supplier Web? Does document package exist in Supplier Web? Is order / document packages visible through LENS? SCMS Support contacts Liaison Support Team Informs the Supplier name and Order ID and Asks whether the issue is content related or not . SCMS Support contacts PDM support Report only BOM and Document Package content issues. 6. Liaison Support Instructions: LIAISON internal task defined internally. Automatic error notifications to SCMS Support: Cannot access PDM (network connectivity issue). Cannot find PDM BOM (content issue). Cannot process PDM BOM (content issue). Cannot find Document Package from PDM (content issue). Cannot download Document Package from PDM (network connectivity issue) and other related errors. 7. Instructions for PDM Support Use cases: SCMS Support reports that OpenText Service cannot access PDM at all or receives timeout. SCMS Support reports that OpenText Service has no rights to read PDM BOM SCMS Support reports that OpenText Service cannot find PDM BOM or process it. SCMS Support reports that OpenText Service cannot find document package from PDM. 8. Service Request for Material Table Update Use cases: one of authorizes KONE employee creates AskIT Service Request to SCMS Support. 9. Material Table Content The material table should contain the following: As mentioned in the below screenshot. Material Code: KM number PDM BOM: 1 – Full Line Item 2 – Part of Line Item PDM Docs: Yes – Download Document Package from PDM No – Do not download Docs BOM Relation: 1 – Order 2 – Material under Order 3 - Material under Line Item 4 – Material Other

short_description: How to handle the tickets created from Defender alerts ?
introduction: How to handle the tickets created from Defender alerts ?
instructions: Microsoft Defender for Endpoint (MDE) Microsoft Defender for Endpoint (MDE) is an enterprise endpoint security platform designed to help enterprise networks prevent, detect, investigate, and respond to advanced threats. It leverages endpoint behavioral sensors that are built into Windows 10 & Windows 11 to collect and process behavioral signals from the OS. In KONE, MDE is the primary security solution. All KONE workstation should be onboarded to MDE. Microsoft Defender Antivirus Microsoft Defender Antivirus is a next-generation protection solution included in all versions of Windows 10 and Windows 11. From the moment you turn on Windows, Microsoft Defender Antivirus continually monitors for malware, viruses, and security threats. In addition to real-time protection, updates are downloaded automatically to help keep your device safe and protect it from threats. Microsoft Defender Antivirus includes real-time, behavior-based, and heuristic antivirus protection. This combination of always-on content scanning, file and process behavior monitoring, and other heuristics effectively prevents security threats. Microsoft Defender Antivirus continually scans for malware and threats and detects and blocks potentially unwanted applications (PUA), applications deemed to negatively impact your device but aren't considered malware. Microsoft Defender Antivirus always-on protection is integrated with cloud-delivered protection, which helps ensure near-instant detection and blocking of new and emerging threats. This combination of local and cloud-delivered technologies including advanced memory scanning, behavior monitoring, and machine learning, provides award-winning protection at home and at work. All the KONE workstations should have Microsoft Defender as default Antivirus. And the device should also be onboarded and report their data to MDE. MDE Alerts MDE notifies of possible malicious events, attributes, and contextual information through alerts. There are 4 alert severities defined in MDE: Informational Low Medium High Understanding Alert Severity: Microsoft Defender Antivirus and MDE alert severities are different because they represent different scopes. The Microsoft Defender Antivirus threat severity represents the absolute severity of the detected threat (malware) and is assigned based on the potential risk to the individual device, if infected. The MDE alert severity represents the severity of the detected behavior, the actual risk to the device but more importantly the potential risk to the organization. So, for example: The severity of a Defender for Endpoint alert about a Microsoft Defender Antivirus detected threat that was prevented and did not infect the device is categorized as "Informational" because there was no actual damage. An alert about a commercial malware was detected while executing, but blocked and remediated by Microsoft Defender Antivirus, is categorized as "Low" because it may have caused some damage to the individual device but poses no organizational threat. An alert about malware detected while executing which can pose a threat not only to the individual device but to the organization, regardless of it was eventually blocked, may be ranked as "Medium" or "High". Suspicious behavioral alerts, which weren't blocked or remediated will be ranked "Low", "Medium" or "High" following the same organizational threat considerations. Alert Management Process Since MDE is the primary security solution in KONE hence all the alerts generated in MDE becomes important and a security risk for the infra. To manage these alerts below process has been defined: Infosys Scope: Alerts where SOURCE is Antivirus and severity of the alert is either one of these LOW, MEDIUM and HIGH There should be one SNOW incident for each MDE alert where alert source is Antivirus and severity of the alert is either one of these LOW, MEDIUM and HIGH. There should be no SNOW ticket for INFORMATIONAL severity alert. And alert can be closed after running a quick AV scan on the device. LOW severity MDE alert and the corresponding SNOW ticket will be managed by AskIT. AskIT is responsible for remediation of the identified evidence(s) (file/exe) from the infected device. For AskIT, the next level of escalation point is KONE.PCSecurity. KONE.PCSecurity team is responsible for managing MEDIUM & HIGH severity alerts and the corresponding SNOW ticket. In case, user is not available/responding for remediation then the infected devices will be partially isolated in MDE. Alert Automation Alert Management via Microsoft Power Automate Power Automate is an online workflow service from Microsoft that automates actions across the most common apps and services. In our case, to fulfill all the process requirement the solution designed in MS Power Automate utilizes Windows Defender API connection. Automating security processes is a standard requirement for every modern security operation. In KONE, Microsoft Defender for Endpoint (MDE) & Microsoft Power Automate has been integrated to automated MDE alert management. Using Power Automate and based on MDE alert severity, 4 workflows are created in Power Automate for each type of MDE alert severity: Informational, Low, Medium & High. Workflow Setup and Email Notification Workflow in MS Power Automate is configured using SVC_DEFENDER_ALERTS@konenet.com . Hence all the emails generated from workflow will be send from SVC_DEFENDER_ALERTS@konenet.com . Below is an example email notification from the MS Power Automate workflow: Workflows Informational Severity Alert: This workflow resolves the informational alert in MDE. Initiates a quick AV scan on the devices. No SNOW tickets get generated for information severity alert. Low Severity Alert: Low severity alert workflow assigns the alert to AskIT@kone.com and changes the alert status to In-Progress in MDE Initiates a quick AV scan on the device. In the end an email notification is triggered to AskIT@kone.com . This email notification automatically creates a ticket in SNOW and assigns the ticket to AskIT. AskIT works on the alert and device remediation. Medium Severity Alert: Medium severity alert workflow assigns the alert to kone.pcsecurity@kone.com and changes the alert status to In-Progress in MDE Initiates a Full AV scan on the device. In the end an email notification is triggered to AskIT@kone.com . This email notification automatically creates a ticket in SNOW and assigns the ticket to AskIT. AskIT assigns the tickets to KONE.PCSecurity for further actions. High Severity Alert: High severity alert workflow assigns the alert to kone.pcsecurity@kone.com and changes the alert status to In-Progress in MDE Initiates a quick AV scan on the device. In the end an email notification is triggered to AskIT@kone.com . This email notification automatically creates a ticket in SNOW and assigns the ticket to AskIT. AskIT then assigns the tickets to KONE.PCSecurity for further actions. AskIT Process AskIT will follow below process to manage MDE alerts: AskIT is responsible to manage both MDE alert and the corresponding SNOW ticket. For AskIT, the scope is any alert where alert source is Antivirus and alert severity is LOW. AskIT will remediate the identified evidence from the device as mentioned in MDE alert. After remediation of threat, AskIT will run the AV scan to complete remediation activity. If threat remediated, AskIT will resolve both the SNOW ticket with artifact and the corresponding MDE alert in Security portal. If threat is not getting remediated OR user is not responding after 3 follow-ups or alert severity is MEDIUM or HIGH, AskIT will assign the ticket to KONE.PCSecurity team for further investigation and remediation. Follow-Up Timeline Below timeline to be followed for follow-ups. On the 10 th device will be isolated if user is not reachable or responding to follow-up Day 1 Tickets get created in SNOW from MDE alert SNOW tickets get assigned to AskIT AskIT sends 1st user communication for remediation Day 2 Contact user over Teams Contact user over Call AskIT sends 1st follow-up email to user keeping manager in loop. Day 3 Contact user over Teams Contact user over Call AskIT sends 2nd follow-up email to user keeping manager & SDM in loop. Also, a warning for device isolation. Day 4 Assign the ticket to KONE.PCSecurity team Contact user over Teams KONE.PCSecurity sends 3rd follow-up email keeping manager and SDM in loop. Also, a warning for device isolation. Day 5 Wait for user response Day 6 Contact user over Teams KONE.PCSecurity sends 4th follow-up email keeping manager and SDM in loop. Also, a warning for device isolation. Day 7 Wait for user response Day 8 Contact user over Teams KONE.PCSecurity sends 5th and final follow-up email to user for isolating the device if not remediated in next 24 hrs. keeping manager and SDM in loop. Day 9

short_description: How to map email address as secondary SMTP for MaxCredible App request
introduction: This KBA is published Only for M365 L2 Support Team on How to map email address as secondary SMTP for MaxCredible app request Purpose: This document provides step-by-step instructions for the M365 L2 Support Team on how to map an email address as a secondary SMTP for MaxCredible app. Procedure: When a request is received to map or route an email address for MaxCredible app, In such please ask them to share approval from snezana.stankovic@kone.com , who is the application owner of MaxCredible. Once the approval is verified, proceed to add the email address as a secondary SMTP using the PowerShell script Example: To map the email address service.de@email.kone.com as a secondary SMTP for the mailbox " Kundenservice KONE ", use the following command: Set-RemoteMailbox " Kundenservice KONE " -EmailAddresses @{add=" service.de@email.kone.com "} Ensure that the email address being has mapped using the PowerShell script Example: To validate does the email address service.de@email.kone.com is mapped as secondary SMTP for the mailbox " Kundenservice KONE ", use the following command: Get-RemoteMailbox " Kundenservice KONE " | Format-List EmailAddresses Note: MaxCredible app has authorized to send emails on behalf of email.kone.com and invoicing.kone.com. So whenever a ticket is logged regarding the MaxCredible app request to routing or mapping xxx@email.kone.com or xxx@invoicing.kone.com to xxx@kone.com address , add the email address as a secondary SMTP. Kindly follow all the above steps only when the approval is granted from snezana.stankovic@kone.com .

instructions: Issue: The tester’s question IDs were incorrectly assigned from the Taco environment. Cause: During the release process, the question set was likely overridden. Resolution: The correct tester question set has been applied via the backend configuration. Next Steps: Testers must now answer the specific questions to ensure accuracy and consistency. Steps to check: Check in FRM#2 record of installations table has testerQuestionSetSequnece starting with SE. verify SE questions there in installations table if there is no correct SE questions , search based on network number in form-builder-active table and take backup of the record and remove that record then trigger an sqs event mentioned in file test_form_generate.ts in ies-core code https://github.com/konecorp/ies-core/blob/master/api/src/scripts/test_form_generate.ts and then validate whether questions and testerQuestionSetSequence are correct for the network .

short_description: ASKIT MAP Installation Request MAP3.
introduction: MAP Installation Request MAP3 Overview This document outlines the process for raising access by End users for MAP3 application via SNOW (ServiceNow). Introduction: MAP(Maintenance and Acquisition Planning) is a Desktop application used Globally. Users are required to be in KONE network to be able to use it.

short_description: How to create a knowledge article / how to review / how to approve a knowledge article
introduction: How to create a knowledge article Step 1 – Open ServiceNow, navigate to all -> Knowledge -> create new Step 2 – Select the template needed. Step 3 – Select the knowledge base. Knowledge base is the set of audience where the knowledge will be available. End user – The articles will be available to everyone in the organization IT – Articles will be only available to people who hold ITSM access Known error – this is used to create articles to record already known issues / incidents Step 4 – Save the progress (By right clicking on the gray pane) Note – save as when needed to keep your work saved and not to loose your work (Autosave is not available) Step 5 – Fill out all the mandatory information to proceed Step 6 – Author - creator or the one responsible for the content, who will be taking care of review / updating the information Service and configuration item – Mention the service for which the article is related to Knowledge owner – knowledge owner will be automatically updated (Pulls data from CMDB) once the service is selected. Step 7 – Fill out the article body with the contents which needed o be displayed in the KBA. Step 8 – Fill the “Meta” field with the keywords in layman terms which related to the content / the use for which the KBA is created. Example – SAP, Password reset, password, reset, admin account Step 9 – once the content is updated, please save your progress once and then click on the “Review” button which triggers a approval task to knowledge owner of the KBA. Step 10 – Scroll down to the bottom of the KBA to check the approval. Once this is approved the KBA will be moved to published state. Once the KBA is published, it will be available to be viewed. How to edit / review KBA’s Step 1 – open the knowledge article and click on the edit button (Top right corner) Step 2 – click on the checkout button KBA’s can only be edited in draft state (clicking on checkout will move your KBA from published to draft state) Once everything is edited, please click on the save button to save the progress, and again click on the review button to trigger an approval to the knowledge owner. For knowledge owners – How to approve knowledge articles Steps to approve - Step 1 - open ServiceNow and open the knowledge in search, after opening click on the edit button on the top right corner Step 2 - scroll down to bottom of the KBA, click on the approvals tab, you will be able to see the "requested" and please click on the same. Step 3 - After opening the "requested" task please enter the comments and click on the approve button in the top right corner.

short_description: DOS-IES-Network not found in IES

instructions: First, check the network details in TACO to see if the network is released or not. If the network is released, then verify in AWS whether the network details are available. instructions: Step-by-step instructions on how to validate the task: Ensure you are working at the KONE office network or have a virtual private network (VPN) connection established. Open a web browser and go to https://pdm.konenet.com/kone_production Log in with your PDM account credentials New users: Recommend to click the”Help” button to open the ’PDM Help Binder’, containing information on how to use PDM in general By scrolling down the PDM main page, you will find “DMP workflow” box Find Tasks is used to search task-level information.
