short_description: How to use the Power BI Connector in ServiceNow
introduction: Using the Power BI Connector in ServiceNow Overview This article explains how to request access to the Power BI Connector in ServiceNow, create a data source, and import data into Microsoft Power BI for reporting and analysis. Request Access to Use the Power BI Connector To use the Power BI Connector, you must have the Power BI Editor role in ServiceNow. This role allows you to create data sources and import data into Power BI. Steps to Request Access: 1. Raise an access request in MyAccess for the role mentioned below (refer to the attached image). 2. Once the role is granted, you will be able to access the Power BI portal in ServiceNow. 3. After that, Raise a service request to the ServiceNow team to obtain the access credentials required for Basic Authentication (OData Feed). Create a Data Source in ServiceNow After access is granted, follow the steps below to create a data source: Go to: Power BI Connector Portal Scroll down to the Data Sources section. Click New to create a data source. Fill in the following fields: Data Source Name Description (optional) Row Limit Click Add Table to configure the data export. In the popup: Search or scroll to find the desired table. Click Add to include the table. Select the fields required for your report. Optionally, apply filters to narrow the data. Click Save to complete the data source creation. Import ServiceNow Data into Microsoft Power BI To import the data source into Power BI from the Power BI Connector page. Find your data source in the Data Sources list (use the filter for quicker access). Click on the actions and select Copy OData URL . Open Power BI Desktop . Click Get Data > OData Feed . Paste the copied URL. When prompted, enter your ServiceNow credentials (basic authentication). In the Navigator window, select the table(s) to import and click Load . You can now begin building your Power BI dashboard using the imported ServiceNow data. Note: Only Basic Authentication is supported when using OData to import data into Power BI. Credentials will be provided by the ServiceNow team upon request approval.

short_description: KTOC NBS Product - Activating new offering in FL Segmentation & Price key in FL PL

instructions: When you can’t be able to select values in drop-down list or you can’t find your desired value to be selected in the list, then you have to activate the values at segmentation in maintenance. Here in the dropdown menu, there only 3 options available to select as COP display type. For example, if you want to select 44; 10.1 inch KONE display then you have to contact the key user to activate this value in the segmentation if it is not done. Please find the below steps how to activate the value in the segmentation Step1: Open the segmentation and select the class (Here COP_NMO) choose the feature You can see the 44; 10.1 inch KONE display option which is the newly added values of this feature. Step2: Click on the activate icon to activate the value in the segmentation Then the newly added value in the segmentation will be added in the drop- down menu on configuration. Step3: If you want to make this option as standard then you have to click on the standard check box to make this as a standard option to be selected. Activating new offering Price key in FL PL As same as activating values in segmentation, you can also activate pricing feature in segmentation too. Step1: Open the segmentation and select the class (Here NMonospace) choose the pricing feature You can add your desired pricing value to be activated in the feature. Step2: Click on the activate icon to activate the pricing value in the segmentation If you’ re unable to identity the solution from this document, please create an ASK IT ticket to KTOC NBS Product service.

short_description: How to Modify the Display name and Owner detail of the Shared mailbox
introduction: How to Modify the display name and owner name of the Shared Mailbox are done by M365 L2 support.
instructions: How to Modify the Display name and Owner detail of the Shared mailbox Introduction: How to Modify the display name and owner name of the Shared Mailbox are done by M365 L2 support. Instructions: Once a ticket is assigned to L1.5 support agent, approval must be obtained from the owner of the associated shared mailbox. If no owner is specified, approval should be sought from a mailbox member with full access rights. In the absence of both, the requester’s line manager should be approached for approval. Upon receiving the necessary approval, the ticket should then be assigned to L2 support. Procedure: As the shared mailbox is hosted on-premises, begin by logging into the PAM server using the following link: https://pam.konenet.com/privx/connections/new Once connected to PAM, select the Exchange Tool 42 server to proceed. Steps to Modify the Display name of the shared mailbox : Open Active Directory Users and Computers . Locate the shared mailbox object by searching with email address. Right-click the mailbox and select Properties . Go to the General tab. Update the Display Name field to the new name. Click OK to save changes. Steps to Modify the owner name of the shared mailbox : Open Active Directory Users and Computers . Locate the shared mailbox object by searching with email address. Right-click the mailbox and select Properties . Go to the Telephone tab. In the Notes section , update the owner detail. Click OK to save changes. Powershell command to modify the Display name of the shared mailbox : To update the display name of a shared mailbox on an on-prem Exchange server, open Windows PowerShell as Administrator and run the following command: Set-RemoteMailbox -Identity "sebsalescommunication@kone.com" -DisplayName "New Display Name"

short_description: DOS-IES- Missing floor or door count.
introduction: If the door or floor count is missing follow this procedure to resolve this issue
instructions: 1. Log in to AWS Console URL: https://koneitcloud.awsapps.com/start/#/?tab=accounts Navigate to the correct AWS account. 2. Access DynamoDB In the AWS console, search for and open "DynamoDB". Select the table: ies-app-prod-sap-persistent-characteristics Click "Explore table items". Use the search bar to query using the network number (e.g., 72243072). If the item exists: Skip creation, no action needed. If the item is not found: Click "Create item". Switch to "JSON view". Paste and edit this JSON: { "networkNumber": "72243072", "sk": "QTY_LDO_A#2", "characteristicName": "QTY_LDO_A", "characteristicValue": "2", "companyCode": "C128", "modifiedAt": "2025-08-17T09:43:33.256Z", "sourceOfCharacteristic": "BUFFER TABLE" } Modify the following fields: networkNumber: Replace with your actual network number. sk: Update floor number, e.g. if floor 3 → "QTY_LDO_A#3". characteristicValue: Should match the floor (e.g., "3"). modifiedAt: Update to current timestamp if required. Click "Create item". 3. Check Installation Table Table: ies-app-prod-installations-persistent-installations-table Query using the network number. Click "Run". If FRM field exists: Skip. If FRM is missing: Find a different network where FRM is present. Copy the entire item (JSON). Create a new item with updated network number 4. Handle Tester/Supervisor Form Data Table: ies-form-prod-form-engine-persistent-form-builder-active-form Search for the network number. Click the item → switch to JSON view. Take a backup of the JSON. Click "Actions" > "Delete" to remove the form for that network. 5. Trigger Step Function In AWS, search: Step Functions. Select the State Machine: iesformprodformenginepersistentformbuilderenginestatemachineC0D7282-9nmIW2m3U4RE Click "Start execution". Paste and edit the below JSON input: { "networkNumber": "72243072", "equipmentNumber": "YourEquipmentNo", "country": "YourCountryCode" } Replace values as needed. Click "Start Execution". Wait for execution to complete. 6. Verify Floors in Installations Table Go back to: ies-app-prod-installations-persistent-installations-table In query, set: Sort Key (SK): Begins with QUE#NO000107 (replace with correct prefix) Click Run. Check the text column for how many floors are landed/available. 7. Share Results If the floor count matches expected values: Inform the user that the update was successful.

short_description: Instructions for ASKIT for being vigilance against Social Engineering Attacks
introduction: Instructions for ASKIT for being vigilance against Social Engineering Attacks Attackers increasingly target KONE employees through social engineering via email, phone, SMS, or messaging apps like WhatsApp and Telegram. Criminals may impersonate employees, vendors, or executives to gain access to credentials or sensitive data. AskIT agents themselves are at risk, as attackers exploit IT helpdesk privileges to request password resets, MFA changes, or access modifications.
instructions: Key threats to watch for: WhatsApp impersonation: Scammers create fake business profiles using random mobile numbers and executive photos. Messages often start with a simple “Hi” to build trust before requesting confidential data or money. Deepfake voice calls: Attackers use AI-generated audio to mimic executives’ voices, making phone calls sound convincingly real. Urgent requests: Messages create a sense of urgency to bypass standard verification procedures. Red flags for AskIT agents: Be cautious when handling user reports of suspicious activity: Urgent or unusual requests for password resets, access rights, or confidential data. Poor grammar, odd formatting, or unfamiliar sender addresses. Attempts to bypass standard procedures or escalate access without proper approvals. Unverified links or attachments in emails, chats or SMS. Calls claiming to be from executives or vendors asking for immediate action. Verification Protocols for critical account actions: It is mandate that no KONE credentials in any circumstances be reset, changed or MFA removed during a single AskIT support phone call. If such is needed, the ASKIT agent need to make a verifying callback/Teams contact to the users manager and crosscheck user details as well as against AD or Entra ID like full name, department and also verify the reported activity (and also create a ticket / record the activity somewhere). In addition use callback verification to the phone number listed in Entra ID. If user is unavailable, contact the user’s line manager to obtain the phone number, then call the user to confirm their identity and manager before proceeding. If phone verification is not possible, the user must visit a KONE office for identity validation and reset. Escalate any suspicious cases to the KONE Cybersecurity team at security@kone.com How AskIT agents should guide users: : ASKIT agents should follow the steps below and instruct users when they suspect a security incident involving impersonation via SMS, WhatsApp, phone calls, or deepfake technology. 1. Assess the situation before acting: Is the message or call from an unknown number or email? Does it create urgency or secrecy? Are sensitive data, credentials, or financial actions being requested? Advise users to block unknown numbers. 2. Avoid interacting with suspicious content: Do not reply or perform requested actions. Do not click links or download attachments from unknown sources. 3. Verify legitimacy through official channels: Use KONE-approved tools like Teams or Outlook. Never rely on contact details provided in suspicious messages. 4. Report incidents immediately: Email security@kone.com Assign the incident to KONE.KTI.Cybersecurity.Operations queue. Document all information gathered and actions taken. By following these protocols, AskIT agents protect both themselves and KONE employees from evolving social engineering attacks.

short_description: User Guide – Automated Story Creation from ServiceNow to JIRA
introduction: 🛠 ️ User Guide – Automated Story Creation from ServiceNow to JIRA 📋 Integration Prerequisites Before initiating the integration, ensure that your Service Manager has added your JIRA ID to the ServiceNow CMDB. How To Add Your JIRA ID in ServiceNow CMDB? Log in to ServiceNow Navigate to Application Services: Use the left-hand navigation or Global Search. Select Application Services under the appropriate module. Locate your Application Service: Use list view or filters. Click the service name to open the record. Enter the JIRA ID in the designated field. Click Save/Update to apply changes. How to Find Your JIRA ID? Log in to JIRA Go to Projects → ⋯ (More Options) → Manage Projects Search for the required project (e.g., ServiceNow Platform ) The Project Key next to the project name is your JIRA ID. JIRA Settings Ensure the JIRA project key is included in the scope of the JIRA Global Automation Rules . 📨 To request inclusion, submit JIRA SERVICE REQUEST form to the JIRA team. 🔄 Workflow 📊 Status Mapping 🎯 Priority Mapping 🧩 ServiceNow Steps Log in to ServiceNow Navigate to: Workspaces → Service Operations Workspace. Locate the Requested Item (RITM) using the Global Search bar. Access Catalog Tasks via Related Records → Catalog Tasks Update the Task: Set Assignment Group to KONE.JIRA.Integration Select your Application Service in the Configuration Item field. 📌 Notes: Click the ℹ️ icon to view the JIRA ID of the selected Application Service. A valid JIRA Project ID is mandatory. If missing, add it to the CMDB (see above). Provide Task Details: Short Description → Mapped to JIRA Story Title Description → Mapped to JIRA Story Description Select the appropriate JIRA Team from the dropdown. Click Save to submit the form. 🔗 Correlation ID Sync (JIRA → ServiceNow) Upon saving the SC Task with valid inputs: A JIRA Story is automatically created. The story links to the Application Service via the JIRA ID. A Correlation ID is generated and populated in the SC Task. This ID confirms successful linkage between ServiceNow and JIRA. 📌 JIRA Log in to JIRA Search using the Correlation ID from ServiceNow. Following details can be found: Title = Short Description in ServiceNow Description = Description field in ServiceNow Team = Jira Team in ServiceNow 🚀 Extended Functionalities 🔄 State Transitions (JIRA → ServiceNow) Status updates in JIRA are automatically reflected in ServiceNow. For final states ( Done , Rejected , Canceled ), ServiceNow moves the task to Awaiting Closure and auto-closes after 3 days. 🔁 Priority Sync (ServiceNow <> JIRA) Priority set in ServiceNow and JIRA are synced in real time. 💬 Comment Updates (JIRA <> ServiceNow) Comments in JIRA and ServiceNow are not auto-synced with each other. To sync a comment, append [send] at the end. ✅ Example: Deployment scheduled for tomorrow [send] → Synced ❌ Example: Deployment scheduled for tomorrow → Not synced 📞 Support Contacts For any questions or assistance related to the ServiceNow–JIRA integration, please reach out to the respective contacts below: ServiceNow Support Fathimuthu Banu 📧 fathimuthu.banu@kone.com JIRA Support Abdul Raheem Mohammed 📧 mohammed.abdulraheem@kone.com DMI Support H Mallinath 📧 mallinath.h@kone.com For Automated Bug Creation from ServiceNow to JIRA (Incident Use Case) – Click to View

short_description: How to Fix POSITION Parameter Type Conflict (Real Number vs Integer) in CAD-Windchill Check-In
introduction: During the CAD model check-in process to Windchill, users may encounter an error related to parameter type mismatch. A common case is the component parameter " POSITION " being defined as a Real Number in the CAD tool, while the corresponding Windchill attribute is defined as an Integer Number. This inconsistency prevents successful check-in of the CAD document.
instructions: This article provides step-by-step instructions to resolve the POSITION parameter type mismatch by modifying the parameter type in the Creo tool. Steps to Resolve Open the model in the CAD tool. In the Model Tree, first check if the POSITION parameter exists (refer to the highlighted example in the image below). Navigate to Tools → Parameters. Change the Filter By option to Current and All Assembly Features and Components. Locate the parameter " POSITION ". Verify the parameter type: If the type is Real Number, delete the parameter Save the model and retry the check-in to Windchill. Additional Steps for Large Assemblies When working with a large assembly, a Re-edit Filter error may appear. Click on Re-edit. Create a new filter by selecting New (refer to the highlighted example in the image below). In the Filter Definition window: Set Scope Control to Filter in Current and All Sublevels. Add the rule as shown in the image under Rule Definition. Activate the rule and click OK. The filter will now display only the POSITION parameter. Check the parameter type: If the type is Real Number, delete the parameter. Best Practice for Adding POSITION Value For both small and large assemblies, the recommended approach to add the POSITION value is: Go to Link-IT → Manage BOM Positions. Add the empty POSITION value. Click OK. Save the model. Proceed with check-in to Windchill. Note: The above steps can also be used to change the type of any parameter, not just the POSITION parameter.

short_description: Restoring Custom Notification Tray Icon in KFM Client for Android 14 and above OS Versions
introduction: Starting with Android 14 and continuing into Android 15, Google has introduced a system-level change affecting how notification tray icons are displayed. Instead of showing custom notification icons, the system now defaults to displaying the application's main launch icon for all Android apps. As a result, the KFM Client application is also impacted by this change. The familiar bucket icon, which was previously shown in the notification tray, is now replaced by the main KFM app icon by default. This behavior is consistent across all apps and is driven by Android's updated notification handling policies. This KBA document provides step-by-step instructions to restore the previous custom Notification Icons (e.g., the bucket icon used in KFM).
instructions: To revert the notification tray icon to the previous custom icon, follow these steps: Step-by-Step Instructions On your Android device, Open the Settings app. Look for Notifications or Apps & Notifications depending on your device model and Android version. Note: The exact path to Notification settings may vary slightly depending on your device manufacturer and Android version. Tap on Advanced Settings within the Notifications screen. Tap on Advanced Settings (or similar option such as More Settings, Notification Categories, or Additional Settings, depending on your device). By default, this toggle is enabled , which causes the app icon to appear in the Notification tray. Disable the toggle for “Show app icon in notifications”. Expected outcome Once the setting is disabled, the Notification tray will display the original custom icon (e.g., the Bucket icon) instead of the KFM Client app icon. *** END OF ARTICLE ***

short_description: DOS-IES-ProjectType Mismatch
introduction: If the user is unable to view the network details, it may be because the project type is set to 'FRB-ESC', which corresponds to an escalator project and is therefore not displayed in IES. To resolve this, the project type should be changed from 'FRB-ESC' to 'FRB-ELE'. We request the SAP team to update the project type accordingly. If this is not possible, we proceed with the following alternate procedure:
instructions: Go to the AWS site https://koneitcloud.awsapps.com/start/#/?tab=accounts and log in with appropriate credentials. Navigate to the IES App Production: In the AWS Management Console, search for "IES App Production". Select it and click on "Admin". Search for DynamoDB: Use the search bar to find "DynamoDB". Click on "Tables". Locate the Target Table: Find and select the table named: ies-app-prod-sap-persistent-networks Explore Table Items: Click on "Explore Table Items". Run a Query: Click on the "Query" tab. Enter the Network Number in the query field. Click "Run" to fetch the network details. Check the Project Type: In the network details, scroll down to find the field named ProjectType . If the value is FRB-ESC , this indicates it's an escalator project and won't show in IES. Update the Project Type: Change the value from FRB-ESC to FRB-ELE . After making the update, ensure to save the changes. Notify the User: Share the updated details with the user to confirm the issue is resolved.

short_description: Altair License Update in Server
introduction: Altair License Update in Server
instructions: Introduction How to Check the Altier License Update To check the License Update Following steps need to be followed: Instruction: Log in to the KCOFIVMMS153 server - > then open File Explorer - > Navigate to the D:\Altair, where the applications are installed. Inside this directory, you'll find a file named Altair_lic.dat , which serves as the default license file. If a new license is obtained, it should be placed in this same folder. New license will be available here by default altair_lic.dat Step 1: Import the file as a zip file received from Vendor to the server(for e.g.: ALSW_KOOY_20250930_865502.ZIP) Step 2: Go to KCOFIVMMS153\Transfers on PrivX Step 3: Copy the file zip file ALSW_KOOY_20250930_865502.ZIP. Step 4: Now Paste it in the D drive. Step 5: Stop the Altair License Server in the Services. Go to services - > Right click on the Altair License Server and Stop the services. Step 6: Go to the D Drive and rename the DAT file as till date because it is an old one (for e.g. here the DAT file is renamed as altair_lic_till_Feb2025.dat) Step 7: Renamed Dat file should be saved inside the old license Step 8: Unzip the zip file and extract it ALSW_KOOY_20250930_865502.ZIP. Step 9: Click on Extract Step 10: Copy the altair_lic.dat file and paste it in the D drive Step 11: Delete the file ALSW_KOOY_20250930_865502 Step 12: Now Start the services Step 13: Right click on the Altier License server and click on properties Step 14: check the config Step 15: The Startup type should be Automatic Step 16: Click on the altair-serv.cfg file and open it via Notepad Step 17: Default license file will be displayed.

short_description: Predictive Intelligence - Agent Assistance for Handling Similar Incidents
introduction: The Agent Assist feature, integrated with ServiceNow’s Predictive Intelligence, empowers support agents by automatically recommending similar incidents—both open and resolved—based on the short description of a newly created ticket. This intelligent assistance reduces manual effort, improv es resolution speed, and ensures consistency in handling recurring issues.
instructions: 1. Business Need When a new incident is created, ServiceNow’s Predictive Intelligence analyzes the short description and description in real-time. It then uses trained ML models to identify and display similar records, including: Similar Open Incidents Helps identify potential major incidents by showing if other users are experiencing the same issue concurrently. This also prevents duplication of tickets. Similar Resolved Incidents Allows agents to view how similar issues were previously resolved, enabling reuse of resolution notes and promoting consistency. These suggestions appear in a dedicated pane within the Incident form of the Service Operation Workspace, reducing the need for manual searches and allowing agents to focus on resolving the issue. 2. Accessing Agent Assist 2.1 Login to ServiceNow: https://koneuat.service-now.com 2.2 Navigate to Service Operations Workspace. 2.3 Go to: All → Incident → Create New 2.4 Fill in mandatory fields and enter a Short Description. 2.5 The Agent Assist Panel will automatically display: Similar Open Incidents Similar Resolved Incidents 3. Role-Based Behavior and Scenarios Scenario 1: Creating a New Incident Condition: Agent enters a short description. Behavior: Agent Assist suggests similar incidents. Clicking Full View redirects to the incident record. Agent can analyze previous actions taken. Scenario 2: Using Suggested Actions Once a similar resolved incident is identified, the agent can: Copy Incident Resolution Copies resolution code and notes from the resolved incident. Saves time and ensures consistency. Link Incident Creates a parent-child relationship. Helps in tracking related issues. Scenario 3: Similar Open Incidents Purpose: Avoid duplicate tickets. Behavior: Agent sees open incidents with similar descriptions. Can decide to update existing or link instead of creating a new one. 4. Actions on Agent Assist Options 4.1 Full View: Opens the full incident record for detailed analysis. 4.2 Copy Incident Resolution: Reuses resolution notes from resolved incidents. 4.3 Link Incident: Establishes a parent-child relationship between related incidents. 4.4 Similar Open Incidents: Helps avoid duplication by showing active tickets with similar issues.

short_description: CRM - How to upload attachments to Opportunity
introduction: This will guide how to mass upload attachments to opportunity in Salesforce. The Data Import Wizard does not support import of Attachments into Salesforce; you need to use the Data Loader for this task.
instructions: Download the compressed attachment from the ticket and extract it to local, and you will need to prepare the import file with the following columns, save the file to CSV format: Insert Attachment – map below fields: ParentId: Opportunity ID (ID of the object associated with the attachment) Name: The name of the attachment file Body: The absolute path to the attachment file's location on your local drive. Insert the records via the Data Loader: Step 1: Open Data Loader and set the batch size to 10. Step 2: Click the Insert button. Login to Data Loader using Salesforce credentials plus the security token. Step 3: Selected “ Shows all Salesforce objects ” to choose Attachment (depending on the type of record you are importing). Step 4: Locate the object from the list of objects and browse for the .csv file. You can then either choose to auto-map or manually map the columns in the .csv file to the fields. Step 5: For every operation, the Data Loader generates two unique .csv log files; one file name starts with "success," while the other starts with "error." Step 6: Click on Browse ... to specify a directory for the log files. Step 7: Click on Finish and click on Yes on the prompt. Step 8: Click OK . After uploading, search out an opportunity -> Notes & Attachments related list to validate that attach file is added successfully.

short_description: Product and Price Book Entries Insertion - Data Management
introduction: Creation of Products and their Standard and Custom Price Books

short_description: Submit MP opportunity for opportunity
introduction: Submit MP opportunity for opportunity
instructions: Impossible to submit MP opportunity for approval In this request the user could not submit the mentioned opportunity for approval First we need to validate the below points: 1. Approval Process Configuration Ensure the Opportunity record meets the entry criteria defined in the approval process. Check if the correct approval process is active and assigned to the Opportunity object. 2. User Permissions Confirm that Laurent BOUBLIL has the necessary permissions to submit records for approval. Make sure Kevin Latreille is correctly assigned as the approver in the approval process. 3. Automation or Triggers Check if there are any Apex triggers, flows, or validation rules that might be silently preventing submission. Review any custom logic that could interfere with the approval submission. Analysis & Resolution: Upon checking with Laurent Boublil during a Teams call, it was identified that the checkbox labeled "Check this box to submit for approval" had not been selected Once the checkbox was selected and the submission retried, the opportunity was successfully submitted for approval. Outcome: The opportunity has now been approved by the user's manager, Kevin Latreille.

short_description: To Insert Contacts, Campaign Members and customer Surveys
introduction: To Insert Contacts, Campaign Members and customer Surveys. This KBA about creation of contacts, creating campaign members and Customer Survey.
instructions: How to upload Installation Survey data, created contact and campaign member. Sample ticket SR00152318. Objects related: 1. Contact (contact): insert new contacts. If contacts already exist, no action required for contact object. 2. Opportunity Contact Role (OpportunityContactRole): insert if opportunity ID available. 3. Campaign Member (CampaignMember): insert. With campaign member adding to campaign, user can start survey in Campaign. 4. Customer Survey (Flow_Survey__c): insert. Used to store flow survey result. Necessary to upload even user not mention this object in ticket. Insert Opportunity Contact Role – map below fields: · Opportunity ID · Contact ID · Contact role After uploading, search out an opportunity -> Contact Role related list to validate that contact role is added successfully. Insert Campaign Member – map below fields: · Campaign ID · Asset ID · Other equipment · Contact ID · Opportunity (if opportunity ID is available) · KONE Installation manager · Survey Business Type For equipment, there 2 objects. Equipment__c and Asset. It’s the Asset to map for campaign member. The data user provides has Equipment Number, no Asset ID for the equipment. Before inserting campaign member, we need to pull Asset ID based on Equipment Number. Add one more column Asset ID in original file, VLOOKUP to find the ID of equipment. Save the file to CSV format. Mapping fields as below. After uploading make sure campaign member is added correctly to campaign. All the details are correct, especially Equipment field which is a link of Asset object. Open campaign. Click Start Survey button. All campaign members inserted should be displayed here. Otherwise user cannot start survey for this contact. Insert Flow Survey – map below fields. · Campaign Member ID · Contact ID · Opportunity ID (if available) · Campaign ID · Survey Business Type · Other equipment · Asset ID · KONE Installation manager/Project Manager Find out the success file of inserting campaign member, use it to insert flow survey as campaign member ID is generated there. Make sure the flow survey is generated successfully after uploading especially the Equipment. Rename success and error files with names to distinguish different objects. Upload to ticket. Sometimes contact doesn’t exist, need to insert. Map below fields: - Salutation - First Name - Last Name - Account number - Contact owner ID - Phone - Email - Mobile - Do not call - Do not survey - Preferred language - Title - Function - Country - Mobile

short_description: NPX User Access Removal
introduction: NPX User Access Removal
instructions: NPX User Access Removal Note: Before proceeding with the removal of users access in NPX, please ensure that user should not be the owner of any records in both CRM and NPX . Step 1 : IN CRM we can validate this using the below query. We can add owner email based on our requirements. Query: Select Id,FollowUp_Owner_Email__c, Escalation_Owner_Email__c, Escalation_Owner2_Email__c from NPX_Action_Owner__c where FollowUp_Owner_Email__c = 'karenmae.delavega@kone.com' OR Escalation_Owner_Email__c = 'karenmae.delavega@kone.com' OR Escalation_Owner2_Email__c = 'karenmae.delavega@kone.com' If we did not find any record, then it’s good that means in CRM there is no any record available under this user. If we find any record in CRM, we need to send an email to respective key users to provide us the replacement of user, so will change the owner of that record as per key user’s suggestions. Step 2 : Now we have to check in NPX, under this user is there any record available or not. Login into NICE Satmetrix, click on Invitations & Responses Apply filter on date, from 1 st Jan 2014 to today’s date and click on Apply Download that file & search using user’s name & email. Most of the time in NPX we don’t find any record in excel under the requested user. Step 3 : Once it is confirmed that no records exist under the requested user in both NPX and CRM, we can proceed with disabling the user. After just open the requested user in NICE Satmetrix, in Active button just make it No & save the changes. If we disable active button from YES to NO user will get disable in NPX.

short_description: Why is the street not updated correct on all accounts in CRM? On this account it has not been updated: 13706270. But on account 11025095, it's updated correctly.
introduction: User is asking that the Street information is in Capital letters in SAP, but it is small letters in SAP. When we checked with SAP team, they confirmed that Street information is in small letters, then we asked to repush the street info to reflect in CRM. After repush it is still showing in capital letters. Then we investigated in Salesforce side, we found that there are two fields existed for Street info, users are entering street info on Street_1__c field, then the login written as it should be saved on “BillingStreet” field, then it will be sent to SAP as it is. In SAP users are manually changing the address info to Small letters. Then that info is passing to “BillingStreet” field, but not to Street_1__c. Hence the street information is still showing as capital letters in CRM.
instructions: If you face any issue like Address information mismatch in SAP and in CRM, First check in CRM side whether the Account is created in salesforce or not then check with SAP team if any address change happened manually from user side or not.

short_description: Sending Acknowledgement emails
introduction: For sending Acknowledgement emails, custom logic was developed in CS_CaseAcknowledgement Apex class First, we are checking these 4 conditions: 1.Automatic emails are not disabled for the related Contact: If related Contact is populated on Case, checkbox field CSD_Disable_Automatic_Emails__c on the Contact record has to be marked as false. 2.Email loop is not detected: If more than 5 emails from the same email have been sent in the last 30 minutes, email loop is detected and Acknowledgement functionality is blocked. The number of emails and time period are defined in CS_AcknowledgementBlockEmailLimit and CS_AcknowledgementBlockDuration Custom Labels respectively. 3.Corresponding CSQueueandConutry__mdt metadata record is found: There is a match in the related CSQueueandConutry__mdt metadata record based on CS_Country_Name__c (CS_A_area__c in certain cases) with respective fields on Case record (Sales_Organization__c and CS_A_area__c). 4.Acknowledgement is not blocked in metadata: The checkbox field Acknowledgment_Blocked__c in related CSQueueandConutry__mdt metadata from the 3rd condition has to be marked as false. Only if all four conditions are fulfilled, an automatic Acknowledgement email is sent. There are following two scenarios for sending Acknowledgement email: manual Case creation automatic Case creation (Email-to-Case functionality) Manual Case creation For manual Case creation, we have the following logic. First, the related CSQueueandConutry__mdt metadata is chosen based on CS_Country_Name__c (CS_A_area__c in certain cases) match with respective fields on Case record (Sales_Organization__c and CS_A_area__c). Then based on the email address (CS_Email_Address__c) from this metadata, related CS_Country_Queue_EmailAlert__mdt metadata is chosen. Finally, based on the Acknowledgement Email Alert from metadata, an email is sent. Example We want to receive an Acknowledgement email in Polish. So while creating the case we set the Country to Poland. Based on that, CS: Poland “CSQueueandCountry” (CSQueueandConutry__mdt) Custom Metadata record is chosen. Based on Email Address field (test_cs_kone.poland@kone.com) related CS: Poland - Customer Service CS_Country_Queue_EmailAlert__mdt mtd is chosen. Finally, based on the Acknowledgement Email Alert field, email alert CS_Poland_Customer_Service_Acknowledgement_Email_Alert is chosen and an email is sent. Automatic Case creation For automatic Case creation via Email-to-Case functionality, we have the following logic. First, the related CS_Country_Queue_EmailAlert__mdt metadata is chosen based on CS_Email_Sent_To_Address__c on Case record. Then based on the Acknowledgement Email Alert from metadata, an email is sent. If no metadata is matched, then the logic for manual Case creation is used. Example We want to receive an Acknowledgement email in Polish. So we sent an email to the address test_cs_kone.poland@kone.com. This email address is stored in Email Sent To Address field on the newly created Case. Based on this field related CS: Poland - Customer Service CS_Country_Queue_EmailAlert__mdt Custom Metadata record is chosen. Finally, based on the Acknowledgement Email Alert field, email alert CS_Poland_Customer_Service_Acknowledgement_Email_Alert is chosen and an email is sent. Some countries want to autopopulate CS_A_Area__c when created via Email-to-Case logic so respective Custom Metadata records were updated with CS_A_Area__c value as part of CSD-2036: Finland: CS&A Area to be pre-defaulted to National (SR01707525)Done . If Case is created for these countries without CS_A_Area__c populated, respective CSQueueandConutry__mdt Custom Metadata would not be found because it would be specified only as {country}+CS_A_Area__c and CS_A_Area__c would be null in that case. In the logic, we want to cover such cases, that is why Custom Metadata records without CS_A_Area__c are added to the map as well. Order by CS_A_Area__c field also was added to the query so null CS_A_Area__c values go first and we add this metadata only when there is no standalone Custom Metadata with the same country and without CS_A_Area__c already in map.

short_description: Bring Your Rooted/Jailbroken Android Device Back to Compliance
introduction: Bring Your Rooted/Jailbroken Android Device Back to Compliance
instructions: Bring Your Rooted/jailbroken Android Device Back to Compliance End‑User Step‑by‑Step (Microsoft Intune • Android) Your device is marked Noncompliant because it is rooted / bootloader‑unlocked (Android). Company apps and email are blocked until you return the device to the official OS and re‑enroll with Intune (Company Portal). Before You Start • Time needed: 20–60 minutes (download times may vary). • You will need: Stable Network, your work account (Microsoft 365), and your Apple ID/Google account. • Data impact: Most fixes require a factory reset. Back up personal data first. • Do not try to hide jailbreak/root—security checks will detect it and keep the device noncompliant. A - Android (Rooted / Bootloader‑Unlocked) Android models differ. The reliable fix is to return to stock (official) firmware, then re‑enroll with Intune. A1) Back up your data • Google One Backup: Settings → Google → Backup → Back up now (plus any manufacturer backup app). A2) Remove root and return to stock 1. If you used Magisk or similar: open the app → Uninstall / Restore images. 2. Relock the bootloader (if it was unlocked). Note: relocking typically erases all data. 3. Factory reset: Settings → System → Reset options → Erase all data (factory reset). 4. After reset, if Company Portal still detects root/unlock, contact IT. (Flashing vendor firmware can be complex and device‑specific; only proceed if IT instructs.) A3) Update Android • Settings → System → System update → install all updates. A4) Re‑enroll with Intune (Company Portal) BYOD (Work Profile) — most personal devices: 1. Open Google Play → install Company Portal. 2. Open Company Portal → Sign in with your work email. If Multi-Factor Authentication (MFA) is already enforced for your account, you may be prompted to approve the sign-in via Authenticator at this point. If Authenticator is not installed, it may redirect you to install it before completing the signin. 3. Tap Begin → Set up work profile → review privacy info → Activate. 4. Wait while Android creates a Work profile. When prompted, allow required permissions. 5. In Company Portal: Devices → select your device → tap Check device settings until it shows Compliant. Corporate‑owned (Fully Managed/Device Owner) - if your company gave you the phone: 1. Factory reset (if the device is not brand‑new). 2. On the setup screen, connect to Wi‑Fi, then enroll using the QR code / DPC token provided by your IT team (this starts Android Enterprise enrollment). 3. Complete Company Portal prompts → open Company Portal → Check device settings until Compliant. If Noncompliant persists on Android: • Open Company Portal → Check device settings / Refresh. • Restart the phone. • Ensure Play Protect is on; Developer options off; Bootloader is Locked (About phone → build number taps to reveal Dev options; if unlocked, you must lock it). • Remove stale device record: Company Portal → Devices → select old entries → Remove → then sign out/in and re‑enroll. Need Help? (Add your details) • Email: kone.mdmadmin@kone.com • 07:00 –19:00 EET, Mon–Fri • Include: your full name, work email, device make/model, OS version, and a screenshot of the message in Company Portal.

short_description: How to analyze the integration issue in the Kone Online
introduction: Step 1: First check for which field the error message is present. Step 2: Check the value for that field or check if field/ record is present in the salesforce and related records of record is present. Step 3: If the record is not present then check with the source system. If a record is available and sent to the salesforce. If record is unavailable, then inform the user same. Step 4: If the record has successfully been sent to salesforce, check with middleware system (DML, Mulesoft ) there was any failure or connectivity issue at that time. Or have they received any error response from salesforce. Step 5: Find the root cause based on analysis. Example: Prod_DML_Custom_Log_Error_Automation - dml-s-maintenanceschedules-sfdc 400 statusCode: 400 success: false errors: [ { "message": "Foreign Key external ID: <ID> not found for field Equipment_SAP_Number__c in entity Asset", "errorCode": "INVALID_FIELD", "fields": [] } ] This error typically occurs when a maintenance schedule is created, but the related Equipment record cannot be found in Salesforce. Root Cause: The error is caused due to a mismatch or missing reference between Equipment and Maintenance Schedule records. Common reasons include: 1. Equipment record not found in Salesforce for the equipment Number given. 2. Timing mismatch – Maintenance schedule created before the Equipment record was created. 3. Data transmission issue – Equipment data not properly sent from the source system (e.g., SAP) to Salesforce. Resolution Steps: Step 1: Verify Equipment Record Search Salesforce for the Equipment_SAP_Number__c mentioned in the error. If not found, request the SAP team to understand the reason behind the missing equipment and to resend the equipment data. Step 2: Check Record Creation Dates If the Equipment record exists, compare the Equipment Creation Date with the Maintenance Schedule Creation Date. If the maintenance schedule was created earlier, it would fail due to the missing dependency. Reach out to surce system, the SAP team to understand the reason for delay. Step 3: Validate Data Transmission If both records are present, engage the source system team (SAP/middleware) to confirm: Was the equipment data sent before the maintenance schedule? Was there any failure during data transfer? Corrective Actions: If equipment is missing → Get the SAP team to re-trigger equipment data. If schedule created before equipment → Re-trigger the maintenance schedule once equipment is available. If data transmission error → Coordinate with the integration team to fix and resend data. Preventive Measures: Ensure equipment creation jobs run before maintenance schedule jobs. Monitor data sync logs between SAP and Salesforce to detect failures early.

short_description: How to indetify if the user request is bug or new requirement in the Kone Online and how to handle it.
introduction: Steps: Step 1: Validate Against Current Functionality Review the supporting documents and existing functionality. Check if the requested feature already exists. Step 2: If It Is a Bug Analyze the issue to identify the reason for missing or non-functional features. Record the findings and raise Jira ticket to the solution team for permanent fix of the issue. Step 3: If It Is a New Requirement Confirm that the requested functionality is not part of the current system. Inform the CO and Service Manager about the request. Advise the user to submit the requirement formally (via Service Manager) and CC the CO in the communication. Example Ticket Number: INC00515272: KOL reporting - Include Site Name is Elevator performance report. Root Cause Categories: 1. Bug – The requested functionality already exists in the current system but is not working as expected or is not visible to the user. 2. New Requirement – The requested functionality does not exist in the current system and must be added as an enhancement. Corrective Actions: Bug: Escalate to the solution team for resolution with analysis. New Requirement: Redirect the request to the requirement/change management process. Preventive Measures: Maintain updated functional documentation for reference. Educate users on how to differentiate between issues (bugs) and enhancements. Establish a clear communication process for handling new requirements

short_description: How to handle data visibility issue in the Kone Online.
introduction: Step 1: check user has right permission to see the record. Step 2: check correct data present in the KOL. Verify the data with the source system. Step 3: Correct the information. Example Ticket Numbers: INC00557438: The user was missing permissions. INC00516681: Please check why the home page shows different info for 24/7 equipment. Root Cause Categories: 1. Permission Issue – User does not have the correct access rights to view 24/7 equipment. 2. Data Mismatch – Count of 24/7 equipment in Salesforce and Kone Online does not match. 3. Single Equipment Issue – Problem isolated to one equipment record only. 4. Incorrect 24/7 Flag – Equipment is not marked as 24/7 in Salesforce or SAP data. Resolution Steps: Step 1: Verify User Permissions Confirm which user is facing the issue. Check if the user has all required permissions to view 24/7 equipment in Salesforce. Step 2: Validate Equipment Count Run query to check number of 24/7 equipment for the account in Salesforce: SELECT Id, Asset.Name, Asset.Id, Data_Service__c FROM ContractLineItem WHERE AccountId = <accountId> AND Data_Service__c = '24/7 Connect' AND Asset.Status = 'Active' Compare the count with Kone Online. If counts match → Inform user that data is consistent. If counts differ → Proceed to Step 3. Step 3: Isolate Single Equipment Issue Run query for the specific equipment by filtering on Equipment Number. If issue exists only for one equipment, ask SAP team to verify the status of the equipment. Step 4: Validate 24/7 Flag with SAP Check whether the equipment is flagged as 24/7 in SAP. If characteristic value = 1 → Equipment is considered 24/7. Then ask the SAP team to re-trigger the service contract. If not, inform the user to reach out to the local SAP Team to get data corrected in the SAP and re-push it. Corrective Actions: If permissions missing → Update user permissions. If Salesforce and Kone Online mismatch → Align data via SAP team. If equipment not flagged correctly → Correct characteristic value in SAP. Preventive Measures: Regularly validate Salesforce 24/7 equipment data against SAP. Establish alerts for mismatched counts between Salesforce and Kone Online. Educate support team to use queries for faster troubleshooting.

short_description: How to handle data sync issues in the SMT.
introduction: Resolution Steps: Step 1: Gather User Information Identify the user(s) facing the issue. Confirm their service territory. Log in to SMT3 and check if you are able to replicate the issue. Step 2: Understand the Issue in Detail Get on a call with the user(s). Identify which tile(s) of the dashboard are not displaying data. Capture a detailed explanation of the issue. Step 3: Validate Dashboard Query Inspect the dashboard page in SMT3 → Check the query used to fetch the data. If a query is not visible, reach out to Sandesh Y for support. Step 4: Simulate User Access in Salesforce Log in as the user in Salesforce. Run the dashboard query in Salesforce Inspector, Data Loader, or Workbench. Validate if the user can see all the required data. Check if the user has access to all fields used in the query filter. If not → Inform the user, obtain appropriate approvals, and provide required field access. Step 5: Validate Salesforce Data If data is still missing or incorrect in Salesforce: Reach out to the source system team. Analyze the synchronization flow. Work with them to identify and resolve the root cause. Root Cause Categories: 1. User Access Issue – User does not have permission to view all fields used in the dashboard query. 2. Dashboard Query Issue – The query behind the SMT3 dashboard may not be fetching correct data. 3. Data Issue in Salesforce – Data is missing or incorrect in Salesforce. 4. Source System Issue – Synchronization issue between Salesforce and the source system. Example: Ticket Number: INC00529426, INC00511063: We have received several reports from our maintenance supervisors that the dashboard in SMT3 is not synchronizing. Corrective Actions: If user access is missing → Provide access after required approvals. If data issue → Get the background query with the help of Sandesh Y. and check if user can see same information. If data mismatch → Escalate to source system team for resolution. Preventive Measures: Maintain updated documentation of queries used in dashboards. Regularly review user access to ensure required fields are available. Monitor SMT3–Salesforce sync logs to proactively detect failures.

short_description: How to handle data related issues in the SMT.
introduction: Resolution Steps: 1. Verify Source Data Check with Konect and confirm the latest status of the equipment. 2. Check Integration Flow Confirm if the update was triggered from Konect to MuleSoft. Review MuleSoft logs to ensure data packets were successfully transmitted to Salesforce. 3. Validate Destination System Check whether Salesforce received and updated the data correctly. Identify if the record failed due to mapping or field update restrictions. 4. User Confirmation Align with the reporting user to confirm whether the status now reflects correctly. Example: Ticket Number: INC00522653, INC00518512: SMT3 shows invalid data regarding an out of order equipment Root Cause Categories: 1. Source System Data Issues Incorrect or missing status in Konect. 2. Integration/Transmission Issues Delay or failure in sending updates from Konect to Salesforce. Errors in integration layer (MuleSoft) while transmitting data. 3. Destination System Issues Data mapping errors in Salesforce/SMT3. Record update failure due to sync error or communication breakdown. Corrective Actions: Re-trigger the failed data update from Konect. Manually correct/update the data in Salesforce/SMT3 after COO approval. Preventive Measures: Monitoring Enhancements: Implement automated monitoring on MuleSoft to capture and alert on failed transactions. Data Validation: Set up reconciliation checks between Konect and Salesforce to highlight mismatches. User Training: Provide guidance to users on how to verify data accuracy before escalation. Process Improvements: Establish periodic sync validations between source and destination systems.

short_description: Similar Incidents Suggestion for Agents via Predictive Intelligence
introduction: This Knowledge Article will help you to understand how Predictive Intelligence in ServiceNow helps to automatically display the below options in Service Operation Workspace for ITIL user/Support Agents: Similar Open Incidents Similar Resolved Incidents
instructions: What’s New? Using Predictive Intelligence, ServiceNow will now automatically display the below options in Service Operation Workspace for ITIL user/Support Agents: Similar Open Incidents Similar Resolved Incidents This feature is integrated directly into the agent workspace view, allowing agents to see relevant incident suggestions in real-time while working on a case. Key Benefits: Faster Resolution Times – Agents can quickly identify patterns and leverage resolutions from past incidents. Improved Accuracy – Ensures consistent issue handling by referencing similar, previously resolved incidents. Enhanced Agent Productivity – Reduces manual effort in searching the incident database. How It Works: When an agent opens or works on an incident, the system uses machine learning models to analyze the incident details and surface a list of similar incidents – both currently open and resolved – directly in the Service Operation workspace View. This assists agents in making more informed decisions and accelerates the overall resolution process. We believe this enhancement will significantly improve the support experience for both agents and end users. Please refer to the below user guide for agents: Agent Assist with Predictive Intelligence for Similar Incidents.pdf

short_description: Patricia New SSL Certificate
introduction: Patricia New SSL Certificate
instructions: Instructions: To get the New Patricia SSL Certificate the Following steps need to be followed: Pre Requisite: Step 1: To obtain the Patricia SSL certificate, the first step is to raise a request. Step 2: To apply the request, Click on Self - > Enter mail id -> and click on Renew Existing Certificate. Step 3: Clicking on this KBA will guide you through the process of obtaining to add the CSR attachment. NOTE: Since the server name, common name, and path name are the same, we are using last year’s configuration. The patenting is also same, so we replaced only one of the four certificates. Step 4: To get the CSR certificate go to previous year folder (i.e. 202411) Go to This Pc -> D drive -> DBShare ->202411 Step 5: Go to This Pc -> D drive -> DBShare ->202411 -> patenting_konenet_com. Step 6: Copy the patenting_konenet_com file, save it to the Transfer on Privx, then download the file and upload it to the request. Step 7: Now Click on "Order now" and the request will be sent. INSTRUCTIONS: To get the New Patricia SSL Certificate Step 1: Login into the EUHCIMSSPTX069 (Patricia) Server . Step 2: Go to This PC -> Transfer on Privx copy all the files. Step 3: Go to This Pc -> D drive -> DBShare -> create a folder for e.g.202509 and paste the files. Step 4: Delete all the files in the Transfer on Privx Step 5: Go to This Pc -> D drive -> DBShare ->202411 Step 6: Copy the PrivateKey file and Patenting_konenet_com file and save it in the new folder ( i.e. 202509 ) Step 7: Copy the JKS file from the previous year's folder (e.g.,202411) and paste it into the current year's folder (e.g.,202509) and modify the folder. Step 8: Paste the JKS file and open it. Step 9: Go to This Pc -> D drive -> DBShare -> Pass Copy the password. Step 10: Now paste the password. Step 11: By entering the password, the certificate's expiry date will be displayed. Step 12: Click on "Import key pair type" and click on "PKCS #8" and click on "OK". Step 13: Enter the "Password" from Pass file and in the PKCS #* Private Key File Field: click on browse and paste the "PrivateKey" File and in the Certificate File: Click on the browse and paste the Certificate file. Step 14: Copy and paste the password and click on "OK". Step 15: A popup message will occurs click on "OK" and save it. Step 16: Now new JKS file is Generated.

short_description: How to Log Into PDM with an Admin Account
introduction: Some administrative tasks cannot be assigned to a regular PDM end user account. Instead, a dedicated admin account shall be used. This article describe how to log in with such admin account.
instructions: Prerequisite: Make sure you have one or more of the below MyAccess role assigned: PDM: Administrator PDM: Product Data Administrator These roles will create an EntraID Admin account automatically. Along with the account creation, you will receive the initial password. Instructions: Web Client When logging into PDM, you will be prompted to pick an account. If you are already logged into PDM with your ordinary account, log out first (or use a private window). In case you need to log in with your secondary (admin) account, select "use another account" Enter your admin account name. The account name is in the format "admin-", your email account without @kone.com and instead "@kone.onmicrosoft.com" Select "Next" and provide your admin account's password. Your initial password has been sent to you via email at the time of account creation. In case you forgot your password, you can reset it via "Forgot my password" If this is the first time you are using this account, you will be prompted to change your password and set up multi-factor authentication (MFA) Choose your preferred authentication method. We recommend Microsoft Authenticator. Follow the MFA set up steps depending on your selection. Select "Sign in" and you will be logged into PDM with your admin account. Remember to log out again once you are done with your administrative tasks. VariClient Note : ENVIRONMENT HOST PORT Auth URL Auth Type SSL Engineering PDM Production e.pdm-prod.aws.kone.com 10000 https://pdm-prod.aws.kone.com/sso/variclient JWT Tick the checkbox Delivery PDM Production d.pdm-prod.aws.kone.com 10000 https://pdm-prod.aws.kone.com/sso/variclient JWT Tick the checkbox Steps: Step 1: Login Variclient (latest Version) Step 2 : Click add button and setup the database. Step 3 : Click the Ok" button to save the database. Step 4 : Now choose the added database and click the "Login" button. Step 5: Click "Use another account" Step 6: Enter your admin account User name and Password Step 7 : Type the Number in your Authenticator mobile App Step 8 : You can able to login WebAPI WebAPI is only able to get the authentication token for AD-based accounts. Since these admin accounts are only in EntraID, a workaround is requried to get the token. 1. Use a web browser to log into PDM with your admin account (refer to the instructions above regarding web client) 2. Get the value for cookie "Variantum_JWT" - Select Ctrl+Shift+I to open the inspector - In the network tab (depending on the browser), find the value for cookie "VARIANTUM_JWT" 3. Open command line tool (e.g. cygwin) 4. Update value of parameter MY_JWT_TOKEN (MY_JWT_TOKEN=eyJhbGciOiJSUzI1xxx) 5. Execute the WebAPI command: curl --header "Cookie: VARIANTUM_JWT=${MY_JWT_TOKEN}" --silent \ --location https://pdm-qa.aws.kone.com/varipdmapi_epdm_qa/schema/database \ | jq --raw-output '"Database,\(.databaseName) Server version,\(.serverVersion | split(",") | .[0] ) Database version,\(.databaseVersion) Server start time,\(.serverStartTime) User Id,\(.userId)"' \ | sed 's/^[ \t]*//' | column --table --separator ','

short_description: Creating PDM Admin Accounts
introduction: In order to separate elevated privileges from ordinary user accounts, some administrative privileges in PDM shall be granted to a secondary account. These privileges are managed via the MyAccess roles below. Due to the fact that PDM does not support managing users directly via Active Directory, accounts (both ordinary and admin) need to be created directly in PDM. Ordinary users can use the User Registration form to request the PDM account, but this shall not be used for admin accounts. Instead, a manual provisioning process via AskIT ticket is initiated once the MyAccess role has been approved. This KBA gives instructions on how to proceed once the manual provisioning ticket has been received. MyAccess roles: PDM: Administrator (uses EntraID Admin Account) - this role provides complete admin access to the data in the PDM. This could be granted only for the PDM IT team PDM: Product Data Administrator (uses EntraID Admin Account) - this role provides admin access over ACG in the PDM. This could be granted for business users tPDM: Administrator (uses Active Directory T2-Account)
instructions: Prerequisite: Manual provisioning ticket received Instructions: Open VariClient and log into ePDM Prod Check, if the relevant user's admin account exists already. If yes, skip to step 5. Select Create - User... Select type "User" and give these mandatory attributes (NOTE: for tPDM: Administrator use the user's T2-account): Object ID (EntraID User Principal Name, format: "admin-firstname.lastname@koneonmicrosoft.com") Full Name (<Firstname> <Lastname> Admin) Email (user's regular email address) Maximum document number (default: 25) [EntraID Object ID (EntraID Object ID)]* 6. Department 7. Country 8. Kone contact person Please collect the needed information from the PDM Normal accounts if needed. Note : Entra ID Object ID Attribute value Mandatory to login the Admin account in PDM. Please Login Azure with the below link and verify the Value and update to the Admin accounts. *PDM has an option to cross-check EntraID users against EntraID Object ID. If this option is enabled, the Entra ID Object ID attribute will be visible in the user profile. If enabled, it is required to provide this attribute value for Entra ID admin accounts. You can find User Principal Name and EntraID Object ID from the user's EntraID profile via Azure Portal . 5. Assign the relevant privilege via Granted privileges (administrator or product_data_administrator) based on the granted MyAccess role. Note : If product_data_administrator role ticket came --> Provide both product_data_administrator and master_system_administrator privilege also. If system_administrator role came --> Provide system_administrator privilege alone. 6. Select "X" as password. This will enforce Active Directory password. Save the new user. 7. Resolve the AskIT ticket

short_description: MyAccess Ticket Handling Process for Service Need manager Tickets - Role Addition
introduction: Instruction to L2 to understand the Process of handling Service Need Manager role addition MyAccess tickets.
instructions: Approval : No need of Separate Approval because MyAccess ticket triggered after the approval process done. Steps : Step 1) Once MyAccess role was approved, PDM team received the ticket like below. Step 2) Open the User account in PDM and click Edit button to edit the System attributes. Step 3) Add Service_need_manager Privilege in the Granted Privilege attributes for the User's Account Step 4) Click save to save the changes.

short_description: MyAccess Ticket Handling Process for Service Need manager Tickets - Role Removal
introduction: Instruction to L2 to understand the Process of handling Service Need Manager role removal MyAccess tickets.
instructions: Approval : No need of Separate Approval because MyAccess ticket triggered after the approval process done. Steps : Step 1) Once MyAccess role was requested and completed, PDM team will receive the provision ticket like below. Step 2) Open the User account in PDM and click Edit button to edit the System attributes. Step 3) Remove the Service_need_manager Privilege in the Granted Privilege attributes for the User's Account. Step 4) Click save to save the changes.

short_description: SPD - How to remove tick from SourcingBudget checkbox in a project
introduction: SPD - How to remove tick from SourcingBudget checkbox in a project
instructions: Login the SPD Database Test: 10.41.148.235\SEKO60Q,51018 Prod: 10.39.67.11\SEKO021P,51001 Use the below 3 tables to check the SourcingBudget checkbox data. select * from [ProjectsOverview] where Code = 'ELK6562' select * from [YearlyFigures] where ProjectUnitID = 275142 select * from [ProjectsMonthlyFigures] where ProjectID = 63666 To remove the tick from SourcingBudget checkbox from the project use the below 3 update queries. update [ProjectsOverview] set BudgetedAction = 0 where Code = 'ELK6562' update [ProjectsMonthlyFigures] set SourcingBudget = 0 where ProjectID = 63666 update [YearlyFigures] set BudgetedAction = 0 where ID in ( 1098621 , 1098622 )

short_description: The code execution cannot proceed because MSVCR110.dll was not found - Autodesk applications
introduction: “The code execution cannot proceed because MSVCR110.dll was not found” (or similar wording) means that your system is missing a Microsoft Visual C++ Redistributable file that your program depends on.
instructions: Resolution for the Issue: To address this problem, it is advised to uninstall the affected application and reinstall it using the CQI installer. Steps to Resolve: Uninstall the software from your system. Reinstall it manually using the CQI installer. For detailed instructions on the uninstallation and reinstallation process, please refer to the relevant Knowledge Base Article (KBA) provided. Reference KBA: KB000A1647

short_description: ProgeCAD Crash Handler Issue.
introduction: ProgeCAD Crash Handler Issue: (Issue while Logging in the ProgeCAD)
instructions: The mentioned Dialog box may occur when the Logging into the ProgeCAD Case 1: 1.Open ProgeCAD Centre 2025. Search for the Application ProgeCAD Center 2025 in Taskbar Then Run the Application and Run as administrator and enter the admin Credentials. 2.Need to Repair the applications. After filling the Admin Credentials. The mentioned Window will appear on the Screen. Then select the option Support Tools. After selecting an option support tools > Click an option Repair. 3.Reset the Application The dialog box appears to restart the application ProgeCAD. Click yes. Then select the following options before resetting. Reset type should be Full reset. BACKUP settings and files before resetting should be enabled. Profiles to reset should be All profiles. Click on start reset Select the location accordingly for the Backup files. Then accept the following option to complete the Backup and reset the application. Hence after completion of the process the ProgeCAD will be reset Successfully. Open the ProgeCAD and check the application. Case 2: Since after following the Case 1 process after resetting the ProgeCAD application and error Appears. If Case 1 process could not fix the issue then follow the Case 2 Process which is to Uninstall and Install the ProgeCAD application. 1.Uninstallation process of ProgeCAD application. Uninstall ProgeCAD Open ProgeCAD Centre 2025. Search for the Application ProgeCAD Center 2025 in Taskbar Then Run the Application and Run as administrator and enter the admin Credentials Select an option as Utility and then select the option as Uninstall ProgeCAD Click yes in the dialog box for the permission to proceed uninstallation. Then wait for the Uninstallation of application to complete and also provide the permissions as YES for all the permissions to Complete the ProgeCAD Uninstallation. 2.Then after completion of ProgeCAD Uninstallation Successfully the the dialog box appears as a Confirmation. Once after the completion of uninstallation successfully from the machine user can not find the application in the Taskbar. 3.Reinstallation of ProgeCAD application. Again, to reinstall the application need to run all the policies via Control panel. To run the policies, Open the Control panel Select the Configuration Manager. Select Option as Actions then click the each option and Click as Run now. After completion of run all the policies via Control panel. 4.Software Centre to Complete the Installation. Open the Software Centre then search for the Application as ProgeCAD then Open the Application and then Install Hence, after Completion of Installation of an Application this issue will be resolved.

short_description: KEO - Clone material catalogue newly from one unit and to other unit
instructions: Modify the below queries with the correct unit id and run the queries for the tables one by one for inserting the material catalogue. Note: If we don't get the details with the name then there might be space issue in the text. TO check that go to the database 'EasyOrder'. Then expand 'Tables'. Then right click on 'Mat.ProductRange'. And select Edit top 100 Rows. It will list down the contents of the table. Go to the expected unit's product range and copy the 'Name' column and run by passing that name. Step 1: Run the below query. declare @FromUnitID int=12 declare @ToUnitID int=94 declare @name nvarchar(max)='General Accessories' declare @ToProductRangeID int Declare @i int=1 Declare @Count int insert into mat.ProductRange(name,FK_Unit) select name,@ToUnitID from MAT.ProductRange where fk_unit=@FromUnitID and [Name]=@name Step 2: Run the below query declare @FromUnitID int=12 declare @ToUnitID int=94 declare @name nvarchar(max)='General Accessories' declare @ToProductRangeID int Declare @i int=1 Declare @Count int create table #ProductRangerole ( ID int Identity(1,1), ProductRangeID int, ProductRangeName nvarchar(max), ProductRangeID_Norway int, RoleID int ) insert into #ProductRangerole(ProductRangeID,RoleID) select FK_ProductRange,FK_Role from MAT.ProductRangerole where fk_productrange in(select pk_productrange from MAT.ProductRange where fk_unit=@FromUnitID and Name=@name) UPDATE #ProductRangerole SET #ProductRangerole.ProductRangeName = PR.Name from #ProductRangerole PRR inner join mat.ProductRange PR on PRR.ProductRangeID=PR.PK_ProductRange and PR.FK_Unit=@FromUnitID UPDATE #ProductRangerole SET #ProductRangerole.ProductRangeID_Norway = PR.PK_ProductRange from #ProductRangerole PRR inner join mat.ProductRange PR on PRR.ProductRangeName=PR.Name and PR.FK_Unit=@ToUnitID Declare @ProductRange int Declare @ProductRange_Norway int Declare @Role int Select @Count=count(*) from #ProductRangerole While @i<=@Count Begin Select @ProductRange=ProductRangeID_Norway,@Role=RoleID from #ProductRangerole where ID=@i if(select count(*) from mat.ProductRangeRole where FK_ProductRange=@ProductRange and FK_Role=@Role)>1 begin delete from #ProductRangerole where ID=@i end Set @i=@i+1 End insert into MAT.ProductRangerole(fk_productrange,fk_role) select ProductRangeID_Norway,RoleID from #ProductRangerole drop table #ProductRangerole Step 3: Run the below query declare @FromUnitID int=12 declare @ToUnitID int=94 declare @name nvarchar(max)='General Accessories' declare @ToProductRangeID int Declare @i int=1 Declare @Count int create table #category ( ID int identity(1,1), ProductRange int, ProductRangeName nvarchar(max), CategoryName nvarchar(max), ProductRange_Norway int, ) insert into #category(ProductRange,CategoryName) select FK_ProductRange,name from mat.category where fk_unit=@FromUnitID and fk_productrange in(select PK_ProductRange from MAT.ProductRange where fk_unit=@FromUnitID and Name=@name) UPDATE #category SET #category.ProductRangeName = PR.Name from #category c inner join mat.ProductRange PR on c.ProductRange=PR.PK_ProductRange and PR.FK_Unit=@FromUnitID UPDATE #category SET #category.ProductRange_Norway = PR.PK_ProductRange from #category c inner join mat.ProductRange PR on c.ProductRangeName=PR.Name and PR.FK_Unit=@ToUnitID Declare @CategoryName nvarchar(max) Declare @ProductRange int Select @i=1,@Count=count(*) from #category While @i<=@Count Begin Select @ProductRange=ProductRange_Norway,@CategoryName=CategoryName from #category where ID=@i if(select count(*) from mat.Category where FK_ProductRange=@ProductRange and Name=@CategoryName and FK_Unit=@ToUnitID)>1 begin delete from #category where ID=@i end Set @i=@i+1 End insert into mat.Category(Name,FK_ProductRange,FK_Unit) select CategoryName,ProductRange_Norway,@ToUnitID from #category drop table #category Step 4: Run the below query declare @FromUnitID int=12 declare @ToUnitID int=94 declare @name nvarchar(max)='General Accessories' declare @ToProductRangeID int Declare @i int=1 Declare @Count int select @ToProductRangeID=PK_ProductRange from MAT.ProductRange where fk_unit=@ToUnitID and Name=@name create table #categoryrole ( ID int identity(1,1), CategoryName nvarchar(max), CategoryID_Denmark int, CategoryID_Norway int, RoleID int ) insert into #categoryrole(CategoryID_Denmark,RoleID,CategoryName) select fk_category,fk_role,c.Name from mat.categoryrole CR left join mat.category C on CR.fk_category=C.PK_Category where fk_category in(select pk_category from mat.category where fk_unit=@FromUnitID and fk_productrange in(select PK_ProductRange from MAT.ProductRange where fk_unit=@FromUnitID and Name=@name)) UPDATE #categoryrole SET #categoryrole.CategoryID_Norway = C.PK_Category from #categoryrole cr inner join mat.category c on cr.CategoryName=c.Name and c.FK_ProductRange=@ToProductRangeID and c.FK_Unit=@ToUnitID Declare @Category int Declare @role int Select @i=1,@Count=count(*) from #categoryrole While @i<=@Count Begin Select @Category=CategoryID_Norway,@role=RoleID from #categoryrole where ID=@i if(select count(*) from mat.CategoryRole where FK_Category=@Category and FK_Role=@role)>1 begin delete from #categoryrole where ID=@i end Set @i=@i+1 End insert into mat.categoryrole (fk_category,fk_role) select categoryid_norway,roleid from #categoryrole drop table #categoryrole Step 5: Run the below query declare @FromUnitID int=12 declare @ToUnitID int=94 declare @name nvarchar(max)='General Accessories' declare @ToProductRangeID int Declare @i int=1 Declare @Count int select @ToProductRangeID=PK_ProductRange from MAT.ProductRange where fk_unit=@ToUnitID and Name=@name create table #SubCategory ( ID int identity(1,1), SubCategoryName nvarchar(max), CategoryID_Denmark int, CategoryName_Denmark nvarchar(max), CategoryID_Norway int ) insert into #SubCategory(SubCategoryName,CategoryID_Denmark) select Name,FK_Category from mat.subcategory where fk_unit=@FromUnitID and fk_category in(select pk_category from mat.category where fk_unit=@FromUnitID and fk_productrange in(select PK_ProductRange from MAT.ProductRange where fk_unit=@FromUnitID and name=@name)) UPDATE #SubCategory SET #SubCategory.CategoryName_Denmark = C.Name from #SubCategory cr inner join mat.category c on cr.CategoryID_Denmark=c.PK_Category UPDATE #SubCategory SET #SubCategory.CategoryID_Norway = C.PK_Category from #SubCategory cr inner join mat.category c on cr.CategoryName_Denmark=c.Name and c.FK_ProductRange=@ToProductRangeID and c.FK_Unit=@ToUnitID Declare @Category int Declare @SubCategory nvarchar(max) Select @i=1,@Count=count(*) from #SubCategory While @i<=@Count Begin Select @Category=CategoryID_Norway,@SubCategory=SubCategoryName from #SubCategory where ID=@i if(select count(*) from mat.subcategory where FK_Category=@Category and fk_unit=@ToUnitID and Name=@SubCategory)>1 begin delete from #SubCategory where ID=@i end Set @i=@i+1 End insert into mat.subcategory (name,fk_category,fk_unit) select SubCategoryName,CategoryID_Norway,@ToUnitID from #SubCategory drop table #SubCategory Step 6: Run the query declare @FromUnitID int=12 declare @ToUnitID int=94 declare @name nvarchar(max)='General Accessories' declare @ToProductRangeID int Declare @i int=1 Declare @Count int select @ToProductRangeID=PK_ProductRange from MAT.ProductRange where fk_unit=@ToUnitID and Name=@name create table #SubCategoryRole ( ID int identity(1,1), SubCategoryID_Denmark int, SubCategoryName_Denmark nvarchar(max), SubCategoryID_Norway int, RoleID int ) insert into #SubCategoryRole(SubCategoryID_Denmark,RoleID,SubCategoryName_Denmark) select FK_SubCategory,FK_Role,c.Name from mat.subcategoryrole SR left join mat.SubCategory C on SR.FK_SubCategory=C.PK_SubCategory where fk_subcategory in(select pk_subcategory from mat.subcategory where fk_unit=@FromUnitID and fk_category in(select pk_category from mat.category where fk_unit=@FromUnitID and fk_productrange in(select PK_ProductRange from MAT.ProductRange where fk_unit=@FromUnitID and name=@name))) UPDATE #SubCategoryRole SET #SubCategoryRole.SubCategoryID_Norway = C.PK_SubCategory from #SubCategoryRole cr inner join mat.SubCategory c on cr.SubCategoryName_Denmark=c.Name and c.FK_Unit=@ToUnitID and c.FK_Category in(select pk_category from mat.category where fk_unit=@ToUnitID and fk_productrange in(@ToProductRangeID)) Declare @SubCategory int Declare @RoleID int Select @i=1,@Count=count(*) from #SubCategoryRole While @i<=@Count Begin Select @SubCategory=SubCategoryID_Norway,@RoleID=RoleID from #SubCategoryRole where ID=@i if(select count(*) from mat.SubCategoryRole where FK_SubCategory=@SubCategory and FK_Role=@RoleID)>1 begin delete from #SubCategoryRole where ID=@i end Set @i=@i+1 End insert into mat.SubCategoryRole(FK_SubCategory,FK_Role) select SubCategoryID_Norway,RoleID from #SubCategoryRole drop table #SubCategoryRole Step 7: Runthe below query declare @FromUnitID int=12 declare @ToUnitID int=94 declare @name nvarchar(max)='General Accessories' declare @ToProductRangeID int Declare @i int=1 Declare @Count int select @ToProductRangeID=PK_ProductRange from MAT.ProductRange where fk_unit=@ToUnitID and Name=@name create table #Product ( ID int identity(1,1), ProductName nvarchar(max), Picture nvarchar(max), UniID int, TechnicalGroupId nvarchar(max) ) insert into #Product(ProductName,Picture,UniID,TechnicalGroupId) select Name,Replace(Picture,sUBSTRING(Picture,0,4),(select [KEY] from glb.unit where pk_unit=@ToUnitID)),@ToUnitID,TechnicalGroupId from MAT.Product WHERE PK_Product in(select FK_Product from MAT.ProductDetails where FK_SubCategory in(select PK_SubCategory from mat.subcategory where fk_unit=@FromUnitID and fk_category in(select pk_category from mat.category where fk_unit=@FromUnitID and fk_productrange in(select PK_ProductRange from MAT.ProductRange where fk_unit=@FromUnitID and
