Step-by-step guide to set up Windows Hello for Business (WHFB), change or reset PINs, and troubleshoot common sign-in issues on Windows 10/11 devices.
introduction: Windows Hello for Business (WHFB) provides secure, passwordless sign-in using PIN, facial recognition, or fingerprint authentication. This article explains how to set up Windows Hello, change or reset your PIN, and resolve common login and configuration issues related to WHFB.
instructions: Windows Hello Setup, PIN Reset, and Troubleshooting Guide Follow the below steps based on the issue — Setup, PIN Change, PIN Reset, or Troubleshooting. A. Windows PIN Change 1. If the user is locked out and unable to log in using PIN: Advise the user to log in using an alternative method (e.g., password). Once logged in, follow these steps: Go to Settings > Accounts > Sign-in options Click PIN (Windows Hello) and select Change PIN Enter the old PIN and set a new one 2. If the user is already logged in: Go to Settings > Accounts > Sign-in options Click PIN (Windows Hello) and select Change PIN Enter the old PIN and set a new one B. Windows PIN Reset (Forgotten PIN) 1. If the user is locked out and unable to log in using PIN: Advise the user to log in using an alternative method (e.g., password). Once logged in, follow these steps: Go to Settings > Accounts > Sign-in options Click PIN (Windows Hello) and select I forgot my PIN Approve the MFA notification for verification Set a new PIN 2. If the user is already logged in: Go to Settings > Accounts > Sign-in options Click PIN (Windows Hello) and select I forgot my PIN Approve the MFA notification for verification Set a new PIN C. WHFB Setup for the First Time Go to Settings > Accounts > Sign-in options Choose a method such as Facial Recognition, Fingerprint Recognition, or PIN Click Set up and follow the on-screen instructions D. WHFB Options Greyed Out or Unavailable Verify if the user’s device is part of the AAD group: Co-mgmt-Win11-Windows Hello for Business-PROD If the options remain greyed out, escalate the case to the RDS team to review and add the device to the required group. E. WHFB for Application Logins Issue: When a user tries to sign in to intranet sites or applications requiring authentication, the PIN/Fingerprint prompt appears repeatedly even after validation. Steps to resolve: Clear and reset the browser cache. Try signing in using alternate credentials (ID & Password). Check in Azure > User > Authentication methods > Usable Authentication Methods if WHFB is added. If the issue persists, escalate to the RDS team for further investigation.

short_description: Unable to access LSP Files in AutoCAD
introduction: Unable to access LSP Files in AutoCAD This document outlines the procedure for uploading LSP (AutoLISP) files in AutoCAD. Follow the steps below to ensure the files are properly added and trusted within the AutoCAD environment.
instructions: 1. Open AutoCAD. 2. Type 'OPTIONS' in the command line and press Enter. 3. In the Options dialog box, go to the 'Files' tab. 4. Expand the 'Trusted Locations' section. 5. Click 'Add' . 6. Before proceeding, save all LSP files in a dedicated folder (Like trusted path) C:\Autodesk 7. Copy the path of the folder where the LSP files are saved. 8. Return to the 'Trusted Locations' section and paste the copied path. 9. Click 'Apply' and then 'OK' to close the dialog box. 10. Type 'APPLOAD' in the command line and press Enter. 11. In the Load/Unload Applications dialog box, navigate to the folder containing the LSP files. 12. Select the desired LSP files and click 'Load' . 13. Confirm that the files have been successfully loaded.

short_description: KONE Palette Issue in AutoCAD – Troubleshooting Guide
introduction: This article provides step-by-step instructions to resolve issues related to the missing or non-functional KONE Palette in AutoCAD.
instructions: Step 1: Verify Palette Availability Launch AutoCAD. In the command line, type: KPINFO and press Enter. If the KONE Palette is available, proceed to Step 2. Step 2: Reset AutoCAD Profile Settings Click Start on your Windows taskbar. Navigate to All Apps. Locate and expand the AutoCAD Subdivision folder. Click on Reset Settings to Default. In the prompt, select Back up and Reset Custom Settings. Choose a location (e.g., Documents) to save the backup file. Click ok to the Reset settings. You will be prompted for admin credentials; enter them to authorize the reset process. Step 3: Finalize and Verify Once the reset is complete, open AutoCAD. Close AutoCAD after the initial launch. Reopen AutoCAD and run the command: KPINFO Confirm if the KONE Palette is now available and functioning. Note: - Ensure you have administrative privileges before performing the reset. - Backing up custom settings is recommended to avoid loss of personalized configurations. - If the above steps do not resolve the issue, please uninstall and reinstall the application.

short_description: Patch Plot Issue – GhostScript Troubleshooting Guide
introduction: This article provides a step-by-step guide to resolve Patch Plot issues caused by missing or misconfigured GhostScript installation. Applicable to users experiencing Patch Plot errors due to GhostScript not being detected or installed correctly.
instructions: Step 1: Download GhostScript 1. Download the GhostScript from the Below Link: Link - https://kone.sharepoint.com/sites/SOLUTIONCREATION-ITEC/_layouts/15/download.aspx?UniqueId=13260f72%2Dcaa5%2D4b90%2Db952%2Da5f052198e0f Step 2: Install GhostScript Run the downloaded installer. By default, GhostScript will be installed in the following directory: C:\Program Files\gs\gs10.03.1\ Step 3: Add GhostScript to PATH If PatchPlot does not detect GhostScript automatically: Press Win + R or Run, type 'sysdm.cpl', and press Enter. Admin Credentials will prompted, enter Admin credentials to authorize the reset process. Navigate to the 'Advanced' tab and click 'Environment Variables'. Add the GhostScript bin path, Path - C:\Program Files\gs\gs10.03.1\bin Click OK and restart your PC. Step 4: Verify Installation Open Command Prompt. Run the following command: " gswin64c " If a version number is displayed, GhostScript is installed correctly.

short_description: Knowledge-Based Instruction: Resolving Plot Style Issues in AutoCAD
introduction: This document provides step-by-step instructions to resolve issues related to Plot Style files in AutoCAD. Ensure that the correct Plot Style file is available and properly configured in the AutoCAD settings.
instructions: Step 1: Verify Plot Style File Location Navigate to the following folder on your computer: C:\Users\<YourUsername>\AppData\Roaming\Autodesk\AutoCAD 2022\R24.1\enu\Plotters\Plot Styles Check if the required Plot Style file is present in this folder. If the file is missing, add the correct Plot Style file to this location. Step 2: Configure Printer Support File Path Open AutoCAD and type 'OPTIONS' in the command line. In the Options dialog box, go to the 'Files' tab. Expand the 'Printer Support File Path' section. short_description: ASKIT - How to apply for Github: Copilot Access from My Access portal?
introduction: How to apply for GitHub: Copilot Access from My Access portal?
instructions: Kindly follow the below steps to raise a request via MyAccess to get GitHub: Copilot Access. To obtain Copilot access, it is mandatory to first have GitHub: Member access. Please confirm whether you currently have GitHub: Member access. If you do not have it, kindly raise a request for GitHub: Member access first. Once this role is approved, you may then proceed to raise a request for Copilot access. 1. Login to MyAccess - https://myaccess.kone.com/identityiq/home.jsf . Select Manage User Access and then search for the user's name and select it by clicking the Tick icon and click on Next. NOTE: For some users the Manage User access tab will not be available. In that case click on Manage My Access and directly go the next step. 2. Please select the role “ GITHUB: Copilot ” by clicking the tick icon and proceed by clicking “Next.” Before submitting the form, kindly ensure that the attachment field is completed. It is mandatory to upload an Excel file containing the requested role details. The Excel should include standard information such as your GitHub ID and a brief business justification explaining the need for Copilot access. Once the request is submitted, approved, and successfully processed, you will be granted access to GitHub Copilot.

short_description: How to check the invoices for Egypt and raise ticket to Edicom portal
introduction: This KBA will help in understanding the invoicing flow for invoices for Egypt and also the role of Edicom in entire invoicing process. This also deals with Edicom vendor ticket creation process on the support portal.
instructions: Edicom vendor is engaged in Egypt invoicing process. The screenshot below suggests the flow if invoices for Egypt. Edicom Invoicing flow Flow of invoice between SAP, CPI, Esxtream and 1. The input for the invoices are entered in SAP. 2. The Idoc file is sent to Edicom and Exstream via CPI. 3. The Idoc which is received in Edicom, the vendor send the digitally signed document back to SAP. 4. The Idoc which is received by Exstream, they create the PDF layout of the invoice and send them back to SAP. 5. The invoices are not sent to customer via Exstream or Edicom, rather the invoices' PDF's are archived once sent from Exstream. Checking the issue with Edicom support team: The edicom support portal can be accessed with the URL MyEdicom Once the portal is accessed with the credentials, you will get the below screen. The support ticket option can be accessed via support button on the left side of the screen as highlighted below. The new ticket can be created with option create request; the existing open tickets can be tracked using the open requests and son on as shown in below highlighted screenshot. The new ticket can be raised with the create request option as shown below. We need to fill the details highlighted in the below screenshot and also we can attach the documents if needed. Once all the details are filled and submitted, we receive a ticket number which can be used to track the case further. Thanks, Abhishek

short_description: Mac Device Enrollment – User Guide
introduction: Prerequisites & What You Need 1. Device should enrolled in ABM (Apple Business Manager) 2. A new or reset Mac device 3. macOS Version Compatibility later recommended 4. Required open Internet access LAN or Mobile Hotspot
instructions: Step 1 : Start Device Enrollment Power on your Mac and wait for the macOS Setup Assistant to launch. When the welcome screen appears, select your Country or Region and click Continue . Select your Language and click Continue . At the Accessibility screen, click Not Now . Connect to the internet using: a wired LAN connection, or a mobile hotspot Then click Continue . Step 2. Remote Management & Login After connecting to the internet, the Remote Management screen will appear. This means the device is ready to be enrolled into the KONE device management system. Click Continue . Enter the username and password provided by KONE. Step 3. Create Local Administrator Account You will now be prompted to create a local admin account: Username: macadmin (must be lowercase) Password: Must meet KONE password policy The username must be macadmin , and the device name must remain unchanged. Step 4. Initial Setup Screens Enable Location Services and click Continue . Click Continue (do not select any additional options). Click Set Up Later for Screen Time . Disable Ask Siri (remove the checkmark) and click Continue . Click Set Up Later for Touch ID , then click Continue . Enrollment is now complete. Step 5. Confirm Enrollment Verify hostname Go to Settings > General > Sharing - Click Advanced Confirm the Local Hostname appears with the device serial number Example: C0XXXXXXX6LR.local Verify configuration profile Go to Settings > General > Device Management Ensure the configuration profiles are installed. Verify domain status Go to Settings > Users & Groups Confirm the device is connected to the domain. Step 6. Business Applications Installation Business apps will automatically install in the background, including: Microsoft Office 365 VPN Company Portal Security tools (GlobalProtect, Xensem, Qualys) This process may take 1–2 hours , depending on network speed. FileVault Activation During installation, a FileVault prompt will appear. Select Enable Now . FileVault disk encryption is mandatory for KONE devices. Step 7. Company Portal Configuration When the Company Portal app is installed, open it. Click Sign In and enter your KONE email and password. After sign-in, check the compliance status: If Compliant , setup is complete. If Not Compliant , you’ll be asked to register the device. Click Register and follow the steps. Step 8. Domain Binding Configuration The AD Binding profile should install automatically. To verify: Go to Settings > General > Device Management If not installed: Open Company Portal Go to Apps Install AD Bind profile Then go to Settings > General > Device Management Select the AD Bind Profile and click Install Verify domain connection: Go to Settings > Users & Groups > Network Account Server Confirm the KONENET domain is connected Step 9. Create Your Mobile Account Sign out of the macadmin account. At the login screen, select Other User . Log in using your KONE email ID and password . This will create your mobile account. If prompted by FileVault, click Enable Now . Step 10. Connect to Office Wi-Fi Connect to KOffice Wi-Fi. Select the certificate when prompted. Enter your KONE username and password. Your Mac is now fully enrolled and ready for use. If you run into any issues or missing items during setup, contact the AskIT or email to KONE_ MacSupport (KONE.macsupport@KONE.COM)

short_description: The code execution cannot proceed because MSVCR110.dll & MSVCR120.dll was not found
introduction: Autodesk applications cannot run because the required files "MSVCR110.dll" and "MSVCR120.dll" are missing from your system. This error indicates that your system lacks the necessary Microsoft Visual C++ Redistributable files, which the application relies on to function properly.
instructions: Ensure you have administrative privileges on the system and access to the Microsoft Visual C++ Redistributable Packages for Visual Studio 2013 for MSVCR120.dll & Microsoft Visual C++ Redistributable Packages for Visual Studio 2012 MSVCR110.dll Steps to Resolve the Issue: Download the Microsoft Visual C++ Redistributable Packages for Visual Studio 2013 from the provided SharePoint link. Link (2013)- Download Visual C++ Redistributable Packages for Visual Studio 2013 from Official Microsoft Download Center Link (2012)- Download Visual C++ Redistributable for Visual Studio 2012 Update 4 from Official Microsoft Download Center Run the installer to install the package. If the both 2012 or 2013 package is already installed, the installer will prompt for a repair option. Select 'Repair' to fix the existing installation. Restart the system after installation or repair is complete.

short_description: How to Manage Dell Command Update
introduction: Dell Command Update is an automated utility that has been deployed across all Dell devices to streamline the driver and security update process. This tool automatically installs critical and security updates to ensure optimal system performance and compliance. No action is required from you.
instructions: Dell Command Update has been successfully deployed across all Dell devices. This utility automatically installs critical and security updates, ensuring your device stays secure and performs optimally. This article provides you with essential information about the tool and what to expect. For detailed visual instructions and step-by-step guidance, please refer to the Dell Command Update End-User Guide attached to the deployment email. What You Need to Know No Action Required This deployment has been completed as a backend activity. You do not need to install or configure anything. The tool is now active on your Dell device and will work automatically in the background. How the Tool Appears For information on how Dell Command Update appears on your device after installation, including where to find it and what the interface looks like, please refer to the Dell Command Update End-User Guide. Update Notifications & Deferral Prompts Dell Command Update will notify you when updates are available. For detailed information on how these notifications behave and how you can manage deferral prompts if needed, please refer to the Dell Command Update End-User Guide. Restart Mechanisms & Your Options Some updates may require your device to restart. For complete information on restart mechanisms and what options are available to you, please refer to the Dell Command Update End-User Guide. Need Help? If you have questions or encounter any issues with Dell Command Update, please contact Ask IT Support. They can assist you with any concerns and have access to the same guide for reference. Note: All managed devices are configured to communicate directly with the Dell server to check for available firmware, BIOS, and driver updates. The system automatically evaluates the current device state against available updates. If the device is already up to date, no action is taken. When updates are detected and installed, the device will initiate a scheduled reboot with advance user notifications to minimize disruption to productivity.

short_description: Manual method to add Mac devices to Apple Business Manager using Apple Configurator 2.
introduction: What is Apple Configurator 2. Apple Configurator 2 is a tool used by IT to manually add and supervise Apple devices—such as MacBooks, iPhones, and iPads—that were not originally purchased through Apple or an authorized reseller. This process allows your device to be added into Apple Business Manager (ABM) and fully managed through Mobile Device Management (MDM) systems like Intune. IT uses Apple Configurator 2 to: Register the device into ABM Apply supervision Enable automated management and configuration Ensure the device receives company policies and apps This is typically performed during device setup. End users only need to follow the guidance provided by IT during the enrollment process. Prerequisites and Requirements 1. Apple Configurator app installed on MacBook, iPhone/iPad (from the App Store). 2. A Managed Apple ID with ABM permissions (Administrator, Device Enrollment Manager, or Owner) 3. Internet access (Wi-Fi or Mobile Hotspot). Recommended: iPhone/iPad and Mac on same Wi-Fi network 4. Device must be factory-reset before enrollment. 5. C type USB cable
instructions: Method 1: Manual Enrollment of a Mac Using Another Mac (Mac → Mac) A. Prepare the Admin Mac Install Apple Configurator from the Mac App Store. Open Apple Configurator and sign in using your Managed Apple ID. Go to Settings (Preferences) > Organizations and ensure your organization is added. Click on Organizations > Click on + sign Click on Next > Select Generate a new supervision identy B. Put the Target Mac into Recovery Mode (For Apple silicon (M1/M2/M-series) Macs) Turn off the target Mac. Press and hold Power button until “Loading Startup Options” appears. Choose Options → Continue → enter Recovery. Connect the target Mac to the admin Mac using a USB-C cable. C. Restore and Prepare the Mac In Apple Configurator on the admin Mac, select the target Mac. Click Prepare. Choose Automatic Enrollment or Manual Configuration. Select Add to Apple Business Manager. Choose: Activate and Complete Enrollment, or Activate Only Select or add your MDM server. Enable Supervision (recommended). Click Prepare and wait for the process to finish. Step 2: Enroll MacBook using iPhone or iPad A. Install and Sign In to Apple Configurator (iPhone/iPad) On the iPhone/iPad, open App Store. Search and install Apple Configurator. Open the app. Sign in using your Managed Apple ID (with device enrollment permissions). B. Prepare the Mac for Enrollment Turn on the Mac. You must stop at the macOS Setup Assistant (Do not proceed with setup). Connect Mac to the internet: - Wi-Fi from the top-right menu -Or connect Ethernet The Setup Assistant must be visible. C. Start “Add Mac to ABM” on the iPhone/iPad Open Apple Configurator on the iPhone/iPad. Tap Add (+) at the top-right. Choose Add Mac. The iPhone/iPad camera will open and be ready to scan the Mac. 4. On the Mac, Open the Pairing Window On the Mac Setup Assistant screen, press Command (⌘) + Option + O + O (Yes, press the letter O twice). A pairing animation will appear on the Mac display. 5. Scan the Mac Using the iPhone/iPad Point the iPhone/iPad camera at the Mac’s animation. Wait for the device to be detected. The Configurator app will show: Mac model and serial number Organization information Tap Add to add the Mac to ABM. 6. Complete the Pairing and Activation. The iPhone/iPad sends the enrollment request. The Mac restarts automatically. After restart, the Setup Assistant will contact Apple and display Remote Management. The device is now added to ABM and linked to your MDM server. 7. Verify the Mac in Apple Business Manager Open https://business.apple.com Sign in with your Managed Apple ID. Go to Devices. Search the MacBook’s serial number — it should now appear under “Added by Apple Configurator (It may take 4–12 hours to appear). 8. Assign the Device to Intune Log in to business.apple.com → Go to Devices. Search the Mac’s serial number. Click Edit MDM Server → select Intune → Save. Device syncs in up to 24 hours, then shows Remote Management during setup. -------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- Contact & Support If you run into any issues during enrolling to ABM, contact the AskIT or email to KONE_ MacSupport (KONE.macsupport@KONE.COM) Thank you !!

short_description: Global Protect VPN installation and configuration method
introduction: This document provides a step-by-step guide for installing, configuring, and connecting to the Global Protect VPN. Follow these instructions to ensure secure access to corporate resources. Prerequisites Before proceeding, ensure the following requirements are met: 1. The Mac device is a corporate device enrolled in Microsoft Intune. 2. The macOS device is connected to the internet (Do not use KOffice Wi-Fi or KONE Wired networks) 3. The Intune Company Portal application is installed and accessible.
instructions: 1. Install Global Protect VPN Open Spotlight or navigate to the Applications folder and search for GlobalProtect. If the application is not found, open the Company Portal. Go to Apps, find GlobalProtect VPN, and click Install. 2. Verify Device Compliance Open the Company Portal. Confirm the device status shows Compliant. If the device appears as Not Compliant, select Check Status to sync with Microsoft Intune. Only compliant devices are allowed to access corporate VPN resources. 3. Configure and Connect to Global Protect VPN Launch the Global Protect app. Enter the portal address: vpn.kone.com Click Connect. A browser window opens for authentication. Use Microsoft Edge as your default browser to avoid authentication issues. Select Always Allow when prompted for Global Protect browser access. Complete authentication using your username and password or Touch ID. 4. Troubleshooting & Gateway Change If connection delays or failures occur: Open Global Protect. Click Change Gateway. Enter a nearby location (Example: Singapore). Select the gateway and try connecting again. -------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- Contact & Support If you run into any issues during Configuration VPN GlobalProtect, contact the AskIT or email to KONE_ MacSupport (KONE.macsupport@KONE.COM)

short_description: How to create a user in SAP PO portal?
introduction: How to create a user in SAP PO portal?
instructions: SAP PO portal is used for system integration and process automation. Access to the POs systems needs to be approved by the TDE, SAP Integrations. In his/her absence, it can be approved by SAP Operations Manager or TDE, SAP Security and GRC. It currently have three environments in scope, which are: PO1 (DEV) PO3 (QA) PO4 (PROD) To create a user in SAP PO portal, login to the environment in which the user needs to be created. Once logged in, the below page will open. Then, click on 'User Management' for creating and modifying users in SAP PO. On the following screen, you can either create a new user by manually assigning roles using the 'Create User' button, or create a new user by replicating the access of an existing user (Clone ID–based access creation) using the 'Copy to New User' button. Create User: When you click on the 'Create User' button, below screen will open. Here, in the General Information tab, enter the Kone User ID of the user to be created in 'Logon ID' field, define initial password for this user, define the First Name, Last Name and Email address for this user. The security policy will be 'Default' for normal dialog user, for technical users, select 'Technical Users'. Password for Technical users need not be changed at first login. In the Account Information tab, enter the start and end date for the user's account validity in PO system: In the Assigned Roles tab, manually search the roles which needs to be assigned to the user by searching the role names in the Search Criteria field, selecting the role and click on 'Add' button to add the roles to the user. If multiple roles needs to be assigned to the user, search one role at a time, add the role and then search for another role. Wildcards are also supported to search role names. You can also click on 'Remove' button under Assigned roles to remove any existing roles already assigned to the user. Click on 'Save' button, the user will be created. Share the User ID and Password with the user. For any basic access in PO portal, assign only below roles: SAP_XI_DISPLAY_J2EE SAP_XI_MONITOR_J2EE NWA_READONLY If a user's need admin or more technical access, create user based on a clone ID's access. Take prior approval from CO before creating any user in PO. Copy to New User : For creating a new user copying access from an existing user in SAP PO, search the Clone/Mirror user ID in Search Criteria field and then click on the 'Go' button. Once the user is displayed, select the user and then click on 'Copy to New User' button. Update the Logon ID with the Kone Network ID of the user who needs to be created, define the initial password, update the First Name, Last Name and email address for the new user. Update the start and end dates for the new user's validity in SAP PO system, if required. In the Assigned Roles, the roles which are assigned to the Clone/Mirror ID will appear by default. You can still search for any roles that needs to be assigned on top of this access in Available Roles. Click on the Save button and the user will be created.

short_description: Internal Error- Data Integrity check fail.
introduction: Binary creation involves compiling the drive software. However, the process is interrupted by the error shown below Internal error: Data integrity check failed on "C:\Program Files\IBM\RationalSDLC\ClearCase\var\cache\CCshmemV3".
instructions: Issue ERROR: Internal error: Data integrity check failed on "C:\Program Files\IBM\RationalSDLC\ClearCase\var\cache\CCshmemV3". Internal error: Data integrity check failed on "C:\Program Files\IBM\RationalSDLC\ClearCase\var\cache\CCshmemV3.S-1-5-21-3302659136-1882306543-3512536600-1012591" Resolution: Regarding the error message "Internal error: Data integrity check failed on "C:\Program Files\IBM\RationalSDLC\ClearCase\var\cache\CCshmemV3"". Please follow the below steps and verify. Step1: Stop the ClearCase services Step2: Go to the cache path "C:\Program Files\IBM\RationalSDLC\ClearCase\var\cache" and delete the file "CCshmemV3" Step3: To delete the cache file run command "dir CCshmemV3" Step4: Press enter. Step5: Run the command "del" Step6: Press enter. Step7: Go to IBM installation manager and Start the ClearCase services. Step8: run the "omake" command again, and the build completed successfully. NOTE: No issues were encountered during binary creation, and it has been confirmed that the ClearCase error does not impact the process. Although the error persists, it does not affect binary creation.

short_description: Self-Guide to generate SAML certificates
introduction: How to renew the certificates for application
instructions: Self-Guide to generate SAML certificates Contents Steps to launch Azure portal and generate SAML certificates by Application owner/Application administrator 2 Setting up new Owners (Skip this section if no owners need not be set up): 3 Generating new SAML Certificates: 3 Uploading XML metadata file provided by Application Vendor team .. 3 Testing for SSO .. 3 End note . 3 Steps to launch Azure portal and generate SAML certificates by Application owner/Application administrator Note: Only application owners will be able to renew the certificate/secrets. Link to launch portal: https://portal.azure.com/ Enter credentials to login to the portal, once you are in Click on View as below Click on Enterprise Applications blade under Manage Setting up new Owners (Skip this section if no owners need not be set up): Navigate to Owners blade and click on Add , search the user and click on Select . Existing owners/new owners will have complete access to the application and make necessary modifications to maintain the application functionality. Note: Once added the Owner will have complete access to Modify the application. Generating new SAML Certificates: Click on Single Sign-on blade, scroll down to SAML Certificates section(3) and click on Edit as highlighted below. You can see the expiring certificate as highlighted. Click on New Certificate You can see that the new certificate is entered as highlighted below, click on Save button The Thumbprint and expiry dates will be auto populated. The certificate will remain Inactive after generating, you need to activate the certificate manually once the required certificates are downloaded and shared with the application vendor team who needs to configure the certificates supported (Base 64/PEM/Raw/Federated XML) in the application host server and enable it first. Click on the 3 dots (pointed arrow), Download and share the supported certificates with application vendor team, and once you confirm that the certificates are activated in application host server end, you can click on Make certificate active button. Alternatively, you can also download the certificates from the Single sign-on main page section (3) To establish connectivity between the Identity Provider (Azure) and the application end, below IdP details needed to be configured in application host server. Generally, all this information will pre-exist for SSO enabled applications. IDP Details - Application ID – (check below how to find Application ID) Tenant ID - 2bb82c64-2eb1-43f7-8862-fdc1d2333b50 Login URL - https://login.microsoftonline.com/2bb82c64-2eb1-43f7-8862-fdc1d2333b50/saml2 Microsoft Entra Identifier - https://sts.windows.net/2bb82c64-2eb1-43f7-8862-fdc1d2333b50/ Logout URL - https://login.microsoftonline.com/2bb82c64-2eb1-43f7-8862-fdc1d2333b50/saml2 App Federation Metadata Url: (Check below image to locate App Federation Medata URL) Certificate details Where to find Application ID? Overview page > Application ID Where to find App Federation Metadata Url? Single Sign-on > Under SAML certificates (section 3) > App Federation Metadata URL. Click on Copy button to copy it (pointed below) Uploading XML metadata file provided by Application Vendor team Click on Single sign-on and Upload metadata file Locate and upload the metadata file by clicking on the box as highlighted on right side and then click on Add button. Once you upload the XML metadata file, enable the certificate by following the same process mentioned above. Testing for SSO Once all the certificates/details are added in Application end, test the application as below by clicking on Test Follow instructions to enter your KONE credentials and complete the MFA to test SSO functionality. End note The Application team should configure the certificates in the Application end and test the application. This completes SSO using SAML configuration. Should they have any queries, reach out to the WebSSO team (SSOSupport@kone.com).

short_description: Client Secret renewal (Self Guide)
introduction: How to renew the client secret for applications
instructions: Client Secret renewal (Self Guide) Contents Steps to launch Azure and navigate to the application to be performed by the Application Owner: 1 Updating Client Secrets . 3 Updating owners for the application . 5 Testing the application . 6 Steps to launch Azure and navigate to the application to be performed by the Application Owner: Note: Only application owners will be able to renew the certificate/secrets. Link to launch portal: https://portal.azure.com/ Enter credentials to login to the portal, once in Click on View as below Navigate on the left side pane and scroll down to find App registrations as shown below, click on App registrations: Search for your application as shown below: Typically, the applications managed by WebSSO will be in the standard format KONE SSO XXXXXXX (where XXXXXXX is the application name) eg: myapp Updating Client Secrets Click on the application (eg:myapp), navigate to Certificates & Secrets section in left pane Click on Certificates & secrets followed by +New client secret as in screenshot below: Enter the description eg: New secret keys for myapp Enter Expires , select 730 days (24 months). Click Add button. Once added, new secret value and secret ID will be created, copy immediately and keep it safe as the same needs to be integrated in application end for security purposes (As the value will be hidden after few minutes and cannot be retrieved if loss) Note that the Value is a string of alphanumeric characters and must be kept confidential as exposing this information will lead to jeopardizing the security of your application. Now, you must update the Value along with the required configurations from the IdP end (Azure portal) in the Application host server to ensure that the application functions smoothly avoiding any downtime before the expiry of the existing values and secrets. Updating owners for the application Ensure that at least two application owners are updated, for efficient management, existing owners/administrators can update owners (add/remove) To update owner, click Owners blade and then click Add owners Select the owner by searching in the search bar, choose correct account/ user ( checking the checkbox ) and click on Select as below. The new owner will now be updated as below Now the owner for the application has been updated successfully, you can proceed to test the application if it works fine. Testing the application Proceed to test the application after updating the new secret value and other configuration details from Azure in the application host server. Coordinate with WebSSO team (SSOSupport@kone.com) if you have any queries/ difficulties in performing the steps mentioned above.

short_description: Invoicing process for FI invoice for Greece and role of Edicom
introduction: In this article we will try to explain the invoicing process for FI invoice for Greece company code 273 and also how to reach out to Edicom vendor support team
instructions: Edicom vendor is engaged in Greece invoicing process for E-invoicing to be verified by government portal. The e-Invoice will enable near real-time validation and storage of transactions, catering to Business-to-Business (B2B) and Business-to-Consumer (B2C). The screenshot below suggests the flow if invoices for Greece. Edicom Invoicing flow 1. List of all the FI invoices created can be extracted from SAP using ZFEID report 2. Invoices are sent to Operator via Idoc and IDOCs are triggered on daily basis via Batch Job 3. Exstream creates Invoice layout and share along with the IDoc xml to Edicom 4. EDICOM processes the xml and reports these invoices to myDATA portal 5. Upon successful validation, Edicom receives a UID and Mark ID from myDATA Portal 6. The clearance information forwarded by Edicom to Exstream 7. Edicom generates the Invoice layout with the UID and Mark ID, sends to SAP via SAP CPI interface, and delivers the invoices to customers via email (as maintained in customer master data) 8. FL can monitor the same using reconciliation report ZFEID 9. Exstream receive the details and send the same to SAP and ECM for archiving Checking the issue with Edicom support team: The edicom support portal can be accessed with the URL MyEdicom Once the portal is accessed with the credentials, you will get the below screen. The support ticket option can be accessed via support button on the left side of the screen as highlighted below. The new ticket can be created with option create request; the existing open tickets can be tracked using the open requests and son on as shown in below highlighted screenshot. The new ticket can be raised with the create request option as shown below. We need to fill the details highlighted in the below screenshot and also we can attach the documents if needed. Once all the details are filled and submitted, we receive a ticket number which can be used to track the case further. Thanks, Abhishek

short_description: How to create ITSM Access request in MyAccess
introduction: This article explains how to request access to ITSM (ServiceNow) using the MyAccess portal.
instructions: Overview : The ITSM platform requires specific roles to be assigned based on your job responsibilities. All ITSM access requests must be submitted through the MyAccess Portal for approval and provisioning. Steps to Request ITSM Access: Step-1: Login to MyAccess < KONE MyAccess - Home > Go to the MyAccess Portal. Step-2: click on Manage Access Step-3: Click on Manage My Access Step-4: Search "ServiceNow" Steo-5: Check the role and click next Step-6: Review and submit the request Please refer the below table for Access Roles and Descriptions: MyAccess Roles MyAccess Description ServiceNow CMDB: Legal&PrivacyAssessment user This roles provides kone legal team to review the cyber security and legal assessment questionaires and update the assessment value in CMDB. It provides visibility to all fields in the CMDB. ServiceNow CMDB: CybersecurityAssessment user This roles provides kone cyber security team to review the cyber security and legal assessment questionaires and update the assessment value in CMDB. It provides visibility to all fields in the CMDB ServiceNow ITSM: ChangeAndRelease_Manager This role provide access to manage change management lifecycle. ServiceNow CMDB: DataOwner_Infra_classes This role provides full visibility into all fields within the CMDB. It allows users to create and update computing-related CIs such as Servers, Databases, Virtual Machine (VM) Instances, Clusters, and Application Servers, along with managing required relationships and cloud relationships. ServiceNow CMDB: DataOwner_OSSUser This role enables Onsite support users to update cmdb related to workstation assignment and status changes ServiceNow CMDB: DataOwner_HAMUser This role enables Hardware asset management team to maintain cmdb workstations data. This will also includes computer CI's, Printer CI's. ServiceNow CMDB: DataOwner_Network_class This role enable Network teams(connectivity Team) to maintain Network related CI's in CMDB ServiceNow CMDB: DataOwner_Printer_class This role enables users to maintain Printers only in CMDB ServiceNow CMDB: DataOwner_RandD_Assets This role enables the users to create and update the Hardware records with Asset Function "R&D Lab Devices" ServiceNow CMDB: DataOwner_OperationsManager/Lead This role provides broad access to ITSM and CMDB modules. It enables users to view and update ITSM records (Incidents, Requests, Changes, Problems, Releases, Tasks), manage CMDB records including Business Applications, Services, Offerings, and Product Models (excluding assessment value fields), and create, edit, and approve Knowledge Articles, Reports, and Dashboards. ServiceNow CMDB: DataOwner_ProductManager/Owner This role provides broad access to ITSM and CMDB modules. It enables users to view and update ITSM records (Incidents, Requests, Changes, Problems, Releases, Tasks), manage CMDB records including Business Applications, Services, Offerings, and Product Models (excluding assessment value fields), and create, edit, and approve Knowledge Articles, Reports, and Dashboards. ServiceNow ITSM: Support_user This role allows users to view and update ITSM records including Incidents, Service Requests, Problems and Tasks in the ServiceNow Console. It provides visibility to all fields in the CMDB and permissions to create and edit Knowledge Articles, Reports, and Dashboards. ServiceNow ITSM: keyUser/approver access This role allows users to view ITSM records including Incidents, Service Requests, Problems and Tasks in the ServiceNow Console. It provides visibility to all fields in the CMDB and permissions to view Knowledge Articles, create/update Reports, and Dashboards. ServiceNow Platform: PowerBI_user This role will enable the reporting functionality in PowerBi(MS Fabric) from ServiceNow through PowerBI(MS Fabric) connector ServiceNow ITSM: Knowledge_managers This role provide extenstive access to edit/update all knowledge articles. ServiceNow ITSM: MI_Managers This role enable Major incident managment funtionalities ServiceNow ITSM: Problem_Managers This role provide access to manage problem lifecycle. ServiceNow Platform: Dev_Admin This role provides admin access for dev instance. ServiceNow Platform: Dev_Tester This role allows users to view and update ITSM records including Incidents, Service Requests, Problems and Tasks in the ServiceNow Console. It provides visibility to all fields in the CMDB and permissions to create and edit Knowledge Articles, Reports, and Dashboards. ServiceNow Platform: UAT_Admin This role provides admin access for UAT instance. ServiceNow Platform: UAT_Tester This role allows users to view and update ITSM records including Incidents, Service Requests, Problems and Tasks in the ServiceNow Console. It provides visibility to all fields in the CMDB and permissions to create and edit Knowledge Articles, Reports, and Dashboards. ServiceNow Platform: Prod_Admin This role provides admin access for Production instance. ServiceNow CMDB: DataOwner_OT_Nonstandard Device This role provides access for users to create OT Devices in CMDB. Note: If any of the above roles are not available in MyAccess, or if you encounter issues while raising a request for the ServiceNow ITSM: Support_User role, please contact us by submitting a generic service request under "ServiceNow Request Fulfillment." Generic Service Request

short_description: Steps to Raise a Microsoft Support Case
instructions: Please find below the screenshots and key points to consider when raising a Microsoft support case. Additionally, a PowerPoint file with more detailed information is attached for your reference Log in to Intune/Azure/Microsoft 365 Admin Center Go to https://intune.microsoft.com/ and sign in with your admin credentials. Navigate to Help & Support On the left navigation pane, click Support → Help & Support Describe Your Issue In the “How can we help?” box, type your issue (e.g., Outlook is stuck at 'Processing' ). Click Contact Support Select Support Provider. Fill in Case Details Title: Enter a short, clear title for the issue. Description: Provide detailed information about the problem. Product: Select the relevant Microsoft product (e.g., OneDrive, Exchange, Intune). Severity: Choose impact level (e.g., B - Moderate impact ) Your availability (Required) Options: 24/7 Support – Select if you are available anytime for Microsoft to contact you. Business Hours – Select if you prefer to be contacted only during your business hours. Confirm your number (Required) Enter your country code (e.g., +91 for India) and your phone number . This is the number Microsoft will use to reach you for updates or troubleshooting. Confirm email addresses of authorized contacts (Required) Provide one or more email addresses of people authorized to work with Microsoft on this case. Example: XYZ@kone.com Recommend providing always team email address. Attach Files (Optional) Click Add a file or screenshot or video to upload supporting evidence. Set Preferred Settings Time zone: Select your preferred time zone. Language: Choose your preferred contact language Submit the Case Click Contact me to create the support request Track Your Case Go to Support → View service requests to monitor status and edit details if needed. You can click Edit to update severity, add notes, or upload more files Table of roles and responsibilities for Microsoft Support Escalation Incident Manager: v-siddarajus@microsoft.com & v-manjer@microsoft.com Customer Success Account Manager: Tony.Mikkola@microsoft.com

short_description: Handling Error in SDI Process issue(Received non-OK response)
introduction: This article provides details on the handling process for POs getting errors stating, ‘Received non-OK response during GetProductCharacteristicDataAsJSON. Status: InternalServerError, description: The remote server returned an error: (404) Not Found.’ and similar issues
instructions: Issue: POs getting errors stating, ‘Received non-OK response during GetProductCharacteristicDataAsJSON. Status: InternalServerError, description: The remote server returned an error: (404) Not Found.’ and similar issues Resolution Process: Whenever an error alert mail is received from OpenText, or any PO is in this error below as seen in the image, For example, PO 4561747736 The Key Users must be notified on the issues with the POs With the error message provided, the exact issue could not be traced, so further detailed error logs for the POs are needed from OpenText end. Below is the sample error logs for this case, Once the error logs are obtained from OpenText, this must be checked with the PDM support team. Ticket must be raised for the PDM team to have this checked and resolved at the earliest. The service that’s not working can either be selected as ePDM or dPDM in ServiceNow as seen in below image. short_description: PDM–SAP Language Code Usage Restriction Workaround.
introduction: In the PDM system, language values are used as common comment values across multiple global attributes. Some of these language values are not supported in SAP, and using them for SAP-integrated attributes can cause data transfer failures or inconsistencies. This KBA applies to all PDM users and administrators managing material attributes linked to SAP integration. It provides guidance on handling unsupported language values without impacting other attributes.
instructions: Issue Context Certain language values in PDM cannot be removed or modified because they are shared across multiple global attributes. Direct deletion or modification would affect all linked attributes, creating potential system-wide data issues. The problem occurs when users select language values that are not synchronized with SAP for attributes that interface with SAP, leading to interface inconsistencies. EX language list: Language in PDM ISO CODE PDM SAP Bengali bn Not available English (GSS) e2 Not available Flemish fl Not available Latin la Not available Philippines phi Not available Test rr Not available Tamil ta Not available Indonesian C4L ix Not available Mex. Spanish mx Not available Tagalog tl Not available Resolution / Workaround The workaround for this request is as follows: 1. Create a new language value list in PDM. 2. Link the new value list only to the specific attribute values that are not synchronized with SAP. 3. Retain the existing global language values for all other attributes to avoid system-wide impact. 4. Ensure SAP-integrated attributes continue using only supported language values. 5. This approach prevents disruption to SAP data transfer while allowing controlled usage of language values for non-SAP attributes. NOTE : The workaround can only done by development team.

short_description: How to terminate order from Jobscheduler
introduction: This article helps to understand How to Terminate Stuck order from Jobscheduler
instructions: Procedure : When an order fails in PDM due to the below error in the Job chain, we should terminate the orders from the job scheduler and rerun them from PDM. [ERROR] COM-80020009 DISP_E_EXCEPTION [step] [] [Z-JAVA-105 Java exception java.lang.RuntimeException: COM-80020009 DISP_E_EXCEPTION [] [SCHEDULER-186 Order order:1137503749-10 is already in job chain /varipdm/epdm_prod/factory/ platform_sublevels_ctrl_noconf_csap_pf (Calling Spooler.Job_chain.add_order)], method=spooler_process()Z [], in Remote_module_instance_server::step], method=call How can we identify the stuck or affected job chains? In the job scheduler log, we can see the highlighted error. Steps: Step 1: Open the Job Scheduler in (ePDM/dPDM) Navigate to the Job chain and search the affected job chains. Step 2: Expand the Job Nodes and Locate the Stuck-up order. Step 3: Terminate the Order Action Not the entire order as shown below. Step 4: Go to Orders and Track the status of Order. It should be "failed" Note : technical-error-report --> Meaning that JS communicate to PDM Done Failed : resembles that Order Terminated in the JS Step 5: Verify the status in Variclient also. Step 6: Now the Stuck-up order got terminated. Now, retrigger the same order through Variclient (As shown below) to Reprocess the order again.

short_description: POC&GR - Equipment Address not importing
introduction: POC&GR - Equipment Address not importing
instructions: Check the log file in server to view error. As checked using the below query the SAP.EquipmentAddress table identity int limit has been reached. SELECT s . name AS SchemaName , t . name AS TableName , c . name AS ColumnName , ty . name AS DataType , ic . seed_value , ic . increment_value , IDENT_CURRENT ( QUOTENAME ( s . name ) + '.' + QUOTENAME ( t . name )) AS CurrentIdentity FROM sys . identity_columns ic JOIN sys . tables t ON ic . object_id = t . object_id JOIN sys . schemas s ON t . schema_id = s . schema_id JOIN sys . columns c ON ic . object_id = c . object_id AND ic . column_id = c . column_id JOIN sys . types ty ON c . user_type_id = ty . user_type_id ORDER BY CurrentIdentity DESC ; Changing the column data type from int to bigint using the below queries. Since the column is primary key we need to drop the primary key constraint. ALTER TABLE SAP . EquipmentAddress DROP CONSTRAINT PK_EquipmentAddress ; Now changing the column to Bigint. ALTER TABLE SAP . EquipmentAddress ALTER COLUMN PK_EquipmentAddress BIGINT ; Updating the column to primary key. ALTER TABLE SAP . EquipmentAddress ADD CONSTRAINT PK_EquipmentAddress PRIMARY KEY ( PK_EquipmentAddress );

short_description: Linux Device Enrollment - User Guide
introduction: This article explains how to install Ubuntu, encrypt the disk, enroll your device into Intune, and configure corporate Wi-Fi using a device certificate. Please follow the steps in order.
instructions: Step 1: Ubuntu Installation Power on the Linux device and wait for the Ubuntu welcome screen. Select your preferred language . Click Next for accessibility options. Select your keyboard layout and click Next . Connect to Wi-Fi or Mobile Hotspot (internet is required). When prompted for updates, skip updates . Select Install Ubuntu → click Next . Choose Interactive Installation. Select Default selection → click Next . Enable Install third-party software for graphics and Wi-Fi hardware → click Next . Step 2: Enable LVM and Disk Encryption When you reach the disk partition section, select 'Erase Disk' option Click Advanced Features Select use LVM and encryption Click ' Next ' to proceed with disk erasure Create a strong encryption passphrase with the following criteria: Max 15 characters Include both numeric and symbol Store this passphrase securely - you'll need it after restart Step 3: Create User Account on Ubuntu Create your user account: Username: Your email username (example: john.doe ) Password: Enter a secure password Review the settings. Click Install to start installation. Wait until installation completes. Click Restart Now when prompted. After restart, enter your disk encryption passphrase to unlock the system. Step 4: Download and Run the Enrollment Script Obtain the Linux enrollment script from the IT team (submit a ticket if not provided) Download the script folder to your system. Open Terminal and Navigate to the script directory: cd ~/Downloads/linuxenrollment Run the enrollment script with sudo access: s udo ./LinuxIntuneEnrollment_v1.12.sh Enter your local user password when prompted. During installation, the following applications will be installed automatically: Company Portal Microsoft Edge Microsoft Teams Qualys GlobalProtect VPN Step 5: Connect to Domain After the core applications are installed, you will be prompted to enter your domain details Give AD domain user name : - Enter your KONE Employee ID Eg : K111222 - Password : Enter your email login password If successful, you will see: Successfully enrolled machine in realm Step 6: Company Portal Setup & Intune Connection Open Company Portal from the Applications menu. Sign in using your KONE email ID and password. Wait for the device to sync with Intune (this may take a few minutes). Check device status: Status should display Compliant Step 7: Generate Device Certificate Request (Wi-Fi) Open Terminal. Run the following command: openssl req -new -passout pass:"setyourstrongpassword" \ -newkey rsa:2048 -keyout serialno.key \ -out serialno.csr For the Common Name field, enter: DeviceSerialNumber + $@xyz.com Eg : 5CG0106MLB$@KONENET.COM ) Note : ($) Dollar Sign is complusory after serial no Skip remaining fields by pressing Enter. Store .csr and .key files securely. Email the .csr file to IT support to receive your device certificate Step 8: Wi-Fi Configuration Using Device Certificate Save the received device certificate (.cer) to: ~/Downloads/linuxenrollment Open Settings → Wi-Fi. Double-click KOffice SSID. Configure Wi-Fi security as follows: Setting Value Authentication TLS Identity Device Hostname (SerialNumber$) CA Certificate OfflineRootCA1.pem User Certificate Device certificate (.cer) Private Key .key file Enter the password used during certificate generation. Click Connect. Troubleshooting & Support If you face any issues during installation, enrollment, or Wi-Fi setup: Contact AskIT Or email Linux Support Team : kone.linuxsupport@kone.com

short_description: KEO - How to get the list of order which are in progress from KEO backend for specific time frame
introduction: KEO - How to get the list of order which are in progress from KEO backend for specific time frame
instructions: Login KEO database. Test: EUDVMMSSQL103\SEKO55D Prod: EUDVMMSSQL206\SEKO080P Execute the below query to get the list of orders which are in progress with price and which level approval the order is awaiting. SELECT o . PK_Order , o . Number , o . UserCostCenter , o . RequesterName , o . UserPersonalID , o . FirstManagerEmail , o . SecondManagerEmail , o . CreationDate , o . FK_OrderStatus , STRING_AGG ( '500' + CAST ( od . FK_Order AS varchar ( 20 )), ', ' ) AS Orders , SUM ( od . Quantity * od . Price ) AS TotalAmount , ow . FK_StepOfValidationStatus , svs . Description AS StepOfValidationStatus , ow . FK_OrderWorkflowStatus , ows . Description AS OrderWorkflowStatus FROM [CMD] . [Order] o JOIN [CMD] . [OrderDetails] od ON od . FK_Order = o . PK_Order LEFT JOIN ( SELECT *, ROW_NUMBER () OVER ( PARTITION BY FK_Order ORDER BY PK_OrderWorkflow DESC ) AS rn FROM [CMD] . [OrderWorkflow] ) ow ON ow . FK_Order = o . PK_Order AND ow . rn = 1 LEFT JOIN [CMD] . [StepOfValidationStatus] svs ON svs . PK_StepOfValidationStatus = ow . FK_StepOfValidationStatus LEFT JOIN [CMD] . [OrderWorkflowStatus] ows ON ows . PK_OrderWorkflowStatus = ow . FK_OrderWorkflowStatus WHERE o . FK_Unit = 73 AND o . FK_OrderStatus = 2 AND o . CreationDate >= '2025-01-01' AND o . CreationDate < '2026-01-01' GROUP BY o . PK_Order , o . Number , o . UserCostCenter , o . RequesterName , o . UserPersonalID , o . FirstManagerEmail , o . SecondManagerEmail , o . CreationDate , o . FK_OrderStatus , ow . FK_StepOfValidationStatus , svs . Description , ow . FK_OrderWorkflowStatus , ows . Description ;

short_description: How to Manage HP Command Update
introduction: HP Update is an automated utility deployed from Intune remediation script which is integrated with HP Portal across all HP devices to streamline driver, firmware, and security update processes. instructions: Instructions HP Update has been successfully deployed across all HP devices. For detailed visuals, feature overview, and step-by-step instructions, please refer to the HP Update User Guide attached to the deployment email. short_description: macOS Upgrade - User Guide
introduction: Before Proceeding with the Update Save all your work and back up important files to OneDrive. Verify that any third-party software is compatible with the new macOS version. Ensure that at least 40 GB of free storage is available on your device. Keep your device connected to the power adapter throughout the upgrade process.
instructions: Steps to Upgrade macOS When you receive the Software Update notification, the download will begin automatically in the background. To defer the update, select “Defer for one hour” or “Later.” The notification will reappear during the next Intune sync cycle. If the download does not start automatically, go to System Settings > Software Update and select “Update Device.” Click “Upgrade Now” to download the macOS update. The download may take 45–60 minutes, depending on network speed. Follow the on-screen instructions once the download is complete. Select “Continue” and review the Terms and Conditions. Select SSD “Macintosh HD” and “Continue” to install Mac OS Enter your username and password when prompted. The installation will begin. 9. During Installation A progress screen will appear showing that macOS Tahoe is being installed on the main system disk (Macintosh HD). A progress bar will display the estimated remaining time for installation. Do not shut down your device, disconnect the charger, or close the laptop lid during this process. Do not click the Cancel button, as this may interrupt the upgrade. The installation process may take 45 to 90 minutes depending on system performance. Your device may restart automatically multiple times. This is normal behavior. After the Upgrade • Open the Company Portal application and sign in using your KONE email credentials to sync with Intune. • Test business applications, VPN access, and Wi-Fi connectivity. User End Troubleshooting Update not showing Restart Mac, recheck Software Update Storage full Clear space, use One drive for backup data Wi-Fi drops Ensure a stable internet connection during update Update stuck Force restart, retry installation Support If you experience any issues during or after the upgrade, please contact the Mac Support Team at: KONE.macsupport@KONE.COM Thank you for your cooperation.

short_description: How to perform delayed schema for the new protocol request
introduction: This KBA helps to PDM support team to perform the new protocol request via delayed schema
instructions: Concept: PDM won't allow to perform the Heavy schema changes in the schema editor. For ex: change the parent for the value list inside the schema. Mean while, we don't have separate action menu for the change of parent item (we only have option to delete) inside the Schema. So, we proceed this with the new-protocol-request. How to perform new-protocol-request in delayed - schema? Step1: Login Varipdm and go to tools --> Communication Log. Step 2: check the box "protocol out". | Step 3: Go to tools--> schema Editor and perform the same action. Step 4: Now again go to the same Communication Log and there we can able to see the log over there. Copy the second line protocol message relevant to the action. Step 5: Go to Special --> more --> Delayed schema changes --> delayed schema changes Step 6: Choose new-protol-request in the New action and click continue. Step 7: Paste the protocol message copied from the communication log and click ok Step 8: Now the protocol added and again add new action as exit and click Ok. Once the schema changes will be submitted. The change will be process and getting changed. It will be done on a daily basis.

short_description: Global Protect VPN installation and configuration method - LINUX
introduction: This knowledge base (KB) article provides step-by-step instructions to help users install, configure, and connect to the GlobalProtect VPN for secure access to corporate resources.
instructions: Prerequisites Before starting, ensure the following requirements are met: Your Linux device is enrolled in Microsoft Intune. Your device is connected to the internet. Do not use KOffice WiFi or KONE Wired networks while connecting to VPN. The GlobalProtect VPN application is installed on your device Steps to Configure and Connect to GlobalProtect VPN Step 1: Launch GlobalProtect Open the GlobalProtect VPN application on your Linux device. Step 2: Enter VPN Portal Address In the portal address field, type: vpn.kone.com Click Connect. Step 3: Authenticate via Browser A browser window will open automatically for authentication. Troubleshooting: Connection Issues and Gateway Change If you experience slow connection, delays, or failure to connect, follow these steps: Step 1: Open GlobalProtect Launch the GlobalProtect application. short_description: Updating IFFOR rates in SAP
introduction: How to update IFFOR rates in SAP?
instructions: Hedge report Treasury IFFOR rates (Month end activity) To perform the month end activity of IFFOR rates for the next month, below steps need to be followed: The AskIT incidents for this activity: IN02034669 Access the excel file with the hedging rates for the next month for various companies with different currency pair. We usually get two different excel sheets for the upcoming month hedge rates. In one sheet we have the rates with the values FROM EURO currency which doesn't require "/" in beginning of the rates. The screenshot below reflects the example data having FROM EURO currency. The second sheet has all the remaining rates which are not FROM EURO currency, and it requires "/" in the beginning of the rates. 1. Log in to SAP portal with your credentials (SSO enabled) so no need of using the password in ZO4 Production(SSO) enabled module of SAP Logon. 2. Put the code SM30 on SAP portal and then enter Z_CA_TCURR to access the table. 3. Click and go on to maintain the data for various companies with different currency pair. Go to new entries and then input the data carefully. 4. From the raw data we need to look for the company codes to enter in the table. 5. The link for the company codes can be found using the image. Note: Add on to the image- CKQ->HCKQ and MAL->H238, NZL->H202 6. The currency pair should be maintained in the table as in the form of from one currency to another currency. Example: EUR/GRB should be entered as From GRB To EUR in the table. 7. Valid from column should contain the beginning date of the next month. Example: If we are doing month end activity in June then Valid from date should be in the format of 01.07.2022 8. Exchange rate should be entered carefully from the table starting with the backslash and decimal as comma in the format /exchange rate. Example: /3,82344 Note: When EUR currency is in "From" then "/" is not required. If EUR is in "TO" then rates to be maintain with "/" 9. Verify the data entered on the portal from the raw data to cross check each exchange rate and company code with their currency pair. 10. Take the screenshot of all the data to attach in the ticket so as user can verify the authenticity of data. 11. Save the table with the input data and don’t directly click on exit and instead use the back button to go back to home page. 12. Log off from the SAP portal..

short_description: SPD - Kone KCI Volume calculation table values missing for specific date range
introduction: SPD - Kone KCI Volume calculation table values missing for specific date range
instructions: Login the SPD database using the below instructions Database: 10.39.67.11\SEKO021P,51001 Check the stored procedure used for the report ‘KONE KCI Volume calculation’ using Report builder. Check the stored procedure to find out the table from which the data is posted in report. Table fetching data is Cdty_Daily_Prices. This table has IsMonthlyValueAmerica column value as 1 but as per the report the IsMonthlyValueAmerica should be 0. SELECT TOP(1) CAST(Period AS Date) AS LatestDate FROM (SELECT Months, Period FROM Cdty_DailyPrices WHERE (Months = 12) AND ([AmericaAluminium] IS NOT NULL) AND ([AmericaAluminium] <>0) AND ([IsMonthlyValueAmerica] = 0) ) AS Tbl Order By Period DESC There is job running ‘SPD Import Daily’ for getting the data to _ImportDailyROW table and if the job fails data will not be loaded to latest date. The query to check the _ImportDailyROW latest date for rest countries expect China for checking latest date. SELECT Region, Commodity, [Daily Price (excl# VAT)], Year, Month, Date FROM _ImportDailyROW WHERE (Region <> N'China') order by date desc

short_description: SAP GRP User Creation – Process Document
introduction: The purpose of this document is to define and formalize the process for creating new users or modifying access for existing users in the SAP GRC – GRP production system. It outlines the standardized procedures to be followed, ensuring consistency, compliance, and proper authorization in user management activities within the SAP GRP environment.
instructions: Request Types for SAP GRP system access The KONE SAP Security and GRC team manages requests that involve the creation of new users or the modification of existing access within the SAP GRP system. These requests typically include: Creation of new users for Infosys consultants (FF access - YGRCC:ACC_FFU_GLB, ABAP access - YGRCC:ABP_DEV_GLB) Creation of new users for Line Managers (Manager access - YGRCC:ACC_LMG_GLB) Creation of new users for Role Approvers, Risk Owners, Firefighter (FF) Owners, and FF Controllers Modification of existing users to assign the appropriate User Group (UG) for new Role Approvers, Risk Owners, FF Owners, and FF Controllers User Creation and Access Modification in SAP GRC – GRP System As the SAP GRC – GRP system is considered a crown jewel application and falls under audit scope, manual user creation directly within the system is strictly prohibited. The only exception is the creation of Line Managers for GRC approval of their direct reportees, which is addressed later in this section. All requests for new user creation or modification of existing user access in the SAP GRP system must be submitted through a GRC Access Request. This ensures proper approvals, compliance, and audit trail maintenance. For Infosys consultants, the functional area to be selected when raising GRC Access Requests must be “KONE IT” (and not “Infosys Employees”), in line with the current structure of the Decision Table. For business/end users requiring assignment of Role Owner or Risk Owner User Groups (UG), either during new user creation or when replacing a Line Manager UG with the approver UG ( YGRCC:ACC_APR_GLB ), the functional area to be selected must be “Global.” Exception Case: A custom program (ZLDAP_READ) is scheduled to run daily, automatically creating Line Managers directly in the SAP GRP system. This ensures that if a GRC Access Request is raised for any of their direct reportees, the Line Managers can approve the request during the Manager stage of the MSMP workflow. However, there may be instances where incidents or emails are received from end users reporting that they are unable to submit a GRC request because the user’s manager is not present in the GRP system. In such cases, the manager may be manually created in the GRP system with the assignment of the User Group YGRCC:ACC_LMG_GLB . This manual creation must always be supported by a valid SNOW ticket number or email from the user.

short_description: How to re enroll Windows devices in Intune
introduction: This guide provides a standardized process for the RDS Team, Service Desk, and Device Management teams to troubleshoot and resolve Intune Windows client connectivity issues. It starts by verifying the device's connection through Access Work or School settings and the Last check-in timestamp in the Intune portal, while confirming that Intune sync completes without errors. If sync issues persist or timestamps are missing/outdated, initiate re-enrollment by checking Azure AD join status ( dsregcmd /status ), removing outdated certificates, and clearing related registry entries. Finally, rejoin Azure AD to trigger automatic Intune re-enrollment within 2-3 hours, restoring full management and compliance.
instructions: Audience – RDS Team, ASKIT, Device Management Purpose- Below troubleshooting steps to be followed to remediate Intune windows clients 1. Take remote session of the device and open Access Work or School from settings 2. Expand KONENET.COM and click on info 3. On the next page drop down the page and check if sync is processing without errors like below 4.If you see sync is showing error code after sync process then follow reenrollment process If you don’t see info button under KONENET as per below picture then you need to reenroll device to Intune. Follow the process to re-enroll to Intune 1. Open command prompt as Administrator and run DSREGCMD /STATUS 2.Check the status of AAD join in the result if AzureADjoined as yes (it should show Yes) And note down last 6 characters of device ID that will use to identify registry hive 3.Perform dsregcmd /leave and run dsregcmd /status to check AzureADJoined should show as No 4.Run certlm.msc on command prompt that will open local computer snippet 5.Double click on Personal > Certificates and if its showing below yellow color highlighted issue by certificates as per device ID , right click on each and remove , keep the other certificates as it is. 6.Type regedit on same command prompt and that will open regeditor and navigate to Computer\HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE\SOFTWARE\Microsoft\Enrollments and expand all the sub keys where you see MS DM Server and your device id is matching with EntDeviceName value which is highlighted below in blue arrow. 7. When you find correct key then you need to delete the key which is highlighted with yellow. 8.After deleting the identified key (folder) , Restart the computer and again open CMD with administrator 9.On CMD check the aad join status by dsregcmd /status and if you see domain join automatically changed to yes so no need to run step number 12 command else you need to run it. 10.To join aad you need to run dsregcmd /join and restart the computer 11.Once device is joined to domain you will need to verify certificate using certlm.msc command 12.The certificate you removed will be automatically reappear related to MS -Organization issue type 13.Intune enrollment is automatically perform after you join Azure AD successfully perhaps you can enforce it by opening work and school under settings and follow step no 1 to 4 from first part of this article. 14.Intune auto enrollment take 2 – 3 hours to appear the computer on Intune portal after above process and it quickly appears in Entra portal after re-join to Entra 15.request user to keep the device online so sync will occur quickly and assigned policy / apps will take place 16.as a best practice inform user to logout user session and login back 17.if you want to see if the device is reporting to Intune so open Intune portal and search device for its checking time as per below screenshot

short_description: ASKIT - How to Apply for PDM access for different Environments?
introduction: This article guides end users on how to request access to different environments in PDM.
instructions: In PDM, we have four distinct environments: PROD (Production), DQ (Data Quality), QA (Quality Assurance), and DEV (Development). Below, you’ll find details on the purpose of each environment and the process for requesting access. Environment Usage How to apply PROD [Production] Applicable to end users, this system enables work within the Production Environment. KB0000A410 DQ [Data Quality] The DQ environment is a replica of the Production system and is primarily used for business testing. You do not need to apply separately for DQ access. Once Production access is granted, your account will be automatically created after the next DQ refresh (typically performed monthly). In case of urgent requirements, please raise an askIT ticket with the PDM team to request DQ access. QA [Quality Assurance] The QA environment is reserved for IT purposes only. It is used exclusively by developers and the PDM IT support team for deployments, testing, and related activities. Please note that the QA environment is not intended for end users. It is designated solely for IT operations and developers. If an end user requires QA access, they must submit an askIT ticket to request it. Dev [Development] The DEV environment is used by PDM team developers for deployments. This system is strictly not intended for end users.

short_description: IT - How to provide the access to the End user's in Different Environment's of PDM?
introduction: This article is intended to assist the PDM Operational Support team in providing access to end users.
instructions: In PDM, there are four different environments: Production (PROD), Data Quality (DQ), Quality Assurance (QA), and Development (DEV). PDM Production (PROD) Usage: For end users to perform business operations in the live environment. Access Process: End users requesting Production access must apply through the registration form by referencing KBA : KB0000A410 . Approval: Required via the registration process. PDM Data Quality (DQ) Usage: Replica of the Production environment, used for business testing and validation. Access Process: Accounts are automatically created after the next DQ refresh (typically monthly). In urgent cases, accounts can be created using the export/import method. Approval: No approval is required. However, users are encouraged to wait for the scheduled DQ refresh for automatic account creation. PDM Quality Assurance (QA) Usage: Reserved for IT support, operational teams, and developers for testing and deployments. Not intended for end user usage. Guidance: End users should instead use the DQ environment for business validation. Access Process: If an end user requires QA access, the application operational team must validate the business reason. If valid, accounts can be created using the export/import method. Approval: No separate approval required; validation is handled internally by the application operational team. Account might have pdm_tester privilege in both epdm_prod and epdm_qa and also Entra ID Object ID also mandatory to the qa accounts. PDM Development (DEV) Usage: Dedicated to the development team for deployments and development activities. Access Process: Not available to end users. Approval: Not applicable.

short_description: Why Material is not shown in PDM?
introduction: Instructions on addressing missing material results under the “Configurations” filter.
instructions: Step 1: Open the WebUI Material Search Page. Step 2: Enter the required Material ID in the search field and Search. Step 3: Check the dropdown for search criteria. If Configurations is selected, the system will only return materials that include configuration variants. Step 4: Since the material does not have configuration information, switch the criteria to All: Select All from the dropdown list. Step 5: Click the Search icon. For general or non‑configurable materials, always use All or Material as search criteria.

short_description: How to create distribution list in MyAccess portal
introduction: This document explains how to create and manage distribution lists as a self-service in MyAccess, detailing the steps to create roles, assign owners and approvers, set descriptions, and configure role properties such as validity and notifications. It emphasizes the necessity of having at least two owners and approvers, and outlines options like making roles non-requestable or setting end dates. The document also highlights business responsibilities including ownership accountability, applying least privilege principles, maintaining accuracy, compliance with policies.
instructions: How to create distribution list in MyAccess as a self-service If you do not own any role (role owner) you need to request MyAccess: Role Owner Rights in MyAccess. Once the role is approved. KONE employees can now create distribution list as self-service in MyAccess. If externals need the access of create Role page they will need to request access for MyAccess: Internal like rights-Role. Note that rule based automated distribution lists, distribution list member as other distribution list, bulk member assignments or permissions are requested via ticket in servicenow portal. With this feature you are only able to create distribution list and add members (up to 30) in edit role page. Create distribution list: Navigate the Manage roles --->create roles Business application - Distribution list Role - this will be the actual distribution list name visible to everyone Description - is visible in MyAccess anyone requesting the role Owners -2 owners are always mandatory Comments - if you want users to add any comments whilst requesting the role If you require attachment to be added when requesting the role Role completion notification will be routed to user when request is completed non-requestable will be hiding the role and users won’t be able to see it in MyAccess Role end date - if you want to delete the role Role assignment validity in days - if the role is valid only for certain period You can choose different types of approval options, what suit for the requirements. Each role must have at least 2 approvals. Once you are done with the creation press confirm twice and you should see the notification in MyAccess landing page “ Role creation request is submitted successfully ” You can modify the role after creation in Edit role- page, i.e adding or remove members, modifying display name, approvers or owners. When distribution list is no longer needed schedule deletion in MyAccess-edit roles: Business Responsibilities Ownership: Assign a Distribution list owners and approvers to users who are accountable for its purpose and membership. Security Principles : Apply least privilege—only grant DL access to those who need it for business purposes Accuracy: Provide correct member details and keep them updated. Review: Periodically review DL membership and remove inactive users.
