short_description: How to Bulk Update Draft States in PDM?
introduction: When multiple materials/documents revisions need to be moved to Draft state, performing the update manually one by one is time‑consuming. VariClient provides a bulk-update feature called State Graphs Group Update, which allows users to update the state of multiple material/document revision objects simultaneously.
instructions: Approval: 1. Person in charge approval is mandatory. If person in charge not there, kindly seek the approval from the created by Person. 2. If both the person is not in KONE. Initially kindly update the Person-in-charge and then go for the change. It will be helpful for the future cases. Step 1: Search for Materials in Bulk Open Vari Finder / Vari Client. Enter the required filters (e.g., Material numbers, latest revision). Click Find to retrieve the list of materials. The results list will show all matching material objects. Step 2: Select and Copy Materials to Group Update Select all materials in the results list (Ctrl + A). Right‑click on the selected items. Choose Copy Results to Group Update → State Graphs Group Update. Step 3: Update “Unset State Graphs” The Group Update window opens with all selected materials listed on the left. Under State Graphs, select Unset State Graphs. Click Update to apply the change. Step 4: Update “KONE Material Revision” After the first update completes, select KONE Material Revision under State Graphs . Click Update again. Step 5: Materials Moved to Draft State After completing both updates: All selected materials will now be moved to the Draft state. Note: 1. KONE Material Revision is only applicable for the Material 'Revision' level bulk change. 2. This process applicable to both the Material and document as well.

short_description: How to get an access to access control group in pdm?
introduction: This Knowledge Base Article provides guidance for users who receive an Access Denied error when attempting to open documents protected by an Access Controlled Group (ACG). It explains how to identify the correct approver and submit an access request.
instructions: Steps to get access: 1. When you see an Access Denied message, check the error for the ACG name, Person in Charge (PIC), and Group Modifiers. 2. Identify the group you need access to based on the error message. 3. Click the Person in Charge (PIC) listed in the message. 4. Your email will automatically open with a pre‑drafted access request message. NOTE : Mention the roll you need Reader or writer or releasers access to the document in the mail. 5. Send the email to request access to the ACG. 6. If the Person in charge is unavailable, click on any of the Group Modifiers instead. short_description: How to clean up inactive JAMA users and remove their access?
introduction: Guidelines for JAMA - user cleanup This process applies to all internal and external JAMA users and to annual or periodic JAMA user access cleanup activities for compliance and license optimization.
instructions: Roles and Responsibilities: 1.JAMA Admin Team: Finds inactive users, prepares the list, and deactivates them. 2.Software Leadership Team (via Marttila Pekka ): Reviews and approves the list. 3. MyAccess Team: Handles the removal of user access within MyAccess and relevant AD groups. Procedure: 1. Identify Inactive JAMA Users Log in to the JAMA application. Jama PROD & Jama QA Go to Admin → Users → Export to download the complete user list Open the exported Excel file and find the Last Login column. Filter the data to find users whose login activity falls outside the agreed inactivity period Exclude all service accounts and pessaral simulators from the results, as they should not be considered for deactivation. Compile a clean, consolidated list of JAMA users who meet the inactivity criteria. 2.Obtain Software Leadership Approval Share the consolidated inactive user list with Marttila Pekka by email for Software Leadership review and approval. Wait to receive the written approval email that confirms which users are approved for access removal. Proceed with the MyAccess removal request and JAMA deactivation activities only after the formal approval is received 3. Request Access Removal in MyAccess Create a Ask IT ticket in the KONE ASKIT Portal using the Generic Service Request form. Write an email to ( MyAccessSupportTeam@KONE.com ), including both the ASKIT ticket number and the approved inactive user list. Requesting the bulk removal of identified inactive users from MyAccess and Active Directory who are assigned to the following roles: Jama: users , Jama: QA Sandbox users . After the users are removed from MyAccess, the MyAccess team will send a notification either through email or via the ASKIT ticket. Retain the confirmation received from MyAccess as required documentation for audit and compliance purposes. 4. Deactivate Users in JAMA Log in to the JAMA Admin Portal. Go to Admin → Users to access the user management page. For each user on the approved list, perform a search and use the Deactivate option in the Action column. After deactivating, click Edit and then select Clear all user groups and save Verify that the status of each processed user is updated to Inactive in both JAMA production and QA environment.

short_description: How to Choose the Correct Resolution Code in Incident Management for AskIT tickets?
introduction: This Knowledge Base Article outlines the meaning of each resolution code and provides guidance on when to select them while closing an incident ticket. Using the appropriate resolution code ensures accurate reporting, helps identify recurring issues, and enhances IT service quality. This article has been specifically created by the PDM Team for their internal alignment.
instructions: 1. No resolution provided Use this only when the issue is closed without a clear action or when no resolution type applies (avoid using unless mandatory). 2. Resolved by caller Select this when the user confirms that the issue is resolved from their end without any action required from IT. 3. 3rd Party Use this if the resolution was completed by an external vendor or third‑party support team. 4. Out of scope Select this when the request does not belong to your team’s responsibility or service area. 5. Resolved as Service Request Use this when the service request instead opened as an incident, but it's actually a Service Request. 6. Resolved – Software Reinstalled Use this when the issue is fixed by reinstalling the application or software. 7. Resolved – Configuration Changed Select this when modifying configuration settings or Configurations of Order changed to resolved the issue. 8. Rebooted/Restarted System/Services Use this when rebooting a system or restarting services fixed the problem. 9. Resolved – Training/Guidance Provided Choose this when the issue is resolved by guiding or training the user, without any technical changes. 10. Resolved – Service Restarted Use this when restarting a specific service fixes the issue. 11. Resolved – Network Restored Select this when the issue is resolved by identifying and fixing a network problem. 12. Resolved – Account Renewed Use this when renewing or extending the user’s account resolves the issue. 13. Resolved – Certificate Renewed Choose this when renewing an expired or invalid certificate resolves the problem. 14. Resolved – Capacity Added Use this when adding additional server capacity (CPU, RAM, storage, etc.) fixes the issue. 15. Resolved – Hardware Replaced/Fixed Select this when the issue is caused by hardware failure and is resolved by repair or replacement. 16. Resolved – Password Reset Use this when resetting the user’s password solves the issue. 17. Resolved – Not Reproducible Choose this only when the issue cannot be reproduced despite testing and investigation. 18. Resolved – Script/Code Fix Select this when modifying or fixing a script/code resolves the problem. 19. Resolved – Upgrade/Patch Use this when the issue is resolved by upgrading the system or applying a patch.

short_description: Using the SAP GRC End User Portal to Raise Access Requests in SAP ECC, Solman & GRC Systems
introduction: How to use the SAP GRC End User Portal to Raise Access Requests in SAP ECC, Solman & GRC Systems?
instructions: This Knowledge Base Article (KBA) outlines the process for Infosys track leads to submit GRC access requests for new team members in the following systems: SAP ECC (Z01, Z02, Z03 and Z04) SAP Solman (SA1, SA4) SAP GRC (GRP) – For FF ID (firefighter) access By following this process, once the GRC request is completed and approved, AMS consultants will be granted access in these SAP systems. This streamlined approach reduces the need to send emails or raise tickets with the KONE SAP Security team each time a new transaction code (t-code) access is required or when a new joiner joins the SAP AMS Infosys team. Important Note: Elevated access is provided only for the SAP Functional Team in SAP Z01 and Z02 systems. It does not apply to higher SAP ECC environments (Z03 and Z04). Access Submission Process in SAP ECC: To submit an access request in SAP Z01, Z02, Z03 and Z04 systems. Open the below link for End User Portal and enter your Kone/Windows' credentials to login to this portal. https://knecigrp9800.konenet.com:44398/sap/bc/webdynpro/sap/grac_uibb_end_user_login?sap-client=100# Submitting a GRC Access Request in SAP ECC Once successfully logged in to the SAP GRC End User Portal, the requestor will be directed to the home screen. From here, click on the “Primary User Group Form” link to initiate an access request for a new joiner in the Z01, Z02, Z03 and Z04 systems. A new page will open. Complete all required fields as follows: KONE Network User ID – Enter the User ID of the individual requiring access in SAP ECC. SAP Landscape/System – Select SAP ERP . Org Level 1 – Select Technology & Innovation (TI) . Org Level 2 – Select GLBL . After entering the above details, open the Primary User Group field by clicking on the double-square icon or pressing the F4 key. A pop-up window will appear. Click the Search button to display all available IT user groups. Select the appropriate user group based on the functional area of the new joiner (e.g., FICO, ABAP, HR). You can decide appropriate user group for the new joiner based on the description of the user group, to be selected according the job and responsibilities. The selected group will then populate in the main form. Next, set the following: Request Type – Select New User Access . Request Reason – Provide a valid business justification. Once all information has been entered correctly, click 'Submit Access Request'. A GRC request will be generated for the user in all the selected systems and the status of this GRC can be tracked using the below link: https://knecigrp9800.konenet.com:44398/sap/bc/webdynpro/sap/zkone_search_request# After the request has been processed and approved through all required stages, the user account will be created in SAP ECC systems with the necessary access to perform day-to-day tasks. If you encounter any error while raising GRC, please open a ticket for SAP Security & GRC team. Once the request is processed and approved, user will get an automatic email notification informing the request completion. Since KONE SAP systems are equipped with Single Sign-On (SSO), password will not be generated and shared. The user can login to the system using SSO. Access Submission Process in SAP ECC: To submit access request for SAP GRC - GRP system, from where the AMS consultants raise access requests for Firefighter (FF ID) access. Submitting a GRC Access Request in SAP GRP Once successfully logged in to the SAP GRC End User Portal (same process as discussed above), the requestor will be directed to the home screen. From here, click on the “Primary User Group Form” link to initiate an access request for a new joiner in the SAP GRP system which is used for raising FF ID requests. Access Submission Process in SAP GRP: To submit an access request in SAP GRC - GRP system which is used for raising firefighter (FF ID) access request. Submitting a GRC Access Request in SAP GRP Once successfully logged in to the SAP GRC End User Portal, the requestor will be directed to the home screen. From here, click on the “Primary User Group Form” link to initiate an access request for a new joiner in the SAP GRP system which is used for raising FF ID requests. A new page will open. Complete all required fields as follows: KONE Network User ID – Enter the User ID of the individual requiring access in SAP ECC. SAP Landscape/System – Select SAP GRC (In the check-boxes, select only GRP GRC Production) Org Level 1 – Select Technology & Innovation (TI) . Org Level 2 – Select GLBL . After entering the above details, open the Primary User Group field by clicking on the double-square icon or pressing the F4 key. A pop-up window will appear. Click the Search button to display all available IT user groups. Select the FF related user group – ACC_FFU_GLB which grants access to Infosys consultants to raise FF access request in SAP GRC system. DO NOT raise GRC for any other user group in SAP GRP system, as it will lead to the rejection of access request. The selected group will then populate in the main form. Next, set the following: Request Type – Select New User Access . Request Reason – Provide a valid business justification. Once all information has been entered correctly, click Submit Access Request. A GRC request will be generated for the user in all the selected systems. Once the request is processed and approved, user will get automatic email notification informing the request completion. Since KONE SAP systems are equipped with Single Sign-On (SSO), password will not be generated and shared. The user can login to the system using SSO. Access Submission Process in SAP SOLMAN: To submit access request for SAP SA4 system, which is used for NC creation and transport management on ChaRM portal. Submitting a GRC Access Request in SAP SOLMAN Once successfully logged in to the SAP GRC End User Portal, the requestor will be directed to the home screen. From here, click on the “SAP Access Request Form” link to initiate an access request for a new joiner in the SAP Solman system which is used for creating/managing NCs on ChaRM. A new page will open. Complete all required fields as follows: SAP Landscape/System – Select SOLMAN (In the check-boxes, select only SA4 SOLMAN PROD) Work Region – SOL_GLBL KONE Network User ID – Enter the User ID of the individual requiring access in SAP ECC. Request Type – New User Account Does user need access based on Clone ID? – Yes Clone User ID - Enter the User ID of the individual from the same team who already has access in Solman. Request Reason – Enter a valid business justification as why access is needed Once all information has been entered correctly, click Submit Access Request. A GRC request will be generated for the user in all the selected systems. Once the request is processed and approved, user will get automatic email notification informing the request completion. Since KONE SAP systems are equipped with Single Sign-On (SSO), password will not be generated and shared. The user can login to the system using SSO. Note: To login to ChaRM portal, login to the SA4 system using SSO and then execute t-code SM_CRM which will open the ChaRM portal and will not require you to enter a password for logging in.

short_description: SMCR - How to check "PROD_DML_dml-s-productionorders-smcr" alert message ?
introduction: Inbound interface to receive production orders from Mulesoft
instructions: Step 1: Login to the SMCR Portal https://smcr.kone.com/ Step 2: Click on the Home tab. Step 3: If you see the error message “None of the punching operations under the production order <SO number>-<line item>/<PO number> contain components – rejecting” as shown in the screenshot below Step 4: Please follow the instructions below. Description Kind Resolution Communication “None of the punching operations under the production order <SO number>-<line item>/<PO number> contain components à rejecting”: when SCMR receives a production order with punching operation(s) but without components, it rejects the production order. This is also known as “no BOM” –error. The reason such production orders are rejected is that components carry a “reservation number” which is needed in the interface towards Mulesoft when manipulating components. When this error occurs, HTTP status code “400 Bad Request” is returned to Mulesoft, triggering an Application Error recorded in Splunk. Splunk, Log, Email notification No need for IT involvement. Key user to check the order if the order actually contains punching, add the operation and re-trigger manually if the order doesn’t have punching, modify the SAP-table for KM-ids that shall not be sent automatically to SMCR Send email to the key user (currently ahti.alanoja2@kone.com)

short_description: How to create ITSM Access request in MyAccess
introduction: This article explains how to request access to ITSM (ServiceNow) using the MyAccess portal.
instructions: Overview : The ITSM platform requires specific roles to be assigned based on your job responsibilities. All ITSM access requests must be submitted through the MyAccess Portal for approval and provisioning. Steps to Request ITSM Access: Step-1: Login to MyAccess < KONE MyAccess - Home > Go to the MyAccess Portal. Step-2: click on Manage Access Step-3: Click on Manage My Access Step-4: Search "ServiceNow" Steo-5: Check the role and click next Step-6: Review and submit the request Please refer the below table for Access Roles and Descriptions: MyAccess Roles MyAccess Description ServiceNow CMDB: Legal&PrivacyAssessment user This role provides kone legal team to review the cyber security and legal assessment questionaires and update the assessment value in CMDB. It provides visibility to all fields in the CMDB. ServiceNow CMDB: CybersecurityAssessment user This role provides kone cyber security team to review the cyber security and legal assessment questionaires and update the assessment value in CMDB. It provides visibility to all fields in the CMDB ServiceNow ITSM: ChangeAndRelease_Manager This role provide access to manage change management lifecycle. ServiceNow CMDB: DataOwner_Infra_classes This role provides full visibility into all fields within the CMDB. It allows users to create and update computing-related CIs such as Servers, Databases, Virtual Machine (VM) Instances, Clusters, and Application Servers, along with managing required relationships and cloud relationships. ServiceNow CMDB: DataOwner_OSSUser This role enables Onsite support users to update cmdb related to workstation assignment and status changes ServiceNow CMDB: DataOwner_HAMUser This role enables Hardware asset management team to maintain cmdb workstations data. This will also include computer CI's, Printer CI's. ServiceNow CMDB: DataOwner_Network_class This role enables Network teams (connectivity Team) to maintain Network related CI's in CMDB ServiceNow CMDB: DataOwner_Printer_class This role enables users to maintain Printers only in CMDB ServiceNow CMDB: DataOwner_RandD_Assets This role enables the users to create and update the Hardware records with Asset Function "R&D Lab Devices" ServiceNow CMDB: DataOwner_OperationsManager/Lead This role provides broad access to ITSM and CMDB modules. It enables users to view and update ITSM records (Incidents, Requests, Changes, Problems, Releases, Tasks), manage CMDB records including Business Applications, Services, Offerings, and Product Models (excluding assessment value fields), and create, edit, and approve Knowledge Articles, Reports, and Dashboards. ServiceNow CMDB: DataOwner_ProductManager/Owner This role provides broad access to ITSM and CMDB modules. It enables users to view and update ITSM records (Incidents, Requests, Changes, Problems, Releases, Tasks), manage CMDB records including Business Applications, Services, Offerings, and Product Models (excluding assessment value fields), and create, edit, and approve Knowledge Articles, Reports, and Dashboards. short_description: SMCR - How to handle SMCR Orders Not Pushed Issue
introduction: To define the standard process to follow when SMCR orders are not pushed despite successful programming.
instructions: Step 1 : Check with user and validate Order Status in SMCR Check total orders vs pushed orders Identify number of pending orders Step 2 : Check with user verify Programming Completion. Confirm programming completed in: OPS Lantek Amada Step 3 : User need to check output files Navigate to output folders Verify XML files are generated for pending orders Step 4 : Identify Recent System Changes Check if server reboot happened Verify if reboot was due to Windows patching or maintenance Step 5 : Validate Ignition File Share Access Check if file shares are mapped Ensure folders are accessible from server Step 6 : Restart Ignition Services Restart all relevant Ignition services Ensure services are running properly Step 7 : Monitor SMCR application Check if pending orders are getting pushed Validate order flow is resumed Step 8 : Confirm resolution Ensure all pending orders are processed No backlog remains in SMCR NOTE : - Commonly occurs after server reboot due to Windows patching. - Always validate file share mapping post reboot.

short_description: How to create ITSM Queue request to Create,Modify or Delete any queue in ServiceNow.
introduction: This article explains How to request Queue creation, updation and deletion use cases in ITSM (ServiceNow).
instructions: Overview: The objective of this article is to provide clear guidance on how users can request queue creation, updation, and deletion use cases within the ITSM (ServiceNow) platform. It helps users understand the proper process for submitting requests related to managing queues, ensuring that service workflows are organized efficiently and accurately. The article aims to: Explain the steps involved in requesting a new queue creation. Describe how to modify or update existing queue details based on operational needs. Outline the procedure for deleting queues that are no longer required. Ensure requests are submitted correctly through ServiceNow to avoid delays or errors. Support efficient ticket routing, workload management, and service desk operations through proper queue administration. Overall, the article is intended to streamline queue management activities and maintain effective service delivery within ITSM. Steps to Request ITSM Queue Request: Step 1: Click on the provided link to open the catalog item in ServiceNow. ServiceNow IT Service Management (ITSM) Queue Requests - Kone Employee center Step 2: Select Self if you are submitting the request for yourself. Step 3: Select Others if you are raising the request for someone else. Step 4: Choose the required action from the available options: Create for a new queue Modify to update an existing queue Delete to remove an existing queue Step 5: If Create is selected, enter the Queue Name, Group Type, Parent group and Queue Manager details. Step 6: If Modify is selected, provide the New Queue Name and New Queue Manager details. Step 7: If Delete is selected, enter the Existing Queue Name. Step 8: Review the entered details and submit the request.

short_description: How to check mail flow
introduction: This KBA is published Only for Office 365 - L1.5 AMSD Support Team Process for Checking Mail Flow Purpose This KBA provides step-by-step guidance for administrators to: Validate email mail flow for users Identify and troubleshoot mail delivery issues Verify whether a user or entity is listed under Restricted Entities Scope This KBA applies when users report: Unable to send emails External emails failing Emails not delivered Policy or restriction errors Internal mail delivery issues Bounce-back / NDR messages Required Details from User (Mandatory) Before troubleshooting, collect: Affected user email address Sender email address Recipient email address Date and time issue occurred Error message or NDR (if available) Do not proceed without these details.
instructions: Procedure How to Check Mail Flow Using Message Trace: Steps to Run Message Trace: 1. Login to Exchange Admin Center https://admin.exchange.microsoft.com/#/ 2. Go to Mail Flow → Message Trace 3. Click Start Trace 4. Provide: Sender email address Recipient email address Subject Date range If the time range provided by the user is within the last 10 days , perform the mail trace from Exchange Admin Center : Exchange Admin Center → Mail Flow → Message Trace If the time range provided by the user is more than 10 days , perform the mail trace using Microsoft 365 Defender Explorer . Steps to Perform Mail Trace in Explorer Log in to Explorer Navigate to the following URL: https://security.microsoft.com/threatexplorerv3?tid=2bb82c64-2eb1-43f7-8862-fdc1d2333b50 Enter the required details: Sender email address Recipient email address Subject Date range Run the search in Explorer to perform a mail trace (up to 30 days) and review the message delivery status and actions taken . Review Message Status Common outcomes: Delivered – Not a mail flow issue Failed – Review failure reason Blocked – Often due to compliance or restricted entity Expanded / Pending – Continue extended trace if needed Process To Validate Users Under Restricted Entities: 1. Sign in to the Microsoft Defender portal using administrator credentials. 2. Navigate to: Email & collaboration → Review → Restricted entities . 3. Therefore, if a user account has been added to Restricted Entities, it will be listed under the Restricted Entities section. If no results are found, the user is not currently restricted Common Reason for Restricted Entity Status A user is automatically added to Restricted Entities when the account exceeds the permitted external recipient limit . The configured limit is 500 external recipients per hour . Once exceeded, the user is blocked from sending emails, resulting in mail delivery failures. Next Actions AMSD L1.5 does not have the required privileges to remove users from Restricted Entities. This case must be escalated to the Cyber Security team for review and to release the user’s email address from Restricted Entities. Additionally: If a mail trace indicates that the user is blocked at the tenant level, the AMSD team will notify the Cyber Security team via email to request unblocking . The case will then be routed to the Cyber Security team for further action and resolution.

short_description: Add / Update Primary Users details via Intune Portal
introduction: Overview The Primary User in Microsoft Intune is the person’s most associated device. This affects multiple key features such as self-service password reset, Company Portal access, and user-targeted app deployments. Assigning the correct primary user ensures that the right policies, apps, and compliance rules are applied to each device. What is a Primary User? When a Windows device is enrolled in Intune, the first person to sign in is automatically set as the Primary User. However, IT Teams can manually change or assign this at any time from the Microsoft Intune admin center based on Intune role granted to the team. Why It Matters • Enables user-based conditional access policies. • Allows self-service BitLocker recovery from My Account portal. • Determines user under Company Portal for their devices. • Required for user-targeted app deployments to work correctly
instructions: Prerequisites Before proceeding, ensure the following requirements are met: • You are signed in to the Microsoft Intune Admin Center • You have proper permissions/Role to perform operation. • The target Windows device is already enrolled in Intune. • The user account exists in Entra ID. Note : Only one Primary User can be assigned per device. Assigning a new primary user will replace the existing one. The change takes effect immediately in the admin center. Assign Primary User via Intune Admin Center This is the most common method and is recommended for individual device assignments. Step-by-Step Instructions 1. Open the Intune Admin Center Navigate to https://intune.microsoft.com and sign in with your -Admin credentials. 2. Navigate to Devices ->In the left navigation pane, click on Devices. This will open the Devices overview blade. 3. Select Windows Devices->Under Devices, click on Windows to filter and view only Windows-enrolled devices. 4. Find the Target Device->Search for the device by name, serial number, or user. Click on the device to open its detail page. 5. Open Properties On the device detail page, click Properties in the top menu or the left-side navigation within the device view. 6. Change Primary User-> Scroll down to find the Primary User field. Click on the current user or the empty field, then search for and select the new user. 7. Save Changes-> Click the Save button at the top to apply the new Primary User assignment. The change is immediate. Verifying the Primary User Assignment After assigning the primary user, follow these steps to confirm the change was applied correctly. Via Intune Admin Center 1. Go to Devices > Windows in the Intune Admin Center 2. Search for and open the target device 3. Click Properties 4. Confirm the Primary User field shows the correct user 5. Verify the Last Sync Time is recent (if not, trigger a Sync) Via Company Portal (User Verification) Ask the user to open the Company Portal app on the device and confirm: • Their name appears at the top of the portal • The device is listed under 'My Devices' • Assigned apps are visible and available for install

short_description: SPD - Calculation of M2M Cum Into Project (EUR)
introduction: The M2M Cum Into Project (EUR) represents the cumulative M2M value calculated based on price differences between 2026 monthly values and 2025 baseline values, adjusted with volume.
instructions: Step 1: Calculate Weighted Average Price for Each Month 2026 (SUM([Fixed Price Purchase currency/T]*[Exchange Rate Local Currency] * [Volume (Tonne)]/1000))*1000/SUM([Volume (Tonne)]) Step 2: Calculate Weighted Average Price for all months in 2025 (SUM([Fixed Price Purchase currency/T]*[Exchange Rate Local Currency] * [Volume (Tonne)]/1000))*1000/SUM([Volume (Tonne)]) Step 3: It will be multiplied with [Volume (Tonne)] which will be divided 1000 for each month. Step 4 : Then all the 12 month data will be summationed to show as M2M cum into project EUR.

short_description: How to create SAP Access Request for ECC using SAP GRC?
introduction: This KBA will provide instructions on how to create GRC request for SAP ECC / ERP systems access.
instructions: SAP Access Request Process for ECC There are three options to choose when user needs access to SAP ECC system(s) (Z0*) 1) How to create an access request for New user to SAP ERP systems. We have two options: a) Clone ID based request b) Transaction/Role based request 2) How to create a Transaction code or role based request on top of existing accesses. User is already having accesses, but something additional is required 3) User group-based request There are user groups created for certain areas/job roles. Find the user group matrix behind the link: KONE Access Requests Approval Matrix.xlsx (sharepoint.com) ECC systems are: Z01, Development (only for IT users) Z02, Test Z03, Pre-Production Z04, Production Note: First two options are covered in this KBA, user group-based request is explained in KBA2725 Open GRC Enduser form Choose the SAP Landscape to be SAP ERP and define the access that is needed for which system(s); development / test / pre-prod / production. Work Region/Unit is a mandatory field and will route the possible Segregation of Duty risks to the correct approver based on given information. This should be chosen based on the unit/country the user is working for that is not necessarily the same as user’s physical location. Enter your KONE Network ID that will work as your SAP ID as well and press enter. This will automatically populate the correct full name details as well as line manager from Active Directory. NOTE! Access request can be also raised on behalf of the user. If you don’t know the KONE Network ID, you can search the user behind the duplicate boxes -button. Next steps are either to create a access request for the New user account or creating the access request for additional/missing accesses for existing user. 1. How to create an access request for New user to SAP ERP systems. We have two options: a) Clone ID based request b) Transaction/Role based request a) Clone ID based request for New User Account User who is not having any accesses in ECC and need access like colleague’s id, who already has access to SAP system and has similar job responsibilities of new user. Select the “Request Type” as “New User Account” from the drop down as shown below. Once the “Request Type” is selected as “New user Account” then new field “Does user need access based on Clone ID?” will be displayed with “Yes/No” options. Select as “Yes”. Once the field “Does user need access based on Clone ID?” is selected as “Yes” then a new field “Clone User ID” will get displayed. Define the Clone User ID whose accesses should be copied to new user’s account. If you don’t know the KONE Network ID, you can search the user behind the duplicate boxes -button. Fill in the “Request Reason” on why the access is required to the user. NOTE: Request Reason is limited to only 500 characters. please fill in accordingly. Once the “Request Reason” is filled in then click on “Submit Access Request” button, there will be a pop-up to make sure that you have selected the correct work region/unit and after that you will get confirmation that request was created. b. Transaction/Role based request for New user Account Select the “Request Type” as “New User Account” from the drop down as shown below. Once the “Request Type” is selected as “New user Account” then new field “Does user need access based on Clone ID?” will be displayed with “Yes/No” options. Select as “No”. Once the field “Does user need access based on Clone ID?” is selected as “No” then “Transaction Codes / Search and add role” section will be added to the screen. Enter Transaction code(s) in the “Transaction codes” field and click on “Search Roles”. If there are multiple transaction codes then use comma (,) as separator by separating transactions with a comma. NOTE: No extra space between transactions. Choose the required roles role(s) for respective system by clicking the box against respective role under “Available roles in the system” section as shown in the below sample screen and selecting “Add selected roles” Note: You may refer to below SharePoint link for list of roles in SAP ERP Production (Z04) system with corresponding t-codes details in respective role for more details. https://kone.sharepoint.com/:x:/r/teams/Common%20Platforms/_layouts/15/Doc.aspx?sourcedoc=%7B497A3EA0-9876-45CB-9DB2-42D3C15C2FAD%7D&file=A%20list%20of%20all%20SAP%20roles%20in%20Z04.xlsx&action=default&mobileredirect=true If there are no roles available for the transaction provided there will be a note to contact KONE SAP Security Service Desk via email. Email: KONE_SAP_Security_GRC@KONE.COM like shown in the below screen. If you know already the role that you need to have, you may search that directly behind “Search and add role” link. After search, click the box to choose the role (same role listed per transactions within that role) and press “OK”. When you have found all the required roles, fill in the “Request Reason” on why the access is required to the user. NOTE: Request Reason is limited to only 500 characters. please fill in accordingly. Once the “Request Reason” is filled in then click on “Submit Access Request” button, there will be a pop-up to make sure that you have selected the correct work region/unit and after that you will get confirmation that request was created. 2. How to create a Transaction code or role based request on top of existing accesses. This option is used if you are already having some accesses but need accesses in addition. Select the “Request Type” as “Additional/missing accesses (added on top of existing accesses)” from the drop down as shown below. Define the missing transaction code in the “Transaction codes” field and search the roles. Choose the required roles role(s) for respective system by clicking the box against respective role under “Available roles in the system” section as shown in the below sample screen and selecting “Add selected roles” Note: You may refer to SharePoint link: Roles and Tcodes mapping for list of roles in SAP ERP Production (Z04) system with corresponding t-codes details in respective role for more details. You can also search for several transactions at the same time for the same work region/unit by separating transactions with a comma. NOTE! No extra space between transactions! If there are no roles available for the transaction provided there will be a note to contact KONE SAP Security Service Desk via email. Email: KONE_SAP_Security_GRC@KONE.COM If you know already the role that you need to have, you may search that directly behind “Search and add role” link. After search, click the box to choose the role (same role listed per transactions within that role) and press “OK”. When you have found all the required roles, fill in the “Request Reason” on why the access is required to the user. NOTE: Request Reason is limited to only 500 characters. please fill in accordingly. Once the “Request Reason” is filled in then click on “Submit Access Request” button, there will be a pop-up to make sure that you have selected the correct work region/unit and after that you will get confirmation that request was created. Access request has been sent to the approval round and once all approvals have been provided and the user access has been provisioned by GRC in the target landscape, you will receive a mail with the request details then login to the respective requested system using the SSO (Single Sign On). You can follow the status of the request via Check status . Instructions provided in KBA1538 Important Note While logging into SAP Access Request Form, in case system shows user ID "GRC_EUIBB" already logged in by some other user then uncheck / untick the "Cancel all existing logons" option and click on continue button as shown in below screen shot. If "Cancel all existing logons" is not unchecked / unticked and then if you have clicked on "Continue" button, then system will close all other user's sessions in the middle who already actively using the tool.

short_description: Ask IT - How to approve SAP Access Request as a Line Manager using SAP GRC?
introduction: How to approve or reject GRC request as line manager?
instructions: SAP Access Request Process for ECC Once an access request is submitted by the end user/key user, an email is triggered automatically to the line manager of the request for the 1st level approval. Approve/Reject SAP Access Request as a Line Manager directly from email: In the email you can see the user who is requesting access to SAP, the reason and the roles / user group that are included in the request. As a line manager your duty is to validate that the user needs SAP in his/her work and that correct accesses are requested; correct user group / correct Clone ID. To Approve / Reject the SAP Access Request, line manager needs to click on respective link. A new window will appear and there is a need to just click Send . Alternatively, you can directly reply to the email sent by GRC system by writing either Approve or Reject. Note : While approving / rejecting the access request, please do not change the subject line of the e-mail. If the subject line of e-mail is changed, system will not understand decision of manager whether request is approved / rejected. Do not use Chinese characters! After actions taken, workflow is routed to the respective role owners/user group approvers for next steps.

short_description: How to approve SAP Access Request as a Role Owner/User Group Approver in SAP GRC system?
introduction: How to approve or reject GRC request as role owner/user group approver?
instructions: SAP Access Request Process for ECC Once the access request is approved by user’s line manager it will be routed to the respective role owners / user group approvers. Role owners are identified per unit/country. User group approvers are nominated for each user group. Please find the role owner approver mapping from the Role matrix: KONE Access Requests Approval Matrix.xlsx (sharepoint.com) and user group approvers from User Group matrix: KONE Access Requests Approval Matrix.xlsx (sharepoint.com) Access request is either created based on the given Clone ID, based on missing transactions, or required user group(s). Role owner/User group approver will receive an email about the new request that shows all the roles / user groups user has requested with the reason. If the request contains roles for several different units e.g. two for Canada and three for US, approvals are requested from respective approvers based on unit/country. Your name is visible in the table of roles that are requiring your actions. You cannot take actions regarding any other roles listed in the request. Approve/Reject SAP Access Request as a Role Owner/User Group Approver directly from email: To Approve / Reject the SAP Access Request, approver needs to click on respective link. A new window will appear and there is a need to just click Send . With this action, you approve/reject all the roles where you were set as role owner/user group approver. Alternatively, you can directly reply to the email sent by GRC system by writing either Approve or Reject. Note : While approving / rejecting the access request, please do not change the subject line of the e-mail. If the subject line of e-mail is changed, system will not understand decision of role owner whether request is approved / rejected. Do not use Chinese character s! Partially Approve the SAP Access Request If the request contains both the roles that you want to approve and some that you want to reject, you need to login to the SAP GRC system to take actions per role following below instructions. 1> Open GRP Production (SSO) from SAP GUI log on pad. 2> Execute NWBC t-code. 3> Click on "Work Inbox" quick link under "My Home" tab in browser screen opened after executing NWBC t-code. 4> Open request from work inbox for approval / rejection actions in access request. Once clicked on request link / open request the request from work inbox as shown above, role owner will be redirected to the GRC SAP Access Request Page where you can see the list of SAP Roles that you are the approver of in this specific request. If the bullet in Risk Violations column is red, it indicates that the requested role is containing such access that is a part of SoD conflict for the user. More details about the transactions that are causing SoD can be seen in Risk Violation tab. By changing the Format to Detail, approver can see the data in the requested role that is causing SoD risk(s). Clicking on the Access Risk ID will open a new pop-up window with description of the Access Risk. After reviewing the reasons for SoD conflicts, select the User Access tab to process the request. Now you can reject the role(s) that are causing SoD conflict(s) by selecting “Reject” from the drop-down menu. This needs to be done to each role that you want to reject. Give your comment behind the “Comments” tab. And then choose “Submit” beside the “Other Actions” tab. Now the request moves to the next stage in the process and is hence no longer visible in the role owner's GRC work inbox. Now you may close the request. If there are some more requests in work inbox, you can follow similar steps as explained above to approve / reject or to partially approve / reject the requests from work inbox. You may close the work inbox screen after required approval / rejections actions taken for requests in work inbox. You may log off from GRC, when you do not have any further actions with respect to Access Requests approvals following below steps. Click on “OK” to close the session

short_description: Ask IT - How to install Standard Software via SCCM
introduction: Ask IT - How to install Standard Software via SCCM?
instructions: How can user raise ticket with Ask IT? User can request the software request via Ask IT portal, Ask IT chat or email to Ask IT. What do Ask IT need to confirm before proceeding with the installation? Ask IT to collect and confirm the below required details - 1. Computer Name: 2. Software /Application Name: 3. Availability of the user: A. How Ask IT to proceed if the software is in SCCM? Check if the software is in SCCM. Proceed with the software installation as per KB0000A576 After the software push, inform the user to keep their device online and connected to the KONE network or VPN for successful installation. Follow up with the user after one day to confirm that the software installation was successful. If installation is successful, upon user confirmation, we can resolve the ticket. If the installation is not successful, Ask IT tries to "push" again the software via SCCM. If the installation fails for 2 consecutive times, please send the ticket to RDS team (applicable for single user cases) for further troubleshooting. B. How Ask IT to proceed if the software is not in SCCM or not in software list? Please check if the software is in SCCM. If neither present in the attached list nor in SCCM, please advise the end-user to contact their respective IT SDM and raise a packaging request with help of Ask IT. Note: If Ask IT is not able to reach the end-user within 5 business days, then Ask IT will apply the 3-strike rule and resolve the ticket. During 2nd and 3rd strike, user's manager to be kept in cc of the email.

short_description: ASKIT - AutoCAD Approval Process
introduction: ASKIT - AutoCAD Approval Process
instructions: Only approved users are allowed to have AutoCAD installed. Please refer to KBA1471 ( https://konewayflow.my.salesforce.com/a2j0O000001CBCP) for further details. AutoCAD approval process: User requests AutoCAD installations Verify, if user is already an approved AutoCAD user. To do so, check if he/she is member of AD Group ADACADUSERS . Even for PC Refresh, verify the group membership. User is member of ADACADUSERS: Jump to step 5. User is not member of ADACADUSERS: Proceed to next step. GSD to explain to user, that we have a limited amount of AutoCAD licenses and that this software is primarily reserved for heavyweight users. Further business case evaluation is required. Alternative software "progeCAD" is usually sufficient and requires only line managers approval. Ask, if progeCAD could be installed instead. If user agrees to install progeCAD instead, ask him/her to request for MyAccess role progeCAD: English / Chinese / German (depending on language preferences). If user insists to have AutoCAD installed, ask him/her to request for MyAccess role AutoCAD: English . A business justification needs to be attached to the MyAccess request. After request has been approved in MyAccess, the software will be installed via SCCM. AutoCAD Mechanical approval process: AutoCAD Mechanical is a version of AutoCAD with extra features used for Mechanical Design. Usually the user would not require this version, and ordinary 2D CAD (AutoCAD / progeCAD) is sufficient. If indeed AutoCAD Mechanical is required, follow the process below. User requests AutoCAD Mechanical installations Ask user to request for MyAccess role AutoCAD Mechanical: English . A business justification needs to be attached to the MyAccess request. After request has been approved in MyAccess, the installation will be triggered via Software Center. AutoCAD Electrical has been ramped down and is no longer used in KONE. Alternative software is EPLAN.

short_description: ASKIT How to give access to PLT (ESS, MSS) - SAP H04
introduction: How to give access to PLT (ESS, MSS) - SAP H04?
instructions: User ID SAP account [ ] Yes [ ] NO access needed to [ ] ESS [ ] MSS contact data: Detailed description of issue: PLT access request Tempalate attached: Approval from HR/HR make the request: Attached the original email containing the attachment (excel file) detailing the access. Please fill in the Service Request form: --------------------------------------------------------------------------------- ----------------------------------------------------- From 1st of May this ticket have to be open on MyIT portal on behalf of the user. Please follow this BG227888 and select the following SR form for fowarding the case the right assigment group: Access and Identity - Request Access - SAP & CPM --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- ----------------------------------------- To grant the ESS (or MSS) access we (GSD SAP UAM team) need to create the user in the SAP system H04: IMPORTANT INFO BEFORE HANDLING THE REQUEST: - Requests pertaining to HR access should ideally be raised by Local/Global HR for PLT access and GED access. - Requests containing Clone ID for H* System access should be rejected . The relevant authorization (Roles) should be clearly mentioned in the request by the requester for Security Team to process the ticket. If the above points have been checked, you can continue with the following Instructions: 1. In the User Maintenance panel, with the same user id insert, click on the “change password” icon. 2. In the “change password” window: select the H04 system and press the “Deactivate Password” icon on the bottom press the tick button to apply the change 3. Now the user creation has been completed and a ticket must be sent to the Sap Security Team in order to finalize the access. 4. In the ticket the agent must specify: - User id - SAP System where the User has been created - Reference user to clone OR specified Role/transaction to add to the user profile (info provided by the requestor) - the manager that is authorizing the order When an HR request is raised, the original email might contain attachment (excel file/PDF file) detailing the requirement. GSD is requested to ensure the file and/or the email is appropriately attached in the ticket . The ticket must then to be routed to CUST.KONE.INT.SAP.SECURITY 5. Once received the ticket, the SAP Security will take care to finalize the SAP account both in the system requested and the ESS (or MSS) portal. ATTENTION: IN CASE OF MULTIPLE ACCESS REQUESTS RAISED AT THE SAME TIME, PLEASE FOLLOW THE BELOW RULES Access to SAP HR and CPM systems. ECC SAP systems (Z04, Z03, Z02, Z01) and Invoice Verification Portal system for less than 20 users the GSD opens 1 ticket for all the requested users and send it to the 2nd level team Access to SAP HR and CPM systems. ECC SAP systems (Z04, Z03, Z02, Z01) and Invoice Verification Portal system for more than 20 users a KWC (KONE change way request) needs to be raised directly from the end-user The below URL contains the requested instruction / information pertaining to KONE Way Change : http://intranet.konenet.com/Toolssupport/Businesstoolsandsupport/kone_way_change/Pages/default.aspx SAP account creation is task from GSD! Please speak with your Expert/ Supervisor in case you dont know, who run the SAP UAM inside GSD.

short_description: ASKIT - How to log a case for China KC3 CRM webKonect incident
introduction: What information needs to be collected to log a case for?
instructions: Do you have an ERROR on the screen? If so, which one? Since when does the problem occurred? Can you please send us a screenshot? Do all agents have/see the same error? How many users are impacted? How many equipment are impacted? (1 equipment = 1 user) Can you please provide the actions performed while getting the error? Also provide more details of the problem Does it have an impact on the external customers, direct or indirect financial impact or security/safety issues? Is there the risk that Entrapment calls of customers can be missed? Is the problem related to: ROUTINE calls, System alert calls, Active Alarm calls, RIF Programming calls, all/general... and describe what is wrong with it?

short_description: How to approve SAP Access Request as a SoD Approver in SAP GRC system?
introduction: How to approve or reject GRC request as SoD approver? How can I understand the SoD that I need to approve?
instructions: SAP Access Request Process for ECC Segregation of Duty risk arises when one user is having so wide accesses that there is a possibility to commit a fraud and have a negative effect on financials of the company. There are currently 80 SoD conflicts defined in KONE Risk Library. In the Risk Library documentation, you can see all the active risks and the transactions that are causing the risk on users’ accesses. Link: KONE Risk Library SoD conflicts are either defined as medium or high. The latter ones are such that if user is having and misusing those accesses (transactions), there is a clear impact on financials of the company. These high-level SoD conflicts need to be separately approved by nominated person of the unit/country. There are also three Critical Actions that need to have an additional global approval. Mapping of the risk approvers can be found behind the link: KONE Access Requests Approval Matrix.xlsx (sharepoint.com) Once an access request is created and approved by the Line manager and either approved or partially approved by Role owner(s), an email is triggered automatically to the SoD owner in case the requested accesses cause new high-level SoD risks with details as below. Approve/Reject SAP Access Request as a SoD approver directly from email: To Approve / Reject the SAP Access Request, approver needs to click on respective link. A new window will appear and there is a need to just click Send . With this action, you approve/reject all the listed SoD conflicts that will arise for the user based on the requested accesses. Alternatively, approver can directly reply to the email sent by GRC system by writing either Approve or Reject. Note : While approving / rejecting the access request, please do not change the subject line of the e-mail. If the subject line of e-mail is changed, system will not understand decision of SoD approver whether request is approved / rejected. Do not use Chinese character s! Partially Approve the SAP Access Request If the request contains both the roles that you want to approve and some that you want to reject, you need to login to the SAP GRC system to take actions per role following below instructions. 1> Open GRP Production (SSO) from SAP GUI log on pad. 2> Execute NWBC t-code. 3> Click on "Work Inbox" quick link under "My Home" tab in browser screen opened after executing NWBC t-code. 4> Open request from work inbox for approval / rejection actions in access request . Once clicked on request link / open request the request from work inbox as shown above, the SoD approver will be redirected to the GRC Access Request screen to see list of SAP roles pending for approval and the SoD conflicts that user will have after approved roles are assigned to him/her. If the bullet in Risk Violations column is red, it indicates that the requested role is containing such access that is a part of SoD conflict for the user. More details about the transactions that are causing SoD can be seen in Risk Violation tab. By changing the Format to Detail, approver can see the data in the requested role that is causing SoD risk(s). Clicking on the Access Risk ID will open a new pop-up window with description of the Access Risk. After reviewing the reasons for SoD conflicts, select the User Access tab to process the request. Now you can reject the role that is causing a SoD conflict for the user by selecting “Reject” from drop-down menu. This needs to be to each role that you want to reject. Give your comment behind the “Comments” tab. And then choose “Submit” beside the “Other Actions” tab. Now the accesses are provisioned to the user according to the approvals/rejections given and request is no longer visible in the SoD approver’s GRC work inbox. Now you may close the request. If there are some more requests in work inbox, you can follow similar steps as explained above to approve / reject or to partially approve / reject the requests from work inbox. You may close the work inbox screen after required approval / rejections actions taken for requests in work inbox. You may log off from GRC, when you do not have any further actions with respect to Access Requests approvals following below steps. Click on “OK” to close the session

short_description: How to set delegation in SAP GRC system?
introduction: How to set delegation in SAP GRC system as an approver? How to send approval requests to someone else during my holiday?
instructions: During the vacation/planned leave Line managers, Role owners and SoD approvers can set delegation in the GRC system so that requests can be processed in timely manner. NOTE! Existing approval requests are not routed to the delegate, only the ones created after delegation is successfully created in SAP GRC. Original approver will still get the email notifications during the leave but those can be left unnoted. HOW TO SET THE DELEGATION: Log on to the GRC via following link: https://knecigrp9800.konenet.com:44398/sap/bc/webdynpro/sap/grac_uibb_end_user_login?sap-client=100# Enter your Network User ID and Password to login to this portal (the same credentials which you use to login to your laptop/PC). Following screen appears where you choose “Approver Delegation”: Use your Network ID together with password for GRC (SAP GRP system) to login: If you don’t have access to GRP system, please send email t o KONE_SAP_Security_GRC@KONE.COM and request them to set up the delegation accordingly. Click on “Delegate” button Define the user who will act as your delegate during the leave. You can search the correct user based on different criteria. Define the correct validity time for the delegation and set the status as “Active”. Delegation can be set for SAP Access Request process (AC) and/or SAP Process Control testing / review. Click on “Save” and the delegation is saved successfully. If you are facing issues, please contact Kone SAP Security team: KONE_SAP_Security_GRC@KONE.COM

short_description: Ask IT - User unable to get the print preview of the output
introduction: User is unable to get the print preview generated of the output
instructions: If the user faces an issue while getting the print preview of the output from any tcode in SAP, then there could be a possibility that the user has not maintained the parameters in SU3 transaction. To maintain the parameters, Kindly give 'LOCL' in the OutputDevice field and check the checkbox for Output Immediately. After maintaining the parameters, save it and check if you are able to get the print preview generated for the same. Note : If the issue still exists, Kindly check with the support team for further investigation.

short_description: Ask IT - Windchill Publish Monitor
introduction: Windchill Publish Monitor
instructions: Publish Monitor Procedure 1. Login to Windchill with user having Site Administrative access 2. Go to Browse > Sites > Utilities 3. Click on " WVS Job Monitor " 4. In Publish Monitoring Window there are multiple jobs with Status “Ready, Executing, Job Successful, Job Failed”. 5. Click on tab ‘ Delete Successful’ to delete successful jobs after check the check box 6. Select ‘ Failed Jobs" from "Job summary for site "drop down to see Only Failed entries. 7. Go back to Utilities Page>Worker Agent Administrator. 8. Click on tab ‘Stop All’ to Stop All running Workers. If any queue is executing on worker it will not stop immediately, so one has to wait until it all worker gets stopped. This step executes one script to stop all Creo sessions of workers & clear respective caches. All Red Flags will change to Green. 9. Click on tab ‘Start All’ to Start All Workers. All Green Flags will change to Red. 10. Go back to Utilities Page>Queue Manager. Go to " Queue Management " 11. There are multiple ‘Publishing Queues’ present in queue manager. 12. Go to Publishing Queue where entries are present. Click on " info " icon to the View Entries. 13. In Entries Page,check the failed entry and right click on "info" then "Reset " 14. Follow above procedure till All Failed Entries in Worker Agent Administrator gets successful. 15. If any entry fails more than 3 times follow “Troubleshooting for Publishing” steps below. Troubleshooting for Publishing : 1. Open the failed cad model/Drawing in Creo 2. Make sure the cad model/Drawing opens successfully in Creo 3. If any failed features reported during the model/drawing opening, need to being creators notice 4. If the model opens successfully in Creo and failed in publishing in Windchill then need to report to L3 team- KONE.SC.CAD.L3

short_description: Ask IT - How to manage interfaces in Konect?
introduction: How do I manage interfaces in Konect?
instructions: KM85 - Interface Monitoring This chapter provides a general overview of the most important functions of the Interface Monitoring screen. Access To open the Interface Monitoring screen, start from the KM97-Welcome screen, and hit button “Interface Monitoring”, located in the Action panel (left part of the screen). This opens the KM85-InterfaceMonitoring window. When your user is defined to see information for more than one Company, you will get a list where you will need to select the Company for which you want to visualise the Interface details. Select a company from the list and hit the “OK” button. Interface details From this window you can have an overview of all interfaces defined for this specific Company, and get all relevant information on each of these. Per default, the overview shows you all interfaces, but you can limit the search result to for instance only show you the ‘Import’ interfaces for this Company, or another type of interface, instead of the total list. This can be done, using the selection buttons : We differenciate 4 types of interface : - Import interface : interface that transmits data from SAP into KONECT. - Export interface : interface that sends back data from KONECT to SAP. - Cronacle interface : in place (not used yet) - SQL interface : in place (not used yet) When looking at the details of a certain Interface, quite some details are directly visible from the table, such as the Status (currently ‘Running’ / ‘Not Running’), the ‘Last Run’ details, and the ‘Last Run Status’. When highlighting a specific interface, the Log file on the last interface run becomes visible. For interfaces that are currently not running, all functions from the ’Control Panel’ are accessible. (For interfaces that are in status ‘Running’, some of these functions are disabled until the interface stops running) Display (and export) interface log files This brings up a screen where you can consult both the interface log file, error file (the unprocesses data records) and/or the backup file (all successful imported data files). You have 5 possible actions: • Display interface file: displays the file to import to Konect or extracted from Konect. If the file is does not exist a ‘The page cannot be displayed’error appears. • Display backup file: when a file is imported in Konect or extracted from Konect, a backup is automatically performed by the system. It contains a list of all successfully imported records. • Display error file: this function is only available for IMPORT interface. When data are imported in Konect, some of them may not be processed (for example if some required data are missing). Those records are stored in a separated file on the Konect server. Use this button to see this file. • Display logging file: displays the log generated by Konect for the selected running date/time. The log file returns acknowledgements upon all separate steps of the import/export process, and where applicable the returned error codes. Note: if you have a text editor associated with this file extention, you can use the regular Save function to 'export' the file and send it ie by email. • Copy Error file for Re-import: this function is only available for IMPORT interface. If, for example, some records are not processed by the interface because some data are missing in Konect (like the organization). It is possible to re-process those records. If you click on this button the error file will be copied to the import directory and renamed to the import file name.

short_description: Ask IT - SAP Access for user
introduction: How to create a Service Request to request new or additional access in SAP?
instructions: ******************************************************************************************************************* Did you know that you can create an "SAP Access Request" via GRC yourself? More details available here: https://kone.sharepoint.com/ourwork/ittools/Pages/SAP-Access-Requests.aspx ******************************************************************************************************************* Procedure: 1) Go to Employee Center or Open the link. https://koneprod.service-now.com/esc 2) For SAP access, click on Work & IT 3) Click on Access under Work & IT 4) Click on SAP ERP Access Request. 5) The access form will be displayed on the popup screen which user needs to fill in. The mandatory fields are denoted by (Required) sign. Note: If the request is for someone else, the user can click on other and enter the email id of the requester. a) enter Access Start date, Access end date (in case end date is till 31.12.9999- select Access without end date as Yes. b) select the systems to which you need access for. Multiple systems can be selected based on your access requirement. c) enter the required Authorization access details like Transaction code or the required roles (based on transaction codes required by you) 1) Line manager of the user applying for access, is responsible for supplying the user with correct roles. 2) Access request should always be made for required roles only. Upon request, you need to provide justification for the roles applied. d) In case you are unable to determine the required transaction codes or roles, you can enter Clone or Reference User ID. (This is not a recommended method – please try to avoid this to the maximum extent possible. ) 1) If access is requested with reference to a clone, then a new separate request needs to be made for removal of any unnecessary roles, that are most likely included. (Employees with long KONE career have typically changed jobs within the Group and thus acquired significant volume of roles that they do not need in their current job. Therefore, it is very common that clone IDs involve several roles that are not required for the new user to perform his/her job.) 2) All the roles/access rights attached to a clone ID must be reviewed thoroughly before access request is initiated based on a clone. 3) If users job requires access that results in SoD risks, the relevant unit's Finance Director is responsible for ensuring that compensated controls are in place to mitigate risk created by broad access rights provided to the user. 4) It should be noted that clone ID based requests that provide broad access right, are likely to result in significant delay in user gaining ANY SAP access as approvers are likely to seek justification for the rights applied, which is time consuming . d) enter the work region/ unit based on your work area from the dropdown. Please select the "Work Region/Unit" carefully (some regions may have multiple units.)– it is important to ensure that the request goes to the right approver. In case you are not sure of your correct work region/unit, please consult your line manager. e) under additional information tab, you can mention a brief justification such as "access is needed to perform XXX responsibilities" f) add attachment (if any). For ex. Line Manager Approval, /nSU53 screenshot/Error Screenshot. 6) Submit.

short_description: Ask IT - How to debug an order 'in error' in KFM Monitoring?
introduction: How to debug an order 'in error' in KFM Monitoring?
instructions: 1. Go to "KFM Monitoring" - you can access this via the KM97-Welcome screen in Konect 2. Search for the order by terminal number, employee, order number or Status 3. Push the button 'Debug' This button is used to process records that are set in Error (ONLY VALID for INPUT tables) This button will not display when you are in one of the tabs of the KONECT to KFM Examples: a callout goes in unreachable and in the list of records in the “Online Input (SY**)” Tab you have an accepted that is set to Error a callout is set to interrupted and you have a record in the “Online Input (SY**)” that is Input type = back reporting + Escalation status = to be processed a technician requested a list of Planned order but he never received it and in the the “Online Input (SY**)” tab you have a record with Input type = Header Request and input status = Error When you see in the tab of KFM to KONECT a record that is in red or has Escalation status = to be processed Note: It may happen that during this "debugging" of an order you get a persistent error regarding timestamps . Timestamp-errors are not bugs. They are typical arising from manual actions or time sync issues on terminals (aka the clock on the device runs behind) in order to force logic in the data received and shared with the customer, the workflow is checking if the actions on orders are logically time stamped. This to show the customer we are not manipulating KPI's as agreed with our customer. example: you cannot back report before you arrived on site. In that case, consult the article about "KFM timestamp " errors.

short_description: Customer Insights - Salesforce & NPX support
introduction: How to support Customer Insights - NPX as AskIT?
instructions: Customer Insights - NPX The service the customer perceives is only as good as the service we provide during each interaction. Supporting KONE’s strategy, we measure our customer’s satisfaction through customer surveys, both on a relational and transactional level. This is done through our Customer Surveying tool NPX (SaaS provided by Satmetrix). The Customer Insight team is responsible for providing the means for capturing the voice of the customer throughout the customer’s relationship with KONE. From KFM/KONECT and SAP service orders/contacts will be collected in salesforce platform. NPX tool provided by satmatrix supplier will be used to manage the survey’s, below is the tool architecture for reference. Targeted audience for this tool are sales personal, supervisors, business owners, KC3 agent, service/operations manager and local admin users. Something doesn’t work Below scenario’s could be considered as an Incident. User would most probably be explaining one of these reasons Existing user cannot login to NPX No records in the manual survey entry page Data is missing from the NPX dashboard Low amount of survey count Contact data is missing Data discrepancy between salesforce and NPX Data update issues Note: Any new user creation request for NPX should be handled via MyAccess Instruction for access request : •Go to MyAccess home page KONE MyAccess – Home •Click “Manage User Access” option and it will redirect you to another page •In the search box, type the username or email address and click search icon •The username should be visible at the bottom of the page, select the user and click “Next” •Now search the NPX roles with the keyword and click on search icon •Select the correct role from the list and click “Next” Example: *NPX: xxxxxxx – For Production *NPX DEV: xxxxxx – For Staging Instance •Review the selection and click “Submit” Instruction for Incident (Something doesn't work) in ServiceNow Click "Something doesn't work" option in Service now ( Kone Employee Center Homepage - Kone Employee center (service-now.com) Service: KONE NPX or NPX SF Module Description: Fill in the details of the issue and preferably add the screenshot of the issue with error message. When did the problem start? What is your expected result? What is the error message? Describe briefly what you have done before and after the error Escalation In case the solution fully down and unavailable, service manager is responsible for communicating escalated unavailability to business owners and supplier. Please contact to this person in the case of unavailability. Service Manager: B Nathiya Email: b.nathiya@kone.com Phone: +918248992112

short_description: Windchill - New Product / Project Creation Process
introduction: What's the process to create a new Product or Project into Windchill?
instructions: Adding new products and projects to Windchill is to be done only by Operation Lead MCAD (ajay.pandey@kone.com) or Tech Lead MCAD (klaus.makela@kone.com). New Windchill products / projects are preferably requested via AskIT Service Request form "Windchill Service Request", found under "I need something new". Whenever such a request is received, follow these steps: 1. Verify, that the necessary information is given - Type (product or project) - Product / Project Name - Description - Primary Owner - Secondary Owner - Site (Project only) - Unit (Project only) If any of these information is missing, please revert back to the requestor 2. Verify, that the name is according to the Windchill Product Naming Convention (see KB000A1908 ). If not, please ask user to review the name according to the naming convention. 3a. New Products need to be approved by Design Quality Manager (Petri Pohjoispuro). So, drop an email to Petri with the necessary above mentioned inputs and Business reason for the Approval including the requester in CC. 3b. New Projects approval process is not applicable. 4. Once approval has been given, Reassign the ticket to Operation Lead MCAD (ajay.pandey@kone.com) or Tech Lead MCAD (klaus.makela@kone.com) and request them to create a Product / Project. Service Manager or Solution Owner will create the new product / project accordingly and revert the ticket back to the support team for assigning the requested members (if applicable).

short_description: Troubleshoot for Creo access at GiantKONE location
introduction: How to fix the Creo access issue at GiantKone location.
instructions: Users in GiantKONE are not necessarily connected to KONE's domain. There is a B2B connection between the two networks in place with the necessary firewall exceptions. So the Windchill servers and Creo license servers should be accessible. However, since GiantKONE's DNS does not contain KONE's servers, server names might not resolve. This means that the end user's machine does know the IP address of for example "apac_ptclicserver". Follow the instructions for troubleshoot Ping the license server from the user computer open the command prompt as an administrator ping the Chinese license server with alias apac_ptclicserver Note that Ping does not respond in any case, as that port is blocked. However, if the name apac_ptclicserver resolves, you should get response "Pinging euaz1mscadlic42.KONENET.COM" 2. If ping does not return the IP address of the server, this usually means, that the user's computer does not find the server's IP based on the alias name. To solve this, add the following entry to his host file in C:\Windows\System32\Drivers\etc\hosts according to the list below: 10.130.24.7 apac_ptclicserver apac_ptclicserver.konenet.com If apac_ptclicserver is already specified in the host file, update the IP address to 10.130.24.7. 3. If the user is still not able to obtain a license after that, make sure that the server is accessible from within KONE network by pinging from a computer connected to konenet. If server is accessible from KONE network, but not GiantKONE network, the issue is in GiantKONE's network or the B2B link to KONE. In such case forward the ticket to GSD with the available information. 4. If server is not accessible also from within KONE network, than contact the Service Manager.

short_description: How to modify ITSM tool Incident categories
introduction: How to modify ITSM tool Incident categories?
instructions: Change existing Incident category name: 1. Service Request is send to manual approval to the respective Service Manager (if requester other than Service Manager) Service Request MUST be approved by the respective Service Manager, if Incident category is requested by someone else than Service Manager 2. Review the new suggested Incident Category name. Pre-requisities: Service Request has received approval from the Service Manager (if requester other than Service Manager) It needs to be named according to naming convention: Service in CMDB - Application in CMDB. E.g. IT Service Management - ITSM tool It needs to be less than 80 digits long If the category is not named according to this naming convention, contact the user to provide new suggestion for Incident Category name 3. When the Incident category is named according to pre-requisities, update the existing Incident category name Go through Remedyforce Administration tab to "Category" view Search and open the existing Incident Category that you want to update Update the existing Category with the new Category name and click 'Save' icon 4. Update the Template name with the new Incident category name NOTE - Check for the references of category in Custom Labels, Custom setting or workflow rules. If present, category name has to updated there also · Go through Remedyforce Administration tab to "Configuration Application and to Templates". Change the name of the template as new Category name. If Queue need to me modified, then select Owner ID from list and click on Down arrow next to search box. Search the new Queue name and click on it. Click on Update . Update Service, Application, Priority in the same way if required Click Save . Update the Category and Template in Metadata Go to the setup. Search for Metadata in Quick Find. Click on Custom Metadata and go to Manage Records left to the Category ID . Search with Old Category name and click on it. Click on Edit . Fill the all the fields with new Category name and new Template name . Click save . 5. Update the new name of Incident category to Category design document https://kone.sharepoint.com/:x:/s/ITERPKnowledgeforInfosys/EU1DPaPB2ORMnrWTqiKtIt4BScXplg-10LBzffU7EvRr_A?e=ehaQmY Re-assign existing Incident category: 1. Service Request is send to manual approval to the respective Service Manager (if requester other than Service Manager) Service Request MUST be approved by the respective Service Manager, if Incident category is requested by someone else than Service Manager 2. Map the new queue to the existing Incident category - Go through Remedyforce Administration tab to "Suggested Owners & Queue Auto Assignments" Search existing Queue Assignment condition by using the 'Arrows' Click on the condition that you want to update and update the new Queue for the condition by selecting the Queue from the dropdown below If the queue doesn't exist, ask the user to fill in Service Request for queue creation from here: https://konewayflow--bmcservicedesk.eu12.visual.force.com/apex/selfservicenew#/support/problem-requests/all/a2Cb0000000c2ZZEAY%25C2%25ACIT%2520Service%2520Management%2520(ITSM)&a2Cb0000000RnXHEA0%25C2%25ACKONE%2520WAY%2520and%2520IT 3. Update the new Queue of Incident category to Category design document https://kone.sharepoint.com/:x:/s/ITERPKnowledgeforInfosys/EU1DPaPB2ORMnrWTqiKtIt4BScXplg-10LBzffU7EvRr_A?e=ehaQmY NOTE!!
