short_description: CAD Applications Setup for External Engineering Contractors
introduction: What are the requirements and recommended setup for external engineering contractors?
instructions: 1. Prerequisites 1.1. Computer, Network, Login External contractors are preferably using their own computers and logging in with their own credentials. When located in their own premises, they are connecting to their company’s network and domain. When travelling, they will be connecting to the internet via other means. While in KONE’s premises, they can use the guest network provided by KONE. For minimum system requirements please refer to PTC’s documentation: http://support.ptc.com/WCMS/files/135225/en/creo2_hw_notes.pdf 1.2. Access to KONE environment Access to KONE environment is established via BPC (Business Partner Channel). Each user needs a KONE AD account, BPC account and needs to be assigned to the necessary application specific BPC role. The roles are usually managed via MyAccess roles. 1.3. Software and Licenses If not agreed otherwise, contractors shall be using their own software licenses. The applications’ setup and environment however needs to be according to KONE’s specifications. When travelling and accessing KONE network via BPC, the contractor’s license server is not accessible. In such case the contractor shall borrow the required license(s) in advance for the duration of the trip. In exceptional cases, KONE might provide a license. This however needs to be agreed upon in advance. 2. License Settings KONE software installation packages will by default specify KONE license servers and keys. In case contractor’s own licenses are to be used, he/she is responsible for updating the settings accordingly. 3. Support KONE’s support is limited to KONE specific setup and customizations. Any support related to hardware, network (other than BPC) or any other software needs to be addressed to the contractor’s IT support. When contacting KONE’s Global Service Desk (GSD), AskIT Portal shall be primarily used to log the ticket. 4. Security Groups Below you can find a list of various applications and the required Security Group in order to access the system via BPC. These particular groups are managed via MyAccess. Creo / Windchill: SEC_B2B_#GRP_PTC#_GLB_GLB_USER (Creo: User / CWS: User / Windchill: PDMLink User) PDM: SEC_B2B_#APP_VARIPDMPRODUCTION#_GLB_GLB_USER SEC_B2B_#APP_VARIPDMTRAINING#_GLB_GLB_USER SEC_B2B_#APP_VARIWEBPRODUCTION#_GLB_GLB_USER SEC_B2B_#CAD_VARI#_GLB_GLB_USER

short_description: Enable an de-active user
introduction: How to enable a user in CRM ?
instructions: Document deals with process to activate a deactivated user in salesforce CRM for Kone. It is performed in 2 steps. For country users, the first step is performed by Kone Key User and second step is performed by Infosys AMS Team. For Global Business Unit User and KONE IT User, both the steps need to be performed by Infosys AMS Team. Prerequisite Approvals Reactivation in Production for Country User- Routed through Country Key user in salesforce. Profile Change in production for Global Business Unit/ KONE IT User KONE Approval Required Profile Change in sandboxes for Global Business Unit/ KONE IT User No Approval required User Organization User Type In Service Desk Purview? Country User No Ask user to contact that country key user Country Key User No Inform Key User on how to do it using KONE CRM Global Business Unit User Yes Check if required Approvals are there and follow process. All steps mentioned in the process need to be done by Infosys AMS Team KONE IT User Yes Check if required Approvals are there and follow process. All steps mentioned in the process need to be done by Infosys AMS Team Reactivation Process Step 1 Key user will trigger request for re-activation by clicking on reactivate button on user detail page in related list page. The input value here is profile. The process will further request key user to review other information and then submit. Submitted request will reach Infosys support through Remedy. Once the above request is submitted, a new record on User Request object is created. Key User must also specify the instruction specific profile, and permission sets need to be assigned to the user. it will await approval by support team to become active. Step 2 Infosys AMS Team Open production URL- https://kone.my.salesforce.com Click on “Kone Login” Go to user request object and check the list view and Click on pending request. User request record will get displayed and scrolling down to the page we can find the option to Approve and reactivate the user. So, basically user reactivation undergoes approval process through SFDC premier support team and this will also be undertaken by SFDC support team. Profile Change in sandboxes for Global Business Unit/ KONE IT User SD needs to raise request through reactivate button on user detail page. The new profile and permission set required if any must be either fetched from Remedy request or from Requestor.

short_description: 2D CAD User Categories and Preferred Solution
introduction: 2D CAD User Groups and Preferred Solution
instructions: 2D CAD users are categorized into 4 categories: - Viewer - Lightweight editors (usage less than 10 hours/week, DO NOT SHARE THIS INFORMATION WITH USER) - Heavyweight editors (usage more than 10 hours/week or other requirements, e.g. KONE Palette , DO NOT SHARE THIS INFORMATION WITH USER) - AutoCAD Mechanical users (exceptional, shall be avoided) Viewers will be using DWG TrueView , no approval required, installation requests via MyAccess role DWG TrueView: User, installation via SCCM (Software Center). Lightweight editors will be using ProgeCAD , line manager approval is required, and installation via MyAccess. Heavyweight editors will be using both ProgeCAD and AutoCAD . On top of the line manager's approval , also approval from the Product Solution team is required Certain roles might require AutoCAD (e.g. layout engineer, CSE), whereas other roles preferably shall use ProgeCAD instead (e.g. R&D engineers). Installation of AutoCAD via MyAccess. Heavyweight editors shall primarily use ProgeCAD, and AutoCAD only for such tasks, where ProgeCAD does not feature the necessary capabilities. AutoCAD Mechanical users will be using AutoCAD Mechanical . ACADM is a version of AutoCAD that has additional mechanical design features. Usually, ordinary AutoCAD or progeCAD is sufficient. As KONE has only a few such licenses, new installations shall be avoided. On top of the line manager's approval, approval from the application team is also required —installation is triggered via the Software Center. Access to the license and software installation needs to be requested via MyAccess. Upon approval in MyAccess, the user will automatically be added to the corresponding AD group. In case of manual provision, an AskIT ticket will be created automatically and assigned to the appropriate support group. MyAccess roles: ProgeCAD: English ProgeCAD: Chinese ProgeCAD: German Revit: Revit <Version> - Editor Revit: Revit <Version> - Reader AutoCAD: English AutoCAD: German AutoCAD: French AutoCAD Mechanical: English AutoCAD Mechanical: German AutoCAD Mechanical: French Autodesk Inventor: User Navisworks Freedom 2024: User Navisworks Simulate 2024: User Navisworks Manage 2024: User 3ds Max: User VRED: User Recap: User DWG TrueView: User

instructions: First you need to locate the planned order in Konect using the Planned SO Backreporting tab in the KM97_Welcome screen: Now look for the button as shown: The only available notification status for MBM order is when the order is closed . To resubmit the entire request, push or view the job details and resubmit to an individual or multiple recipients, push as described in the KM01H (archived callout process). to refresh to Exit

short_description: How to check and resend a KARCH from Closed orders (KM01H) in Konect?
introduction: How to check and resend a Customer Notification from Closed orders (KM01H) in Konect?
instructions: From a Closed Order (Modify KM01H-ServiceOrder) When a callout is closed and there is no notifications defined on the contract then a new button will appear in the upper left of the screen When this button is clicked you will see another 2 pained window similar to the customer notification window. In the windows you will be able to see pending (top) and completed jobs (bottom). you can highlight a completed job. Click the details button This opens the detail window as before but the “Address” column is blank and the Dazel queue is only “karch”. Clicking the button will resend the job back to the Dazel server using the details already submitted during the 1 st script run. The button is used to re-queue the job to run again at night so that the perl script is re-launched again from scratch. This would be useful if the 1 st automatic run failed (as well as all automatic resubmissions). to refresh to Exit

short_description: User Access in NPX
introduction: How to give user access for NPX?
instructions: On login to NPX, click on ‘User’ section under Admin tab. A list of Usernames will be displayed. For granting new user access, click on “Add” icon. Provide necessary user details like Name, Email and Username under the Profile tab. (Username and Email should be same) Next move to Permissions tab and remove the End user role assignment. Add the requested country name under Hierarchy Node. (Saving before updating Permissions tab is not suggested) To add Country specific roles click “Assign Roles +” button If the user belongs to Interviewers type, add KONE KC3 Agent role as well. Finally move to Settings tab, update the user language and time zone, if needed. Click Save to complete user access creation.

short_description: KONE ONLINE Password Reset for community users
introduction: How to reset the password for community users?
instructions: Step:1 User enters the login page and selects “Forgot your password?” Step:2 User is directed to “Password Reset” page where user fills in the username and selects “Send Password Reset Email”. Step:3 An password reset email with an activation link is sent to user’s email address Step:4 User sets a new password by clicking the activation link and entering the new password to the set password form Step:5 After creating the new password user is automatically directed to the Community.

short_description: KRMSC New User Creation
introduction: How to create New User in KRMSC
instructions: New user creation happens in three steps. 1st step is per formed by KONE Key User and 2nd step performed by Infosys AMS team and after that step 3 will again be performed by KONE key user. Step 1. Action performed by KONE Key User KONE Key User is responsible for raising new user creation request and SFDC Support create the new user. If the request is raised by KONE Key User. The overall steps are as below: 1. KONE Key User submit user request with record type ‘New User’. In Instruction to field he will put permission sets (In this case KRMSC User), profiles (KONE Standard User) and any other important information. Request is in Pending status after submit. 2. The request is assigned to SFDC Support for approval. SFDC Support view the request in User Requests tab and can approve or reject it. New user is created automatically after approval. Below screen shots demonstrate the detailed steps: KONE Key User Open User Requests tab and create a new request by filling in necessary user information. Key User save then submit the request for approval by clicking ‘Submit for Approval’ button. Step 2. Action performed by Infosys AMS Team SFDC Support team will Open User Requests tab in Production and can select the proper view to filter all new user creation requests in Pending status. Here is User Request details page. ‘Approval History’ related list displays that the request is currently pending for SFDC Support’s action. SFDC Support click ‘Approve & Activate’ button to approve the request. Step 3. Password communication by KONE Key User Once the user is created, KONE Key user will communicate the password to new user. Alternative Steps for User Creation Step1. Check if requisite Approvals are in place from Kone Step2. Check if required profile, and permission sets are already provided in request. In case not reach out to requestor for these information. Step3. All steps mention for Country user must be performed by Infosys AMS team

short_description: Ask IT - 2D CAD Installations
introduction: 2D CAD Installations
instructions: 2D CAD Application Installation Process: User requests AutoCAD, progeCAD or other 2D CAD application installation Categorize the user according to KBA 1471 a) if categorized as Viewer : User is not eligible for AutoCAD or progeCAD, DWG True View needs to be used instead explain to the end user, that in his case, DWG True View needs to be used instead of AutoCAD Verify, if DWG True View is already installed If not yet installed, request deployment of DWG True View SCCM package by forwarding the ticket to Incident Management queue. b) if categorized as Lightweight Editor : User is not eligible for AutoCAD, install or progeCAD instead explain to the end user, that in his case, progeCAD needs to be used instead of AutoCAD Verify, if progeCAD is already installed If not yet installed, ask user to request the appropriate MyAccess role: progeCAD: English progeCAD: Chinese progeCAD: German After line manager's approval in MyAccess, the software installation is triggered via SCCM. c) if categorized as Heavyweight Editor : ask user to request AutoCAD: English role in MyAccess. A business justification needs to be attached to the request. After line manager's approval, the request is routed to the Autodesk support team to review the business justification. If business justification is approved, the software installation will be triggered via SCCM.

short_description: Ask IT - Reset KFM Password
introduction: How to reset a KFM password from a technician/fitter?
instructions: ! Read this first - Make sure the mobile has got a working network connection (WiFi) AD-password reset can be done with a service "Microsoft Online Password Reset" at https://passwordreset.microsoftonline.com . if you would get an error regarding 'upn mismatch', please read article KBA2783 . IMPORTANT NOTE: The prerequisite for the service to work, is that the user has saved his Work mobile phone number in Workday in a international format (+ country area subscriber) . This way the password reset can send out the necessary text message containing a verification code, after which the user can change his/her AD-password. Example of international format of phone numbers: +32 499 123456 General Note regarding the examples below: “The page uses international language automatically based on device and/or browser setting. On an Android phone, it uses the language configured in Settings – General Management – Language and Input – Language. The screenshots were taken on a phone configured for English language.” 1. To reset the AD-password, go to https://passwordreset.microsoftonline.com . 2. Type in your User ID ( firstname.lastname@kone.com ) and the requested characters for verification from the generated image: 3. Type in your mobile phone number in international format (as it is in Workday) and select “Text my mobile phone”: 4. Type in the one-time verification code you received from the text message and press Next: 5. Type in your new AD-password and press Finish: 6. Your password has been reset: *** END OF ARTICLE ***

short_description: Vertex ED installation for end users
introduction: How to get Vertex ED (formerly known as SVertex) into use
instructions: If you are in Finland, Chennai or Kunshan, you can install the Vertex ED network client yourself. Follow this instruction: https://kone.sharepoint.com/teams/VertexED/Shared%20Documents/vertex_ed24_installation.pdf Please notice that proper AD group membership is required (groups listed in instruction). Vertex is latency sensitive SW. This is why in other locations, a standalone installation is needed. Please request role "Vertex ED: Standalone Client" from MyAccess . Role requires line manager and 2nd level approval. Once approved, the installation of Vertex ED will automatically be triggered via Software Center.

short_description: Vertex ED installation for support personnel
introduction: What to reply/do when end user requests Vertex ED (formerly known as Svertex) installation?
instructions: If the end user is in Finland, Chennai or Kunshan, end user can install the Vertex ED network client by him/herself. End user can follow this instruction: https://kone.sharepoint.com/teams/VertexED/Shared%20Documents/vertex_ed24_installation.pdf Please notice that proper AD group membership is required (groups listed in instruction). Vertex is latency sensitive SW. This is why in other locations, support should make standalone installation to end user's PC. The software is installed with SCCM via MyAccess role Vertex ED: Standalone Client . The role requires line manager and 2nd level approval.

short_description: How to check status of your SAP Access request raised via GRC?
introduction: This KBA explains the instructions on how to check the status of GRC request.
instructions: SAP Access Request Process When access request is created, it will first go to the user’s line manager for approval. After this respective role owners need to take actions and if there will be high level SoD conflicts on user’s accesses, the final approval is requested from respective SoD approver. How to check status of your SAP Access request raised via GRC? After successful access request creation user will receive email with the request number. Click on the below link to check the status of the SAP access request raised via GRC. https://knecigrp9800.konenet.com:44398/sap/bc/webdynpro/sap/zkone_search_request?sap-client=100&sap-language=EN# Once the link is clicked below sample screen will be displayed. Enter the GRC request number to get the Status, stage, and name of the approver with whom the access request is pending. Once the GRC request number is input, press Enter to get the details of the access request. You can contact the approver e.g., via email or Teams to remember to act on pending request. Once all approvals have been provided and the user access has been provisioned by GRC in the target landscape, then user will receive a mail as respective GRC request is approved / completed.

short_description: Creating Windchill User Groups & adding/removing members in a group
introduction: How to create Windchill User Groups & add/remove members in a group
instructions: PRE CHECK When a new request comes in, verify that all required information is given - Group Name - Primary Owner - Secondary Owner Note : Group name should be in the below format. If not, please request user to send it in the below format APPROVAL FOR GROUP CREATION Forward the request to Petri Pohjoispuro for approval. Drop a approval mail to Petri with the details of the group for approval, set status to "Pending", save the changes in the ticket. After approval from Petri, proceed to create the group as described below. MyAccess Role Windchill User group members are managed in MyAccess. Therefore, any new group needs to be added also to MyAccess. Please follow KBA7576 to implement this. PROCEDURE 1. Login to Windchill using admin login 2. Go to site and click Utilities 3. Click participant administrator 4. Click New group icon 5. Enter the group name under name column and enter all the owners name in description column 6. Click Finish PERMISSION TO USER GROUP For providing permission to user group refer KB000A1753 ADDING/REMOVING MEMBERS FOR EXISTING GROUP APPROVAL PROCESS Please request end user to follow the MyAccess request for group access: Kindly Open My Access portal using the following link : https://myaccess.kone.com/identityiq/home.jsf Kindly click Manage My Access Select the user ID - Next Enter your group name in the search box and select the Windchill user group - Next - Submit User Groups members are managed via Active Directory groups. Do not add users manually to user groups. CHANGES TO GROUP OWNERSHIP Changes to group ownership can be directly managed by the current group owners in MyAccess. To do so, follow KB000A6697 .

short_description: How to forward an interrupted/rejected order to a new technician?
introduction: How to forward an interrupted/rejected order to a new technician?
instructions: When a technician leaves the site without finishing the job, the order becomes 'interrupted'. However, the order is still active on his kfm device . BEFORE an order can be assigned to another technician, the first technician should 'REJECT' his order. This Rejection can be done by either the technician or the KC3 staff (orders to follow-up). When the "Rejected" order is being forwarded to a new technician to continue the order, the order must be set to 'Hold'. This way we avoid errors in the backreport afterwards. Summary: Leave Site, Interrupted, Rejected, Hold (+new technician)

short_description: Ask IT - CAD Robot Tickets
introduction: How to process tickets in CAD Robot queue?
instructions: When a new ticket comes into KONE.SC.CADROBOT queue, please follow this process: 1. Review content of ticket. 2. If the ticket has incorrectly been routed to CAD Robot queue, add a note that this is not related to CAD Robots and reassign to appropriate queue (Incident Management, if appropriate queue is not known). 3. In case the ticket seems to be correctly related to CAD Robots, CAD Console, CWS, etc., follow these rules: Tickets handled by L2 support (ITC) Access request: Moved to my access for both CAD console & CWS console, if the request is for QA inform the user that QA is for developers Request for CWS Client Installation: follow KB000A1567 Tickets handled by L2.5 support (ITEC team) Request for Process Test Run for New Product or New Revision: follow KB000A2028 Request for Go-Live: follow KB000A2028 Request for Quick Fix Update: follow KB000A2771 Request for Changes to CAD Robot Settings: follow KB000A2773 Request for Removal of Obsolete Product from CAD Robot: follow KB000A2774 Request for prioritizing pending job: follow KB000A2312 Waiting for PDM State in CWS : follow KB000A4817 Unable to cast to COM objects : follow KB000A7331 Tickets to be assigned directly to KONE.SC.CADROBOT.L3 queue Request for a New Distribution Site: follow KB000A2792 Incidents related to failed jobs: follow KB000A5642 Incident, which cannot be resolved by L2.5 support (ITEC team) If none of the cases above applies or it's unclear what the ticket is related to then, ITEC L2.5 support shall be consulted immediately where as the L2 team (ITC) can keep ticket assigned to them.

short_description: How to manage and maintain an organization in Konect (Plant, Planner and Workcenter)
introduction: How do I create, update and delete Plants, Plannergroups and (Main) workcenters in Konect? "The Fusion Process": Allow the frontlines to maintain (create or delete) any level of the organizational structure: Plant section, Planner Group and Work Center Allow frontlines to migrate any level of the existing organization structure to a new organization. Allow frontlines to schedule when an organizational structure must be migrated (including master data conversion - ie. Callouts, Equipments,…) ***FUTURE Release***
instructions: Note: users who need to manage the org structure of a company need to have the 'Fusion' option activated in their user profile (see User Management on the Welcome screen) 2.3 Maintain Plant Section 2.3.1 Screen 2.3.2 Add new plant section 2.3.3 Deactivate a plant section 2.3.4 Deletion of a plant section 2.3.5 Replace a plant section 2.3.6 Cancelling an action 2.4 Maintain Planner Group 2.4.1 Screen 2.4.2 Add new planner group 2.4.3 Deactivate a plant section 2.4.4 Deletion of a planner group 2.4.5 Replace a planner group 2.4.6 Cancelling an action 2.5 Maintain Work center 2.5.1 Search criteria 2.5.2 Adding new work center 2.5.3 Activate a Work center 2.5.4 Deactivate a Work center 2.5.5 Deactivate & Move a work center 2.5.6 Cancelling an action 2.5.7 Deletion of a Work center 2.5.8 Replace a Work center (or employee) The screen is accessible from the Organization tab in the welcome screen (see new Maintain button). Permission should be limited to Local Administrator only: This opens the new Konect Organization screen: By default, when the screen appears the list of Plant Section is automatically refreshed. Only one line can be selected. The selection is displayed also in the fields Company and Plant Section 2.3 Maintain Plant Section 2.3.1 Screen By default, when the screen appears the list of Plant Section is automatically refreshed. Only one line can be selected. The selection is displayed also in the fields Company and Plant Section Following actions are possible on the selected Plant section: • Add a new plant section • Deactivate a plant section • Delete a plant section • Replace a plant section (future usage) • Cancel an action (future usage) 2.3.2 Add new plant section To add a new plant section, you must click on the ‘New’ button: This will give you the access to the Company and Plant Section field: Enter manually the Company and the new plant section code. The company must already exist. The Plant section code cannot exceed 3 positions. To confirm the creation of the new plant section, click on the SAVE button: To cancel the creation, click on the cancel button (or select any line of the plant section table) When a new Plant Section is created, his status is always ‘inactive’. The Activation of a plant section is only done when at least one Active work center is assigned to it! 2.3.3 Deactivate a plant section A plant section that is Deactivated is no longer visible in any Konect screen or drop-down menu. This operation can only be possible if all planner group and work center belonging to the plant section are also Inactive It is possible to deactivate a plant section even if a “Replacement” is scheduled. But then, when the replace date is reached, the plant section will be re-activated! Note that the deactivation is always done manually and cannot be scheduled. Select the Plant Section you want to Deactivate: Click on the Deactivate button: 2.3.4 Deletion of a plant section It is possible to remove a plant section from the list. Only ‘Inactive’ plant sections, without any “Replacement” scheduled, can be permanently deleted! To delete an ‘Inactive’ plant section: First select the plant section you want to delete: Click on the Delete button. The Delete confirmation button appears: Click again on the (red) button to confirm deletion. 2.4 Maintain Planner Group 2.4.1 Screen By default, when the screen appears the list of Planner Group is automatically refreshed. Only one line can be selected. The selection is displayed also in the fields Company, Plant Section and Planner Group Following actions are possible on the selected Planner Group: • Add a new planner group • Deactivate a planner group • Delete a planner group • Replace a planner group (future release) • Cancelling an action 2.4.2 Add new planner group To add a new planner group, you must click on the ‘New’ button: This will give you the access to the Company, Plant Section and Planner group field: Enter manually the Company, the plant section and the new planner group code. The company and plant section must already exist. The Plant section code cannot exceed 3 positions. To confirm the creation of the new planner group, click on the SAVE button: To cancel the creation, click on the cancel button (or select any line of the planner group table) When a new Planner Group is created, his status is always ‘inactive’. The Activation of a planner section is only done when at least one Active work center is assigned to it! 2.4.3 Deactivate a plant section A planner group that is Deactivated is no longer visible in any Konect screen or drop-down menu. This operation can only be possible if all work center belonging to the planner group are also Inactive It is possible to deactivate a planner group even if a “Replacement” is scheduled. But then, when the replace date is reached, the planner group will be re-activated! Note that the deactivation is always done manually and cannot be scheduled. Select the Planner group you want to Deactivate: Click on the Deactivate button: 2.4.4 Deletion of a planner group It is possible to remove a planner group from the list. Only ‘Inactive’ plant sections, without any “Replacement” scheduled, can be permanently deleted! To delete an ‘Inactive’ planner group: First select the plant section you want to delete: Click on the Delete button. The Delete confirmation button appears: Click again on the button to confirm deletion. 2.5 Maintain Work center 2.5.1 Search criteria By default, when the screen appears, all Work center are displayed. It is possible to filter the list of work center by company, plant section or planner group. First enter the search criteria (either manually or using the attached menu), and then click on the Search icon. Selected line is displayed under the table Click on the details icon to view the employee details: Several actions are possible: • Adding a new work center • Activate a work center • Deactivate a work center • Deactivate and move a work center (future release) • Delete a work center • Replace a work center (future release) • Cancelling an action (future release) 2.5.2 Adding new work center Remark: if you use SAP interfaces, this process is not applicable! To add a new work center, you must click on the ‘New’ button: The Work Center Creation screen appears: Enter manually the Company, plant section, planner group, work center and employee number: • Make sure that the company, plant section, planner group and employee number exist • All fields value are mandatory (except employee number) • The work center code must start with the company code and is max 8 position long If you already want to assign an employee to the new work center, you can either enter the employee number manually OR use the search icon to open the Search Employee screen Click on the SAVE button to confirm the Work center creation: Note that the new work center created as ALWAYS the status ‘Inactive’ (see next section to learn more on the Activation process) All values are reset to allows you to enter a new work center. If you want to keep the previous values you have entered, select the ‘Keep?’ option before you click on the save button. This will not reset the fields after the work center creation (except for the work center field and the employee number (if provided)) To close the Work Center Creation screen, click on the Cancel button: 2.5.3 Activate a Work center When a new work center is created, his status is ‘Deactivate’. This means that this work center will not appear in any organization menu or screen in Konect. It is only when the work center is ‘Active’ that it will ‘visible’ in Konect. A work center can be manually activated OR automatically activated at a certain date/time. Follow the below steps to Activate a work center: First, select the work center to Activate. The Activation Date field is then visible Enter the Activation date/time + click on the Activate button: • When an activation date/time is provided, then the work center remains ‘Inactive’ until the Activation date is reached • If you have not provided an activation date, then the work center is directly Activated: Remark: if you activate a new work center that belongs to a Plant section or planner that is not Active, then the plant section (or planner group) will automatically be activate at the same time than the Work center! 2.5.4 Deletion of a Work center Remark: It is only possible to delete an ‘Inactive’ work center! To delete an ‘Inactive’ plant section: • Select the inactive work center to delete: Click on the Delete button. The Delete confirmation button appears: Click on the button to confirm deletion.

short_description: Airtame - Support and Information
introduction: Airtame - Support and Information
instructions: What is it Airtame ( www.airtame.com ) is an HDMI dongle, which is put in all screens (HDMI for connectivity and USB for power) in NL007 (The Hague) and NL009 (Veenendaal. Also there’s a few Airtames in Belgium running (Niels Plas is responsible there). Once an Airtame is in the screen (and configured) it is connected to (hidden) SSID KAirtame. Firewall rules allow for people connected to KOffice (KONE computers) to reach the Airtames wirelessly and their resources like Internet access and they shared network drives. For KONE guests (KVisitor) the firewall rules mean they keep their Internet access, can present wirelessly via the Airtame but of course can not reach anything inside of KONE network. The information to present it shown on the screen (IP address). For the screens in common areas, only narrow casting will be used, showing KONE news. What is needed To present, people download the application (either via Self Service SCCM or from the Internet ), install the application, start the application, type in the IP that is shown on the screen and click “start”. Once done with presenting, they click “stop”. This goes for both internal and external computers, but of course these computers need to be connected to the LAN. (KOffice or KVisitor). Support 1 At the moment there’s one known issues and that is lag (delay) between the computer and the screen on which is presented. " Manual mode" Needs to be turned off for the delay to be corrected. Support 2 If an Airtame has issues (lost network config, reset to factory settings etc) a ticket will be created by GSD and assigned to Onsite support the Netherlands . They own a manual on how to add an Airtame (new one or reset one) to the environment. Cloud All Airtames are managed from a cloud environment ( https://airtame.cloud ). Onsite support the Netherlands has an account for this portal . Here all devices can be managed, content, function, URL, timezone, firmware updates, etc.

short_description: Reupdate the phone numbers created in a wrong format.

short_description: How to create a report in the KONECT Web?
introduction: How to create a report in the KONECT Web?
instructions: When you click New to create a report, you must first define the type of report, its associated form, and the report name in the New Report screen. Depending on the type of report you are creating, BMC Remedy AR System then opens either the report design screen of the Report Console (for Web reports) or the ReportCreator form (for BMC Remedy AR System reports). Starting a new report In a browser, click the BMC Remedy AR System Report Console link on the home page to open the Report Console. Click New . In the Type field of the New Report window, select the Web or BMC Remedy AR System report type. This field is required. In the Form field, enter the name of the form to use for the report. This field is required. ( Optional ) To limit the list of forms to those that are already used in other reports, select Forms Used in Existing Reports . This can speed up retrieval of the list of forms, but any form that is not already used in some report does appear on the list. To find the form quickly, type the first few letters of the form name into the field. For example, type "Sample" to select from the list of forms related to the Sample application. Select or clear the " Add default fields and sort order " check box: Selected — Fields that appear in the form's results list after a search are automatically added to the report definition, along with the default sort order. You can remove or change these fields and sort order later if necessary. Not selected — No fields are added to the report definition automatically. In the Name field, type a name for the report. This field is required. The report name must be unique. The maximum length is 128 characters. Also, you cannot change the name of the report after you exit this screen, so use care in assigning a report name. Note Each report must have a unique Name/Locale combination. For example, two reports can both be name "Monday", if the locale for each report is different. Click OK .

short_description: RQM Jama - How to create a new user account
introduction: RQM Jama - How to create a new user account
instructions: Jama help: https://konerqm.jamacloud.com/help/ Jama User Guide / Administration / Organization administrator / Manage users / Add new user KONE Jama user management concept document for background information AskIT Ticket coming via MyAccess Line manager has already approved the request. Jama user request Grant requested license. Add user to "Jama users" group in case KONE internal. Check for External (Contingent ) users if there are any mentions about only the project specific access needs. In those cases check from requestor and project admin that the role is right and not to supposed to be as External reviewers. In case there is a mention of any project specific access guide user to contact directly project admin. Jama QA Sandbox user request Grant Creator(float) license in QA Add user to "Jama QA Sandbox users" groups in QA environment. No "Jama users" group membership in QA. Jama External reviewer request Check that Jama target project is given in MyAccess comments and from Project admin that the access can be granted. Create Project specific group "<Project> External reviewers" in given project. Add user to "<Project> External reviewers" group Grant Collaborator license Jama user termination When user account is terminated in AD AskIT ticket will inform it. "Role Jama-Users has been removed from the user <user name> in MyAccess. You are receiving this notification as the provisioner of the access right. Please carry out provision task in the target system and inform user about completion..." Remove user group memberships and deactivate user account in Production and in QA (if exists there) Jama: Organization Admin access request This group membership is managed by Anja Airasvirta and there should not be updates usually. User is granted the Organization administrator access in all environments. AskIT ticket not from MyAccess, directly from end user A: Requesting access to Jama Guide end user to use MyAccess tool for access request. New ticket will come from there. B: Requesting access to specific project Check that user has “Jama user” MyAccess role and Jama group memberships and guide to contact Project admin directly. In case no “Jama user” or "External reviewer" MyAccess role and/or Jama users group membership in Jama guide to raise MyAccess request first and then ask project specific access from project admin. C: Requesting license update Check that user has membership in MyAccess Role “Jama users” and user has an account with group membership to Jama users is in place. In case no current Jama users group membership guide to raise MyAccess ticket. Change the license e.g. from Collaborator to Creator (float) if requested. This is applicable only for Jama users, not for "External reviewers"who would have only Collaborator license. D. Requesting Test generator Jenkins actor access Please contact for Anja Airasvirta for more details. User is requesting addition of specific Jenkins Bot account to be added into Project or Test execution project. E.g. Itec jenkins to be added into projects: KCE, KCE KCELIFT MODULE Execution, KCE Lift C-Process SW Test Execution. Bot Itecjenkins account is added to Jama groups: KCE Admin, KCE KCELIFT MODULE Execution Admin, KCE Lift C-Process SW Test Execution Admin NOTE: In case you get this error message when opening user account: Please Cancel the action and contact Anja Airasvirta for user update. Selected user is member of some Secret project that is not visible to you and in case you make any changes to this user, user will lose membership to all current secret projects groups. Account creation and Access granting: All new users after the line managers approval via MyAccess can be added to "Jama users " group that grants basic reader access to libraries and R&D projects. NOTE: Except the external reviewers who shall have only the project specific access via " <Project> External reviewers " group. These are external users coming form e.g. auditing organizations. Account creation Navigate to Admin > Users. This will display a list of Active users in Jama. Check if the account already exists. Tick “View Inactive Users” Search for the new user UPN A: No account found Select New User. Username = UPN and Email address: E-mail. Please check that you are using the active e-mail address for username. Only @kone.com addresses shall be used Complete the required fields. First Name and Last Name: Name as in AD. This will update in first login from the AD. License type: requested license Collaborator or Creator(float) . NOTE: In case of a new account creation together with a new project creation use Creator(float) and add user to project’s Admin access group. User Groups based on target environment: PROD: User shall be added to “Jama users” group in case not External reviewer QA: User shall be added only to “QA Sandbox users” . External reviewers shall be only project specific group. Create project specific group in case not yet created. Save the user account by selecting Save. B: Account found Account with e.g. Trial license, Reviewer license or inactive user Account has no group memberships or is missing “Jama users” membership Activate inactive account Click Edit action on user row Add user to "Jama users" group in Prod, In “QA Sandbox users" in QA and in case External reviewer to "<Project> External reviewer" group Set the requested license. Collaborator for external reviewers. Save changes Account with membership of “Jama users” Basic account is already granted. Check from requestor which user name user is using for login in case access is not working. Guide requestor to contact project admin to grant project specific permissions. Update requestor with following links in e-mail: Jama production environment: https://konerqm.jamacloud.com Training and support material: https://kone.sharepoint.com/sites/konerequirementmanagement Project specific creator access can be requested directly from Project admins. Their names are usually visible on project dashboard. In case of QA sandbox access the link to QA is: https://konerqm-qa.jamacloud.com/ Account Creation for External Users Same above steps to be taken for account creation for external users also, but the mail ID which uses from KONE Active Directory only different as below 1 . We have some external user accounts in KONE AD with their organization mail ID in mail field, while enabling access to these users you should use the UPN value from AD Example : For below external user we have to use erik.dragomirecky@kone.com as shown in right side below image, for Jama account creation. 2. Update the same external user information in Track_File ( This information to identify external accounts in Jama in future ) 3. If you identified that the user UPN value in AD as @KONENET.COM then it should be corrected as @kone.com , for this you can move the ticket to CUST.KONE.INT.SD 4. Once the account created you can communicate with user that they can access Jama using KONE mail ID (UPN) and KONE password.

short_description: RQM Jama - How to create a new project
introduction: How to create a new project in Jama? Following information is needed from requestor, Project manager. Project name: R&D project / Library/Empty / Test execution Project type: Blue / Purple / Library / Product Updates (CR) / Software / Other Project category: Elevator / Escalator / Building doors / Library / Software / IT / Other Project Manager name: Jama R&D projects are located in 4 main folders (currently in focus of AskIT tickets): 1. Codes and Standards Library 2. Stakeholder Requirements 3. Technology Library 4.KONE R&D Projects: R&D specific projects for elevators, escalators, SW, etc) Folders 5. Delivery projects, 6. Core model and Examples and Training are in use of RQM team for specific purposes. In addition there is folders for Jama maintenance purposes Jama Maintenance: Templates, Monitoring project, Jama testing and training KONE IT: IT specific projects. This is also placeholder for other IT specific projects
instructions: Approval to create new project When a new R&D project is requested get first the approval from Juuso Auvinen Juuso.auvinen@kone.com . Please check that the project name is unique and there is not yet a project with the same name. Remember to check also the secret projects list in maintenance folder. In case a project exists with the same name ask update for the name. No new IT specific projects are supposed to be created yet. A) Create a project Based on the project template type duplicate the corresponding template as new project. · Admin/Manage all projects/Actions/Duplicate Project) Templates locate here: Jama Maintenance / Jama Project templates folder. Jama project template 2.0 Test execution project template Note: in case of library or empty create new project without template. Duplicate: · Update the New Project Name as requested Naming of the Test execution project is as follows: Mother project - Component /system/subsystem - Testing level - Execution All these parts will be given in additional information field: Mother project Component / System / Subsystem Testing level · Create short prefix as New Project Key from the project name (max 8 char) o Project manager can later change the key if needed · Tick Project settings · Tick Releases B) Create a Technology Library or Codes and Standards library project For Library projects, there is no template. Library projects are created without any predefined item set structure (Admin/Manage all projects/Add/Add Project). 1. Contact Juuso Auvinen for details for new library project: location in structure and item set in case not clear enough in ticket. 2. Enable similar set of item types as "Jama project template 2.0" has. 3. Apply “Jama 2.0 ” Relationship configuration to new Category library project (Admin/Relationships , "Jama 2.0 "/Add Project) C) Create an Empty project This is an special case and needs to be discussed separately with Juuso Auvinen or Anja. 1. Add new project 2. Enable separately specified set of item types for project 3. Apply separately specified relationship configuration for project In case there is additional information as "Test execution project" please use then the corresponding template when creating a project from a template. Update Project settings Use given information for basic configuration. · Update Project manager as the contact for approving additional access requests to this project · Start date is the project creation date · Leave end date as empty · Set Status as what is given in Project type: Blue, Purple,.. · Update category as in request Check Settings tab that Versioning is set on. Move newly created project in requested location in project tree Location is based on the given information: Project type and Project category. Codes and Standards library projects directly in folder with same name. Library projects in Technology Library folders. Stakeholder Requirements in corresponding folder. Please ask more information from Juuso Auvinen for the project location details in case not specified in ticket. KONE R&D projects in Product specific folders. Based on Project Category and Project type select the target folder. Grant permissions to newly created project Newly created project will inherit the organization group permissions form the parent folder of hierarchy. Project specific groups shall be created and permissions granted in Configure project view. Create project groups Use Groups tab “Add Group” action to create a new project group. Basic set of project specific groups are: · <Project> Admin · <Project> Core team · <Project> Collaborators Add the given Project manager in <Project> Admin group. In case the project creation requester was not the Project manager grant the <Project> Admin membership to him/her too. Rest of the users can be asked via separate access request ticket. Note: A Project Admin can manage groups of users that already have access to a project. These groups are valuable when fine tuning permissions or creating email lists that are different than the project as a whole. Project groups can be used for permission management and also as distribution lists inside Jama tool. When updating project members : NOTE: In case you get this error message when opening user account: Please Cancel the action and contact Anja Airasvirta for user update. Selected user is member of some Secret project that is not visible to you and in case you make any changes to this user, user will lose membership to all current secret projects groups. Edit group members In Groups tab, you can add and remove users who already have access to jama and have Jama user role in MyAccess. Grant project permissions In general project permissions are same for the whole project and permissions are set on project root level. “Add permissions” enables admin to use new project groups (created in Groups tab) and configure the permission for each group. Admin can also change the permissions in action column actions. Grant default permission for project specific user groups: Admin, Core team, and Collaborators. Jama project confidentiality and permission setup guidelines for KONE R&D projects KONE Confidential and Sensitive projects Projects will inherit common permissions when moved to project folders Codes and standards, Stakeholder requirements and Technology Library folder: Group Manage Project Create/Edit Read <Project> Core team x x <Project> Collaborators x <Project> Admin x x x Jama users x Organization Admin x x x Reporting x VersionOne and Jira Integration KONE R&D Projects Group Manage Project Create/Edit Read <Project> Core team x x <Project> Collaborators x <Project> Admin x x x Jama users x Organization Admin x x x Reporting x VersionOne and Jira Integration x x KONE Secret projects (only special projects) Some projects are secret and requires individuals sign the project NDA. In this case, all Jama Administrators do not have access to manage project. Project specific Project Admin group is needed where at least one of the Jama administrators has membership in addition to couple of project key members. Project specific collaborator group is needed to grant reader access for project collaborators. Organization Admin and Jama users groups do not have visibility to this project by default. Reporting and VersionOne and Jira integration permissions are granted. Please contact Anja Airasvirta for permission granting of Secret projects before modifying the default permissions. Example here: Group Manage Project Create/Edit Read <Project> Core team x x <Project> Collaborators x <Project> Admin x x x Jama users Organization Admin Reporting x VersionOne and Jira Integration x x Add new project members Check that the project admin has already account created in Jama and Jama user role in MyAccess tool. Grant the <Project> Admin group membership with create(float license) to Project manager(s) given in ticket. Rest of the project users will be requested in separate access request ticket. See Knowledge article KB000A1594 Update project dashboard Add similar project dashboards on newly created project as what is on project template. Rich text box for Project contacts and link to training documentation Project summary Activity stream Pie charts with following filters, Item types by status (not needed for test execution projects) 2. Business requirements (this project) 2. Stakeholder requirements (this project) 2. Codes (this project) 2. System requirements (this project) 2. Subsystem requirements (this project) Example of setting:

short_description: Autodesk Inventor Installation Process
introduction: Autodesk Inventor Installation Process
instructions: The preferred 3D Mechanical CAD solution in KONE is Creo . Whenever possible, Inventor shall be avoided. For requesting Inventor installation and access to Inventor license, please request the role Autodesk Inventor: User in MyAccess . This role requires a business justification to be attached to the request. Please refer to Knowledge Article KBA 6126 for further details. After the request has been approved in MyAccess, the software installation will automatically be initiated via Software Center. It might take a few hours before the installation has been completed. For users based in United Kingdom, the individual role Autodesk Inventor: User - United Kingdom shall be used. This role grants access to other than only the KONE official version of Inventor. Installation of Inventor to UK users is done by the local Inventor team.

short_description: How to create a data extension in a BU?
introduction: How to create a data extension in a BU?
instructions: Document deals with process of create a data extension in a BU? Step 1 1.Login to mc.exacttarget.com The below home page will be displayed. Step 2 Select the ‘KONE’ drop down tab and choose the business unit (Country)where the data extension will be created. Step 3 Select ‘Subscriber’ tab and then select ‘Data extensions’. Step 4 Select the folder where data extension has to be created and then click on ‘Create’ as located on the right side. Step 5 choose ‘Standard data extension’ as method of data extension creation. From the information provided in the ticket which is created by user, Fill the name of data extension, description, click ‘is sendable’ checkbox if nothing is mentioned in the ticket. Also check Campaign if mentioned in the ticket. click ‘Next’ Step 6 : For Data retention policy, do not change any settings, click ‘Next’ From the excel provided in the ticket created by the user. Enter the field names in the below window and define the data type accordingly. We need to select one Field as primary key because while importing the data the system will ask for a primary key in DE. Make Contact ID (Email address if contact id is not there) as primary key. Select Email Address in Send relationship drop down. Click on Create. Step 7: Data Extension will be created as show below. Step 8: Open the DE and move to records. Click on Import to import the excel records in the DE. Step 9 : Save the file as CSV if the provided file is in some other format. Browse the file in the system and select, choose delimiter as comma, choose the date format with appropriate date formatting required, choose the Import type as ADD only as it is 1 st time import to this DE. Click on next. Step 10 The mapping will be done automatically if header is same. Otherwise create mapping manually. Click on next. Step 11. Check the details in the Review and Import section. Click on Import. You will get a popup for Import as shown below. You will be notified by email when the import process completes. You will receive the Email with the record inserted and failed and error message. Notify the Client with all the details.

short_description: User not able to view MS Dates of equipment in My Portfolio
introduction: How to resolve the issue when a user not able to view MS Dates of equipment in My Portfolio
instructions: Document deals with process how to analyzing the problem that user not able to view MS Dates of equipment in My Portfolio. Issue: An incident reported from key user that one user of his country cannot view the MS Dates of equipment in Convention tab of My Portfolio. See below screen shot. While the key user can view the dates. How to analyze and resolve: 1. Actually the information shown on Convention tab contains two objects. One is equipment, Equipment Name, Building Name, Sales Order, Act MS4, Plan MS5, Act MS5, LIFS Contract#. The other is opportunity, Convention Opportunity, Stage, Close Date, Lead Source, Probability, Alarm Contract. Now user is not able to view MS Dates fields which is associated with equipment object. Therefore need to check user’s visibility to equipment object. 2. By clicking the Equipment Name column, it turned out that user has no any access to this record. 3. Then I logged in as this user and tried to open other equipment. User cannot access any equipment. 4. In Full sandbox, I manual share the equipment to this user. While this user still cannot access the equipment. Now I’m sure that this user has no access to equipment at his profile and permission set level. 5. I informed the key user for the reason. And country key user added necessary permission set to this user by himself. Issue was resolved.

short_description: How to approve SAP Access Request through Reminder emails using SAP GRC?
introduction: The different sections in this document are: 1. Approve / Reject SAP Access Request as a Line manager / Role Owner / SoD approver directly from the email. 2. Approve / Reject SAP Access Request through Reminder from GRC Work Inbox 3. Approving the complete SAP GRC request 4. Rejecting the complete SAP GRC request 5. Partially approving / rejecting SAP GRC request
instructions: OBJECTIVE : On completing the training, the user should have an understanding of how to approve/reject SAP Access Request through Reminder emails using SAP GRC. 1. Approve / Reject SAP Access Request as a Line manager / Role Owner / SoD approver directly from the email Once the reminder is sent for the access request is submitted by the end user, an email is triggered automatically to the line manager / role owner / SoD approver of the request - with details as shown below - for approval/rejection or partial approval/rejection of the GRC access request. To approve the access request directly from the mail click on “Approve” link and then click on ”send” button in next email window. 2. Approve / Reject SAP Access Request through Reminder from GRC Work Inbox Once the line manager / role owner / SoD approver receives an email they need to follow the instructions as shown in the below notification Open GRP Production (SSO) from SAP GUI log on pad. Execute NWBC t-code. Click on "Work Inbox" quick link under "My Home" tab in browser screen opened after executing NWBC t-code. Open request from work inbox for approval / rejection actions in access request. Select whichever request is required to be approved as per the reminder email and open the same - the user will be able to see all details as shown below for review. 3. Approving the complete SAP GRC request The form can be approved at an overall level (i.e. for all roles requested) by clicking Submit - as the default selection for each role assignment is "Approve" as shown in screenshot below. Entering COMMENTS relating to APPROVAL / REJECTION However, before clicking on Submit the user will need to click on Comments tab to record approval related comments. If this is missed the system will show an error as comments are mandatory. As shown below, the user can then give the comments relating to the approval in the comments tab. And then click on "Submit" button to approve the request. 4. Rejecting the complete SAP GRC request After review, in case the line manager / role owner / SoD approver needs to Reject ALL requested role assignments, s/he needs to click on "Other Actions" button and then click on the Reject option as shown below. However, before clicking on Reject the user will need to click on Comments tab to record rejection related comments. If this is missed the system will show an error as comments are mandatory. 5. Partially Approving / Rejecting SAP GRC request For partial approvals, the line manager / role owners / SoD approver needs to specify Approve / Reject in the Approval Column - as required, at the line item level for each individual role assignment as shown below. And then clicking on Submit. However, before clicking on Submit the user will need to click on Comments tab to record approval / rejection related comments. If this is missed the system will show an error as comments are mandatory. On approving / rejecting / partially approving the access request, it moves to the next stage in the process and is hence no longer visible in the GRC work inbox for line manager / role owner / SoD approver.

short_description: RQM Jama - How to request access to Requirements management tool Jama

instructions: Jama access is requested via MyAccess tool: https://myaccess.kone.com/ If needed check "How to raise access request in MyAccess" in AskIT Self Help Articles. Jama specific user roles: In my access you can search for "Jama user" as keyword Production: Jama: Users (access to all open projects) Jama: External reviewers (this is for requesting Jama project specific access only. E.g. external auditors. Usually project admin requests the access for these users. These users too need to have KONE account created as no access outside KONE domain cannot be provided even for reviewers.) QA sandbox : Jama: QA Sandbox users (trial usage only. short_description: PPMT - How to provide access to R&D PPMT
introduction: How to process PPMT access requests? How to remove access?
instructions: PPMT access requests are coming via MyAccess tool. Note: No template for any more for user access request. In case new user just leave all the user role details as is in Additional Information section. Only the User is External tick box needs to be updated in case external users ! In case user needs to be used as project resource user will provide the needed information via separate AskIT ticket. Then fill in the Additional information section fields. Provide access: Example of new access request: Description: New role PPMT-User has been added to user Lindholm, Noora in MyAccess. You are receiving this notification as the provisioner of the access right. Please carry out provision task in the target system and inform user about completion. Information related to the user can be seen below: Access Name: PPMT-User User Name: Lindholm, Noora Type: Employee KONE Role: Systems Engineer - RnD WorkerID: 64059862 User SamaccountName: CON_NLINDHOL User Email: NOORA.LINDHOLM@KONE.COM User Username: noora.lindholm@kone.com Step 1: Check if all the information is available to process the request. Step 2: Validate the person if he/she is internal or external. Type = Employee / Contingent. Note that e.g. GiantKONE is counted as external even though the Type is “Employee” in MyAccess. For externals remember to tick the external info box in account sheet. Step 3 : Login to https://ppmt.kone.com/prod/ Scroll down menu items and select Maintenance / Settings Click on users Check if an existing user account: Type user UPN (last row in ticket) as login name, Is skill = No, Is External = All and In Use = All Press enter to execute. In case the account is found you can select and modify it. Otherwise add a new user by pressing the button on top left. Step 4: In case of existing user check and modify if needed. Fill the information (collected from AskIT ticket and attachment) as below. Update only the marked fields and leave the others as defaults. ( NOTE : Additional information only External user update only if needed ) Additional information You can fill in the resource ID if you like. This can help the identifying of some users with similar names in case the e-mail address or UPN changes later. Skills, Roles, Hour rates and Cost center you can leave not set. In case later user needs to be used as internal project resource user will create new AskIT ticket for that update. Tick the external user in case contingent/external/GiantKone user Hour rate values for user currency to be set for new user user as resource . Only one value to be added: EUR = 52.85 USD = 53.00 MXN = 654.00 INR = 1058.22 CNY = 358.79 EUR is the default hour rate in case no other currency is defined. Step 5: After the basic account is created role specific permissions shall be granted. Role / Access Name Actions in short PPMT-User Please create PPMT account for the new user. No additional permission groups to be granted PPMT-Administrator Please check and create the account in PPMT if needed. Please update the user " Admin - Administrators " group. MyAccess ticket is approved already by Anja Airasvirta or Muraliganesh D. PPMT-PPMT Milestone reviewer Please check and create the account in PPMT if needed Add User in “ Audit - PD Stakeholder ” MyAccess ticket is approved by M Kumaravel (or Anja Airasvirta) PPMT-PPMT PD flow user Please check and create the account in PPMT if needed. Add user in " PD flow input " group MyAccess ticket is approved by M Kumaravel or Petrisor Claudia (or Anja Airasvirta) PPMT-PPMT Project manager Please check and create the account in PPMT if needed. Add user to " Project management-own projects " group. MyAccess ticket is approved by M Kumaravel (or Anja Airasvirta) PPMT-Purple KPI Readers Please check and create the account in PPMT if needed. Add user to " Purple KPI metrics readers " group in PPMT. AND For QlikSense report access user needs to be added into this excel as user: PPMT-Projects (access control of this file Amit Arya) MyAccess ticket is approved by M Kumaravel or Petrisor Claudia (or Anja Airasvirta) PPMT - HATLogistics Team member Please check and create the account in PPMT if needed. Add user to Material - HATLogistics Group. MyAccess ticket is approved by HAT Logistics team managers: Lehti Leena and Lehtonen Marko Note: If we receive any MyAccess request for the role PPMT-Administrator For Support Team member: Add Admin - Administrators group access (only for Support Team members). For other users, we need to check with Airasvirta Anja <anja.airasvirta@kone.com> and she will suggest the right group. Then we have to provide access to that particular group. Step 6: Once the new user is added to the right group, please inform them through Remedy email Then the ticket can be resolved. Remove access: PPMT user role termination case is usually when user is terminated and leaving KONE. Other reason for role removals are very rare and they can be checked case by case with Anja Airasvirta if needed. Example of remove ticket Description: Role PPMT-User has been removed from the user Repola, Jani in MyAccess. You are receiving this notification as the provisioner of the access right. Please carry out provision task in the target system and inform user about completion. Information related to the user can be seen below: Access Name: PPMT-User User Name: Repola, Jani Type: Contingent KONE Role: EXT-Other consultant WorkerID: 64089161 User SamaccountName: k64089161 User Email: Jani.repola@kone.com User Username: jani.repola@kone.com PPMT - HATLogistics Team member Remove user form "Materials - HATLogistics" group. In case terminated then you can remove all permissions. Note that in case user is leaving KONE and account is terminated MyAccess will generate ticket for each role.

short_description: How to grant THD access to a user?
introduction: How to grant THD access to a user? THD access to a user, Technical help desk access to a user.
instructions: Note: User who grants access must have 'System admin' profile in Business Org Prod. There are two type of scenario : 1 : The user is already available in system. 2 : User is not available in system. In Scenario 1 : Steps to follow: 1. If user is already available in system and requested for THD access, first we need to check if the request is from KEY user or not. 2. If the request is from Key User then no approval required directly follow the steps 4-6. 3. If the request is from non key user then first take approval from his country key user. 4. Now, first check if the existing profile is "KONE: Helpdesk Agent" then in that case just enable the "Service Cloud User" checkbox and change the user role as per the attached "THD_Countries&Roles" sheet. 5. If the existing profile is not "KONE: Helpdesk Agent", then grant the 'Technical help desk' and 'THD Lightning Console' permissions under 'Permission set assignments'. 6. Click on edit assignment button and select the permission set and then click on 'Save' button. 7. After assigning permission set, enable the "Service cloud checkbox" on user detail page and change the user role as per the attached "THD_Countries&Roles" sheet. In Scenario 2 : Steps to follow: 1. If user is not available in system, ask the requestor to provide approval from his CRM key user(if no User request form exists). 2. Once we received user request form, just make sure after creating user via user request form that "Service Cloud" checkbox should be enabled. 3. If the profile is not ""KONE: Helpdesk Agent" in user request form and user requested for THD access then assign the " Technical help desk' and 'THD Lightning Console' permissions set. (Refer the above given steps for assigning the permission set) 4. Verify the user Role as per the attached "THD_Countries&Roles" sheet, if the role is different in sheet then confirm with Key-user once and accordingly assign the role. Note : The "THD_Countries&Roles" sheet provided by business owner and as per them deviations are minimal and can be managed on an individual basis.

short_description: System returns ‘URL doesn’t exist’ message when clicking Sales Cockpit bar.
introduction: How to resolve issue 'URL doesn't exist' in Sales Cockpit
instructions: Knowledge Artifact Document deals with incidents raised from user about ‘URL No Longer Exists’ issue for Sales Cockpit report. Detailed Issue: It happened several times that user raised incident saying that system returns ‘URL No Longer Exists’ error when they click the Sales Cockpit bar. They expect to have a report opened. Steps to resolve the issue 1. Log in as user. Open Sales Cockpit, find out the target which is having the issue. 2. Open the Sales Team, click Manage Target button to go into team target detailed page. Search out the target name here to find out the exact target name because the target name in Sales Cockpit may not have the business type like VB. 3. Log out as user. Search the target metric name. Check ‘Report Parameters’ value. If it’s joined_report, then system works as expected. Because this target metric is returning a share or rate value. As per Sales Cockpit Instruction CRM document, not able to access report for joined report due to Salesforce limitation. Explain this to user. If ‘Report Parameters’ is having the normal parameter value like ‘ pv0={lob}&pv4={usr}&pv5={rptdstart}&pv6={rptdend}&pv7={broff}’, then check the ‘Currency Report Mapping’ field. Open the report ID to see whether the report ID is valid or not. Sometimes, the report ID no longer exists.

short_description: How to resolve duplicate sales target issue for sales team or sales person?
introduction: How to resolve duplicate sales target issue for sales team or sales person?
instructions: Knowledge Artifact Document deals with the issue that duplicate sales target data appears in Sales Cockpit. Detailed Problem: Problem 1: Duplicate sales target with blank value appear in Sales Cockpit. Sample ticket: IN00177074. In below screen shot, next to the Dec, there are also targets (in Red mark) which are duplicate with the ones from Jan to Dec. For every target metric for a sales team or individual sales person, there should be 12 records from Jan to Dec. The ones in Red mark is duplicate ones. Below screen shot is for duplicate targets with blank value. Below screen shot is for duplicate targets with 0 value. The resolution is to delete the duplicate target data from target assignment object. For this problem, we should delete the blank targets which were mostly created by Premier Support before for user to manually filling in target data. Also the 0 targets (this is more complicated because 0 value could be the correct target, must make sure the 0 targets being deleted are duplicate ones, make sure there are correct targets for every month). Steps for blank value issue: Extract target data from target assignment object for the sales team / individual sales person who is suffering this problem. Sample SQL: Select Id, OwnerId, IsDeleted, Name, CurrencyIsoCode, CreatedDate, CreatedById, LastModifiedDate, LastModifiedById, SystemModstamp, LastActivityDate, LastViewedDate, LastReferencedDate, Active__c, Amount__c, Business_Line__c, Business_Type__c, Month__c, Sales_Team__c, Salesperson__c, Target_Category__c, Target_Metric__c, Target__c, Year__c, Clone_To_All_Members__c, IsTeamMemberTarget__c FROM Target_Assignment__c where salesperson__c in ( 'a1ww000000Ae682', 'a1ww000000A5zP3', 'a1ww0000000Q607', 'a1ww000000A9lB9', 'a1ww0000000Q60R') In the extract file, filter to keep data for only current year (year 2018). Filter column ‘TARGET__C’ the blank values. You can check the CREATEDATE and CREATEBYID of the filtered records. These blank records were created by Salesforce Premier Support on year 2017. Delete these blank records in Data Loader. Steps for 0 value issue: More completed than deleting blank value, not all 0 values are incorrect ones. Some 0 values are intended to set target as 0. Therefore, map the extract file with Sales Cockpit -> Manage Target page UI to decide which 0 values should be removed. Filter the exact file by ‘TARGET__C’ 0. Select a sales person. Open the target page for this sales person in Sales Cockpit -> Manage Target page. The point is there should be 12 records for Jan to Dec respectively after removing non-meaningful 0 values. The CREATEDATE and CREATEBYID columns could also be referred because normally the non-meaningful 0 values are created in one batch. After deletion, check the target UI again to make sure there are only targets from Jan to Dec. Problem 2: Duplicate sales target with non-blank value appear in Sales Cockpit. Sample ticket: IN00171769. Similar resolution but the target data to delete are with non-blank or non-zero values. If the duplicate data records are with the same value, it’s easy, just delete one of them. If the duplicate data records are with different values, we’d better to confirm with user which one to delete. For example in IN00171769, a batch data was uploaded on Jan 23th, another batch data was uploaded on Jan 24 th . Confirmed with user which date’s data to delete. The final resolution was to delete all existing data and user provided new data for upload.

short_description: Give access to new user in Apptio tool
introduction: How to give access to a new user in Apptio tool?
instructions: 1) A new user must request access to Apptio via MyAccess. The request will go to approval based on the requested access role. Also adding additional access roles needs a request via MyAccess. Adding access to additional content (like a new project) is requested via service request or key user. 2) In case access is approved, service request will be automatically created. Go to https://kone.apptio.com and from the Settings menu, select Access Administration. A new tab is opened for https://frontdoor-ui-eu.apptio.com/home?domain=kone. Access is given in two steps: 1. Add the new user and 2. Assign a role to the user. 2.1.) Add a new user and assign a role On Home tab of Access administration, add the new user by clicking Add User(s) -button on the right hand side of the screen. It will take you to the Access Wizard. Select task Add User(s) and domain kone, if not already selected. Click Next. Fill in following information. Full Name: Lastname Firstname (as in KONE Outlook address book) Email: KONE email address (as in KONE Outlook address book) Username: KONE email address (as in KONE Outlook address book) Account type: Standard Leave Persona as None, user can select this themselves. Click Next. Select "Grant Roles" and "Do not send User an activation email". Check that the user details are correct and click Confirm to continue. Select task "Manage Environment Access", customer "kone.com" and select the correct environment based on the role approved for the user. Click Next. Check that the user is mentioned in the Selected users and click Next. Select the approved role(s) from the list by clicking the plus icon on the correct row(s). Check that the Added Roles contains the correct selections and click Next. Check that the Customer is kone.com and Environment is correct, and the user roles are correct.
