short_description: CAD Console Access Management
introduction: CAD Console Access Management
instructions: Access to CAD Console and CWS Console is managed via MyAccess. User needs to have role CAD Console: User or CWS Console: User assigned in order to be able to access the system. CAD Console consists of two parts: a) Production and b) Verification. As a first action, verify, to which console access is required. Production (CAD Console Prod) is where the CAD packages are published and then triggered to the mCAD Workers. Verification (CWS Console or Dev CAD Console) is where the CAD packages are verified before publishing them to the Production CAD Console. 1. Log into the CAD Console with your AD account and password. Preferred browser is Mozilla Firefox or Chrome. Production: CAD Console Prod (https://cadconsole-prod.konenet.com) Verification: CWS Console (https://cwsconsole-prod.konenet.com) 2. Open user administration view via Administrative tools - Users and filter for the user you need to update: 3. If the user does not exist yet, ask him/her to request role CAD Console (or CWS Console respectively) from MyAccess . 4. Select the user name to view the permissions Here you can add permissions to new KM numbers, change access level of existing permissions (arrow up or down) or remove a certain KM number. The various access types can be seen in the header. Read, Write, Upload and Admin-permissions require the Operations Engineer's approval . Preferably the material's Admin himself/herself assigns permissions to their materials . Superuser and System manager access needs to be requested via MyAccess role CAD Console: Admin or CWS Console: Admin and require approval from Jussi Salminen or Claudio Halter. To find a certain item number's Operations Engineer (OPS), follow these steps: 1. Open the CAD packages view 2. Search on this page for the item number in question, for example with CTRL+F The name of the Operations Engineer is highlighted in blue. If the name is missing or specified as "undefined", reach out to Jussi Salminen or Claudio Halter in order to specify the OPS value. NOTE: The ordinary end user access does not grant access to the QA environments. If the user needs to access CAD Console QA or CWS Console QA for validating new releases of the consoles, he/she requires MyAccess roles CAD Console: Key User or CWS Console: Key User.

short_description: OSS - Blue screens on HP x360 1020 G2 models
introduction: How to fix recurrent ble screens on HP x360 1020 G2 models
instructions: N.B : This KBA is meant for OSS, Ask IT isn't supposed to make any registry changes. Q. How to fix recurrent ble screens on HP 1020 models You need to add adds the following key to Trend settings in registry: "PowerMonitorTime"=dword:00000010 It adds a delay to the start-up of Trend Micro services when laptop wakes up from Connected Standby mode (which x360 and tablet models use).

short_description: How to manually correct Windchill associations
introduction: How to manually correct Windchill associations When an incorrect association exists between CAD and KM, the following issues occur: An incorrect structure is populated in the KM BOM. The wrong structure is subsequently imported into PDM. Example : Material KM is under “C” Revision and associated CAD objects assembly under “B” Revision
instructions: Windchill rights required: The CAD objects having draft state, end user can correct the association The CAD objects having any state other than draft state, Windchill admin need to correct the association Prerequisite: Create a new workspace in Windchill Steps: Open affected assembly revision “B” in separate tab and check for the related objects Click Actions and select Edit Associations Select affected revision of target CAD object Note : You need to select only one affected CAD files at once, if you select more than one affected CAD files you will experience an error. If there are multiple CAD files revision affected, then first correct lower level of revision. Example: Here KM material "C" and "B" revision are affected but lower revision is “B”, so break association of KM “B” link with “A” revision assembly. Select KM material and click remove association Then the KM material and CAD object get checkout under the new work space Select the KM material, Check in and Submit Select the CAD object, Undo check out and Submit Now remove all files from your current work space Repeat the above steps till you get correct association at each revision Note: BOM refresh refer KB000A1736

We coordinate central, branch and customer tasks in the system to understand every contract negotiation and it’s status. We also use the ECMS system as our formal contract repository. For support, email: ECMS@kone.com. This will automatically create the ticket and response should happen inside of one business day. For escalation: contact luke.greene@kone.com, debbie.sorenson@kone.com TAKE NOTE: This is short term solution until which time a defined support channel is created in AskIT Portal for ECMS. introduction: This Article guides PDM Support team on How to Inactive user account in PDM?
instructions: Note : 1. We do not Deactivate/Inactivate the Service account. Some of the Service accounts listed below. 2. Never delete the user from PDM, instead of it makes the user inactive. Sample service accounts : extsap, extsap1, svc_mulesoft_epdm, svc_mulesoft_tpdm, c4lm, sys_msms_user. If the contact person is not mentioned, get approval from the line manager. Steps: Search and open the user account in VariClient. Right-click on Granted privilege and Copy Attribute Value. Right-click Revoked privileges and Paste Attribute Value. Go to Department & set it to "OUT" Go to State > State user state as Inactive > Click OK Click save Steps for Bulk inactive: Step 1: Open the Variclient finder and search the object (user ID) in a bulk way. Step 2: Select all --> Right click --> Copy results to group update --> State group update Step 3: Client will automatically navigate to the new window. Choose "Inactive" and click update. Step 4: Once updated, click "ok" in the prompt. Step 5: Select all Inactive state accounts and click --> Export results objects and save as excel format. Step 6: Copy the granted_privileges to the revoked_privileges column and set the department as 'OUT' Step 7: Import the file through Variclient. Tools --> Import Step 8: Verify the changes through Finder.

short_description: How to install EPLAN P8 Electric
introduction: 1.1 How to install SQL Server Express 1.2 How to Install EPLAN Electric P8 1.3 How to synchronize KONE Library 1.4 How to configure EPLAN License 1.5 How to setup EPLAN Databases 1.6 How to reconfigure PDM Integration
instructions: This guide gives instructions on how to install EPLAN. Please note that the role EPLAN Electric P8: User first has to be requested and approved in MyAccess. This will create a RemedyForce ticket for manual installation. Example support scenarios with necessary steps: Note: User requires administrative privileges with his logon account for EPLAN installation! Fresh installation : 1.1 Install SQL Server Express 1.2 Install EPLAN Electric P8 1.3 Synchronize EPLAN Library 1.4 Configure EPLAN License 1.5 Setup EPLAN Databases VariClient installation : 1.6 Automatic installation of Eplan-PDM integration on top of Variclient EPLAN Library is outdated: 1.3 Synchronize EPLAN Library EPLAN version upgrade: 1.2 Install EPLAN Electric P8 1.3 Synchronize EPLAN Library 1.5 Setup EPLAN Databases Choose a local network share for EPLAN installation files which is closest to the user's region: Location <server_dir> Allen \\uskmscad101\eplan Chennai \\keimscad101\eplan Hyvinkaa \\kcofivmms154\eplan Kunshan \\chnvmmscad101\eplan Moline \\uskvmmsecad001\eplan Munich \\euhcimsecad402\eplan 1.1 How to install SQL Server Express Only required if this is the very first Eplan installation on this PC Go to directory<server_dir>\EPLAN\install_script\sql_express\2014 Execute script sql_express_install.bat (run as administrator) Wait for the installation to finish. This may take a really long time! When ready, DOS prompt(command window) says" installation finished Reboot the PC 1.2 Install new Eplan Electric P8 Go to directory<server_dir>\EPLAN\install_script\2.9 Execute script client_install.bat (run as administrator) Wait for the installation to finish Hit a key always when asked. 1.3 How to synchronize KONE Library Make sure EplanElectric P8 is closed Go to directory<server_dir>\EPLAN\library_script\2.9 Execute script library_sync.bat Wait for the script to finish. Press a key always when asked Latest KONE Library is now synchronized to user’s system This same script should be run frequently, e.g. once per day. 1.4 How to configure EPLAN License Invoke EPLAN Electric P8 In case of a fresh installation, you are asked to specify a license server. In the Validate license window, select Connect to License Manager Click OK For Global License Type in the License Manager name eplanlicense When successful, there is a confirmation message about used server Master Data Update During the very first invocation, system also asks about masterdata update–Click No This is not necessary, because KONE library includes the necessary masterdata. 1.5 How to setup EPLAN Databases Open Options → Settings via Menu toolbar On the Settings tree browser on right, select and expand “User” category. Necessary settings can be found from categories: Management Translation Parts database setup Navigate to “User > Management > Parts” Select server and database Click OK in both dialogs to save settings Dictionary database setup In the sameway, set DICTIONARY_V29 as Dictionary Project database setup In the sameway, set PROJECTS_V29 as Project management database. 1.6 Automatic installation of Eplan-PDM integration on top of Variclient As a prerequisite,Variclient needs to be installed with Packman utility. short_description: PRO - How to process a Project creation requests - Biz Org
introduction: When we receive a project creation request for PRO in Biz Org, what steps should we follow.
instructions: Knowledge Artifact This Article is used to guide users when we receive a ticket to create missing project to PRO (MP/VP). Always projects should be automatically created from CRM to PRO via the interface. We should never create a project manually by navigating to a location. If a user is requesting to create a project for which CRM opportunity do not exist, then inform the users that we are not going to have a PRO Project if we do not have a CRM Opportunity. Step 1 Login to Salesforce Business org. Step 2 Find the opportunity for which project needs to be created manually. Major Projects Investgate the potential root cause why project is not created in PRO Opportunity is moved from earlier stage to RFQ – not created directy to RFQ Opportunity is between RFQ – Order Received (won) stage Major project template is being used in CRM Ask requestor to contact to opportunity owner in CRM to open opportunity for editing and save it – this should trigger the push from CRM to PRO if above mentioned conditions are met Once the user confirms that project is now available in PRO no further actions are needed from our end, just close the ticket Volume Projects - (for Volume projects to be created Opty business type should not be Maintenance (SEB)/ VB Repairs/ VA Repairs/ Spares, for NEB optys – Business types should not be doors along with the previous conditions) If an opty record type is Opportunity/VB Opportunity/NEB Opportunity then if we edit and just save, the project won’t be created, so we need to check if the project complexity is medium/large or Area is ENA (though project complexity is null or small this method of editing stage will work for ENA area optys), if so then we should ask the requestor to contact the CRM opportunity owner to change the opty stage to some other value (like on hold or any other stages) and then update it to RFQ or Order received (won) or Lost ot No Bid or Cancelled what ever is appropriate in this opportunity, so that project gets created automatically. If the opty record type is Opportunity/VB Opportunity/NEB Opportunity and project complexity is null or small (area is not ENA), then in that case request the user to consult CRM opportunity owner to update the project complexity to Medium or large and then change the Opty stage to and for as said above (change the opty stage to some other value (like on hold or any other stages) and then update it to RFQ or Order received (won) or Lost ot No Bid or Cancelled what ever is appropriate). PRO Project will then be created. If CRM opportunity owner denay to change the project complexity then inform users that we are not going to have a PRO project whose opty is of small or null complexity and then close the ticket. In case in any way the project is not being created, then based on Juha approvals only we are supposed to create them using below code. In developer console click on Open execute anonymous window and copy paste the below code. Update the highlighted text in yellow with the opty record id for which PRO project should be created and click on execute button. private static List<XactKone__PROProjectRecord__e> allEventsTest = new List<XactKone__PROProjectRecord__e>(); List<XactKone__PROProjectRecord__e> allEvents = new List<XactKone__PROProjectRecord__e>(); XactKone__PROProjectRecord__e platformEvent = new XactKone__PROProjectRecord__e(); platformEvent.XactKone__OriginatingRecordId__c = '006w000XXXXXXXX'; allEvents.add(platformEvent); allEventsTest.add(platformEvent); List<Database.SaveResult> sr = EventBus.publish(allEvents); Once this code is run, PRO project will be created and displayed in Org hierarchy as intended. No other manual intervention is required. So in case if the Opty is satisfying above conditions but the project is still not being created though we used the code, then check if the opty name if beyond 80 chars and if so request CRM to rename the opty such that the opty name is below 80 chars and then try following the above steps such that PRO project for this opty is is automatically created.

STEPS Login as Windchill Admin and Go to Site à Utilities Go to System Administration à Purge Management Define Purge Query: Go to Purge Jobs à Open collection criteria manager Define Collection Query : New Query à Add name & Description à Click Next (Advisable to add Ticket number or object ID number) Define Context criteria: Context All à Select Target Context à Select Folder à Click Next (Note : Only Target folder need to be selected , be specific for selecting target folder) Define Selection Criteria : Criteria drop down à Define object Type à Add Define object Type : Object Type à Click Find Define object Type : Object Type drop down à Select respective type à Click Ok Define object Type Added Define one more criteria : Iteration à Add Define number of iteration : All but last à Number of iterations à Click finish Collection Query created Run Query : Click New Job Add New job Name Description à Next Query à Find Search by filter criteria à Select à Ok Query à Next Define override conflicts : Query à Select both option as per screen shot à Next Scheduling options: Select preview now à Next Cross check Query objects: Click Affected objects List Save and open txt file à Affected objects List Check list of object selected for Purge : Confirm the list à New Job Window à Finish Confirm à Ok Purge in Progress Job completed Check logs: Click "i" info button of completed job à Action à Purge Logs

Before starting the session, you will see the below screen popup: IMPORTANT On this screen, enter the below information before starting the firefighting session without miss as this is important from audit and approving the FF logs perspective (refer below screenshot for details): Select the proper ' Reason Code ' from the drop down menu based on the nature of your activity. Enter the ServiceNow incident/ticket or RITM or Service Request (SR) or Change Request (CR) number in the field below Reason Code. In the section ' Please enter the actions that you anticipate to perform ', enter the list of all the transactions/t-codes which you anticipate to execute using this FF ID Click - You will then be logged on the target system (in our case, it is Z02 system, as shown below) where you can perform your actions. Click on Start SAP access to work in the target system with the FF ID. In order to log off from the FF session click on and user will come to initial login screen of FF ID Once user comes to below screen after clicking in target system, user need to unblock / free the FF ID from GRP system. Click “Unblock / Free” to free the FF ID.

short_description: How to delete objects in Windchill
introduction: How to delete objects in Windchill
instructions: PRE CHECK If the object is in draft state, get an approval from "Modified By" (last modifier) If the object is in any other state apart from draft, get approval from "Created By" (creator) Inform the Last Modifier/Creator to take a back up of all objects as it will not be available after deletion If associated objects need to be deleted like Material, Drawing etc.., provide the complete list to requester and ask for confirmation PROCEDURE Using the below steps you can delete only the latest Iteration/Revision of an object or a complete object. In order to delete the non latest Iteration/Revision refer KB000A1890 Search the required object and go to history If there exists any promotion request as displayed above under Authorization, the same need to be deleted first. Click the Promotion Request as shown above Click "Actions" and select "Delete" It will ask for confirmation, click OK Go to the required objects and click Actions Click "Delete" from the "Actions" drop down list Select the collect related drawings icon and collect related parts icon as shown in red box below ONLY if it is required to delete associated objects too. NOTE: You can't delete library components You need to select type of deletion as shown in green box above Delete all iterations of each object included in the table - Enables you to delete all iterations of latest revision (ex: It will delete B.1 to B.3) Delete all revisions of each object included in the table - Enables you to delete all revision of a object (ex: It will delete -.1 to B.3) Delete latest iterations of each object included in the table - Enables you to delete only the latest iteration of the latest revision (ex: It will delete only B.3) NOTE: The type of deletion is based on end user request. If you are not sure, kindly confirm the same with end user and then proceed. It may throw error, click resolve conflicts icon as shown below in blue box In conflicts management window, click Retry icon to remove objects from workspace Repeat the above steps to remove all conflicts It will display processing screen Later check whether it got deleted successfully

short_description: How to configure report permissions and visibility settings in a Konect Web report?
introduction: How to configure report permissions and visibility settings in a Konect Web report?
instructions: Configuring report permissions and visibility settings When a new report is created, it is automatically available for any users who are members of the same groups as the user who created the report, except for Public. (If the user creating the report is a member only of Public, then the report is available to Public.) The groups that have permission to a report are listed in the Assignee Groups field of the Report form. There are several settings you can change to control whether other users can see a report: Mark the report private — For Web reports, select the Private check box in the Report Designer. This removes all groups from the Assignee Groups field in the Report form when the report is saved. In this case, only the report creator and Administrator can see the report. This is the default setting when a new report is created. Set report permissions — Add or remove groups in the Assignee Groups field in the Report form. Mark the report invisible — To prevent a report from appearing in the Report Console or the ReportSelection form, but still allow workflow to run the report, set the Visible in Console field in the Report form to No . Set status to inactive or pending — To prevent a report from appearing in the Report Console or ReportSelection forms, and prevent workflow from running the report, set the Status field to Inactive or Pending . You can use the Pending status to let reviewers know that the report is ready for review.

short_description: Vertex license server installation
introduction: How to install Vertex license server (to the Azure cloud)?
instructions: This article exmplains how to install Vertex License Server 2.0 to Azure cloud server and shall only be followed by Vertex application team. Accessing the cloud server 1. Log on to Windows "bastion" server 10.130.1.6 using Remote desktop 2. From there, take Remote desktop connection to actual license server 10.130.21.132 Use your "normal" Windows account for RDP. Use the admin account only for tasks, which require administrator privileges, e.g. for starting or stopping a service. Server address Server IP address 10.130.21.132 is fixed because of BPC firewall rules. The DNS name for license server is "EUAZ1MSVERTEX41.konenet.com". Service name is "vertexlicense.konenet.com". All the installations should refer to the service name. Installing license server Original installation instruction from Vertex is here: https://kb.vertex.fi/vxinfoen/vertex-license-server-2-0-installation In a brief: 1.Download the installation package. 2.Extract lt_lserv.exe from the package to the computer where the service will be installed 3.Start Windows 'Command Prompt (Admin)' 4.Run the service installation command that fetches automatically your license file over the internet lt_lserv.exe -i [activation code] Kone activation code is at the moment 289740141322 Connection to Vertex and proxy It is required for the license service to connect https://ls.vertex.fi:443 If connection fails, set up proxy connection like this: 1. Open command prompt as administrator 2. Give command 'netsh winhttp set proxy 10.130.1.68:3128' Proxy setting can be checked from command prompt like this: netsh winhttp show proxy Current WinHTTP proxy settings: Proxy Server(s) : 10.130.1.68:3128 Bypass List : (none) License server port setting The license service port setting is a row in file C:\Windows\System32\drivers\etc\services: lt_lserv 2405/tcp #VERTEX License Server Kone port is 2405. For the change to take effect, the service needs to be restarted. To verify port setting, run as admin command netstat -a -n -b Active Connections Proto Local Address Foreign Address State ... TCP 0.0.0.0:2405 0.0.0.0:0 LISTENING [lt_lserv.exe] Port forwarding Legacy installations of KVertex and Vertex G4 point to different server ports than 2405. This is why they have been forwarded on the server (in command prompt as administrator): netsh interface portproxy add v4tov4 listenaddress=10.130.21.132 listenport=2402 connectaddress=10.130.21.132 connectport=2405 protocol=tcp netsh interface portproxy add v4tov4 listenaddress=10.130.21.132 listenport=2404 connectaddress=10.130.21.132 connectport=2405 protocol=tcp Server monitoring Service status can be monitored from web browser: http://10.130.21.132:2405/?passcode=3180908139 The relevant license codes are: s1 - Vertex ED (SVertex) s4 - KVertex (Vertex 2D) s1048580 - Vertex G4 Other notes License server runs as a normal Windows service and can be started/stopped/restarted via Task Manager. The amount and quality of licenses is updated dynamically from Vertex, but in case of connection/update problem it is good to know that the file controlling the licenses is C:\Windows\System32\drivers\etc\lt_llist . License and option codes explained here: https://kb.vertex.fi/g42019fi/usein-kysyttyjae-kysymyksiae/lisenssipalvelimen-suojaustiedoston-sisaelloen-tarkastaminen

short_description: CAD Robot - Request for Process Test Run
introduction: CAD Robot - Request for Process Test Run
instructions: Required input: New product's KM code and revision CAD output requirements Distribution site Test order numbers in Training PDM Prerequisites: CAD package has been verified in CWS Console Process Test Run: 1. Download CAD content from CWS Console a) Browse to CWS Console b) Select CAD packages - CAD packages c) Scroll to the appropriate package and select the filename to download the CAD Package content d) Check that the CAD Package content is properly zipped and do remedial actions if needed: - 7z or winzip format - content is flat list of CAD files (no sub-folders) - instance index name = <revision>.idx 2. Create new CAD Package in CAD Console Production: CAD Package editing through CAD Console UI allowed only for existing materials. Please use the CAD Package editor in Creo/LinkIt UI for new materials. Instructions: Link to PDM 3. Set CAD Robot options in CAD Robot workers Get the clear specification for the output requirements from the requestor and then set the CAD Robot options accordingly: a) Download an existing CAD options package as a starting point. To do so, select CAD Packages - CAD options . b) Save and unzip the package to your local hard drive for further modifications c) After updating the options according to the specifications, zip the files into an zip archive and upload the file via CAD packages - Upload CAD Option Packages . Note! option files are product-specific, but common for all product’s revisions. 4. Run end to end process semi-automatically with given test orders a) Using VariClient , log into PDM Training environment b) Search for test order (order number to be provided by requestor) c) Export order in Excel form via Tools - Export... d) Open the exported file and verify, that SAP Status is E0002 . Orders are automatically submitted from PDM to CAD Console in certain SAP Status (e.g. E0006, E0007, E0008). To ensure, that the order is not yet directly submitted, change the status to E0002 before importing the order. e) Open another VariPDM client session and log into the Production environment f) Import order via Tools - Import... g) In order for PDM to be able to send order to CAD Console, the "CAD Stores" list need to be updated with the new product: Browse to http://pdm.kone.com/kone_production/#!object?obj_id=client_setting:cad-info Enter Edit mode Add a new "CAD Stores" entry with the material number, URL of the CAD Console and the Type (order / configuration). See instructions 51149542D01_en_-.1_KONE_Configuration_Automation_(3).doc in PDM for further details. h) Browse to the order in VariPDM web client and run action “Configure and send to CAD” or “Send orders(s) to Plan C CADRobot” on the test order. Which action to choose depends on how the product is modeled in PDM. 5. CAD Console should now receive the test order and start processing it. After successful execution, ask user to validate the deliverables in PDM.

short_description: How to resolve Salesforce limit 135kb error in My Portfolio application

instructions: Problem Details: After Salesforce November Release in year 2018, Salesforce limit 135kb error happens when user clicking My Portfolio tab to try to access My Portfolio application. This error happens when there are huge data of this user. Background of the Problem: Custom contract object was replaced by standard service contract in Salesforce November Release in year 2018. Custom contract object got retired. Steps to Resolve the Problem: A new custom setting was created along with the release which can resolve the error. Custom Setting -> MyPortfolio Optimizer For user who is facing the limit error, create a new optimizer record for him/her. Name: User ID in Salesforce Disable default view load: Check it, then the default view load will not work for this user Number of records per page: By default 50 With the setting, 50 records will show in every page in My Portfolio for this user. User can switch between pages with pagination buttons.

short_description: KONE- Verification of shipment from Supplier Web to EPIL
introduction: How to verify the shipment arrived from Supplier Web to EPIL?
instructions: Below are the steps to verify the Shipment in which is correctly moved to EPIL application. 1) Login to Supplier Web application. 2) Click in shipments Tab to view list of shipments which are ready to deliver. or we can search the shipments using different parameters like PO, Hu's to see the related shipments. 3) once we find out the related shipment we can verify the column with name Worst EPIL status which needs to saved and printed. that means EPIL lines were updated to that shipment and items were transmitted in the form XML file to SAP PI.. 4) as per EPIL architecture we can see all EPIL files from different sources were transmitted in xml format and reached to SAP PI 5) once SAP PI was process the files items will reached to EPIL DB. 6) from EPIL db we can retrieve package item lists into application on various ways of search like HU',s, PO, Package number WARNING: please remember that the SupplierWeb package in EPIL is visible only when also the Outbound delivery messages YOIG that is containing the same material has been sent from SAP two messages need to match in EPIL. usually YOIG is sent in advance but remember this can be the cause why package is not visible

short_description: Training Document for SAP GRC - 1
introduction: 1. How to create GRC Access request using SAP standard access request form ? 2. How to approve / reject any GRC Access request as an administrator? 3. GRC request cancellation steps 4. Frequently asked questions.
instructions: Description while creating access request using standard form for Infosys team Note: Below is the sample template filled in with required access details. User ID : CON_SGANDE Name : Srinivas Gande Track : SAP AMS Security Role : Infosys SAP GRC Consultant System : Z03 Clone ID / T-code : K64116539 Brief justification : Access is required to perform regular AMS related activities. Approver Name : Jangawad Maltesh Approver Role : Infosys SAP GRC Lead How to create GRC Access request using SAP standard access request form Login to GRC production system and execute transaction code: NWBC Go to Access Management Tab --> Access Request Creation --> Access Request GRC request with / without attachment (mainly for Infosys team) based on transaction code After clicking on Access Request the standard GRC access request form will appear wherein you need provide some details as mentioned below Request type: New Account / Change Account as per requirement. Request for: Self / Other Provide the username if the request is for Other (After entering user ID you need to press enter so that GRC can fetch the details of the entered User ID from AD system) Select the Functional area i.e. Region for which the request is being raised. For Infosys colleagues please select Functional area as Infosys Employees Click on Add button to add roles in the access request for providing access to User. Once clicked on Role, it will open a new window wherein you need to enter System, Role name, Action (T-codes) and some other fields which seems to be necessary for getting the relevant roles and click on Search. If you want to select single role then just select the respective role and click on the button which is highlighted with blue color. if you want to select all the roles from the list then click on the button which is highlighted in red color. Once the user has selected the role the role will be moved to Selected section as shown below and then click on OK button if everything looks to be fine. After clicks on OK the selected roles will be added to the main access request page wherein requestor can change the valid to and valid through date if required. Click on Custom Data tab and add all the details as mentioned below. Request Reason- Select appropriate Request Reason for the request. Request Reason Continued- Mention why the request is being raising. Tco de(s)/ CloneID- mention transaction codes requested or given CloneID. Additional Details- mention ticket number and other details if any. If the request is for Infosys support team member then mention filled in access request template details which is approved by respective track lead. As shown in the below screen you need to click on Submit button and the access request will be submitted for the selected User: CON_SGANDE for 1 role for the requested system with validity 31.12.9999. NOTE: By default "Valid To" date for the roles is 31.12.9999, if user requests to change "Valid To" date then you can change "Valid To" date to the user provided date. If required, you can change/ update the manager ID of the user in the request by going into User Details tab as shown below and press enter button after changing the Manager field, so the full name and email ID is populated in the system from AD. Go to Attachment tab to attach any of the document /file from your local machine to the access request which are related to respective user’s access. Note : If the access request is related to Infosys support team member then please attach approval email from the respective track lead for the filled in access request template. Creating GRC request with / without attachment based on Clone ID Go to Access Management Tab --> Access Request Creation --> Model User, after clicking on Model User system will redirect you to the access request page which is based on Model / Clone ID There are 4 steps which are mentioned below Select User Select Model Access Enter Request Details Review & Submit In the Select User step fill in the Request for: Self / Other. Enter the User ID for whom you want to create access request / grant access (press Enter button after filled in User ID in order to let system fetch the details of the user from the AD system to GRC) If required, requestor can change the Manager ID once the details is populated from the AD. Click on Next after providing all the details then you move to Second step which is Select Model User ID. Select the User (Clone ID/ Model user ID) i.e. the Clone user whose roles / access needs to be copied or added to the new / existing user. (Press Enter after selecting the user so GRC system pulls all the roles / system in which the model user has access) Select Request type: New Account / Change Account. Select the system and in the text fill in the system ID where user requires access. For example, if the user need role for Z01 system then select the filter as System and in text fill in *Z01* and press enter so in the below table you will get only those roles which are with model / clone user in Z01 system. If you want to select single role then just select the respective role and click on the button which is highlighted with blue color. if you want to select all the roles from the list then click on the button which is highlighted in red color. Once the roles have been selected same will be moved to the next below table which is Selected as shown below and then click on Next button to move to the next i.e. third step Enter Request Details if everything looks to be fine. Select the Functional area i.e. Region for which the request is being raised. For Infosys colleagues please select Functional area as Infosys Employees. Click on Custom Data and provide details. As shown in the above screen you need to click on Next button after changing validity dates of the role and can also change the provisioning status i.e. Assign / Remove / Retain as required. The fourth step Review & Submit wherein you just get a preview of whole access request based on model user which further can be submitted by clicking on Submit button so request will be submitted for User: CON_SGANDE with model user: K64116539 for all roles of Z01 system with validity of 31.12.9999. NOTE: By default "Valid To" date for the roles is 31.12.9999, if user requests to change "Valid To" date then you can change "Valid To" date to the user provided date. 2. How to approve any GRC Access request as an administrator? Full approve Go to NWBC --> Access Management tab --> Access Request Administration --> Search Request. Once clicks on Search Request, enter the GRC Request Number in the text box to get the status of the request so user / administrators can Approve the request through Administration ( NOTE : We need to process the access request through admin mode only when we have required approvals through email for the respective approval stage from the respective approvers) After you provide request number and click on Search the request number will be displayed wherein you need to check the Instance approval status like Decision Pending / Finished. If the GRC is at status Decision Pending and if the Administrator has the mail approval then click on Administration after selecting the GRC access request. Further the system will show the step / stage where it is pending so click on the particular stage colored in Blue. The GRC access request will be opened as soon as clicked on the link. Further go to attachment tab and attach the approval mail which you have received from the relevant approver for audit purpose. After attaching the email approval/ relevant documents go to the Comments tab and then enter proper comments such as · GRC: 50856 (In this case) approved as received approval from the respective approver(s). · Click on Submit button in order to approve the whole request through Administration mode . Full reject Go to NWBC --> Access Management tab --> Access Request Administration --> Search Request. Once clicks on Search Request, enter the GRC Request Number in the text box to get the status of the request so user / administrators can Reject the request through Administration ( NOTE : We need to process the access request through admin mode only when we have required approvals through email for the respective approval stage from the respective approvers) After you provide request number and click on Search the request number will be displayed wherein you need to check the Instance approval status like Decision Pending / Finished. If the GRC is at status Decision Pending and if the Administrator has the mail approval then click on Administration after selecting the GRC access request. Further the system will show the step / stage where it is pending so click on the particular stage colored in Blue. The GRC access request will be opened as soon as clicked on the link. Further go to attachment tab and attach the approval which you have received from the relevant approver for Rejecting the GRC request for audit purpose. After attaching the email approval/ relevant documents go to the Comments tab and then enter proper comments such as · GRC: 50856 (In this case) rejected as received approval from Manager to reject the request as the access is no more required / reason whichever seems to be relevant. Click on “ Other Actions” button in order to Reject the whole request through Administration mode as shown below. Partial approval · Go to NWBC --> Access Management tab --> Access Request Administration --> Search Request. Once clicks on Search Request, enter the GRC Request Number in the text box to get the status of the request so user / administrators can Partially Approve the request through Administration ( NOTE : We need to process the access request through admin mode only when we have required approvals through email for the respective approval stage from the respective approvers) After you provide request number and click on Search the request number will be displayed wherein you need to check the Instance approval status like Decision Pending / Finished. If the GRC is at status Decision Pending and if the Administrator has the mail approval then click on Administration after selecting the GRC access request. Further the system will show the step / stage where it is pending so click on the particular stage colored in Blue. The GRC access request will be opened as soon as clicked on the link. Further go to attachment tab and attach the approval which you have received from the relevant approver for Partially approving the GRC request for audit purpose. After attaching the email approval/ relevant documents go to the Comments tab and then enter proper comments such as · Partially approving GRC: 50856 (In this case) at the Line Manager stage after approval received from the manager to approve only 1 role out of 3 / reason whichever seems to be relevant. Select the line item individually which needs to be approved / rejected from the request itself as shown below. Once finalized regarding roles which needs be approved or rejected click on Submit button and the request will be approved with one role as approved and two roles as rejected as shown above. 3. GRC request cancellation steps Go to NWBC --> Access Management tab --> Access Request Administration --> Search Request. Once clicks on Search Request, enter the GRC Request Number in the text box to get the status of the request so user / administrators can Cancel the request through Administration ( NOTE : We need to Cancel the access request through admin mode only when we have required approvals through email or respective ticket). After you provide request number and click on Search the request number will be displayed wherein you need to check the Instance approval status like Decision Pending / Finished. If the GRC is at status Decision Pending then click on Cancel Instance after selecting the GRC access request. If the request is with Finished status then we cannot cancel the request instead we need to inform user / requestor that request is approved / completed. If the user / requestor still needs to remove the access that is requested / raised using the request then we need to raise a GRC request for removal of access that is requested / raised using the request which is with Finished status. Once clicked on Cancel Instance a pop will be opened then click on Yes then another pop with text box will be opened then enter the appropriate reason for cancelling the access request and click on OK to cancel the request. Once clicked on OK you will get a message as Request number :XXXXX ,MSMP Workflow Instance has been cancelled then click on close Icon to get pop up closed. Click on Search to see the latest status of the request and once the reques...

short_description: How to change CR receiver
introduction: How to change CR receiver
instructions: APPROVAL PROCESS: If the request is created from Existing CR receiver then no need of an approval. If the request is created from new CR receiver then the approval from existing CR receiver is needed. If the existing CR receiver has left the organization. We can proceed further to do the changes without any approval. PROCEDURE: Login to VariClient Go to tools and select Schema Editor Select Value Lists and click Find icon In the find window, enter the required CR receiver and click Find Next Select the CR receiver and go to custom properties Double click the existing username under value In "Edit Custom Property Value" window change the new CR receiver's username Click ok NOTE: Changing the username of new CR receiver will be reflected for exiting CR's under same CR value. Right click the CR receiver and click "Edit Short Description" Enter the display name of the new CR receiver Click OK

short_description: How to re-process an order 'in error' in KFM Monitoring (Konect)?
introduction: How to re-process an order 'in error' in KFM Monitoring (Konect)
instructions: Go to "KFM Screen" - you can access using KM97-WelcomeWEB in Konect: Search for the order by terminal number, employee, order number or Status. Generally criteria are: Order Number and Country as in this case: 3. Push the button 'Debug' This button is used to process records that are set in Error (ONLY VALID for INPUT tables) This button will not display when you are in one of the tabs of the KONECT to KFM Examples: a callout goes in unreachable and in the list of records in the “Online Input (SY**)” Tab you have an accepted that is set to Error a callout is set to interrupted and you have a record in the “Online Input (SY**)” that is Input type = back reporting + Escalation status = to be processed a technician requested a list of Planned order but he never received it and in the the “Online Input (SY**)” tab you have a record with Input type = Header Request and input status = Error When you see in the tab of KFM to KONECT a record that is in red or has Escalation status = to be processed Note: It may happen that during this "debugging" of an order you get a persistent error regarding timestamps . Timestamp-errors are not bugs. They are typical arising from manual actions or time sync issues on terminals (aka the clock on the device runs behind) in order to force logic in the data received and shared with the customer, the workflow is checking if the actions on orders are logically time stamped. This to show the customer we are not manipulating KPI's as agreed with our customer. example: you cannot back report before you arrived on site. In that case, consult the article about "KFM timestamp " errors.

Search for ErrorLog.xml and share the latest one to support team or attach it to AskIT ticket. short_description: Training Document for SAP GRC - 2
introduction: 1. How to check the status of already raised GRC access request? 2. How to assign mitigation controls using SAP GRC Access request via Administration? 3. How to approve / reject GRC from work inbox? 4. How to copy a request in GRC? 5. How to run risk analysis for user using user level simulation option in GRC? 6. How to add resolution comments for the tickets which are created by end users? 7. How to set the approver delegation as GRC administrator? 8. Frequently Asked Questions.
instructions: Description while creating access request using standard form for Infosys team Note: Below is the sample template filled in with required access details. User ID : CON_SGANDE Name : Srinivas Gande Track : SAP AMS Security Role : Infosys SAP GRC Consultant System : Z03 Clone ID / T-code : K64116539 Brief justification : Access is required to perform regular AMS related activities. Approver Name : Jangawad Maltesh Approver Role : Infosys SAP GRC Lead 1. How to check the status of already raised GRC access request? Please refer to given below KBA which shows all the steps of checking any raised access request. https://koneprod.service-now.com/now/nav/ui/classic/params/target/kb_view.do%3Fsysparm_article%3DKBA1538 Link for Search request page. https://knecigrp9800.konenet.com:44398/sap/bc/webdynpro/sap/zkone_search_request? sap-client=100&sap-language=EN# 2. How to assign mitigation controls using SAP GRC Access request via Administration? First open the GRC request from Search Request then click on Administration. Once the request is opened click on Risk Violation tab to Mitigate the risks. In the risk violation tab click on Run Risk Analysis to get the risks that are present for the user ID. Select all the risks which are shown in the Result. After selecting all the risks click on the Mitigate Risk button. A new screen will open with the list of mitigation controls defined in the GRC system for access risk IDs. Approver needs to select the available mitigation control IDs defined in the system and click on SAVE Button after finding relevant Mitigation control based on the region for which the access request is created. As shown above click on Control ID column and you will get the whole list of Mitigation Control ID which are defined in the system based on the risk library. Mitigation Controls will be assigned to the user and the above screen will be closed. Once the mitigation controls are assigned, the admin needs to click on Run risk analysis and go back to User Access tab after risk analysis is completed and then approve / reject / close the request as per the mail / incident received. 3. How to approve / reject GRC requests from work inbox? As we have seen the approval / rejection / partial approval via administration (in KB Article: KBA2066) in the same way you can approve / reject / partial approve a request from the Work Inbox, if you are mapped as approver . Go to NWBC --> My Home tab --> Work Inbox Click on any of the GRC request link and approve / reject / partial approve in the same way which we have seen in the above section of approving / rejecting / partial approve as Administrator (As shown in KB Article - KBA2066). Repeat the same step in work inbox to approve/ reject/ partially approve the all GRC requests one by one from inbox. 4. How to copy a request in GRC? Go to NWBC --> Access Management tab --> Copy Request. Enter the existing GRC request number from which you want to create a copy. Select the attributes which you want to copy while creating new request and click on Next button to move to next stage Select User. Once requestor clicks on next, all the attributes selected in the above screen will come in the next screen and you need to provide User ID (after entering user ID click on enter) for which user wants to create a same GRC request based on the data which was given in GRC: 35823 in our case. Click on Next to move to Next step i.e. Enter Request Details In this page you get all the details same as GRC: 35823 in our case i.e. Description, roles, validity date from and to, attachment if you have added previously, comments. NOTE: By default "Valid To" date for the roles is 31.12.9999, if user requests to change "Valid To" date then you can change "Valid To" date to the user provided date. If required make the changes as shown below. Click on Next to move to Next step i.e., Review & Submit wherein you need to review the request and click on Submit button if everything seems to be fine. Once the requestor clicks on submit button new GRC request number will be displayed on the screen. 5. How to run risk analysis for user using user level simulation option in GRC? Go to NWBC --> Access Management tab --> User level Simulation Enter required details in the below screen such as System and user ID. NOTE: By default, Risk Level, Rule Set and User Type are selected. If you require to change Risk Level, Rule Set and User Type that can be changed according to the requirement. You can select type of Risk Analysis i.e., select “Permission Level” for SoD risk analysis and select “Critical Action” for critical action risk analysis. Changes in the format can be done such as Summary report, Detail report, Management summary and Executive Summary. In the above screen admin can add action(s) (transaction code) or role which they are planning to assign to the user ID. Once the desired t-codes/ roles or both are entered then admin can run simulation which will provide the analysis of risk by taking into consideration of current authorizations which the user is having and the additional t-codes/ roles which are entered in the second step. The result will have existing roles of user and additional t-codes/ roles that are added. Run the Risk Analysis and provide the results that if some combination of access to t-codes / roles or both are given user then what would be risks that user may have. Click on “Run in foreground” button if there are less number of users or run in background mode if there are more number of users in the analysis. Given below screen refers to the same. Below is the result of User level simulation on user ID K64116539 for Z04 system. when FB01 transaction code is added to the user then what are the access risks will arise. 6. Resolution comments for the tickets which are created by end users (If GRC Requests are created by Security Team). Once GRC form request is created, please add the below comments into Resolution section of the Remedy ticket. Note : In the below comments before adding to the ticket, please replace requester name in < requester name> and GRC request number created in <GRC request number>. --------------------------------------------------- Hello <Requester name>, We have Replaced your ticket for access to Z0* systems by creating GRC request: <GRC request number>. From 2019 onwards, we are processing all SAP access requests through GRC. Please note that access will be provided upon resolution of GRC request. For checking the GRC request status please follow the link: https://knecigrp9800.konenet.com:44398/sap/bc/webdynpro/sap/zkone_search_request?sap-client=100&sap-language=EN# Did you know that GRC Access Request in now LIVE for your region? In the future, for SAP access requests for Z0* systems, you can raise a GRC request – resolution is FASTER since it is automated! See “SAP Access Requests” section on kw portal : https://kwportal.konenet.com/irj/portal KBA on How to raise GRC request is available there. You can login with your KONE credentials. In case you have any queries, please send a mail to KONE_SAP_Security_GRC@KONE.COM --------------------------------------------------------- 7. How to set the approver delegation as GRC Administrator? Log in to GRC system and execute transaction code NWBC Go to NWBC à Access Management tab à Admin Delegation. Click on Delegate button and add Approver ID (i.e., primary approver) and add Delegated approver ID (i.e., secondary approver). Provide Valid from and To date and set the status as Active. Select the Delegation Type. We have AC (Access Control), PC (Process Control) and both options to select. Once details are provided review details and click on “Save” button. Delegation will be set up successfully for secondary approver as per the dates mentioned. Until delegation validity period ends, both primary approver (delegator) and secondary approver (Delegated) will receive the approval notification emails and both of them have chance to action (approve / reject) on the request. 8. Frequently Asked Question(s) Please refer to KBA article to check the FAQ(s) for SAP GRC ARM https://koneprod.service-now.com/now/nav/ui/classic/params/target/kb_view.do%3Fsys_kb_id%3D7dcc5e1fc36186505d2900e9d001311b

short_description: Ask IT - OneDrive File Recovery
introduction: Can I restore deleted files from ONE DRIVE ?
instructions: OneDrive File Recovery: a Maximum of 30 Days Limit When you happened to delete files and folders from OneDrive storage, don't worry, you still have a chance to restore them back within 30 days. According to Microsoft, users who removed documents, photos, video games, music, movies and program data files from OneDrive in Windows 10 are not lost at all at once. Instead, OneDrive keeps the deleted items in its recycle bin for a maximum 30 days and a minimum 3 days. To restore deleted files from OneDrive in Windows 10, follow the instruction in below part. STEP 1. Right-click OneDrive icon and select view online . STEP 2. Sign in your OneDrive account on the OneDrive website . STEP 3. Click the Recycle Bin button in the left pane. STEP 4. All the deleted files and folders will be displayed in the right pane. To restore specific files or folders, pick them by selecting their checkbox, to restore all items, tap or click Restore all items . NOTE: When you delete files on OneDrive using File Explorer, they're moved to your computer's desktop Recycle Bin. You can simply restore them from there unless you've emptied the Windows Recycle Bin or the Recycle Bin is overflowing, At this point, old items are removed automatically. How to Recover Permanently Deleted Files from OneDrive? Your OneDrive folder location is set as a default at C:\Users\[your username] , which contains all the synced files and folders. When you delete something by whichever means, even after the 30 days limit exceeds or OneDrive & Windows Recycle Bin is cleared and emptied, there is one way to recover permanently deleted OneDrive files. Try a third-party data recovery tool to recover original files that were stored on HDD or external USB drive, SD card or other storage devices & emptied Windows Recycle Bin. Among all, we recommend EaseUS file recovery software , which stands out for its full capability of deleted files recovery on Windows platforms. Prepare the storage drive that saved the original copy of the deleted files on OneDrive, and follow the guide to restore them back. Notice: 1. If you delete the cloud device (like Dropbox, OneDrive, Google Drive) data, and the local files are in sync with the cloud device, your local files will also be deleted. You can use EaseUS Data Recovery Wizard to recover the local files. However, this file recovery software cannot recover data from a cloud device. It can only recover data on the local disk. 2. EaseUS Data Recovery software cannot recover local disk data if the disk has been overwritten! Detailed Steps to Recover Data: Step 1. Select the disk partition or storage location where you lost cloud files and click " Scan ". Step 2. Wait for the scanning process to finish. The scanning process may take a little bit of time, please be patient. Step 3. Recover the files. After the scan, preview all the listed recoverable files and select those you want. Finally, click the " Recover " button to get them back.

short_description: How to deactivate PRM User
introduction: How to deactivate PRM User
instructions: I nstructions to ASKIT - request for PRM/ Community user deactivation 1. When ASKIT team receives a request form CRM Key user to deactivate PRM/Community user, provide the following info to the CRM Key user: ”There are 2 options for deactivation: A) non-permanent: In oder to deactivate the PRM/Community portal user, a CRM Key user can go to the Contact related to the PRM/Comminity user, click at the top of the page ” Manage external user - View partner user” . That will redirect you to the partner user page – there you can click ”Edit” and untick the checkbox ”Active”. This way you CRM Key user can re-activate the user if needed in the future and also the records that belong to this user, still will be visible in CRM for other users. B) Permanent deactivation: Please inform the CRM Key user: ” For a complete deactivation of the user and disabling portal log in to the user, all the records that belong to this user, will become invisible to KONE as well as to the user (as he is not active anymore). In order for ASKIT to proceed with a permanent deactivation ,CRM Key user needs to confirm to ASKIT that all the records that belong to this user had been already moved to another active PRM user” If option A is selected by CRM Key user, the case can be closed. If option B - follow the standard deactivation process, currently described in ”AID-PRM” latest version 1.CRM Key user can go to Contact – Manage external user - View partner user 2.Click ”Edit” and untick the checkbox ”Active”. This way you CRM Key user can re-activate the user if needed in the future and also the records that belong to this user, still will be visible in CRM for other users. Alternatively, for a complete deactivation of the user , all his records that belong to this user, will become invisible to KONE as well as to the user (as he is not active anymore): You need first move all the records that belong to this user to another active PRM user and then make a case to AKSIT requesting the user deactivation according to the standard process

short_description: How to create PRM/Community user
introduction: How to create a new PRM/Community user? How to enable existing PRM user as "Community" user?
instructions: Knowledge Artifact Document deals with process of enabling the existing PRM users for "Community " access Creating users for "Community". Any request related to PRM /Community should be approved by Larisa Vallee . Request for enabling the PRM users for "Community " access will come in a free format from CRM Key user. Request to create a new "Community" user will come in an Excel Format with all the required information. Note! As of 1.12.18 Requests for creating new "PRM" users should not be accepted - notify the requester that from 1.1.2018 only "Community" users are created and the template for creating new "Community " users should be used. HOW TO ENABLE EXISTING PRM USER FOR COMMUNITY receive a Request from CRM Key user to grant access to “Community” for PRM user /or to assign the permission set KONE PRM Distributor Community Access to the existing PRM user Go to the PRM Partner portal user profile (license Gold Partner) and assign 2 permission sets: "KONE PRM Distributor Community Access" and "PRM Community MP record type Field Access" Ensure that the check box “Send welcome email” IS selected, before actual members are added to the community HOW TO CREATE A NEW COMMUNITY USER (FORMERLY "PRM") The below points are prerequisite for a creating new partner user: The account record type should be Distributor/Agent. The shared excel should have ‘Contact Id’ (SFDC Id – 15 or 18 digit), if the contact Id is not given then two scenarios are possible: The ‘Contact ID’ is not shared and can be asked by ticket requestor again. The Contact doesn’t exist in salesforce, in which case we need to ask Ticket Requestor to share details for Contact to be created. CRM Key User or Larisa will create a ticket with the request to enable the contacts as Partner Portal Users with role “Partner Executive” (sees all data below in the hierarchy) or with the role “Partner User” (will only see their own data). If Account is not enabled for portal access, it is needed first to enable the Account of the contact with the record type “Distributor/Agent” as Partner Portal. Then enable the contacts for portal access and assign needed permission sets. Step 1 Check if user exists (we need to check both active and inactive users). If the user doesn’t exist, check if the account is enabled as Partner by clicking on the “Manage External Account” on Account and enable it by clicking on ‘Enable As Partner’. You will be directed to the User creation page. Provide the License – Gold Partner. Profile as KONE: Gold Partner User. The SAP instance field should be left blank. For KTOC users, the Sales office and KTOC USER check box and KTOC Manager should be populated. From the contact’s country populate the Country field and user country picklist on the user detail page. Provide the other details in the user page like Name, Alias, Email, Username, Nickname, user language, user country, Time zone, Locale, Language, currency and Save. Note: Please uncheck the Generate new password and notify user Immediately before saving. The saved user will have a contact field. From here you can switch to the contact page. Step 2 Assign the permission set KONE PRM Distributor Community Access, do the following a. Assign: permission set KONE PRM Distributor Community Access. b. Assign also the permission set: PRM Community MP record type Field Access. c. Ensure that the check box “Send welcome email” is NOT selected, before actual members are added to the community Add the role for this particular partner account to the relevant PRM: __ All Distributor Public group (i.e. PRM: KDI All Distributors). If not mentioned in the ticket, ask the CRM user submitting the case, which of the groups below should the new user be added to. This is for the reports folder visibility. There are 5 distributer groups – PRM: KDI All Distributors PRM: KSM All Distributors PRM: RUS All Distributors PRM: KUA All Distributors PRM: THA All Distributors Step 3 If we don’t have an existing sharing rule for the Role, we need to create new rule for the Role. Creation of sharing rule requires approval from Larisa Vallee . Create a sharing rule to enable read/write on Accounts, Contacts, Opportunity for Owner in Portal Role and Subordinates: [Partner Portal Account Name Role] to share with Owner in Portal Role and Subordinates: [Partner Portal Account Name Role] Step 4 Once user is created and permission sets are assigned, ASKIT support team should inform the CRM key user about the user creation and provide the link to the newly created user profile. Step 5 CRM Key user will reset the password to the new Community user and the user will receive an email informing him about the new password to access to KONE PRM Distributor Community.

short_description: Access Request Submission in SAP GRC - Chinese
introduction: a) 使用Tcode提交权限申请 (How to raise a Access request using Transaction code(s)) b) 使用role访问请求界面。(How to raise a Access request using Role(s)) c) 使用克隆 ID 访问请求界面。(How to raise a Access request using Clone ID)
instructions: 重要说明： 在提出/创建访问请求之前，请先遵循以下链接中的说明/验证。 KB000A7350 https://koneprod.service-now.com/now/nav/ui/classic/params/target/kb_view.do%3Fsys_kb_id%3Ddc909e93c3ed46505d2900e9d0013149%26sysparm_rank%3D7%26sysparm_tsqueryId%3Dbc1f53b947278290041f8f33036d4301 注意：以下任何系统的访问请求创建都是与SAP ERP环境/系统相关的访问。 1> Z04 ERP Production 2> Z03 ERP Pre-Prod 3> Z02 ERP Acceptance 4> Z01 ERP Development SAP GRC 访问请求的流程： 使用 GRC 访问请求的流程如下所示。 请遵循以下流程，根据Tcode/Role在SAP系统中提出新用户/现有用户访问配置的GRC请求。 1. 從 SAP GUI 登入板開啟 GRP Production (SSO) 2. 執行 ZFORM t 程式碼 a） 使用 Tcode提交权限申请 登录后，将打开如下所示的权限申请界面。 带*的文本框表示必填字段。 1.输入通力ID，然后按Enter键自动填充个人信息和经理ID。 2.访问结束日期是指有效截止日期。默认设置为31.12.9999 3.在Landscape列表中有四个选项可供选择。 选择Landscape为SAP ERP，以提供对SAP Z01/Z02/Z03/Z04系统的访问。 选择要提出访问请求的系统。您可以根据用户请求选择单个系统以及多个系统。 4.不要输入任何克隆ID，因为请求基于Tcode/role（注意：用户输入是Tcode/role） 5. Work Region/Unit 必须要填，并且要小心。请注意，错误选择将导致请求被拒绝或批准的重大延迟 6. GRC系统连接到LDAP/Active Directory，因此在填写KONE ID并“回车”后，将自动填充Manager ID。 在填写GRC访问请求表时，中国security team必须为中国前线用户手动将GRC表中的“ Manager ID ”替换为相应的“ change manager's ID ”。 如果根据模板，用户属于 “ 中国FL（CHN）-NEB”或“中国FL（CHN）-FICO”，则填写的模板必须由Change Manager审核和批准：CHNAMPEN（Amy Jia Peng） 如果用户根据模板属于中国FL（CHN）-SEB，则填写的模板必须由Change Manager 审查和批准：CHNJUZHU（Julia Zhu Li Min） 7.仅当用户在基于克隆ID提出请求时，请求删除现有访问权限，才选择“删除现有roles/access”（即，此选项仅适用于ID已存在于相应SAP系统中的用户）。 8. 請提供請求存取的適當原因。 從下拉清單中選擇適當的請求原因，並在「請求理由」中填寫使用者提供的相關「存取原因」詳細資訊。 9.在“Transaction Code”字段中输入Tcode，如果需要访问多个Tcode，则输入仅以逗号（，）作为分隔符。（注意：不要在逗号（，）分隔符之后或之前添加空格） 10.输入Tcode后，单击“Search Available Roles Based on T-code(S)”按钮，获取与访问选择的Work Region/ Unit和系统相关的所需Tcode的roles。 输入Tcode后，单击“Search Available Roles Based on T-code(s)”按钮，查看所选系统中所需Tcode的role。 选中system旁边的复选框后，单击“Add Selected Roles”按钮将所有role移动到“Selected Roles”部分。 Role把需要的role移动到selected roles后，单击Submit Access Request按钮。 请按照上述顺序填写所有字段，下图为单个Tcode申请填写的界面。 下面是输入了多个Tcode的界面 。 注意：如果选择了错误的Work Region/ Unit，则关闭弹出窗口并更正Work Region/ Unit，然后重复前面界面中的所有步骤。 提交请求后，GRC号将显示在屏幕顶部，可以记录下来，以便与用户沟通以供参考，并跟踪请求状态 。 b ） 使用role访问请求界面。 选择Landscape为SAP ERP时，将出现下面的界面，其中包含Landscape的相关选项。 请按照上述顺序填写所有字段，如下图所示。 单击“Find Role to Transaction Code Mapping”后，将显示一个弹出窗口，如下图所示。 在“Role”一栏中，输入用户请求的role，然后单击“Search”按钮，在“Role – Transaction Code Details”中查看role。 显示role后，选择System旁边的勾选框以选择role，然后单击“确定”按钮。 然后单击“Submit Access Request”按钮以提交访问请求。 单击下面的“Submit Access Request”按钮后，将显示弹出窗口，以查看所选的Work Region/ Unit。将用户提供的申请权限模板和表单中选定的Work Region/ Unit 进行对比检查 ，然后单击“submit”按钮。 注意 ：如果选择了错误的Work Region/ Unit，则关闭弹出窗口并更正Work Region/ Unit，然后单击界面底部的“Submit Access Request”按钮。 提交请求后，GRC号将显示在屏幕顶部，可以记录下来，以便与用户沟通以供参考，并跟踪请求状态 提交请求后，用户将收到一封包含请求详细信息的自动通知电子邮件。 下面是向用户发送此类通知电子邮件的示例。 c ） 使用克隆ID访问请求界面。 登录后，将打开如下所示的访问界面。 带*的文本框表示必填字段。 1.访问结束日期是指有效截止日期。默认设置为31.12.9999 2.在Landscape列表中有四个选项可供选择。 选择Landscape为SAP ERP，以提供对SAP Z01/Z02/Z03/Z04系统的访问。 选择要提出访问请求的系统。您可以根据用户请求选择单个系统以及多个系统。 3.可以根据克隆ID或T code 提出访问请求。（注意-本KBA解释了基于克隆ID的创建权限申请） 克隆ID是申请权限的用户需要权限的参考用户ID。 4. Work Region/Unit 必须要填，并且要小心。请注意，错误选择将导致请求被拒绝或批准的重大延迟 5.GRC系统连接到LDAP/Active Directory，因此在填写KONE ID并“回车”后，将自动填充Manager ID。 在填写GRC访问请求表时，中国security team必须为中国前线用户手动将GRC表中的“ Manager ID ”替换为相应的“ change manager's ID ”。 如果根据模板，用户属于 “ 中国FL（CHN）-NEB”或“中国FL（CHN）-FICO”，则填写的模板必须由Change Manager审核和批准：CHNAMPEN（Amy Jia Peng） 如果用户根据模板属于中国FL（CHN）-SEB，则填写的模板必须由Change Manager 审查和批准：CHNJUZHU（Julia Zhu Li Min） 6.仅当用户在基于克隆ID提出请求时，请求删除现有访问权限，才选择“删除现有roles/access”（即，此选项仅适用于ID已存在于相应SAP系统中的用户）。 7. 請提供請求存取的適當原因。 從下拉清單中選擇適當的請求原因，並在「請求理由」中填寫使用者提供的相關「存取原因」詳細資訊。 选择 Landscape 作为SAP ERP时，将出现下面的界面，其中包含所选 Landscape 的相关选项。 请按照下面屏幕所示的顺序填写上述所有字段。 输入用户提供的所有数据后，单击提交访问请求按钮。 點擊下面的「提交存取請求」按鈕後，將顯示彈出視窗以查看所選的工作區域/單位。將表單中選定的工作區域/單位與使用者共用的存取請求範本表單進行交叉檢查，然後按一下「提交」按鈕。 注意：如果選擇了錯誤的工作區域/單位，請關閉彈出視窗並修正工作區域/單位，然後重複前面畫面中說明的所有步驟。 提交请求后，GRC号将显示在屏幕顶部，可以记录下来，以便与用户沟通以供参考，并跟踪请求状态。 提交请求后，用户将收到一封包含请求详细信息的自动通知电子邮件。 下面是向用户发送此类通知电子邮件的示例。

short_description: Access Request Approval by Line Manager in GRC - Chinese
introduction: 1. Line Manager如何审批GRC权限申请? (How line manager approves GRC permission requests?) 2. How 在离开/休假期间委派后援（代理）? (How to d elegate responsibility to backup during absence / vacation / leave )?
instructions: GRC 系统的概念简介与 流程概览 作为通力服务改进计划的一部分，我们正在推出 SAP GRC 权限请求管理（ ARM ）功能。 SAP GRC 用于在 SAP 系统后台给用户分配角色和配置文件 权限请求管理（ ARM ）提供工作流引擎来驱动 SAP 环境中的用户角色分配过程。这些过程具有可审计，可验证的优点。审批流程， SoD 流程清晰，可配置 。 申请者填写权限申请表来发起申请。在提交申请时，基于申请中的具体选项触发相应的工作流。 在每个批准点 ( 阶段 ) ，审批人收到权限申请，他 / 她可以分析 / 运行风险分析，根据风险分析的结果，他 / 她可以选择批准 / 拒绝 / 暂扣权限申请。 在所有审批完成，系统会自动给用户分配角色和配置文件。 1. Line Manager 如何 审批 GRC 权限申请 工作流 直接回复邮件以批准 GRC 权限申请。 一旦最终用户提交了权限申请，系统会自动触发电子邮件给不同的审批人，以便审批人可以直接通过回复邮件的方式批准 / 拒 绝 GRC SAP 权限申请。详情如下所示： 邮件发件人显示为 “ User Workflow sapgrp@grp.kone.com ” • 如果要批准 SAP 权限申请，则审批人需要点击“批准”链接，如下所示。 • 从电子邮件中点击“批准 ”，审批人将 看到下面的自动生成的电子邮件。审批人只需点击“发送” 按钮， SAP 权限申请就会在 GRC 系统中得到批准。 注意： 请不要改变邮件主题。此邮件主题用于识别 SAP GRC 系统中的请求号以进行适当的操作 。 • 审批人也可以通过答复电子邮件的方式来批准此 SAP 权限申请：回复 “Approve”并 点击 “发送” 。如下图所示： 注意： 回复时，除了 ”Approve”, 请不要输入其他任何内容 , 否则系统无法识别。 直接回复邮件以拒绝 GRC 权限申请 • 一旦最终用户提交了权限申请，就会触发系统自动向不同的审批人发送电子邮件，以便审批人可以通过直接回复邮件的方式批准 / 拒绝 GRC SAP 权限申请。详情如下所示： • 邮件发件人显示为 “ User Workflow sapgrp@grp.kone.com ” • 审批人也可以通过答复电子邮件的方式来拒绝此SAP权限申请：点击“拒绝”并点击“发送” 。如下图所示： 在电子邮件中点击“拒绝 ”后，审批人将收到一封系统自动生成的邮 件。审批者只需点击“发送 ”， GRC 系统后台就会拒绝用户的 SAP 权限申请。 注意： 请不要改变邮件主题。此邮件主题用于识别 SAP GRC 系统中的请求号以进行适当的操作。 通过回复电子邮件拒绝 GRC 中的 SAP 权限申请 • 审批人还可以通过答复电子邮件的方式拒绝该 SAP 权限申请：回复“ Reject”并 单击“发送” 。 注意 ：回复时，除了 ”Reject”, 请不要输入其他任何内容 , 否则系统无法识别。 2. How 在缺席 / 请假 / 休假期间委派负责备份 ? • 若要在 SAP GRC 系统中设置委托代理，请参考下面的知识分享文档（ KBA ）。文档中包含步骤说明，包括屏幕截图。 KB0010885: https://koneprod.service-now.com/now/nav/ui/classic/params/target/kb_view.do%3Fsysparm_article%3DKB0010885 KBAs 其它的相关主题 • 如果操作錯誤導致GRC申請無法通過郵件審核，您可以登入GRC工作箱進行審核。請依照以下說明使用工作收件匣批准/拒絕存取請求。

short_description: Access Request Approval by Role Owner in GRC - Chinese
introduction: 1. 如何 Role Owner 审批GRC权限申请 ? ( How role owner approves permissions request in GRC? ) 2. How role Owner拒绝GRC权限申请 ? 3. How Role Owner 批准部分GRC权限申请 ?
instructions: 1 . 如何 Role Owner 审批 GRC 权限申请 ? 工作流 Role Owner 批准权限 • 在直线经理批准权限申请后，系统会向 Role Owner 发送一封关于权限详细信息的邮件 • Role Owner 可以通过邮件直接批准 / 拒绝申请 • 角色所有者还可以按照以下屏幕中突出显示的步骤从 GRC 工作收件箱查看 GRC 请求详细信息。 登陆后 Role Owner 将会进入到 GRC 系统查看待审批申请 打开任意一个申请， Role Owner 将看到申请详细信息 点击“ Risk Violations ”标签，查看该权限的风险分析 • 点击“ Access Risk ID ” 可查看风险描述 • Ro le Owner 可以通过“ Format ”下拉菜单调整报表的显示格式 • Role Owner 也可以通过“ Type ”下拉单修改报表格式，比如“ Action Level ”（查看 t-code ），“ Permission Level ”（查看 Object ） 查看风险分析之后， Role Owner 需要点击“ User Access ” 去批准此申请 点击 “ Submit ”后，申请会被全部审批 Role Owner 在点击“ Submit ”之前，需要先点击“ Comments ”标签填写审批说明 最后点击“ Submit ”按钮批准申请， Role Owner 阶段的审批到此结束。 2. How ro le Owner 拒绝 GRC 权限申请 ? • 若需要拒绝此申请，需点击“ Other Actions ”按钮然后点击“ Reject ” • 在点击“ Reject ”之前同样需要到“ Comments ”标签去填写拒绝说明 • “ Comments ”是必填项，若不填写则无法拒绝此申请 3. How role Owner 批准部分权限 ? • Role Owner 可以在每个权限的第一列点击 Approve/Reject • 点击“ Submit ”之前， Role Owner 仍需要到“ Comments ”标签填写说明 • “ Comments ”是必填项，若不填写则无法“ Reject ” • 在提交批准后，此申请流程将进入下一阶段， Role Owner 在 GRC 系统的列表中将不在显示此申请 • 在提交全部拒绝后，此申请即结束， Role Owner 在 GRC 系统的列表中将不在显示此申请

short_description: Access Request Approval by SoD Approver in GRC - Chinese
introduction: 1. SoD 审批者如何审批GRC权限 ? (How SoD approvers Approve GRC permissions?) 2. How 拒绝有 SoD 风险的权限申请 ? (How to reject access request with SoD violations?) 3. How 部分批准有 SoD 风险的权限 ? (How to partially approve access request with SoD risks?) 4. How SoD 审批人管控风险? (How SoD approver assigns mitigation controls while approving access request? )
instructions: 1. SoD 审批者如何审批 GRC 权限 ? 工作流 批准有 SoD 风险的权限 • 在直线经理批准权限申请后，系统会向 SoD 审批人发送一封关于权限详细信息的邮件 • 邮件包含存在可能风险的权限及信息 • SoD 審核者需要按照電子郵件通知中提到的說明開啟來自 work inox 的 GRC 請求 打開工作收件匣後，點擊相應的 GRC 請求連結以查看 GRC 請求。 审查风险分析 • 如下图所示，点击 Risk Violation s 标签查看风险分析的结果 • 点击 Access Risk ID 会打开一个弹出式窗口显示风险描述 • SoD 审批人可以更改报表的显示格式 • SoD 审批人也可根据想看的内容变更报告，如 Action level （关于 T-code 详情）， Permission level （关于 Object 详情) 审查了风险分析后 , SoD 审批人需要点击 User Access 标签来批准此申请 批准申请 2. How 拒 绝有 SoD 风险的权限申 请 ? • 审查后，如果 SoD 审批人需要拒绝所有的权限申请，需要点击“ Comments ”按钮来填写拒绝原因（此为必填项） • 然后可以点击 “Other Actions” 按钮，最后点击“ Reject ”选项 3. How 部分批准有 SoD 风险的权限 ? • 若需要批准部分权限，需要在对应角色来选择批准 / 拒绝，然后点击“ Submit ” • 在点击“ Submit ”提交之前， SoD 审批人需要点击“ Comments ”填写批准 / 拒绝相关的注释（此步骤为强制性） • 在批准 / 部分批准此申请后，该申请将会在 SoD 审批人的 GRC 工作列表中消失 • 在拒绝此申请后，该申请将变为完成状态，并在 SoD 审批人的 GRC 工作列表中消失 4. How SoD 审批人管控风险 ? SoD 审批人在批准 SAP 权限之前，需要将 Risk ID 分配给用户 将 Mitigation Control ID分配给用户 为风险 ID 分配缓解控制 审批结束 KBA 的文章链接 KBA 1225 关于如何让 SOD 审批人利用 GRC 批准 SAP 访问请求 ： https://koneprod.service-now.com/now/nav/ui/classic/params/target/kb_view.do%3Fsys_kb_id%3Db5c5de17c32186505d2900e9d0013157

short_description: KONE FSM Field Service Critical Service Notes insertion using Data Loader
introduction: How To insert Critical Service Note for any new Service territory from KONECT to FSM application using Data Loader?
instructions: Pre-Requisite: Data Loader should be setup on the desktop of the AMS support resource. AMS support resource should have the admin access of the Business Org. Business should provide the data in pre-defined format. Steps: 1) Business will provide the Field Service Note file in the attached file format. (File Name: Critical FieldServiceNotes File). 2) The file will have values of either of the tree columns i.e. Account__r.Customer_SAP_Number__c (Account), Asset__r.Equipment_SAP_Number__c (Asset), FSM_Location__r.Functional_Location_SAP_Number__c (Location We need to extract the salesforce Id of the records of either asset, Account or Location which ever value is provided in the sheet. 3) Using Lookup function, replace the account, asset and Location values with their respective salesforce id and also replace the date type field value with the proper date format value. Prepare the .csv (comma separated file) of the Field Service Notes to insert into the system. Pls see the attachment. (File Name: CriticalNoteUploadFile) 4) Open the data loader and connect it with the business org. 5) Select the ‘Field Service Note’ object in the salesforce object list and select the .csv file by browsing the file available on the local machine. 6) Click on Next and select the option of ‘Auto-Match Fields to Columns’ to match the file columns with the system fields and if there are still some columns left for matching then match them manually. 7) In the next move, selection the location where the success and failure log file should get generated and saved. 8) Click next and complete the Field Service note insertion process. 9) Now check the failure log file to see if there are any failed records and correct these failed records according to the failure reason and try to insert them by following the same above 6 steps.

short_description: KONE FSM: Time Entry Code insertion using Data Loader
introduction: How To insert Time Entry Code in FSM application using Data Loader?
instructions: Pre-Requisite: Data Loader should be setup on the desktop of the AMS support resource. AMS support resource should have the admin access of the Business Org. Business should provide the data in pre-defined format. Steps: 1) Business will provide the Time Entry Code file in the below format or we should take the records for the said Sales org and language from the specified sandbox in the below format.While extracting records from sandbox keep this extra query field PARENT__R.EXTERNAL_ID__C” so that we can do Vlook up for the field values later. (File Name: TimeEntryCodeFormat). 2) The received or extracted file could contain Menu Items and Non Menu Item related time entry codes. So we have to divide the file in to two parts. One containing Menu Items and other one containing non menu Items. a) Keep filters in excel for Menu items = true and select all the records displayed and put in other file and name the file as 310a Time Entry Code (just a naming convention for easy reference). Please see the attached file format (File Name: 310a TEC Template) b) Now keep the filter in excel for Menu items = false and select all the records displayed and put in other file and name the file as 310b Time Entry Code. Please see the attached file format (File Name: 310b TEC Upload Template) 3) Now as we are ready with the files we can start with upload. But please take care of below. Few menu items will contain PARENT__R.EXTERNAL_ID__C populated, so after our first upload make sure we do Vlook up to populate PARENT__C field with the record Ids for respective PARENT__R.EXTERNAL_ID__C value. Every record which has PARENT__R.EXTERNAL_ID__C Column populated should have PARENT__C column also. 4) Open the data loader and connect it with the business org. 5) Select the “Time Entry Code” object in the salesforce object list and select the310a Time Entry Code.csv file by browsing the file available on the local machine. 6) Click on Next and select the option of ‘Auto-Match Fields to Columns’ to match the file columns with the system fields and if there are still some columns left for matching then match them manually. 7) In the next move, selection the location where the success and failure log file should get generated and saved.10) After upserting the records in these 3 objects, we need to upsert the translations in FSM_Service_Need_Code_Translation__c (for FSM_Service_Need_code__c) and FSM_Service_Need_Action_Code_Translation__c (for FSM_Service_Need_Action_Code__c) objects. 8) Click next and complete the first insertion process for Menu Item Time Entry Codes. 9) As this is our first upload if any of our records has parent record that are inserted with this upload then we should now do a Vlook up to update the record ids in Parent__C column. Now Since our success file is handy with record ids, user the same file and perform the above vlookup. Use the same file for our second upload. Remove all the extra columns and just keep only the columns as per the attachment here. (File Name: 310a TEC Second Upload Template) Though PARENT__R.EXTERNAL_ID__C column is there, we don’t need to map it during upload. 10) Repeat the same process as said above but just map id and Parent__C fields.With this all Menu Items will be uploaded successfully. 11) Now we need to upload Non menu Items. For Nnon menu Items mostly we will get Parent__C id form our previous upload. So do Vlookup on PARENT__R.EXTERNAL_ID__C and have all the PARENT__C ids updated.Follow the above steps (on the same object) and perform the insertion operation. Map almost all the columns possible including PARENT__C. 12) Now check the failure log file to see if there are any failed records and correct these failed records according to the failure reason and try to insert them by following the same steps above.
