short_description: How to configure Repetitive failure emails in Konect?
introduction: How to configure Repetitive failure emails in Konect?
instructions: What is a Repetitive Failure? An Equipment with 1 or more call-outs in a 1 - 31 day period (configured at Planner Group level), elevators and escalators. Only Visit Type Codes Y01 or Y02, plus Callouts in Status ‘Finished’ are included in the calculation, in either the Open Callout Table (KM01), or Closed Callout Table (KM01H). Dummy equipment are not included! How to configure Repetitive email sending? NOTE! Only Local Administrators have permissions to change the set up, although most users can view. From KM97-Welcome screen, click Organization and select the Planner Group (Branch) to be configured. Then click ‘View Planner Group’, this will open the configuration dialogue box. Repetitive Failure details can be created and modified from this screen. Notes: All Repetitive Failure periods are default to 15 days, but can be configured between 1 – 31 days. The Repetitive Failure period for Message, or Email must be less than the Main value. Up to 3 levels of Email notification can be set, only the Level 1 escalation is compulsory. For local language message, the message must be translated in the form KONE Model Messages, message ID ‘KM01-090’.

short_description: GRC - Access Requests - Report
introduction: 1. How to pull a report on access requests submitted in GRC?
instructions: 1. How to pull a report on access requests submitted in GRC? Introduction: This KB article explains step by step procedure to pull a report on access requests submitted / created by requestor(s) in a given time period along with status of requests in SAP GRC. Step 1> Log in to SAP GRC production system and then execute transaction code "NWBC" Step 2> Upon executing NWBC t-code, system will open a NWBC (Netweaver Business Client) portal screen in browser. Then navigate to Work Center "Access Management" --> "Search Requests" under Access Request Administration Work Set. Step 3> Search request screen opens in a new browser session and select the filter criteria as below and then click on "Search" button. Select "Creation Date" filter from drop down, "Is between" in second column and then enter the "From Date" and "To Date" as per required period. Select "Created by User ID" as second filter, "IS" in second column and then enter the valid user ID (i.e. user ID of requestor who submitted access requests in GRC) in 3 rd. column of filter. If the report to be pulled for all requests submitted by different requestors, then add as many "Created by User ID" filters as required based on number of requestors by clicking on + (Plus) button at the end of the filter. Then enter different requestor's user ID in each filter. Step 4> The results of search criteria will be displayed in bottom pane of the browser screen with all "access requests" with different statuses (i.e. Approved, Rejected, Cancelled, Finished, Running, Aborted etc. as applicable) which are created / submitted during the time period selected in filter criteria. Step 5> The result of the report can be exported to a excel sheet following below two steps. Click on "Select All" option at the top left corner of "Results" (bottom) pane. Then click on Export --> Export to Microsoft Excel option. Step 6> Then Save / Download the report by clicking on "Save" or "Open" appears at bottom of browser after clicking on "Export to Microsoft Excel" option. Downloaded Excel Report Screen appears as below: Step 7> If this report has to be run periodically / frequently, then the filters selection criteria can be saved as variant. Enter a variant name (Ex. "Access Requests") then click on Save. When report has to be run from second time, by selecting the variant from "Saved Variants" option, all filter selection criteria will be loaded which is saved in variant. Then review and change the inputs for filters as required each time when report has to be run.

short_description: Ask IT - How to access Active Directory
introduction: how to AD
instructions: Active Directory/ Groups Active Directory (AD) is a Microsoft technology used to manage computers and other devices on a network . It is a primary feature of Windows Server, an operating system that runs both local and Internet-based servers . Active Directory allows network administrators to create and manage domains , users, and objects within a network. For example, an admin can create a group of users and give them specific access privileges to certain directories on the server. As a network grows, Active Directory provides a way to organize a large number of users into logical groups and subgroups, while providing access control at each level. The Active Directory structure includes three main tiers: 1) domains, 2) trees, and 3) forests. Several objects (users or devices) that all use the same database may be grouped in to a single domain . Multiple domains can be combined into a single group called a tree . Multiple trees may be grouped into a collection called a forest . Each one of these levels can be assigned specific access rights and communication privileges. Active Directory provides several different services, which fall under the umbrella of "Active Directory Domain Services," or AD DS. These services include: 1. Domain Services – stores centralized data and manages communication between users and domains; includes login authentication and search functionality 2. Certificate Services – creates, distributes, and manages secure certificates 3. Lightweight Directory Services – supports directory-enabled applications using the open ( LDAP ) protocol 4. Directory Federation Services – provides single-sign-on (SSO) to authenticate a user in multiple web applications in a single session 5. Rights Management – protects copyrighted information by preventing unauthorized use and distribution of digital content AD DS is included with Windows Server (including Windows Server 10) and is designed to manage client systems. While systems running the regular version of Windows do not have the administrative features of AD DS, they do support Active Directory. This means any Windows computer can connect to a Windows workgroup, provided the user has the correct login credentials. In AD we usually check for: - user detail information contacts (phone mail) - If account is active - position and user’s manager name - certificates issued - what group is user member of -reset password / unlock account -etc. In case advance support is needed or you found you don’t have enough privileges to add groups or do requested changes. Please assign ticket to dedicated team. ****************************************************************************************** We can access via Jump servers : 10.41.148.32 or 10.41.148.42 Then go to: Double click to open: From here we can search for objects in AD. Right click on KONENET.COM Click on Find… From there we can check users , computers , groups , etc. If your case is related to user you will reach to this panel with options you can chose from upper section of this panel:

short_description: Add/remove subcontractors in IRMA (Installation Resource Management)
introduction: How to add/remove subcontractors in IRMA application
instructions: Adding/removing subcontractors in IRMA Adding/removing subcontractors in IRMA is done in two steps: 1.. Add/remove the subcontractor in IRMA Vendor list in SAP 2.. Push SAP Subcontractor Information to IRMA 1. ADD/REMOVE SUBCONTRACTORS IN IRMA VENDOR LIST IN SAP Login to SAP Console. Run transaction ZC_SUBC_LIST, and you will see the following list: To add subcontractor click “New Entries”, and add the company code(s) and Vendor ID(s) for the new entries. Click “Save” to confirm the changes. To remove a subcontractor, search for the correct subcontractor using “Position” Type in the Vendor ID and click the green check mark. Select the correct row, right-click on your mouse and select “Delete” Click save button (Highlighted in red) to save the added subcontractor to the list. Important! There are some merged FLs, which are handled as one Working Unit in IRMA, but have multiple Country Codes in SAP, e.g. Working Unit “Finland & Baltics” includes Finland (CoCd 217), Estonia(CoCd 268), Latvia (CoCd 269)and Lithuania (CoCd 270). The company code to be used is checked from the mapping table. To go to mapping table follow following steps 1. Go to SM30 2. Use transaction ZPS_IRMA_CCODE in the highlighted region and press display underlined in red in the image above. Now check in the table what is the Dataset the FL is mapped to. In our example 268 (Estonia), 269 (Latvia) and 270 (Lithuania) company codes are mapped to 217 (Finland). So we use 217 as the company code if we have to add subcotractors for 268, 269 and 270. 2. PUSH SAP SUBCONTRACTOR INFORMATION TO IRMA 1. We need to create IDOC for information to be pushed to IRMA. 2. To create an IDOC use transaction ZV_IMT_QUINTIQ_SUBC 3. We only need to add company code i.e the SAP company code. (For the merged working unit use the main dataset company code for example in the above case it is 217) 4. Click execute button . Once it's done, you will see message "1 idoc transmitted will come as msg at the bottom of the page. 5. When adding new subcontractors, you can check in IRMA Workforce Planner if you can see the subcontractor . The subcontractors can be find in “Team assignments” view, which you can access via the “Views” option in the top menu (note: the first time you have to select “Manage views” to add the views you normally use into the drop-down list, see e.g. IRMA quick guide for this). In the “Team assignments” view, the employee list is in the “Subcontractor crews” view. You can sort based on the ID column (or search in the ID column) to find the subcontractor you are looking for. 6. In case you have removed subcontractors, your changes will not be immediately visible, the subcontractors will only be “soft-deleted”. The actual deletion is done by the Monthly daemon run (this is a Quintiq term, in reality the daemon is scheduled to run weekly). The deletion will take place for all soft-deleted objects which in addition to being soft-deleted also have the “Can be deleted” attribute set to true. The criteria for “Can be deleted” is that there are no activity assignments connected to the subcontractor crews of that vendor. The Monthly daemon is configured separately for each working unit, you can check when it is running in the Workforce Planner application by choosing from the top menu “Edit” -> “Configure daemons”. In the configuration of “Monthly daemon”, you can see when it is scheduled to run. In the example below, taken from production and Finland dataset, the monthly daemon will run every week, each week two hours later than the previous week, as the frequency has been set to 7 days, 2 hours. The start time is 1 st March 2016, which was a Tuesday, but due to the fact that the daemon is not running at the same time each week, it’s very difficult to determine which day it will be running on now. For working units/datasets where the daemon runs at the same time each week, the day will also remain unchanged. It is also possible to run the daemon manually by pressing “Execute now”. This will not affect the time of the next scheduled daemon execution, but normally we don’t run it manually.

short_description: SFDC : Insert, Update, or Delete Data Using Salesforce Data Loader.
introduction: How to Insert, Update, or Delete Data in any object in Salesforce Using Data Loader.
instructions: Install Dataloader by following the steps in the below link : https://help.salesforce.com/articleView?id=000239784&language=en_US&type=1 After installation Verify/configure below settings in the Dataloader tool before you start: Use the Data Loader wizards to add, modify, or delete records. The upsert wizard combines inserting and updating a record. If a record in your file matches an existing record, the existing record is updated with the values in your file. If no match is found, a new record is created. When you hard-delete records, the deleted records are not stored in the Recycle Bin and are eligible for deletion. For more information, see Configure Data Loader. User Permissions Needed To insert records: Create on the record To update records: Edit on the record To upsert records: Create or Edit on the record To delete records: Delete on the record To hard delete records: Delete on the record To mass delete records: Modify All Data Open the Data Loader. Click Insert , Update , Upsert , Delete , or Hard Delete . These commands are also listed in the File menu. Enter your KONE IT/Business Org's username and password. To log in, click Log in . When you are logged in, click Next . (Until you log out or close the program, you are not asked to log in again.) Choose an object. For example, if you are inserting Account records, select Account . If your object name does not display in the default list, select Show all objects to see a complete list of the objects that you can access. The objects are listed by localized label name, with the developer name noted in parentheses. To select your CSV file, click Browse . For example, if you are inserting Account records, you could specify a CSV file called insertaccounts.csv containing a Name column for the names of the new accounts. Click Next . After the object and CSV file are initialized, click OK . From the dropdown list, select which field to use for matching. Click Next to continue. Define how the columns in your CSV file map to Salesforce fields. Click Next . For each operation, the Data Loader generates two unique CSV log files. One file name starts with “success,” and the other starts with “error.” Click Browse to specify a directory for these files. To complete the operation, click Finish , and then click Yes to confirm. As the operation proceeds, a progress information window reports the status of the data movement. To view your success or error files, click View Successes or View Errors . To close the wizard, click OK . For more information, see Review Data Loader Output Files . Key Points to remember before starting the data load. Record backup (through SFDC extraction) before any data operation except Insert. This will help in re-instating old data if required Record backup to be saved in central repository could be remedy itself. Provides access of backup data when needed Success and Failure Files as we do currently for all data operations. Again will help in understanding the changes made successfully

short_description: ECM - How to check monitoring alerts from Microsoft Azure
introduction: How to take actions when you receive a CPU alert/ Disk space alert/ Memory alert from Microsoft Azure?
instructions: 1. Actions to be taken when we receive CPU alert Step 1: RDP to VM the CPU alert was created for. Open Task manager in server and again Resource Monitor: From Resource Monitor you should be able to detect the processes consuming most of the CPU time. It’s okay if for example M-Files processes consume much CPU time. If we get these alerts often then, depending on the process consuming CPU time, we may consider upgrading the corresponding VM to have more computing capacity. All in all – it’s ok for the VM to consume CPU time even 100%, temporarily. Step 2: Also, we need to monitor the CPU utilization in Azure portal. For this, login Azure portal --> Click on All Resources --> Search for APP 41 in search box(shown in below image). Choose EUAZ1MSECMAPP41 (Virtual machine). In the Monitoring, select Metrics --> Enter the Metric Namespace as Virtual Machine Host, Metric as Percentage CPU and choose the Aggregation as Min/Max. (Also we can select the time range). 2. Actions to be taken when we receive Memory alert Step 1: RDP to VM the memory (RAM consumption) alert was created for. Open Task manager and again Resource Monitor: From Resource Monitor you should be able to detect the processes consuming most of the RAM. It’s okay if for example M-Files processes consume much RAM. If we get these alerts often then, depending on the process consuming CPU time, we may consider upgrading the corresponding VM to have more RAM. All in all – it’s ok for the VM to consume much RAM, even 100%, temporarily, providing the process consuming it does not crash. Step 2: Also, we need to monitor the memory usage in Azure portal. For this, login Azure portal --> Click on All Resources --> Search for APP 41 in search box(shown in below image). Choose EUAZ1MSECMAPP41 (Virtual machine). In the Monitoring, from the metrics, we can be able to monitor the memory usage. We can check for the memory in use by changing the Metric Namespace as Guest(classic), Metric as \Memory\% Committed Bytes in Use and choose the Aggregation as Min/Max. (Also we can select the time range). 3. Actions to be taken when we receive Disk space alert RDP to VM the disk alert was created for and check in File Explorer what’s the status of the VM OS and data disk: Check disk space on drives C (OS), E or F (stores all files stored in Vault) and M (virtual drive for M-Files desktop use). OS disk can run out of space e.g. when the log files (IIS) grow too many and big. They may need cleaning. In case disk space is running out contact ranjith.m@kone.com to take action. He can request for more disk space through Azure portal. In any case do the below actions. C Drive: Web and application servers Select the Start button, type disk cleanup , then select Disk Cleanup from the list of results. Select the check box next to the type of files you want to delete. (To view a description of each type of file, select the name.) By default, Downloaded Program Files , Temporary Internet Files , and Thumbnails are selected. Select the below types of files and hit “OK” to start. If C:\FTP is defined delete all unnecessary files there. Open File Explorer and scan C-drive for big files, see sample below. Delete any unnecessary large files, like old versions of installers. Do not delete any files without knowing what it is to not compromise the system integrity! Web server IIS log file folders W3SVC1, W3SVC2 and so on at C:\inetpub\logs\LogFiles. Delete all files older than 1 month. E or F-drive and M-drive Only on application servers. Do not clean up these drives. Clean-up may cause unpredictable symptoms for application integrity or data may be lost. How to scan the files in a folder? To scan the files inside the folder, we need to run the windirstat file which is available in the location F:\BACKUP. When we run the windirstat file it will ask to choose the folder, once the folder is chosen it will scan all the files inside it.

short_description: ECM - How to start troubleshooting when the application is down
introduction: How to start troubleshooting when the application is down?
instructions: When you receive notifications from HA team that login is failing for all 4 sites please start troubleshooting following these steps. We need to create a ticket to Infosys and for M-Files (support@m-files.com) to start the investigation. If the issue cannot be figured out or resolved in 1 hour, inform Operations Manager (muraliganesh.d@kone.com) to raise Major incident. As a support team, we can check the following: Step 1: Check if the M-files desktop client and web client are accessible. If not, go to step 2. Step 2: Check if application server and proxy server are connected via PAM. Once logged in to the server, open the M-Files Admin, try to extract the document vaults, if works fine without any issues then Ok. If it throws error or connection is not working, then some issues are there. Now go to step 3. Step 3: To verify the server running status on Azure Portal ( https://portal.azure.com/ ). Click on All resources->Search App41 in search box (shown in below image). Choose EUAZ1MSECMAPP41 (Virtual machine). In the overview, check if the server status is running. Next go to help --> Resource health --> check the availability status of server. Step 4: If any of the above options does not help, we can check if the M-Files services are running properly. To check the services running, login PAM --> Connect to APP41 server -->Search for services. When the services is opened, Check if the M-Files related services are running. Step 5: To check for Prod application server log files through event viewer. To view the event log, login PAM --> Connect to APP41 server -->Search for Event viewer. When the event viewer is opened, go to Application under Windows Logs and check for the error via filter. In F drive, we will have the backup of event logs, we can zip the folder and send it to M-Files if requested. Step 6: To check IIS logs in the proxy server. To view the IIS log, login PAM --> Connect to Proxy31 server --->Search for IIS. When the IIS is opened, go to Application Pools --> Sites --> Default Website --> Bindings. Report all the related logs to M-Files and if they ask for more logs, follow the instructions on M-Files support email and provide accordingly.

short_description: ECM - How to Provide materials for troubleshooting to M-Files
introduction: ECM - How to Provide materials for troubleshooting to M-Files
instructions: Providing materials for troubleshooting In case ticket for 3 rd tier application support has been created, M-Files team may request for memory dump files, log files etc. stuff to help in troubleshooting. For data transfer storage account ecmprodstorage01 has been created for production environment. This storage account has a file share called prodbackups1tb that is listed in Azure portal under production resources: The prodbackups1tb file share has been mounted on all ECM application and proxy servers: Once files to send to M-Files are uploaded into prodbackups1tb file share they can be downloaded via Azure portal for sending/forwarding to M-files. Use this file share only to transfer files between VMs and your PC. Note : Remember to clean-up files after they have been sent over to M-Files! Send the event logs to M-Files support as a standard procedure, and provide the rest only upon their request. 1. Providing event logs Login to the server via PAM, check for Prod application server log files through event viewer. To view the event log, login PAM --> Connect to APP41 server -->Search for Event viewer. When the event viewer is opened, go to Application under Windows Logs and check for the error via filter. In F drive, we will have the backup of event logs, we can zip the folder and send it to M-Files if requested. 2. Creating M-Files memory dumps M-Files’ instructions on how to collect application memory dumps from hang/lockup situation in Windows. 3. Providing Event Viewer log files Please follow these instructions to provide M-Files Event Viewer log files . 4. M-Files Admin Tool’s Server Activity Monitor M-Files support team may request information shown in Server Activity Monitor in M-Files Admin: Take a screenshot of whatever information they have requested and upload to M-Files Support Portal. 5. Uploading files to M-Files support portal Share the logs through cryptshare by following the below link. https://m-files.force.com/s/article/How-to-Send-Files-via-CryptShare-Portal We need to share the password and tracking ID once the file is uploaded in CryptShare. URL: https://fileshare.m-files.com Password: Tracking ID: Remember M-Files support upload portal has a single file size restriction of 21GB. Any files larger than this must be zipped and/or split into multiple volumes e.g. using 7-zip.

short_description: How to reset to draft objects in Windchill
introduction: How to Reset Objects to Draft State in Windchill
instructions: PRECHECK Check the master system of the required object If the object found in windchill then the master system is Windchill. You have to reset the state to draft only in Windchill If the object not found in Windchill, then the master system is not windchill For other master system you can reset state in VariClient Only the latest revision of the object should be moved to draft state Note: Admin does not have permission to change the object ID other than draft state APPROVAL PROCESS If the request is from "Promotor", then no approval is required If the request is from other than "Promotor" / "Created By" Person , get approval from Promotor. To find Promotor, Open "KONE Custom promote request XXXX..." -> Find "Created By" PROCEDURE Open Windchill and search the latest revision of the object ID which need to be changed to draft state Select check box > Actions > Set state Select check box > Collect related CAD documents Note: Even the user requested only WT parts(KM) reset to draft state , it is mandatory to collect CAD Objects (Assembly or part ) also Select check box >Target state > Draft > Ok *If the request from user says, reset xxxyyy.prt (CAD Model), all the related drawings and WT material to be reset after confirming with user *If the request from user says, reset xxxyyy.asm (CAD Assembly), the Set State operation on xxxyyy.asm picks all the related parts and standard components used in the assembly. Choose only exact WT Material and drawing. Please refer below images "Remove selected objects" operation keeps only xxxyyy.asm in the collection window. *In case of Family table components, collect "CAD Models - All Generic and Instances", "WT Material - WT of all instances" and "Drawings - Drawings of All instances and Generic" to be Reset to Draft.

short_description: How to replace Avaya EMC (Avaya Aura Call Center Elite) Target string ?
introduction: How to replace replace the Target string for EMC Malaysia? Here are some background of this question: Normally There should be a dedicated package created for each country . Eg. Package Avaya_AuraEMCDesktop_6.5.41670.1747_China_x64_03 is dedicated for China, package Avaya_AuraEMCDesktop_6.5.41670.1747_Malaysia is for Malaysia. But in some situation , Package is not pushed based on each country, then will get a error pop up with the message like 'configuration loading failed'. When get this error, the right way is to uninstalled the current package then to installed the correct one which is dedicated for this country user. There is another way to fix is to replace the Target string on the EMC. See steps in Answer.
instructions: Here is steps: Right click shortcut of Avaya EMC Go to properties Go to tap 'shortcut' Replace the Target field with " C:\Program Files (x86)\Avaya\Avaya Aura CC Elite Multichannel\Desktop\CC Elite Multichannel Desktop\ASGUIHost.exe" /z CC Elite Multichannel Desktop Malaysia /s 10.104.144.29 /p 29091 /a U=%%U Click 'Apply' then OK to save the change. After this , double click EMC again to open it , should able to load the configuration. If this is still not fix the issue, go to Orange team for avaya support.

short_description: How to review Firefighter access logs?
introduction: 1. How to review Firefighter access logs by Firefighter ID controller?
instructions: Introduction: This knowledge base article explains how firefighter ID controller can review and approve the firefighter access logs after firefighter (Emergency Access) session executed / completed by user while fixing an issue / working on a business requirement in respective KONE SAP system. Each firefighter ID created in GRC system for specific business functional area (Finance, Sales, Material Management etc.) is assigned with a controller related to respective business function. Firefighter ID controller has to review and approve the logs of firefighter session (i.e. list of transaction codes executed during firefighter session = Emergency Access session). 1. How to review Firefighter access logs by Firefighter ID controller? Firefighter ID controller will receive a "Firefighter Log in" notification e-mail as below when Firefighter user log in / starts firefighter session. Once firefighter user completes firefighter session and log off from firefighter session, firefighter controller will receive another notification e-mail related to "Firefighter Log notification" as below. This notification e-mail consists of a link to "Log Details". Controller has to click on "Log Details" screen and then enter GRC log in credentials to view firefighter logs report. Option1: Controller can approve the request directly by reviewing the attached excel sheet by clicking on “Approve” link and then click on ”send” button in next email window. Alternatively, Controller can reply to the original email with text: Approve only to Approve the SAP Access Request. Option2: Alternatively, controller can also review the online Firefighter log report by clicking on the link “Log Details” and by entering GRC Production log in credentials to view the report. To access the "Log Details" link / log in, controller has to access the link from KONE network. Once logged in controller can see the details of firefighter session executed by user along with list of transaction codes executed and it can be approved from the same screen by clicking on the “Submit” button. Below is sample screenshot. Controller can review log details and confirm transactions executed is as per business requirement Note 1: While reviewing logs, controller can seek clarifications / justifications from firefighter user if any of suspected transaction codes were executed by user which are not as per business requirement. Controller can add relevant notes and attachments related to any e-mail conversations happened over log review under attachments section of log review screen. Note 2: Firefighter log reviews are activated only for KONE SAP Production systems and for non-production systems Firefighter controller will receive email notification only. Note 3: If the Firefighter Controller wants more details for a specific line item within the logs, they need to raise ticket to Common Platforms Service Desk (CPSD) to check what is available in system logs for the same – like for e.g. any change documents (if the transaction code relates to document editing etc.). They will need to provide details of the FF User ID, T-code used and exact time of usage etc. to CPSD for this.

There are several ways to move messages from a user's primary mailbox to their archive mailbox: Move or copy messages manually Manually move or copy messages in the primary mailbox or a .pst file to the archive mailbox. Description visible in on Packing label is different compared to Description (MAKT-MAKTX) available in Material Master Data, which follow KOS-000018! short_description: KONE FSM: Updating the Pricelist, Due date and Update Status to create Service Appointments for each Work Order
introduction: How to update the work order created by MuleSoft to ensure Service Appointments are created?
instructions: Pre-Requisite: AMS support resource should have the admin access of the Business Org. Business should provide the Work order details, Pricelist to be associated to the work order and Due dates available at SAP side. Steps to resolve the issue Search for the Price list name provided by the business under Price Books tab and get the Id of the pricelist. Update the selected pricelist Id on the Work Orders through workbench. If Update Status of the Work Order is in ‘ERROR’ status, provide the service order number to SAP team and request them to re-push the mentioned service orders. Once the SAP team has re-pushed the service orders, validate the Update Status - It should be set as ‘Complete’. Confirm if the Due Date is not available, if not, request the business to provide the Due Date to be updated in the work orders. Once the due dates are available, Update the Due date in the work order. If the Due date belongs to past, then update the due date with 7 days’ later date from today’s date. Example: update the due date=09/05/2019 if today’s date = 02/05/2019 Set the Update Status field to ‘Updating’ and save the work order. Set the Update Status field to ‘Complete’ and save the work order. Validate whether the Service Appointment got created for the work order.

short_description: KONE FSM: Technician users account modification (Deactivation/modification)
introduction: How to update the Technician user account / resource record if technician is no more working OR his role is changed.
instructions: Pre-Requisite: 1) AMS support resource should have the admin access of the Business Org. 2) Business should provide the new role/profile if technician role in the organization is getting changed. 3) Business should provide the approval if the technician is no more working in the organization. THERE ARE TWO SCENARIOS WHERE THE TECHNICIAN USER ACCOUNT AND THE CORRESPONDING SERVICE RESOURCE RECORD NEED TO BE UPDATED. SCENARIO 1: THE TECHNICIAN IS NO MORE WORKING IN THE ORGANIZATION. Step 1: GO to the technician user account. Step 2: Remove the below mentioned permission sets assigned to him so that the permission set license get freed. FSL Dispatcher Permissions FSL Mobile License FSL Resource License FSL Resource Permissions Step 3: Un-check the ‘Active’ checkbox on the user record and save the record. Step 4: Search the corresponding service resource record and open it in edit mode. Step 5: Un-check the two check boxes i.e. a) Active b) Include in Scheduling Optimization and save the record. Step 6: Goto the Service territory related list of the service resource record and click on edit link of the service territory member record where the end date field has future date. Step 7: Change the date to past date and save the record. Step 8: Repeat step 6 and 7 for all the service territory member records where the end date field has future date. Step 9: Goto the Work Center assignment related list and edit the record where the end date is future date. Step 10: Change the end date to past date and save the record. Step 11: repeat the steps 9 & 10 for all the work center assignment records where the end date field has future date. Step 12: Goto the Shifts related list and edit the record where the end date field has future date. Step 13: Change the end date to past date and save the record. Step 14: Repeat the steps 12 & 13 for all the work center assignment records where the end date field has future date. Step 15: Run the below query to get all the future scheduled service appointment assigned to this service resource and export the data using data loader: SELECT Id,SchedEndTime,SchedStartTime,Status FROM ServiceAppointment WHERE SchedStartTime >= today AND Assigned_Service_Resource__c = <salesforce id of the service resource record' Step 16: Change the status = 'New' and make the SchedStartTime and SchedStartTime blank and update the service appointment records. SCENARIO 2: THE TECHNICIAN IS NO MORE WORKING AS TECHNICIAN AND HIS ROLE IS CHANGED IN THE ORGANIZATION. Step 1: GO to the technician user account. Step 2: Change the profile and role as per the new role of the technician and save the record. Step 3: Remove the below mentioned permission sets assigned to him so that the permission set license get freed. FSL Dispatcher Permissions FSL Mobile License FSL Resource License FSL Resource Permissions Step 4: Search the corresponding service resource record and open it in edit mode. Step 5: Un-check the two check boxes i.e. a) Active b) Include in Scheduling Optimization and save the record. Step 6: Goto the Service territory related list of the service resource record and click on edit link of the service territory member record where the end date field has future date. Step 7: Change the date to past date and save the record. Step 8: Repeat step 6 and 7 for all the service territory member records where the end date field has future date. Step 9: Goto the Work Center assignment related list and edit the record where the end date is future date. Step10: Change the end date to past date and save the record. Step11: repeat the steps 9 & 10 for all the work center assignment records where the end date field has future date. Step 12: Goto the Shifts related list and edit the record where the end date field has future date. Step 13: Change the end date to past date and save the record. Step 14: Repeat the steps 12 & 13 for all the work center assignment records where the end date field has future date. Step 15: Run the below query to get all the future scheduled service appointment assigned to this service resource and export the data using data loader: SELECT Id,SchedEndTime,SchedStartTime,Status FROM ServiceAppointment WHERE SchedStartTime >= today AND Assigned_Service_Resource__c = <salesforce id of the service resource record' Step 16: Change the status = 'New' and make the SchedStartTime and SchedStartTime blank and update the service appointment records

short_description: SAP OSS Connection Opening Via Firefighter Access
introduction: 1> What is the process of opening OSS / Remote connection to SAP?
instructions: 1> What is the process of opening OSS / Remote connection to SAP? This KB Article explains the process of opening SAP OSS remote connection and providing access via Emergency Access Mgt module (Firefighter) of SAP GRC system. In the context of recent change in process related to SAP OSS connection to KONE SAP systems and compliance perspective, OSS connection to SAP will be opened via Firefighter ID as it tracks all the activities carried out and sends logs to the Firefighter controller after particular firefighter session is closed. Thus activities conducted may be checked also later on in case need arises. Actions by Requestor: Requestor has to open a service request to "Infosys SAP Security Team" in Remedy requesting to open connection to SAP with reference to OSS incident number, SAP System (E.g. Z03) and module/track (E.g. MM, PS, etc.) to which access is required. Once request is created, please inform the request number to Infosys Security Team through e-mail ID: " Infosys_SAP_Security_DL@kone.com " to process the request. Requestor has to inform SAP that connection is opened to SAP GRC Production (GRP) system, after receiving the respective communication from SAP Security Team. Then SAP can log in to SAP GRC (GRP) system and execute Firefighter session to troubleshoot issue in target SAP system (i.e. ECC / BW / BH etc.) referring to steps provided in below linked "How to use FF (Firefighter) ID” document to troubleshoot the issue. Will open the connection to SAP GRC Production (GRP) system. Will send an e-mail communication to requestor saying SAP can now log in to SAP GRC Production (GRP) system and execute Firefighter session to troubleshoot the issue in respective target SAP system (i.e. ECC / BW / BH etc.). short_description: The work order remain in ‘In Progress’ status even after completing the service appointment
introduction: Document deals with analyzing the work order that remain in ‘In Progress’ status even after completing the service appointment
instructions: Pre-Requisite: 1) AMS support resource should have the admin access of the Business Org. Steps to resolve the issue An incident# 536856 was reported by business that the WO# 9B0000068909 status is not changed to Finished even after the corresponding service appointment SA-33028 was completed by the technician. This is not an issue and the system is working as designed. But to conclude on this, we need to follow the below mentioned steps to analyze how the service appointment is completed by the technician. Step 1: Login into the system and search for the work order given by business user that is still in ‘In Progress’ status. Step 2: Go to service appointments related list against the work order and check a) The service appointment (given by business) is actually in ‘Completed’ status? b) Is there any other service appointment for future and not completed? Step 3: If the service appointment (given by business) is completed but there is one more service appointment and the status of this service appointment is not ‘Completed’. Under such scenario, the technician has back reported the first service appointment with Equipment Condition On Departure = ‘Equipment not running or still be out of order’ OR Equipment Condition On Departure = ‘Equipment is running but not finally fixed’. And as per the system design (if the technician backreports that the equipment is still not running), a follow-up service appointment is created to complete the pending work and the work order will remain in ‘In Progress’ or ‘On Hold’ status. Step 4: If the service appointment (given by business) is itself not completed then the work order will always remain in ‘In Progress’ status. This is not an incident and system is working as expected. Step 5: If all the service appointments are completed then we need to complete the work order by changing the status of work order to ‘Finish’ If the WO and all the service appointments are completed and the site visit work order is not completed then follow the steps given in the below link to completed the site Visit work order: https://kone.sharepoint.com/teams/FSM/Wiki/Ams%20Instructions/Troubleshooting-Tips-and-Known-Issues.aspx

short_description: The callout service appointment which is scheduled by scheduling engine later during the day (or next day) and ignore the callout SLA response time
introduction: Document deals with analyzing the callout service appointment which is scheduled by scheduling engine later during the day (or next day) and ignore the callout SLA response time
instructions: Pre-Requisite: 1) AMS support resource should have the admin access of the Business Org. Steps to resolve the issue An incident# IN00528812 was reported by business that the service appointment # SA-52904 is scheduled next day by the scheduling engine which is quite late as compared to the callout SLA response time. Step 1: Login into the system and search for the service appointment given by business user. The service appointment should be a callout i.e. Maintenance Activity Type=’Call out’. Check and note down the first scheduling date/time. Step 2: Click on the parent field value to open the corresponding work order of the service appointment. Step 3: Click on the SLA field value (Callout) on the work order and check the response time & callout hours. Now compare the response time and the scheduled date/time. If the SA is scheduled after the response time, then we need to continue with the investigation. Step 4: Go back to the work order record and click on the Case field value and open the corresponding case which was created by the call center agent. Step 5: Check the ‘Earliest Start Date’ on the case. If it is not visible on the UI then query the case record in work bench to check the value. Actually, system takes the Earliest Start Date as an input with high preference and ignore the Callout SLA response time and schedule the service appointment after the Earliest Start Date. If in the reported incident, the system has followed the above logic then this is expected and it is not an issue.

short_description: The complex work is completed by creating multiple service appoints but one service appointment has put the status of the WO to ‘On Hold’
introduction: Document deals with analyzing The complex work is completed by creating multiple service appoints but one service appointment (completed) has put the status of the WO to ‘On Hold’ which will prevent the other technician to complete his service appointment.
instructions: Pre-Requisite: 1) AMS support resource should have the admin access of the Business Org. Steps to resolve the issue An incident# IN00536289 was reported by business that Nicolas Louckx was working on SA-53200 and cannot complete it. The status of the WO 9B0083578783 was ‘On Hold’ which is preventing the technician to complete the service appointment. Step 1: Login into the system and search for the service appointment (blocked one) given by business user. Check the if the ‘Assistant SA’ check box is true/False. In case of complex work ‘Assistant SA’ checkbox should be true. Step 2: If the ‘Assistant SA’= False then the WO will be changed to ‘On Hold’ as soon as the another service appointment of the same work order is completed by another technician. It is dispatcher responsibility that he makes the checkbox ‘Assistant SA’= True immediately after creating the 2 nd service appointment for complex work before dispatching the service appointment to 2 nd technician. Step 3: Click on the Parent field value to open the corresponding work order and change the status from ‘On Hold’ to ‘In Progress’ to let the 2 nd technician to complete the service appointment. For more details related to complex work where multiple technicians are working on the same equipment and work order, pls see the wiki page: https://kone.sharepoint.com/teams/FSM/Wiki/Key%20Concepts/Multi-technician%20jobs.aspx

short_description: Handling and communicating on receiving the splunk alert that the Global Optimization job is running for more than 2 hours.
introduction: Document deals with dealing and communicating on receiving the splunk alert that the Global Optimization job is running for more than 2 hours.
instructions: Pre-Requisite: 1) AMS support resource should have the admin access of the Business Org. Background and context · Nightly jobs are scheduling service appointments every night. There is one optimization job per service territory. · Once the optimization is done, the service appointments are assigned and dispatched to the technicians with a separate job. · The nightly optimization job and the dispatching job are scheduled to be run 2 hours from each other in the night. The scope of optimization this monitoring item is to monitor that the nightly optimization jobs a) get completed and b) the job execution does not exceed 2 hours. Process instructions for OCC OCC Monitoring has detected that there are Nightly Optimization Request(s) that have not completed in 2 hours. Please send an email alert to CPSD informing that the job is not completed. Process instructions for CPSD If the status of the nightly optimization request is "In Progress", it is possible that the actual optimization results are returned but it's only the status of the optimization request that is stuck to 'In Progress'. · See KBA2429 for detailed instructions on how to analyze if this is the case. · If this is the case, manually update the status to 'Failed'. This is to avoid a subsequent RSO optimization request to fail because there is already an outstanding other optimization request running. · One possible root cause is that the is a Service Resource record associated to a user that is no longer a technician. That Service Resource should be excluded from the optimization. See KBA2424 for details. If the optimization results are NOT returned for all included service appointments and the optimization request is indeed still in progress · Raise a P2 case to Premiere+ support · Initiate KONE Major Incident Process. The business impact is that service appointments are not getting scheduled properly and callouts to the impacted service territories are not getting automatically dispatched as there is already a running optimization request. · Call the Dispatchers of all the affected KONE call centers and inform them that the nightly optimization job has not completed normally. Ask them to review the schedules of the technicians and dispatch work manually in FSM. .

short_description: Handling and communicating on receiving the splunk alert that the RSO job is running for more than 3 min.
introduction: Document deals with handling and communicating on receiving the splunk alert that the RSO job is running for more than 3 min.
instructions: Pre-Requisite: 1) AMS support resource should have the admin access of the Business Org. Background and context · RSO Optimization Requests are sent to Click Software's integration endpoint. Click Software is a 3rd party of Salesforce i.e. the service belongs to the responsibility of SFDC. · The RSO optimization requests are normally picked for execution (status 'In Progress') immediately and they are completed in a couple of minutes. · If the status of an RSO Optimization Request is "queued" for more than 3 minutes, it means that the optimization has not even started yet and there is something wrong. · The business impact to KONE is that callouts do not get dispatched automatically and if the call centers start to dispatch callouts manually, the remaining of the day will be full of overlaps because RSO does not automatically re-schedule the rest of the day. Process instructions for OCC OCC Monitoring has detected that there are RSO Optimization Request(s) stuck in "queued" status. Business impact is that there are callouts that are not getting automatically dispatched and most likely next callouts will not get dispatched either. In addition to this, when the call centers will manually dispatch the callouts, the schedules will not be updated and the technicians will have overlaps in their daily plans. Steps to resolve the issue Check if a manual RSO run will also get stuck to 'Queued' status. · Choose one of the Optimization Requests that is in status 'Queued'. Observe the Service Resource of this Optimization Request. Manually change the status of the Optimization Request to 'Failed'. · Go to the Gantt chart and manually run RSO for the same Service Resource that the original RSO Optimization Request was for. Observe if the status of this Optimization Request will get to 'In Progress' and 'Completed'. RSO jobs should go to 'In Progress' almost immediately and get completed in a couple of minutes. Note: It is possible that the Dispatcher has already changed the Optimization Request status to 'Failed' and manually run RSO. Do **not** manually run RSO again for the same technician if the Dispatcher has already done this as RSO will re-schedule the rest of the technician's day. If also the manual RSO Optimization Request stays is status 'Queued', there is something severely wrong. · Raise a P1 Premier Plus case by calling them. · Initiate a KONE major incident process. · Call the Dispatchers of all KONE call centers that use FSM and inform them that automatic dispatching and RSO is not currently working. KC3 agents need to manually dispatch the callouts in FSM until the issue is fixed. Remember that callout handling is extremely time critical business operation so please use phone and not email when communicating with the dispatchers. Link of Dispatcher Contact Details: https://kone.sharepoint.com/:x:/r/teams/FSM/_layouts/15/Doc.aspx?sourcedoc=%7B114f2ed6-e948-47be-828b-cf868b7a4bdf%7D&action=default&uid=%7B114F2ED6-E948-47BE-828B-CF868B7A4BDF%7D&ListItemId=4101&ListId=%7B2EAC5F40-74ED-40B0-8F48-2BE7BC5E8CFF%7D&odsp=1&env=prod If there was only a single Optimization Request that had been stuck in 'Queued' but a manual RSO Optimization Request was successful (either by yourself or the Dispatcher had manually run RSO) · Raise a P2 Premier Plus case and inform them that there has recently been an Optimization Request that had been stuck in 'Queued' status which indicates that ClickSoftware's optimization service is not working properly. Ask Premier Plus to check the health of ClickSoftware's optimization service. If the manual RSO runs seem to work but there is a backlog of previous RSO Optimization Requests in status 'Queued' · Call the Dispatchers of the affected KONE call centers. Agree with the Dispatcher if they want to set the status of 'Queued' Optimization Requests to 'Failed' themselves and manually run the RSO or do they want CPSD to help with RSO re-runs. · This must be explicitly agreed to avoid a situation where multiple people are trying to run RSO for the same technician at the same time. · Remember that callout handling is extremely time critical business operation so please use phone and not email when communicating with the dispatchers. .

short_description: How to create SAP Access Request for CPM using SAP GRC?
introduction: This KBA will provide instructions on how to create GRC request for CPM(BW) systems access.
instructions: SAP Access Request Process Open GRC End user form Choose the SAP Landscape to be CPM . By choosing CPM as the landscape, you will automatically get access to all CPM systems. If there is need to have access to just one system e.g., test system, please contact SAP Security team. Email: KONE_SAP_Security_GRC@KONE.COM Define the correct work region/unit where you need the access to. Enter your KONE Network ID that will work as your SAP ID as well and press enter. This will automatically populate the correct full name details as well as line manager from Active Directory. NOTE! Access request can be also raised on behalf of the user. If you don’t know the KONE Network ID, you can search the user behind the duplicate boxes -button. Accesses to CPM are defined based on the KONE Way Role. Please choose the correct one from the list. select the “Request Type” from the drop down as shown below. Fill in the “Request Reason” on why the access is required to the user. NOTE: Request Reason is limited to only 500 characters. please fill in accordingly. Once the “Request Reason” is filled in then click on “Submit Access Request Based on KoneWay Role” button and after that you will get confirmation that request was created. Access request has been sent to the approval round and once all approvals have been provided and the user access has been provisioned by GRC in the target landscape, you will receive a mail with the request details then login to the respective requested system using the SSO (Single Sign On). Multiple KONE Way Roles and/or countries Sometimes there is a need to get accesses for several different countries data. That can be done by ticking the “Select Checkbox in case User requires multiple KoneWay Roles Access” -box. This will open additional field. Choose the correct KONE Way role from the list. Press “+ Add Selected KoneWay Role -button and the role gets visible on the list. Repeat the steps for Work Region/Unit and KoneWay Role to get all required roles listed before submitting the request. NOTE! Before submitting the request, please confirm the KoneWay roles from the table: Added/Selected KoneWay roles for CPM Access. In case if the user mistakenly adds any KoneWay role to the table, it can be deleted by selecting the appropriate line item and by clicking on "Delete KoneWay Role from Table" Access request has been sent to the approval round and once all approvals have been provided and the user access has been provisioned by GRC in the target landscape, you will receive a mail with the request details then login to the respective requested system using the SSO (Single Sign On). You can follow the status of the request via Check status . Instructions provided in KBA1538 Important Note While logging into SAP Access Request Form, In case if the system shows user ID "GRC_EUIBB" already logged in by some other user then uncheck/untick the "Cancel all existing logons" option and click on continue button as shown in below screenshot. If "Cancel all existing logons" is not unchecked/unticked and then if you have clicked on "Continue" button then system will close all other user's sessions in the middle who already actively using the tool.

short_description: How to process RPA, Robotic Process Automation related tickets via AskIT
introduction: How to process RPA related tickets via AskIT
instructions: Robotic Process Automation (RPA) is a process automation based on the notion of software robots, artificial intelligence and machine learning can be used as more advanced technology. When an RPA robot is at work, it performs tasks just like a human would: logging in, operating applications, entering data, performing complex calculations and logging out. It can work 24/7. Process must be rule based and not dependent on human decisions. Kone uses Blueprism as RPA platform. Blueprism is supplied by Kone RPA partner Digital Workforce as RPA as a Service. Blueprism and RPA robots are stored in external Azure cloud managed by Digital Workforce. Digital Workforce is responsible for technical support of the platform and Run Management for the robots, NordCloud supports Express Routing which enables connectivity between Kone network and Blueprism cloud. In case any ticket related to RPA, Robotic Process Automation, Robotics, Blueprism, BOT, Robot, Digital Worker, AUT_P0XXXXX will be raised, please treat it with priority and reassign to appropriate queue. Tickets related to RPA can be routed to AskIT team (via phone, mail or AskITPortal) and AskIT team will (if needed) create a ticket to RF with the following info: Service / Application / IT category Centralized transactions / RPA Robotics Process Automation, BluePrism Queue to be used to forward the tickets is CUST.KONE.INT.SD Application field in the ticket should be BluePrism Service field in the ticket should be Centralized Transactions Following are examples of incidents which can be raised: Incidents related to network are managed by NordCloud. Standard NordCloud procedure should be applied, including Major Incidents. Example of issue where Major Incident Procedure has to be applied: Connectivity between Blueprism environment and Kone network is broken (Nordcloud to be contacted Link ) Separate KBA 720 is created for creation of RPA dedicated Windows ID . Processes in Production and its criticality: AUT_P0002_1 – SOB to SOA extracts Criticality – standard Escalation points: Solution Owner for RPA – Aneta Pawluczuk Aneta Service Manager for RPA – Hanni Tuominiemi · Scheduling of BOT / Robot / RPA / Digital Worker is not correct · BOT / Robot / RPA / Digital Worker didn't start its work on time · BOT / Robot / RPA / Digital Worker doesn't perform its work as required / doesn't follow instructions · There are mistakes in logic of BOT / Robot / RPA / Digital Worker · Error message appears for BOT / Robot / RPA / Digital Worker

short_description: KONE FSM: Unblocking technician for error "unable to get exclusive access to a record" or for other reason.
introduction: How to unblock a technician if he get an error msg "unable to get exclusive access to a record" on mobile device or technician is blocked for other reason?
instructions: Pre-Requisite: 1) AMS support resource should have the admin access of the Business Org. a) If the technician got the error "unable to get exclusive access to a record", request the technician to complete the pending uploads because these errors sometimes just happen once because of other concurrent processing in the salesforce platform is working on the same record/object and technician is unable to make his changes in the system. b) Sometimes, the steps followed by agents to assign a service appointment to technician are not correct and the assigned resource (related list) record is not created against service appointment and technician get blocked to work on the service appointment. To unblock the technician, create manually the assigned record against the SA. If this step does not unblock the user then check if if there is any pending upload that is trying to create the assigned record against the SA. If yes, then delete the assigned record against the SA and let the mobile user sync the mobile to create the assigned record against the SA.

short_description: Modify or delete user in Apptio tool
introduction: How to modify or delete user in Apptio tool?
instructions: 1) Adding additional access roles or changing access roles needs a request via MyAccess. The request will go to approval based on the requested access roles. Adding access to additional content (like a new project) is requested via service request or key user and done in row-level security settings. See KBA (to be added) for the row-level security instructions. 2) In case MyAccess request is approved, service request will be automatically created. Go to https://kone.apptio.com and from the Settings menu, select Access Administration. A new tab is opened for https://frontdoor-ui-eu.apptio.com/home?domain=kone. 3) Select the user to be modified or deleted in the Home -> Users tab. You can use the Search Users field to find the user. You can select multiple users at once if the same change is needed for them all. 4) Deleting user: select the user from the list by ticking the box on the left side of the Username field. Actions above the list are activated once at least one user is selected. Select Delete User -action. Pop-up window will appear where you need to confirm that you want to revoke all accesses and delete the user by ticking the box and then Confirm. 5) Changing access roles for one user: click on the Username of the selected user to see what access roles they already have in different environments. Deleting access roles can be done by clicking the x in the correct role on the correct environment row. Adding more access roles can be done by clicking the plus sign on the correct environment row. When deleting roles, a pop-up window will appear asking for confirmation for revoking the role from the user. Click Revoke Role to continue. When adding roles, a pop-up window will appear with the list of the roles for the selected environment. Find the correct role to be added and click on the plus sign on that row. Select more roles if needed, and finally confirm selections with Add Role(s) button. 6) Changing access roles for multiple users: This can be done in the Access Wizard. For adding or revoking a role for multiple users, select Manage Environment Access -task, kone.com customer and the environment where the role to be added/revoked exists, then click Next. Then select all the users to be changed by clicking the plus sign next to the Username. Selected users will become visible in the Selected Users -part of the screen. Once all the needed users are selected, click Next. In the next step, you need to select the roles to be added and / or revoked by using the plus and minus signs in the list. Selected roles will become visible in the Added Roles or Revoked Roles. Check that these are correct and then click Next. In the next page you will see the Existing Roles, Added and/or Revoked Roles by selected user. Check that these are correct and click Confirm if they are. If they are not as intended, you can go back with the Back button and do the necessary changes. You can also Cancel completely from this point by clicking the Cancel. 7) Change of username or email address: Full Name and Email address of the user are editable in Apptio, but Username is not. As we use email address as Username, change in the email address usually means it is best to create a new user with same access rights the user currently has, and then delete the user with the old information. See knowledge article KB000A1712 for creating a new user. Deleting a user can be found in this document in point 4. In case only Full Name or Persona needs to be changed, that can be done in Home -> Users -tab. Find the user you need to modify and click on the username to open the user view. Click on View User Profile on the right side of the screen. Click Edit on the Properties -tab. Certain fields become editable. Do the needed changes and click Confirm to save them. Exit from the user profile by clicking the x in the right top corner or the arrow next to User header.

short_description: How to downgrade Java for IRMA error: “Could not connect to Gateway at ‘irma02.konenet.com:44’, service ‘KONE IRMA’: null.”
introduction: How to downgrade Java for IRMA error: “Could not connect to Gateway at ‘irma02.konenet.com:44’, service ‘KONE IRMA’: null.”
instructions: Oracle released Java version 8, update 221. IRMA is not compatible with this Java version update, users who have installed it will experience that IRMA will start and crash down While the Quintiq software vendor is working on a permanent fix for the issue, they advise us to downgrade to previous Java version update to be able to use IRMA. This is done in 2 steps. 1. Uninstall the current Java version 2. Install the previous working Java (Java version 8 update 192). We have gotten this from Oracle web page and uploaded it in IOS sharepoint IRMA folder, see instruction below for link. Step 1: Uninstall the current Java version 1. Go to control panel of your computer. 2. In control panel go to Programs and Features folder. 3. In Programs and Features folder search Java and uninstall the application (Note! If you are having the issue with IRMA, your current version which needs to be uninstalled is Java 8 Update 221 ). Step 2: Install the previous working Java (Java version 8 update 192) 1. Please go to the following link (copy-paste into your browser): https://kone.sharepoint.com/:u:/r/sites/IRMAandIBR/Shared%20Documents/IRMA/jre-8u192-windows-x64.exe?csf=1 2. You can either save the file and then run it, or run directly and click next/OK until finished: When the version installation is complete, you can login to IRMA.

short_description: How to create SAP Access Request for ECC systems based on User Groups using SAP GRC?
introduction: This KBA will provide instructions on submitting GRC request for ECC systems based on User Group.
instructions: SAP Access Request Process There are three options to choose when user needs access to SAP ECC system(s) (Z0*) 1) Clone ID based request · Used when user is not having any accesses in ECC, or there is a need to change the clone ID whose accesses are required 2) Transaction code or technical role-based request · User is already having accesses, but something additional is required 3) User group-based request · There are user groups created for certain areas/job roles. Find the user group matrix behind the link: KONE Access Requests Approval Matrix.xlsx (sharepoint.com) ECC systems are: Z01, Development (only for IT users) Z02, Test Z03, Pre-Production Z04, Production This KBA is created to explain the steps for option 3, user group-based request. Options 1 & 2 are covered in KBA1169 Open User Group Based Access Request Form First enter your KONE NetworkID that will work as your SAP ID as well and press enter. NOTE! Access request can be also raised on behalf of the user. If you don’t know the KONE Network ID, you can search the user behind the duplicate boxes -button. Choose the correct SAP Landscape and define is the access is needed for which system(s); development / test / pre-prod / production. Choose the correct Org Level 1: Org Level 2 options are available based on selected Org Level 1. Open the Primary User Group options from the duplicate boxes -button and press “Go” to see all the available user groups based on the given search criteria. By clicking the dot next to the user group, it will get selected. “Remove existing roles / access” tick box is selected by default. If this option ticked / selected while access request submission, then system will remove all existing access / roles except release strategy and Fiori application access along with assignment of User Group access requested to user in SAP system(s). Note : Here removal of existing roles / access assignments will be done only in SAP production (ERP / GRC) system even if non-production systems selected along with SAP production system while submitting the access request. Note: If “Remove existing roles / access” option is unticked / not selected while submitting the access request, system will automatically remove and replace existing “Primary User Group” access with newly requested Primary User Group in SAP systems selected while submitting the access request. Pick the correct reason for the request from “Request Type” drop down menu and specify the reason on why the access is needed in the “Request Reason” field. Then Submit the access request. Note: User group mapping for Org Level 1 and Org Level 2 is available in User Group matrix. Access request has been sent to the approval round and once all approvals have been provided and the user access has been provisioned by GRC in the target landscape, you will receive a mail with the request details then login to the respective requested system using the SSO (Single Sign On). You can follow the status of the request. Instructions provided in KBA1538 Secondary User Group Sometimes there might be a temporary need to have several user groups assigned at the same time. This can be due to the holiday season or other backup purposed. There user groups should always be added with the appropriate validity time. Currently secondary user group is possible only for Genpact and MAKE process areas. To request secondary user group, Open: Secondary User Group Based SAP Access Request Form Fill in the data and search for the correct user group that is needed temporarily. If there is a need to have several secondary user groups, please tick the box “Select Checkbox to Add Multiple Secondary User Group(s). New option “+Add Secondary User Group” will be visible and allows you to move the selection to the table. By clicking the button, user group is removed to the table, and you can search for another one. If you are already having some secondary user groups but there is a need to have something else, please tick the box “Replace existing Secondary User Group(s)” and the old ones are automatically removed. This has no effect on your Primary User Group! After selecting correct secondary user group(s), define the request type from the drop-down menu and specify the reason on why the access is needed in the “Request Reason” field. Submit the request. Access request has been sent to the approval round and once all approvals have been provided and the user access has been provisioned by GRC in the target landscape, you will receive a mail with the request details then login to the respective requested system using the SSO (Single Sign On). You can follow the status of the request. Instructions provided in KBA1538.

short_description: Provide Developer/Editor access for QPR
introduction: How to provide Developer/Editor access in QPR?
instructions: We may receive MyAccess requests from end users seeking QPR: Editor access. In such cases, we must verify the specific model details for which the edit access is required. If the model details are not provided, please contact the user to obtain this information. Once the model details are collected, get approval from phong.truong@kone.com . To enable the user to have edit access, we need to add them to the QPR: Developer group by logging into the QPR User Management Client. Log in to the QPR User Management Client with the Admin username and password. Search for the user by typing the email address. If the user has logged into QPR earlier, the user account details will be available; otherwise, they will not. If the user login name is not available, request the user to log in to the URL " kone.onqpr.com " so that the user account will be auto-created and by default, the user will be added to the QPR_KONE_viewers group. To grant the user edit access to the specific model they requested, we need to enable User Rights by logging into the QPR Modeling Client. Log in to the QPR Modeling Client. Open the folder, navigate to the requested model, select the model under development, and open it for viewing. After opening the model, click on User Rights to access the User Administration window. Locate the user by entering their email address. Once the user account is found, change the rights for the specific model from view only to modify, then click close. Once access is granted, please inform the user that edit access has been enabled for the specified model.

short_description: Ask IT - Unable to sign in to Outlook
instructions: Some similar issues reported by the users; Outlook something went wrong – This problem can occur while trying to access Outlook, and it’s usually related to your browser. To fix the problem, clear your cache and check if the issue is still there. Can’t login to Outlook email – If you can’t login to Outlook email, you might want to try using a desktop email client. Windows already has a built-in email client, but there are also many other great email clients as well. Something went wrong Outlook 365 – This issue can also occur with Outlook 365, but you should be able to fix the problem using one of our solutions. Can’t log into Outlook web app – If you’re unable to access Outlook web app, the issue might be your browser. Several users reported that reinstalling their browser fixed the issue, so you might want to try that. Solution –1: Check your antivirus: If you can’t access Outlook due to Something went wrong and we can’t sign you in right now message, the problem might be your antivirus. To fix the problem, be sure to check your antivirus settings and make sure that Outlook and similar websites aren’t blocked. If everything is in order in terms of settings, disable your antivirus and check if that helps. In case the problem is still there, you might have to remove your antivirus software completely. Once you remove the antivirus, restart your PC and check if the issue is still there. If the problem doesn’t reappear, you should consider switching to a different antivirus. Solution-2: browser cache, cookies and Internet history: If something’s wrong with your browser, like some corrupted file or something, there’s a chance you won’t be able log into your Outlook account. So, before you try anything else, just clear browsing data on your current browser, and you should be able to connect. In most cases, the problem is related to your cache, and in order to fix it, it’s advised to clear cache in your browser. You can do that with ease by following these steps: Step-1: Click the Menu icon in the top right corner and choose Settings . Step-2: Scroll all the way down and click on Advanced . Step-3: Now locate Clear browsing data option and click it. Step-4: Set Time range to All Time and check all options. Now click Clear data button. After clearing the cache, try to login to the Outlook web app and check if the problem is resolved. Solution-3: Check if Microsoft’s servers are down In case you’re not able to connect from any browser, something’s probably wrong with Microsoft’s servers. If Microsoft’s servers are down, there’s basically nothing you can do to connect to your Outlook account, but to wait for Microsoft to get servers back online. Solution-4: Make sure that cookies for Outlook and Microsoft are enabled Many websites rely on cookies in order to work properly, and if you blocked cookies from Microsoft or Outlook, you might encounter certain issues. Users reported that they fixed Something went wrong and we can’t sign you in right now message simply by making a few changes in their browser. To do that, just follow these steps: Step-1: Click the Menu button in the top right corner and choose Settings from the menu. Step-2: Scroll all the way down and click Advanced to reveal the advanced settings. Select Content settings . Step-3: Select Popups from the menu. Step-4: In the Allow section click the Add button. Now add both Microsoft and Outlook URLs to the list. After allowing popups from these two websites, the issue should be resolved and you’ll be able to access Outlook once again. Other Solutions: Try reinstalling your browser or use a different browser.

short_description: SCMS - Support Process For SDI Related Issues
introduction: What is the Support Process involved for SDI related issue?
instructions: Contents 1. Purpose 2. Scope 3 . Overview 4 . KONE IT Support Model 5 . Instructions for SCMS Support 6 . Liaison Support 7 . Instructions for PDM Support 8 . Service Request for Material Table Update 9 . Material Table Content 1. Purpose The purpose of this document is for the understanding of the support process involved for SDI related issues. 2. Scope This document is prepared for KONE – SCMS Team and provides the instructions and processes, the SCMS, Liaison and PDM team follows for SDI related issues. 3 . Overview The SDI project provides enhancements to EDI communication and Supplier Web content. There are no new applications introduced. 4 . KONE IT Support Model The KONE IT Support Model is as follows: SCMS Support Team is the primary contact point for all EDI/Supplier Web issues. KONE Key Users contact SCMS support team to resolve Supplier Web related issues. Liaison Service team sends automatic emails to SCMS Support in specific error conditions. Firstly, SCMS support team contacts Liaison support team and reports the related issues. Secondly, SCMS support team contacts PDM support team, when it is clear that the issue is related to PDM. Secondly, SCMS support team contacts NORDCLOUD if issue is network connectity related (Amazon) The Material Table Updates will be handled as Service Requests . 5 . Instructions for SCMS Support Use cases: Key User reports that Supplier has not received Order Information. Key User reports that Supplier cannot find order specific document package from Supplier Web. Liaison Service sends automatic error notifications ( see 6. Liaison Support ). SCMS Support checking: SCMS Support logins to Supplier Web first to check the following - Does order exist in Supplier Web? Does document package exist in Supplier Web? Is order / document packages visible through LENS? SCMS Support contacts Liaison Support Team Informs the Supplier name and Order ID and Asks whether the issue is content related or not . SCMS Support contacts PDM support Report only BOM and Document Package content issues. 6 . Liaison Support Instructions : LIAISON internal task defined internally. Automatic error notifications to SCMS Support : Cannot access PDM (network connectivity issue). Cannot find PDM BOM (content issue). Cannot process PDM BOM (content issue). Cannot find Document Package from PDM (content issue). Cannot download Document Package from PDM (network connectivity issue) and other related errors. 7 . Instructions for PDM Support Use cases : SCMS Support reports that LIAISON Service cannot access PDM at all or receives timeout. SCMS Support reports that LIAISON Service has no rights to read PDM BOM SCMS Support reports that LIAISON Service cannot find PDM BOM or process it. SCMS Support reports that LIAISON Service cannot find document package from PDM. 8 . Service Request for Material Table Update Use cases : one of authorizes KONE employee creates AskIT Service Request to SCMS Support. 9 . Material Table Content The material table should contain the following: As mentioned in the below screenshot. Material Code : KM number PDM BOM : 1 – Full Line Item 2 – Part of Line Item PDM Docs : Yes – Download Document Package from PDM No – Do not download Docs BOM Relation : 1 – Order 2 – Material under Order 3 - Material under Line Item 4 – Material Other
