short_description: CAD Robot - Quick Fix Update
introduction: CAD Robot - Quick Fix Update
instructions: Updating CAD Packages is a task that end users with the appropriate permissions can perform by themselves. Primarily, the CAD Package Creator add-on in Creo shall be used to create and update CAD packages. However, if you receive such a request, here are to instructions on how to update CAD Packages in CAD Console: Download ZIP package from the location specified by the requestor Check that the CAD Package content is properly zipped: all files are located in root (no subfolders in zip file) instance index name is of type <revision>.idx Update CAD Package in CAD Console Production CAD Package - Update CAD Packages Select existing CAD Package from the dropdown list Browse for the updated zip file De- select "Do the incremental update" Select Update to upload the new file Inform requestor, that the task has been completed In case requestor only sent the updated CAD files, download and update the existing zip package. Remember to update the index file (see KBA2772 for further instructions). "Do the incremental update" is a feature that allows you to upload only changed files. To do so, create a zip package of the file(s) to be uploaded and upload with the incremental update option enabled. The CAD Console will set the previous CAD Package to Retired-state and combine the old and new packages into a new one.

short_description: Creo - Update Index File
introduction: Creo- Update Index File
instructions: Instance index files enable you to retrieve instances by entering their instance name only. An instance index file displays the total number of instances, followed by a column of instance names on the left and a column of their corresponding generic names on the right. The instance index file is named <directoryname>.idx (where directoryname is the directory in which the file is saved). This is how you update the Creo index file: Create new folder structure C:\PROE_WORK\<Material Number>\<Revision> Example C:\PROE_WORK\KM51513250V000\B Unzip CAD Package into newly created revision-specific folder In Creo, select this folder as working directory (Home - Select Working Directory) Update Index via File - Manage Session - Update Index Accept "Which directory ?" default Accept "Store index in ?" default Index file is now updated.

short_description: CAD Console - Update CAD Options

To Upload / Update CAD Options: Download the existing options package from CAD Console (CAD packages - CAD options) Extract the zip package to a temporary location on your computer Modify the appropriate option file according to the requirement Zip the option files and upload the modified package via CAD packages - Upload CAD Option Packages Inform requestor that change has been implemented

short_description: CAD Console - Remove Obsolete Products
introduction: CAD Console - Remove Obsolete Products
instructions: The first step is to identify, if only a certain revision needs to be removed or the entire product with all revisions. Removing an Obsolete Revision From the menu "CAD packages", select "CAD packages" to list all packages Identify the appropriate CAD Package. Ensure that you search for the appropriate revision Delete it by selecting the Trash button Take remote access to all physical CAD workers and manually delete the revision subfolder C:\CAD_files\<product>\<revision> (optional) DO NOT delete the CAD Option package, as this applies to all revisions of a certain material. If applicable, ensure that Revision Mapping in PDM is updated (Order Revision Map). This is where a certain SAP revision can be mapped to a certain PDM revision. Removing an Obsolete Product In PDM, remove all Order Actions for this particular material Order Actions empty Auto Configuration SAP Statuses empty Order actions enabled = False Order Actions (Table) empty Old order actions enabled = False In PDM Cad-info , delete material from CAD Stores In CAD Console under CAD packages, delete all packages of this particular material In CAD Console under CAD options, search and delete the material's CAD Option package Take remote access to all physical CAD workers and manually delete the material's folder C:\CAD_files\<product> including all subfolders (optional)

short_description: How to resolve SSO related issues in Apptio
introduction: How to resolve SSO related issues in Apptio?
instructions: 1. In case of SSO problems, check that the user has access to Apptio, i.e. you can find the user from the user list in Apptio 2. If yes, check with other users to see if they can use the SSO login to Apptio 3. Check if the user can access other SSO applications (for instance Workday) 4. If the user cannot access any applications using SSO, and other Apptio users can access them normally, the problem is most likely in the user’s profile (for instance, security group settings). Assign the ticket to the AD Services Team for that one user. Queue: KONE.MYACCESS.SUPPORT 5. In case of SSO problems that covers all Apptio users (Mostly this should be a Major Incident, Check with the Operation Lead of Apptio), create a ticket in Ask IT and request to assign it to the SSO support Team. If the ticket was created to the Apptio queue by an end user, re-assign it to the SSO support team: Queue: KONE.WEBSSO.SUPPORT 6. In case you are contacted by Apptio Support about SSO certification update (Keep Operation Lead of Apptio informed about this), create a ticket in Ask IT Portal, assign it to the Web SSO Support Team, and ask them to provide the updated certificate. They can either send it directly to Apptio Support email referring to the case number given by Apptio, or you can forward it to Apptio Support. In case KONE Cloud Services Team contacts you about the SSO certification update and there's a ticket already in Service Now, create a ticket in Apptio support (Via portal https://www.ibm.com/mysupport ) and provide them the updated certificate and ask them to change it.

short_description: How to resolve the system based issues in Apptio
introduction: How to resolve the system based issues in Apptio?
instructions: 1. Check is the problem only in certain environment (Dev, Staging or Prod), or all of them. 2. Check if some other users are able to access the same environment(s). Preferably someone with similar access than the user who has the problem, and one with different access (e.g. admin). 3. If other users are able to access normally, it could be a local caching issue. Ask user to delete the browsing history and re-open the browser to try again. (ticket #00517040) 4. If step 3 doesn’t help, check if the user has had new access roles added recently and is trying to use that new role for the first time. If yes, try to impersonate the user, and change the role to the one he/she is trying to use. Check again if the user is able to access after that. (ticket #00303368) Related articles in Apptio community: Frontdoor R12 (Admin Console) Essentials 5. If the above steps don’t help, create a ticket in Apptio Support. It is possible that allocated resource limits are reached and system is therefore not responsive (ticket #00303368).

short_description: How to perform interface functioning related checks with Taxware from SAP ECC?
introduction: How to perform interface functioning related checks with Taxware from SAP ECC?
instructions: There are no checks to be done by SAP team logging into Taxware system. However, some checks can be performed by SAP team from SAP system as detailed below. Connectivity tests can be done by the following methods from Z04: Check the connection using SM59. Open any FI/SD transaction which has a tax code/calculation (FB02/VA02/etc) and try clicking on ‘calculate tax’ option. If you get a response, connection is fine; if you get RFC error, the connection is down, or the Taxware server needs a restart, etc. Alternatively, execute transaction VA42, navigate to Pricing Conditions screen, by clicking the Update button in the functionality available there and then selecting option ‘G’ (Copy pricing elements unchanged and redetermine taxes), you can check that the update is happening. If you get RFC error, the connection is down, or the Taxware server needs a restart, etc. In addition to the 2 methods for checking the connections as mentioned above, the following method is useful since it will display the number of Taxware connections available in SAP. T-code SM51 Double-click on the CI server Open a new session on the CI server for t-code SMGW (/oSMGW) Select the Menu option Goto~Logged on Clients Sort the column “TP Name” in Descending order Observe the number of instances for TAXUSA and TAXUPD Normally, there should be 3 instances of each. More than 3 is OK. Less than 3 usually indicates a potential problem. Zero instances indicate the connection is down. TAXUSA is the read-only connection that is critical to daily US operations. If that connection is down, not much will work in SAP for the US business. TAXUPD is the update connection. If that connection is down, the entries sent from SAP to Taxware are stuck in the AVP_UPD queue, which can be viewed in SMQ1. This will not halt US business operations, but the US Tax Dept cannot run their legally required monthly reports until all entries reach the Taxware system. As indicated in the screen shot above, the Taxware connections are of type Registered Server. Taxware is installed on a Linux box, which is managed by T-Systems. If either connection goes down, T-Systems restarts both connections by running a script on the Linux box. Incidents in Q2-Q3 2019 regarding the Taxware connections were related to the TAXUPD connection going down because there were some entries stuck in the queue. According to records, starting in November, this has happened in 5 of the last 9 months during the US Maintenance Billing run. There were no issues during the run in the end of July. (Incidents were raised in Nov, Dec, Mar, May, Jun.) We will continue to monitor this. There are no specific checks that we can be done by SAP team by logging into the Taxware system.

short_description: How to check for Firefighter user ID related to OSS incidents and how to maintain the credentials in Secure area?
introduction: How to Identify relevant Firefighter user ID in SAP GRP system? How to save the credentials in Secure Area?
instructions: Logon to https://support.sap.com/ with your user ID and password. Click on Services & Support. Use the Incident Number, which was given by the functional team. Go through the incident reported by the functional team and identify the track like MM, FI, SD, PS, etc. For finding the Firefighter user ID which is currently not in use, login to GRP Client 100 system and open SUIM T-code. Click on "Users by Complex Selection criteria" as shown below. For instance let us take the Function module as FI, today's date is 01.05.2024 and SAP target system is Z04. So we need to filter the User ID as OSS*FI*Z04* and Validity to date as 30.04.2024. Below are results based upon the selection criteria. Select any of the available Firefighter user ID and unlock it in SU01 t-code. Also, extend the validity of the firefighter user by 15 days and reset the initial password. Maintain the credentials in secure as area which was explained in section 2 of this KBA. Login to GRP Client 100 system and navigate to NWBC->Setup->Emergency Access Maintenance->Firefighters. Click on Firefighter and filter the Firefighter (which was unlocked in GRP) as shown below. Open the firefighter and maintain the comments with the corresponding OSS Incident number. Select the Firefighter ID which is same as Firefighter user ID i.e. OSS_FI_Z04_36is case, see below. By default the Firefighter ID was assigned with Validity date as 31.12.9999. DO NOT DO ANY CHANGES other than adding a comment with the OSS incident name. After maintaining the appropriate comment click on Save. 2. Next step is to maintain this user ID and password details in Secure area for the incident reported by the functional team. Go to Launchpad, search the incident and click on Details -> Logon Details. By default, it will take you to the system for which the incident was raised. Please change the System as per the requirement using Switch System option. After selecting the respective system, scroll down to see the Credentials Section. Click on Add option to maintain the OSS credentials. Maintain all the Logon Details as shown below and click on Save. Please ensure you provide a strong password and also to maintain the Delete at Date and time. Maintain Delete at date same as the validity to date for Firefighter ID i.e. for 15 days. Note: Repeat the above steps to maintain the same credentials in the secure area of GRP system as well. Since the SAP team will login to SAP GRP sytem, we should also maintain the credentials in GRP system as well along with the target system. While replyint to email from functional team, please use the below text. 1. Log on to the SAP GRP system using the credentials of OSS_[Insert rest of User ID] . 2. Once logged in, enter T-code "GRAC_SPM" 3. The SAP team will be able to see a list of Fire Fighter ID’s (here OSS_[Insert rest of User ID]) which is assigned by the security team. 4. Click on the relevant “Logon” button to log in to the target system to start the firefighter. 5. From among the reason codes drop down, please select “SAP OSS Support” 6. In the reason code text box, please input the OSS incident ticket number under which this firefighter session has been initiated. Also, please enter the T-codes you expect to use in the session. 7. The SAP team will then be logged on to the BH4 system. 8. Once done kindly unblock the Firefighter ID. Note: In case of any issues faced by SAP in connecting to GRP and using FF ID during any high priority incidents, please change the user type of corresponding OSS* FF ID in target system to Dialog, and set password to same as in step (1) above (if known) and provide credentials + open connection directly to relevant target SAP system.

short_description: TIP - How to publish content to TIP
introduction: 1) How to publish content from AEM to TIP? 2) How to verify published content available in TIP?
instructions: How to publish content from AEM to TIP? We may receive tickets from end users to publish the DITAMAP from AEM to TIP. The steps to publish the content is as described below: 1 . Login AEM ( https://kone-aem.adobecqms.net/assets.html/content/dam ) and open the DITAMAP which needs to be published in TIP. 2 . Open Map Console Window --> Output Presets --> Select output as TIP --> Generate. The output will be generated. 3 . If we need to use any baseline then goto Output presets --> Edit --> Use baseline --> Select the baseline --> Done. 4 . Now generate the output. Select TIP --> Generate. 5 . Check the status of the output in OUTPUTS tab. How to verify directly published content available in TIP? If we publish the DITAMAP/document directly from AEM to TIP then we can check whether the publish is successful or not as follows : Once the DITAMAP is published to TIP, the outputs tab in the map console window will display the output history. If the TIP publish is successful then the output generated will be indicated by the green color. The TIP output history is as below: But sometimes even if it shows that the TIP publish is successful in AEM it will fail in TIP portal which can be identified in TIP output history. In such cases, the publications history should be checked to identify the errors which fails the TIP publish. The errors can be identified with the help of “DitaOT report for the publication” file in TIP.

short_description: SCMS - PA.Net Support Training Material_PA.Net
introduction: PA.Net Support Training Material
instructions: 1. INTRODUCTION This document contains basic information related to PA.NET and has been created as training material for GD application center support members. 2. PURPOSE OF PA.NET APPLICATION PA.NET is application used in KONE factories. Application works based on reading barcodes of components that are packed separately to a packing box – eg blisters, additional brackets, assembly part packed (door panels, frames, ceiling etc…). It should help us to control to pack everything needed for a product specification and don’t pack different variant of a component needed for exact order specification – shortly avoid missing parts and wrong parts delivered to a site / customer. PA.NET is separate application running on SQL server and the base data are downloaded from SAP to a PA:NET database. 3. SETTING IN SAP 3.1. Product setting We have to do the following setting in SAP to activate a product so that production ordes will be downloaded to the PA.NET database. Adding a product to a product group – MC86 Product group is one of input value for production orders downloading program Characteristic values download for a production order – we have to define a group in an interface design for a product and add there characteristics to be downloaded and imported to PA.NET database. CU50 – setting of the group is the following – every unit has its own product groups 3.1.1 MRP list fulfilling Downloading program pick up production orders from MRP list that is filled in after MRP run – there are all open POs for a product. To turn on the filling in the MRP list, we have to turn it on in tr MD20 3.2. Component setting 3.2.1 Component activation for PA.NET Component activation for PA.NET Components that we want to control by barcoding in PA.NET have to be “activated” in PA.NET db table. We activate and define label type per a material by the following. following: Fields to be set up per a material: Label type (ETIAR) / Label form (ETIFO) – values entered based on label type required for the component (see section available label types). Old material number (BISMT) SN type 3.2.2 Additional setting for orderbound materials Orderbound materials are materials bought specially for orders. Basically these materials have a variable property – eg variable size / color etc and the material is linked to a sales order. We have always separate PO item for the material per each sales order item / production order. These materials require purchase order and item, where they have been ordered for specific sales order item. We have to add orderbound materials to separate product group so that the purchase orders are downloaded from SAP and then right barcode string is required from PA.NET. MC86 – example below – every unit has its own product groups 3.2.3 Barcode setting for internally produced parts We can also barcode internally produced part (subassemblies) that are packed alone at the packing station – eg sill assembly, uprights etc. These assemblies are not set up as subassemblies identified by a material number in SAP. If we want to barcode and control these asseblies at packing station, we have to define “barcode requirements” for them in SAP – we name them Z items. We have create material first in SAP and activate for PA.NET as above. We have to set up these materials so that no reservation, purchase requisitions, planned orders are created because usage of the materials is only to generate a barcode request in PA.NET. It is enough to create the following views in material master data and define the following fields: Views Ø Basic data 1 Ø Basic data 2 Ø General plant data / storage 1 Ø General plant data / storage 2 Fields: Ø Material description Ø Base unit of measure = PC Ø Material group = A70 Ø Old material number (based on label definition) Ø Label type Ø Label form Then we have to allocate these materials in BOM and define them as Z item – CS02 – these items will be resented in a production order bill of material, -> so downloaded to PA.NET database and then barcode will be required in PA.NET workstation at exact packing station. 3.3. Available barcode labels There are the following barcode labels available SAP / PA.NET. Define values in material master data at PA.NET arcode active materials accordingly. Label Type 1 : Barcode Material Name This kind of label contains only information about Material Code. It is generally used for stock material. Label Type 2 : Barcode Material Name + Sales Order Number This kind of label contains information about Material Code and Sales Order Number. It is generally used for Internal Procure Order Bound Material. Label Type 3 : Barcode Purchase Order This kind of label contains information about Purchase Order Number and Purchase Order Position. It is generally used for External Procure Order Bound Material. The Start number “03” is used to recognize the type of label. Label Type 4 : Barcode Sales Order + Old Material Name This kind of label contains information about Sales Order Number and Old Material Name. It is generally used for External Procure Order Bound Material by factory machine. The Start char “E” is used to recognize the type of label. Material Parameter In order to chose for each material the kind of barcode labels you want, you have to specify two types of parameter: · Label Type · Label Form Label Form is used to distinguish between Order Bound (OB) material and Stock (ST) Material. Label type is used to specify when you want to use barcode label. In the table below, you can see the different types of use: Label Type Use P1 Production material P2 Vendor barcode P3 Send labels to vendor P4 Send file to vendor P5 Labeling at goods receipt P6 Labeling at picking P7 Automatic labeled production material · P1 is used for internal procure material · P2 is used for external procure material where vendors print label with his material code. · P3 is used for external procures material: the labels containing Purchase Order Reference. · P4 is like P3, but is used for “Phantom Material“ (see below). It is used for Configurable Material · P5, P6 is not used · P7 is used for Material produced by factory machinery. How parameter influence the labels type is shown in this table: Code Order Bound (OB) Stock (ST) P1 Type 2 Type 1 P2 is not possible Type 1 (With Vendor Code) P3 Type 3 Type 1 P4 Type 3 Type 1 P5 Type 2 Type 1 P6 Type 2 Type 1 P7 Type 4 Type 1 (with old Material Name) 3.4. Component allocations Standardly we define component allocation for master bill of material. Additional setting for PA.NET is to mark barcode active operations there CA02 – go to operation details and activate the operation 3.5. SAP downloading program There is the program Z_MM_T_EXTPLAN in SAP that pick up all needed data from SAP and save a file to a share SAP folder. The program runs every night and it has to run after MRP so that MRP list contains all actual open items SE38 - Z_MM_T_EXTPLAN – there are already define variants – every unit has separate file. We have to be careful with running of this program !! 4. SQL PART SETTING 4.1. SQL servers Available SQL servers are listed in the file PA.NET servers.xls located in PA.NET WS in the main folder. We access them via SQL Server Management Studio tool Base SQL server structure can be seen on left side. Important sections from support point of view are “Databases”, where we can find all data tables including setting and data from SAP and “SQL server agent”, where we can see all running jobs on the server 4.2. Most important SQL tables The most important tables that are frequently used and browsed are located in the folder “DBProductionTool” and “DBSap”. Important tables and description dbo.BarCodedItems Overview of barcoded items and related details dbo.ManualBarCodedItems Overview of manually barcoded items and related details dbo.ConfirmsHistory Overview of PO confirmations successfully posted to SAP Dbo.OrdersToConfirm Recorded PO confirmations that are pending to be posted to SAP dbo.TPACKMATERIAL Product and sub parts configuration – used for order / order operation confirmation and for virtual packaging dbo.TPickingLabelsFormat Serial number related setting for barcoded materials dbo.IT_PRODORDHR Overview of production orders downloaded from SAP dbo.IT_PO_OPER Overview of operation structure for production orders downloaded from SAP dbo.IT_CHARPO Characteristic values from order specification for production orders downloaded from SAP dbo.IT_MATPO Bill of material for production orders downloaded from SAP dbo.IT_PROC Overview of purchase orders for orderbound materials dbo.IT:EKPO Overview of purchase orders overall dbo.IT_MARD Component stock overview dbo.ZEP_S_PK_HU_DATA Overview of handling units downloaded from SAP dbo.ZSD_S_SALES_LABEL Overview of sales order details and related data dbo.EMailReceivers List of users, who can receive an email sent from PA.NET 4.3. Browsing SQL tables We use queries in SQL server management studio tool to browse database tables. We can use the following ways to built a query. We have to be very carefull to update any database table – especially tables containing data downloaded from SAP !!! Ø By right mouse click on a table and select a query Ø By using query builder – click new query and build a query – you can write a code directly or use a guide as shown below add a table you want to browse, define query type, define fields you want to see or filter etc. When it is ready, click OK. The SQL code is written in down part of the screen based on your set up (pay attention so that you don’t mix commands from previous set up if you modify the query) You can see the code of your built query. Click execute if it is OK Result is visible in the down part of the screen If you want to return back to the same query and modify it in query builder, mark the code and click query editor 4.4. Most important SQL server jobs Jobs running on a server are listed in SQL server agent. Here are most important and description OS_DbSAPInsert Job picking up data from downloaded file from SAP and import the data to the databse – most important OS_HUCleaner Job cleaning old / unuseful handling unit (HU) from the database OS_HURetrieve Job picking up HU data directly from SAP OS_HUUpdater Job updating HU data in SAP that were changed from PA.NET application OS_POConfirmation Job sending PO confirmation to SAP that were finished from PA.NET application and so confirm POs in SAP 4.5. Running SQL server job In some cases we could need to run a job out of its standard scheduled time. Eg MRP job run in SAP longer than normally and finished after the DBSapInsert had already started – means that downloaded file had not been updated yet and so the import job didn’t import new data from SAP. Then if it is urgent, we can run the job manually. BUT we have to be very careful to run a job and run it only in urgent cases ! We are not allowed to make any changes in the defined jobs. It has to be maintained by project team. To see a job properties and scheduling time of it and orther staff, we can open job properties 5. CONTACTS Here are is GD contact list Janne Kollin – GD solution owner of PA.NET Xian Fei Tang – 3 rd level functional person Patrizio Strambi – PA.NET developer Alexander Luecke – PA.NET developer IT objects Vít Polehňák – PA.NET support person for EU / US Ma Wei Winnie – PA.NET support person for Asia

short_description: End User - How to create SAP Access Request for Beacon (BH) Access.
introduction: This KBA will provide instructions on how to create GRC request for Beacon(BH) systems access.
instructions: SAP Access Request Process for Beacon NOTE! Users shall raise GRC request only for Country/Area-based access by themselves. If Corporate/Global/Admin accesses are required, please contact Financeauthorization@kone.com The Finance Authorization team will procure necessary offline approval and submit GRC request on the behalf of the user. Corporate/ Global Beacon Accesses are: T_CORPORATE_R T_CORPORATE_W T_CORPORATE_TREASURY T_DATAMGR T_KONE_BU ADMIN ADMIN_VIEW BEACON_ADMIN T_CORPORATE_ADMIN Open GRC Enduser form Choose the SAP Landscape to be Beacon . By choosing Beacon as the landscape, you will automatically get access to all Beacon systems. If there is need to have access to just one e.g., test system, please contact SAP Security team. Email: KONE_SAP_Security_GRC@KONE.COM Enter your KONE Network ID that will work as your SAP ID as well and press enter. This will automatically populate the correct full name details as well as line manager from Active Directory. NOTE! Access request can be also raised on behalf of the user. If you don’t know the KONE Network ID, you can search the user behind the duplicate boxes -button. Accesses to Beacon are defined based on the specified Team IDs. Please choose the correct one from the list. W in Data Access column indicates Write access, and R is for Read. select the “Request Type” from the drop down as shown below. Fill in the request reason on why the access is required to the user. NOTE: Request reason is limited to only 500 characters. please fill in accordingly. Once all the details are filled in then click on "Submit Access Request Based on Team ID". Access request has been sent to the approval round and once all approvals have been provided and the user access has been provisioned by GRC in the target landscape, you will receive a mail with the request details then login to the respective requested system using the SSO (Single Sign On). Multiple Team Name Sometimes there is a need to get accesses for several Team IDs. That can be done by ticking the “Select Checkbox in case User requires multiple Team IDs access” -box. This will open additional field. Choose the correct Team ID from the list. And then Press “+ Add Selected Team NameID -button and the role gets visible on the list. Repeat the steps for all the required Team IDs to get them listed before submitting the request. NOTE! Before submitting the request, please confirm the Team IDs from the table: Added/Selected Team IDs for Beacon Access. In case if the user mistakenly adds any Team IDs to the table, it can be deleted by selecting the appropriate line item and by clicking on "Delete Team NameID From Table" Access request has been sent to the approval round and once all approvals have been provided and the user access has been provisioned by GRC in the target landscape, you will receive a mail with the request details then login to the respective requested system using the SSO (Single Sign On). You can follow the status of the request via Check status . Instructions provided in KBA1538. Note: In case any issue while raising the request for Beacon Access please reach out to ASK IT team( askit@kone.com ). short_description: KONE FSM: Unblocking technician if the technician is unable to see the dispatched service appointment on his mobile device.
introduction: How to Unblock a technician if the technician is unable to see the dispatched service appointment on his mobile device.
instructions: Document deals with making the service appointment visible on the technician’s mobile device if the dispatched service appointment is not visible to technicians. Pre-Requisite: AMS support resource should have the admin access of the Business Org. Steps to resolve the issue An incident# IN00662857 was reported by business that technician was not able to see the service appointment on the mobile device even though the service appointment was dispatched to him by the dispatcher. Following are the steps to fix the issue: 1) Open the service appointment not visible on mobile device reported by business users. 2) Check in the related list if the assigned resource record exists. a. If the record exits, the check if the estimated travel time field has some value. If it is blank, then edit the record and put 15 on the field and save it. b. If the record does not exit then create a new record and enter the service resource with estimated travel time and save the record. 3) After completing the 2 nd step, ask the user to log off and log in to sync the mobile and the service appointment should be visible on the mobile device.

short_description: KONE Employee Management
introduction: How to create Employee? How to disable Employee? How to delete Employee? How to rehire an Employee?
instructions: KONE Employee Management Table of Contents: Employee Creation in Workday and MyAccess How and when Employee user account is disabled? How and when Employee Requested Access will be deleted? How and when Employee user account is deleted? How Employee user account is reactivated? How to update or edit Employee data? Note : Queue Name for My Access Support Issues is " CUST.KONE.INT.SD " Employee creation in Workday and MyAccess Each employee needs to be created in Workday HR system and MyAccess will pull that data on regular basis as explained below: 1.User is hired or re-hired in Workday with all the relevant information like firstName, lastName, Job family, Type=Employee. All user creation in Workday is handled by manager and they can get help from the HR Team. 2.When all the relevant information is present in Workday HR system for a user, the information is passed on to MyAccess portal within 5 minutes and user account is created. Manager of the user is notified via email along with the credentials of new user. The users are also provisioned with the default roles automatically at the time of creation in MyAccess portal. User account can be verified by user’s line manager in MyAccess by navigating through following path Login into MyAccess URL: https://myaccess.kone.com 1.After logging into URL, manager will be redirected to the homepage of MyAccess: 2.Direct reports associated with the manager can be seen on the home page. Managers can look for a new created Employee under this Direct Report widget: If required, Managers can request for additional access of an employee by clicking on the key icon. 3.When manager click on user under Direct Reports widget, user information like Attributes, Accounts and access can be seen on home page. Alternatively, user details can be seen using View Identity under tab Manage Identity. 4.Under Attributes tab, all attributes value associated with the user can be seen: 5.Active directory is also provisioned for the employee by default based on his role, which can be verified in accounts tab. Mailbox access is also granted to the Employee by default at the time of creation. Please refer to attached guide in order to know how email ID, displayName and UPN are created for a user in MyAccess. Users can verify their accounts and access by logging in below link Login into MyAccess URL: https://myaccess.kone.com How and when an employee account is disabled: Employee’s end date is populated in the workday Application and when an update takes place, the changes are pushed into MyAccess Application. From the end date, the employee is disabled in MyAccess the next day. MyAccess will Update the User status inactive = True and disable the user account automatically. Manager will receive an email notification that employee account has been disabled. Please refer the below link for modification of end date in Workday Application. https://kone.portal.dovetailnow.com/page/1960 It can be requested by end user’s manager as well. Manager launches termination (EE) and End Contingent Worker Contract task (CW) The separately requested roles with ‘Immediate removal of inactive members’ attribute value as true will be removed immediately after the account is disabled in My Access and the users needs to separately request the role back if they re-join in KONE. Note: Also, If employee has to be disabled before the last day we just need to Update the end date in Workday Application. How and when employee Requested Access will be deleted: User’s Access are removed from MyAccess Application after 7 days from the end date. Employee’s end date is populated in the workday Application and when an update takes place, the changes are pushed into MyAccess Application. MyAccess will Remove the Requested Accesses of the user along with the mailbox which cannot be recovered How and when an employee account is deleted: Deletion of a user account in MyAccess will be done after 30 days from the end date of the user. 2.After the user account is deleted from MyAccess Application, all the requested accesses are deleted along with the mailbox which cannot be recovered. MyAccess will update the attribute “Deleted” as “True” and email notification is sent to manager to inform that the user’s account is deleted. How employee user account is reactivated: Employee can be rehired in the organization and user account can be reactivated. MyAccess will notice the new hire date from Workday Application and will reactivate the disabled user account or creates a new one in case the old one has gone deleted. Employee hire date modification process is taken care by AskHR, In case of any queries, pleas raise a request to AskHR. 2.If the employee is rehired again within 30 days, user will have application accounts in disabled state as shown below: 3.When MyAccess recognizes that the End Date has been extended, If AD account for user exists, then application accounts are enabled again and updation of attribute changes takes place. If AD account doesn’t exist, it is created for the user based on the role associated with the user. An email notification is sent to the manager of the user about reactivation of account. How to update or edit Employee data? Office address Modification for the Employees, refer below link: https://kone.portal.dovetailnow.com/page/1947 Modify the Manager details for the Employees, refer below link: https://kone.portal.dovetailnow.com/page/1948 How to update Phone numbers for Employees : Employees can change their phone number in Workday via Actions > Personal data > Change My Work Contact Information They also need to raise End Contingent Worker Contract task (CW), please raise a request with AskHR team. How to modify the First Name and last name of the Employees: Employees can change their name in Workday via Actions > Personal data They also need to raise End Contingent Worker Contract task (CW), please raise a request with AskHR team.

short_description: Ask IT - How to remove access in MyAccess
introduction: How to remove a role in MyAccess? How to take a role away from a user in MyAccess? How to delete a role from user profile?
instructions: How to remove access in MyAccess Table of contents: How to remove my own or other user’s access? (by KONE Employee) How to remove my own access? (by Contingent Workers and Supplier-Distributors) All employees can request removal of anyone’s access. Contingent workers and Supplier-Distributor users can request removal of their own access only. Note : Queue Name for My Access Support Issues is "KONE.MYACCESS.SUPPORT" How to remove my own or other user’s access? (by KONE Employee) All employees can request removal of their own or anyone’s access. Login to MyAccess: https://myaccess.kone.com After successful login, you will be landed on the home page of the MyAccess. 2.On the Home page you can see one of the tabs (Quick links) “Manage user access”, Click on that and you will be navigated to the below screen: 3.Search the name of the user whose access you want to remove: 4.Click on the next button and it will navigate you to the Manage Access tab. 5.Go to the Remove Access Tab: 6.Choose the filter option, select Role Type as “Role” and click on search. All the roles associated with the users will be listed. 7.Select the “X” symbol in front of the Role to be removed and press next button, it will navigate you to the last tab i.e. Review and submit. 8.At last validate the username and access you are about to remove and click on submit button. y 9.After submitting the request, it will redirect you to the home page of MyAccess with a message that “Your request has been submitted successfully”. 10.You can track your request, Please refer to KBA: How to track request for KBA 11.Open the Access Request and then check for required approvals. Please refer to Approval Flow for the removal request at end of this document. 12.Check with required approvers in order to get the request approved if required. 13.After, all approvals are done, request will be completed and then requested access will be removed from the user. 14.An email notification will be sent to the user when the request will be completed. How to remove my own access? (by Contingent Workers and Supplier-Distributors) Contingent workers and Supplier-Distributor users can request removal of their own access only. Go to the link: https://myaccess.kone.com Once you login into the link you will be redirected to the home page of the MyAccess. 2.On the Home page you can see one of the tabs (Quick links) “Manage My access”, Click on that and you will be navigated to the below screen: 3.Go to the Remove Access Tab. You may apply filter to search for specific access. You will see a list of all the accesses associated with you. 4.Select the access and press next button, it will navigate you to the last tab i.e. Review and submit. 5.Finally, validate the username and access that you are about to remove and click on submit button. 6.After submitting the request, it will redirect you to the home page of MyAccess with a message that “Your request has been submitted successfully”. Some accesses may require approval from manager or from other defined approvers before the access gets removed. You can track the progress of your request, please refer to KBA: How to track request in MyAccess. 7.Open the Access Request and then check for required approvals. Please refer to Manager Flow for the removal request Check with required approvers in order to get the request approved if required. 8.After, all approvals are done, request will be completed and then access is removed from the user. 9.An email notification will be send to the user once request will be completed. Additional info: Approval Flow for the Access Removal The possible request removal approval flows that can be defined for a role are No approval needed -> no approvals, access will be de-provisioned automatically Manager approval -> line manager (or supplier-distributor's KONE contact) must approve the request to remove the access Named approvers -> one person from the defined approver group must approve the request to remove the access Manager, named approvers -> line manager (or supplier-distributor's KONE contact) must approve the request to remove the access, after which one person from the defined approver group must approve the request to remove the access Please note that Manager approval step in scenario 2 and scenario 4 is always skipped if the manager is the requestor. This means that in scenario 2 the access will be immediately de-provisioned and in scenario 4 only Named approvers are required. In scenario 3 and 4, if the person belongs to the "Named approvers" of the role the “Named approvers” step is skipped.

short_description: Supplier-Distributor User Management in MyAccess
introduction: Who is Supplier-Distributor? What is the definition of Supplier-Distributor? How to create a Supplier-Distributor user account in MyAccess? How to manage or edit Supplier-Distributor user account in MyAccess? How to disable a Supplier-Distributor user account in MyAccess?
instructions: Answer details in the KBA2871 Supplier-Distributor User Management in MyAccess: MyAccess is the master source for all Supplier-Distributors user data. Dedicated KONE Contact person can create, edit and disable Supplier-Distributor accounts in MyAccess Supplier-Distributors are users who are working outside KONE premises for supplier or distributor company They connect KONE Network with Business Partner Channel Old accounts can still have UserIDs in format EXT_NNAME Supplier and Distributor user accounts are managed by dedicated KONE Contact person who has a specific Supplier-Distributor Manager role Dedicated KONE Contact person can manage those Supplier-Distributor he/she is set as manager of in MyAccess No O365 licenses are permitted-office applications A specific Supplier-Distributor Manager Role can be requested in MyAccess tool. All Supplier-Distributor related issues should be addressed to MyAccess via AskIt support KONE.MYACCESS.SUPPORT Content of the attachment document: Supplier-Distributor User Account and Access Management in MyAccess Contents 1. Who is Supplier-Distributor? . 1 2. What accesses Supplier-Distributors can get 1 3. Who can create and maintain Supplier-Distributor accounts in MyAccess? . 1 4. How to create a Supplier-Distributor user account in MyAccess? . 1 5. How to edit Supplier-Distributor user data, close or extend contract? . 3 6. How Supplier Distributor user account is disabled in MyAccess? . 5 7. How Supplier Distributor user account is deleted in MyAccess? . 5 8. How Supplier Distributor user account is reactivated in MyAccess? . 5 1. Who is Supplier-Distributor? Suppliers-Distributors are users who are working in Supplier company producing for example parts for KONE products. They can also be users working in Distributor companies selling KONE products. Supplier-Distributors user accounts are created and maintained in MyAccess. MyAccess is the master source for all Supplier-Distributor user data, which serves as the basis for disabling, deleting or reactivating user’s accesses to KONE applications. Pls Note: External consultants, contractors working in KONE premises like factories, storages or installation business are not Supplier-Distributors. They are classified as Externals and they needs to be added to Workday as Externals. 2. What accesses Supplier-Distributors can get Supplier-Distributor users normally need access to KONE Network for getting technical documents from PDM, sales related documentation or access to give feedback via QFB. Supplier-Distributors get access to KONE Network via Business Partner Channel (BPC). KONE Network access (AD account) is automatically created for each new Supplier-Distributor . New Supplier-Distributors will get: KONE Network ID is format [d]+[MyAccess ID number] d37001234. User Principal Name (UPN) will be in format firstname.lastname@kone.com. Pls. note UPN is used for cloud login and it is not an email address. Supplier-Distributors use their own company email and it is save to mail attribute field in AD PDM and SAP Accesses are requested same way as earlier for EXT-users 3. Who can create and maintain Supplier-Distributor accounts in MyAccess? User accounts and accesses for Supplier-Distributor are created and maintained by dedicated KONE contact persons who have the required “MyAccess - Supplier Distributor Manager Access” role. Dedicated persons work usually in KONE Sourcing units. In order to get the respective access to create/edit Supplier distributor users, KONE employees need to request “MyAccess - Supplier Distributor Manager Access” in MyAccess. Note! Only authorized members can get that access. 4. How to create a Supplier-Distributor user account in MyAccess? Please note: To be able to create or modify Supplier-Distributor records you need “MyAccess – Supplier Distributor Manager Access” . It can be requested via MyAccess. Go to MyAccess: https://myaccess.kone.com On the Home page, you will see “Create Supplier Distributor” link. In case you are not able to find the link, click on the drop down icon on the top left corner next to Home tab and you will be given options like My tasks, Manage Access, Manage identity. Navigate to Manage Identity -> Create Supplier Distributor. After clicking Create Supplier-Distributor link a form will appear. Fill in the form with the details: First Name: Please specify the first name of the user. Last Name : Please specify the last name of the user. Company Email : Please specify here the company email address of the user in which he /she is working (not a KONE email). KONE Contact : Specify the name of the person who is contact person for KONE. He/She must have “MyAccess - Supplier Distributor Manager Access” role in MyAccess. Vendor Company : Specify the company in which the user is associated with. If the Vendor Company is not present, please raise a ticket to AskIT and MyAccess Support team will add the Vendor Company to the drop down. Country: User’s country. If suitable country is not found, please select the closest possible country. Company Mobile Phone Number : Please always specify phone numbers for users in the international format starting with “+” symbol. Example: +3226769325343 You can provide an additional phone number as well. Start date : Please select the date the user starts to be active. End date : Please select the end date (contract end date for example). End date cannot be more than 12 months from the Start Date. (will be changed to 13 days in the next release) Once the form is completed, click Submit button. This will navigate you to the next page where you can confirm the details. Check name and details carefully and click on Submit button again Approval request will be sent to the responsible KONE Contact. Request will be automatically approved if KONE contact creates record by her/himself After approval The Supplier-Distributor user profile is created in MyAccess and new user account information and credentials to KONE Network are delivered to the KONE contact via email. Note: Supplier-Distributors do not normally need KONE email and they should work with their own company email and license. Other accesses like PDM and SAP accesses needs to be requested same way as earlier. 5. How to edit Supplier-Distributor user data, close or extend contract? Go to MyAccess: https://myaccess.kone.com You will be landed on the home page of the MyAccess. 3.Click “Edit Supplier Distributor User” link on the home page In case you are not able to find the link, click on the drop down icon on the top left corner next to Home tab and you will be given options like My tasks, Manage Access, Manage identity. Navigate to Manage Identity -> Create Supplier Distributor. 4.Select the correct Supplier-Distributor user from the drop down and press Edit button. Please note: you can search only with first name, last name or MyAccess ID number 5. You will be navigated to the page to Edit the form for Supplier Distributor data. End date field appears as empty and current end date can be checked by clicking calendar icon. Select new end date. Please note maximum extension is currently 12 months. If you want to close accounts the date must be in the future i.e. earliest tomorrow. Once all the required changes are made in the form, press Submit button. Approval request will be sent to the responsible KONE Contact. Request will be automatically approved if KONE contact creates record by her/himself. KONE Contact (manager) and the user himself (CC) will get an email notification on 30, 15, and 1 day(s) before the end date. 6. How Supplier Distributor user account is disabled in MyAccess? Supplier Distributor user accounts are disabled from MyAccess after their end date. MyAccess identifies the end date and on the given date it automatically sets user as Inactive. Users KONE Network account (AD account) and roles assigned in MyAccess will removed from the user. SAP and PDM accesses will be also closed automatically. if User is re-activated in 7 days all the accesses he/she had will be activated again. The separately requested roles with ‘ Immediate removal of inactive members’ attribute value as true will be removed immediately after the account is disabled in MyAccess and the users needs to separately request the role back if they re-join KONE. Note : Accesses which are neither managed by MyAccess nor authenticated against AD are not automatically removed. Reminder is sent from MyAccess to the supplier’s/distributor’s KONE contact (manager) and the user himself 30, 15, and 1 day(s) before the end date. 7. How Supplier Distributor user Accesses are Removed in MyAccess? Supplier Distributor’s user’s separately Requested Accesses will be removed in MyAccess after 7 days of end Date. Access Removal will be processed in AD. 8. How Supplier Distributor user account is deleted in MyAccess? Supplier Distributor’s user account is deleted from MyAccess after 30 days from the end date visible in MyAccess. Deletion will be processed also in AD so that KONE Network account cannot be reactivated again. 9. How Supplier Distributor user account is reactivated in MyAccess? Supplier-Distributors account can be reactivated by changing the end date to the future in MyAccess if it has been disabled less than 30 days ago. If account is disabled more than 30 days ago it is already deleted from MyAccess and cannot be reactivated. In that case a new user account needs to be created.

short_description: How to Track request in MyAccess
introduction: How to Track request in MyAccess> How to see request for self in MyAccess? How to see request for others in MyAccess?
instructions: How to Track request in MyAccess? Note : Queue Name for My Access Support Issues is " kone.myaccess.support " Login to MyAccess URL: https://myaccess.kone.com After logging in you will be redirected to the home page of the MyAccess. 3.On the Home page you can see one of the tabs (Quick links) “ Track My Request ”, Click on that and you will be navigated to the below screen: OR You can also Go to the My Work Tab on the home screen and select Access Requests, it will navigate you to the same Access requests search page. 4.Employees can search the request of any other user by clicking on the filter option and entering name in the Requestee field and Apply the filter. 5.All the access requests submitted by the user’s name will appear on the screen. 6.In case of Contingent Workers and Supplier Distributors , they can only track their own requests which will appear directly on the screen . 7.Once the Access request appears on the screen, you can see the details of it by clicking on the details button: 8.Once you click the details, you will be able to see all the details of the request i.e. Request ID, Status, entitlement to which access has been requested and which all have been completed or failed. You can note the Access Request ID and it can be used for searching the request via request ID. Access request ID can be directly entered into search field and request will appear on the screen. In case an access request shows "Request pending", it may be pending for approval with/at any of the below persons/stages: Manager approval -> line manager (or supplier-distributor's KONE contact) must approve the request to add the access Named approvers -> one person from the defined approver group must approve the request to add the access Manager and Named approvers -> line manager (or supplier-distributor's KONE contact) must approve the request to add the access, after which one person from the defined approver group must also approve the request to add the access Please note that Manager approval step in scenario 1 and scenario 3 is always skipped if the person themselves is the manager of the person on behalf of whom the request is raised. This means that, if the requestor is the manager of the person on behalf of whom the request is raised, in scenario 1 the access will be immediately provisioned - and in scenario 3 only Named approvers approval is required. In scenario 2 and 3, if the requestor belongs to the group of Named approvers (for that specific role), then the “Named approvers” step is skipped.

short_description: How To Request Access in MyAccess tool and how to approve requests
introduction: How To Request for additional Access in MyAccess tool? What is the approval process in MyAccess?
instructions: How to Request Access/role in Myaccess tool; Distribution list membership, shared drive access and other roles. Document have the approval flow guidance document with necessary screeshots . Note : This document is for KONE Employees only Note : Queue Name for My Access Support Issues is "KONE.MYACCESS.SUPPORT" This document will help you with steps on how to request new access for any user. Snippets for each step are attached to help understand the steps better. Open the Myaccess tool from browser. Link to Myaccess is: https://myaccess.kone.com/identityiq/home.jsf 2.Home Page of myaccess looks like below. It has options to navigate to different tasks. It shows the name of the user logged in. 3.‘Manage User Access’ option is used to raise request for self and others. Hence, click on ‘Manage User Access’ 4.In the field for Search Users, type in the name or id of the user for whom you need to raise access request. Hit ‘Enter’ key or click on search icon. List of users with similar names will be seen on the page. 5.Filter Option can also be used to search a user(s) with specific details if the name is not known or to find a group of users with same value. 6.Select the specific user by clicking on the ‘tick mark’ besides user’s name. On selecting the button will be seen in green color. This indicated the user you have successfully selected. Click on Next button at the bottom of the page to proceed. 7.On this page of Manage Access, make sure “Add Access” is selected. In the Search Access box enter the Role name which needs to be requested for the user. Roles with similar names will be seen on the page. Each Role’s description and owner can be read below the Role name to be sure of the Role selected. 8.Select the specific access (Role) by clicking on the ‘tick mark’ besides Role name. On selecting the button will be seen in green color. This indicated the Role you have successfully selected. Click on Next button at the bottom of the page to proceed. 9.On the next page of Review and Submit, verify the access request being submitted for the user. Remove the ones not needed by clicking on ‘cross mark’ besides the Role name. After verification, click on Submit button to submit the request or Cancel to cancel the whole request. 10.Request once submitted will go the respective approvers for their inputs. Based on their approval, provisioning of access will happen. 11.Track My Request Option from Home Page will show all the requests raised by you and their respective status. 12. You can view the Request details by clicking on Details, see the status of the request, Cancel the request before it is completed and send an email to the approver by clicking on message icon. Snippet below shows all the 4 options. Approval levels can be: Manager 1 st level approver only Manager + 1 st level approver Manager +2 nd level approver Below is the snippet of the approval notification seen on the approver myaccess home page Approver can navigate to Approvals à select and review the request pending for his/her approval by clicking on the View option for each Request. Approver can also forward the request for some other person’s approval. This can be done by clicking on ‘forwarding arrow and entering the name of the individual to whom request should be sent for approval. Comments can be entered while forwarding the request. 2.After clicking on view, below will be the view of Request. Provide inputs for approving or rejecting the request. Approver can also enter the comments for his/her decision. 3.If the request is approved, corresponding access is provisioned to the user and notification is sent in email. 4.If the request is not approved, user is notified about the approver’s decision in email. Important Points : Multiple users can be selected if they all need same access. One user can be selected, and multiple access be selected for that user. It is not an option to select multiple users and different access for set of users selected.

short_description: How to perform SAP User Access Review in GRC?
introduction: SAP User Access Review (UAR) is an annual audit requirement to keep our SAP user’s accesses up to date and this KB Article explains the instructions on How to perform User Access Review (UAR) through user access reviewer notification mail and SAP GRC system.
instructions: OBJECTIVE: Upon completing this KBA, SAP user access reviewer / approver should have an understanding on how existing SAP user's access can be reviewed and take required actions to retain or remove access of users according to user's current job responsibilities. SAP User Access Review (UAR) instructions / steps: As part of yearly audit cycle, SAP user access reviewer / approver will receive an automated User Access Review (UAR) request notification mail(s) as shown in below screenshot with "UAR_REPORT"excel file as attachment which contains list of users in SAP system who has access to respective User Group / Role which need to be reviewed and take required actions to retain or remove. Open attached excel file from above notification mail and review the list of user's access to respective User Group / role in SAP system and take decision whether to retain (or) remove access to User Group / role to user as per respective user's current job responsibilities. Once user's access in excel file reviewed then review decision could fall into any of below four cases: Case 1: Approve to retain access to respective User Group / role for all users in UAR_REPORT. Case 2: Remove User Group / Role access for all users in UAR_REPORT. Case 3: Remove User Group / Role access for some of the users in UAR_REPORT. Case 4: Replace existing User Group / Role access with new User Group / Role for any user(s) in UAR_REPORT. Case 1> Approve to retain access to respective User Group / role for all users in UAR_REPORT . In order to approve to retain access for all users in "UAR_Report" excel file, approver / reviewer can approve the respective UAR request from GRC automated notification mail itself following either of below 2 options. Option 1: Click on Approve link in the notification mail and then click on "Send" in the new mail window that pops up. Option 2: Reply to the automated notification mail from GRC by only writing Approve (OR) Approved. Note: Kindly do not add any other text in the body of the mail apart from Approve (or) Approved. Important Note: While approving the User Access Review (UAR) request from notification mail following either of above 2 options, kindly do not add / change anything in the subject line of notification mail. Case 2: Remove User Group / Role access for all users in UAR_REPORT. In order to remove User Group / Role access for all user(s) in "UAR_Report" excel file, please follow below steps. Step 1: Open GRP Production (SSO) from SAP GUI log on pad. Step 2: Execute NWBC t-code. Step 3: Click on "Work Inbox" quick link under "My Home" tab. Step 4: Open "User Access Review (UAR) request" from work inbox by clicking on respective request. Note: If there are more than one User Access Review request for review then work inbox shows each request entry separately as shown in below sample screenshot. Step 5: Once User Access Review request opened, in order to remove User Group / Role access for all users in UAR request, click on Select All -> Remove Role -> Submit in the UAR request. Once clicked on Submit, UAR request will be marked as approved (= review and actions completed). Case 3: Remove User Group / Role access for some of the users in UAR_REPORT. In order to remove User Group / Role access for some user(s) in "UAR_Report" excel file, please follow below steps: Step 1: Open GRP Production (SSO) from SAP GUI log on pad. Step 2: Execute NWBC t-code. Step 3: Click on "Work Inbox" quick link under "My Home" tab. Step 4: Open "User Access Review (UAR) request" from work inbox by clicking on respective request. Note: If there are more than one User Access Review request for review then work inbox shows each request entry separately as shown in below sample screenshot. Step 5: Once User Access Review request opened, in order to remove User Group / Role access for some users, click on Select All -> Approve. Then select those users records and click on "Remove Role" one by one for whom access must be removed and Submit in UAR request following below instructions. As shown in below screenshot, Select All --> Approve Now, select those user records / rows for whom access to User Group / role should be removed in respective SAP system and click on "remove role." (To select multiple user records / rows, hold the control button and then select those user's rows for whom access to be removed.). Now, click on "Submit" to complete the UAR request review and actions in the request. Case 4: Replace existing User Group / Role access with new User Group / Role for any user(s) in UAR_REPORT. While reviewing the User Access Review (UAR) request, if the decision made to replace the existing User Group/role access with new User Group / role in respective SAP system, then you may approve access for respective user in UAR request. Once UAR request approved, you may send an e-mail to financeauthorization@kone.com with details: User ID, User Full Name, SAP System, Existing User Group / Role, New User Group / Role requesting for replacing existing User Group / role with new User Group / role. Note: 1 In case of any queries / issues with respect to SAP User Access Review and process, kindly contact / send an e-mail to financeauthorization@kone.com. Note: 2 List of t-code(s) / access present in respective User Group / role can be found in excel file available at below SharePoint location. Application Management Unit - User Groups to T-codes Mapping - All Documents (sharepoint.com)

short_description: How to Manage Exceptions in GRC Process Control ?
introduction: How to Manage Exceptions in GRC Process Control?
instructions: This document serves as a guide for the Process Owners in the Management of Continuous Monitoring Exceptions generated by SAP GRC PC 12.0 (GRC PC) Continuous Monitoring. It covers navigation and steps for managing Continuous Monitoring exceptions from an end-user perspective. Expected Audience: The guide is intended for the Process Owners and any other teams that are assigned to manage Continuous Monitoring exceptions. 1. Continuous Controls Monitoring Framework The Continuous Controls Monitoring platform (CCM) in SAP GRC Process Control 12.0 (GRC PC) enables KONE to automate the monitoring of controls and data (master and transactional data) that reside in SAP system(s). The CCM platform uses automated business rules to determine the exception data which is extracted from the underlying SAP ECC systems. Automated business rules are the means that enable SAP GRC PC to perform CCM. The business rules are based on KONE’s requirements. The CCM framework is the technical infrastructure in SAP GRC Process Control for creating/ maintaining automated monitoring rules and managing Continuous Monitoring exceptions from an end-user perspective. Automated business rules can cover most of the commonly encountered scenarios such as: Configuration monitoring Master data monitoring (relevant for tests related to master data) SAP reports (standard and custom) Transactional data analysis 1.1 Navigating the Work Inbox 1. Login to GRC Production (GRP) system using the SSO and then execute the t-code NWBC. From the Home page, navigate to the “My Home” work centre by clicking on the “My Home” link (the red square). 2. On the “My Home” screen, go to the upper left side of the screen and click on “Work Inbox”. 1.2 Evaluating the exception(s) In order to analyse the exceptions that have been automatically generated by SAP GRC PC, the Process Owner needs to open the workflow item in his/her work inbox. 3. Locate the workflow item task called “Remediate Exception: Automated Monitoring” and click on the task as indicated in below image. 4. The workflow task will open and the “Issue” will appear as initial screen (Refer to the next image). 5. The issue is automatically created by SAP GRC PC. SAP GRC PC automatically generates an issue name starting with the “System ID” followed by the “Business Rule description” (80 characters limitation). Refer to #2 in the next image. 6. The process owner can change the Issue priority level if required (Refer to #3 in below image). 7. In order to identify the exceptions that have been detected by the business rule, go to tab “Evaluation” (Refer to #1 in the next image). 8. The number of exceptions that have been detected by SAP GRC PC are highlighted in column “Exceptions” (Refer to #2 in “ Evaluation” tab in the next image). 9. In order to analyse the number of exceptions in detail the Process Owner needs to click on “Fail” (Refer to #3 in “ Evaluation” tab in the below image). 10. The process owner can select from a drop down list any of the deficiency fields that have been defined in the business rule in order to display the relevant exceptions (Refer to #1 in “ Evaluation” tab in the next image). Note : The process owner will need to select each individual field (one by one) in order to be able to see the related exceptions. SAP GRC PC can only display the list of exceptions per deficiency field (standard SAP GRC PC behaviour). 11. In order to identify the exceptions that have been generated by SAP GRC PC, the Process Owner can take a look at the “Details” section in the bottom of the screen (Refer to #2 in “ Evaluation” tab in the below image). 12. The following information is automatically captured as a part of the audit trail (standard SAP GRC PC behaviour): Change type: gives an indication if the field has been created, updated or deleted. Changed value: provides the ability to check the old value vs. new value of the changed field. Changed on (date): provides the field change date. Changed at (time): provides the field change time. Change by: provides the User Name (SAP User ID) of the person who performed the change. Note : When the changes have been performed in the DEV system and transported to the ECC production system, the “Change by” information will provide an indication of the Change Request number instead of the User Name (SAP User ID). 13. Once the list of generated exceptions have been reviewed and analyzed, the Process Owner needs to click again on the “Issues” tab (Refer to #1 in “Issue” tab in the next image). 14. Based on the analysis of the Exceptions (and offline enquiries with Functional specialist, IT team, etc.), the Process Owner will need to determine which actions should be undertaken. The Process Owner can choose one of the following actions: Assign Remediation Plan: in this case, the Process Owner has determined that the reported exceptions are valid ones and require corrective actions (Refer to #2 in “Issue” tab in the below image). Close without Plan: in this case, the Process Owner is not able to determine if the reported exceptions are valid/ invalid and prefers to reassign the task to another person within the organization (Refer to #3 in “Issue” tab in the below image). Reassign the issue: in this case, the Process Owner has determined that the reported exceptions are valid ones but don’t require corrective actions (Refer to #4 in “Issue” tab in the below image). Void: in this case, the Process Owner has determined that the reported exceptions are invalid ones (e.g. changes have been approved by an authorized person and consequently the exception reported by SAP GRC PC is correct but invalid) and consequently the Process Owner has decided to “Void” the exceptions (Refer to #5 in “Issue” tab in the below image). Note : If the Process Owner decides to “Assign Remediation Plan” or “Close without Plan”, the control rating (evaluation) will be automatically marked as " 2 - Requires Improvement or 1 – Ineffective" in relevant monitoring reports (the control rating will depend on the Issue level as this is a standard SAP GRC PC behaviour). Note : If the Process Owner decides to “Void”, the control rating (evaluation) will be automatically marked as "3 - Effective" in relevant monitoring reports (standard SAP GRC PC behaviour). 15. If the Process Owner wants to assign a remediation plan, a pop-up window will appear prompting him/her to specify the following information (Refer to #1 in “Remediation Plan” in the below image) : Plan Name Start Date of the Remediation process Due Date of the Remediation process (this will be linked to the Reminder and Escalation mechanisms) Owner of the Remediation plan: in most of the cases, the Remediation Owner will be the “Control Owner”. 16. Once all the relevant information is entered, the Process Owner should click “OK” to register the Plan in the workflow task (Refer to #2 in “Remediation Plan” in the below image) . 17. Once the Remediation Plan has been entered, a new tab called “Remediation Plan” is automatically created by SAP GRC PC (Refer to #1 in “Remediation” tab in the below image) . 18. Once the remediation plan is registered, the Process Owner can process the workflow task and send it to the designated Remediation Owner by clicking the “Submit” button (Refer to “Remediation” tab in the below image) . 2. Result You have successfully completed the guide on processing and managing exceptions in GRC PC. You will now be able to manage continuous monitoring exceptions generated by the SAP GRC PC tool.
