short_description: How to perform Segregation of Duties Review in SAP GRC
introduction: How to perform Segregation of Duties review in SAP GRC?
instructions: 1 Receive and Review SOD violations for users: KONE Segregation of Duties Review requires that: User rights are reviewed periodically by the Risk Approver to ensure that SOD violations do not exist for users in the KONE SAP system (i.e. ECC production system Z04). As result of the review, remedial actions are triggered via SAP GRC system. The review and the resulting actions are documented in SAP GRC. KONE periodic SODR makes this task easier for Risk Approvers: · A review campaign will be triggered centrally according to the defined review period. · All Risk Approvers will receive a corresponding task containing a report on the risk within his/her area of responsibility and respective User Group assignments causing SOD violations. · Risk Approvers can either approve or remove a User Group assignment, the removal will be done automatically by the system. · The Segregation of Duties (SODR) is recorded in the SAP GRC system as an audit trail. 1.1 Receive mail alert and Remove risks based on review The Risk Owner (SOD reviewer) receives the mail notification for the risks owned by them. Within GRC system, there is option to “Mitigate Risk” or perform “Actual Removal” – instructions for both options are provided in below sub-sections along with screenshots. Alternatively, Risk Owner, can, in consultation with the User Group Owner, choose to remove the risk-causing tcode(s) from the underlying User Group(s). This will require a Request to be raised with SAP Security EFS team for the change. Instructions are not being included in this document as there are separate Process documents for the same published by Change and Release Management team. However, for a quick reference on how to initiate this, please refer the below mentioned Knowledge Base Article: KBA2269 - How to create New Requirement to SAP Security team ? Link: https://konewayflow.lightning.force.com/lightning/r/BMCServiceDesk__KM_KnowledgeArticle__c/a2j1r00000AkDBHAA3/view 1.2 Assigning Mitigation Control to a User Login as Risk Owner to perform actions of SOD RISK REVIEWER as below: Reviewer will click on the link which is available in the email notification. Upon clicking on the link the below screen will be displayed (or) through below link login to the SAP GRC > Work Inbox > Click Request item. https://knecigrp9800.kone.com:44398/nwbc/?sap-nwbc-node=0000000328&sap-nwbc-context=03HM33B030D633D533D23336317009760C88770F72748EF70B77723630B43040C81919181B8080B17B508081B121880900&sap-nwbc-history_item=&sap-theme=sap_corbu Please access the Work Inbox link within the "Work Inbox" section. Click on the request for review and action accordingly. Initially identify the risks that must be mitigated. Then select them and click on the comments option next to collapse all and update comments accordingly. Comments will be updated as below: Now select the users to be mitigated and click on Mitigate risk. A new pop window opens with the respective mitigating control ids. Identify the respective mitigating control and assign accordingly. Select the user line items and click save. Click SAVE for Mitigation Control Entries and Comments to be saved in the request. Enter the comments for the entire request in the Comments tab Click on Submit once done. Automated closure notification to SoD reviewer / risk owner will be sent. Also an automated closure notification e-mail sent to KONE ISCTEAM- similar to above. Below screen shot shows the Mitigation Controls applied during review. 1.3 SOD Risk Reviewer removes the role assignment (User group) for the user Click on the Work Inbox to access the SODR Click on the required SODR for review and action The Risk shows on the top level with all users holding this risk listed under it. Select the respective user and Click Actual Removal on the above line of options Provide comments under Comments and Comments tab as well Click on Submit & Approve on the top left on the request Reviewing is done, now click on close button to exit from the window. 1.4 Forward risk to another Risk Owner In case responsibility for a risk is changing or has changed, you can forward to an alternative Risk Owner for review. It is advised to add a comment as to the reason for forwarding the review task. A comment can be added by clicking on Add Comments on the marked role. Provide a short comment for the new Reviewer. Click on the arrow to enter the Notes Enter the text as above into the input field. Proceed via the OK button. Now click on Other Action s and Forward to select the new Reviewer. Click on the look-up icon to search for the user you need. Enter user id into the active input field. Click on Go to run your search. In this example, choose RISKOWNER2 . The user is selected, confirm with the OK button. Forward the role to the new Reviewer by clicking on OK . The role is no longer with you. Click on Close to leave this window. The Alternate Risk Owner receives a mail notification of the new request forwarded to him as below. NOTE: In addition to notification to another Alternate Risk Owner, for information of Coordinator, notification is also sent to Coordinator. This is just for information and so no action is required by the Coordinator on the request here. 1.5 Initial Approver Submits the request returned by another Risk Owner Once the New Risk Owner takes the respective action and submits the request, an email notification will be sent to you. Use the link that is provided in this email to start processing. Reviewer will click on the link which is available in the email notification. Upon clicking on the link the below screen will be displayed (or) login to the SAP GRC > Work Inbox > Click Request item. Please access the Work Inbox link within the "Work Inbox" section as shown below. The Work Inbox shows that there is an approval request pending. Access the request via the Approval needed for SOD review request hyperlink. Review notes provided by the New Risk Owner, provide comments and submit the request. 1.6 Forward items to Coordinators (KONE ISC Access Management) for clarifications Forwarding request to Coordinator for additional information: In case you require additional information for a user, you update the comments against the specific user line item starting with the Coordinator id. This will help the Coordinator to identify his own action items and provide information for those respective users only in the request. In this example, we have chosen ARUN RACHARLA (Coordinator). It is required to action of either Actual Removal or Mitigate Risk for each line item and SAVE without fail. NOTE: The user id of the coordinator will be an id belonging to the KONE ISC team member while performing production users SoD review. Please use the same to forward the request to the coordinator. After updating the comments for the user, click Save, than select the user line item and Click on the Other actions, forward option to forward the request to the coordinator. NOTE: It is not possible to forward a single user to an alternate Risk Owner or a Coordinator. You can only update comments against each user and then forward the request to the alternate approver or Coordinator for additional information. Search and choose the user id of the coordinator using the Coordinator name displayed at the top of the window. Check option “forward with return” for you to receive the request back from the coordinator once he provides the inquired details in the request. Provide the requirement in the notes field as shown below. The request is forwarded to the Coordinator as below. The coordinator receives a mail notification of the forwarded request as below. NOTE: In addition to notification to coordinator, for information of Risk Owner, notification is also sent to Risk Owner. This is just for information and so no action is required by the Risk Owner on the request here.

short_description: How to use Job Monitor report in GRC PC
introduction: How to use Job Monitor report in GRC PC?
instructions: How to use Job Monitor report in GRC PC 1.1 Document Purpose This document serves as a reference guide monitoring of the Continuous Control Monitoring jobs in SAP GRC PC 12.0 (GRC PC) Continuous Monitoring. This job aid covers the navigation and steps to for using the Job Monitor report. 1.2 Instructions After a job is scheduled, the results are viewed in SAP GRC PC to identify whether the job was successful. The job results include the configuration changes. The menu path to view job results is as follows: GRC Portal Home page -> Rule Setup -> Scheduling -> Job Monitor. 1. Open GRP Production (SSO) from SAP GUI log on pad. 2. Execute NWBC t-code. 3. Navigate to Rule Setup è Rule Setup -> Scheduling -> Job Monitor as indicated in the figure below. 4. Navigate to the “Job Monitor” screen using the link provided as indicated in the figure below; 5. On the “Job Monitor” screen, populate the following search criteria fields: a) Job Name; (Refer (a) in figure below); b) Frequency (Refer (b) in figure below); c) Execution Date From/To (Refer (c) in figure below); 6. Click on the “Search” button (Refer (a) in figure below); 7. A list of all the scheduled jobs for the specified search criteria is generated as indicated in figure below. The “Status” field will show if the job has “Completed” and the “Deficiency Type” filed will indicate if the job is “Adequate” or not. (Refer figure below); 8. When the Deficiency type has the status as Adequate then the Deficiency Count will be Zero. This means the control monitoring did not detect any deficiency or issues as indicated in figure below; 9. The following options can be used on clicking the header of any column in the application: a) Drop down listing of all the line items in the list based on column header o An example for Job Name column is indicated in figure below. o Sorting of the line items in ascending or descending order b) User Defined Filter enables us to search for a specific line item by entering respective input. c) An example for Deficiency Type - High is indicated in figure below; d) The results are displayed as per filter selection above indicated in figure below; 10. Select and highlight the relevant job (refer (a) in figure below); and click on “View Results” (refer (b) in figure below); 11. The results for the specified job will be displayed as indicated in figure below; 2. Result You have successfully completed the process to monitor jobs. You will now be able to do continuous monitoring of these controls.

short_description: KONE FSM: handling the situation where technician is facing sync and pending upload issue due to the week network strength.
introduction: How to handle the situation where technician is facing sync and pending upload issue due to the week network strength.
instructions: Document deals with the handling the situation where technician is facing sync and pending upload issue due to the week network strength. Steps to resolve the issue Since, the 4G mobile network is completely controlled by the mobile service provider, there are very few mitigation steps to handle the situation. However, few below steps helps to get have better network and fix the sync and pending upload issue. 1) If the technician is facing network issue, then pls ask the user to go to the place where the network strength is good. OR if the user is in a building then request the user to near to the window or come out the building to have the better network. 2) If the above steps don’t fix the issue, then ask the technician to bring the mobile device into the flight mode from the setting and then come out from the flight after 15 min to get the better connection to fix the sync and pending upload issue. These 2 steps are work around and does not guarantee that it will fix the network, sync and pending upload issues.

short_description: Ask IT - How to troubleshoot in case of Laptop not turning up (HP 840 G3/G4 Laptops)
introduction: I am unable to switch-on laptop.
instructions: Please take machine name from user and go to below mentioned link to check what is the name of the HP Series to which that laptop belongs. Once machine name is entered here it will show the Series of Laptop along with the warranty status. URL : https://support.hp.com/us-en/checkwarranty If in case laptop is HP 840 G3/G5 Series please follow below mentioned steps as first steps of troubleshooting. Battery Reset procedure is simple and is equivalent of removing / replacing the battery and doing a deep power cycle. · Plug the laptop into mains power · Hold down Wi-Fi button together with mute button · While these buttons are held down also hold down the power button - hold down for 10-20 seconds · Release the power button while continuing to hold down other 2x buttons for an extra 2-5 seconds · Release all buttons The computer should now turn on normally. · For HP 830 G5 units – the F5 button replaces the (Wi-Fi +mute) buttons but the process is otherwise the same. Please ensure that above mentioned steps are getting followed before sending this to Onsite team.

short_description: Workbench usage to get user base for CSD
introduction: How to use workbench usage to get user base for CSD?
instructions: As it is not possible to report on users based on the assigned permission sets, the user base data for the application can be queried and exported from workbench with the following steps: 1. Login to " https://workbench.developerforce.com " 2. Go to queries -> SOQL queries 3. Select the object "PermissionSetAssignment" 4. Select 'View As' -> 'Bulk CSV' radio button to export the data to csv file format. 5. Query: SELECT AssigneeId,PermissionSetId FROM PermissionSetAssignment WHERE PermissionSetId = '0PS1r000001HNVJ' The id " 0PS1r000001HNVJ" is for the permission set: CS: Kone Customer Service. The id ' AssigneeId ' will return the user id's which have been given the basic access to Customer Service Desktop application ie.(perm set: CS: Kone Customer Service).

short_description: How to Approve SAP Access Request as a Risk Owner using GRC
introduction: How to Approve SAP Access Request as a Risk Owner using GRC
instructions: OBJECTIVE : On completion of the training, the Risk Owner should have an understanding on how to Approve/Reject Access Request at Risk Owner Stage in SAP GRC. Once an access request is created and approved by the Line manager, Role Owner and SoD Approver an email will be triggered automatically to the Risk Owner with details as below in case the requested accesses cause new critical actions access risks to user. Approve/Reject SAP Access Request as a Risk Owner directly from email: To Approve / Reject the SAP Access Request, approver needs to click on respective link. A new window will appear and there is a need to just click Send . With this action, you approve/reject all the listed Critical Action risk that will arise for the user based on the requested accesses. Alternatively, approver can directly reply to the email sent by GRC system by writing either Approve or Reject. Alternatively, to approve / reject request (OR) to partially approve / reject request from GRC work inbox , follow below instructions 1> Open GRP Production (SSO) from SAP GUI log on pad. 2> Execute NWBC t-code. 3> Click on "Work Inbox" quick link under "My Home" tab in browser screen opened after executing NWBC t-code. 4> Open request from work inbox for approval / rejection actions in access request. Once clicked on respective GRC request number link it will be redirected to the GRC SAP Access Request Page where he/she can see the list of SAP Roles requested. Note: Roles which have Risk, will come to the respective Risk Owner who is maintained in GRC System. As shown below, click on Risk violation tab to review the results of risk analysis for the role which is requested by the user. The Risk owner can change the display format of the report - between options like Summary, Detail, Management Summary and Executive Summary, which is shown in below screenshot. Clicking on the Access Risk ID will open a new pop-up window with description of the Access Risk. The Risk Owner can also change the type of report which he/she wants to see, like Permission level (Authorization object specific) and Critical Actions (Transaction Code specific). Once the risk analysis has been reviewed, the Risk Owner needs to click on User Access tab in order to partially approve the GRC request. For partial approvals, the Risk Owner needs to specify Approve / Reject in the Approval Column - as required, at the line item level for each individual role assignment as shown below. And then clicking on Submit. However, before clicking on Submit the Risk Owner will need to click on Comments tab to record approval/rejection related comments. If this is missed the system will show an error as comments are mandatory. Now the request is approved /completed and is hence no longer visible in the risk owner's GRC work inbox. Now you may close the request. If there are some more requests in work inbox, you can follow similar steps as explained above to approve / reject or to partially approve / reject the requests from work inbox. You may close the work inbox screen after required approval / rejections actions taken for requests in work inbox. You may log off from GRC, when you do not have any further actions with respect to Access Requests approvals following below steps. Click on “OK” to close the session If any Role to be Rejected please drop an email to the End User using below template. Template: In case, upon review this Access Request needs to be rejected, please send mail to User with the below details: GRC Access Request #: Role(s) rejected in Access Request: List of Critical Action(s) / T-Code(s) included in Rejected role(s): Rejection Reason: Rejected By: (User ID and Full Name) Further Action: Please check and raise a new access request for an alternate “SAP Role” having the required t-codes but which do not have above rejected t-codes. To find an alternate role, please refer to KB Article (Link to be added here once KB Article created) for step by step instructions. If you do not find an alternate SAP Role for the rejected role, you may raise / initiate a KONE Way Change Request to create a new SAP role with all required t-codes (but excluding the above rejected t-codes). If you are facing any difficulties in finding alternate SAP Role or raising KONE Way Change Request, please send an email to KONE_SAP_Security_GRC@KONE.COM with all above details for assistance.

short_description: How to select Alternative role for required Transaction code
introduction: How to select Alternative role for required Transaction code?
instructions: Objective: On completing the training user should be able to find the alternative role for required Transaction code. Let us take a case where user need access to T-code: FB03 and submitted a GRC request for the role: ZECCD_SS_FI_FIN-FUNC_ASC. After submission, request will be forwarded to approvers. After Line Manager, Role owner and SoD approvals request will be forwarded to Risk Owner. If the role ZECCD_SS_FI_FIN-FUNC_ASC has Critical Actions/Transaction Codes (Let us say F.01) then Risk Owner will Reject the role. Then Risk Owner will send an E-mail to End User with the Below Details. GRC Access Request #:XXXXX Role(s) rejected in Access Request: ZECCD_SS_FI_FIN-FUNC_ASC List of Critical Action(s) / T-Code(s) included in Rejected role(s): F.01 Rejection Reason: Request has critical actions/transaction codes Rejected By: (User ID and Full Name) To find the alternative role for required Transaction code, u ser need to use the following link: https://knecigrp9800.konenet.com:44398/sap/bc/webdynpro/sap/zkone_end_users# Below screen will appear once user clicks on the above link. Once user selects the Landscape as ECC, user will get more options as shown below. To find the T-code and Roles mapping click on the option " Find Role to Transaction Cod Mapping ". Below screen will appear, when the user clicks on Find Roles to Transaction code Mapping option. In this case, user need access to Transaction code: FB03. Enter the Transaction code as FB03 and click on Search. User will get the list of roles which are available in SAP. In this case, we have 2 roles which contains FB03 transaction code. ZECCD_SS_FI_FIN-FUNC-DP_ASC ZECCD_SS_FI_FIN-FUNC_ASC Since the role: ZECCD_SS_FI_FIN-FUNC_ASC was already Rejected by Risk Owner, due to the critical Transaction codes (T-codes), let us check for the other role (ZECCD_SS_FI_FIN-FUNC-DP_ASC) for the list of T-codes available. We need to make sure that this role doesn't contains any Rejected T-codes which was provided by Risk Owner in the email (Let us say this case F.01). Now give the Role Name as ZECCD_SS_FI_FIN-FUNC-DP_ASC in the Role field and remove the T-code name inthe Transaction code field and click on Search as shown below. If we scroll down and check all the Transcation codes available in the role, we shouldn't have F.01. After confirming that this role doesn't have F.01, we can add this role to the current request. Role will be added to the request, click on Submit Access Request option to submit the request. Incase if user do not find an alternate SAP Role for the rejected role, you may raise / initiate a KONE Way Change Request to create a new SAP role with all required t-codes (but excluding the above rejected t-codes). If user is facing any difficulties in finding alternate SAP Role or raising KONE Way Change Request, please send an email to CommonPlatforms.ServiceDesk@kone.com with all above details for assistance.

If the service request is related to data management , reports and dashboards , set up or customizations, Click on Maintenance Operations and select from the following options: Field Service Management (FSM) - Data Managemen t- Use this form if you need to import, export, delete or update mass records in the FSM database. short_description: How to change translation on standard objects, standard fields, custom fields in salesforce?
introduction: How can we change translation on standard objects, standard fields, custom fields in salesforce?
instructions: Salesforce provides translation labels out of the box only for standard components (tabs, objects, fields etc.) Depending on the user’s language preference, these components will automatically start showing in the preferred language. Salesforce also allows to add/change translations from ‘Translation Workbench’ and ‘Rename tabs and Labels’. Translation Workbench: “Translation Workbench” is used to specify translated texts for custom components like custom fields, picklist values etc. For translation using Translation Workbench, go to: n Setup ->User Interface -> Translation Workbench -> translate. n Select the language that you need to search translation for, then select the Setup Component and finally the object. Under ‘Master Field label’, can be found in the original value. Under ‘Field label translation’, the translated value can be given. For details on translatable components from workbench, you can refer: https://help.salesforce.com/articleView?id=translatable_customizations.htm&type=5 #NOTE: If mass translation is required, the translations have to be done by exporting .stf file. Refer KBA3039 Rename Tabs and labels: “Rename tabs and fields” is used to rename standard objects and standard fields in a particular language. This not really a “translation” feature, but more of a workaround for standard objects and fields. For translation using ’Rename Tabs and Labels’, go to: n Setup -> User Interface -> Rename Tabs and Labels 1. Select the language in which the translation needs to be changed. 2. Select object whose labels translations needs to be changed. 3. In step 1, the tab name for the object can be changed. 4. In step 2, the standard field labels and other labels can be changed.

short_description: Adding mass translations from workbench?
introduction: How can we add mass translations from workbench?
instructions: For changing/ adding mass translations, we need to export the .stf file, change translations and import it to salesforce. Steps: Go to Setup -> Translation Workbench -> Select radio button : Bilingual -> Export 2. The user will receive an email with link to download the translations files as zip. Or go to documents tab where the files will be available. 3. Open the zip file and click on 'View File' to download the file. The file will be downloaded and extract the file. 4. Open the .stf file with Notepad++ and copy the data to an excel. excel: The key gives the key field, labels are the original value for that field and translation gives the translation for that label. Out of Date should be given as '-' The translations can be changed/ added in the Translation column. Label should not be changed. 5. Once the translations are done, copy the content of excel to .stf file and save the file. 6. Go to Setup -> Translation workbench -> Import -> Chose the .stf file and import to salesforce org.

short_description: How to load data from SAP to sales force for new country activation
introduction: How to load data from SAP to sales force for new country activation
instructions: We have 3 kinds of data to load from SAP system. 1) Equipment 2) Service orders 3) Invoices These three data we will get from the sales force team. They will extract the data from SAP and provide us. 1) Equipment data: To load the equipment we have a buffer table in SAP. Table is ZPM_MSOFT_EQUI. We need to upload the data in this table. We can upload the data using the program Z_PM_T_EQUIMAS_SEND. Please check the table after uploading the equipment through this program. Job Z_PM_T_EQUI01_SEND is already scheduled which will take the data from above mentioned table and push the data to sales force. Program: Z_PM_T_EQUIMAS_SEND Execute the program and provide the equipment number in batches of 2000 at a time. Execute it foreground. Now check the table ZPM_MSOFT_EQUI, all equipment uploaded using above program will be there and once the job has run, the data will be sent to sales force. To verify this, please check the table for the equipment, also you can ask sales force team is they can find the equipment in their system. 2) Service orders: For service order also we have a buffer table ZPM_SEB_IORDER. We can directly upload the service order into this table and a scheduled job SEND_IORDER_PRODBATCH in every 20 min which will fetch the data from buffer table. Table: ZPM_SEB_IORDER Click on maintain and then new entry. This can be done using LSMW as well if you have a lot of data. Job runs in every 20 min, please check and the table to verify the data else you can sales force team for the same. 3) Invoice: For invoice data we have a program: Z_SD_T_INVOIC which will send the IDocs. Choose select invoice documents and provide the billing document in batch of 1000 at a time. Also mention the receiver partner as MULESOPRD and job parameter. Schedule a job in background. It will create the invoice IDocs. Program: Z_SD_T_INVOIC Execute the program and provide the invoice number in batches of 1000 at a time and date. Provide the receiver partner and job name. Execute the job and schedule in background. Check in SM37 for the job status. Once the job is finished verify from sales force team for the invoices.

Creating Knowledge Article: The CS users can create knowledge article by two ways: i) From Knowledge List View tab: Go to Knowledge list view -> New ii) From CS case lightning page: From Case Detail Page -> Knowledge -> New Article 2. If the user has access to multiple knowledge record types, the user has to select the record type as: “CS Kone Care Knowledge” 3. The Agent can submit the knowledge article for Approval and submit for translation from the buttons on the article page: 'Submit for Approval' for approval and 'Submit for Translation' for translation. For Approval: If Agent submits Knowledge Article for approval: It will assigned to the knowledge approver queue and the Knowledge Managers(queue members) for that country will receive an email. Also check the ‘Send Email Notification’ checkbox. The assigned translator will receive an email as following: From the knowledge link as given in the email, the translator can open the translation draft. The translator can change the edit this article, make translations and publish the translated articles. The agents can view the translated article by selecting the language.

short_description: Escalated Status in CSD case
introduction: How can an internal admin/ Customer service case be escalated?
instructions: Steps for case escalation: Manual: 1. status should be in progress 2. case classification should be mandatory 3. CS&A should be filled 4. Below details need to provide escalated to escalated function escalated comment escalated check box Escalation by email: 1.goto email & files 2. send email 3. escalated function should be filled 3. A new CS Task will be created whenever the case is Escalated. Actions to be taken by Escalated to user: The User can either send a reply email to the email received when case is escalated which will change the status of task created to Completed automatically and the status of case will be changed to Open. The Agent can also manually change the status of task to Completed which again will the status of case will be changed to Open automatically. SLA for escalated status: The SLA of the case keeps on running for the escalated status normally. 4.When Feedback Request status for Data Request was changed to ‘resolved’ and there are no other Feedback Request records in status ‘new’, related to the same case, Data Request Status automatically is changed back to ‘Sent to SSC’ and Case Status is changed to "Escalated". 5.On successful http request to send Data Request data to KPM, CS_Data_Request_Status__c is set to ‘Sent to SSC' and case Status is set to 'Escalated’.

short_description: TOORMA – Import and Update Tirak Data
introduction: TOORMA – Import and Update Tirak Data
instructions: To import and update tirak data in the TOORMA application, follow below steps: 1. Open the workforce planner application and on top, go to menu Forms -> Manage working units -> select the working unit for which you need to update the tirak data (E.g. Singapore) 2. Next, go to File -> Development -> Edit knowledge for active window. The dialog with knowledge base for the selected working unit will open. 3. Lock the knowledge base before editing to prevent other users from editing the same KB, and potentially losing your changes. To lock, right click on KB with name ‘ WP_Definition ’ and click on Lock. You will see the next to the KB row turned into showing that the KB is locked. 4. Once the KB is locked, right click on it (WP_Definition) and choose Edit. The knowledge base editor dialog will open. 5. Go to table “TirakList”, right click and choose Excel -> Import from Excel. 6. Choose the excel from which you want to import the data from and click on Open. You will get a pop-up message asking for confirmation of overwriting the table. Click on Yes to continue. 7. Once the data is imported from excel, it will be reflected in the table. Also, you will notice a save image next to the table name. Click on button ‘Apply’ to save the table, and then on button ‘OK’ to close the ‘Knowledge base editor’ dialog. 8. Once the ‘Knowledge base editor’ dialog is closed, you will have to save the knowledge base. To do so, right click on the KB (WP_Definition) and click on Save. You will get the ‘Dave knowledge base’ dialog. 9. Kindly add a meaningful description, mentioning what you changed in the KB, and click on button OK. 10. Once the KB is saved, the next to the KB row will turn into showing that the KB is unlocked and is again available for other users to edit it. You can now close the knowledge dialog. Once the tirak data is imported into the table, it needs to be synced. To synchronize it go to menu File and click on Synchronize tools data . The tirak data will be updated and synchronized in the application. NOTE: If the data is not in the suitable format, Quintiq will not accept it, and will throw error when trying to import it. Hence to be sure to the format, I would suggest exporting the template from Quintiq, by going to the KB -> table TirakList, right click and choose Export -> Export to file.

short_description: FSM User Creation
introduction: How to do user setup for FSM users?
instructions: There are five types of FSM users: • FSM Admin User • KC3 Agents • Technician Users • Supervisor Users • THD Agents 1. FSM Admin Setup Profile User needs to have KONE: Field Service Administrator profile Role There are two different level to Admin Role in FSM: a) Suggested Profile for the FSM Country Admin is the top for the country's hierarchy, e.g. FSM: Netherlands or FSM: Belgium. b) For global admins it is FSM: Field Service Management Permission Sets Assign the following Permission Sets to the Country Administrator user: • FSM KONE Local Admin Permissions • FSL Admin License • ADBC Read External Objects (for access to archived records) For FSM Global Admin also assign permission set: • KONE: Field Service Administrator • FSL Admin Permissions instead of FSM KONE Local Admin Permissions 2. KC3 user setup Profile The KC3 user needs to have the "KONE: KC3 Agent" profile assigned. Make sure that the profile has the System Permission "Field Service Standard" enabled, as this gives the user access to the Field Service Lightning specific objects in Salesforce. The external KC3 users need "KONE: External KC3 Agent" profile assigned. Role Please refer to User Profiles and Public Groups for the correct Profile for the KC3 user. Permission Sets Assign the following Permission Sets to the KC3 user: • FSL Agent Permissions • FSL Dispatcher License • FSL Dispatcher Permissions Permission Set Licenses The permission set licenses will be automatically assigned via the Permission Sets. Make sure the KC3 user only has the following permission set license assigned: • Field Service Dispatcher Sales Organization The Sales Organization of the KC3 user must be populated with the right value (picklist) to reflect the Sales Organization the KC3 user is part of. This value is used in several areas of the application. Service Cloud User The Service Cloud User checkbox must be checked on the user record to give the user access to KONE Service Console. Knowledge User The Knowledge User checkbox must be checked on the user record to give the user access to the Knowledge base. Call Center Assignment The Call Center lookup needs to be populated with "Avaya CRM Connector 2.0" call center. User Territories To give the KC3 user the correct permissions for using the Field Service Dispatch board (Gantt), create for each Service Territory the KC3 agent should have access to a User Territory record: 1. Navigate to All Tabs -> User Territories 2. Click New to create a new record 3. Select the KC3 user 4. Select the Service Territory 5. Click Save 6. Repeat for every Service Territory. 3. Technician user setup Profile The Technician user needs to have the "KONE: Field Service Technician" profile assigned. Make sure the that profile has the System Permission "Field Service Standard" enabled, as this gives the user access to the Field Service Lightning specific objects in Salesforce. Role Please refer to User Profiles and Public Groups for the correct Profile for the Technician user. Permission Sets Assign the following Permission Sets to the FSM Technician user: • FSL Mobile License • FSL Resource License • FSL Resource Permissions • FSL Dispatcher Permissions • This is needed in order to be able to perform automatic rescheduling of Service Appointments (i.e., access FSL Scheduling API) as well as running Resource Schedule Optimizations (RSO) based on the actions performed by the Technician • FSM Parts Request • Only needed for technicians who will be creating parts requests Permission Set Licenses The permission set licenses will be automatically assigned via the Permission Sets. Make sure the KC3 user only has the following permission set license assigned: • Field Service Mobile • Field Service Scheduling Sales Organization The Sales Organization of the Technician user must be populated with the right value (picklist) to reflect the Sales Organization the Technician user is part of. User Language "User Language" field needs to be set corresponding on which service territory user is associated. Eg. in Belgium this field needs to be set as "French". Service Resource Create a Service Resource record for the Technician user. You need to have system administrator or FSM Administrator permissions in order to do that. 1. Navigate to All Tabs -> Service Resources 2. Click New to create a new record 3. Use the Technician's full name as Name 4. Select the Technician user 5. Add the mobile phone number of Technician 6. Check the Active checkbox 7. Check the "Include in Scheduling Optimization " checkbox 8. Select correct value for "Time Entry Code Permission Group" field 9. Select the correct Storage Location (van stock) 10. Optional select Pay Calendar Override if the technician has to follow pay period that if different to one defined for country or collective agreement. See Pay Calendars 11. Click Save After service resource is saved, add skills using the Skills Editor. NOTE: Make sure not to add the Prevent scheduling before required parts are available skill to any technicians! 4. Supervisor user setup Profile The Supervisor user needs to have the "KONE: Field Service Supervisor" profile assigned. Make sure the profile has the System Permission "Field Service Standard" enabled, as this gives the user access to the Field Service Lightning specific objects in Salesforce. Role Please refer to User Profiles and Public Groups for the correct Profile for the user. Permission Sets Assign the following Permission Sets to the Supervisor user: • FSL Agent Permissions • FSL Dispatcher License • FSL Dispatcher Permissions • FSM Timesheet Approval Permission Set Licenses The permission set licenses will be automatically assigned via the Permission Sets. Make sure the Supervisor user only has the following permission set license assigned: • Field Service Dispatcher Sales Organization The Sales Organization of the Supervisor user must be populated with the right value (picklist) to reflect the Sales Organization the Supervisor user is part of. This value is used in several areas of the application. User Territories To give the Supervisor user the correct permissions for using the Field Service Dispatch board (Gantt), create for each Service Territory the Supervisor agent should have access to a User Territory record: 1. Navigate to All Tabs -> User Territories 2. Click New to create a new record 3. Select the Supervisor user 4. Select the Service Territory 5. Click Save 6. Repeat for every Service Territory 5. THD User If the supervisor is working as a THD user also in the territory, then assign the permission set "Technical Helpdesk " to him for the access of THD functionalities. Profile The THD Agent user needs to have the "KONE: HelpDesk Agent" profile or "KONE: Troubleshoooter V1" assigned. System permission "Field Service Standard" has to be enabled. In order for FSM Work Orders and Service Appointments to be visible to THD agent, the following conditions must be met: - User must have "FSL Agent Permissions" and "FSL Dispatcher License" assigned - Sharing settings must be in place for WO and SA objects Existing CRM users acting as THD have a "Technical Helpdesk" permission set. Role Please contact local THD Key User or THD Manager for the correct Profile for the THD user. User needs to be able to see service appointments and work orders. Permission Sets Assign the following Permission Sets to the THD user: • FSL Mobile License (for THD technicians) • FSL Resource License (for THD technicians) • FSL Resource permissions (for THD technicians) • FSL Agent Permissions • FSL Dispatcher License • FSL Dispatcher Permissions Permission Set Licenses The permission set licenses will be automatically assigned via the Permission Sets. Make sure the THD user has the following permission set license assigned: • Field Service Dispatcher Sales Organization The Sales Organization of the THD user must be populated with the right value (picklist) to reflect the Sales Organization the THD user is part of. This value is used in several areas of the application. Additional user licenses Service Cloud User The Service Cloud User checkbox must be checked on the user record to give the user access to KONE Service Console. Knowledge User The Knowledge User checkbox must be checked on the user record to give the user access to the Knowledge base. Service Resource Setup For each THD agent who need to act as a technician, create a service resource record. This action is to be performed by System admin or country admins for each ST. • Use the THD Agent full name as Name • Select the THD Agent user record • Add the mobile phone number of Technician • Check the Active checkbox • Un-check the "Include in Scheduling Optimization " checkbox • Select correct value for "Time Entry Code Permission Group" field • On Resource Schedule Optimization section, disable all the fields • Check if mobile is online before RSO • Run RSO on Rejection • Run RSO on SA scheduling • Run RSO on Absence • Those parameters are checked when running RSO for a SR to optimize his daily plan. • which is not needed for THD expert. Please refer to the following link to visit FSM Wiki for more details: https://kone.sharepoint.com/teams/FSM/Wiki/Home.aspx

short_description: SMT 3.0 - How to grant SMT access to user?
introduction: SMT 3.0- How to grant SMT access to user?
instructions: SMT application access : For providing SMT application access, we have two permission set: 1. ‘SMT 3.0’ permission set : Permission set to grant SMT 3.0 users acces 2. ‘SMT 3.0 System Admin’ permission set : Permission set to grant SMT 3.0 System Admins access to all SMT-generated records - for maintenance purposes. Note : Below process should be followed whenever SMT User access SR comes to AMS When the user selects User Type = ‘Supervisor’ then do the following: • Change profile to ‘KONE: Field Service Supervisor’ • Assign ‘SMT 3.0’ and ‘Lightning Enabled’ permission set • Make sure Sales Organization in User Details is correct • Set the user as owner of the Service Territory specified in “Planner Group Responsibility”. • Add additional permission set from “Other permission sets if needed”, if any. When the user selects User Type = ‘Other’ then do the following: • Assign ‘SMT 3.0’ permission set only • Make sure Sales Organization in User Details is correct • Add additional permission set from “Other permission sets if needed”, if any. SMT key users list will be updated occasionally whenever any new key user will be added. The list can be accessed from the Notes and Attachments section of this knowledge article. Steps to follow to give SMT3.0 permission set to the user : Check if the user is having User License = Salesforce, if not then access cannot be provided. Check if the SR is raised by key user from the SMT key user's list. If not, then check the requested user's country and based on that , look for the SMT key user for that country in the key user list. Take approval over email from the SMT key user for that country, keeping the client's email in cc (The person who has raised the SR). Once approved, provide all required access and close the ticket by notifying over email with proper resolution details.

Click on [Change] beside Owner field and provide the name of the user who needs to be the owner of the planner group. 3. Click on 'Send Notification Email' checkbox and save the record. The user will be set as owner of that planner group. Note : SMT key users cannot be set owner of any planner group. Contact Francesca/Joe if key users request for ownership of any planner group. O nly the real supervisors should be set as owner.

short_description: Ask IT - Software Center is not loading, Failing to load
introduction: Software Center is not loading, Failing to load
instructions: First step will be to check if machine is available in AD or not to check for Domain Issue. If available in AD then proceed for below mentioned steps. Also please ensure machine is connected in KONE Network. 1) We need to take the user’s machine remote and go to the Services.msc. Open Services.msc as Admin and then we need to stop the SMS_AgentHost Service and then we need to restart it. And after that on the User’s Machine go to the Control Panel ----- Configuration Manager------- Actions Tab and run the below mentioned policies. After that clicked on OK button and wait for 10-15 Minutes. Policies to be run in the sequence in which they are mentioned. A. Machine Policy Retrieval & Evaluation Cycle. B. Application Deployment Evaluation Cycle C. User Policy Retrieval & Evaluation Cycle. D. Discovery Data Collection Cycle. 2. Now check if Software Center is loading or not. If not, then proceed for Step 3. 3. You can check the site code on the machine For this take the user’s machine remote go to the Control Panel ----- Configuration Manager------- Sites Tab you can find the site code. Please click on Find Site. If Site Code is incorrect or Blank, it will automatically detect it and update it. If it is correct, it will let you know that as well. Once done please wait for next 10-15 minutes and then check. 4. If still does not work, then You can increase or manage the Cache size. For this take the user’s machine remote go to the Control Panel ----- Configuration Manager------- Cache Tab Click on Configuration Settings. You can manage the cache size by click on the bar below “Amount of Disk Space to user (MB)”. You can also delete the temp folder of windows %temp% to increase the size. In most of the cases The Software Center will be install on user’s machine after some time, but if still the Software Center is not installed so please follow KB number KB000A4860 to re install the SCCM client, in case you are still not able to fix so kindly raise ticket with SCCM (device Management)

short_description: FSM specific Sandbox Refresh instruction.
introduction: FSM specific post refresh steps to ensure all user details are available and WO integration is working as expected. After the FULL and INT sandbox refresh with the production data, following two problems are reported: 1) The FSM users setup (used by testing team) are no more exist. 2) The images are broken in the service report template. 3) The WO integration between SAP and FSM is broken. Under such scenario, the testing team is blocked to continue with their regression testing.
instructions: Knowledge Artifact Document deals with taking the backup of the FSM test users and reinstating them, fixing the image issue on the service report template and fixing the WO SAP-FSM integration after the FULL and INT sandbox refresh. Pre-Requisite: 1) Data Loader should be setup on the desktop of the deployment team 2) Deployment team should have the admin access (Profile: System Administrator, KONE: Field Service Administrator) of the Business Org sanbox. 3) Testing team need to provide the username of all the KC3, technician and supervisor users before sandbox refresh. Resolving steps for FSM user backup and re-instating them back: 1. Extracting Supervisor Details: To extract the technician user details from the sandbox before refresh. Execute the following query to extract details in the .csv file format a. Get the owner id of all the active service territories: Query : SELECT Active_In_FSM__c, Id, IsActive, Name, OwnerId FROM ServiceTerritory WHERE Active_In_FSM__c = true AND IsActive = true b. Extract the supervisor user details: Query: select Id,Employeeo,WEEKLY_LOGIN__C, USERPERMISSIONSSUPPORTUSER, USERPERMISSIONSKNOWLEDGEUSER, USER_LANGUAGE__C, USER_COUNTRY__C, USERROLEID, USERNAME, TIMEZONESIDKEY,SAP_INSTANCE__C, SALES_ORGANIZATION__C,SALES_OFFICE__C, Risk__Selected__c,REGION__C, PROFILEID,LOCALESIDKEY,LANGUAGELOCALEKEY, KCOL_Account_ShowTime__c,IS_SALES_PERSON__C,IS_MAINTENANCE_SUPERVISOR__C, IS_AUTHORISED_TO_SEE_CMII__C,ISACTIVE,LASTNAME, FIRSTNAME,FEDERATIONIDENTIFIER, EXCLUDE_FROM_KCOL_PERM_BATCH__C,EMAILENCODINGKEY,EMAIL,COMMUNITYNICKNAME,CAMOS_USER__C,AREA__C,AM_PM_USER__C,ALIAS from User where id in ( ids extracted in the query a) c. Extract all the permission sets for all the users with the below query: SELECT AssigneeId,PermissionSetId FROM PermissionSetAssignment where AssigneeId in ( ( ids extracted in the query a) 2. Extracting Technician Details: To extract the technician user details from the sandbox before refresh. Execute the following query to extract details in the .csv file format from different objects. a. User: Query: Select Manager Id,Employee No,WEEKLY_LOGIN__C,USERPERMISSIONSSUPPORTUSER,USERPERMISSIONSKNOWLEDGEUSER,USER_LANGUAGE__C,USER_COUNTRY__C,USERROLEID,USERNAME,TIMEZONESIDKEY,SAP_INSTANCE__C,SALES_ORGANIZATION__C,SALES_OFFICE__C,Risk__Selected__c,REGION__C,PROFILEID,LOCALESIDKEY,LANGUAGELOCALEKEY,KCOL_Account_ShowTime__c,IS_SALES_PERSON__C,IS_MAINTENANCE_SUPERVISOR__C,IS_AUTHORISED_TO_SEE_CMII__C,ISACTIVE,LASTNAME,FIRSTNAME,FEDERATIONIDENTIFIER,EXCLUDE_FROM_KCOL_PERM_BATCH__C,EMAILENCODINGKEY,EMAIL,COMMUNITYNICKNAME,CAMOS_USER__C,AREA__C,AM_PM_USER__C,ALIAS from User where username in ( copy the username from the excel) b. Extract all the permission sets for all the users with the below query: SELECT AssigneeId,PermissionSetId FROM PermissionSetAssignment where AssigneeId in (ids extracted in the query a) c. Service resource (API: ServiceResource) SELECT Anonymize__c,City__c,Country__c,CurrencyIsoCode,Description, FSL__Efficiency__c, FSL__GanttLabel__c,FSL__Online_Offset__c,FSL__Picture_Link__c, FSL__Priority__c,FSL__Travel_Speed__c,FSM_Actual_Duration__c,FSM_Deviation_Duration__c,FSM_Estimated_Duration__c,FSM_RSORequestAcknowledgement__c,FSM_RSO_CheckIfMobileIsOnline__c,FSM_RSO_EnableLogging__c,FSM_RSO_RunForTechnicianWhoRejectedSASV__c,FSM_RSO_RunWhenAbsenceIsCreated__c,FSM_RSO_RunWhenSAIsScheduled__c,FSM_Run_RSO_forWorkAddedByTechnician__c,Id,IsActive,IsCapacityBased,IsOptimizationCapable,Language__c,LastKnownLatitude,LastKnownLocation,LastKnownLocationDate,LastKnownLongitude,LastModifiedById,LastModifiedDate,LastReferencedDate,LastViewedDate,LocationId,Name,Off_Duty_DirectTime__c,Off_Duty_TimeEntry__c,OwnerId,Pay_Calendar_Override__c,Pay_Calendar__c,Phone__c,Postal_Code__c,RecordTypeId,RelatedRecordId,ResourceType,Sales_Organization__c,ServiceCrewId,Street__c,SystemModstamp,Time_Entry_Code_Permission_Group__c,Travel_At_Day_StartEnd_Is_Not_Work_Time__c FROM ServiceResource Where RelatedRecordId in ( record id of the records extracted from query a) d. Shift Details (API: Shift): SELECT CurrencyIsoCode, EndTime,Id,Label,LastReferencedDate, LastViewedDate, ServiceResourceId, ServiceTerritoryId,ShiftNumber, StartTime,Status,StatusCategory, SystemModstamp,TimeSlotType FROM Shift Where ServiceResourceId in ( record id of the records extracted in query b ) e. Work Center Assignment (API: FSM_Work_Center_Assignment__c): SELECT ConnectionReceivedId,ConnectionSentId, CurrencyIsoCode, End_Date__c,Id,Name,Preference_Type__c, Service_Resource__c, Start_Date__c, SystemModstamp,Work_Center__c, Work_Type__c FROM FSM_Work_Center_Assignment__c Where Service_Resource__c in ( record id of the record extracted in query b) f. Service Resource Skill (API: ServiceResourceSkill): SELECT CurrencyIsoCode,EffectiveEndDate,EffectiveStartDate,Id,LastReferencedDate,LastViewedDate,ServiceResourceId,SkillId,SkillLevel,SkillNumber,SystemModstamp FROM ServiceResourceSkill Where ServiceResourceId in (record id of the record extracted in query b) 3. Extracting KC3 Agent Details: Extract the Kc3 user details from the sandbox before refresh. Execute the following query to extract details in the .csv file format from different objects. a. User: Query: Select Manager Id,Employee No,WEEKLY_LOGIN__C,USERPERMISSIONSSUPPORTUSER,USERPERMISSIONSKNOWLEDGEUSER,USER_LANGUAGE__C,USER_COUNTRY__C,USERROLEID,USERNAME,TIMEZONESIDKEY,SAP_INSTANCE__C,SALES_ORGANIZATION__C,SALES_OFFICE__C,Risk__Selected__c,REGION__C,PROFILEID,LOCALESIDKEY,LANGUAGELOCALEKEY,KCOL_Account_ShowTime__c,IS_SALES_PERSON__C,IS_MAINTENANCE_SUPERVISOR__C,IS_AUTHORISED_TO_SEE_CMII__C,ISACTIVE,LASTNAME,FIRSTNAME,FEDERATIONIDENTIFIER,EXCLUDE_FROM_KCOL_PERM_BATCH__C,EMAILENCODINGKEY,EMAIL,COMMUNITYNICKNAME,CAMOS_USER__C,AREA__C,AM_PM_USER__C,ALIAS from User where username in ( copy the username from the excel) b. Extract all the permission sets for all the users with the below query: SELECT AssigneeId,PermissionSetId FROM PermissionSetAssignment where AssigneeId in (ids extracted in the query a) c. User Territory (API: FSL__User_Territory__c) Query: SELECT CreatedById,FSL__ServiceTerritory__c, FSL__Territory_Name__c, FSL__User__c FROM FSL__User_Territory__c where id in ( record id of the records extracted in query a) 4. Upserting the Supervisor details: a. Ensure to activate all the service territories which are extracted in the step 1.a by setting isActive = true and Valid From = today’s date. b. Upsert the supervisor user detail in the user object. These user details are extracted in the step 1.b c. Upsert the PermissionSetAssignment as extracted in step 1.c d. Set the owner of active service territories as extracted in step 1.a 5. Upserting the Technician details: a. Upsert the technician extracted data in the following sequence: i. Users ii. PermissionSetAssignment iii. Service resource iv. Shift v. Work Center Assignment vi. Skill b. After inserting the data, open each service resource record and click on the “Process Shift” button available in the Shift tab of record feed. 6. Upserting the KC3 details: a. Upsert the KC3 agents’ extracted data in the following sequence: i. User ii. PermissionSetAssignment iii. User Territory. Resolving steps for fixing the service report template: 1. Go to setup à service reports. 2. Edit the active template. 3. Double click on the user added image 4. A new pop-up screen will open up and then click again on the user added image: 5. Copy the URL on a note pade and change it with the sandbox URL: Example: https://kone--c.eu12.content.force.com /servlet/servlet.ImageServer?id=015w0000005RwR9&oid=00D3E0000009nRO&lastMod=1497508473000 The above link is taken from FULL environment, but it very much looks like production URL. This URL need to be changed into FULL env. URL. After changing the URL, it will look like: https://kone--full.cs82.my.salesforce.com/ servlet/servlet.ImageServer?id=015w0000005RwR9&oid=00D3E0000009nRO&lastMod=1497508473000 6. Repeat the setps from 2 to 5 for all the remaining active service report template. Resolving steps for fixing the FSM-SAP WO Integration: 1. Raise SAP AMS ticket to pause interface the job “job Z_SD_T_EXPORT_FSM_SERV_ORD”. If it is the FULL sandbox refresh then the job need be paused in Z03 else if it is INT sandbox refresh then the job need to paused in Z02. 2. Get the most recent ‘Service Order Number’ from Z02/Z03 for the date when the sandbox is activated. 3. Search the service order Number in the salesforce sandbox and determine the created date of the work order. 4. Extract the work order data from the sandbox using data loader with the below query: QUERY: Select id, Service_Order_Number__c, WorkOrderNumber from workorder where recordtypeid = 012w0000000V9N1AAK AND CreatedDate >= <created date determined in step 3> AND CreatedDate <= <today’s date> Example : SELECT id, Service_Order_Number__c, WorkOrderNumber FROM TimeSheet WHERE CreatedDate > 2020-01-10T14:00:01.000Z AND CreatedDate <= today Please note if the difference in dates is more than 5 days then do the multiple extractions by splitting the date in multiple queries. 5. Open the csv files created in the Step-4 and follow the below steps: · create a new Service_Order_Number__c and apply the following excel formula =CONCATENATE("ZZZ", RIGHT( D2 ,8)) · Here D2 is the service order column field · Ensure to apply this formula for the rows of the excel. · This will create a new set of service order number which are unique and does not exit in SAP. · Delete the old Service_Order_Number__c column from the file. 6. Upsert the updated file (containing id, Service_Order_Number__c) using the data loader in workorder object in the sandbox. 7. Repeat the step-5 for all the files which are extracted as part of the step-4. 8. After updating the work order records, we need to extract the work order line item records. Please use the below query for the work order line item. QUERY: Select id, Operation_SAP_Key__c from WorkOrderLineItem where CreatedDate >= <created date determined in step 3> AND CreatedDate <= <today’s date> Example : SELECT id, Operation_SAP_Key__c FROM WorkOrderLineItem WHERE CreatedDate > 2020-01-10T14:00:01.000Z AND CreatedDate <= today Please note if the difference in dates is more than 5 days then do the multiple extractions by splitting the dates in multiple queries. 9. Open the csv files created in the Step-8 and follow the below steps: · Create a new Operation_SAP_Key__c column and apply the following excel formula =CONCATENATE("ZZZ", RIGHT( C2 ,18)) · Here C2 is the Operation_SAP_Key__c column field with the existing values. · Ensure to apply this formula for the rows of the excel. · This will create a new set of Operation_SAP_Key__c which are unique and does not exit in SAP. · Delete the old Operation_SAP_Key__c column from the file. 10. Upsert the updated file (containing id, Operation_SAP_Key__c) using the data loader in WorkOrderLineItem object in the sandbox. 11. Raise SAP PO ticket to activate the PO3/PO2 to ensure the WO data is flowing from SAP to FSM. Give the reference of ticket# IN00758193 12. Create an SAP AMS ticket to refresh the certificate in SAP to ensure the outbound message flow from FSM to SAP. Give the reference of ticket# IN00759374

short_description: ECM - How to request access to ECM (M-Files) through MyAccess Tool
introduction: How to request access to ECM (M-Files) through MyAccess Tool
instructions: 1. Open the MyAccess tool from the browser. Link to MyAccess is: https://myaccess.kone.com/identityiq/home.jsf 2. Home Page of MyAccess looks like below. It has options to navigate to different tasks. It shows the name of the user logged in. 3. ‘ Manage User Access ’ option is used to raise request for self and others. Hence, click on ‘Manage User Access’ 4. In the field for Search Users, type in the name or id of the user for whom you need to raise access request. Hit ‘Enter’ key or click on search icon. List of users with similar names will be seen on the page. 5. Filter Option can also be used to search a user(s) with specific details if the name is not known or to find a group of users with same value. 6. Select the specific user by clicking on the ‘tick mark’ besides user’s name. On selecting the button will be seen in green color. This indicated the user you have successfully selected. Click on Next button at the bottom of the page to proceed. 7. On this page of Manage Access, make sure “Add Access” is selected. In the Search Access box enter the Role name which needs to be requested for the user. ECM access roles and key user details can be find here ECM MyAccess Roles & Key User List 8. Select the specific access (Role) by clicking on the ‘tick mark’ besides Role name. On selecting the button will be seen in green color. This indicated the Role you have successfully selected. Click on Next button at the bottom of the page to proceed. 9. On the next page of Review and Submit, verify the access request being submitted for the user. Remove the ones not needed by clicking on ‘cross mark’ besides the Role name. After verification, click on Submit button to submit the request or Cancel to cancel the whole request. 10. Request once submitted will go the respective approvers for their inputs. Based on their approval, provisioning of access will happen. Keyusers can approve from this approvals box and also see notifications from the bell icon. 11. Track My Request Option from Home Page will show all the requests raised by you and their respective status. 12. Approver can navigate to Approvals --> select and review the request pending for his/her approval by clicking on the View option for each Request. Approver can also forward the request for some other person’s approval. This can be done by clicking on ‘forwarding arrow and entering the name of the individual to whom request should be sent for approval. Comments can be entered while forwarding the request. 13. After clicking on view, below will be the view of Request. Provide inputs for approving or rejecting the request. Approver can also enter the comments for his/her decision. 14. If the request is approved, corresponding access is provisioned to the user and notification is sent in email. Please Note once your access request is approved, it may take up to 24 hours for the access to reflect in ECM. 15. If the request is not approved, user is notified about the approver’s decision in email. Important Points : 1. Multiple users can be selected if they all need same access. 2. One user can be selected, and multiple access be selected for that user. 3. It is not an option to select multiple users and different access for set of users selected

short_description: Ask IT - HR tools (Workday and Workday help) related ASKIT cases.
introduction: Why can't I login to Workday or Dovetail?
instructions: Check ALWAYS attributes first: Login password for Workday is the same as with KONE AD. Employee is complaining they cannot log into Workday. When you open MyAccess and go to their application accounts, you notice, that email (mail) is present in Active Directory, but is missing from Workday account (EMAIL_ADDRESS_WORK) or the mail is different. -> solution is to writeback email from AD to Workday Mail, KONE Network ID and UPN attributes can be checked in a following way in MyAccess: mail in Active Directory account = EMAIL_ADDRESS_WORK in Workday account This is email address UserPrincipalName in AD = USER_ID in WD Commonly referred to as UPN. In form resembles email address, but in truth is a separate attribute, a sort of username by which you log into Workday and other systems. sAMAccountName in AD = KONE_Network_ID in WD This is your Network ID, for new hires it consists of letter k and their employee ID Fields are matching --> open ticket at AskHR Fields are NOT matching --> dispatch to MyAccess team and write back the data Data origin Master for HR data (for example name details / phone numbers) is Workday. MyAccess is a master of following details integrated to HR tools (Workday, Workday Help): Mail (only @kone.com mails) KONE Network ID (sAMAccountname) UPN (userPrincipalName) MyAccess is a master of Display Name, which is to follow name details from Workday Issues Typical HR tool (Workday, Workday Help) related ASKIT cases: User cannot login to Workday / Workday Help User is missing mail / KONE Network Account from Workday There is a mismatch with mail between MyAccess and Workday Ticket routing Only KONE internal employees can access HR tools like Workday, Workday Help or Percipio. All of these 3 attributes (Mail, KONE Network ID, UPN) in AD details should have matching record in Workday details. If any of the 3 attributes is missing, incorrect or need a change, assign ASKIT case to MyAccess team . If Display Name change is requested, assign ASKIT case to MyAccess team. If all records exist and are correct, ask user to contact ASKHR If local HR application pls check actions from Local HR applications list below If any other HR related issue, ask user to contact ASKHR. Other HR Applications UK: ADP (payroll) - Ask user to contact local payroll Percipio (external learning platform) - Ask user to contact AskHR

short_description: How to delete an Object in PDM?
introduction: HOW TO BULK DELETE OBJECT/RELATIONSHIP FROM PDM.
instructions: PREREQUISITE Read the request carefully, ensure that the request has all required information Check the material state & SAP transfer history in PDM Note : 1. If the history says that it has transferred to SAP then it should not be deleted, If it is history says that it has not transferred then we can delete and note that SAP transfer history will be only for material not for other objects 2. If the request is the created by the Person in Charge then proceed to delete the request. If request is created by others then we need person in charge approval. 3. If the master system is "Windchill" then object might be deleted in master system initially. Without deleting the object in master system we can't proceed to delete the object in PDM. Also, once after deleted the object in master system please seek the Person in charge approval and go for an approval. PROCEDURE Login in VariClient For Bulk Deletion of Material and Document First we need to break the Relationship (Is Part & Has Part) Steps to Delete Relationship: The below steps need to be followed. Step 1: Open the Object in Variclient Finder. Step 2: Copy the Object ID from the finder. It might be looks like this. [For Ex: document:51394130D03/en/P.1] Step 3 : Open a new finder and go to Relationship toggle. Now Search for the Source Object and paste the Object ID then delete the Relationships. Do the same for the Target object. Step 4 : Open the finder and open the Object. Now delete along with the files. Note : For Document Kind, files also needs to be deleted. Confirm the same by clicking OK. Step 5 : Object will be deleted. Note : 1. If the Object approval task is linked. That object approval task and related relationships also needs to be deleted. 2. For bulk relationships created. Please try to delete from inside the Structure. 3. Also, please ensure with User again if the objects linked with more relations. (for example : more than 50,000 relationships). 4. If the Object (especially item) linked with the Base object, the configuration root also might be deleted. and if the root is linked with so many configuration, Person in charge is responsible for the Bulk Configuration deletion. 5. For some cases, if the object was linked with one or two objects --> we can have an option to remove it from Backend.

short_description: The RSO job is getting failed many times for different reasons.
introduction: How to prevent the situation of RSO failure by making appropriate configuration changes by user?
instructions: Preventing Steps: There are many instances in the past where the RSO got failed for many different reasons. There are few configuration changes for the user (FSM Admin) that can prevent the RSO failure: 1) For any given service resource, to make any change in the “Service Territory Membership” (STM), always make the changed in the shift. It can be adjusting the existing shits records or creating shift records. After adjusting the shift records, click on the “Process Shifts” button available on the update shift tab of the feed. Do not make any change in the “Service Territory Membership” (STM) records. This will many times cause the failure of the RSO job. 2) The time period of the “Service Territory Membership” (STM) must be at least 24 hours long. So always create the shift records with minimum 24 hrs. period and then press the “Process Shift” button. 3) Do not make any changes in the “Shifts” or “Service Territory Membership” (STM) records in between of the day for the same day. i.e. if the technician is working on any given day then don’t adjust the “Shifts” or “Service Territory Membership” (STM) records for the same day. This will create breaks in the “Service Territory Membership” (STM) records for the same day. 4) Please ensure that the “Shifts” or “Service Territory Membership” (STM) records have the end date/time. The blank end/time of the “Shifts” or “Service Territory Membership” (STM) records can also cause the failure of RSO/Optimization job.

introduction: Master Knowledge Article for working with Verizon: (contains links to all other Verizon knowledge articles) How to get in contact with Verizon Service Desk? Which incidents go to Verizon? How to open a ticket with e-bonding to Verizon? How to do service troubleshooting?
instructions: Verizon contact details Please call these numbers for NOC call support +441189054060 (UK) - NOC 000 8000016095 (India Tollfree) ) - NOC Please call these number to escalate to Verizon MIM team to expedite the resolution 000 800 0016349 - VERIZON MIM (India Tollfree) Which incident tickets go to Verizon? Tickets linked to LAN, WLAN , Internet access or office network access (WAN) should be sent to Verizon. Please be aware that Office Network (WAN) connections are still under transformation, the below link shows which sites are still managed by previous vendors (Telia or AT&T). Master site list: See article KBA3222 at link https://konewayflow.lightning.force.com/lightning/r/a2j1r00000DD2TxAAL/view There are two types of tickets for Verizon services: • Reactive tickets – an end User reports an incident to the KONE AskIT - Verizon refers to this as ‘Reactive’ • Proactive tickets – Verizon is proactively monitoring all network components in KONE Office Network and the Network Event Mgt. monitoring detects and automatically reports incidents - Verizon refers to this as ‘Proactive’. These incidents are sent as Proactive tickets also to KONE RemedyForce. How to open a ticket at Verizon (Reactive tickets)? Use ebonding for opening tickets to Verizon, never use email! If ebonding is broken, please call Verizon service desk. Detailed instructions for opening Verizon incidents in KBA3223: https://konewayflow.lightning.force.com/lightning/r/a2j1r00000DD2U2AAL/view Verizon incident communication What do the Status updates mean? See article KBA3221 at link https://konewayflow.lightning.force.com/lightning/r/a2j1r00000DD2TsAAL/view Verizon to Remedy incident e-bonding For Guidelines: See article KBA3224 at link https://konewayflow.lightning.force.com/lightning/r/a2j1r00000DD2U7AAL/view Ebonding issues? short_description: User Information - Office Network - How to open ticket at Verizon
introduction: How to open ticket at Verizon?
instructions: KONE - Verizon incident communication uses integration (ebonding) between RemedyForce and Verizon ticket tool. OPENING A NEW INCIDENT TOWARDS VERIZON (REACTIVE TICKETS): For incident communication always use the ebonding via Remedy Force, do not email Verizon. Before opening a ticket to Verizon, please check that there are no proactive tickets yet opened for the same issue (typical if network is down for a site). If proactive ticket is there, follow Major Incident instructions in KBA3225. 1) open a new ticket 2) assign it first to AskIT queue and save 3) use the "Impacted site ID field" to identify the correct site where the issue is. It is important to check with the end user that are they reporting the issue for the site which is marked as their office by AD or for another site. 4) make sure the "Service" is Office Network 5) create a vendor reference ticket (Queue: Verizon Interface Queue and Staff: Mulesoft Staff) 6) use the template " Ask IT - Network (LAN/WAN/Wireless/Internet Access)" and copy the template information into the vendor reference ticket "Comment to vendor" section once the vendor reference ticket has been created 7) *If for some reason you can't get the vendor reference ticket the ebonding is probably broken and please call Verizon to open the ticket. Additionally open a ticket to Remedy Force team to report the issue with ticket opening. Please see the following Video if you have any hesitation about the ticket opening KBA3224 article https://konewayflow.lightning.force.com/lightning/r/BMCServiceDesk__KM_KnowledgeArticle__c/a2j1r00000DD2U7AAL/view

short_description: Office Network - Verizon to Remedy incident e-bonding - guidelines -
introduction: How to work with Verizon - Remedy e-bonding? What to do if issues in e-bonding with Verizon?
instructions: VIDEO TUTORIAL: The attached video is a tutorial for opening a ticket to Verizon using RemedyForce Console: it describes the creation steps as well as the key step for handling an ebonded ticket. KEY THINGS TO REMEMBER: -Open ticket by placing it to AskIT queue (and save) -Add impacted site ID -Verizon Interface queue and Mulesoft as staff and save ticket again -Remember to use template: Ask IT - Network (LAN/WAN/Wireless/Internet Access) and copy the information of the template to the vendor reference ticket as a new "comment to vendor" The attached Visio files explains the step-by-step for both proactive tickets (originated by Verizon) and reactive ticket (originated by KONE)

short_description: Office Network - How to involve Verizon to Major Incidents
introduction: How to Involve Verizon to Major Invidents?
instructions: AskIT Instructions Major Incidents: What should AskIT do in case of a MIM related to Office Network: 1) Create the ticket in Remedy force as P2 based on business impact reported by the user Or if there is an existing incident ticket opened as self service upgrade it to P2 as incident 2) Contact users by phone to validate the site ID where the impact is happening (Note: users do not know site ID's so you need to ask the office address and map that to a site ID ) 3) Check also with the user the AskIT-Network template questions and answer to them (needed to all Verizon tickets but extremely important to give sufficient information in MIM cases). Copy answers to comment to vendor section in the ticket. *********************************************** 1. Issue start Date and time ? (CET) 2. Were there or are there currently any Site power issues ? (has power been verified on the site?) 3. Any change/update to user’s devices (laptop, desktop) ? 4. Any other service/application not working? 5. Number of affected users ? 6. Best time to contact the user :- (with timezone) 7. Mode to contact the user? phone/email/chat - 8. Does user speak English? Y/N ? 9. Local IT availability at the site ? Y/N ? Network information :- a. Ping test results (from end user machine) - b. Machine IP Address - c. MAC Address - d. Trace route - e. Error messages and applications impacted - 4) Assign the ticket to Verizon Interface queue 5) Call HCL MIM team and inform them to invoke the MIM process Instructions for HCL MIM team for engaging Verizon MIM team: IMPORTANT : MIM tickets meant for Verizon must NOT be moved to KONE MIM team queue otherwise the ebonding will be broken!! ebonded tickets can ONLY be locate either in Verizon Interface Queue or in any of the Ask IT queues 1) Please call Verizon MIM numbers below and provide Verizon (ETMS) ticket ID: • Freephone 00800 5786 2514 + Pin No. • US Domestic: 855-786-2514 + Pin • Terminating +44 118 905 4478 + Pin No. • For all please use KONE PIN number: 10651 2) Send the meeting invitation to ETMS@verizon.com with the Verizon ticket number in the subject heading. The Verizon MIM will join the KONE Microsoft team bridge and will involve the Verizon NOC or Security Team when needed. Verizon NOC team might have an internal call, or a Verizon call with Third Party or carrier. Verizon will not involve the Verizon Third Parties or carriers on the KONE MIM call, unless absolutely necessary (for example for testing). What to do if Verizon MIM is not joining the bridge on timely manner Call again the Verizon MIM number again. Updates on tickets No status emails from Verizon MIM to KONE Management team needed. Updates should all be communicated by KONE MIM or on the call. Unless other communication strategies agreed during the call. E.g. if resolution is taking long time it can be agreed that VZ MIM will send email updates to KONE MIM and the call is closed. Major incident closure As soon as the Major incident has been resolved and a problem ticket has been opened, the Verizon service Desk will close the Verizon ticket. The Verizon ticket can not be resolved and/or closed by a Third party or Ebonding. Within KONE, any major incident ticket (P1 or 2) needs to be closed by a KONE user. The ticket can not be closed by a third party or Ebonding. The result will be that resolution of the ticket will have to be agreed in the major incident bridge. After this agreement, both parties will resolve (which stops the SLA timer) and close their own ticket using their own parameters. At Closure of major incident – the problem ticket will be raised by the MIM, and if applicable, placed in the Verizon Problem management queue.
