short_description: How to perform Role import and Role Update in SAP GRC
introduction: How to import and update SAP roles into GRC system?
instructions: · Login to GRC system and goto transaction code: NWBC · Go to Access Management Tab --> Role Mass Maintenance à Role import · Give the below selection criteria: Note: Source system will be Z04 for production system · After clicking on Role import the above form will open · Role type: Technical Role · Role Attribute Source: User Input · Role Authorization Source: Backend System · Application Type :SAP · Landscape: KONE SAP ECC · Overwrite Existing Role: Yes( for old roles) · Role From: <role name to be imported> · Methodology Status: Complete · Click on Next · Once clicked we move to step 2(select role data) · Attribute Selection: User defined Attributes · Project Release: as per project · Role status: Production · Business Process: Basis( for all ECC roles) · Subprocess: GRP · Functional area will be as per region/country eg, china · Select list of Owners/Approvers for roles · While assigning owners check “assignment approver and role content approver” · Make sure user id is added to “Access control owners” table( explained in detail in KBA<kBA name>) · Click on next · We can preview the roles before submitting as shown above · Click on next · If number of roles are less, you can schedule it as Foreground job, if number of roles are more, you can schedule it as a Background job. · Click on Submit. The status of role import will be shown as above. Role update is used to update roles in GRC , i.e, changes in Role owners Pre-requisites : User to be maintained as role owner, must be present in GRC systems. Must be defined as a " Approver " in Access Control Owner table Steps to be followed to maintain an user id in Access control owner table Go to nwbc-->Setup-->Access control Owner Click on create button: Maintain as Role owner and save. Steps for Role owner update: Go to nwbc-->access management-->role update Click on Add: Select All roles that are to be updated: Select Criteria after clicking on Next. Here we are updating the role owner, so select the Attribute as Owners. Action: ADD/Update/Delete: If we are adding a new role owner, select add, incase of replacement of old owner with a new owner select update and in case of deletion select Delete. After giving the Selection criteria, click on next. We now need to select the values. In this step, we need to provide the Old Approver name as well as New Approver name. Replace with new role owner(mark both Assignment approver and Role content approver) and click next. We can see the list of roles, which are successful and unsuccessful (if the roles didn't match the selection criteria, role owner can't be updated.)

short_description: How to change the Unit of Measure in PDM
introduction: How to change the Unit of Measure in PDM
instructions: Approval: Please get the Person in-charge approval before proceeding the unit of Measure change. Pre check: First we need to check whether the material is sent to sap. short_description: Ask IT - How to Request for Power BI License
introduction: Dear User, Power BI licenses are available through MyAccess . If you want to use Power BI, please read Power BI at KONE , and request access via MyAccess and the Role " Microsoft Power BI Pro License ". Power BI Development Models Citizen Development: Business users with minimal technical skills can create reports and dashboards using Power BI’s drag-and-drop interface, pre-built connectors, and simple data transformations. IT provides a Pro license, but you are responsible for everything else, including support. Advanced Citizen Development: Users with some technical expertise can leverage Power Query, DAX (Data Analysis Expressions), and custom visuals for enhanced data modeling and reporting. If your report requires frequent updates (e.g., more than eight times a day) or involves large datasets , it falls under this category. IT provides the platform and governance, but you remain responsible for report content, support, and related infrastructure costs. IT-Supported Development: For complex data models, governance, security, and large-scale deployments, IT teams manage platform development and support, ensuring data accuracy and compliance. For further queries please reach out to 'bi.team@kone.com'

short_description: Proximity inventory Set-up
instructions: Procedure This document will show you how to set up the frontline inventory runs for both the service vans and the main branch offices. These are run based on an Excel schedule provided to GD by the business. The reason GD is involved is the fact that in order to set up the variants to run by the SAP program, you have to have access to SE38. Step 1 – Excel Schedule There are two specific individuals that control the inventory process for the frontline in the USA. They are KEISMEE and KEISRAJ3 based out of ISC in India. These user ID’s will be used so remember them when setting up the batch jobs. Both of these individuals or Jennifer/Tom will send you an Excel sheet for the schedule. The Excel sheet will give you a list of branches and vans that will run each week for the inventory process. You use the information to set up the jobs then according to their schedule. The “planned count date” is always on a = Monday except specific requests. The “actual batch job run date” is always the = Sunday (right before this specific Monday) except specific requests. We always start the first batch job to run at 11 am, then each successive batch job is set up to run 5 minutes apart from each other. (i.e., so on a Sunday they would start at 11:00:00am, then 11:05:00, then 11:10:00, and so on and so forth). Step 2 – using SE38 Use SAP transaction SE38 to set up the program variants each week. The variant contains specific dates, so you need to set up each individual branch and van to run in their own variant. In order to get access to SE38, you must use FIREFIGHTER in Z04. So, for GD this is a normal way to access SE38. Then you run the transaction, you will use program = ZUSMR016. Then you can start to set up the details within the variant based on the information that you get from the Excel schedule you received from the business. I have used in the past a standard way of naming the variant. This is how it works. If the name of the variant is for example = BPI VAN 4243. Then this means = Branch Physical Inventory Van 4243. If the name of the variant is for example = BPI BR 240. Then this means = Branch Physical Inventory Branch 240 (or for Los Angeles). Also for some Branches there are sub branches, in that case do not forget to include the Main Branch number along with the numbers for all the subs, these can be either defined as a range or independent values in the definition of variant. Save each variant with this naming convention since you will use this later when you actually set up the batch job using SM36. A sample variant is shown on next page: Step 3 – using SM36 After you have completed setting up the specific variants for that week, you can now set up the batch job which will use those specific program variants to run the specific branch and vans for that week according to the Excel schedule received by the business. In SM36 you will start at this screen below --- this is where you put in the consistent naming convention for the job name. You then select = icon on toolbar of SM36. You will get this screen then where you will enter the user ID of the person who controls the inventory document. It will either be KEISMEE or KEISRAJ3 for BRANCHES and VANS. By putting in their user ID it lets them process the output of the batch job which is a job they will then execute in SM35 in order to get their inventory documents. As you can see above, the USER field holds the user ID of KEISMEE or KEISRAJ3. I put in the physical inventory program name = ZUSMR016. I put in the specifc name of the program variant that I wanted to run inventory for which is = BPI BR 0230. Which means = BRANCH PHYSICAL INVENTORY for BRANCH 0230. I then select this icon in the dialog pop up box screen = I can then set the print program settings to what is shown below for all VANS and all BRANCHES exactly the same each time. The output of the job processing goes out as a pdf attachment in the mail to the relevant user who can download the same and print at his place. I then select the green check mark to go to the next step. I then select the icon to save my settings in this specific variant for VAN 3102. After I select the SAVE icon, it will then show me this screen below --- I just select again right away the and then I see the next screen below where I can then set up the date & time for the batch job to run. On the screen above you can then select the icon where you can then set up the date and time for the batch job. You will then see this pop up dialog box below --- You can then select the icon in this pop up dialog box. It will then allow you to set specific date and time details as show below. As I said before, we always set up the batch job date for a SUNDAY prior to the Excel sheet schedule which is always showing the “planned count date” for the inventory. So, in this case, my Excel sheet said --- So, my SUNDAY date prior to this MONDAY date is = May 31, 2015. The first job will start at the hour of 11am on that SUNDAY. So, as you can see, this time is = 11:00:00. We use military time for all batch job set up. We then select for our job to be saved for that inventory run. Then select immediately again for the final job to be saved and released into the schedule. You will get the “Job Released” message at the bottom of the SAP screen which lets you know you were successful in setting up the batch job. Job BPI BR 0230 saved with status: Released I then always to into SM37 and pull up all of the batch jobs set up that day and verify the total quantity set up with the total quantity in the Excel sheet to make sure they match. You can run SM37 to find your batch jobs on the SUNDAY you have specified as shown below. Once you have run SM37 with these settings you will then get this result show below. You will get the “Job Released” message at the bottom of the SAP screen which lets you know you were successful in setting up the batch job. Job BPI BR 0230 saved with status: Released I then always to into SM37 and pull up all of the batch jobs set up that day and verify the total quantity set up with the total quantity in the Excel sheet to make sure they match. You can run SM37 to find your batch jobs on the SUNDAY you have specified as shown below. Once you have run SM37 with these settings you will then get this result show below. short_description: How to delete queues in CSD ?
introduction: How to delete queues in CSD?
instructions: Queue deletion Before deleting the queue, we need to find all the references for the queue in the org and update the same in the excel attached such that all the references are in place and deletion is easier. Step 1 . Find all references manually as well as through GIT and update the same in the excel sheet. Run below GIT command to find queue reference in apex code in the org. git grep -n 'queue name' Step 2. Once all references are found, start deleting it one by one. There are manual as well as deletion through GIT defined for all the references as below : a) For Public Group - Take approval over email before deletion. By using git, create a deprecated package and delete it. b) For Case Assignment Rule - By using git, create a deprecated package and delete it. c) For Email-to-Case - Find auto-forwarding for email-to-case ( Email Services Address ). It should not be part of salesforce service address but should be to a another shared mailbox and this shared mailbox reference should be there im email-case setup only then delete the queue , Deletion is manual. d) For Apex code - Find the references in apex code , if reference found in apex code , then its is not in AMS scope e) For List View - If manually created,it would not be maintained in GIT so Deletion is manual, else Deletion is through GIT f) For Approval process, Delegated Admin Group and Sharing setting - By using git, create a deprecated package and delete it. g) For Knowledge Approval Process , if the queue is referred then this has to be replaced with the new queue (has to be asked from Key user) , if key user doe not provide the alternate queue , it can not be deleted as this will hinder knowledge approval process.

short_description: How to export date to spreadsheet (Excel format)
introduction: How to Export to Spreadsheet?
instructions: All SAP time reports can be exported to an Excel spreadsheet. The purpose of exporting the SAP time reports is to retain, sort AND use critical data WITHOUT having to re-run the SAP Time Reports repeatedly or print the report. Execute the T-code from where data need to be export (Ex: TCJO4 Table data). 2. From the Menu bar of the Transaction, CLICK on the Export button. 3. From the list dropdown box Spreadsheet. 4. In the pop-up box, select Formats (Excel) When the ‘Save As’ pop-up appears, save your document to your Desktop. TYPE the desired name of your report in the ‘File Name’. Save as type EXCEL files (*xls), and click on Save. button.

short_description: Windchill users removal working instruction & Windchill ProjectLink license removal working instruction
introduction: How to remove inactive users in Windchill? How to remove Windchill ProjectLink license?
instructions: Windchill users removal working instruction User removal process Windchill licensing has been changed. Amount of nominated users affect directly to the license costs. As a solution to this, inactive users need to be deleted from Windchill During the 2019 feasible cleaning horizon was found to be 6 months. In case the user activity in Windchill is older, the user shall be deleted. As of now deletion interval is quarter year Main steps Create user activity report from Windchill Process report data Exclude the users lately added (no login activity yet) Notify via email the users that are about to be removed Remove users from Windchill Remove users from KONE AD group ‘ADPROEUSERS’ 1.Create user activity report from Windchill: Step 1/4 Step 2/4 Step 3/4 Step 4/4 2.Process report data: Step 1/3 Step 2/3 AuditData.xlsm - https://kone.sharepoint.com/:x:/s/SOLUTIONCREATION-ITEC/EW4VHgtYYipKvBblU_Ecj9UBWkXK1a4KNywNtIPHqGT9fg?e=Hui2fT Step 3/3 3.Exclude the users lately added (no login activity yet) 4.Notify via email the users who are deleted This communication for sending the users will be taken care by Makela Klaus / Halter Claudio / Koskinen Jani 5.Remove the users from Windchill 6.Remove the users from KONE AD group ‘ADPROEUSERS’ Windchill ProjectLink license removal working instruction User removal process Windchill licensing has been changed a.Users accessing to Windchill Projects (ProjectLink) require a separate license b.inactive users need to be deleted Amount of licenses limit the the horizon to 2 months (at initial phase) a.In case the user activity in Windchill Projects is older, the user shall be deleted Deletion interval is quarter year This presentation is about the steps to conduct the removal Main steps Create user activity report from Windchill Process report data Notify the users losing their license via email Remove the users from ProjectLink license pool on Windchill 1.Create user activity report from Windchill 1/4 1.Create user activity report from Windchill 2/4 1.Create user activity report from Windchill 3/4 1.Create user activity report from Windchill 4/4 2.Process report data 1/6 2.Process report data 2/6 2.Process report data 3/6 2.Process report data 4/6 2.Process report data 5/6 2.Process report data 6/6 3.Notify the users losing their license via email 4.Remove the users from ProjectLink license pool on Windchill

short_description: How to raise case with Salesforce product support team.
introduction: How to raise case with Salesforce product support team.
instructions: PLEASE NOTE: you will only have access to this area if you are a designated contact for raising support related cases toward Salesforce Premium Support + agents. Please contact Teemu Kauhanen, Samasoorya Alakkad, Christophe Poirier, or Rakesh Alane for further assistance if you need to be appointed a Designated Contact to raise cases. For any product related issue, log a case with Salesforce by following the steps mentioned below once you have been appointed access to raise the cases through the help portal of SF: Step 1: Login into your salesforce account using the CUSTOM KONE URL for the said environment (IT ORG = konewayflow.my.salesforce.com / BUS ORG = kone.my.salesforce.com) - Step 2: Click on “Help & Training” You will directed to https://help.salesforce.com/home Step 3: Scroll down and locate the case creation section on the right side- Step 4: Note down the org id and click on the new creation symbol – You will be directed to the following page: Step 5: Select the relevant topic. For example, say you have some issue/ queries with user creation, then select the topic “Configuration Services”. You will be directed to the following page- Step 6: Select relevant category. Considering the above example, select “Setup & Customization”. You will be directed to the following page- Step 7: Select relevant task. Considering the above example, select “User Creation / Maintenance”. You will be directed to the following page case detail page- Step 8: Fill in relevant and required details. The “Severity Level” will be Level 4 – Medium by default. You can mention appropriate impact to raise the severity level. Step 9: The specific object or record can be mentioned here- Step 10: You can add more people in the case communication in the “Case Collaborators” section by looking up the person and clicking “Add”- The salesforce team will send email to all the team members mentioned in the above section. If the Salesforce product support team ask to grant login access, follow these steps- a. Click on your name. In the drop down list click on “My Settings” b. Click on “Personal” tab on the left from the resulting page- c. Click on “Grant Account Login Access” d. Grant required access to “Salesforce.com Support” and click Save.

short_description: How to check KFM client data connectivity from LOGON screen

This will open the 'Test Connection' screen, which will automatically perform 3 connectivity tests : test 1 to validate the data connection of the device test 2 to validate internet connectivity test 3 to check if KFM servers can be reached To be able to logon, all 3 tests needs to be successful (green) as seen in image 2.

short_description: How to change approver email address to SMTP email address in GRC system?
introduction: How to identify the SMTP email address? How to change approver email address to SMTP email address in GRC system?
instructions: Objective: How to change the approver's email address in GRC Production system same as SMTP email address, when offline approval / E-Mail approval functionality is not working. Manager / any approver, when they approve via email, GRC system will always check if the sender's email address is same as the email ID which was maintained in GRACUSER table. If the email address is not same, system will not accept the approval / rejection action, which was take by the approver. How to identify the SMTP email address? We can get the SMTP email address of approver via outlook or via SCOT t-code. Via outlook: Give the approver ID / name in outlook and check for the 'outlook properties'. Goto email address tab and check for the SMTP email address which is capital / block letters. 2. via SCOT t-code: When approver send the response via email, it will be captured in SCOT t-code. Login to GRC Production system, then go to SCOT t-code. Under Administration node, double click on Inbound Send Requests option. Give the document title and time period details and press ENTER / click on Update. The sender's email address can be found as shown in below screenshot. How to change approver email address to SMTP email address in GRC system? Go to SE16 and open GRACUSER table. Make sure that Email ID should be same as SMTP email address and connector should be LDAP_KNECIGRP9800. If these 2 fields are not matching in that case we need to update the user details. While checking the table for a specific user, make sure that user ID is in capital letters for all the characters. E.g. for user ID: MKOTOt, it should be enter as: MKOTOT To update the details, Go to custom transaction code: ZEMAIL_UPDATE_GRC and give the user ID & STMP email address and click on execute. If the details are updated in the system, you will get an information message saying 'Email Id Successfully Updated!!!'. After changing the email address, please reply to the approver with the below text. It seems your SMTP email ID was not correctly synched to the SAP GRC system which is must for offline approval email functionality to work seamlessly. Your email ID was incorrectly mapped as : XYZ@KONE.com instead of ABC@KONE.com. We have corrected this now, you will not face similar issue again while approving SAP Access Requests directly via email. Kindly approve the current GRC by replying to the original email from GRC Workflow as Approved.

short_description: Ask IT - How to Add user/user group in ACG or user in user group through VariPDM
introduction: How to Add user/user group in ACG or user in user group through VariPDM
instructions: Prerequisite: User need to contact the Person in charge or modifiers of the user group or ACG, because they are the responsible for such modification. If request came for adding user, then we required an approval from “Person in Charge” of ACG. If Person in Charge in unavailable, then take the approval from any “Group Modifiers” user of that ACG. If the access need and type not mentioned in request, then ask the requester, “What kind of access he/she required?” (Reader/Writer) as well as the Purpose/Reason of such access in ACG. IMPORTANT NOTES 1] Readers : Reader access allow the user to see the attributes or item under the ACG. 2] Special Readers : Special Reader access allow the user to download and see the document or item under the ACG. 3] Writers : Writers access allow the user to see, edit as well as download the document or item under the ACG. 4] Releasers : Releaser access allow the user to promote/change current state, additional with writer access. As per the necessary permission, Administrator should follow the Steps 9 to Step 11 for necessary Access type. Solution: Ø Login to PDM Variweb Ø Go To – Step 1: Select “Access Control group ” from the drop-down listed values. Step 2: Choose “ID or Full text”. Step 3: Enter the necessary ACG “ID or Name”, click on search icon. Step 4: Validate the necessary ACG and open it by clicking on it. Step 5: Identify the “Person in Charge” of the ACG for the necessary approval to Add or Remove the user from ACG (Access Control Group). Step 6: If Person in Charge of ACG is unavailable, then we can take the approval from “Group Modifiers (users)”, they are also equally responsible for the group modification. Step 7: After getting the necessary approval, click on “Edit” to Add or Remove the user from the ACG. Step 8: Look for the “Document ACG” Step 9: If you want to Add user as a Reader in ACG then select “Readers (users)”, otherwise to Add user group as a Reader in ACG please select “Readers (user groups)” Step 10: Click on “Add” to add new row. Step 11: Need to type the “User ID” , which we want to add in the ACG (Access Control Group). In case of user group, type the “User group ID” Step 11.A: Click on “…” to find the user from all PDM users. Step 11.B: Enter the user ID and click on search icon. Step 11.C: Select the right user, if unable to find the user it means he/she is not the part of PDM users. Step 11.D: Click on “Select”. Step 12: Click on “Save” to save the all necessary changes or click on “Cancel” to discard the all changes.

instructions: Step-1: Open Outlook through Web Access. Step-2: Click Settings option & select “View all Outlook settings.” Step-3: Select Calendar option & click on “Shared Calendars” Step-4: Click on Calendar you want to share from “Select a calendar” drop down list. Step-5: Enter the name of the employee with whom you want to share the calendar with (for E.g.: anita.saraf@kone.com ) Step-6: Once you select the name of the employee; select the access level that needs to be granted & then click Share. What permissions people have to your calendar You can choose from several different permission levels when sharing your calendar. § Can view when I'm busy . Those sharing your calendar with this permission level can only see which times you're available, like this: § Can view titles and locations . Those sharing your calendar with this permission level will see availability and the subject and meeting location, like this: § Can view all details . Those sharing your calendar with this permission level will see all details of your appointments, just like what you see. § Can Edit : Those sharing your calendar with this permission level will able to view, edit & delete events. § Not Shared : Those sharing your calendar with this permission level won’t be able to see information about your calendar, including when you are busy.

short_description: Service Account Management in MyAccess
introduction: 1. What is Service account? 2. How do I create a new Service account? 3. How do I update my Service account? 4. Warning messages before Service account will be disabled 5. Disable/Enable/Delete Service account 6. Requesting accesses for Service account
instructions: Answers to most common questions on Service accounts: MyAccess is the master source for Service accounts MyAccess is used to create new Service accounts and maintain life cycle of Service accounts. Service account is to be created only for system, application and services usage and it is never used by a human to login to resources. Each Service account must be mapped to a system in CMDB. Service accounts cannot be used for Console or RDP login. Each Service account must have two owners. If you need to add access to the service account and the access can not be found from MyAccess, please raise an AskIT ticket to MyAccess support team. In order to update service account you need to be either Owner or Deputy Owner of the account: if you need to become the owner, please contact the existing Owners and request them to add you as an owner in MyAccess. If your account is not yet in MyAccess Service Account Management, please reach out to MyAccess Support via AskIT and the team will immediately help to move your account to MyAccess! More information and instructions as an attachment ***************************************** Service account management from MyAccess 1. What is Service account? Service account is a non-person identity that has an AD account. Service accounts are created only for system, application and services usage and it is never used by a human to login to resources. Service accounts are often used to run a script or software on a server. Main features of service accounts are that they are not internet facing, they are not allowed for Console or RDP login and their passwords are more complex than normally and the password is set not to expire in AD. 2. How do I create a new Service account? Go to https://myaccess.kone.com and click on the navigation icon in the upper left corner. Click Manage Identity and Create AD Account Form will appear. Select Service account and click Create New form will appear for you to provide the details for account creation. Please fill in the details below and click Next. Name of the System/Application should be as in CMDB . Please note that the account name will be generated with “SVC_” + the name of the system/application. The System Name doesn't accept _T1, _T2, _T3 or _ADM. Name Extension will become mandatory field if “SVC_” + name of the system/application already exist. Please add some identifier like running number, letter or extension word. Country refers to the region where service runs. This is requested merely for technical reasons. Valid from refers to date since when account is created. Valid refers to the date until when the account is active. Service account will expire and get disabled after this date if no actions are taken by the account owners to extend the validity period. You can select a date for a maximum of 13 months in future. Description of the Account is mandatory field. Please describe what the account is used for. Owner is the primary owner of the account who will receive the initial service account password. You can optionally provide more than one owner for the service account. If you do so, at least one of the owners must be a KONE employee. In this case, the password will be delivered to all the owners. Deputy Owner is the other owner of the account who will also be able to update the account during its life cycle. You can optionally provide more than one deputy owner for the service account. If you do so, at least one of the deputy owners must be a KONE employee. Confirmation page opens for you to review the data before sending. If you want to still modify the data, click Back. If not, click Submit. On successful submission of the request, a message will be shown on the screen as below and the account will be created within 30 minutes time. The Service Account will be created with ‘Not Delegated’ flag by default. Account Owner will next receive an email notification “New Service Account Created….” with the initial password, account details and instructions for password reset and storage. 3. How do I update my Service account or Shared account? In order to update service account or Shared account you need to be either Owner or Deputy Owner of the account. Go to https://myaccess.kone.com and click on the navigation icon in the upper left corner. Click Manage Identity and Edit AD Account Form will appear requesting you to select the account that you need to update/edit. The drop down will show you only the accounts which are having” Active Directory” account that you are owner or deputy owner of. After selecting the account, click Edit A new form will appear with the account details, and you will be able to update the account description, Valid to -date, the owners and Delegation of the account. The extra field mentioned below is available in the edit form. Delegation: This field is used to turn ON/OF delegation option of the Service Account. After you are ready with the changes, click Confirm. On successful submission of the request, a message will be shown on the screen below and the account will be updated within 30 minutes time. 4. Warning messages before Service account will be disabled MyAccess will send multiple warnings to both Owner and Deputy Owner before account is disabled so that the owners will have enough time to go extend the validity if needed. The warnings are being sent 60, 30, 14, 7 and 1 day(s) before the validity of the account ends. The notification will look as below. The warning message also contains instructions on how to navigate in MyAccess to update the account validity. 5. Disable/Enable/Delete Service account MyAccess will disable Service account whenever it has reached its validity period. Owner and Deputy Owner will receive notification when service account has been disabled including the instructions on how to re-enable the account again. Service account can be re-enabled within 60 days from disablement. After that, Service account will be permanently deleted. 6. Requesting accesses for Service account If you need to request any access to service account, you can do so via MyAccess à Manage User Access. Please refer to Knowledge Article# KBA2858 for detailed instructions. If you can not find the correct AD group/access in MyAccess, please contact MyAccess Support via AskIT for help. 7. One of the account owners go inactive If one of the service account owners will go inactive, the remaining owner of the account will receive a notification, and a work item will be pending in MyAccess expecting for the remaining owner to update second owner.

short_description: Points to ponder while making any dashboard dynamic from static dashboard in CRM.
introduction: How to change existing static dashboard to dynamic dashboard and what process should be followed?
instructions: Introduction: Q1. What is dynamic dashboard? A1 . Dynamic dashboards enable each user to see the data they have access to. With a dynamic dashboard, you can control data visibility without having to create a separate dashboard, with its own running user and folder, for each level of data access. Q2. How to make dashboard dynamic? A2. The View Dashboard As option should be selected as The dashboard viewer for dashboard to be dynamic . The dashboard viewer : A dynamic dashboard runs using the security settings of the user viewing the dashboard. Each user sees the dashboard according to his or her own access level. This approach helps administrators share one common set of dashboard components to users with different levels of access. NOTE You can't save dynamic dashboards to personal folders. You can't schedule refreshes for dynamic dashboards. They must be refreshed manually. Steps to be followed: Step 1: There are fixed number of licenses available for Dynamic Dashboards. Therefore approval must be taken from Business Team . Next steps can be followed only after approval is received. Step 2: When you are creating new dynamic dashboard then while saving it Select View Dashboard As as The dashboard viewer . This makes dashboard a dynamic dashboard. NOTE : Optionally, select Let dashboard viewers choose whom they view the dashboard as to enable those with permission to change the running user on the dashboard view page. Users with “View My Team's Dashboards” can view the dashboard as any user below them in the role hierarchy. Users with “View All Data” can edit the dashboard and view it as any user in their organization. Step 4: When changing existing dashboard to dynamic dashboard. Open dashboard --> Click on Edit Now click on settings (Gear icon) Now s elect View Dashboard As as The dashboard viewer . This makes dashboard a dynamic dashboard. Click on Save. NOTE : Optionally, select Let dashboard viewers choose whom they view the dashboard as to enable those with permission to change the running user on the dashboard view page. Users with “View My Team's Dashboards” can view the dashboard as any user below them in the role hierarchy. Users with “View All Data” can edit the dashboard and view it as any user in their organization.

short_description: ECM - How to change ECM M-files Network Drive Letter
introduction: After M-Files installation it uses the M drive which already in use for other network drive ,How Change ECM M-files Drive Letter ?
instructions: After M-Files installation it uses the " M " letter drive by default and some time in user machine " M " drive letter may already in use for other network share, so the M-Files separate drive will not be created and cannot access M-Files. User need to change the Drive letter of M-Files from default " M " drive letter to some other free drive letter as follow by below steps. 1. Open M-Files Desktop Settings from start button search or Open M-Files and press alt in keyboard to show the menu bar. Else in the taskbar , click on Show hidden icons --> Right click on M-Files --> Settings --> M-Files Desktop Settings . 2. Go to Settings --> Computer specific settings --> Drive and cache options --> Drive ( here you can choose any other free drive letter) and press OK. 3. Wait for some time and after that you can access M-Files Vault from the configured drive letter and also your earlier network drive with “M” drive letter will work as expected.

short_description: How to troubleshoot the e.pil issue
introduction: How to troubleshoot the e.pil issue
instructions: A STEPS TO TROUBLESHOOT E.PIL ISSUE ? If the orders not found in the e.pil. First, We have analysis whether the issue comes from Liaison or PA.Net. Then have to check with concern team PA.Net or Liaison. If everything works good then need to check with SAP PI team. E.PIL data – Data should be derives from Liaison/PA.Net à SAP PI à Database à E.PIL 1.1 HOW TO CHECK YOIG & YOIP OUTPUT STATUS USING OUTBOUND DELIVERY NUMBER Step : 1 We have to check YOIG & YOIP message status using T- code VL03N in the SAP. Step : 2 Insert Outbound delivery and click “enter” Delivery number – 710860165 Step : 3 Then page will be display. Step : 4 Go to Extras ->Delivery output-> Header Step : 5 We can find the YOIG & YOIP message indicates in green color status box successfully. Step : 6 To check the IDOC details. Go to YOIG & YOIP message. Then, click the Processing log . Step : 7 The output of processing analysis dialogue box will be appears in that you can find the IDOC number. Step : 8 Using outbound delivery IDOC details have to check with SAP PI team for delivered status. Once the SAP PI team will confirm the IDOC status. The IDOCs are successfully delivered from PO. 1.2 HOW TO CHECK YOIG & YOIG MESSAGE OUTPUT HAVEN’T TRIGGERED FOR THE DELIVERY NUMBER For the delivery number Ex : 710860731 haven’t view the YOIG & YOIP message. NOTE : If there is not YOIG and YOIP messages, then FL has not ordered the shipment. The YOIG and YOIP messages are created when the FL confirms us that is ok to ship. Step : 1 Using T-code SE16 in SAP Step : 2 Insert table name LIKP and enter Step : 3 Enter delivery number 710860731 Step : 4 From Table : LIKP – you can notice that Shipping Point : FI72 + Delivery Type : YSLD . Step :1 Using T-Code vv23 to check the condition for YOIG & YOIP message not triggerd issue. Output Type : YOIG & YOIP YOIG : For the highlighted Key Combination. From the below screenshot, you can identify – Output – EDI will get triggered only, when Saving the application. Output Type : YOIP YOIP : For the highlighted Key Combination. 1.3 HOW TO CHECK USER ACTIVITY DETAILS FOR THE DELIVERY NUMBER Step : 1 Go to T-code VL03N in SAP. Step : 2 Insert Outbound delivery and click “enter” Delivery number – 710860165 Step : 3 Go to Environment à Changes Step : 4 Enter the delivery number and click “execute”. Step : 5 We can find the user activity details for the delivery number. Note : In general, every time that someone makes any changes manually in an order, those changes are saved with the user name of the person that has modified the order. This is valid for all the activities that in some way modify the order, for example creation of outbound delivery, change of weights, change of delivery address, change of status and so on. Every person that can access the order in change mode can also modify the order; if the change is saved, you will see the user name of that person for that specific activity. For an example : OBD : 710862547 The ODB has got created on 20.05.2020. But the EDI output : YOIG & YOIP – has got created only on 26.05.2020, as the user had tried to modify or make changes on 26.05.2020 and when saving the document the output has got triggered and saved successfully. As explained in the first screenshot : YOIG & YOIP – Output condition record , This output type will be triggered ‘Only when Saving the Application’. As this is an existing setup, we confirm from SAP end that the system behavior is working fine as expected and defined. Step : 1 Go to T-code VL03N in SAP Step : 2 Insert Outbound delivery and click “enter” Step : 3 Using GOTO -> Header -> Administration 1.4 HOW TO CHECK WITH SAP TEAM CONNECTION FROM PO TO APPIAN DB STATUS Connection from PO to Appian DB is working fine as we can see many delivered messages daily to appian. We are using below details : Connection : jdbc:inetdae7://EUDMSSQLAPP30Q.konenet.com:1433/appian_prod Username : appian Please find the screenshot: 1.5 TO CHECK THE IDOC STATUS IN SAP AND SHARE THE STATUS TO DB TEAM TO FIX THE ISSUE IN PROACTIVE BASIS Step : 1 To check the status of IDOC in SAP, need access for WE02 t-code. Step : 2 E nter the required details like IDOC type, date etc and click on execute. Step : 3 Status 39 indicates IDOC’s delivered to target system. If the outbound IDOCs shows 39 delivered status. Then have to check with Database team.

short_description: Update standard parts or components to the Project
introduction: How to retrieve the standard part from the Project? How to update standard component to the Project? How to update project?
instructions: How to identify the issue? Check the version and iteration of the Standard Component which is used in Project and the Library Standard Component. If is there any difference, then we can follow the below steps. If you get below error at the time of adding the Standard Component in user workspace. Note – Now you can conclude that, some component/standard component/parts iteration is mismatching with Project. Pre-Check: Check who is the requester of the request, if Project Owner is requesting for this then we can proceed further by skipping the approval process. (This can be done by the Project Owner. If they need any help in doing, support team can help) If the request is from other than the Project owner, Inform the user to contact the Project Owner to update the components to the Project. In case, if there are no Project owner information available, then support team can help user to update only the standard components to the project. Procedure : Using the below steps, you can able to retrieve the standard component for the Project. Search for the necessary Project in Engineering Windchill. Go to the Project – Folder Search for Standard Part under the Project Component. Select the necessary Standard Part, which we are going to update. Click on Action – Update Project Basically, this command is used for the updating the all component belongs to Project. But we are authorized to update the Standard Part Only. Make sure other parts are excluded from the update list. After clicking on Update Project, you can identify whether is there any update available for the Standard Part or not. If you can see the name of standard part, then check the Project Version & Updated Version. If you can’t see the Standard Part name in Pop up window, then there is no Update for that Standard Part. NOTE: Once we updated the Standard Part, then we can cross check and make sure whether it is updated or not by executing Update Project once more.

short_description: SMT 3.0 - How to import new Technician Data?
introduction: SMT 3.0 - How to import new Technician Data?
instructions: There are two ways that we can proceed: First way -Retrieve all the technician data from the SFDC Production Org -Compare it with the technician data that needs to be imported which will consist of new technicians/technicians who left company/technicians with new details (e.g. phone number) : List out the technician's data in separate CSV files for insert and update. Ensure that all relevant details are filled for User Insertion or Updation. -Define the ones which is not existing anymore -Import all the new technician data Second Way - Try upsert via data loader using external Id field ServiceResource.Employee_Number__c - Any errors will tell you what is wrong for example new one will give you User who is missing - Mention all the users not existing anymore and inactive users. -For new Ones create SR Note: You can also opt for second way if users already exist Steps to perform for new technician insert : 1. Create a user.csv file having all mandatory fields filled for creating any new user. Refer to the User_Insert_Template.csv file to insert the users. · Check the format of the Alias and Nickname field in particular. As mentioned, work with CONCAT function to get the correct format. · Username and Email field Please remember to include sandbox name to the end of the username and “. invalid” to end of the email when inserting to sandbox Example: Username: eden.hilel@kone.com.int Email: eden.hilel@kone.com.invalid · Check the format of the Name field in particular. Please use the “PROPER” function in excel to convert to the proper case (first letter uppercase, rest lower). · User field “Manager” should be mapped from ServiceTerritory.Owner Example: If Technician “EDEN HILEL” is linked to Service Territory “312:I11” which has owner “Guy Levi”, get the Id of the owner and set it as a value in the Manager field. 2. Once the user gets created in the org, we need to create a Service Resource for these users. 3. Create ServiceResource.csv file as per the template available. · Use “Konect technician” in Description field · Get the User’s Id from the Id field of the success file created in first step (Easier way to find created Ids from the success file in first step) and map those ids in the RELATEDUSERID column in ServiceResource.csv file along with the Employee Number. · Insert the Service Resource data for the new users created. While doing the insert through data loader/workbench, map the RELATEDUSERID to RelatedRecordId field of Service Resource object. 4. Once Service Resource gets created, get the id of these service resources (from success file) and create a mapping file for service territory member insert. Refer the template for the same. 5. Insert the Service Resource Territory Member as per the mapping provided. Important points to note while doing Data Export/Import: 1. If any user already exists in the org, update the user details manually as per the format provided. 2. If any update is required on the fields of User/Service Resource/Service Resource Member data, prepare the csv file as per the format mentioned above and then do the update. Make sure the format requirement is met while updating the data. 3. Always refer the helper files provided in the request.

short_description: User cannot confirm milestone - IMT3
introduction: How to check milestone status for network in SAP
instructions: 1.1. To validate this, first check in SAP what is the milestone status for this network. Step : 1 - Transaction code CN23 – Display Network. Step : 2 - Type in the network number and click “Enter” (the green check mark). Step : 3 - Choose the “Activity Overview” (Green bar) to see the list of activities planned for this network. Step : 4 - You can see that for this example network, the milestone 0B has been confirmed (CNF) on 26.3.2018. The next milestones are 3B and 5. You should see the same in IMT – for this case IMT is showing correct information (milestone planned dates and confirmation status) as can be seen in below screenshot. 1.2. To Check expected IMT milestone behavior for network Step : 1 - Next we need to check the IMT configuration to see how these milestones are supposed to behave in IMT. IMT configuration is done per network scenario (which defines the type of installation process to be followed for the network), so we first have to see which scenario is assigned to this network in SAP. This is done with SAP transaction ZP_SCENARIO_MASS Our example network is in South Africa, so we choose Plant: 280. Then input the network number, check “Assign default scenario” and “Test mode”, then click “Execute”. The result shows there is No valid scenario assigned to this network, thus a default scenario is applied. Normally, you would see here the name of the scenario (e.g. “NEB_C”). The existing scenarios are defined by the FLs themselves. 1.3 - To check the scenario configuration information Step : 1 - Now that we know which scenario this network’s installation follows (default scenario), we can go check the IMT configuration, i.e. which milestones are applied to this network and how those milestones behave in IMT. Run transaction SM30 – Call View Maintenance in SAP. Then input the name of the table you want to view or edit, in this case it is IMT configuration table ZPS_IMT_CONFIG. Now since we are just checking data you can click “Display”, but “Maintain” will also work. When the table opens up, you need to find the correct scenario to see the listed milestones and their behavior. You can do this either by scrolling or, by searching as shown below. Click “Selection” -> “By Contents”. We want to search the scenario, so choose “FL Scenario” and click Ok (green check mark) In our example case, there was no valid scenario assigned to the network, so it’s installation should follow the default scenario “KW_GENER”, type that in and click “Choose” (if you do have a valid scenario name, type that in instead). Now you will see only the rows which have “KW_GENER” in the Scenario field. The remaining milestones to be confirmed for our example network were 3B and 5, so we will search for those to chek their behavior. On the first line with milestone 3B, the field “Plant” is blank – this is the global configuration. Below it you can see other rows specifying configuration for milestone 3B with a specific plant. If there is a specific row for the plant the network is in, this configuration will be applied to milestone 3B. If not, the configuration of the row with field “Plant” remaining blank is applied. In our example network, the Plant is 280 (South Africa). There is no specific configuration for plant 280, so the global configuration is applied. You can see it says “Behaviour – Display only”, which means that in IMT, the milestone is only displayed, it cannot be modified. This means that in IMT, the user can see the milestone but cannot reschedule it or confirm it – this must be done directly in SAP by someone else – if we go back to the SAP transaction Display Network (CN23) and look at activities, we can see the description of 3B activity was “SL Material shipped (B)”. Here SL stands for “Supply Line”, meaning it is the supply line (factory), who must confirm this milestone. Next we will check milestone 5. Again there is no local configuration for plant 280, so the global setting is applied. The Behaviour of milestone 5 is “Plan and confirm”, meaning that it is possible to reschedule (plan) and confirm the milestone in IMT. You can also see that the checkbox “Not In Seq(uence)” is unchecked – this means that the milestone IS in sequence, i.e. it cannot be confirmed before the previous milestone is confirmed. So in this case the Error which the user is getting when trying to confirm milestone 5 is normal -> he cannot confirm it until the previous milestone 3B has been confirmed, and 3B is a supply line milestone which must be confirmed in SAP by the supply line. If the result of the investigation is that IMT is not working correctly (user should be able to confirm the milestone based on SAP configuration, but is still seeing the error), issue needs to be moved to L3 (Siili Solutions) for further investigation.

Step 6 à If Resolution is not confirmed by ticket creator or the ticket requires more details for T-System to process, please start from Step 1 until resolution of the ticket ( Step 5 )

short_description: License management activities to be done on last day of the month
introduction: List the activities that are to be done on last day of the month to make sure that the licences are correctly updated
instructions: Below activities are to be done on last day of the month. For understanding lets take current month to be August 1. Before 11.30 PM IST on August 31st ,Change the filter of the report (last login date) to last day of current month - 3, eg if current month is August (8) set the last login date to (8-3 = 5 ie May) 5/31 https://kone.my.salesforce.com/00Ow0000005QB9n Change the Filter for PRO user deactivation to 9 months that is Last login date less than equal to 9 months 2. Automated inactivation job runs at 11.30 PM IST (KONE_UserDeactivationBatch). Validate that its run is successful from Apex Jobs view 3. Take out the complete extract of custom settings and change the month in the extract to 9. Three columns have to be changed: Name, Month and Search 4. Re-upload the custom settings and send it to Mudit for addition of forecast for the next month ie 9 (September) 5. Mudit to send back the updated file on 1st September 6. Re-upload the custom settings again 7. On 31st August while the Inactivation job is running, take out complete extract of custom settings from INT and FULL and set the licenses number 100 for each record. Also change the month as explained in step 3. 8. Upload the custom settings respectively to INT and FULL

short_description: INSTRUCTIONS ON O365 ACCESS REQUESTS FOR DISABLED ACCOUNTS
introduction: What is procedure to grant access on user mailbox (Disabled accounts)
instructions: INSTRUCTIONS ON O365 ACCESS REQUESTS FOR DISABLED ACCOUNTS Contents 1. Basic principles for operating with disabled AD accounts 2. Ask IT process for granting access to O365/emails of disabled user 3. Exception policy in case O365 account is already deleted 4. Conditions for sharing email account content of an active (enabled) email account 5. Attachments Definitions · Account = the disabled O365 account to which access is requested · Account Holder = the KONE employee to whose O365 account the access is requested · Consent Form = the form used by the Account Holder to give consent for access. · O365 Access Request Form = the form required to be filled in by the Requestor when making a request. · Requestor = the KONE employee requesting O365 access 1. BASIC PRINCIPLES FOR OPERATING WITH DISABLED AD ACCOUNTS 1. Granting access to a disabled O365 account is always an exception. Before an employee leaves KONE, and during the handover process, the manager should ensure any business-critical information is stored with manager before the employees last day with KONE. If this is followed, there is no need to subsequently grant access to a disabled account. 2. Out-of-office message with accurate contact information can always be set to the disabled Account by AskIT and it will be valid max. 30 days after the Account was disabled. AskIT can only use a standard form text for the out-of-office reply, or the wording provided by the O365 Account Holder. Standard form text: This e-mail address is no longer in use. Please direct your inquiries to [insert email address] . Please note that your email will not be forwarded automatically. 3. Auto forward from personal email to another email address is never allowed at KONE and must not be done. If AskIT receives a request to auto forward emails, AskIT can use the following standard reply: Please note that automatic forwarding of emails from a personal email to another email address is never allowed at KONE and cannot be done. If you wish to seek approval to access the user’s email inbox for a limited period of time, please modify the questionnaire accordingly and submit it back to AskIT. 4. We will never enable terminated users AD account. 5. We will never provide a password for a disabled Account . 6. Workday records are never re-activated for a person who do not have valid employment or contingent worker contract. 7. After 30 days from the date of disabling the Account , all data will be permanently deleted , and access cannot be granted under any circumstances. 8. In case of any illegal or other suspicious activity contact always security@kone.com . 2. ASKIT PROCESS FOR GRANTING ACCESS TO O365 / EMAILS OF DISABLED USER A disabled O365 Account is visible in Active Directory for 30 days after the Account is disabled, and access can be granted during those 30 days by following the standard process outlined in this Section 1 ( AskIT Process for granting access to O365 / emails of Disabled Users ). STEP 1 – INITIAL REQUEST 1. AskIT reviews the O365 Access Request Form and asks further information if the request is unclear. Inform the Requestor that there is only limited time before the Account is closed. O365 Access Request Form is mandatory, and all fields must be filled in. If any field is missing, AskIT asks the Requestor to complete the form: Please fill in all sections in the questionnaire and ensure that descriptions / additional information is provided when needed. Note that personaldatarequest@kone.com will not process incomplete requests . It is the responsibility of AskIT to check that all fields are filled in before proceeding with the request. 2. Ticket to be in the respective AskIT queue until the O365 Access Request Form has been fully completed. 3. AskIT checks whether the Account is still active in Active Directory: (i) If the Account is visible in Active Directory, proceed to STEP 2. (ii) If the Account is NOT visible in Active Directory, inform the Requestor that the Account has been permanently deleted and cannot be restored. STEP 2 – WRITTEN CONSENT 1. If the Account Holder’s written consent is not attached, AskIT makes a request for the Requestor to provide it. AskIT may send the following wording in the email to the Requestor and attach the Consent Form to the email: Please provide proof of written consent from the individual for which account access is requested. Due to the KONE internal process and/or applicable laws, we must always contact the account holder first to obtain written consent, even if the account holder is no longer working with KONE. The individual may provide consent either by signing the attached consent form or by submitting their consent clearly via email/SMS. 2. If written consent cannot be obtained, proceed to STEP 3. 3. If a written consent from the Account Holder is provided and the O365 Access Request Form has been fully completed, proceed to STEP 4. STEP 3 – DATA PROTECTION TEAM’S APPROVAL 1. AskIT sends the completed O365 Access Request Form to personaldatarequest@kone.com and informs that written consent could not be obtained. 2. The Data Protection team will determine whether access can be granted. 3. Ticket to be in the respective AskIT queue until the approval from personaldatarequest@kone.com is received. 4. If the access is approved by personaldatarequest@kone.co m, AskIT archives the approval from personaldatarequest@kone.com in the ticket log. 5. If the access is denied by personaldatarequest@kone.com , they inform AskIT and the Requestor of the same. STEP 4 – VERIFICATION AND ACCESS 1. AskIT verifies that the approvals are in place and archives the Account Holder’s written consent in the ticket log. 2. AskIT proceeds with granting full mailbox access to the Account. The full mailbox access can be granted to a maximum of 2 authorized individuals for a maximum of 30 days from the date the Account was disabled. After that time, the Account is automatically deleted. 3. AskIT informs the Requestor about the access and closes the ticket upon proper confirmation. Standard reply: Access to the Account Holder’s inbox has hereby been granted. The access is given for 30 days from the date the O365 account was disabled. Please ensure that during this time, any business-critical emails or materials are stored appropriately elsewhere. Please note that any emails or other documents which are clearly private in nature (e.g., by title or sender information) shall not be read nor opened. 3. EXCEPTION POLICY IN CASE O365 ACCOUNT IS ALREADY DELETED If the Account is not visible in Active Directory, the 30 days from the date of Account disablement have elapsed. Before granting access, AskIT must ensure that one of the following is met: a) Local MD or Global Unit Head has approved a business-critical reason for the access and (i) the Account Holder’s written consent is attached (STEP 2 – WRITTEN CONSENT); or (ii) approval from personaldatarequest@kone.com has been received; b) Local/Global Legal Counsel is requesting restoration via email (O365 Access Request Form not needed); or c) Local/Global Compliance team is requesting restoration and the O365 Access Request Form has been approved by personaldatarequest@kone.com Scenario 1: Rehired user request for a data restoration from the old account. Process: AskIT should set an expectation to the user that only Mailbox and OneDrive data can be restored, no other data and permissions can be restored. AskIT creates a ticket and assigns it to the O365 L2 Support Team with all the required approvals (only if within 30 days from account deletion date and the ticket should be assigned before 28th day). O365 team restores the Mailbox and OneDrive data to the new account (no other data or permissions from the old account will be restored). The O365 Support Team informs the requestor on the completion of data restoration, and after receiving an acknowledgement from the requestor, they can close the ticket. Scenario 2: User left the KONE, his/her manager or a colleague is seeking access to the user's mailbox. Process: AskIT creates a ticket to MyAccess support team requesting restoration to disabled status, removing the pager number from the AD record, and adding following text in the description field. Account restored by exception, ticket number [insert ticket number] After the account is set to “disabled” status, MyAccess support team should return the ticket back to AskIT and AskIT should assign the ticket to O365 support teams with the approval file. O365 support team will inform the requestor when full mailbox access to the disabled account is granted and after confirmation from the requester, they can close the ticket and send an email to MyAccess Support team to delete the account after 15 days. Note: In these exceptional cases, it is important to ensure that the account is deleted after 15 days. After 60 days from the date of disablement, access cannot be granted under any circumstances, i.e. this exception applies when 30-60 days have elapsed from the date of account disablement 4. CONDITIONS FOR SHARING EMAIL ACCOUNT CONTENT OF AN ACTIVE (ENABLED) EMAIL ACCOUNT 1. The Account Holder can by themselves do any of the following: o delegate access for example to an assistant via Outlook Account settings; o delegate access by the “delegate access” functionality in Outlook; or o set an out-of-office reply. 2. Direct forwarding of emails (auto forwarding) is never allowed. For guidance, see Section 1 ( Basic principles for operating with disabled AD accounts ). 3. If the Account Holder still works at KONE, Ask IT informs the Requestor with the following: Thank you for your request. As the Account Holder is still with KONE, please instruct them to store any business-critical emails or materials elsewhere, such as a shared SharePoint site. The manager of the Account Holder should inform the Account Holder of the out-of-office message that they should set prior to the last day of their employment. If needed in exceptional cases, the access to anyone’s O365 account can only be approved if the Account Holder is unavailable from accessing their O365 account, for example due to prolonged illness or if the employee has already left KONE. During holidays, primarily access should be granted by the employee themself by utilizing the “delegate access” function prior to leaving KONE. 4. In case of exceptional urgent need due to a critical business, compliance, security, legal hold or legal reason it is possible to request a supervised opening. In such case, approval must be requested by Local/Global Compliance team with the fully completed O365 Access Request Form from personaldatarequest@kone.com 5. ATTACHMENTS O365 Data Access Request – Initial Legal Questionnaire O365 Data Access Request – User consent form Frequently Asked Questions (FAQ) - KBA8915

short_description: Export a Report in Lightning Experience and Classic view in Salesforce
instructions: Export a Report in Lightning Experience From Reports, next to the report you want to export, click Export . Choose an Export View. Formatted Report exports the report as it appears in Salesforce, with the report header, groupings, and filter details. Because formatting is retained, you can't choose an encoding and the only supported formatted export file type is .XLSX . Details Only exports each detail row without formatting and is useful for doing further calculations in a spreadsheet. If exporting as Details Only , set an Encoding appropriate for your language and locale. If exporting as Details Only , set the Format to Excel Format .xls or Comma Delimited .csv . Click Export . If prompted by a browser dialog, choose to save the file. Export a Report in Salesforce Classic (for those that have access still to Classic UI) From Reports, next to the report you want to export, click Export . If you set the “Do not save encrypted pages to disk” option in Internet Explorer, you can’t open your report online in Excel. Instead, save the exported report to your computer, and then open it in Excel. To change this setting in Internet Explorer, deselect “Do not save encrypted pages to disk” under Internet options. Set an Encoding appropriate for your language. Set the Format to Excel Format .xls or Comma Delimited .csv . Click Export . If prompted by a browser dialog, choose to save the file. If exporting as Details Only, set an Encoding appropriate for your language and locale. If exporting as Details Only, set the Format to Excel Format .xls or Comma Delimited .csv. Click Export. If prompted by a browser dialog, choose to save the file.

short_description: MES - LES MCA Work Instructions
introduction: MES - LES MCA Work Instructions
instructions: 1. Login to Apriso portal. 2. Type MCA in search box and click on the MCA. 3. MCA screen will be visible. In this screen user can see a few empty fields and a button to create TO as below: 4. User will able to perform following actions on LES MCA screen. Functionality on this screen. User has to follow steps as below: a. Select proper facility. b. Enter required material and its quantity. c. If material is order bounded, enter Sales Order and Sales order item fields (mandatory). d. If material is stock/bulk, Sales order & item fields are not mandatory. e. Enter destination location where the material is to be called. f. Click on ‘Create TO’ button. 5. After clicking on the ‘Create TO’ button a TO is created successfully, given that all validations are passed. 6. We can check this TO on REX screen. 7. The source location for the TO is automatically generated by system as below: · In case of order bound material, there can be only one location in system => source location is known for transfer order. · In case of stock or bulk material, system will define source location for transfer order based on FIFO from warehouse. · If there is no material at all in whole factory, then source location would be empty. · Source location priority sequence: 1. WH location, unrestricted (yes) 2. WH consignment (yes) 3. WH quality location, unrestricted (yes) 4. WH location, quality inspection (yes) 5. WH quality location, quality inspection (yes) 6. Production location, unrestricted (yes) 7. Production quality location, unrestricted (yes) 8. Production location, quality inspection (yes) 9. Production quality location, quality inspection (yes) · Locations that will not be considered for Source location: 1. WH location, blocked (no) 2. WH quality location, blocked (no) 3. Production location, blocked (no) 4. Production quality location, blocked (no) If material is present at above mentioned 4 locations, source location will be empty.

short_description: KIS Knowledge on Standard Service Requests
introduction: How to handle the template for each Service Request
instructions: Any change(s) in the existing information/ mapping is to be raised under a Service request. While for addition of new information qualifies as a ‘New Requirement’. KW Global Invoice Layouts https://kone.sharepoint.com/:x:/r/sites/KIS/_layouts/15/Doc.aspx?sourcedoc=%7B9FA37534-3B48-47E0-90D9-8B4A01CA9038%7D&file=KW%20invoice%20layouts.xlsx&action=default&mobileredirect=true Below are the templates for a Service Request. *NOTE: Always the communication to o2c goes through M&C finance shared mail box 1. Contact Detail Changes (Email, Phone number) Contact Details are maintained in the footer of Invoice layouts. This is hard-coded at the vendor’s end. Action Needed from Support: To ask or identify all the billing types, layouts, As is and to-be contact information. 2. Office Address changes Office address is present in the footer of the invoice layout. It is also hard-coded by the vendor at their end. NOTE: There is a second field for the address called “KONE return address”. You will find it in the top left portion of layout. In case(s) of a SR for an address change, practice followed should be to change the content of both the address in the footer and ‘within the invoice’ return address (if is included). · For Address Change in the Footer: Support need to ask or identify all the billing types, layouts, As is and to-be address information · For ‘within the invoice’ return address: Support need to ask or identify all the billing types, layouts, As is and to-be address information. (If applicable) for the return address in the invoice header area; make sure to have this address confirmed as well. NOTE: Return address is used only by some countries. Make sure to check some sample invoice. If return address is absent, that is fine. But if it is applicable, include the details about this. to understand 'if' Return address is used by them. *Table name and IDOC segment needed here. Make sure to check the countries for ‘Return address’. Only some have it printed in the invoice. Eg. Netherlands, Switzerland This can be done by downloading some invoice from the portal or SAP and check. If return address is absent, that is fine. But if it is applicable, ask user & include the details about this. 3. Invoice layout title changes Invoice title is the header of any invoice layout. It is maintained in SAP for all the vendor’s but Billtrust. The data corresponds to the respective IDOC segment mapped to Field number ‘1’ (always). Table changes are done in table TVFKT. Where IDOC segment is E1EDK01.ZE1EDK1/TYPETEXT Support need to ask Title(s) for various billing types & layouts. Table changes are done in table ZSD_E_BILL_TITLE. The following content needs to be maintained in table: Sales organization, billing type, language(s) and Title NOTE! It needs to maintain title to both the two fields to get it to IDOC! Make sure to first test in Z03 environment. Text should not be long enough to only appear partially in the layout. NOTE: As, in the SAP tables, the text can have a long length but in Invoice layout it may only appear the initial allowed characters Exception: For Billtrust, the Invoice title is hard-coded and maintained in the invoice layout by Billtrust. 4. Payment term description changes (Only local language) Payment Term description data flows from SAP to the respective IDOC segments. In the invoice layout they can be seen under field ‘74’. Problem Statement: I need to update the translation text for ‘Terms of Payment’ Support need to have the new text in the local language and business justification on it. Data for payment terms is maintained in TVZBT table ( Where TVZBT-ZTERM = VBRK-ZTERM and TVZBT-SPRAS = KNVT-SPRAS) and the corresponding IDOC segment is ZE1EDK2/TEXT1 NOTE: Description for Terms of payment must be justified with the Terms of payment. *I believe this template is just to get the text updated and not the terms of payment. 5. Invoice text and label changes Invoice text is soft-coded data maintained in SAP. Support need to trace the mapping back to the invoice/ customer master data. Have the table name where text is maintained, SAP Segment and IDOC segment. NOTE: not always text is maintained in SAP. For some text, it is with o2c. Support need to understand and check for those text(s) Invoice label is hard-coded text maintained at vendor’s end. This is defined based on the CC and KIS layout. Support must have details of the corresponding IDOC segment and the field. Have layout of Billing type(s), As is and to-be label, Make sure SR's are rightly approved by the KIS Solution Design Owner before any change or maintenance is done. 6. Invoice list calendar updates
