short_description: MES - Quality Setup
introduction: MES - Quality Setup
instructions: 1. In the Quick Access field type “Characteristics” and open the screen 2. The screen displays the list of existing characteristics. 3. Click on the “ ” button to create a new characteristic 4. In the popup type the Name of your characteristics, select the Type of characteristic - "Attribute" and set Usage to “Inspection”. Set the Revision to 1. 5. After you click OK a screen with Inspection Characteristics details will be displayed. You have to click “Save” first in order to continue 6. In “General” tab enter the Description of the test. This description will be displayed in MES 7. Go to “Attributes” tab. Click on the “ ” button to add an attribute. One attribute represents one possible answer to the test. You can add multiple answers. You can select at least one as “Conforming” i.e. the correct answer 8. Click “Save”. The characteristic is created now 1.1 Create Variable Inspection Characteristic 1. In the Quick Access field type “Characteristics” and open the screen. 2. The screen displays the list of existing characteristics. 3. Click on the “ ” button to create a new characteristic. Enter the Name of the characteristic, select type – “Variable” and Usage - “Inspection”. Set revision to 1 and click “OK” . 4. In the “General” tab enter the description of the test. 5. Go to Limits tab. Select “UOM” (you can use the “ ” button or just start typing in this field and the suggestion will be displayed). 6. Enter the values for limits – target value and upper control limit and lower control limit. 7. Click “Save”. The characteristic is created now. 2 Inspection Plan Setup 2.1 Add Inspection Plan 1 In the Quick Access field start typing “Inspection Plan” and open appropriate screen. 2 The screen displays the list of Inspection Plans. Click on “ ” button to add new Inspection Plan. 3 In the popup fill in the following fields: a Inspection Plan – the name of the Inspection Plan b Type – General c Class – "PO Context" or "Determination Context". "PO Context" means that the tests will be used for all Work Stations. "Determination Context" means that tests will be assigned to Work Stations based on Inspection Determination configuration. d Revision – revision number, it should start from 1. e Status – only Inspection Plans in status "New" or "Released" are treated as active. f Description – optional Click “Create Inspection Plan” to save the changes. 4 The Inspection Plan is created. 5 Right click on the root element on the tree view (left panel) and from the context menu select “Create Inspection Line”. 6 In the popup fill in the following fields: a LineNo – a number, b Line Code – name of inspection Line. Click on “Create Inspection Line” to confirm. 7 Repeat the previous step to create as many Inspection Lines as need e.g. one Inspection Line per one Work Station. 8 From the tree view in left panel select the Inspection Line which you would like to modify. Right click on it and from the context menu select “Create Inspection Characteristic”. 9 In the popup fill in the following fields. "Sequence" – a consecutive number. 10 In the “Characteristic” field – link an already created Inspection Characteristic. You can just start typing in that field and select one of the suggestions or click on “ ” button and select the Characteristic from the popup. 11 Go to “Custom Attributes” tab. In this tab you can assign characteristic determining test type and feature. Use the “ ” button to assign new Characteristic. 12 From the drop-down menu select the characteristic you would like to assign. 13 The characteristics which are meaningful for Inspection Characteristics are: a K1X_QualityTest_Type. Possible values are: i FQC (Final Quality Check) performed by Quality Inspector using QEX screen, FQC tab. ii IPQC (In Process Quality Check) performed by Quality Inspector using QEX screen, IPQC tab. iii OQC (Outgoing Quality Check) performed by Quality Inspector using QEX screen, OQC tab iv PD (Production Department) performed by Production Operator on POE (Quality Tab) or QMO screen. b K1X_Quality_DoubleCheckPD – it means that test is both PD and IPQC test and will be displayed in POE (Quality tab), QMO and QEX. Available values are Y and N (Yes and No). c K1X_Quality_CriticalToQuality – it means that test is critical to quality and this information will be displayed in QEX and POE screen to inform the operator. Available values are Y and N (Yes and No). d K1X_Quality_CriticalToSafety - it means that test is critical to safety and this information will be displayed in QEX and POE screen to inform the operator. Available values are Y and N (Yes and No). e K1X_Quality_TestPosition - this attribute is set when Work Station has multiple Work Position. It defines for which Work Position the test is assigned. 14 Go to the “Control Parameters” tab. You can select the “Inherit data from Characteristic” checkbox. In that case the list of possible attributes will be inherited from Inspection Characteristic. If they are modified in the Characteristic the changes will be reflected in the Inspection Plan. If you do not select this checkbox you can manually modify the list of possible attributes in this tab. 15 Click on “Create Inspection Characteristic” to confirm. Inspection Characteristic is now assigned to inspection line. 16 You can repeat that step for each Inspection Line as many times at it is necessary. 3 Inspection Determination Setup 3.1 Add Inspection Determination 1 In the Quick Acess field start typing “Inspection Determination” and open aproppriate screen. 2 A screen lists all of defined Inspection Determinations. In order to add a new Inspection Determination click on “ (Add)” button. In the popup select the Type – “Characteristic” and set the value of Revision to 1. 3 The Inspection Determination properties should open. On the first tab – “General” – enter the description – we suggest the name of Work Station for which the determination is defined. 4 Go to tab “Key Elements 1” tab. Fill the field "Facility" (e.g. KCC2). You can start typing in that field on click on “ (Link)” button and select from the list that pops up. 5 Select Product for which the Determination should be used. You can start typing in that field or click on “ (Link)” button and select the Product from the list. 6 Select “Wip Order Type” – Production Order. 7 Select Resource – in this case we need to assign the Work Station. Click on “Link” button. 8 In the list set the “Resource Type” filter to “6 – Equipment Resource Type”. In the “Equipment” column there is a list of Work Stations defined in the system. Select one. 9 Now all the necessary keys are defined, save it. 10 You can now assign Inspection Characteristic to that setup. Click on “ (Add)” button on the bottom list. 11 Search the list for the Inspection Characteristic you would like to assign. Double click on the Inspection Characteristic or click one and press the “ (Select)” button. 12 Characteristic is assigned to Inspection Determination. 13 You can modify this characteristic. Select it and click on " (Properties)" button. 14 Go to "Details" tab. You can modify the attribute list there. If you check the checkbox "Inherit data from Characteristic" you will not be able to modify attributes, and any changes done on the Inspection Characteristic level will be reflected here. 15 Repeat previous steps to assign more Inspection Characteristics. You can assign as many characteristics as you want, and save the changes you’ve done. 16 The Inspection Determination is now created. 4 Quality Gate Setup The Work Station is treated as Quality Gate only if there is a Unit Characteristic called K1X_WorkStation_QualitGate with Attribute Y assigned to it. If the attribute value is N or there is no such characteristic – it is not a Quality Gate. 4.1 Quality Gate Setup 1 In the Quick Access field start typing “Equipment Explorer” and open appropriate screen. 2 The list will display all equipment's(workstations) defined in the system. Select the on which you would like to set as Quality Gate and double click on it. You can also select it and click on “ (Properties)” button. 3 Equipment properties will be displayed. 4 Go to “Links” tab and click on “Unit Characteristics”. 5 The list of Unit Characteristics assigned to that equipment will be displayed. 6 The characteristic called “K1X_WorkStation_QualityGate” determines if the Work Station is Quality Gate. You need to assign it to Work Station and set its attribute to “Y”. Click on “ (Add)” button to assign the Characteristic. 7 Enter the Characteristic name. You can just start typing in the field “Characteristic”. The name you are looking for is “K1X_WorkStation_QualityGate”. 8 Fill in the Attribute value, Press “Save” button and close the screen. 9 The characteristic is now assigned.

short_description: QFB & QIM - How to create and map users for QFB QIM application
introduction: How To create and map users for QFB & QIM in Appian
instructions: Introduction: This document describes the steps involved in User Creation for QFB. There are two levels involved in the user creation process. Database Level Appian Level Database Level: 1. A excel file which contains the users to be created will be sent by vesa or ISC Team. 2. We have to create a file to import into the table “ QFB_REF_EMPLOYEE_DETAILS ” Below contains the column name of the table to be matched with the file that contains the values for user creation: · EMAIL_ADDRESS à KONE mail address · MOBILE_NUMBER à Mobile Phone(or landline if no mobile) · BRANCH à branch office · FIRST_NAME à Surname, First name · LAST_NAME à Surname, First name · KONE_USER_ID à Kone ID · SUPERVISOR_USER_NAME à Default supervisor in appian · POSITION à Appian Role · FRONT_LINE à will be given by vesa or ISC team · LANGUAGE_KEY à Default Language · COUNTRY_CODE à will be given by vesa or ISC team · IS_ACTIVE à 1 (default value) · SOURCE à Manual Data (Default value) · EMPOLYEE_ID à No need to specify (Primary Key) 3. Convert the created file in the ods format (make sure that the tab name of the sheet should be the exact name of the Table) 4. Import the File into the database. 5. After creating the user , using update query update the values for the CREATED_ON, CREATED-BY column. Whenever the values to be updated in the table after its creation, using update query update the values for the UPDATED_ON, UPDATED_BY column. APPIAN LEVEL: After importing the ods file into the database, we have to create a new version files for the documents QFB User Mapping and QFB User Creation QFB User Creation: 1. Open the blank Excel 2. Create three columns as shown in below · Username à KONE_USER_ID · Name à FIRST_NAME LAST_NAME · Email à EMAIL_ADDRESS 3. Save the file with the name as QFB User creation 4. In the QFB USER CREATION application upload this file as the new version for the QFB user creation document. QFB User Mapping: 1. Open the blank Excel 2. Create two columns as shown below · UserID à KONE_USER_ID · Groups à Groups to be added for the user. Groups are categorized based on the position of the users. 3. Save the file with the name as QFB User Mapping 4. In the QFB USER CREATION application upload this file as the new version for the QFB user Mapping document. 5. Run the process model QFB wrapper process for user creation to create the users in the Appian 6. Run the process model QFB Wrapper for Add users to Groups for mapping the users to the group. 7. While creating users in Prod, We need to make sure that 'AZURE' value should be given in Custom Field2 (User Profile Property). So User will be only added into 'KEA Azure Group' not added into 'KEA SAML Group'. Error Handling: · If the user is already present in the system, the process model for user creation will throw an error. · If the version file doesn’t contains the exact structure as mentioned in the screenshot, the process model will throws an error · If there are any issues or group mismatch in the excel the process model will be errored out. Double check the file that is created as the new version file to be loaded to avoid these mentioned errors. FOR QIM Please add extra group for the users QIM All Users

short_description: How to request access to Apptio Roles using MyAccess
introduction: How to request access to Apptio Roles using MyAccess
instructions: 1. Open the MyAccess tool from browser. Link to MyAccess is: MyAccess 2. Home Page of MyAccess looks like below. It has options to navigate to different tasks. It shows the name of the user logged in. 3. ‘Manage My Access’ option is used to raise request for self. Hence, click on ‘Manage My Access’ 4. In the field for Search Access, type in Apptio and hit ‘Enter’ key or click on search icon. List of Apptio Roles will be seen on the page as shown below. 5. Select the specific Role by clicking on the ‘tick mark’ besides Role name. On selecting the button will be seen in green color. This indicated the user you have successfully selected. Click on Next button at the bottom of the page to proceed. 6. You can select multiple roles if you want to request access for multiple roles. (Please note that the approver can reject your request if they see that you don’t need the access to the multiple roles you have requested for. In such case, kindly confirm with the Approver, which is the correct role for you and request for the same.) 7. On the next page of Review and Submit, verify the access request being submitted for the user. Remove the ones not needed by clicking on ‘cross mark’ besides the Role name. After verification, make sure that enough comments are added, click on Submit button to submit the request or Cancel to cancel the whole request. 8. Request once submitted will go the respective approvers for their inputs. Based on their approval, provisioning of access will happen. (The request will be pending for approval with approver for 6 days after which it will be closed by the system. In such cases, you can request for the same access again after your old request has been closed by the system.) 9. Track My Request Option from Home Page will show all the requests raised by you and their respective status. 10. You can view the Request details by clicking on Details, see the status of the request, Cancel the request before it is completed and send an email to the approver by clicking on message icon. 11. Also note that , you cannot re-request for the same role until your previous request is closed by the system or rejected by the approver.

short_description: Ask IT - Application failing to Download in Software center
introduction: The purpose of this knowledge article is to outline Application failing to download/Install in software center. This article will help in identifying if application is failing in download or installing phase.
instructions: Application installed from software centre, generally take place in two phases: a) Download of Application b) Installation of Application Download of Application : ü Once installation is initiated from software centre, first source file gets downloaded in c:\windows\ccmcache directory. Status will change to downloading ü If downloading is stuck to 0% or just get failed or retry error, then check the ccmcache folder size and if it’s greater than 10gb, then clean-up the ccmcache folder. Installation of Application: ü Once downloading phase is completed the installation phase initiates and status changes to installing ü once status is changed to installing i.e. application’s source file has been downloaded (source file can be checked in ccmcache directory) and installation is triggered. ü You can install the application from source media as well. a) Open cmd as admin b) Copy the directory path of source media which has install.vbs file and paste it in cmd. c) Once you are in the source directory type in cscript install.vbs file to initiate the installation. d) Cursor in cmd will start flashing until the application gets installed.

short_description: MES - Clear Cache & Temp Files (Desktop/Laptop) - Restarting MES System
instructions: Clear chrome browser cache 1. Go to Google Chrome browser, type the following URL chrome://settings/clearBrowserData 2. New screen open, At the top of the "Clear browsing data" window, click Advanced 3. Select the following : a. Browsing history b. Download history c. Cookies and other site data d. Cached images and files 4. From the "Time range" drop-down menu, you can choose the period of time for which you want to clear cached information. To clear your entire cache, select All time. 5. Click CLEAR DATA. Exit/quit all browser windows and re-open the browser. Clear Temp Files Programs often store temporary files on your hard drive. Over time, these files can start to take up a lot of space. If you are running low on hard drive space, clearing temporary files is a good way to reclaim additional disk storage space. 1.Press the Windows Button + R to open the "Run" dialog box and Enter below Text %temp% 2.Click "OK." This will open your temp folder 3.Press Ctrl+A to select All 4.Press "Delete" on your keyboard and click "Yes" to confirm. 5.All temporary files will now be deleted. Note: Some files may not be able to be deleted. If you are prompted about files that are missing or in use, click the check box next to "Do this for all current items" and click "Skip.". Restarting MES System Reboot you client machine on weekly basis on which MES system is running. Restarting your Computer Flushes RAM. RAM stands for Random Access Memory and is your computer's main type of memory. Fast Performance. Reboots are known to keep computers running quickly. Stops Memory Leaks. These occur when a program doesn't close properly. Fixes Internet Connection issues

short_description: Link & Instructions to KPM2 site
introduction: Link & Instructions to KPM2 site
instructions: Dear KPM User, If you face any challenge with logging in to the new KPM site, please follow below steps. If the issue is not resolved even after trying below steps please raise an incident with CPSD team ( CommonPlatforms.ServiceDesk@kone.com ). Step 1: Use below KPM2 URL: https://konebpm.appiancloud.com/suite/sites/kpmsoalandingpage Login using KONENET\username or your email address and system password Step 2: Click on button “Navigate to KPM New UI” to navigate to new KPM2 site. Use this link to create and process new requests including both email and manual. HOME TAB Click on the home tab to perform below actions. · Create Request: Click on the “Create Request” button for creating new request. · Search Workflow Requests: Click on “Search Workflow Requests” to search for any request related information. · Tempo Actions: Click on “Tempo Actions” button to access Tempo actions present in KPM. · KPM Dashboard Application: Click on “KPM Dashboard Application” button to navigate to KPM2 Dashboard application page. · Consistency Check: Click on the “Consistency Check” button to perform the consistency check run. · User Profile Settings: Click on “Profile” button to view your profile. Click on “Settings” to view and change the profile settings like: language, time zone, Calendar type, email and mobile notification. After the changes are done click on “Save Changes” button to save the changes or on “Cancel” to cancel all the changes done. Click on “Sign Out” button to log out from the KPM application. TASKS TAB Click on the “Tasks” tab to access all task reports. DASHBOARD TAB Click on “Dashboard” tab to navigate to the KPM2- Reporting Dashboard to view all SSC and E2E open requests.

short_description: Link & Instructions for reporting database issues
introduction: Link & Instructions for reporting database issues
instructions: 1. Description : Add below field in the table ETL_CompanyCode_Area Field Name: SAPCompanyCodeAndName Datatype: Varchar(200) Query : alter table ETL_CompanyCode_Area add SAPCompanyCodeAndName varchar(200) Solution : Open the Sql server select the apian_report04 database run the above query. 2. Description : Insert New_SSC_Mapping1 column in below 3 tables in appian_report04 Table names : REP_Request_Feedback_Details REP_ProcessNotes REP_Snapshot Query : alter table REP_Request_Feedback_Details add New_SSC_Mapping1 varchar(260) alter table REP_ProcessNote add New_SSC_Mapping1 varchar(260) alter table REP_Snapshot add New_SSC_Mapping1 varchar(260) Solution : Open the Sql server select the apian_report04 database run the above queries. 3. Description : Delete the Request id 3755547 from REP_WorkflowToolRequests table appian_report04 db. Query: delete from [REP_WorkflowToolRequests] where RequestID ='3755547' Solution: Open the Sql server select the apian_report04 database run the above queries 4. Description : Add the below script in the [dbo].[sp_RequestCalculatedFields UPDATE REP_WorkflowToolRequests SET New_SSC_Mapping1 = Site_Center Where New_SSC_Mapping1 <> Site_Center and (Site_Center <> '' Or Site_Center is not NULL) and [ProcessStartTime] >'2020-04-01 00:00:00' Solution: Open the Sql server choose apian_report04-> Programmability-> Stored Procedure-> [dbo].[sp_RequestCalculatedFields] update the above script. 5. Description: ETL_Staging_Appian_Report Data Push 12:00 AM Job fail Error : Msg 1205, Level 13, State 51, Line 6 transaction (Process ID 62) was deadlocked on lock resources with another process and has been chosen as the deadlock victim. Rerun the transaction. Solution: Open the Sql server choose sql server agent->Jobs-> ETL_Staging_Appian_Report Data Push 12:00 AM Job fail->Right click-> start job 6. Description: Add the below script in SLA stored procedure in to ETL jobs UPDATE REP_SLA SET StatusClosed = FROM (SELECT RequestID FROM [REP_WorkflowToolRequests] WHERE [RequestStatus] = 'Closed' and [RequestEndDate] < (Today()-150) ) AS RWT WHERE REP_SLA.RequestID = RWT.RequestID Solution: Open the Sql server choose apian_report04-> Programmability-> Stored Procedure-> [dbo]. [sp_SLA_Fill] update the above script. 7. Description : Change the time lapse in recent history views from 3 days 4 days ALTER VIEW [dbo] . [vw_RecentHistory] AS SELECT a_id , requestid , updatetype , updatetime , requeststatus , openqueue , phase , LEFT( extrainformation , 4000 ) AS ExtraInformation FROM appian_prod . dbo . wftrequesthistoryupdate WITH ( NOLOCK ) WHERE ( updatetime > DATEADD ( hh , - 72 , GETDATE ())) GO Solution: Open the Sql server choose appian_report04->Views-> Open vw_RecentHistory as per above script mention 96 instead of 72.

short_description: QFB - Automation Data Load Process
introduction: Automation Data Load Process- QFB
instructions: 1. Introduction This document describes a high level of technical design from SharePoint, Appian and Database level. 2. Purpose of the process Currently ISC Team sends Excel file through email, and One person verifies data and load data in reference tables like QFB_REF_EQUIPMENT_DETAILS, QFB_REF_ADDRESS_DETAILS & QFB_REF_ADDRESS_RELATION_DETAILS. In order to reduce manual effort, We have created one process which extracts files from SharePoint, Verify data against the Main Table in Appian and load data. 3. Sharepoint Level ISC Team places the file in Sharepoint Folder. To achieve this, Some Folders have been created under Attachments Application and security is given them to access it. 1. INPUT : ISC Team places the file into this folder. There are two sub folders : ● EquipmentWithAddressDetails - The file which has equipment details, address details and address relation details should be placed under this folder ● EquipmentWithoutAddressDetails - The file which has only equipment details (To Update Existing Details, or added new equipment details for existing project) should be placed under this folder. 2. WIP (Work In Progress ) : When Appian extracts file from Input folder then places it under WIP folder. If there is any issue or data could not be loaded into DB then file will remains in this folder 3. DONE : When Data is loaded successfully then the file will be moved from WIP folder to Done Folder. It contains all files whichever are loaded successfully. 4. LOGFILE : It contains all log files how many entries got inserted and for which file. 4. Appian Level: Please find out the used Process Models below: ● QFB SHP Master Data Load (PM) - This process model is called a Master Process which runs @8:00 PM Europe time zone everyday and triggers wrapper process models to extract files from Sharepoint and take it further. ● QFB SHP Wrapper Data Load (PM) - This process is used to extract files from Sharepoint and pass documents (one by one) to the Data Load Process. And Execute Stored Procedure to truncate all Temporary tables. ● QFB SHP Data Load (PM) - This process is used to load data into temporary tables and execute Stored Procedures for loading data into Main Tables. If there is any error occurred then Email will be sent to Data Admin. Please find out the used Integrations: ● QFB_SHP_moveDocument ● QFB_SHP_downloadDocumentFromFolder ● QFB_SHP_getDocumentFromFolder ● QFB_SHP_uploadDocument Please find out the used other objects: ● QFB_TEMP_AddressRelationDetails ● QFB_TEMP_REF_AddressDetails ● QFB_TEMP_REF_EquipmentDetails ● QFB_getWordingForDataLoadLogFile ● QFB_getDateFromExcelFormatData ● QFB_SP_REF_EQUIPMENT_DETAILS ● QFB_SP_TRUNCATE_REF_TABLES ● QFB_SP_REF_ADDRESS_AND_ADDRESS_RELATION_DETAILS ● QFB_FLAG_FOR_DATA_LOAD_PROCESS 5. Database Level: There are 3 temporary tables and 3 stored procedures have been created for this process. Please find the tables & stored procedures: ● QFB_TEMP_REF_EQUIPMENT_DETAILS ● QFB_TEMP_REF_ADDRESS_DETAILS ● QFB_TEMP_REF_ADDRESS_RELATION_DETAILS ● QFB_SP_REF_EQUIPMENT_DETAILS -- This SP is used to update existing equipment details and insert new equipment details. The logic is added based on SL_ORDER_NUMBER and KEN to identify the existing details. ● QFB_SP_REF_ADDRESS_AND_ADDRESS_RELATION_DETAILS - This SP is used to check whether the data is already present in Address Relation table, if yes then delete those entries from Temporary tables otherwise insert data into QFB_REF_ADDRESS_DETAILS, take AddressId and map into Address Relation Data. ● QFB_SP_TRUNCATE_TEMP_TABLES -- This SP is used to truncate the temporary tables. 6 . Error Handling : There are some error handling based on Sharepoint and database. Please find out the errors below: ● The file is opened by others then integration will be error out and email will be sent to Vesa and Payal. (Please add email id to this constant QFB_EMAIL_ADDRESS_FOR_APPIAN_ADMIN) ● If the database is down then email will be sent to Appian Admin (Vesa & Payal) ● If there is any issue in Excel file then Rollback will be done, file will be placed into WIP Process and email will be sent to ISC Team for the same. 7. QFB Data Load Tasks: QFB Data load task should be submitted to load data into QFB Reference Tables. Please follow the below steps: Click on this link: https://konetest.appiancloud.com/suite/tempo/actions Click on Action tab then Click on QFB Data Load Action After Clicking, Click on Submit button to start the Data Load Process Once the Submit button is clicked, Data Load Process will be started. As of Now, I have given security to Vesa, Divynath, Amita and me for this process. If someone else needs to have this access then please add into this group QFB Data Load Users .

short_description: Escalated Status in CSD case
introduction: How can an internal admin/ Customer service case be escalated?
instructions: The customer can escalate a case by writing an email. How to Escalate: 1. In order to escalate the case the below mandatory fields must be filled: · CS&Area · Escalated To · Case Classification · Escalated Checkbox · Escalation Comment. 2. Then, whenever the agent sent the mail to a user other than contact email/supplied email the status will be changed to escalated automatically and the field "Approver Email" will be filled with the recipient email address automatically. 3. A new CS Task will be created whenever the case is Escalated. Actions to be taken by Escalated to user: The User can either send a reply email to the email received when case is escalated which will change the status of task created to Completed automatically and the status of case will be changed to Open. The Agent can also manually change the status of task to Completed which again will the status of case will be changed to Open automatically. SLA for escalated status: The SLA of the case keeps on running for the escalated status normally. 4. When Feedback Request status for Data Request was changed to ‘resolved’ and there are no other Feedback Request records in status ‘new’, related to the same case, Data Request Status automatically is changed back to ‘Sent to SSC’ and Case Status is changed to "Escalated". 5.On successful http request to send Data Request data to KPM, CS_Data_Request_Status__c is set to ‘Sent to SSC' and case Status is set to 'Escalated’.

short_description: Ask IT - User account lockdown /account lock
introduction: After password change user unable to log in to the device, The account is locked several times per day, The unknown reason, unable to track the source of the locks
instructions: Below guide can help you resolve the issue , Please consider other devices as well, tablets, phones , all account should be sign out and sign back in , 1) Open Control Panel > User Account > Credential Manager > Remove all Saved Password in Web Credentials and Windows Credentials or 2) Start -> run ->type control userpasswords2 select Advanced -> Manage passwords and remove all the stored passwords. 3) Remove passwords by clicking on Start => Run => rundll32.exe keymgr.dll KRShowKeyMgr Delete the Domain-related passwords 4) Remove passwords in Internet Explorer => Tools => Internet Options =>Content => Auto Complete => Delete AutoComplete history; 5) Delete cookies in Internet Explorer => Tools => Internet Options =>General=>Delete=>check the Password tickbox and select Delete; 6) Disconnect all networks drives (note the path before disconnecting), reboot, then map them again; 7) Reconfigure Your mobile Setting if your Active sync enabled. 8) Check if any saved or scheduled task is configured for user account

short_description: PRO Access in Management Org
introduction: What are the steps to provide PRO access in management org.
instructions: PRO Management Org Access Request Process Step 1: Approvals The approval needs to be taken from Management org Key Stakeholder (Tommi Paavilainen) However, if the bulk user access request needs access for more than 10 users, the first approval needs to be taken from Solution Owner - (Kirsi Anturaniemi) and then, the second approval needs to be taken from Management org Key Stakeholder (Tommi Paavilainen) If the request is raised by Management org Key Stakeholder (Tommi Paavilainen), then there is no need to take any approval from Management org Key Stakeholder (Tommi Paavilainen). To send approval request, update Approving manager on ticket and then press 'submit for approval'. Ticket status will change to Pending automatically. Once the ticket is approved by approver, it will automatically change to In Progress. (In absence of SO, take the approval from SDO) Steps to approve a request, incase the approver asks: Ask IT Portal > Manage Approvals > Approve/Reject Step 2: Create or Edit User Details Search for user in global search. If given user is not present, create new user from the Setup. For user details to be filled, follow new user details mapping mentioned for Business org - KBA1492 Note: Skip the field mapping mentioned for fields not present in Management org. Profile must always be 'Standard platform user'. Access in this org is governed by Permission sets. Role must always be 'Employees' 'Generate new password and notify' checkbox: Always keep this unchecked. Once you find the user, select User detail as mentioned below. Step 2: Edit following User Details: User license: Make sure the user license is always 'Salesforce Platform' and profile must be 'Standard platform user' Email: If ".invalid" is present at the end of the email, remove it. Make sure the email id is in sync with IT org. Federation ID: This Id should be kept same as is available against the user in IT org. 'Generate new password and notify' checkbox: Always keep this unchecked. Step 3: Add permission set: To add permission set, go to permission Set Assignments and add the PRO permission set either (read/editor) mentioned in the ticket. i.e. XACT KONE Enterprise Editor/XACT KONE Enterprise Read Only Step 4: Add Risk Manager License: To add License, in user detail go to Managed Packages, click on Assign Licenses and add Risk Manager license. Step 5: Add user to public group: We have two public groups for each branch in org hierarchy; read public group for Read Only request and Read/Write Group for Editor Request. Go to Setup > Public Groups, select suitable group based on the Editor or Read only access and select Edit. eg: Australia-Xactium-R/W for having editor access over Australia Branch. Search for your user in the list and add. Requested Access is now provided to the user. Inform the users via email and ask for confirmation. Step 6: End User communication Email Single User : Once the access is provided to the users, we should send them an email from Remedy force that he/she is granted the access to PRO and he/she should now start using the tool. Multiple/Bulk Users : If we have to process mass access requests, send out a common email from Outlook to all the users, keeping all the users in BCC, keeping Kirsi Anturaniemi and Balamurugan Vijayabalan in CC. Kindly also attach the User Guide to this email which you will find in the files related list of this KBA. Email format: (Email body) Hi <user:firstname>, Greetings! You have been provided access to PRO Management org. In order to login and access the system please follow the instructions given in attached user guide document. This service request is resolved. In case of any issues with the access, please reopen the service request within 5 days before it is closed. Kindly, note that once the service request is set to closed status, it cannot be reopened, and a new service request needs to be created. Note: Please login to the system within 30 days after the access has been granted. Failing which, the access will be revoked automatically. Regards, PRO Staff Note: User Guide is attached in Files (At the top, under Related List Quick Links)

short_description: Ask IT - How to Run antivirus Scan on Defender Windows 7/Windows 10
introduction: How to Run antivirus Scan on Defender Windows 7/Windows 10
instructions: How to run Antivirus scan on Windows Defender Windows 10 1) Select Start, and then select Settings > Update & Security > Windows Security > Virus & threat protection. 2) Click on Scan Options 3) Select “Full Scan” and select Scan now Windows 7 System Center 2012 Endpoint Protection offers three types of scans: 1) Quick Scan - this will scan only these items on your computer which are exposed the most to the risk of having a virus and where the impact of having it is the highest. Therefore it will scan the following: in-memory processes, files in user profiles, files in the system directory and files in the Program Files folder. 2) Full Scan - this will scan all in-memory process and all files stored on local disks of your computer. Full scan will take much longer to complete then the quick scan. 3) Custom Scan - this will allow you to select specific locations on your disks which you would like to examine. Click on the scan option to run full scan .

short_description: MES - Instruction to Use Operation Grid Configuration (OGC)
instructions: Click here >> to access the full document Operation Grid Configuration (OGC) Process 1. Open Apriso Desktop Client and login to the application. 2. Start Typing OGC in the search field and click on it to open the screen. 3. Screen will open and it will show already configured workcenter and you will find the Add, Duplicate, Remove, Edit and Details button on the right bottom of the screen. 4. When user clicks on the Add button, a pop will open with Save and Cancel Button. Here user can enter the profile name and select the screen name for which the configuration will be applicable. User can select the Facility, Production Line, Work Center and Employee. You can also leave all those fields empty. If you set, for e.g., the Production Line – the configuration will apply only to this Production Line. If you leave it empty – it will apply to all Production Lines. It works in the same way for Work Center, Facility and Employee. If the checkbox Mandatory is checked, you will not be able to delete this profile. Save the configuration by clicking on the Save Button. 5. Newly created profile will be visible on the Screen. 6. Select the record and click on Details button. (When user select the profile all the disabled button will be enabled). On doing so a new Window OGC-20 will open. This window displays all the configured columns which will be visible when using the profile. (If no column is added then it will show the empty screen.). It will also show the Back, Add and Remove Button. 7. When user clicks on the Add button a new pop up will open where user will enter the column name, Sequence No, Select the Sort type ASC or DESC, Visible check box. 8. On click of save button the newly created record will be visible on the OGC-20 Screen. This way user can add multiple columns in their created profile. 9. User can also edit the created profile by selecting it and clicking on edit button, where the changes can be made in the pop-up window that opens. 10. User will be able to see the changes made by clicking on the save button. 11. The user can duplicate a record by clicking on the duplicate button. 12. Similarly the records can be removed y selecting them and clicking on remove button. When user clicks on yes button the profiles will get deleted.

short_description: Synchronize new attribute in 1Plan application
introduction: What are the activities need to be performed after adding or deleting any attribute or attribute value in 1Plan?
instructions: When attributes or attribute values are added in 1Plan Demand Planner, the existing spaces using those attributes become out of sync. This is because of the new base forecast units which are required to be created with respect to the new attributes or values. For example: After adding a new attribute/attribute value in Org level 6. All those spaces which were configured to include all attributes from Org Level 6, will now be out of sync. Because these spaces do not contain the new base forecast units. To fix this following activity can be performed: 1. Go to Forms >> master data >> Attribute spaces 2. In the Attribute spaces form right click on the attribute space with (the constraint) and select the option “Update base forecast units in space” Secondly, after the base forecast units (BFUs) are updated. The storage profiles which are using the effected Attribute Spaces are also needs to be refreshed. To do this follow the steps mentioned below: 1. Go to Form >> Other configuration >> Profiles >> Storage profiles 2. In the form Storage profiles, right click on the effected storage profile (with constraint) and click “Refresh storage forecast units” Conclusion: After adding / removing any attribute/attribute value the related attribute spaces and storage profiles related to those attribute space should be updated using the steps mentioned above.

short_description: Test Account Management in MyAccess
introduction: What is test account? Who can create? How to create Test account in MyAccess? How to update Test account in MyAccess? How to view Test account in MyAccess? Warning Messages before disable of Test account. Disable/Enable/Delete Test account.
instructions: What is Test account? · Software and testing teams often need to create test accounts to AD for manual testing, but the accounts are often "forgotten" after testing and they stay enabled in AD without owner information. These forgotten enabled accounts pose a security risk for KONE. Therefore, all test accounts are to be managed from MyAccess. · Test account name will be auto generated with “TEST_”. Who can create? · Test accounts for software testing can be created to AD and life cycle managed from MyAccess only by users who have a role “MyAccess: Test Account Manager” assigned in MyAccess. · If user does not have above role, option for ‘Test Account’ will be not be shown on “Create AD account” -page. · You can raise a request in MyAccess for this by searching for role “MyAccess: Test Account Manager”. The request will come for review and approval of Identity and ccess Management team. How to create Test account in MyAccess · Login to MyAccess: https://myaccess.kone.com · On the Home page you need to select from top-left corner's drop down (icon with 3 lines) Manage Access > Manage Identity > Create AD Account · On click of ‘Create AD account’, account type selection form is presented to user with option for ‘Test Account’ · If user does not have role ‘MyAccess: Test Account Manager’, option for ‘Test Account’ will be not be shown. In that case, you need to navigate to “Manage User Access” and request for yourself that role. · If user has the role, user is presented with account creation form with fields to be filled up such as – o Full Account Name Please note that the full account name will be generated with “TEST_”+ the account name given in field. o Account Name (required field) § Max characters allowed 15. § No Special characters allowed. § Account Name should be unique. o System/Application Name (required field) o Valid from (required field) refers to date since when account is created. o Valid to (required field) refers to the date until when the account will be active. Test account will expire and get disabled after this date if no actions are taken by the account owners to extend the validity period. You can select a date maximum 180 days in future. o Country (required field) refers to the region where Test Account belongs. This is requested merely for technical reasons. o Description of account (required field) Please describe what is the account used for. § Max characters allowed 50. o Owner (required field) is the primary owner of the account who will receive the initial Test account password. o Deputy owner (required field) is the other owner of the account who will be able to also update the account during its life cycle. · On filling up the fields of form, user need to click ‘Next’ button on right corner. This will present a read only form with entered details for different fields. To make any changes to field values, user need to click ‘Back’ button on left corner. If entered details are correct, then user need to click ‘Submit’ button on right corner of UI page. · Test Account will be created in Active Directory. Newly created test account will be memberOf AD group -TestAccountGroup. Password for created account is sent by email to account owner. How to update test account in MyAccess Login to MyAccess https://myaccess.kone.com . On the Home page you need to select from top-left corner's drop down (icon with 3 lines) Manage Access > Manage Identity > Edit Test account. · By clicking ‘Edit AD account’, account selection form is presented to user with dropdown list option to select the account for edit. · On click of ‘Edit’ button on right corner, Edit Test Account page opens with some of the fields available for update. Fields available for update are o System/Application Name o Valid To o Description o Deputy Owner o Owner · On filling up the fields of form, user need to click ‘Confirm’ button on right corner. This will present a read only form with entered details for different fields. To make any changes to field values, user need to click ‘Back’ button on left corner. If entered details are correct, then user need to click ‘Submit’ button on right corner of UI page. This will submit the form for Test Account update. How to view Test account in MyAccess? Login to MyAccess: https://myaccess.kone.com . On the Home page you need to select from top-left corner's drop down (icon with 3 lines) > Manage Identity > View Identity. On this page, in search box enter the created test account name and click on search. This should show the created Test account. Note : Only Internals can view the all the Test Accounts created (through search option). Warning Message before Test Account Disabled. MyAccess will send multiple warnings to both Owner and Deputy Owner before Test account is disabled so that the owners will have enough time to go extend the validity if needed. The warnings are being sent 30, 14, 7 and 1 day(s) before the validity of the account ends. Disable/Enable/Delete Test Account MyAccess will disable Test account whenever it has reached its validity period. Owner and Deputy Owner will receive notification when Test account has been disabled including the instructions on how to re-enable the account again. Test account can be re-enabled within 30 days from disablement. After that, Test account will be permanently deleted. Owners will receive automatic notification when test account has been deleted by the system.

introduction: How to request SAP Invoice Verification access? How to request invoice approver access? How to request invoice reviewer access? How to request SAP Invoice Verification access removal?
instructions: Request for reviewer access § Go to ServiceNow ESC link: Kone Employee Center Homepage - Kone Employee center (service-now.com) § Search and select the following catalog item - SAP Offering for Finance and Reporting § From the dropdown service type, choose "SAP Fiori Invoice Verification Access" § Request for reviewer access § Select “New user in invoice verification/approval” if the request is made for a user who does not yet have invoice verification access . This ensures that the correct SAP role is assigned to the user: § Select Access Type “Reviewer”: § Select the Company Code from drop down list: § The request can be sent for information by selecting the person in field “Notify”. Please note that only one person can be selected: Note : Request can be created for both reviewer and approver access at the same time by selecting both options. § Additional comments and attachments can be included if needed, but these are not required. § Click “Submit” when ready § System sends notification email of the created service request to the requestor § Request for reviewer authorization does not require approval. Please note that addition of the SAP role (in case of a new user) requires Line Manager approval. Request for approval will be sent to the Line Manager via email. § Requestor receives notification email when the request is completed Note : Request can be created also on behalf of another person by selecting “Other” and by searching the person by name. Both, Requested by and Requested for, persons receive the notifications of the request. Request for approver access § Go to ServiceNow ESC link: Kone Employee Center Homepage - Kone Employee center (service-now.com) § Search and select the following catalog item - SAP Offering for Finance and Reporting § From the dropdown service type, choose "SAP Fiori Invoice Verification Access" Note : Request can be created also on behalf of another person by selecting “Other" and by searching the person by name. Both, the Requested by and Requested for, persons receive the notifications of the request. § Select “New user in invoice verification/approval” if the request is made for a user who does not yet have invoice verification access . This ensures that the correct SAP role is assigned to the user: § Select Access Type “Approver” § Select the Company Code and Approval Limit Category from the drop-down lists: Note : Approval limit categories A to E are restricted only to nominated Area and Global roles. The limit will be granted only after the request is approved by Head of Internal Controls § Select also the local currency if the limit should be assigned in local currency. The default currency is EUR and if no other currency is given, the limit will be assigned in EUR Note : If any other currency is selected other than EUR, the conversion from the EUR limit amount is done according to predefined conversion table. The conversion table can be found by clicking the symbol: Note : Request can be created for both reviewer and approver authorization at the same time by selecting both options. The request can be sent for information by selecting the person in field “Notify”. Please note that only one person can be selected § Comments and attachments can be included if needed, but these are not required. § Click “Submit” when ready § System sends notification email of the created service request to the requestor § Note : Approver access request for categories F to L is approved by the Line Manager of the person (Requested for). The Line Manager receives the request to approve via email. § Note : Categories A to E are restricted only to nominated Area and Global roles, and the limit is granted only after the request is approved by Head of Internal Controls § The addition of SAP role (in case of a new user) requires Line Manager approval. Request for approval will be sent to the Line Manager via email. § Requestor receives notification email when the request is completed § If the request is rejected, the Requestor receives a notification email with information of the reason for rejection Request for reviewer and approver access removal - Option 1 § Go to ServiceNow ESC link: Kone Employee Center Homepage - Kone Employee center (service-now.com) § Search and select the following catalog item - SAP Offering for Finance and Reporting § From the dropdown service type, choose "SAP Fiori Invoice Verification Access" § Select “Revoke the access level”: § Select Access Type to be removed: § Select the applicable Company Code from drop down list: § If the removal is for Approver access, select also the approval limit to be removed: § Note : Request can be created for both reviewer and approver access removal at the same time by selecting both options. § The request can be sent for information by selecting the person in field “Notify”. Please note that only one person can be selected: § Additional comments and attachments can be included if needed, but these are not required. § Click “Submit” when ready Request for reviewer and approver access removal – Option 2 § To request for removal of reviewer or approver access, send email to CommonPlatforms.ServiceDesk@kone.com , cc DL_Infosys_AMS_FICO@KONE.com § Include in the request the username, SAP user ID and Company Code for which the removal is pertaining to Request for Invoice Verification Portal (IVT) access removal § All KONE units globally will deploy Fiori as invoice verification tool during 2021. § SAP accesses to IVT will be removed for all users during 2021 § However, if user wants to remove the SAP access to IVT before the removal is done centrally, then the request should be done as follows: – Go to the following ServiceNow link - Report an Issue(Incident) - Kone Employee center (service-now.com) – Fill in the details Global Invoice Approval Limit Categories

Producing hash values for accessing data or for security . A hash value (or simply hash ), also called a message digest, is a number generated from a string of text. ... short_description: How to restart the Nakisa service when URL is not accessible
introduction: How to restart the Nakisa service when URL is not accessible
instructions: If the Nakisa URL is not accessible, following steps to be followed to restart the server. Step 1: Login to the Remote Desktop 10.41.244.53 where Nakisa server is located. Step 2: Open the task manager and check CPU utilization. If the Common Daemons Service Runner is constantly on the higher side of the CPU usage then the Tomcat service needs to be restarted. Step 3: open Run and enter services.msc in the search box. Step 4: Once the services page is opened, select Apache Tomcat service and click on restart. Step 5: If the step 4 throws error only then end task from task manager. Step 6 : Check if the Nakisa Production URL is accessible.

short_description: AEM - PDF Publishing issue
introduction: How to fix PDF publishing issue in AEM Image not being loaded when transforming to PDF Expected scheme-specific part at index 5: x-wc Asset “Does not exist or cannot be loaded” Found a table row with more entries than allowed Missing navtitle attribute or element for non-DITA resource The column-number or number of cells in the row overflows the number of columns specified for the table Unable to parse invalid href attribute value for Reltable links Unable to parse invalid href attribute value for images Index 1 out of bounds for length 1 XML document structures must start and end within the same entity
instructions: How to fix PDF publishing issue in AEM AEM Support KBA - This document helps to fix DITA-OT FOP ERRORS which gives during PDF Publishing. Usually the FOP Error doesn’t point any topics instead gives the “topic.fo” line/column numbers. In this case it’s difficult to debug in AEM. So we are debugging in local DITA-OT installed with KONE stylesheets. Then the topic.fo log file to be given to user for further investigation. 1. Download the DITAMAP .zip file from AEM. Go to Topics tab in map console window à Select Download map à Click on Download Note: If you need to download using baseline then “use baseline”. Sometimes PDF publishing throws error for Publishing with particular baselines. 2. Extract the DITAMAP and save it in local temp folder( C:\temp ) 3. Go to DITA-OT folder in local and run the batch file. The command prompt window will open and press enter key to continue. 4. Check the ditamap path in AEM, find the same in downloaded ditamap. Run the same path in command prompt window as below dita -i=filename.ditamap -f=kone-pdf -t=temp Where, i = input file; f= output format; t= temp folder 5. The out and temp folder will be generated, if there is no error then the pdf will be generated in output folder. In case if there is any error then the output will not be generated. In the command window screen end part of the logs the line number of topic.fo should be displayed in brackets (e.g. 3252, 24). Check for topic.fo file in temp folder and identify the faulty topics. 6. In topic.fo file the topic title can be identified for debugging using the keyword fo:inline-container with the help of line / column number referred from AEM log file. i.e. 1 st fo:inline-container before the line numbers. Some of the common errors are listed below i) Image not being loaded when transforming to PDF The error [topic-reader] [DOTX008E][ERROR] File 'file:/content/dam/tech-doc/global/modular_shaft/pictures/pit_template.svg' does not exist or cannot be loaded may occur while generating PDF. In this case check for the image path in the xml code as follows : 1 . Download the DITAMAP .zip file from AEM. Go to Topics tab in map console window à Select Download map à Click on Download Note: If you need to download using baseline then “use baseline”. Sometimes PDF publishing throws error for Publishing with particular baselines. 2 . Extract the DITAMAP and c heck the DITAMAP path in AEM, find the same in downloaded DITAMAP. 3 . Right click the DITAMAP --> Edit with Notepad++. The DITAMAP's xml code will be opened. 4 . Search for the image used in the DITAMAP(ctrl+F -->Find in Files --> Enter Find what, Filters as *.* and the Directory --> Find all). The search results will be displayed at the bottom. 5 . The topic in which the mentioned image used can be found. Check for the image path used in all the topics (Double click on the Line number mentioned in the search result opens the respective topic). If it is not correct inform the end user to correct the image path used and generate the PDF. ii) Expected scheme-specific part at index 5: x-wc The error Expected scheme-specific part at index 5: x-wc may occur while generating PDF indicating the error in cross reference. This error w ill persist with all migrated topics with wrong cross reference. Dita-OT error log line as following: /mnt/crx/author/crx-quickstart/profiles/ditaot-8784935107382607754/DITA-OT/plugins/org.dita.base/build_preprocess2.xml:169: java.lang.RuntimeException: Expected scheme-specific part at index 5: x-wc: This error might happen if there's an imbalance in the table cells, likely caused by merging cells across different rows. Solution Identify topic with error. Locate the topic name or id closest to the X-WC error and inform end user. End user action: t\To go to the topic and r emove the internal cross-references (as it does not comply with KTD modular rules) iii) Asset “Does not exist or cannot be loaded” The error .dita ' does not exist or cannot be loaded may occur while generating PDF indicating the error in invalid file reference. This error w ill occur when an invalid file is referred. Dita-OT error log line as following: [topic-reader] [DOTX008E][ERROR] File 'file:/mnt/crx/author/crx-quickstart/profiles/ditamaps/ditamap4520623685520574437/ zh-CN/reusable_assets/legal/legal-notice-internal-document.dita ' does not exist or cannot be loaded. This error might happen if an invalid asset is referenced, which could be due to the asset being deleted or the user not having access to it. Solution Identify the topic that contains the error by referring to the error log to locate the specific topic or ditamap and inform end user. 2. End user action: To Navigate to the asset which has invalid file reference, remove the invalid reference file, and ensure the correct asset is in place. iv) Found a table row with more entries than allowed The error Found a table row with more entries than allowed may occur while generating PDF. Dita-OT error log line as following: This error might happen if there's an imbalance in the table cells, likely caused by merging cells across different rows. This error requires some validation within the XML editor. Solution Identify the topic with error (Manually from notepad ++) and inform end user. Suggest end user to seek support from the Key user and to c heck the instructions to resolve the issue. End user action: Open the source view of the topic with error. Check for “morerows” attribute and fix accordingly. v) Missing navtitle attribute or element for non-DITA resource The error Missing navtitle attribute or element for non-DITA resource may occur while generating PDF indicating the error where the external topics are not referred with the required attributes. Dita-OT error log line as following: [move-meta] file:/mnt/crx/author/crx-quickstart/profiles/ditamaps/ditamap6311583111744284527/en-us/elevator_components/car_slings/-AM-07.03.072-_ISCS10-Car-Sling.ditamap:1:5718: [DOTX021E][ERROR]: Missing navtitle attribute or element for non-DITA resource "39048c3ec30056e97a445a7fc8daf19e0db89a6b.dita". References must provide a local navigation title when the target is not a local DITA resource. [move-meta] file:/mnt/crx/author/crx-quickstart/profiles/ditamaps/ditamap6311583111744284527/en-us/elevator_components/car_slings/-AM-07.03.072-_ISCS10-Car-Sling.ditamap:1:5718: [DOTX025E][ERROR]: Missing linktext and navtitle for non-DITA resource "39048c3ec30056e97a445a7fc8daf19e0db89a6b.dita". References must provide a local navigation title when the target is not a local DITA resource. This error might happen if the required attributes like scope might not be added. Solution Identify the topic/document number of the external reference from the error log and inform end user. End user action: Go to the source view of the DITAMAP and search for the doc number identified from the error log (Ctrl + F in editor). Check the reltable topic attributes format, scope, topicref are correct. If unable to locate error forward to Keyuser. vi) The column-number or number of cells in the row overflows the number of columns specified for the table The error The column-number or number of cells in the row overflows the number of columns specified for the table may occur while generating PDF. This error might require some validation within XML editor. Old Windchill topics will have this error. Dita-OT error log line as following: This error might happen if there's an imbalance in the table cells, likely caused by merging cells across different rows. This error requires some validation within the XML editor. Solution Identify the topic with error (Manually from notepad ++) and inform end user. Suggest end user to seek support from the Key user and to c heck the instructions to resolve the issue. End user action: Open the source view of the topic with error. Check for “namest” & "namend" attributes and fix accordingly. vii) Unable to parse invalid href attribute value for Reltable links The error Unable to parse invalid href attribute value may occur while generating PDF when an invalid href is used in reltable. Dita-OT error log line as following: This error might happen when an invalid href is used. Mostly a para tag element while copying document number from PDM which is visible as space in the error log and in AEM source. Solution Copy the document number from the error log and share with the end user. End user action: Navigate to the source view of the DITAMAP and search for the document number (Ctrl +F search inside the xml editor). In the reltable href, remove the extra space at the end. viii) Unable to parse invalid href attribute value for images The error Unable to parse invalid href attribute value may occur while generating PDF when an invalid character is used in the image title whereas the new files will no longer allow invalid characters in the image title. Dita-OT error log line as following: [topic-reader] file:/mnt/crx/author/crx-quickstart/profiles/ditamaps/ditamap8600835219889287250/en-us/elevator_components/guide_rails/topics/Installation-requirement-for-anchor-channels-in-a-concrete-wall.dita:1:679: [DOTJ054E][ERROR] Unable to parse invalid href attribute value "../pictures/anchor channels in a concrete wall.svg", using '../pictures/anchor%20channels%20in%20a%20concrete%20wall.svg'. This error might happen when an invalid href is used, where the image title contains any spaces, special characters, etc,. Solution Identify the image name from the error log and share with the end user. End user action: Locate the image from the path mentioned in the error log. Check if the file name of the image has any invalid character. If yes, rename the file (Admin access required - end users may request support to rename the image). ix) Index 1 out of bounds for length 1 The error Index 1 out of bounds for length 1 may occur while generating PDF. Dita-OT error log line as following: This error might happen when empty header row is used in the table. This error requires some validation within the XML editor. Solution Identify the topic from the error log and share with the end user. End user action: Open the source view of the topic with error. Check for empty header and fix accordingly. x) XML document structures must start and end within the same entity The error XML document structures must start and end within the same entity may occur while generating PDF. Dita-OT error log line as following: This error might happen when incorrect references from the translation temp folder is used in the ditamap. This error requires some validation within the ditamap references. Solution Identify the reference(FMLANG-GUID) from the...

short_description: AEM - Translation process Manual Steps in AEM
introduction: A. Translation package missing from projects , how to create New Translation package manually . B. ERROR “Fetching Baseline” or invisible if they export baseline in “translation” tab C. Export Baseline fails due to "null"
instructions: Sometime even after creating translation package by end users, from projects folder unable to view the concern package, If this kind of issue reported by end users then support team needs to create manual package for the same. A. New Translation process manual /steps in AEM 1. Open the “en-us” DITAMAP which needs to send for translation. In the xml editor, under Manage tab, Go to translation --> Select target language -- > Baseline (select the baseline which has to be translated) --> Click on Apply. 2. Select the topics to be translated, then click on Send for Translation à Select a project à Create new translation project à enter the project title à Submit. The success banner will appear. 3. Close the DITAMAP and check for the notification. Click on the notification which will take to projects folder where the project created for translation is available. 4. Click on the drop down button in the Translation job card which will help to export and import the files. 5. The zip file will be downloaded which will be sent to vendor for translation. 6. After translation, the zip file is imported similar to export and the status of the translation job is changed to approved --> complete. 7. After translation, the status will be changed to Insync in manage tab. 8. Now translation is completed after see “Insync” in all assets. We can ensure by filtering by “Translation Status” won’t have “out of sync” or “missing copy” or “In Progress”. 9 . Now we need to “Export Baseline”. Once the “Export Baseline” clicked then the baselines are exported to translated target language DITAMAP with respect to new translated assets revisions. Note : If any issues in the translation then we might get failed notification in the “Export translation” like below: (we need to analyse based on error message). B. ERROR “Fetching Baseline” or invisible if they export baseline in “translation” tab This issue relates to baseline corrupted in the CRXDE(backend). We need remove the corrupted baselines in CRXDE backend then displays in AEM UI. C. Export Baseline fails due to "null" If the export baseline fails due to “null” in translation process then follow below steps : 1. Edit and rename the old corrupted baseline like “OLD” e.g. OLD_AM-01.01.251_M.1 baseline for publishing 2. Recreate new English(en-US) baseline with the same naming format as provided in the DITAMAP. 3. Use the same DITAMAP version in the existing corrupted Baseline. In the xml editor, Go to Manage --> Baselines tab --> Create Baseline --> Enter the Baseline name --> Click on Apply. The new baseline will be generated. Check for the ditamap version and update the same in the new baseline. 4. Go to “Translation” tab. Check the translation status with respect to “in progress”, “out of sync” and “Missing copy” using the new created baseline. If any such cases reported then we need to update our new baseline with the correct version (as per old baseline). 5. All the assets should be “insync” (i.e. no “in progress” or “missing copy” or “out of sync”) then fine. 6. The topics should be “insync” in translation tab for the respective target language and the baseline used. If any of the topics translation status is “OUT OF DATE” then change the version of the respective topics. 7. When the translation status of all the topics under the chosen DITAMAP are “Insync” click on “Export Baseline”. 8. The baseline will be exported successfully to the respective target language. 9. The exported baseline can be viewed in the respective target language folder.

short_description: Privileged Access Management KBA
introduction: How to Onboard New Application In PAM ? PAM Application Access and User Access Issue ? PAM Application host and Session connections Issue ? End User Troubleshooting Guide
instructions: New Application Onboarding In PAM 1. For an overview about PAM, please refer the attachment "What is PAM". 2. To check if the server is already on-boarded to PAM, verify against the attachment “Servers onboarded to PAM” which contains the list of servers that are already onboarded to PAM. We will be updating this attachment with newly onboarded servers every month. 3. Any new server onboarding in PAM is taken care by PAM Implementation team as per the scope defined. Ø In case user is requesting to onboard any application server Azure/AWS/On-Premise on urgent basis in PAM which is not yet onboarded, service request can be raised and routed to KONE.PAM.SUPPORT. Ø User need to fill required details in the attached format(sampleonboardingtemplate) and attach in the Service Request. 4. If server is already on-boarded, refer KBA # KBA5571 which has the instructions on how to request access to servers from MyAccess and login steps to PAM. 5. In case user is unable to find any particular application role to request from MyAccess, then service request can be raised for the same to KONE.PAM.SUPPORT. 6. Tickets where users are requesting normal RDP access for the servers that are not in PAM, should not be routed to PAM Support queue. Application Access and User Access Issue 1. User has to open Incident ticket in AskIt and ask agent route the ticket to KONE.PAM.SUPPORT assignmentgroup. 2. Description tab in ticket should be updated with error message or issue details. 3. Any Access issue when user try to login PAM, incident ticket can be routed to ‘ KONE.PAM.SUPPORT’ ’ Below are the common issue screenshots: 1.) PAM URL is not accessible for User Case1 : Issue: if URL is not accessible for multiple users (External users and internal users both) Root cause: · Privx Instances are down · Privx database is down · Network Issue Steps to be followed: Major Incident should be raised as P1 priority. Case 2: Issue: If URL is not accessible for single user Root cause: · User do not have any role for accessing PAM. · User have not been mapped to required role. · Role configuration for particular application is not correct 1: Internal Users >User is having Kone laptop and accessing it inside Kone network. Steps to be verified: >user should have minimum 1 PAM role assigned to him >If yes and still url is not accessible route the ticket to BPC/VPN team. >If not having any role, please ask him request role from MyAccess and get it aproved. Refer KBA- # KBA5571(ASKIT Portal) 2: External Users > User is not having Kone laptop and accessing laptop outside Kone network. Steps to be verified: >Validate from user that he/she is connected to KONE network via VPN(Pulse secure). >Validate that user has the role ‘PAM: BPC Access’ assigned (This role provides BPC (Business Partner Channel) remote access to PAM solution for external users who need to access the portal without KONE laptop) >Verify whether user is having at least one PAM role assigned in MyAccess Case 3: User’s MFA authentication is not working Root cause: · User has changed mobile device Solution : Please route ticket to PAM queue KONE.PAM.SUPPORT for resetting MFA. Windows or Linux host session connection Issue: Below are the screenshot related to session disconnection error: 1.) Session connection issues User is unable to connect to Windows/ Linux server Via PAM > Please route ticket to KONE.PAM.SUPPORT 2.) Required Host / Server is not visible in PAM Steps to be followed: >Validate that user has been added to roles required for the host in MyAccess >If Yes: Please route ticket to KONE.PAM.SUPPORT for further verifications >If not: instruct user to request for the PAM roles that he/she needs(follow KBA- # KBA5571) End User Guide Refer KBA5571 which will guide you how to request roles from MyAccess, login to PAM and access your required application target server.

short_description: How to improve report and dashboard performance?
introduction: How to improve report and dashboard performance?
instructions: Why Reports and Dashboards take time to load on UI? While we open Report or Dashboards, it will run the query on the background. If this query fetch huge data then it will take some time to display those records on UI. What you can do if you face performance issue for Report and Dashboards? You can check first if your machine meets the minimum technical requirements for Lightning Reports and Dashboards. 1. Lightning Reports and Dashboards Technical Requirements: https://help.salesforce.com/articleView?id=rd_reports_dashboards_technical_requirements.htm&type=5 2. Speed Test: KONE Business Org: https://kone.my.salesforce.com/speedtest.jsp KONE IT Org: https://konewayflow.my.salesforce.com/speedtest.jsp If your machines meet technical requirements, please check below best practices for created Report/Dashboards: 1. Improve Report Performance: Best Practices: https://help.salesforce.com/articleView?id=improving_report_performance.htm&type=5 2. Improve Dashboard Performance: Best Practices: https://help.salesforce.com/articleView?id=improving_dashboard_performance.htm&type=5 What IF above steps didn't resolve the performance of Lightning Reports/Dashboards? If your system matches above mention requirement and you are following best practice for the Report/Dashboards and still facing performance issue in Lightning Report/Dashboards, then please follow below step: 1: Create Ticket from ASK IT Portal. 2: Mention Report/Dashboard Ids. 3: Add screenshot of the Speed Test done by above link (Record loading time depend upon your system and network speed of your Internet.). What action we (AMS Team) will take? We will check from our side that can we add any filters in Reports/Dashboards? Still issue is there, then we will open Salesforce Case for you. We will work with them and update you.

instructions: This KBA for AEM Support Team common troubleshooting purpose 1.
