short_description: End user & Ask IT - Accessing PAM and connecting to the hosts using browser
introduction: Accessing PAM and connecting to the hosts using browser? Prerequisites to Access PAM? Steps to Login to PAM? Copy/Paste Option in Connected servers? Upload/Download files during session? Open multiple sessions? Edit Roles Functionality for Role Owners?
instructions: Accessing PAM and connecting to the hosts using browser >Prerequisites to Access PAM For Accessing PAM firstly, you need to request access to required target host from MyAccess portal. Follow below steps to request access from MyAccess Steps 1: Go to MyAccess portal https://myaccess.kone.com/identityiq/home.jsf Step 2: Navigate Manage Access>Manage User Access and select your identity A Step 3: Enter Role name based on application you want to access: To search for the required Role as per your application server requirement, you can search PAM: <Application name> - <Admin>/<Developer>/<Reader> - <Production>/<DEVQA> For Example : if your application name is IDM, you need to login as Developer and access Production environment. so you will search < PAM: IDM (MyAccess) - Developer - Production> NOTE- PLEASE DO NOT REQUEST FOR IDM APPLICATION IF YOU DON’T NEED TO ACCESS IDM APPLICATION SERVERS. BELOW IS JUST AN EXAMPLE REQUEST. This Application is just an example to explain how to request role to get access to your required application server. Please make sure you request for required access only i.e. if you need Developer access than you only request for developer role and not for Admin or Reader Roles. The Admin role gives unrestricted access to the system. With Developer role, user will be still part of server’s “Power Users” group and can still work on the system, folders and files. The permissions that are NOT in scope for developer role are: · Run/restart services · Reboot server · Change local permissions on the server · Change network settings · View/edit scheduled tasks · View connection details on IIS Manager With Reader role, user will have read-only access, example: user can view the logs but cannot execute any commands. If a user has Administrator role, then Developer or Reader role is not required for the user as Administrator has higher privilege than Developer and Reader. Step 4: Click on next and submit your request. Corresponding approvers will be notified via Email for approval and once approved, the corresponding servers can be accessed via PAM. NOTE – Once the requested roles are approved it will get reflected in PAM. If there is any issue in requesting Roles in MyAccess, Please contact AskIT/PAM Support Team. >Steps to login to PAM Step 1 : Check that you have MFA authenticator application installed in your mobile phone. It is needed during PrivX login. Suitable apps are, for example Microsoft Authenticator App (used in KONE) or Google authenticator. If the App is not installed in advance, the login gets complicated Step 2 : If you are outside KONE network then connect through VPN otherwise if are already inside KONE network than go to step 3. Step 3 : Open PrivX using any browser(i.e., Internet Explorer, Chrome, Firefox, Safari, Microsoft edge) and browse pam URL https://pam.konenet.com Below login page will appear, Enter your Kone AD username and password to login. Step 4: PrivX application’s own MFA page with QR code will appear when you login to PAM first time ever. At this point, you should have Authenticator app installed to your mobile phone. Start the application, Go to Add account>Work or school account>Scan the QR-code. You can also choose to manually enter the code to the application. This option is visible at the bottom of your mobile phone screen once you choose the scan option. Step 5: Enter the pin which you will receive on your phone after scanning the QR code. After scanning the QR code for first time login to PAM you will get one time pin code generated in your authenticator App every time you login to PAM Solution. Please contact AskIT/ PAM Support team if you have an issue with MFA. Step 6 : This will log you into PAM portal front page. Step 7 : Navigate to the Connections→Available Hosts page. The hosts you can connect to are listed under Available hosts . Step 8 : Click on the Required host you want to connect, Expand a connection entry to display it's available services Connection to Linux hosts is established using pamadministrator/pamdeveloper/pamreader accounts. Connection to Windows hosts is established using personal PAM admin account, which has a suffix “_t1”. Using T1 account, user can login both as administrator or developer but in case user has both administrator and developer roles, then using t1 account user will login on target host as administrator (due to higher privilege). You will be able to connect to the target host machine and can perform required task. >Copy/Paste Option: On a Windows or Linux host, you can select text and right-click to use the context menu. On windows we can use clipboard to copy/paste text on the host machine. When clipboard synchronization is enabled, you can directly copy-paste between your host and the target host. To check clipboard- synchronization support, and to toggle it on/off, see Settings . Clipboard synchronization is only available on Edge, Safari, and Chrome browsers. When using Chrome or Edge (chromium) to make SSH connections you can use ctrl-shift-c and ctrl-shift-v to copy and paste. On both cases, a permission from the user is required since it gives programmatic access to the clipboard. The browser will pop up a dialog to request access to the clipboard. If the user denies the access, then these features [ctrl-shift-c, ctrl-shift-v, RDP clipboard sync] are disabled. >Upload/Download files during session: Click on files to upload or download any file >Open multiple sessions: On the tab bar, click + to open additional terminals. >Edit Roles functionality for Role Owners: We now have "Edit roles" functionality available for role owners to add/modify/remove role owners. It is no longer needed to request "Approver group roles" in case a new approver should be added to a role. Step 1: Navigate to MyAccess homepage KONE MyAccess - Home and click on the menu option at the top left. Expand Manage Roles and click on Edit Roles. Step 2: Provide the role name or select the role name from the list and click on Edit . This will open the Edit Role form. Step 3: Scroll down to the section Approval Details Adding Roles and add the name of the approver that you wish to add. You can also remove an existing approver by clicking on ‘X’ against the name. Step 4: Once all changes are made, click Confirm to save the changes. If you do not wish to save the changes made, click Cancel . Important Note: Please do NOT modify PAM Role Display name or Role Description since all the PAM roles follow a strict naming convention, and the role names are updated accordingly by PAM Onboarding Team. Please contact PAM Support Team via AskIT ticket in case of any issues related to PAM. PAM Support Queue: KONE.PAM.SUPPORT

short_description: How to create Event with specific timezone
introduction: How to create Event with specific timezone?
instructions: Open the data sheet given by the user Convert the given file to .csv format and open .csv file Select the column containing Start Date/End Date excluding header (or select all values) Right Click --> Format Cells In Number section, select Category as Date, then select Custom In Type section, follow below steps: Type YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM:SS.000Z, click ' OK ' Save & Close the file Open this saved file again Ctrl+H In Find what, put '.000Z' In Replace with, put '.000GMT+05:30' Finally format looks like: "YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM:SS.000GMT+05:30" Here "GMT+05:30" directs to India, you can adjust this as per country specified in the ticket or file given Ex: -->Given by user: "10/1/2020 10:15:00 AM" -->After Formatting: "2020-10-01T10:15:00.000GMT+05:30"

short_description: AEM - Steps to recreate missing baselines manually
introduction: AEM - Steps to recreate missing baselines manually
instructions: During Delta migration, some of the baselines were missed in some documents. In that case, if the request is raised that the baseline is missing, then support team need to create the baseline manually with the following steps. 1 . Check the version history of the given DITAMAP and select the latest revision or the relevant one. AEM à search tab à Use Filter option à Enter the DITAMAP number à Select Preview Map option In Preview Map Window à Select Version History The DITAMAP with all the revisions will be displayed. From that note the relevant or the latest revision of the DITAMAP. 2 . Then create a new baseline with the naming format as provided ( eg. AM-51.12.003_Release_A ) and with latest DITAMAP revision or with the relevant revision. Map Console Window à Baselines tab à Create à Enter the Baseline name à Select DITAMAP revision à Click on Save. The new baseline will be generated. 3 . Now, generate the PDF output with the newly created baseline. Map Console Window à Output Presets à Click on edit to generate the output with the baseline Check Use Baseline box à Select the Baseline à Click on Done. Select the output format as PDF A4 à Click on Generate option to generate the output. A) If the pdf publishing is successful then go to step 4. The PDF generated will be visible under outputs tab. The PDF can be downloaded by clicking on PDF A4. B) In case if it fails, then need to debug based on the error log. 4. Download the same document from PDM with the help of document number and KTD issue. Compare both the PDF generated in AEM and the one which is downloaded from PDM. Check manually step by step that both the documents are similar or not. If it was similar, then OK else need to change the version of the topics similar to PDM.

short_description: How to load data when there are special characters used like in Turkish language
introduction: How to load data when there are special characters used like in Turkish language
instructions: Why data uploaded showed unwanted special characters? KONE AMS to ensure that they load any CSV data with UTF-8 encoding https://help.salesforce.com/articleView?id=000336266&type=1&mode=1. Considerations for Data Loader, special characters, file encoding, and import Knowledge Article Number 000336266 Description You may find that you're seeing mixed results when trying to import special characters such as á, ã, ä, ç, é, í, ó, ö, ô, ú via the Data Loader. The result in Salesforce displays unexpected tokens such as � instead of appropriate characters once the data load operation completes. Resolution This behavior is the result of a combination of your import file's encoding and the Data Loader settings you have selected and is not an unexpected issue with, or due to a changes in, the Data Loader application. When Configuring Data Loader you'll see the "Read all CSVs with UTF-8 encoding" setting. By checking this preference you're forcing the application to attempt to render any CSV you choose for a data operation as if it has been saved with UTF-8 encoding despite whether the file may or may not be. The combination of enabling this setting and using a file that is not encoded in UTF-8 can result in the unexpected characters. Although the Data Loader setting influences this behavior, the source of the issue is the way the file's encoding is set on saving the CSV file. When using Microsoft® Excel® to save a CSV file, its encoding is automatically set using machine-specific ANSI encoding and not UTF-8. It is not possible to explicitly configure or manually change this CSV file encoding behavior in Windows® or Excel®. To resolve the unexpected character import behavior you have two options: 1. Leave the "Read all CSVs with UTF-8 encoding" setting enabled If you commonly work with files containing special characters, this method is recommended to ensure all of the characters are captured properly. However, all CSV files used with the Data Loader need to be encoded using UTF-8. You can use a text editor program such as Notepad® to manually change the CSV's encoding to UTF-8 from ANSI so your import file is compatible with this Data Loader setting. To manually set file encoding follow the instructions outlined in the documentation Why isn’t Data Loader importing special characters? 2. Disable the "Read all CSVs with UTF-8 encoding" preference in the Data Loader This will allow the default ANSI character encoding set by Excel® to be passed without interference from the Data Loader. Why have I never had to change this Data Loader setting or manually set file encoding until now? It's likely that this is due to previously supplied files already being saved with UTF-8 encoding or they may not have contained special characters. Alternatively, the way Excel® is setting file encoding on saving CSVs may have changed on your local machine. Note : It is recommended that you continue to leave the "Write all CSVs with UTF-8 encoding" setting enabled to ensure that a CSV generated from an export via the Data Loader will be saved with UTF-8 encoding by default to allow support for special characters. If you would like to learn more about special character imports, please review Import or update Chinese, Korean, or Japanese characters .

short_description: Ask IT - Intune exclusions – guidelines
introduction: o Intune rollout: if issues arise with SOTI and Airwatch users (guidance for Ask IT) o Intune rollout scope at KONE o How do we exclude SOTI and AirWatch users from the scope?
instructions: o Intune rollout: if issues arise with SOTI and Airwatch users (guidance for Ask IT) o Intune rollout scope at KONE o How do we exclude SOTI and Airwatch users from the scope? 1. Intune rollout: if issues arise with SOTI and AirWatch users (guidance for Ask IT) Use case: SOTI or AirWatch user contacts Ask IT after the Secure mobile work (Intune rollout in 2020-2021) switch because not being able to access Office 365 applications on mobile device and not being able to enroll the device in Intune because already having the device managed (the device is asking to be enrolled in Intune before access to Office 365 mobile applications is granted). Note: SOTI and Airwatch users can enroll their non-SOTI and non-Airwatch devices in Intune and that’s even recommended but we need to exclude them from the scope, i.e. the policies are not applied on them as that would impact also their SOTI and Airwatch devices (policies impact on account level) Note: SOTI users start enrolling their devices in Intune in H1/2024 (SOTI and Intune can co-exist on devices) and once the enrollment process has been completed the security policy exclusions for SOTI users will be removed. This change is done as part of the Intune for field users change. Check if the user is using SOTI or AirWatch user by asking from the user SOTI user = user who has SOTI Mob iControl installed on at least one device (the user should be able to tell this) Airwatch user = user who has Airwatch Intelligent Hub installed on at least one device (the user should be able to tell this) A user without SOTI or Airwatch must not be added into the exclusion groups. They must enroll their device in Intune. Ticket will be assigned to KONE.ITO.MDM queue if user needs guidance 2. Check where the user is located. The action depends on that ENA =US, Canada -> Airwatch users Australia, New Zealand -> Australian and New Zealand SOTI users 3. Take the needed action per the group depending on the tool in use (SOTI or Airwatch) and the location Global SOTI users : Ask IT agent transfers the ticket to KONE.ITO.MDM Intune service support contacts sangilipandy.arumugam@kone.com to get the SOTI: Global Users MyAccess role assigned. The assignment will remove Intune related policies from the user latest on the following day Do not request the role by yourself and do not guide the user about the role assignment ENA Airwatch users ( Airwatch users in ENA) : Ask IT agent guides the user to request the Intune: Exclusion for ENA MyAccess role in MyAccess*. The role assignment requires manager approval. The Intune related policies are removed from the user latest on the following day after the approval Australian and New Zealand SOTI users (SOTI users in Australia and New Zealand) : Ask IT agent guides the user to request the Intune: Exclusion for KEA SOTI Users MyAccess role in MyAccess*. The role assignment requires an approval. The Intune related policies are removed from the user latest on the following day after the approval *This is how you request the role for someone in MyAccess 1. Go to https://myaccess.kone.com 2. Click “Manage User Access” 3. Search for and select the person’s name + click “Next” 4. Search for a key word, e.g. “Intune: Exclusion” + select the right role 5. Click “Next” and “Submit” Once the request is submitted and approved, it might take up to 24 hours until the person is removed from the Intune related security group. In MyAccess you can also check if someone already has a role for being excluded from Secure mobile work: Go to https://myaccess.kone.com Click “Manage User Access” Search for and select the person’s name + click “Next” Search for a key word, e.g. “Intune: Exclusion” + select the right role If the role is already assigned for the person, you get the below notification: Background information: 2. Intune rollout scope in 2020-2021 at KONE Secure mobile work project targets to improve cybersecurity at KONE by rolling out Intune mobile device management solution and security policies which state which devices can access and in which way KONE data and services. Target groups: · All KONE office people (internal and external) with KONE Office 365 E3 and E5 license Out of scope groups: · Office people located in GCN are excluded · Office people with another MDM (mobile device management) tool, Airwatch or SOTI, provided by KONE are excluded · External office people without kone.com account will not be impacted · Field operatives (Office 365 F1 license users) are excluded. They are using other MDM tools such as SOTI and Airwatch · Certain KONE roles are excluded as they are typical field roles or roles using SOTI or Airwatch. Excluded KONE roles: EXT-Installer, Installer, Tester, EXT-Maintenance Technician. 3. How do we exclude SOTI and Airwatch users from the scope? KONE office people with another MDM (mobile device management) tool, Airwatch or SOTI, provided by KONE are out scoped from the Intune rollout. One device can only be managed by one MDM tool Most of the SOTI and Airwatch users are not able to enroll their devices in Intune Some SOTI users have successfully enrolled their device in Intune. In these cases, no need to un-enroll If we miss to exclude SOTI or Airwatch users from the Intune rollout scope, they may not be able to access Office 365 mobile apps on their mobile device after the switch SOTI and Airwatch users can enroll their non-SOTI and non-Airwatch devices in Intune and that’s even recommended but we need to exclude them from the scope, i.e. the policies are not applied on them as that would impact also their SOTI and Airwatch devices (policies impact on account level) Note: SOTI users start enrolling their devices in Intune in H1/2024 (SOTI and Intune can co-exist on devices) and once the enrollment process has been completed the security policy exclusions for SOTI users will be removed SOTI and Airwatch users are excluded from Intune rollout scope with help of MyAccess roles / AD groups: ENA SOTI and AirWatch users / Intune: Exclusion for ENA My Access role : All SOTI and Airwatch users in ENA should have this role assigned to be excluded from the Intune related policies. Owner: David Near Approvers: Dwayne Davis, Jonathan Heiniger Australian and New Zealand SOTI users / Intune: Exclusion for KEA SOTI Users My Access role: Owner: Rae Raymond Approver: List of approvers (SOTI contacts)

short_description: 1PLAN - Unable to open 1Plan in Kone office network

short_description: How to Provide access to MAP 3 Azure Application
introduction: How to Provide access to MAP 3 Azure Application (Acceptance/Production) Overview This document outlines the process for providing Key User (Superuser) and Read and analyze access to the MAP (Maintenance and Acquisition Planning) application. The access provider must have the necessary permissions to modify user roles within the application. Prerequisites Before proceeding with granting key user access, ensure the following prerequisites are met: Admin Access: The access provider must have admin access to the MAP application. Access Request: Ensure the user has raised an access request via ServiceNow. If the user has not raised the request, provide them with the following link for guidance: ServiceNow Access Request . Instructions Step-by-Step Process for Providing Access Step 1: Receive the Access Request Service Request or ASKIT Incident ticket: Receive the access request through either a Service Request or an ASKIT Incident ticket. If received via an Incident ticket, cancel it and open a new Service Request ticket on behalf of the user, or share the link above so the user can create the request themselves. Step 2: Gather Required Details for Access- Collect the following information to proceed with access provisioning: Country Branch/Planner group Environment (QA/PROD) Role (Read, Analyze, or Key User) Step 3: Verify Missing Information If any user details are missing, request the information from the user before proceeding. Step 4: Obtain Approval from MAP Business Obtain formal approval for granting access from the MAP Business: Email Alessandro Mancuso (alessandro.mancuso@kone.com) and keep Pallavi Tiwari (pallavi.tiwari@kone.com) and the AMS team (raju.bitode@kone.com, rathin.roy@kone.com) in CC. Step 5: Grant Access Upon Approval Once approval is obtained, provide the necessary access to the user according to the requested role.

short_description: Ask IT - How to Provide access to Kone Field Forms Application
introduction: How to Provide access to Kone Field Forms Application(QA/Production)?
instructions: Providing Access to users in Kone Field Form(KFF) The Access provider should have the access to the KFF application with at least Supervisor admin privileges. Step 1: We should know the name of the user, userId of the user, role for which the user wants the access to and information about which country/company/Branch/Planner Group the user wants the access to. Step 2: Login to KFF Application through the following URL depending upon which environment we have to give the access: These URLs works through open internet also. (It does not works when connected to VPN, so connect through 10.41.167.16 jump server when connected to VPN) https://kff.kone.com (Production) https://qa-kff.kone.com/ (Acceptance) Use your Kone Credentials to Login into KFF. Step 3: Navigate through the menu and select the “User Administration” tab. *We can open Group Management from menu, to check privileges of each role. We can see all the groups on the top, which indicates the different roles in the application. User will ask for one of the following Roles: IT-Objects, Kone, Supervisor Admin, BackOffice, Supervisor, Global Admin, Service Manager, Supervisor Responsibility Usually user asks for Supervisor Access but if any other access is required, take the following approvals. IT-Objects - From the Configuration Owner of KFF : "Mastromatteo Cesare" Cesare.Mastromatteo@kone.com KONE - From Configuration Owner of KFF Global Admin - Configuration owner of KFF Supervisor Admin - Usually assigned to KFF Key user - Needs confirmation from previous KFF Key user OR Global Manager ("Seminckx Nicolas" Nicolas.Seminckx@kone.com .) BackOffice - Key user of that country Supervisors - Key user of that country, usually included in the ticket. Service Manager - Key user of that country (it can be that the Service manager/key user are the same person) Supervisor Responsibility - Key user of the country If there is any doubts in approvals, we can contact "Ayrookuzhy Joe Kuriakose" joekuriakose.ayrookuzhy@kone.com . Step 4: Select the tab (Role), to which we have to give the access to (in this case supervisor access example is taken). Step 5: Click on Add user Icon, at the top right corner to add new user. We will see a new “Edit User” box loaded on the screen Step 6: Search for the name of the user in the text box, it will search through the active directory of the Kone. Use the format of search as “Last name” SPACE ” First name”, as it will not search through incomplete names, userId or email Id as of now. Select the correct user from the searched list, to give the access. Step 7: Information about the user’s permissions will be loaded on the same dialog box. In the below screenshot we can see that, Sharma Mitanshu, has Access to County: Denmark. For a new User, the permissions will be blank, and nothing will be there. The 4 add buttons at the bottom, is used to add the access of Country/Company/Branch/Planner Group to that user respectively. It is a tree structure, so If we will give access to the Country , the user will automatically get access to all Company, Branches and Planner groups for that company. Similarly, if we give access to company , user will have access to all Branches and Planner groups for that company and so on. Planner group access is the narrowest access we can give to the user. Step 8: According to the user’s requirement, we can provide access to whole country, Company, Branch or Planner Group. Adding The country: Adding the Company: Adding the Branch: Adding the Planner Group: We can delete the access for the user’s particular Country/Company/Branch/Planner Group by clicking on the delete button. Sometimes, the user will want us to delete their previous access and add new access, then we can delete the old access and add the new one, depending on the requirement of the user. If the user has access from the application, but unable to see the data in the application, checking the data filters in the application can be suggested. Which can be navigated through Menu->Settings->DataFilter. If user uses Company codes to request the access refer to the table in the attached file. Reply to the user to test and confirm the access. Examples of tickets and reference ticket available in the SOP attached, not able to mention here because of words constaints.

For SAP BW and BH systems, requestor needs to obtain and provide mail approval from Kone BW Config Owner ( nishant.udgirkar@kone.com ) to SAP Security team, for processing this request. For non-Infosys personnel: For all SAP systems, requestor needs to obtain approval from harshawardhan.kulkarni@kone.com , Kone ABAP Config Owner – and provide the same to Infosys_SAP_Security_DL@kone.com , to request SSCR Developer key Creation “The SSCR (SAP Software Change Registration) is a procedure which registers all manual changes to SAP sources and SAP Dictionary objects. If an ABAP developer wants to change them in an SAP system, the system will prompt for two access keys: Developer key to register a particular user as a developer. This key must be entered only once. Object key to allow changes to SAP sources or SAP dictionary objects. An object must be registered when it is changed by a registered developer for the first time. If the object is changed at a later point in time, the key is no longer requested. If you modify several programs, individual object keys will be required for each of them. Both keys can be requested through the SAP Software Change Request (SSCR) Key application.” The application can be found in SAP Support Portal. https://me.sap.com/app/sscr

short_description: Error code for Email Delivery
introduction: How to identify if the email sent to the sever is successful or not? If not then what is the error and how to resolve the issue?
instructions: After every delivery stage, a three-digit response code is sent from the mail server. 1- The first number indicates whether the command was successful or unsuccessful. 2- The second digit indicates the type of error. 3- The third provides additional information to resolve the potential problem. These codes are found in Salesforce by running an Email Log File: In Classic: Setup > Logs > Email Log Files In LEX: Setup > Environments > Logs > Email Log File Each code is composed of three digits (X.X.X). The first digit gives the status of the email message: '2' means the email was successfully sent ; '4' means there was a temporary problem while sending the email (your email server may try to send it again or you must resend it, depending on your server settings). Such error messages are using codes like 4.X.X, where X.X are used in order to give more precise information about the error; '5' means there is a permanent/fatal error related to the email (the email address of the receiver does not exist, it doesn't accept emails from you, etc.). Such error messages are using codes like 5.X.X, where X.X are used in order to give more precise information about the error. To learn more about the frequently occurring errors in Salesforce, refer the below link. https://help.salesforce.com/articleView?id=000342855&language=en_US&mode=1&type=1 https://help.salesforce.com/articleView?id=000332655&type=1&mode=1

short_description: Lost mobile device PIN code – reset through Intune company portal
introduction: How to reset Pin through Intune Company Portal?
instructions: Users have lost / forgotten PIN code / passcode (the code used to unlock the device) for a mobile device. If the mobile device is enrolled in Intune, users may be able to reset the PIN code by themselves by logging into Intune Company Portal webpage , selecting the correct device and then selecting Reset Passcode . That will allow the users to the set a new PIN code in the mobile device. This feature is available for iOS devices. If users do not have another device to login to the Intune Company Portal webpage to reset the passcode, they should contact Ask IT which might help resetting the code. However, if the Reset Passcode option is not available in the Intune Company Portal webpage or if the device is rebooted, neither the support team can help with the passcode reset meaning that the device must be reset back to the factory settings. Note! The device must not be rebooted! Rebooting the device means the passcode cannot be reset in the Intune company portal or in the Intune admin side. In practice that means that the device must be reset to factory settings. For this reason, it is always important to keep backups up to date.

short_description: Ask IT - Mobile device details available in Intune admin portal
introduction: How to get the device details for an enrolled device from Intune?
instructions: If mobile device or BYOD computer technical details, such as serial number or operating system, are needed for trouble shooting, they can be got from the Intune admin portal for the devices that have been enrolled in Intune. In these cases, please do not ask for the details from the user. How to get the device details for an enrolled device: 1. Log into the Microsoft Endpoint Manager admin center: https://endpoint.microsoft.com/ 2. Select Devices from the left-hand side panel and the All devices from the middle panel. Then you can search for a specific device e.g. with the UPN of the user. If the enrollment is not complete and the device has not been connected to the user, you can search for the device e.g. with IMEI code or serial number. 3. Select the right device by clicking on the device row. The O verview gives the below details (picture). The Hardware tab gives more information on the device. The Discovered apps tab tells which work side applications are installed on the device. The Device compliance tab tells if the device is compliant or not and reveals which is the missing factor if the compliancy is not met.

short_description: Ask IT - Outlook pst file size increase
introduction: How to configure the size limit for both .pst and .ost files in Outlook
instructions: 1. Close Outlook application 2. Open regedit: to do so, click the Start button and then in the Start Menu, either in the Run box or in the Search box, type regedit and press Enter. 3. Navigate to HKEY_CURRENT_USER\Software\Microsoft\Office\16.0\Outlook\PST 4. Right click on screen and select new -> DWORD (32-bit) Value -> enter name as MaxLargeFileSize. Select decimal and enter value as 100000 5. Right click on screen and select new -> DWORD (32-bit) Value -> enter name as WarnLargeFileSize. Select decimal and enter value as 95000 6. Close registry editor and restart outlook

short_description: PAM - RDP and SSH connections with Native Clients
introduction: RDP Connections with Native Clients Connecting Interactively Connecting Directly SSH Connections with Native Clients Connecting Interactively Connecting Directly File Transfer using WinSCP Connecting Interactively Connecting Directly
instructions: This KBA describes how to establish RDP and SSH connections to application servers/ hosts using native clients without having to login to PAM web interface. Prerequisites · If you are outside KONE network, first connect through VPN using Pulse Secure before proceeding. Ignore this step if you are already inside KONE network. · For first time PAM use, web portal login is required for MFA setup (Please refer AskIT Knowledge Article# KBA5577 for PAM MFA setup instructions). After that, it is ok to use native client. · You can RDP/SSH only to hosts where you have been authorized to connect via MyAccess. Refer AskIT Knowledge Article# KBA5571 for requesting access from MyAccess and logging in to PAM. RDP Connections with Native Clients Users can connect to target hosts using the RDP clients installed on their workstations, without needing to use the PrivX/PAM GUI. All connections are authenticated against PAM and all the connection-specific events are internally logged within PAM. For example, if PAM allows you to access a target account with password authentication, your native clients will also connect using password authentication without prompting you for target-account credentials. Connections to hosts using native RDP clients are established through the PrivX RDP Bastion. The bastion is running at the following address: "pam-client.konenet.com" NOTE: · RDP Certificates based access is not yet supported. The following steps for RDP connection to hosts work only for applications that use stored password PrivX provides the following connection modes: Interactive: Access PrivX RDP Bastion to list and select possible targets Direct: Specify your connection target directly to the native client Connecting Interactively To connect to targets with native clients interactively: 1. Launch the Remote Desktop Connection application from your PC. 2. Provide the bastion address “pam-client.konenet.com” as the Computer name and click Connect. 3 . In the next screen, enter your PrivX username and password and click the arrow to proceed. Login: Your KONE email Password: Your KONE password 4. You are presented with an MFA Challenge. Enter the response code and click OK to be authenticated. 5. You will be presented with a list of targets that you are authorized to access. Select a target and click Connect to establish a connection. Connecting Directly To directly connect to a target that you know: 1. Launch the Remote Desktop Connection app from your PC. 2. Provide the bastion address “pam-client.konenet.com” as the Computer name and click Connect. 3. Provide the Login information in the following format and provide your password. Ensure to use fully qualified domain name of the target. Login: <target_username>%<target_hostname>%<privx_username> Example: s ys_ cadrobot%CZC8207ZCL.konenet.com%vamshika.kb@kone.com Password: Your KONE password 4. You are presented with an MFA Challenge. Enter the response code and click OK to authenticate and connect to the target. SSH Connections with Native Clients Users can connect to target hosts/accounts using the SSH clients installed on their workstations, without needing to use the PrivX/PAM GUI. All connections are authenticated against PAM and all the connection-specific events are internally logged within PAM. For example, if PAM allows you to access a target account with certificate authentication, your native clients will also connect using certificate authentication without prompting you for target-account credentials. Connections to hosts using native SSH clients are established through the PrivX SSH Bastion. The bastion is running at the following address: "pam-client.konenet.com" PrivX SSH Bastion provides the following connection modes: Interactive: Access PrivX Bastion to list and select possible targets Direct: Specify your connection target directly to the native client Connecting Interactively 1. Launch the SSH client from your PC. 2. Connect to the PrivX SSH Bastion by specifying the below parameters and click ‘Open’. Host Name (or IP address): your-kone-email@pam-client.konenet.com Example: vamshika.kb@kone.com@pam-client.konenet.com Port: 2222 3. Terminal will prompt for your password. Provide your password, hit enter. 4. Terminal will prompt for your MFA PIN code. Provide your MFA PIN code, hit enter. 5. You will be presented with a list of targets that you are authorized to access. Enter the number for the target that you want to connect to and hit enter and your connection will be established to the desired target. Example: Connection to second target server in the list is established by entering 2 Connecting Directly 1. Launch the SSH client from your PC. 2. Connect to the target host directly by specifying the below parameters and click ‘Open’. Host Name (or IP address): targetuser%targethostaddress%your-kone-email@pam-client.konenet.com Example: pamdeveloper%10.129.32.175%vamshika.kb@kone.com@pam-client.konenet.com Port: 2222 3. Terminal will prompt for your password. Provide your password, hit enter. 4. Terminal will prompt for your MFA PIN code. Provide your MFA PIN code, hit enter. 5. Your connection will be established to the desired target. File Transfer using WinSCP Users can carry out file transfer activities from/to target hosts using the WinSCP client installed on their workstations, without needing to use the PrivX/PAM GUI. All connections are authenticated against PAM and all the connection-specific events are internally logged within PAM. Connections to hosts using WinSCP client are established through the PrivX Bastion. The bastion is running at the following address: "pam-client.konenet.com" PrivX Bastion provides the following connection modes: Interactive: Access PrivX Bastion to list and select possible targets Direct: Specify your connection target directly to the WinSCP client Connecting Interactively 1. Launch the WinSCP client from your PC. 2. Connect to the PrivX Bastion by specifying the below parameters and click ‘Login. Host Name: pam-client.konenet.com Username: your-kone-email Port: 2222 Password: Your KONE password 3. Terminal will prompt for your MFA PIN code. Provide your MFA PIN code, click OK. 4. You will be presented with a list of targets that you are authorized to access. Enter the number for the target that you want to connect to and hit enter. Example: Connection to second target server in the list is established by entering 2 5. Click ‘OK’ to accept the message displayed and you will be connected to the home directory of the selected target. Connecting Directly 1. Launch the WinSCP client from your PC. 2. Connect to the target host directly by specifying the below parameters and click 'Login’. Host Name: pam-client.konenet.com Username: targetuser%targethostaddress%your-kone-email Example: pamdeveloper%10.129.32.175%vamshika.kb@kone.com Port: 2222 Password: Your KONE password 3. Terminal will prompt for your MFA PIN code. Provide your MFA PIN code, click OK. 4. Click ‘OK’ to accept the message displayed and you will be connected to the home directory of the specified target.

short_description: Process for monitoring the CP Escalation mailbox and Acknowledging the Escalation- For (Salesforce) AMSD Use
introduction: What to is the process to monitor the CPescalation mailbox and acknowledge the escalation ?
instructions: Check every 2 hours or early (before 9AM IST and after 7 PM IST on Weekdays, 24*7 during Public holidays and Weekends). Case 1 A. Identify if there is any client request for Major Incident initiation i. Look for words ‘MI’, ‘Major Incident’,’ 24’, ‘Critical’, ‘Emergency’, ‘affecting business’. ii. If such words exist refer the MI manager roaster and ping/call the available MI manager. iii. Also include the MIM DL (AMUMIM ) in the email thread and respond as below. “We acknowledge the call for Major Incident management for support. We have also contacted the MI manager and the available manager in the shift will initiate the MI bridge shortly. Please be available to join the bridge to further discuss on the business impact and Urgency and the way forward” a. And then follow the instruction from the MI Manager. b. If the MI manager is not available or does not respond contact the SM’s immediately. Case 1 ends Case 2 1. If such words do not exist, please read through an email and capture the following subjects: a. Ticket number (Incident/SR). b. Identify the reason for escalation- Business Impact, Urgency, Delay, re-prioritization. c. Impacted Application and functionality. d. If any of the above is not visible in the body of the email or in the ticket details in RF, please probe the above to the user requesting the escalation. e. Tick the “Escalation flag” field on the ticket in RF. f. Once all the above information is gathered. Please respond to the escalation email copying the Solution Lead and the Service Manager (KONE and Infosys) of the application as below: “We acknowledge the call for escalation on ticket number: “****”. We have updated the escalation request on the ticket. Either the Solution Lead of the application: “*****” or the respective application Service Manager will address the escalation soonest possible during the day or latest by the next business day” Case 2 end

short_description: Add known senders to the Safe Senders list to ensure that their email will not go to your Junk E-mail folder.
introduction: User says that his/her mails from known senders are going to Junk folder
instructions: Step-1: On the Ribbon , from the Home tab, in the Delete group, select Junk . Step-2: From the drop-down list, select Junk E-Mail Options. Step-3: Select the Safe Senders tab & Click Add. Step-4: In the Add address or domain dialog box, type an email address or domain & Click Ok. short_description: Questions for Initial Assessment & Priority for Common Platform Applications
introduction: 1. Which System is impacted ? and details of the system (Production or Non-Production) ? 2. Is this affecting Month End Closing (MEC) ? 3.1 If any workaround available ? 3.2 What is the business & Financial impact ? 3.3 Multiple users are impacted ? 4. Is this related to performance degradation issue ? 4.1 Is it on system level or Individual Level ? 4.2 If any workaround available ? 4.3 What is the business & Financial impact ? 4.4 Multiple users are impacted ? Overall, when you do warm transfer to CPSD, please provide below information. a. Which system is impacted ? b. What is the business impact ? c. What is the financial impact and how many users are affected ? d. Do we have any workaround available ? e. Is this related to Month End Closing (or) Performance ?
instructions: 1.) This answer will help for further assessment. Also please pass this to resolver group. 2.) If Yes. goto 2.1. If not, goto 3 2.1) Production, then goto 2.2 2.1) Non-Production, then goto 2.3 2.2) if more than 1, then P1. 2.3) Normal incident process 3.) If yes, goto 3.1 If not, goto 4. 3.1) If yes, then this comes under normal incident process. 3.1) If no workaround, create P2 and goto 3.2 3.2) Pass this details to CPSD / HCL MIM goto 3.3 3.3) Pass this details to CPSD. 4.) If yes, goto 4.1 4.1) If system level, create P2 and inform CPSD. 4.1) If Individual level then Goto 4.2 4.2) If yes, then Normal Incident Process. short_description: GSS pricing Kone parts pricing authorization
introduction: How to provide GSS pricing Kone parts pricing authorization
instructions: Symptom of the issue : User mentions in the incident that they are unable to see the prices in kone parts/Minerva. Suggested Fix: · If requesting user is from following specific countries, then get authorization from respective members for their respective countries as given below. Specific Countries : Bulgaria (306): Golosmanov Niko Niko.Golosmanov@KONE.com Bosnia & Croatia (334 & 335): Brlek Miro Miro.Brlek@KONE.com France (SFA): Franquin Pierre Pierre.Franquin@KONE.com Italy (KIT, C128): Ghezzi Alberto Giovanni Alberto.Ghezzi@kone.com Montenegro & Serbia (308 & 307): Pavlovic-Stankovic Snezana Snezana.Pavlovic-Stankovic@KONE.com Saudi-Arabia: Kamran Muhib kamran.muhib@kone.com · If requesting user is from following other countries then no need for any authorization. Other Countries: Andorra (263), Belgium (BEL), Egypt (325), Finland, Israel (312), Middle East (279, 272, 274, 275), Luxembourg (KDL), Netherlands (237), Portugal (261), Romania (276), Spain (262), UK (KOU), USA (MKO). 1. if some of the requestor’s colleagues already have the same access, check which level of access requestor’s colleagues have, to do that find the user id from outlook and check their access in Admin page.(for the above other countries) 2. If no one has access from above other countries , then check one-above manager for approval before granting the access. · If requesting user is seeking access from other general countries then we need to follow the process mentioned below Other General Countries: For the other countries access can be given automatically by informing requestor’s one-above manager when sending the closing mail, if manager sends feedback then modify access according to manager’s instruction. Steps to follow: After getting authorization from above mentioned steps whichever is applicable to the case then proceed forward with following steps: 1. Go to User maintenance page ( http://euaz1mskpbatch4.konenet.com:4100/KS/Cat_UserAdmin.asp# ) 2. Search for the user using name or alias (preferred) 3. Then click to view details from the interface. Then following window will prompt . (Required info in Red) Customer code for GSS (SAP) : this determines which TP list is visible to the user. This field needs to be filled in if TP visibility is needed. This field should fill in automatically according to user’s settings. There are three exceptions: Users from KONE industrial (company code 216): need to check the correct department from Outlook and choose the customer code based on that . Users from KONE Finland (company code 217): need to check the correct department from Outlook and choose the customer code based on that . Users from KONE Corporation (company code KNE): need to check the correct department from Outlook and choose the customer code based on that. If user appears with customer code AAAA, it means that the automatic mapping has not been able to find the correct customer code for the user. Then field needs to be filled in manually. Pricing Sales organization (SAP): this determines which KRRP list is visible to the user. Use the same organization as the person has in Outlook. This field needs to be filled in if KGRP / KRRP / LLP visibility is required. If user needs only TP, this can be left blank. (in green) Level of access : this determines which prices are visible for the user. Thick boxes for the correct prices. If user has not specified which prices are needed, grant TP & KRRP (note exceptions mentioned below) Exceptions: a. People from Sourcing functions (all units): default is only TP visibility, even if anything else is requested b. Finland: for users from company codes 216, 217 and KNE; contact GSS pricing to define the correct settings (mapping will be provided during Jan 2020) c. France (SFA): full price access means KRRP, LLP & TP, this is granted for all users after confirmation from the authorizing person (unless he specifies otherwise). At the end press Save on the bottom right corner of the page which will reflect the rights to be given to the requesting user. If anything, unclear check with L2 team.

short_description: Ask IT -Microsoft Outlook Crash or How to Clear the Outlook Cache from the system
introduction: Microsoft Outlook not responding.. Unable to access Microsoft outlook Can not start Microsoft outlook Can not open the Outlook Window Delete the Outlook Cache
instructions: Clearing the Outlook cache from the your system Save all mails and close Outlook. Press Windows key+R. In the Run dialog box, enter %localappdata%\Microsoft\Outlook and press Enter. Double-click the RoamCache folder to view the cache files. Back up the cache files so that you can restore the files if something goes wrong. Delete the cache files Outlook automatically makes new cache files when you open Outlook. Quit the application before shutting down the computer.

short_description: AskIT : Procedure to reset password and extend KONE SAP user account validity
introduction: 1) How to reset password/ unlock user ID in SAP systems 2) How to extend SAP account validity date
instructions: NOTE: Assignment Group: KONE.AskIT.INDIA Systems in Scope: a. SAP ECC systems i.e Z01 (ECC Development), Z02 (ECC Acceptance), Z03 (ECC Pre-Production) and Z04 (ECC Production) b. SAP BW systems i.e BW1 (BI HANA Development), BW3 (BI HANA Pre-Production) and BW4 (BI HANA Production) c. SAP BH systems i.e BH1 (BPC HANA Development), BH3 (BPC HANA Pre-Production) and BH4 (BPC HANA Production) d. SAP Solman systems i.e. SA1 (Solman Development), SA4 (Solman Production) Pa ssword Reset Process Kindly note that SAP system(s) are enabled with Single Sign On (SSO). Hence, you do not require User ID and Password to access the SSO enabled SAP system(s) through SAP GUI. Just double click on SAP SSO enabled system to which you required to login in the SAP Logon pad, and you’ll redirected to system directly without User ID and Password. To access SAP using SSO, User should have SAP SECURE CLIENT installed and connected in the system. For installation and connection, user need to contact ASKIT( AskIT@kone.com ). If user is connected to SAP SECURE CLIENT and still unable to login using SSO, then ticket should be forwarded to SAP SECURITY team. If user still want to reset the SAP password, then check if a) password status is initial or expired. If user’s password is initial or expired and account is in Valid status, proceed to reset password and communicate to user directly. b) account has been locked due to multiple incorrect attempts If account got locked due to multiple logon attempts and is in Valid status, unlock account and proceed to reset password and communicate to user directly. Steps to follow: 1) Login to respective SAP system using the SAP Logon Pad with SSO or with password . 2) To login with password ,provide Client, KONE ID and password of the respective system to login. 3) Open transaction code SU01. 4) A new window will open where you can search for the User by entering his/her KONE user ID. Under “User Maintenance”, you have 3 options to click: - “Display” gives you information about user - “Lock/Unlock” gives you the option to lock or unlock a user account - “Change Password” gives you the option to reset a user’s SAP password Please enter the affected KONE user name, open in display mode. Check for Password status. In Logon Data tab, if password status is initial or production, click on "Change Password". Enter the new password and click Enter. In Logon Data tab, if its locked due to multiple incorrect logon attempts, unlock the ID first and then proceed to “Change Password”. Enter the new password and click Enter. You will be able to reset password here for the system you have logged on to. Also, AskIT can inform to the user that Password Self-Service is now available via KONE Way portal and send below details to the user via mail : ************************************************************************************** Did you know that by using the new Password self-service portal you can speed up your SAP password reset requests. Please use the link http://kwportal.konenet.com/irj/portal/ The instructions for using the tool are available in below Knowledge Base Article – SAP password reset instructions.docx KB0000A520 - https://koneprod.service-now.com/now/nav/ui/classic/params/target/kb_view.do%3Fsys_kb_id%3Dde1ba5e0c30d5ed4c9c1fd4ce0013141 **************************************************************************************************************** 2. Validity Extension Process If user requests for validity extension or if user is facing an error “User Account not in validity date”, f irstly, it needs to be checked if User ID is within validity - this can be checked in extensionAttribute2 in Active Directory. ( Please follow KBA # KB00030223 on How to download AD explorer and check user attributes. https://koneprod.service-now.com/now/nav/ui/classic/params/target/kb_view.do%3Fsysparm_article%3DKB00030223 ) - If the validity date of user ( extentionattribute2 in AD) is in past date – we should not extend validity. - If the validity date of user (extentionattribute2 in AD) is a future date – but even if it is less than 31.12.9999, it is not an issue, you can extend validity to 31.12.9999. If User ID is expired, AskIT will ask end user to contact his manager for user's details updation in Workday. If KONE User ID is still within validity period, then SAP User ID can also be extended. Steps to follow: 1) Enter User ID and click edit button 2) Open Logon Data tab, Change Validity period : Valid to 31.12.9999 Account no: ACTIVE User Group: Remove DORMANT , if this is set to “DORMANT” user group and set the value to previous value before it was DORMANT or as requested by user explicitly. To check the previous value of user group for user, follow the below steps: Go to tcode suim -> change document -> user -> For users Enter the user id and remove from date and time: In selection criteria select below two option: Execute Check the old value column to get the value maintained before “DORMANT” user group and maintain the same , in above example, its “IT_ASKIT_GLB” If value maintained previously is “Terminated” , Keep new value as blank. If your getting error while maintaining the previous value, maintain new value as blank. This could happen if the user group maintained previously is removed from SAP. Example: As user group GSS doesn’t exist it in system , it gave error , so maintain new value as Blank. 3) Click on Save. Reset Password if required as mentioned in “Password Reset Process”.

short_description: Ask IT - Set up Multi Factor Authentication (MFA)
introduction: How to set up MFA on your KONE account ? How to set up MFA How to Set up MFA on Mobile How to Set up authentication application Microsoft authenticator app
instructions: MFA Activation instructions This security feature is automatically enabled for all users, but you can manually activate it: Go to https://aka.ms/setupmfa Select an authentication method from Option 1: Mobile App (RECOMMENDED!) - Notification or code - Quickest - Works offline when using codes Option 2: Mobile phone - SMS text - Does not work if you have no connection You will receive a message telling you that more information is required to keep your account secured. Click Next Decide if you want to use: The Authenticator mobile app (recommended) Mobile phone (text, not recommended) Option A Set up a Mobile app for two-step verification First, install the Microsoft Authenticator app for Android or iOS. from the Company Portal. Follow the instructions from the setup page. Click next on the screen on your PC, and a QR code will appear On your phone or tablet, open the Authenticator app and select + to add an account. (On Android devices, select the three dots, then Add account.) Select Work or school account. A QR code scanner will open on your phone. Scan the QR code picture that appeared on your PC screen for configuring the mobile app. Select Done. Approve the notification that should appear in the Authenticator app. When you approve the notification, the setup page on your PC should recognize this and show “Notification approved”. Click Next. You are now ready to use MFA verification with the Authenticator app. Option B Set up mobile phone text for MFA verification Select Authentication Phone from the list. Choose your country code from the drop-down list, enter your mobile phone number and select “Text me a code” If you selected “Text me a code”, You will be sent a code by text message. Enter the code and press next You are now ready to use MFA with text (SMS). Note: If https://aka.ms/setupmfa doesn’t work Sign in to https://myapps.microsoft.com Select your account name in the top right, then select the profile. Select Edit security info. Microsoft instructions: https://support.microsoft.com/en-us/account-billing/add-your-work-or-school-account-to-the-microsoft-authenticator-app-43a73ab5-b4e8-446d-9e54-2a4cb8e4e93c The above link should automatically resolve to your browser language preference, but at the bottom left there's an option to change language, as well. Also, you can check our local intranet page for other languages, link below https://kone.sharepoint.com/sites/ITCommunications2/Shared%20Documents/Forms/AllItems.aspx?id=%2Fsites%2FITCommunications2%2FShared%20Documents%2FMFA%20and%20password%20reset%20instructions%20%2D%20local%20language%20versions&p=true&ga=1 Questions and Answers What is Multi-Factor Authentication (MFA)? Multi-Factor Authentication (MFA) is an authentication method that adds an extra layer of security to your account. In addition to your username and password, MFA requires a second form of verification to confirm your identity. At KONE, MFA is a standard Microsoft security capability used across Microsoft Entra ID (formerly Azure Active Directory) and Microsoft 365 services, and it is required for almost all applications. What is authentication? Authentication is the process of verifying your identity when you sign in to your PC, applications, or online services. This typically involves providing your credentials (username and password). With MFA enabled, an additional verification step is required. Why is MFA implemented? MFA is a critical security control that helps protect KONE accounts from cyberattacks such as phishing and credential theft. As a company, we are responsible for safeguarding KONE and our customers’ data. MFA significantly reduces the risk of unauthorized access to email, applications, files, and other sensitive information. You will also be alerted if someone attempts to access your account using your credentials. Why should I use MFA? Every KONE employee is responsible for protecting KONE and customer-related data. MFA enables secure access to applications and services from anywhere, including remote work and non-KONE devices, while significantly improving overall security. What does this mean in practice? When MFA is enabled, you will be asked to verify your identity with an additional authentication method when accessing KONE applications and services. MFA is required when accessing services from non-trusted devices such as home PCs, Macs, tablets, or mobile phones. A KONE-managed PC is typically treated as a trusted device, and additional verification may not be required depending on access conditions and security policies. Supported verification methods include (but not limited): Microsoft Authenticator app (push notification or verification code) Text message (SMS) Who does MFA apply to? MFA is mandatory for all KONE users. In practice, MFA is required for almost all KONE applications, not only Microsoft 365. Where can I find information about MFA activation? MFA has been activated for all mandatory roles. Users are encouraged to set up MFA proactively to avoid disruption. If MFA is enforced and not yet configured: You will be prompted to set up MFA immediately. Access to applications will be blocked until MFA setup and verification are completed. Can I access the KONE intranet without MFA? No. The KONE intranet is part of the Microsoft 365 environment and requires MFA when accessed from non-trusted devices. From a KONE-managed PC, your username and password are typically sufficient, as the device is considered trusted. Are there any tools or platforms I can access without MFA? Some tools or platforms outside the Microsoft 365 environment may not require MFA. However, MFA is increasingly enforced across KONE systems, especially for applications handling sensitive data. Additional applications may require MFA in the future. Can I access data on my PC’s local hard drive without MFA? Yes. MFA applies to application and service access. Local access to files stored on your PC’s hard drive does not require MFA. Will using MFA generate extra costs on my mobile phone? The Microsoft Authenticator app does not generate additional costs and can also be used without an internet connection when using verification codes. If you use SMS while roaming abroad, your mobile operator may apply additional charges. How will I know when MFA is activated for my account? You will receive an email notification informing you that MFA is available or required for your account. If MFA is not set up during the self-activation period, enforcement will occur automatically. You will be notified in advance. Why should I set up MFA myself? Setting up MFA proactively allows you to choose a convenient time and avoid interruptions to your access when MFA enforcement is applied automatically. How long does MFA setup take? MFA setup typically takes only a few minutes, regardless of the verification method you choose. When can I set up MFA? You can set up MFA as soon as you receive the activation or notification email, or at any time by visiting: https://aka.ms/setupmfa How will I know MFA setup is complete? After setup, you will be prompted for MFA during sign-in within a short period. Successful completion of the additional verification confirms that MFA is active. What is the recommended verification method? The recommended method is the Microsoft Authenticator mobile app, available from your device’s app store or Company Portal. I already have the Microsoft Authenticator app If the Microsoft Authenticator app is already installed, you do not need to reinstall it. Simply register it as your MFA method during setup. I don’t have a mobile phone Select the Authentication phone option and provide a phone number where you can be reached. Verification will be performed via an automated SMS. I can’t install apps on my mobile phone Select the Authentication phone option and choose text message (SMS) as your verification method. I don’t have a company phone. Do I have to use my personal phone? Yes. Users must register at least one contact method for identity verification. If you use a personal mobile phone, installing the Microsoft Authenticator app is recommended. It is lightweight and does not generate additional costs. Alternatively, SMS verification can be used. Does the Microsoft Authenticator app require an internet connection? Push notifications require an internet connection. Verification codes generated by the app do not require an internet connection. If no internet connection is available, you can choose an alternative method such as phone call or SMS. How do the verification methods work? Mobile app – notification: Approve the sign-in request on your phone. Mobile app – verification code: Enter the code shown in the app during sign-in. Authentication phone – text message: Enter the code received via SMS. Process for users blocked due to MFA not being set up If a user is blocked because MFA was not configured within the required timeframe: Request an MFA reset via the Ask IT portal. Go to https://aka.ms/setupmfa and complete MFA setup. After an MFA reset, the user has a limited time window (up to 24 hours) to complete setup before enforcement is applied again.

short_description: RPA User access to SAP systems
introduction: How to request new / additional access for RPA user in SAP system
instructions: This KBA outlines the process and guidelines for requesting new or additional access for RPA user in SAP systems. This KBA applies to the Kone SAP Security team and may also be used by the RPA or automation team to understand the correct process for requesting new or additional SAP access for an RPA user. Note: Requests for creating a new RPA user ID in an SAP system must follow the process detailed later in this KBA (refer Case 1 section). New RPA user requests must be initiated through an EFS Jira Story by contacting the GRC Product Owner, rather than by submitting a Service Request. For existing RPA IDs, you may continue to use the service catalog to raise a Service Request to the SAP Security team for additional access using the below process. Process for RPA (Automation/Bot) ID Access Requests The SAP Security team typically receives two categories of access requests related to RPA, automation, or bot user IDs: Creation of a new RPA ID in the SAP landscape Additional access requirements for an existing RPA ID Case 1: Creation of a New RPA ID in the SAP Landscape (EFS Activity) The creation of a new RPA ID in SAP ECC systems must be managed through the EFS track where Product Owner, SAP GRC will validate the requirement and will create JIRA story for SAP Security team to work on the requirement. New Role will be created which will be specific to this BOT ID right from Dev system to QA & once SAP Security team receives testing confirmation from the requester, then only it will be transported to Production system by following the standard change management procedure. RPA team is supposed to provide Assignment Approver and content approver for the newly created Role once it moves to Production system. This is because the process requires detailed requirement gathering to determine the specific transactions and authorizations the bot will need. Note: Ensure that the new RPA ID is already available in Active Directory (AD), else the RPA account will be deleted by the job running in SAP. If the RPA team provides a reference or mirror ID of another RPA account and requests that the new RPA ID be assigned the same access as the clone ID, the action usage for the past 13 months must be reviewed for the mirror ID. This analysis helps identify the relevant transactions that can be assigned to the new RPA ID. Action usage data can be extracted from the GRACACTUSAGE table in the GRP backend system. Since this table logs each execution timestamp for a transaction, the ‘Action’ column will contain duplicate entries. These duplicates must be removed before proceeding with the analysis. Alternatively, action usage can be generated from the GRC NWBC portal by navigating to: Reports and Analytics → Action Usage by User, Role, and Profile (Ensure that ‘All’ is selected in the Report Type field). Once the action usage data is available for the clone RPA ID (or, if clone ID is unavailable, the RPA team or business provides the list of required transactions for the bot), the identified transaction codes must be reviewed to ensure they do not introduce any conflicts based on the risk library. This assessment can be performed using the Role-Level Online Risk Analysis in GRC NWBC or the Risk Terminator tool in the backend Z01 system. A confirmation must then be obtained from Shikha Koria, Product Owner, GRC, regarding the new bot-specific role to be created, including the list of transaction codes to be assigned. Organizational-level restrictions for the new bot role should either be derived from the clone RPA ID or confirmed by the business, specifying the regions for which the bot requires access. Each new role created for an RPA ID must be unique and maintain a one-to-one relationship with that specific bot ID. These roles must not be assigned to any other RPA ID or to end users. The naming convention for RPA-specific roles is as follows: ZECCS_XX_XX_ZZZZZZZZZZZ Where: ZECCS – Fixed prefix for single roles in the ECC system XX_XX – Placeholder for business process and sub-process (use as-is when undefined) ZZZZZZZZ – Free-text field to be replaced with the KONE ID of the RPA account Example: ZECCS_XX_XX_PA_P0086B02 — This role is created specifically for bot PA_P0086B02, whose ID appears in the role name to replace the free-text ZZZZZZZZZZZ. After the new role is transported to the Z04 system, the GRC team must be contacted to upload the role for provisioning. The role owner and content approver for this bot-specific role must be agreed upon with the RPA Team Lead (Gulamuddin Ansari) and the relevant business or process owners who requested the automation. This ensures accountabilities for all actions performed using the bot ID. Once a GRC request is submitted for assigning this new role to the RPA ID, the person who created the role must monitor the request. After all approval stages are completed, it is their responsibility to update the role status to DEV in GRC, ensuring that the role cannot be requested for any other user. (The processor of JIRA story must keep it open until the GRC Request is approved so that this step does not go unnoticed, otherwise get a Jira story created for the GRC team to update the role status to DEV.) If the RPA ID requires additional transactions or changes to organizational-level access in the future, these must first be discussed with the Role Content Approver. Any required authorizations must then be incorporated into the same bot-specific role originally created for that RPA ID. Note: No Additional Role (Business specific or IT Role) to be assigned to RPA ID once created in Z04 system and all new requirements must be served through EFS track where Product Owner, SAP GRC will raise a requirement for SAP Security team. In future, it is proposed to create one GRC PC Rule which will identify and generate alert for SAP GRC team if any additional role is assigned to the new created BOT ID. It is in discussion and will be done on request from Product owner, SAP GRC. Please note that all RPA IDs should be assigned with TUG_RPA user group in user master record. Case 2: Additional Access Requirements for an Existing RPA ID (AMS Activity) For requesting additional access for existing RPA IDs in SAP, please submit the request using the following service catalog link. RFC, RPA or System User in SAP or Raise request in Employee Center: Open the below link. https://koneprod.service-now.com/esc Search with KEY word (RFC, RPA). Select RFC, RPA or System User in SAP Form. Fill in the required details below and click on Order Now button to submit the request. When additional access is requested for an existing RPA ID that is not assigned a bot‑specific role and is instead using business roles, the current process is to identify the appropriate region‑specific role variant based on the required transaction and the work region specified by the business or RPA team. A GRC access request must then be raised for the RPA account, requesting assignment of the identified business role. Note: While raising GRCs for Bot ID, ensure to manually update the Manager field in Access Request form Gulamuddin Ansari’s user ID. If the RPA ID is already assigned a bot‑specific single role and requires additional access, approval must first be obtained from the Role Content Approver. It must be ensured that the inclusion of new authorizations does not introduce any Segregation of Duties (SoD) risks. Once confirmed, the necessary updates can be made to the existing bot‑specific role. If there are any SODs coming due to additional requested access then it must be communicated to Internal controls team + Product Owner, SAP GRC.

short_description: How to define a backup for a technician / fitter in KONECT?
introduction: How do I define a backup for a technician / fitter in KONECT?
instructions: Call Center Management Backups Fitter To cope with the fact that an employee that is allocated to a profile is unavailable for some reason (or no employee is defined for the active profile), there are a number of ways to define backup employees. Normal Backup schema (for profile “0” Planned Absence) If the default employee for the work center of the equipment is not available when Profile “0” is active because he is holiday or training (planned absence) he can agree with another employee that will act as his backup. This new employee is called the ‘Absence Backup employee’. It is defined in KM16-Employee. In our example , we could have the following backups defined: Scenario A Masterdata => Employee search for the fitter insert the fitter and save in the Absense Backup (profile 0 field) It’s possible also insert the fitter and save in the Absense Backup (outside profile 0 field) Scenario B: in case the backup is occasionally not “Absence Backup employee” set in the km16 schema, there is the possibility during the absence process to insert a different fitter in During profile 0 and Outsite profile 0. this setting temporarily replaces the setting of the km16 scheme Normal Backup schema (for profile “0” Unplanned Absence) The employee is not available (when Profile “0” is active) because he his busy or he has been injured during his job ( Unplanned absence ), the system can use the backup-work centers that are defined within the “KM34” schema. For each work center, you can define up to 3 work centers that act as backups for the defined work center. Of course, it is the aim to have work centers that are close to the original one as backup. In our example , we could have the following backups defined: Sheduling =>Employee Scheduling Choose your plant section or Planner Group => Select profile 0 tab and managing This process is very important for our kc3 to be able to quickly manage our customers' requests

short_description: How to run Risk Analysis at User level and Role level
introduction: How to run Risk Analysis at User level and Role level
instructions: 1. How to run User Level Risk Analysis? q Login SAP GRC Production (GRP) system and access transaction code NWBC. q To access User Level risk analysis tab, Navigate to Access Management à Access Risk Analysis à User Level q Once “User Level” is selected, it takes you to the next screen Note: Alternatively, we can run the risk analysis report in Background mode as well if we wish to choose large number of users as input; thus, saving system resource Format can be selected from the list from below q Summary q Detail q Management Summary q Executive Summary q Action Level − Perform SoD analysis at action (transaction) level. q Permission Level − Perform SoD analysis at action (transaction)and permission (authorization object) levels. q Critical Actions − Analyze the users who have access to one of the critical functions. q Critical Permissions − This can be used to analyze users having access to one critical function. q Critical Roles/Profiles − This can be used to analyze the users who has access to critical roles or profiles . q User Level Risk Analysis- Summary q User Level Risk Analysis- Detail q The Risk Analysis results can be exported/downloaded to a local file as below. q In case background mode is scheduled for risk analysis, the risk analysis results can be downloaded using transaction : GRAC_EXPORT_RA q For downloading the results, go to transaction: GRAC_EXPORT_RA User Level Risk Analysis by providing User Group: q We can run risk analysis for complete set of users falling under the same user group. q User group is maintained in transaction SU01 in the backend system 2. How to run Role Level Risk Analysis? q Role Risk Analysis- Summary q Role Risk Analysis- Detail 3. How to run User Level / Role Level Risk Simulation? q Risk from Simulation means the simulation results will display the results of risks or violations which will be obtained from the COMBINATION of user's existing assignment and the new assignments q Simulation Criteria can be -Action -Role -Profiles Steps for User Level Risk Simulation: In the first step, we need to provide the user and system details In step 2 provide the Actions for which we want to check the Risk After providing the details click on Run In Foreground option
