short_description: Ask IT - How to handle Monitoring tickets from TSystems
introduction: How to handle the monitoring related tickets that will appear in Remedyforce? Monitoring alerts related to P1-P4 incidents
instructions: P1/P2 Incident triggered by TSY monitoring: T-Systems side: 1. Monitoring incident ticket is automatically created in SM9 from a monitoring alert (if the threshold is reached) – it is assigned to TSY Technical Group. 2. In case TSY needs to contact KONE - TSY OPM involves TSY LIM (TSY internal assessment call). 3. TSY LIMs shall send the email to HCL MIM < KONE_MIM-SI@kone.com > and <ITOSI@kone@com> with the following details: · SM9 ticket number · server ID · the technical explanation what action TSY requests to be perfromed by KONE (e.g. hardware check LDR, disk cleanup etc.) 4. TSY LIMs shall call Ask IT - inform them about SM9 ticket number and Priority (P1 or P2): · Ask IT India - 0008000402312 or Toll free numbers +1 3124799442 ; +44 2037886264 Ask IT / KONE side: 5. Ticket shall be transferred from SM9 to RF by Ask IT (SM9 team) during the office hours (08:00 am – 17:00 pm CET) or SIAM team outside of the office hours. 6. Ask IT handles the incident ticket on RF side – tickets come from SM9 to RF totally blank (P4 by default). 7. Ask IT triggers HCL MIMs 8. Ask IT and HCL MIMs shall initiate investigation on KONE side 9. HCL MIMs shall open the bridge 10. After ticket is checked on KONE side Ask IT / (SM9 team) shall send the ticket back from RF to SM9 with the update from investigation. 11. Incident ticket is transferred in SM9 to TSY technical team – Ask IT (SM9 team) during the office hours (08:00 am – 17:00 pm CET) or SIAM team outside of the office hours. T-Systems side: 12. TSY technical team shall resolve the incident ticket. 13. Resolve status from SM9 is sent to RF and Monitoring console via interface. =================================================================================== P3/P4 Incident triggered by TSY monitoring: T-Systems side: 1. Monitoring incident ticket is automatically created in SM9 from a monitoring alert (if the threshold is reached) – it is assigned to TSY Technical Group. 2. In case TSY needs to contact KONE - the incident tickets is assigned to DS.ISD.INT.KONE.GSD in SM9 Ask IT / KONE side: 3. Ticket shall be transferred from SM9 to RF by Ask IT (SM9 team) during the office hours (08:00 am – 17:00 pm CET) or SIAM team outside of the office hours. 4. Ask IT handles the incident ticket on RF side 5. All Application CI's are stored on this link: https://konewayflow.lightning.force.com/lightning/r/Report/00O6900000BuVjdEAF/view?queryScope=userFolders 6. Ask IT shall initiate investigation on KONE side 7. After ticket is checked on KONE side Ask IT shall send the ticket back from RF to SM9 with the update from investigation. 8. Incident ticket is transferred in SM9 to TSY technical team – Ask IT (SM9 team) during the office hours (08:00 am – 17:00 pm CET). T-Systems side: 9. TSY technical team shall resolve the incident ticket. 10. Resolve status from SM9 is sent to RF and Monitoring console via interface. Assignment Queue: CUST.KONE.INT.SM4C

short_description: Ask IT - Adding Rules to Shared mailbox
introduction: How to Add Rules to Shared mailbox?
instructions: Assignment Group: CUST.KONE.INT.SD If you receive request like adding rules to shared mailbox then you need to follow below prerequisites. When shared mailbox owner has raised a request – · Move ahead and make required changes as per this KBA When others have raised the request – · Check the owner of the shared mailbox and route ticket to askIT to get approval from respected owner · If there is no owner of the shared mailbox then get approval from one of the shared mailbox members who has full access to it, or else can get approval from user’s line manager to proceed further. Process [A]: Before adding rule, you must check below steps in order to Add account into Outlook client Application 1. In Outlook go to File Tab 2. Go to Account Settings 3. Check if the shared mailbox has already added in Email list then follow Process [B] only if not then continue with this process. Click on the Email tab select New 4. In the email address field, enter the email address for the shared account and click on connect button (note: In the Password field, enter the password for your email account (your full mailbox) since the shared mailbox doesn’t have a password) 5. Click on use a different account and click on Ok button 6. Enter user’s email address and its password here, again use an account that is a full mailbox which connect to the shared mailbox. 7. Click on OK to finish this process. You may need to restart Outlook for the changes to take effect. Process [B]: Adding rules for shared mailbox using O365 Outlook client Application Open Outlook Application and Go to File 2. Click on Manage Rules and Alerts . 3. On the Email Rules tab, select the shared mailbox from the drop-down menu labeled: Apply changes to this folder. 4. Click on New Rule and proceed to create your rule. Method – 2: Adding Rules to shared mailbox Over Web Application ( recommended to use:- Chrome or MS Edge browser as per Kone security policy ) 1. Login your personal outlook email account OWA ( https://outlook.office365.com/mail ) 2. Go to Account manager icon and click on Open another mailbox and type shared mailbox address into Open another mailbox window 3. After clicking on Open button Shared mailbox will be opened into New Tab. Click on Settings icon and then click on View all outlook settings 4. Go to Mail >> Rules >> Add new rule and start adding rules as per requirement

short_description: How to raise Blackline Tool access request in MyAccess, Blackline support ticket
instructions: Who can request? Users who have access to KONE Network can request accesses for themselves https://myaccess.kone.com KONE Employees can request accesses for any user Externals (Genpact Employees) can request access for them selves If Access request is failing please make a AskIT ticket to MyAccess (IDM) team If user is not visible in MyAccess manager needs to contact AskHR Pre-requisites: New user is visible in MyAccess Login credentials are same as KONE AD account. How to start MyAccess request Login to MyAccess URL : https://myaccess.kone.com . On the Home page you can see “Manage My access” or if you are requesting for another person you need to select from top-left corner's drop down (icon with 3 lines) Manage Access > Manage User Access. How to apply for Blackline tool access? If you select “Manage My Access” you will be directed to “Search Access” , "Search by keywords' fill in blackline If you use “Manage User Access” you will be directed to “Search User”. Type last name or employee or contingent ID number and tick the circle next to the name (green when selected). After that click Next and you will be directed to access search. Select the role that you would like to request for. Please be noticed, there is roles in 'All' level or 'company code' level, multi selection is possible. Requestor will receive notification email from My access with subject ' New Access Right Request has been created for users xxx' and you can check approval status through ' Track My Request'. Approval flow Approval requests will be sent automatically to approvers if the requested access requires approval. KONE roles, access request requires manager approval and Area Process Specialist approval. and Genpact roles , access request requires manager approval and one of Process Manager approval. Approval flow is defined separately for each access (role) and 2 levels of approvals can be required . You can use “Track my requests” functionality to follow up approval process. After submitting the request, you will be redirected to the home page of MyAccess with a message that “Your request has been submitted successfully”. Once the request is approved (by all the approver levels) . Notification will be send to blackline admin. Note: In case there is no license available, Blackline admin will reach you and your line manager offline for further discussion offline. Whom to contact when We got Askit ticket blackline_genpactl1support@kone.com, blackline.admin@kone.com, rajendersingh.sardar@kone.com

short_description: Month End Closing Dashboard
introduction: Why are we doing this? What was the project methodology followed ? What our end vision is ?
instructions: Why are we doing this? We were lacking approach to monitor the automated process → priority get solution (our project phase 1 topic) We have multiple excels for task & close progress → target is to harmonize and bring one view to units, areas, global teams We lacked visibility to the Genpact unit specific manual activity progress – Genpact activities → target is to replace unit specific excels with this dashboard What was the project methodology followed ? We proceed with this work in iterative manner Starting with critical ones , learning + improving → adding more scope Thus we need your feedback + patience First version might not be ideal for units point of view but based on feedback this solution is flexible to build further What might be missing from first version not all tasks yet included to the tool not yet ideal grouping of tasks (for mgmt. view), not yet fully globally harmonized task list (This we need to check if they have harmonized or if there can be same task in many names?) What our end vision is On-time visibility from different angles: unit, AREA, Global teams can all see the progress of close activities Post close performance information: after the close this data can be leveraged to analyze performance (timeliness…) Link to MEC Dashboard https://flpnwc-a885a3dfd.dispatcher.hana.ondemand.com/sites/konecloud#Shell-home Grid View This view shows the total jobs aggregated for each region color coded by status aggregated to overall KONE Grid View Functionalities Table View This view shows the data at the most granular(individual job status at company code) level. Additional options to further filter, sort at column level. Also option to download the data Table for tasks will have option to edit and save while one for jobs will not Table View Functionalities Graph View Functionalities This view shows the data aggregated to region based on different filters. Data is also grouped by job category and further grouped by statuses Reassign Task

short_description: Splunk universal forward installation instruction
introduction: How to install the Splunk universal forwarder?
instructions: Steps to install the universal forwarder : 1. Download the universal forwarder from splunk.com based upon the host type (select a package accordingly). Setup a login by clicking on Free Splunk, as that’s necessary to be able to download the package. 2. Double-click the MSI fiel to start the installation. 3. (Optional) To view the license agreement, click the "View License Agreement" button. Select the Check this box to accept the License Agreement check box. 4. Click the "Customize Options" button. the installer presents you with the following options. The installer puts the universal forwarder into the C:\Program Files\SplunkUniversalForwarder directory by default. (Optional) Click Change to specify a different installation directory. 5. ( Not Required ) Select an SSL certificate to verify the identity of this machine. Depending on your certificate requirements, you might need to specify a password and a Root Certificate Authority (CA) certificate to verify the identity of the certificate. If not, these fields can be left blank. 6. Select the Local System or Domain Account check box and click Next . If you specify Local System , the installer displays the Enable Windows Inputs dialog box. As per KONE security policy we should select this Domain Account 7. If you specify Domain account , the installer displays a second dialog box where you enter domain and user information. Specify the user name in domain\username format only, or the installation can fail. Note : Kindly create a dedicated Service account for the Splunk UF installation , please refer to KBA3478 in AskIT for the steps to create service account , the service account will be owned by the respective application team , please follow the below nomenclature when you create Service account for Splunk Universal forwarder installation and drop an email to SplunkSupport Splunksupport@kone.com (FYI) On the service user that has been created for your specific application SVC_SplunkUF_<Application Name> 8. Select one or more Windows inputs from the list and click Next . 9. Create credentials for the Splunk administrator user, then click Next Note : As per KONE security password policy, password must be 14 characters (1 - Numeric, 1 - Lower case, 1 - Upper case, 1 - Special character) and We should not leave it as blank. Please don't share this user credentials. 10. ( Not Required ) Click Next . 11. Click Install to proceed with the installation. 12. The installer runs and displays the Installation Completed dialog. The universal forwarder starts automatically. 13. From running Windows Services (services.msc), confirm that the SplunkForwarder service runs Steps to forward data to a particular index are as follows: 1. Download the splunkclouduf.spl file from Splunk cloud. (This file is being provided along with this document or Based on the request, Splunk AMS support team will share this license file) 2. Place the .spl file under $SPLUNK_HOME/etc/apps (e.g. If you are using windows server place it under C:\Program Files\Splunk\etc\apps). 3. Rename the file extension from .spl to .tar.gz. So now the new filename should be splunkclouduf.tar.gz and file extension should show as GZ file. 4. Now run the CMD as administrator and navigate to $SPLUNK_HOME/etc/apps (e.g. If you are using windows server navigate to C:\Program Files\Splunk\etc\apps) 5. Run command tar xvf splunkclouduf.tar.gz 6. After completion, check if C:\Program Files\Splunk\etc\apps has now a folder named as 100_koneSplunkCloud. 7. Add perfmon stanzas in inputs.conf. Navigate to path > $SPLUNK_HOME\etc\system\local\inputs.conf (e.g. If you are using windows server navigate to C:\Program Files\Splunk\etc\apps). Add index=xxx to already present default stanza and save the configuration. For example, [default] host = XXXXXXXXXX (the host name should be already present) index = xxx (the index must be added specifically) Below is the snippet for inputs.conf file [default] host = XXXXX index=xxx [WinEventLog://Application] checkpointInterval = 5 current_only = 0 disabled = 0 start_from = oldest [WinEventLog://Security] checkpointInterval = 5 current_only = 0 disabled = 0 start_from = oldest [WinEventLog://System] checkpointInterval = 5 current_only = 0 disabled = 0 start_from = oldest [WinEventLog://ForwardedEvents] checkpointInterval = 5 current_only = 0 disabled = 0 start_from = oldest [WinEventLog://Setup] checkpointInterval = 5 current_only = 0 disabled = 0 start_from = oldest [perfmon://CPU Load] counters = % Processor Time;% User Time instances = _Total interval = 10 object = Processor [perfmon://Available Memory] counters = Available Bytes interval = 10 object = Memory [perfmon://Free Disk Space] counters = Free Megabytes;% Free Space instances = _Total interval = 3600 object = LogicalDisk [perfmon://Network Interface] counters = Bytes Received/sec;Bytes Sent/sec instances = * interval = 10 object = Network Interface After making the change, restart the Splunk : 1. Open the CMD as admin and navigate to $SPLUNK_HOME\bin (e.g. If you are using windows server navigate to C:\Program Files\SplunkUniversalForwarder\bin). 2. Run command splunk.exe restart. After its completion data should start flowing to cloud in the index you have specified in inputs.conf

short_description: Edit Roles in MyAccess
introduction: In MyAccess ROLE OWNERS can edit any role. This document helps you to modify any role you own-- application access roles, distribution lists, shared drives role owners details, display names, description or delete any role, for example distribution list, shared drive security group, application role.
instructions: Edit Role functionality allows Role Owners to modify existing Roles in MyAccess Functionality is available in MyAccess only for Role Owners. Distribution which are migrated lists are also managed as roles. After completion of role edit, role owners are notified with email for either success of role edit, or failure of role edit. NOTE: Distribution list display name will only change in MyAccess. DL alias name change is done in AD via ticket. Role Owners can modify following role details in MyAccess: Add and remove Role Owners (recommended to have at least two Role Owners) Modify Role Display name and Role Description Modify Attachment requirements (required or not) and Attachment related text Modify Approval Flow (Approval types). Table of different approval types is below in this document Add and remove Approvers i.e. Approver group members Schedule a role deletion Add members in bulk How to navigate to Edit Role : Select menu from the top left corner in MyAccess. Select from the drop down menu Manage Roles à Edit Roles. Please Note: Role Edit form opening will take about 15 seconds. Select Role from the drop down menu or search by typing part of the role name and press Edit. Edit Role form will open and you can make needed changes and save them by clicking Confirm. Please Note: MyAccess will process changes and they will be fully updated and visible for users in one hour. Owners can edit Role Display Name. (Role Name and Business Application name will stay as are technical details of the role.) If there is a need to modify Role Name or Business Application name, please open a ticket to MyAccess support via AskIT. Role Owners can add and remove other owners . It is recommended to have at least two owners per role. Please note: At least one Owner must be KONE Employee. Note that role owners is also able to remove or add users to the role. in Role Edit Page, display all the users who have the role currently assigned and allow the role owner to remove/add users. When a user is removed, the role should be de-assigned from the user and action is audited with appropriate comments Once the role is assigned, role owner and users (who have this role assigned ) should be notified. Please note: At least one Role Owner must be KONE Employee. Otherwise, an error message "Please add at least a KONE Employee as owner" appears. Approval flow can be modified for Role Requests and for Role Removal Requests . Different Approval flow types are visible in the Approval Type drop down menu. Different approval flow options are explained in the table below. Note : More than one approver should be selected under “First Level Approvers” and “Second Level Approvers”, otherwise error message “More than one Approver is required” appears. Attachment can be set as mandatory for the Role request by selecting “attachment required”. The Attachment text box will appear, and owner needs to add text to explain what kind of attachment is needed. Text box can contain a link to the needed attachment template of form. Comments can be set as mandatory for the Role request by selecting “Comments Required”. Non-requestable can be set as true for the Role request by selecting “non-requestable”. Delete role: Role End Date can be set/modified for the Role by providing a date. The role will be deleted from MyAccess. Also, the associated entitlement/group will be deleted from the application. You are also able to schedule role validity min 1 day up to 1095 (3 years) days: Role owner can now add or remove the members from role. You can search users by email, KONE ID. When you add users to the Add/remove box the section add or remove tab is appearing. Once you have added correct members you can confirm the change. Assignment notes will be delivered to the members added/removed and role owners. After completion of role edit, role owners are notified with email notification for either success of role edit, or failure of role edit. Following are details - Email Subject Description Information: Role <role name> has been successfully updated in MyAccess. Success email template used to provide information to role owner(s) about role edit success. This email has a role name and attributes values with old and updated new values. This email also has information about who has performed role edit . For role owner and approver members update, identities display name is provided in email content. Information: Role <role name> update failed in MyAccess Failure email template used to provide information to role owner(s) about role edit failure. This email has role name and information about who has performed role edit. For role edit success or failure audit events are also logged. Audit Description RoleEdit This audit event provides information about role edit success or failure. In case of success old and new values of attributes are displayed . In case of failures error message/information is displayed.

short_description: ECM - How to add ECM Add-in (M-Files) in SharePoint & Teams
introduction: How to add ECM Add-in in SharePoint & Teams?
instructions: M-Files Add-In for Teams and SharePoint Online enables you to access documents and information stored in M-Files (KONE ECM) directly from the Teams or SharePoint. This document explains how to take the add-ins into use. If most of the users in your team or process having ECM access and using via ECM Desktop client or web, you may extend this the use in to SharePoint or Team ECM Add-in complete your document review,upload,approve task in there itself. short_description: Splunk Prod Access from MyAccess
introduction: How to Get or Revoke Splunk Prod Access from MyAccess ?
instructions: Steps to be followed to Get the access : 1.Click on the below URL to go MyAccess application (Provide your KONE Credentials if ask for Login to MyAccess application) https://myaccess.kone.com/ 2.Go to “ Manage My Access ” and search for “ splunk ” in search box under “ Add access ” Tab 3.Select appropriate role for which user need access to Splunk from below: Here are some scenarios which can help to decide the appropriate role. Prod Admin Users : This role can let the user edit roles, change user/role mapping, change authentication settings, change forwarder settings, edit index cluster and other similar tasks. (This role is intended for Splunk developers , administrator and consultants. To opt for this role you need business justification and approval to have admin access) Prod Users : This role can let the user create and edit its own saved searches, run searches, edit its own preferences, create and edit event types and other similar tasks. (If you are an end users who wanted to see a particular dashboard / report in Splunk , then this role would be appropriate. ) Note : Splunk access will be handled by My Access Team and Splunk support team via AskIT ticket(autogenerated by MyAccess tool). 4.Verify the roles selected for access and click on “ Submit ” to complete the request. 5.After request submitted by user, it is routed to Service Manager of Splunk for the approval. 6.Once the request has been approved by Service manager, the request will be processed by Splunk support team and User will be notified on the access request completion via email. 7.User can track the access request in “Track My Request” tab Steps to be followed to Revoke the access : 1.Click on the below URL to go MyAccess application (Provide your KONE Credentials if ask for Login to MyAccess application) https://myaccess.kone.com/ 2.Go to “ Manage My Access ” and search for “ splunk ” in search box under “ Remove access ” Tab 3.Select appropriate role for which user needs to revoke the access to Splunk and click on Next (Refer attached excel for Role and approver details) Note : splunk revoke access will be handled by My Access Team and Splunk support team via AskIt ticket(autogenerated by MyAccess tool). 4.Verify the roles selected for access and click on “ Submit ” to complete the request. 5.After request submitted by user, it is routed to Service Manager of Splunk for the approval. 6.Once the ticket has been approved by Service Manager , the request will be processed by Splunk support team and User will get notified for revoke access completion via email. 7.User can track the access request in “Track My Request” tab

short_description: Ask IT - Inbound connections blocked to KONE workstations also inside KONE network
introduction: Inbound connections blocked to KONE workstations also inside KONE network
instructions: Inbound connections blocked to KONE workstations also inside KONE network Generic information about the restriction: We need to better secure KONE data and services by minimizing the possibilities of cyber-attacks initiated also inside KONE network. For this reason, inbound connections to KONE workstations also within KONE network have been blocked during 2H/2021 impacting all KONE managed Windows workstations (incl. VDIs) globally, excluding factory floor and tester workstations. This is done with the GPO: KONENET-KEE-Workstation-Firewall . Blocking inbound connections means that KONE workstations cannot be connected by other devices in KONE network. For an average KONE office worker there is no impact on daily work. For a small part of the users, this might mean a need to adjust the ways of work as peer-to-peer connections have been blocked. For example, if a user has provided support to another user by accessing the admin shares or remote registry, that won’t be possible anymore. Guidance for Ask IT: Issues with individual devices : If people contact Ask IT for having issues due to this restriction and if the issue is critical, i.e., they cannot overcome it by changing their ways of working, ask for the details below and assign the ticket to the PC Security Team (KONE.ITO.PCSECURITY) for exclusion. the device name device type: factory / tester / office business justification for getting excluded from the policy target Instructions for Ask IT to connect with end user workstations remotely: The way to remotely contact a workstation is by using Teams, SCCM remote console or Windows Quick Assist. Remote accesses have been made available from hop servers (EUDVMMSHOPSRV03, EUDVMMSHOPSRV04). Accessing a workstation directly from SCCM server is not possible anymore. Guidance for OSS teams: An issue with a factory floor or tester workstation : Make sure the device is in the correct factory or tester AD organizational unit (OU). Having a workstation in a factory or tester OU will exclude it from the workstation policy GPO: KONENET-KEE-Workstation-Firewall . Iinstead, GPO: KONENET-KEE-Workstations-Firewall-FactoryMachines or GPO: KONENET-KEE-Workstations-Firewall-TesterMachines is applied. If a device is moved to another OU to get the right policies applied, run a GPupdate to get the new rules applied, i.e. remove the policy blocking the inbound connections, without a waiting time. If that does not help, create a ticket to the PC security team (KONE.ITO.PCSECURITY). Temporary allow FW rules : If there is a need to temporarily allow additional traffic, proceed according to the below guidelines. Please note that the FW rules must be removed for temporary basis only and only when there is a valid business justification to do that. If the below steps do not help, consult the PC security team (KONE.ITO.PCSECURITY). GPO: KONENET-KEE-Workstations-Firewall-Domain-Allow policy allows all inbound traffic in Domain network. To apply the GPO on a device, the device must be added into the AD group: GPO-KONENET-KEE-Workstations-Firewall-Domain-Allow . Contact the PC security team (KONE.ITO.PCSECURITY) for this. GPO: KONENET-KEE-Workstations-Firewall-All-Allow policy allows all traffic (inbound/outbound) for all type of network (Domain/Private/Public). To apply the GPO on a device, the device must be added into the AD group: GPO-KONENET-KEE-Workstations-Firewall-All-Allow . Contact the PC security team (KONE.ITO.PCSECURITY) for this. If there is a need to temporarily disable all FWs for issue troubleshooting, then forward this request to Kimmo Saine, DS Platform owner (email: kimmo.saine@kone.com ) with business justification. Application specific exceptions: Specific FW exceptions have been implemented for certain applications. These exceptions can be identified by checking the group memberships of the computer and checking the GPO's which are applied to the OU. The group name reflects the applied exception GPO. The application specific exceptions are documented here . Please contact the PC security team (KONE.ITO.PCSECURITY) for more information or when there is a need for an additional exclusion.

short_description: Teams Group Modification ( Add/Remove Members,Guest & Owners) by O365 Admin
introduction: How to Add/Remove Member, Guest & Owners in Teams Group by O365 Admin?
instructions: Subject 1: Adding Member in Teams Group:- 1. Navigate to Microsoft 365 Admin Center -> Groups -> Active Groups and search with Group name or Microsoft 365 address 2. Select desired Group navigate to Members -> View All and Manage Members 3. Click on + Add Members and add your requested Members in the group and click on ADD Subject 2: Removing Members:- 1. Follow till step 1-2 from Subject 1 2. Select requested Member from the list and click on Remove Members Subject 3: Adding Owners 1. Navigate to Microsoft 365 Admin Center -> Groups -> Active Groups and search with Group name or Microsoft 365 address 2. Click on Members tab and go to View All and Manage Owners 3. Click on +Add Owners and Search requested user to be added as an owner then click on ADD Subject 4: Removing Owners:- 1. Follow till step 1-2 from Subject 3 2. Select requested Member from the list and click on Remove Owners Subject 5: Manage guest access in Microsoft 365 groups 1. Manage groups guest access If you want to enable or disable guest access in groups, you can do so in the Groups . 1.1 In the admin center, go to Show all > Settings > Org settings and on the Services tab, select Microsoft 365 Groups . 1.2 On the Microsoft 365 Groups page, choose whether you want to let people outside your organization access group resources or let group owners add people outside your organization to groups. 1. Add guests to a Microsoft 365 group from the admin center If the guest already exists in your directory, you can add them to your groups from the Microsoft 365 admin center . (Groups with dynamic membership must be managed in Azure Active Directory .) 2.1 In the admin center, go to the Groups > Groups . 2.2 Click the group you want to add the guest to, and select View all and manage members on the Members tab. 2.3 Select Add members , and choose the name of the guest you want to add. 2.4 Select Save . Note:- Approval is needed from Teams Group owner to perform any changes in the group. In case Owner is not available for the group, approval can be taken from Existing members or Line Manager of the user to proceed further.

short_description: Ask IT - Kone-Enable IE Mode- SOP for AskIT
introduction: Enable IE Mode & Import IE Favorites to Edge - SOP for AskIT
instructions: Introduction: KONE is moving forward with the adoption of MS Edge on all workstations. IE will be restricted from all devices except for the users that require IE mode (legacy mode). EUD team has prepared a script that will enable IE mode within Edge application. The same has been tested successfully and now it’s available as application in SCCM for production deployment. Document Name: Kone- Enable IE Mode- SOP for AskIT Document Purpose: Purpose of this document is to empower AskIT agents to respond KONE User’s requests\complaints for Kone- Enable IE Mode application as this will be a new deployment and AskIT should be well aware about the change and can respond accordingly. Application Details: · Application Name: Kone- Enable IE Mode Deployment Mode: This is AD-group based (User group) deployment, that means AskIT will add USERID in AD group only. SCCM collection will fetch AD group members and accordingly application will be available on user’s primary workstation. This Deployment will be in “Available Mode”, means Application will be available in Software Center, User need to install from Software Center. · Installation Prerequisite: Device should be connected to KONE Network. · AD Group Name: Kone-EnableIE-Mode · SCCM Collection Name (User Collection) : Kone-EnableIE-Mode * Once USERID is added to respective AD group, it will take 6-12 hours to automatically update SCCM collection and accordingly Software will be available to User’s Software Center. ** Once the Application is installed, Users won’t be able to use Internet Explorer as a native browser. Issues Reported: Nothing as of today. Possible issues: 1. Software is not available in Software Center Common deployment related issue, please check User’s login ID has been added to the deployment AD group. Check SCCN Collection has been updated or not if its been > 6 hours when you added the User ID to the AD group. You can update SCCM collection. 2. Application installation Failed This could be a generic failure. Restart SMS Agent service in Services. wait for 2-3 min to start all sccm-related components. Install the Application. Still issue, follow advanced steps as mentioned below. 3. URL does not open in IE mode\ URL is not in my Site List: Instruction attached in this document, same has been shared on Kone Portal to Users. KBA6863 | Salesforce Enable IE Mode Troubleshooting Steps (Advanced): Below steps will guide you to enable IE mode on Windows 10 workstation manually. You must check these on machines while troubleshooting the case. Step -1:- First need to check Microsoft Edge Chromium version is installed or not, to check that, Open “Control Panel” from Start menu, Click Programs and Features under the Programs section. Check Microsoft Edge installed in the list with the correct Version 91.0 or higher and the Icon should be looks like Step -2: Second to check Internet Explorer is enabled or not. To check that Open “Control Panel” from Start menu, Click Programs and Features under the Programs section. From the left side pane Click on “Turn Windows feature on or off” Make sure that “Internet Explorer 11” checkbox is Enabled. Step -3:- Open “ Registry Editor” with Administrative privilege, to open that go to Start menu and search for the Registry Editor utility . Right-click the "Registry Editor" utility and choose "Run as Administrator" from the context menu to launch the Registry Editor with elevated privileges. Once it open browse the below two locations: - 1. HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE\SOFTWARE\Policies\Microsoft\Internet Explorer\Main On the right side check the Value Name of “NotifyDisableIEOptions”, and value must be set as 2 2. HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE\SOFTWARE\Policies\Microsoft\Edge On the right side check the registry keys and their respective values. · “InternetExplorerIntegrationLevel” : value must be set as 1 · “InternetExplorerIntegrationSiteList” :value must be set as \\EUDVMMSSCCM003\PackageSource\Microsoft\Edge\IEModeSiteList\ie_site_list.xml · “InternetExplorerIntegrationSiteRedirect” : value must be set as 2 You can create reg keys and insert values if any of them you find missing. How to import your Internet Explorer Favorites in Edge Browser :- Step 1 - In Microsoft Edge, click on the three dots in the upper-right-hand corner, then on Settings. Step 2 - Select Profiles settings and Click on "Import Browser Data" Step 3 - Click on "Choose what to Import" Step 4 - Make sure in Import from you select "Microsoft Internet Explorer" and click on "Imports" END of the Document

short_description: Ask IT - Official end of Internet Explorer on KONE Workstations - Moving to Microsoft Edge
introduction: Official end of Internet Explorer on KONE Workstations - Moving to Microsoft Edge
instructions: Official end of Internet Explorer on KONE Workstations Please be advised, KONE IT Team will disable Internet Explorer (IE) from all workstations as it is end of life. Internet Explorer will be restricted and not available for normal use for all devices that currently have IE set as the default browser. Users are required to use Microsoft Edge as the default browser. Website or URLs will open in Edge even if attempted to open in IE. There could be legacy websites which do not function as expected in Edge or other browsers (Chrome, Firefox). For these exceptional cases, legacy IE mode can be enabled in Microsoft Edge. Users will be able to open these sites\URLs in IE legacy mode within Edge. Deployment Date: September 6, 2021 KONE End User Platforms Team has created a PowerShell script that will enable IE mode in MS Edge. The script has been packaged as an application and published in Software Center for user-based deployment on demand. 1. Users are required to contact their country SDM with these URLs and the SDM can request the URLs be added as exceptions. The master list will be maintained by the End User Platforms Team. 2. Users can raise an AskIT request to get IE mode enabled. AskIT team will add respective user ID to SCCM application group as in any normal application requests. 3. Once added, the solution will be available in Software Center for installation and users can initiate the installation. 4. Please follow the instructions below to install the script from Software Center and check/update the local copy of the site list on the device. How to get IE mode enabled in Edge: SCCM Application Name: “Kone- Enable IE Mode” Prerequisite: Device must be connected to KONE network, either at a KONE office or VPN, before installing the solution. 1. Go to windows search box (click on Start) and type “Software Center” in search option and click enter or select open (or go to Start, Microsoft Endpoint Manager, Software Center) 2. Install “Kone- Enable IE Mode” from Software Center. 3. Restart Required: Users may need to restart the device or Edge according to current device configuration. Check\Update Site List: Users should update the local copy of the site list on their devices as required. Instructions to check\update site list are as follows: 1. Launch Microsoft Edge 2. Type “Edge://Compat/Enterprise” and Press Enter 3. The site list and Force Update button will be displayed as shown below. Check to see if your site is listed or not. If so, press “ Force Update ” to refresh the list. Make sure you are connected to KONE network before hitting “Force Update”. **If your site URL is not listed here, reach out to your SDM with the URL to be added in the global list. Validate if IE mode is enabled for the listed sites from the “Engine” column. Open your website or any of the listed sites in your Edge browser and check for the IE icon as highlighted below.

short_description: Granting access to a sharepoint site
introduction: How to grant access of a Share point site to an user
instructions: Providing access to a user for a SharePoint site 1-) Once approval from site owner is received to grant the requestor access to the SharePoint site, we navigate to the SharePoint Admin Center. 2-) Search for the site in the Active Sites section (Keep search query filter to “All sites”) 3-) Search for the site in the search bar and make yourself the admin of the site by clicking on the site name once search results come up, then navigating to Permissions-> Site Admins-> Manage-> (Add yourself) 4-) Once added, open the SharePoint site by clicking on the URL. 5-) Once opened, click on the settings gear icon at the top right corner and click on site contents. 6-) A new window opens up, click on site settings. 7-) Click on the Site permissions on the window which opens up 8-) Click on Grant permissions to provide access to the user. 9-) Enter the email address of the user to whom the access needs to be given. Select the permission to be given from the dropdown at the bottom. Check the tick if you want to notify the user of the access grant. Click on Share to grant access. 10-) Click on Check permissions if permission for a user needs to be checked. 11-) Enter the email address to check the permission of the user for the site.

short_description: COR-TF incident follow up
introduction: This instruction is only meant for Infosys AMSD team to guide how the amount of incidents there are supposed to be followed up.
instructions: There are unfortunately unnormal continous high volume on incidents with COR-TF app. They are followed weekly with this report: Incident Report-Treasury | Salesforce ... where the priorisation is categorized based on the Remedy standards: 1 – Critical 2 - High 3 - Average 4 - Low Obs! For 1 critical and 2 high -priorities a queue in Remedy have to be KONE.CUST.M&C.3RDLEVEL instead of C UST.KONE.INT.SD as they are not MI (major incident) Obs! Once resolving a ticket with queue KONE.CUST.M&C.3RDLEVEL you have to first move it to queue CUST.KONE.INT.SD and then c losing the ticket to get stats right Important is once opening or maintaining tickets in Remedy to keep the priorisation with them aligned with the ticktets in Surecomp side at: https://jira.surecomp.com Those are weekly reviewed both in business call on friday morning and later the same day in IT call with Surecomp too. The priorisation have to be also done among the ongoing new requirements and project related work where unfortunately the same team in Surecomp is doing them too in top of the standard application support. Tips1: Similar to Remedy it is possible to keep Surecomp JiRa tickets up to date with eMail trafic too by keeping their ticket number in the subject line (ex KONE-xxx) and looping eMail customersupport@surecomp.com Tips2: In case of emergencies it is possible to use teams chat with Surecomp personnel - they are using O365 too Tips3: First row in short description field to be used for 'keywords' what users are calling their cases to ease up the communication in the meetings Tips4: Surecomp don't eMail their updates directly to KONE. Instead they update the tickets in their JiRa which then is sending eMail to 'reporter' (the one who did open the ticket) and also 'watchers' (volunteers who want to be informed on any updates too) Tips5 JiRa_1: Ticket status 'Pending customer' - once you want to put them back to Surecomp you have to choose 'Back to In Progress' -button and write comments Tips5 JiRa_1: There is dashboard 'KONE COR-TF' from which you can see all open ticket with different categories including 'Pending customer' and also 'Resolved' With resolved you can choose 'close' for the ones which are closed in Remedy to keep Remedy and JiRa aligned Tips5 How to maintain all details of cortf tickets i ncluding details of Jira, Remedy etc. Go on below sharepoint link and create new tickets with Remedy incident number and jira number and save all details with follow up so that treasury team can easily get updates on daily basis. short_description: Send automatic out of office replies from Outlook for Active users and Disabled users
introduction: How to set automatic out of office replies from Outlook?
instructions: Out of Office replies from Outlook can be setup either through Application or the browser itself (OWA) ­. To set Automatic Out of Office reply, user need to have F3/ E3 license. Process 1: Out of office for Disabled users To Set out of office for Disabled users, Ticket to be created through AskIT portal with Manager's approval or AskHR's Approvel's is Mandatory The ticket should be assigned to Office 365 CUST.KONE.INT.SD Queue Process 2 : Out of office for Active users To set Out of office for active user's the below methods to followed. Method [ A] – Out of Office replies through Outlook Application 1. Select File > Automatic Replies . 2. In the Automatic Replies box, select Send automatic replies . Optionally, set a date range for your automatic replies. This will turn off automatic replies at the date and time you enter for the end time. Otherwise, you'll need to turn off automatic replies manually. Note: If you don't see Automatic Replies , use Rules and Alerts to set up your out-of-office message. 3. On the Inside My Organization tab, type the response that you want to send to teammates or colleagues while you are out of the office. Note: Sending automatic replies to anyone outside my organization will send your automatic reply to every email, including newsletters, advertisements, and potentially, junk email. If you want to send automatic replies to those outside your organization, we recommend choosing My contacts only . 4. Select OK to save your settings. Turn off automatic out-of-office replies When Outlook is setup to send automatic replies, you'll see a message under the ribbon with this information. Select Turn off to disable automatic out-of-office replies. If you want to modify the dates for your automatic reply or the message sent, use the steps above to modify your settings. Method [B] – Out of Office replies Over Web Application On your browser, hit https://outlook.office365.com , then follow below steps: 1. Click on Settings icon: 2. Click on View All Outlook Settings: 3. Click on Automatic Replies: 4. Turn on Automatic replies, check “Send replies only during a time period”: 5. Draft an Out of Office message and click on save:

short_description: How to get access to mobile site survey application
introduction: How to get access to mobile site survey application?
instructions: PDM REQUEST FOR MOBILE SITE SURVEY 1. New PDM account for Site Survey User: How to apply PDM account · Before applying PDM account, users need to have KONENET ID, and they need to be in KONE network. · For non-KONE people, beside KONENET ID, KONE contact person need to request GSD to add person to correct supplier group and activate on- demand tokens before requesting PDM account. · In PDM Web authentication window, there is option to press Cancel-button, which opens Unauthorized-window and there is a link to registration form. Address to PDM: pdm.kone.com Registration form can be filled in by anyone or key users (KONE contact person for externals) can help with it. Registration form – KONENET ID · Type in KONENET ID and select New PDM account (if you have had account with other KONENET ID, for example external id, type that and select Change request to PDM account). Click Send. · If system says that account already exists, go back to authentication, type in your KONENET ID and same password what you’re using for example to sign into your computer. · If system gives error that KONENET ID cannot be found, contact your supervisor or KONE contact (externals) to get KONENET ID first. Registration form – contact person · After KONENET ID has been confirmed, select country where you’re working if you’re KONE personnel. – NOTE! Externals users select non-KONE to find correct company. (If correct company isn’t in list, please contact your KONE contact to get instructions for correct selections) · Select your unit’s contact person from given options by clicking Select- button. Registration form - Fields · Check that given name, country and email are correct, if there are errors let your supervisor know, because that information is coming from AD. Correcting those to the form, won’t correct those to AD. · Select your unit from selection list. · Type your City, function you work for, and Manager’s name. · If not working for KONE, type also your company and KONE contact name. · Select the role as Mobile Site Survey user. · Submit-button sends your request to approval and to PDM support. PDM support creates account after contact person has been approved it. New account created · After PDM Contact Person has approved the new request, PDM support will create it. o Once account is created, user gets automatic email (PDM contact Person as cc) that account is created and some helpful information. o PDM Team then notifies Mobile Site Survey application support team if the User Role is selected as Mobile Site Survey User while filing the form. o Mobile Site Survey support team validates the users PDM privilege and add the users to the Site Survey User group. o To access mobile site survey app the users should have Subcontractor and Tracepdm_Portal_User privileges. o Please note that old users will have Product_data_reader, Product_data_writer and Tracecountry_Analyst. Change request to existing PDM account for Site survey user · How to ask more privileges? How to ask changes for example to the name, department, or user ID? · Registration form – KONENET ID & PDM Contact Person · Type in KONENET ID and select Change request to PDM account. · Select Country where you’re working. NOTE! Externals users select non-KONE to find correct company. (If correct company isn’t in list, please contact your KONE contact to get instructions for correct selections) · Select your unit’s contact person by clicking Select-button. Registration form – fields · System shows current information (these cannot be modified!). · Select new role, if job description has been changed and/or type into Requested changes field what kind of changes are needed, for example name, department or country has been changed or more privileges (in our case Mobile Site Survey User) are needed (always explain why, what you’re trying to do to need new privileges). · Submit-button sends asked changes to be approved by contact person and after that support will do changes. · After approval PDM team will update the access rights and notify Site Survey team. PDM Privileges to access MSS APP · To access mobile site survey app the users should have Subcontractor and Tracepdm_Portal_User privileges. · Please note that old users may still have Product_data_reader, Product_data_writer and Tracecountry_Analyst. · The Exist in Active Directory column should read True in PDM

short_description: How to review the GRC PC notifications on Debug (Change) access and take actions via GRC?
introduction: How to review the GRC PC notifications on Debug (Change) access and take actions via GRC?
instructions: OBJECTIVE : On completing this training, the user should have an understanding on how to review the GRC PC notifications on Debug (Change) access in Production and process it. How to review the GRC PC notifications on Debug (Change) access and take actions via GRC? Login to GRC system User can login in two ways, 1a> Control evaluator will receive an email notification to his/her outlook from which they can directly login to GRC and access their work inbox by clicking the Work Inbox / Issue Remediation hyperlinks mentioned in the email 1b> Open GRP Production (SSO) from SAP GUI log on pad. 2> Logon to NetWeaver Business Client Once you are logged on to the SAP GRC server, you can run the transaction for NWBC in SAP Easy access. It will open NetWeaver Business Client screen and you will be re-directed to the Home screen of the SAP NetWeaver Business Client 3> Work Inbox: Navigation and Utilization On the Home screen different components of the GRC automated Solution (CCM) (such as Work Inbox, My Objects etc.) are displayed. Click on the hyperlink “Work Inbox” which will provide you a comprehensive list of all your GRC workflow tasks. You will then be re-directed to a screen where all the workflow tasks that need your attention are displayed: Under Workitems Select Process control as highlighted and click the request under subject which needs to be reviewed 4> Review the deficiency under Evaluation Go to Evaluation and click on review required which is matching the description of the request opened Analysis results are visible, click on export option to extract the report Reports have all the user details who have access to Debug at column “A” 5> Raise a ticket to security team Raise a ticket to security team from ASKIT to remove the debug access for user id as per the users list in column “A” (e.g., user id above KEHCPA) as highlighted which is deficiency as per the report and send email to security team with ticket details to action Once security team confirms the DEBUG access is revoked , attach the email confirmation evidence following below instructions as per screenshots 6> Submit the Request for closure Now go to issues, click on Void as shown in screen, update the comments as per the attachment evidence access is revoked and you can click on submit and close this review Click on submit and request is closed.

short_description: Add instances back into family table that was deleted from generic in Windchill
introduction: How to add instances back into family table that was deleted from generic in Windchill
instructions: PROCEDURE Delete the latest stand-alone iteration of the child object from Windchill. T o delete the latest iteration/revision refer to KB000A1916 Add the generic part/ assembly to the workspace and CHECK OUT the same. Adding Instance to the workspace Perform an Add to Workspace of the instances that need to be added back into the family without any other objects selected 1) Toggle to the Advanced collector tab 2) Select the instance(s) being added back into the family and toggle to CHECK OUT . 3) Click on OK Access the Event Manager > Conflict Manager for the Add event and see the message: “This instance belongs to the Generic version that is different from what is added to the Workspace. To proceed with this task, you must merge this instance into the workspace version of the Generic” It throws an error, click the resolve conflicts icon as shown below in the blue box Select the Retry Option “Merge this instance into Generic” and select Retry ---- The Add to Workspace action is retried. The previously removed iteration is added to the workspace (checked out) and also merged back into the family table. Both the instance and the top generic are marked in the workspace as “Modifications Uploaded” (in the embedded browser) or “Modified” (in a standalone browser). CHECK-IN the instance and the generic.

introduction: How to Delete a Family Table Instance from Windchill

short_description: AskIT - How to raise GRC access request for end users based on Clone ID for ERP(Z0*) systems
introduction: This KBA will provide instructions on how to create GRC request for SAP ECC / ERP systems access.
instructions: OBJECTIVE: On completing this training, the AskIT consultant should have an understanding on how to create a GRC request using custom access request form based on the clone ID. Scope Change Update related to Access Requests submission: Note: 1> There is a change in process from 25Jun2025 where AskIT team should handle only Clone ID based access requests for "New User" in GRC Access Request Form. Note 2> For any existing / change user's access requests as well as T-code / Roles based requests for New User Account , ASKIT team need to route the request to KONE SAP Security support team(SNOW Queue: KONE.SAP.SECURITY.AMS) by attaching duly filled respective user's access request template shared by user to ServiceNow request. Important Note: Before raising / creating the access request, please must follow instructions / validations explained in below knowledge base article link as pre-requisite. https://koneprod.service-now.com/now/nav/ui/classic/params/target/kb_view.do%3Fsys_kb_id%3Ddc909e93c3ed46505d2900e9d0013149%26sysparm_rank%3D7%26sysparm_tsqueryId%3Dbc1f53b947278290041f8f33036d4301 Process flow of SAP GRC Access request The process flow for using GRC Access request is as shown below. On receiving a request for SAP access from end user, please ask him/ her to provide the required details by sharing the SAP Access form template attached to this KBA and the same template is also available in SharePoint link: SAP GRC excel templates Please follow below steps to raise a GRC request for new user access provisioning in SAP systems based on the clone ID. You can navigate to the GRC SAP Access Request form by following the below instructions. 1. Open GRP Production (SSO) from SAP GUI log on pad. 2. Execute ZFORM t-code. After executing the "ZFORM" t-code, an Access Request Form as shown below will be opened. Text-box with * indicates Mandatory fields. 1. There are four options to choose from in the Landscape dropdown. Select Landscape as SAP ERP for providing access to SAP Z01/Z02/Z03/Z04 systems. Select the systems for which access request is to be raised. You can select a single system as well as multiple systems based on user’s request. 2. Work Region/Unit is mandatory to be selected. Please be careful while choosing work region/org unit and note that wrong selection will result in rejection of request or significant delay of approvals. 3. Enter KONE Network USER ID and Press Enter to populate Manager from Active Directory. 4. The GRC system is connected to LDAP / Active Directory, so the Manager ID will be populated automatically after filling the KONE Network User ID and clicked on “Enter”. 5. Access end date means the valid till date. By default, it is set as 31.12.9999 6. select Request Type as “New User Account” as Clone ID access can be raised only for New Users. 7.Select “Does user need access based on Clone ID?” as Yes to raise the GRC access request using the Clone ID. 8.Enter the Clone User ID. Clone ID is the reference user ID whose access is required for the user who is requesting the access. 9. Fill in the “Request Reason” with the relevant “ reason for access ” details provided by user. On selecting Landscape as “SAP ERP”, Request Type as “New User Account” and Does user need access based on Clone ID? as “Yes” below screen will appear with relevant options of selected Landscape. Please fill in all the fields as explained above in the sequence shown in below sample screen. Once all data provided by user is entered, click on Submit Access Request button. Once Clicked on “Submit Access Request” button below popup window will be displayed to review Work Region/ Unit selected. Cross check the selected Work Region/ Unit in the form with the access request template form shared by the user and then click on “submit” button. NOTE: In case wrong Work Region/ Unit selected then close the popup window and correct the Work Region/ Unit and repeat all the steps explained in the previous screens. Once request is submitted, GRC Request number will be displayed on popup window which can be noted down to communicate to the user for their reference and to track the request status. User will receive an automated notification email with request details after request submission. Below is the sample screen of such notification email to the user. Once GRC form request is created, please add the below comments into Resolution section of the ServiceNow ticket . Note : In the below comments before adding to the ticket, please replace requester name in < requester name> and GRC request number created in <GRC request number>. --------------------------------------------------- Hello <Requester name>, We have replaced your ticket for access to Z0* systems by creating GRC request: <GRC request number>. Please note that access will provide upon resolution of GRC request. For checking the GRC request status please follow the link: https://knecigrp9800.konenet.com:44398/sap/bc/webdynpro/sap/zkone_search_request?sap-client=100&sap-language=EN# In the future, for SAP access requests for Z0* systems, you can raise a GRC request yourself and resolution is FASTER since it is automated! please use following link: https://knecigrp9800.konenet.com:44398/sap/bc/webdynpro/sap/zkone_end_users# Refer KBA1169 for instructions on how to raise GRC request yourself. In case you have any queries, please send a mail to KONE_SAP_Security_GRC@KONE.COM --------------------------------------------------------- How to check the status of already raised Access request? After creation of access request and the automated notification mail received, the user can also check the status of an already raised access request using below URL. https://knecigrp9800.konenet.com:44398/sap/bc/webdynpro/sap/zkone_search_request# As shown below, enter the GRC request number to get the Status of the request, approval stage and name of the approver with whom the access request is pending for approval. Once the GRC request number is input, click on Enter button in order to get the details of the access request status (below is the sample screen which shows the status of entered request). Once approvals have been received at all approval stages, then access request will be processed automatically and user will get access in respective SAP system(s) for which access has been requested. Also, user will receive an automated notification email on completion of the request.

short_description: MyAccess Role Reports
introduction: Who can export MyAccess Role Reports? How to find Role Reports in MyAccess? How to understand the column details in “KONE-Report-IdentityRolesReport”?
instructions: Who can export Role Reports? Role Owners can run Role Reports, Role Details Report or Role Membership reports. Role Owner or group of owners is defined for each role. Owner details of a certain role can be seen by opening MyAccess and navigating from the top left corner to Manage Roles > View Role Details. That view is open for all users. How to find Role Reports in MyAccess? Login to MyAccess URL: https://myaccess.kone.com and select from the top left corner drop down Manage Roles > Export Role Reports How to export Role Reports Select Role Details Report or Role Membership report and press Submit. Do not select both at the same time. For the Role Details Report you need to select only the output format: pdf or csv and submit the request. The report will be processed, and the output file will be sent to your email. For the Role Membership Report you need to select output format and MyAccess Roles from the drop down. After selecting roles click submit and report will be sent to your email. To get a report of all the Roles, leave the value blank. How to understand the column details in “KONE-Report-IdentityRolesReport”? Column Name Description Name Display Name of the User Role Name Name of the role Assigned or Detected It indicates whether each role was assigned to or detected by the user. Assigned means that role has been requested via MyAccess/is granted through bulk assignment. Detected indicates user is present in the corresponding AD (Active Directory) security group/application access and has the access completely mapped. If the Role is seen only Assigned but not Detected, it indicates the access is partially granted/in process. These details will not be as described for the Role which are inherited in other Role/are granted as part of another Role. Worker ID Employees or Contingent Workers ID number from Workday or MyAccess record ID in case of Supplier-Distributor UserName Account name for login in to KONE Network (AD account, sAMAccountname) Email Email of the user UPN User Principal Name (login ID to cloud-based applications, not an email) Last Certification Certification is an access review process which will be done by role owner. Certifications are now activated only for a limited group of roles.

short_description: Process for GL Account Master Data Request in AskIT
introduction: How to raise a request for GL Account Master Data in AskIT
instructions: The GL Account Master Data request can be raised through its own channel and Form to be filled in the AskIT Portal Request moves immediately to R2R analysis and after approval request automatically moves to IT team to create changes to GL account master data Better transparency to all requests, enabling efficient monitoring of GL account master data requests; status and progress It has an accelerated turnaround time Note: Approval is sent via email to requestor and stored in remedy console, information for IT The Status Flow of the Request will be Opened, In-Progress, Resolved and Closed For the GL Master Data Request Template is Shown below Here in the below Table, it represents the Current status of the Request we have raised: Now coming to the Work Flow for the request , it is divided into three stages. Stage-01: The Requestor should fill the GL account request form in AskIT. Stage-02: The Process Owner will receive the request and start Analysis. He will will submit the request for Approval or Reject the request after the Analysis and if required after contacting the user for details. Stage-03: The SAP FICO team will receive the request and create a GL Account Master Data change in SAP and informs the User. After validation from User, they will close the request. You can see the below Flowchart representation of the above 3 stages: You can check the Status and Queue of your request in 'View in my Activity' If it is in 'OPENED' status it is with RSR GPO team , whereas in 'IN PROGRESS' status it has been assigned to SAP team. The following are the Queues and processes done in that Queues: KONE.F&C.R2R Staff: R2R Process Owners & Specialists Activity: analysis, approval Status: opened, pending, in progress, rejected, awaiting from user CUST.KONE.INT.SD Status Opened Staff: Service Desk Activity: move request to correct IT SAP AMS team CUST.KONE.INT.SAP.ERP.AMS Global KOCA G/L Account MDM team Staff: IT SAP FICO Support (Infosys GL master data & config maintenance) Opened, in progress, resolved, closed Assign ticket to person and create master data (and config) There will be Monthly Finance KOCA Governance meeting be reviewing & monitoring progress of KOCA and G/L account master data requests.

short_description: Major Incident Management Team – Engagement Process
introduction: Step-by-step procedure for proposing a Major Incident (MI) candidate using the ServiceNow Tool. Information about the roster plan, Contact Details and escalation points of contact (POCs) for the MI Team?
instructions: There are two ways to propose an incident as a Major Incident: Contact ASK IT through phone, email, or the chatbot to create an incident and request them to propose it as a Major Incident. If you have access to ServiceNow, create an incident via the "Employee Center" and propose it as a Major Incident. We recommend the second option for quicker processing, it’s not necessary to go through ASK IT to reach the MI team for Major Incidents. Please find the step-by-step process for proposing a Major Incident in the ServiceNow tool: Step 1: Upon incident creation, assign the incident to assignee’s name which will change the incident state from “New” to “In Progress.” Step 2: Re-Assign the incident to the MIM assignment group: “KONE.SIAM”. Step 3: Right-Click on the context menu (the grey colored line) or Click on the “Additional Actions” on the top left-hand side and select the “Propose Major Incident” option. (Note: The Proposal option will be enabled only if the incident is in the “In-Progress” state). Step 4: A window with the MI Questionnaire will be populated where the user needs to fill in all the details and click on “Propose”. Step 5: A call-out to the Major Incident team is mandatory upon proposing the incident. Step 6: The MI Manager will assess the impact with the required stakeholders and decide whether to PROMOTE or REJECT the MI candidate accordingly. NOTE: Please write/direct all email correspondence with the MIM team to - kone.mimanager@kone.com / kone.incidentmanagement@kone.com . MI Team Roster: The roster for the MIM team is in the below SharePoint location. First-time users can open the link and request access. The MIM team will approve the access at their earliest convenience. (Please click the below as Crtl+ Link) KONE MIM_Shift Rota_2026.xlsx Contact Details: Name Email Address Contact Number Sravan Kumar Garjala A garjalaa.sravankumar@kone.com +91 7824865551 Sankara Narayanan M sankaranarayanan.m@kone.com +91 7824865552 Vivek M vivek.matheswaran@kone.com +91 7824865553 Caroline Sylviya caroline.sylviya@kone.com +91 7824865554 Venkatraman Gurusamy venkatraman.gurusamy@kone.com +91 7823961298 Vignesh J vignesh.j@kone.com +91 9884320641 Abilash abilash.manayathnorbert@kone.com +91 7823924710 For Escalations: Please reach out to KONE SIAM Lead Poornima Krishnamoorthy at +91 9791042088.

short_description: Archiving Job Scheduling - SOP
instructions: Monitoring team had reported a high alert on the number of cancelled updates the ARVBTC user id in Z04. To prevent this in future the following points needs to be considered before scheduling jobs in Z04 1. The rule of -3/+5 days should be followed strictly during the month end freeze period. 2. Only changes (TR) related to legal requirements can be moved in Z04, during the month end freeze period 3. The archiving jobs should be run/scheduled in designated production server instance/s (Target : <SAP_ARCHIVE> ) 4. During scheduling the archiving jobs, put a time restriction of 4 hours. The person who is scheduling the archiving job, should check the status of the job & if it is causing dump, then it should be terminated 5. No parallel jobs should be scheduled in Z04 6. For heavy archiving jobs, the data should be spilt in order to minimize the time period 7. Please keep the rest of the team informed, when scheduling the jobs in Z04

short_description: AskIT - How to raise GRC access request for end users based on KoneWay Role and Region for CPM systems
introduction: This KBA will provide instructions on how to create GRC request for CPM(BW) systems access.
instructions: This KBA will provide instructions on how to create GRC request for CPM(BW) systems access. Instructions OBJECTIVE: On completing this training, the AskIT consultant should have an understanding on how to create a GRC request using custom access request form for CPM (BW*) systems based on KoneWay Role and Work Region/ Unit. Note: Access request creation for any of below systems is nothing but CPM (BW) system related access. 1> BW4 CPM (BI HANA) Production 2> BW3 CPM (BI HANA) Pre-Production 3> BW1 CPM (BI HANA) Development Process flow of SAP GRC Access request The process flow for using GRC Access request is as shown below. On receiving a request for CPM systems access from end user, please ask him/ her to provide the required details by sharing the CPM Access form template attached to this KBA and the same template is also available in SharePoint link: SAP GRC excel templates Please follow below process to raise a GRC request for new user/ existing user access provisioning in CPM (BW*) systems based on KoneWay Role and Work Region/ Unit. You can navigate to the GRC SAP Access Request form by following the below instructions. 1. Open GRP Production (SSO) from SAP GUI log on pad. 2. Execute ZFORM t-code. After executing the “ZFORM” t-code, an Access Request Form as shown below will open. Text-box with * indicates Mandatory fields. 1. There are four options to choose from in the Landscape dropdown. Select Landscape as CPM for providing access to CPM systems. 2. Work Region/Unit is mandatory to be selected. Please be careful while choosing work region/org unit. Note that wrong selection will result in rejection of request or significant delay of approvals 3. Enter KONE Network User ID and press enter to populate Manager User ID. The GRC system is connected to LDAP / Active Directory, so the Manager ID will be populated automatically after filling the KONE Network User ID and clicked on “Enter”. 4. Access end date means the valid till date. By default, it is set as 31.12.9999 5. Select the KoneWay Role to which user requested for access. 6. Select check box “Select Check Box in case User requires multiple KoneWay Role Access” for adding multiple KoneWay Roles for the same or different Work Region/ Unit based on the user requirement. 7. Select appropriate request type from dropdown. 8. Fill in the “Request Reason” with the relevant “ reason for access ” details provided by user. Please provide the appropriate reason for the access being requested. On selecting Landscape as CPM, below screen will appear with relevant options of selected Landscape. Please fill in all the fields as explained above in the sequence shown in below sample screen. Once all details/ inputs provided by user are entered, click on “Submit Access Request Based on KoneWay Role”. Below is the sample screen for raising access request to only one KoneWay Role based for the required region based on user requirement. Below is the sample screen for raising access request to multiple KoneWay Roles with same or different Work Region/ Unit based on the user requirement. Once you add all the KoneWay Roles for the requested Work Regions/ Units click on “Submit Access Request Based on KoneWay Role” Once request is submitted, GRC Request number will be displayed on top of the screen which can be noted down to communicate to the user for their reference and to track the request status. User will receive an automated notification email with request details after request submission. Below is the sample screen of such notification email to the user. Once GRC form request is created, please add the below comments into Resolution section of the Remedy ticket. Note : In the below comments before adding to the ticket, please replace requester name in < requester name> and GRC request number created in <GRC request number>. Hello <Requester name>, We have Replaced your ticket for access to CPM(BW*) systems by creating GRC request: <GRC request number>. Please note that access will be provided upon resolution of GRC request. For checking the GRC request status please follow the link: https://knecigrp9800.konenet.com:44398/sap/bc/webdynpro/sap/zkone_search_request?sap-client=100&sap-language=EN# In the future, for SAP access requests for CPM(BW) systems, you can raise a GRC request yourself and resolution is FASTER since it is automated! please use following link: https://knecigrp9800.konenet.com:44398/sap/bc/webdynpro/sap/zkone_end_users# Refer KBA1169 for instructions on how to raise GRC request yourself. In case you have any queries, please send a mail to KONE_SAP_Security_GRC@KONE.COM --------------------------------------------------------- How to check the status of already raised Access request? After creation of access request and the mail is received, the user can also check the status of an already raised access request using below URL. https://knecigrp9800.konenet.com:44398/sap/bc/webdynpro/sap/zkone_search_request# As shown below, enter the GRC request number to get the Status of the request, approval stage and name of the approver with whom the access request is pending.
