short_description: How to raise GRC access request for end users based on Team Name/ ID for BEACON systems
introduction: How to raise GRC access request for end users based on Team Name/ ID for BEACON systems?
instructions: OBJECTIVE: On completing this training, the AskIT consultant should have an understanding on how to create a GRC request using custom access request form for Beacon (BH*) systems based on Team Name/ ID. Scope Change Update related to Access Requests submission: Note: 1> There is a change in process from 01Nov2022 where AskIT team should handle only "New User" access requests creation for users in GRC Access Request Form. Note 2> For any existing / change user's access requests, ASKIT team need to route the request to Kone SAP Security Team by attaching duly filled respective user's access request template shared by user to Remedy request. Note: Access request creation for any of below systems is nothing but Beacon (BH) system related access. 1> BH4 Beacon (BPC HANA) Production 2> BH3 Beacon (BPC HANA) Pre-Production 3> BH1 Beacon (BPC HANA) Development Process flow of SAP GRC Access request The process flow for using GRC Access request is as shown below. On receiving a request for BEACON systems access request from end user, please ask him/ her to provide the required details by sharing the BEACON Access form template attached to this KBA in "Notes & Attachments " link and the same template is also available in below SharePoint link. Shift Left- HCL & Infy - General - All Documents (sharepoint.com) Please follow below process to raise a GRC request for new user/ existing user access provisioning in Beacon (BH*) systems based on Team Name/ ID . You can navigate to the GRC SAP Access Request form by following the below instructions. 1.Open GRP Production (SSO) from SAP GUI log on pad. 2. Execute ZFORM t-code. After executing the “ZFORM” t-code, an Access Request Form as shown below will open. Text-box with * indicates Mandatory fields. 1.Enter KONE Network User ID and click on enter. 2. Access end date means the valid till date. By default, it is set as 31.12.9999 3. There are four options to choose from in the Landscape dropdown. Select Landscape as BEACON for providing access to Beacon systems. 4. Select Team Name/ ID based on the user requirement. 5. Finance Approver will be populated automatically after filling the Team Name/ ID and clicked on “Enter”. 6. Select the tick box “Select CheckBox in case User requires multiple Teams IDs Access” for multiple Team Names/ IDs access based on the user requirement. 7. Please provide the appropriate reason for the access being requested. Select appropriate request reason from dropdown and also fill in the “Request Justification” with the relevant “ reason for access ” details provided by user. On selecting Landscape as BEACON, below screen will appear with relevant options of selected Landscape. Please fill in all the fields as explained above in the sequence shown in below sample screen. Once all details/ inputs provided by user are entered, click on “Submit Access Request Based on Team ID” button. Below is the sample screen for raising access request for only one Team Name/ ID based on the user requirement. Below is the sample screen for raising access request for multiple Team Names/ IDs based on the user requirement. Select tick box “Select CheckBox in case User requires multiple Team IDs Access” for raising access to multiple Team Names/ IDs. Then select Team Name/ ID then click on “Add selected Team Name/ ID” and repeat the same step for adding all the Team Names/ IDs into the form based on the user requirement. Once all the Team Names/ IDs are added to the form then click on “Submit Access Request Based on Team ID” Once request is submitted, GRC Request number will be displayed on top of the screen which can be noted down to communicate to the user for their reference and to track the request status. User will receive an automated notification email with request details after request submission. Below is the sample screen of such notification email to the user. Once GRC form request is created, please add the below comments into Resolution section of the Remedy ticket. Note : In the below comments before adding to the ticket, please replace requester name in < requester name> and GRC request number created in <GRC request number>. --------------------------------------------------- Hello <Requester name>, We have Replaced your ticket for access to BEACON systems by creating GRC request: <GRC request number>. From 2019 onwards, we are processing all SAP access requests through GRC. Please note that access will provided upon resolution of GRC request. For checking the GRC request status please follow the link: https://knecigrp9800.konenet.com:44398/sap/bc/webdynpro/sap/zkone_search_request?sap-client=100&sap-language=EN# Did you know that GRC Access Request in now LIVE for your region? In the future, for SAP access requests for BEACON systems, you can raise a GRC request – resolution is FASTER since it is automated! please follow the link: https://knecigrp9800.konenet.com:44398/sap/bc/webdynpro/sap/zkone_end_users# KBA on How to raise GRC request is available there. You can login with your KONE credentials. In case you have any queries, please send a mail to CommonPlatforms.ServiceDesk@kone.com --------------------------------------------------------- How to check the status of already raised Access request? After creation of access request and the mail is received, the user can also check the status of an already raised access request using below URL. https://knecigrp9800.konenet.com:44398/sap/bc/webdynpro/sap/zkone_search_request# As shown below, enter the GRC request number to get the Status of the request, approval stage and name of the approver with whom the access request is pending. Once the GRC request number is input, click on Enter button in order to get the details of the access request. Below is the sample/ example screen of request status of entered request number. Once approvals have been received at all approval stages, then access request will be processed automatically and user will get access in respective SAP system(s) for which access has been requested. Also, user will receive an automated notification email on completion of the request. Notes: 1> If the user is not responding to request to fill in access request excel template then follow the 3 strike rule and close the ticket. 2> If the user DO NOT want to fill in the access request excel template then forward / route such requests to SAP Security team.

short_description: Packman - Accessing Packman via VDI with Changing Hostname
introduction: Packman - Accessing Packman via VDI with Changing Hostname
instructions: Packman is managing, to which computer a certain installation media / updates have to be installed. The issue arises when using Packman-managed tools via VDI (Virtual Desktop). The computer name changes frequently, so when the same user is accessing a new VDI session, the computer name might be different. This is causing challenges with distributing updates via Packman. To overcome this issue, the computer name can be "hard-coded" via user-specific environmental variables: Add an account-specific environmental variable (not system environmental variable) as follows: Variable Name: PACKMAN_CLIENT_NAME Variable Value: <Computer Name>, where <Computer Name> matches the existing computer name in Packman Now when starting the update process, the "hard-coded" computer name is used to identify the host.

short_description: Ask IT - How to troubleshoot Office apps access issues after password change
introduction: How to troubleshoot Office apps access issues after password change
instructions: What was the first error user saw after pwd change/reset? Take screenshots What is the current error user is seeing? Take screenshots Was password reset or changed? reset / changed When password was changed or reset? date and time as accurately as possible How password was reset? Reset by admin via Entra ID? Microsoft Entra ID -> Identity -> Users -> All Users -> Search User -> Reset Password Reset by user via Azure Self-Reset portal: http://passwordreset.kone.com ( https://passwordreset.microsoftonline.com/?whr=kone.com ) How password was changed? Changed by user via Windows workstation While in Windows profile, press keys CTRL-ALT-DEL -> Change a password Changed by user via Entra ID profile Profile picture in Office 365 browser window -> View Account "Password" in the left menu changed by user via Azure Self-Change portal https://passwordchange.kone.com/ ( https://account.activedirectory.windowsazure.com/ChangePassword.aspx ) Is user able to access https://outlook.office.com with the new password? Open Edge - Create new Edge profile for testing Type and navigate to address https://outlook.office.com If Edge asks to "switch profiles" answer --> "Don't ask me again" Browser should ask for username and password. Try new email address + new password i f asked to "Stay signed in" answer --> "No" If sign-in is successful, new password has been synced to Azure AD Is there audit log event visible in Entra ID for the affected user? https://entra.microsoft.com -> Identity -> Users -> All Users Search user by email address If found, click user name Click "Audit logs" in the left navigation, under "Activity" Portal lists all events from the last 1 month, check if any rows with "password" mentioned in "Activity" column What is the status of Hybrid Azure AD Join in the workstation after password reset? Run as affected user from affected workstation, using Command Prompt: dsregcmd /status In "Device State" part of the output, check that following settings are as listed: AzureAdJoined : YES EnterpriseJoined : NO DomainJoined : YES In "User State" part of the output, check that following settings are as listed: WorkplaceJoined : NO If "Diagnostics Data" is shown, there's something wrong. Check the following info: Previous Registration Error Phase Client ErrorCode More information about status output: Troubleshoot devices by using the dsregcmd command - Azure Active Directory | Microsoft Docs Is user workstation visible in Entra ID? https://entra.microsoft.com -> Identity -> Devices -> All Devices Search device by device name (workstation hostname) If found, click device name "Join type" should be "Microsoft Entra hybrid joined" "Registered" should have date information If join type is not "Microsoft Entra hybrid joined" or "Registered" date is missing there's something wrong with Hybrid Entra ID Join. Check password change timestamps from local AD user object Browse user using ADUC and check PasswordLastSet -attribute Or check by using AD PowerShell Get-ADUser <accountname> -properties * | Format-List PasswordExpired, PasswordLastSet Where <accountname> is short account name (samaccountname) like "k12345678" Compare Entra ID password change audit log entry time and AD object time. Are both Entra ID and local AD user objects updated? If not, there's issues with replication/sync Is there significant gap between password change times? Which one was updated first?

short_description: AskIT - How to raise GRC access request for end users based on Transaction Code(s)/ Role(s) for ERP(Z0*) systems
introduction: How to raise GRC access request for end users based on Transaction Code(s)/ Role(s) for ERP(Z0*) systems? How to check if user already assigned with User Group Role(s) in SAP ERP (Z0*) systems?
instructions: OBJECTIVE: On completing this training, the AskIT consultant should have an understanding on how to create a GRC request using custom access request form based on the Transaction Code(s)/ Role(s) for SAP ERP systems. Scope Change Update related to Access Requests submission: Note: 1> There is a change in process from 01Nov2022 where AskIT team should handle only "New User" access requests creation for users in GRC Access Request Form. Note 2> For any existing / change user's access requests, ASKIT team need to route the request to Infosys support team (SAP Security Team) by attaching duly filled respective user's access request template shared by user to Remedy request. Important Note: Before raising / creating the access request, please must follow instructions / validations explained in below knowledge base article link as pre-requisite. https://koneprod.service-now.com/now/nav/ui/classic/params/target/kb_view.do%3Fsys_kb_id%3Ddc909e93c3ed46505d2900e9d0013149%26sysparm_rank%3D7%26sysparm_tsqueryId%3Dbc1f53b947278290041f8f33036d4301 Note: Access request creation for any of below systems is nothing but SAP ERP Landscape / system related access. 1> Z04 ERP Production 2> Z03 ERP Pre-Prod 3> Z02 ERP Acceptance 4> Z01 ERP Development Process flow of SAP GRC Access request The process flow for using GRC Access request is as shown below. On receiving a request for SAP access from end user, please ask him/her to provide the required details by sharing the SAP Access form template attached to this KBA in “Notes & Attachments” link and the same template is also available in below SharePoint link. Shift Left- HCL & Infy - General - All Documents (sharepoint.com) Please follow below process to raise a GRC request for new user/ existing user access provisioning in SAP systems based on the Transaction Code(s)/ Role(s). You can navigate to the GRC SAP Access Request form by following the below instructions. 1.Open GRP Production (SSO) from SAP GUI log on pad. 2.Execute ZFORM t-code. a) Raising the Access request form using Transaction Code(s) After executing the “ZFORM” t-code, an Access Request Form as shown below will open. Text-box with * indicates Mandatory fields. 1.Enter KONE Network User ID and press enter to populate Manager from Active Directory. 2. Access end date means the valid till date. By default, it is set as 31.12.9999 3. There are four options to choose from in the Landscape dropdown. Select Landscape as SAP ERP for providing access to SAP Z01/Z02/Z03/Z04 systems. Select the systems for which access request is to be raised. You can select a single system as well as multiple systems based on users request. 4. Do not enter any Clone ID as the request is based on Transaction Code(s)/ Role(s) ( NOTE : User inputs are Transaction Code(s)/ Role(s)) 5. Work Region/Unit is mandatory to be selected. Please be careful while choosing work region/org unit. Note that wrong selection will result in rejection of request or significant delay of approvals 6. The GRC system is connected to LDAP / Active Directory, so the Manager ID will be populated automatically after filling the KONE Network User ID and clicked on “Enter”. 7. Select “ Remove existing roles/ access ” ONLY when user asks for removing the existing access while raising request based on Clone ID (i.e. this option is applicable only for users whose ID already exists in the respective SAP system). 8. Please provide the appropriate reason for the access being requested. Select appropriate request reason from dropdown and also fill in the “Request Justification” with the relevant “ reason for access ” details provided by user. 9. In the “Transaction Code” field enter the Transaction Code(s) and If access needed for multiple Transaction Codes then enter Transaction codes with only comma (,) as separator. ( NOTE : Do not add space after or before comma (,) separator) NOTE1: If the access request template form contains more than 10 transaction codes then route/ reassign those requests to Infosys Support team along with filled in template by the user. NOTE2: If the Transaction Code(s) are requested for multiple Work Regions/ Units then route/ reassign those requests to Infosys Support team along with filled in template by the user. 10. After entering Transaction Code(s) click on “Search Available Roles Based on T-code(S)” button to get list of available roles for required Transaction Code(s) access related to Work Region/ Unit and System selected in the access request form. On selecting Landscape as SAP ERP, below screen will appear with relevant options of selected Landscape. Once the Transaction code is entered, click on “Search Available Roles Based on T-code(s)” button to view the list of available roles in the selected system(s) for the required Transaction Code. Once the tick box beside the system is selected then click on “Add Selected Roles” button to move all the roles to “Selected Roles” section. Once the roles moved to selected roles section, click on Submit Access Request button. Please fill in all the fields as explained above in the sequence and below is the sample screen filled for single Transaction Code access. Below is the sample screen with multiple Transaction Codes entered. Once Clicked on “Submit Access Request” button below popup window will be displayed to review Work Region/ Unit selected. Cross check the selected Work Region/ Unit in the form with the access request template form shared by the user and then click on “submit” button. NOTE: In case wrong Work Region/ Unit selected then close the popup window and correct the Work Region/ Unit and repeat all the steps explained in the previous screens. Once request is submitted, GRC Request number will be displayed on top of the screen which can be noted down to communicate to the user for their reference and to track the request status. b) Raising access request form using Role(s). On selecting Landscape as SAP ERP, below screen will appear with relevant options of selected Landscape. Please fill in all the fields as explained above in the sequence and as shown in the below sample screen. Once clicked on “Find Role to Transaction Code Mapping” hyper link a popup window will be displayed as shown in the below sample screen. In the Role filed enter the role requested by the user and click on the “Search” button to view the role in the “Role – Transaction Code Details” section. Once the role is displayed select the tick box beside the system to get role selected and click on “OK” button. On clicked “OK” button below sample screen displays then click on “Submit Access Request” button to submit the access request. NOTE : If the user requested for multiple roles repeat all the above steps for adding all the roles into the form one after other. Once Clicked on “Submit Access Request” button below popup window will be displayed to review Work Region/ Unit selected. Cross check the selected Work Region/ Unit in the form with the access request template form shared by the user and then click on “submit” button. NOTE: In case wrong Work Region/ Unit selected then close the popup window and correct the Work Region/ Unit and then click on “Submit Access Request” button at the bottom of access request form screen. Once request is submitted, GRC Request number will be displayed on top of the screen which can be noted down to communicate to the user for their reference and to track the request status. User will receive an automated notification email with request details after request submission. Below is the sample screen of such notification email to the user. Once GRC form request is created, please add the below comments into Resolution section of the Remedy ticket. Note : In the below comments before adding to the ticket, please replace requester name in < requester name> and GRC request number created in <GRC request number>. --------------------------------------------------- Hello <Requester name>, We have Replaced your ticket for access to Z0* systems by creating GRC request: <GRC request number>. From 2019 onwards, we are processing all SAP access requests through GRC. Please note that access will provided upon resolution of GRC request. For checking the GRC request status please follow the link: https://knecigrp9800.konenet.com:44398/sap/bc/webdynpro/sap/zkone_search_request?sap-client=100&sap-language=EN# Did you know that GRC Access Request in now LIVE for your region? In the future, for SAP access requests for Z0* systems, you can raise a GRC request – resolution is FASTER since it is automated! please use following link : https://knecigrp9800.konenet.com:44398/sap/bc/webdynpro/sap/zkone_end_users# to raise GRC request yourself. Refer KBA1169 on How to raise GRC request. You can login with your KONE credentials. In case you have any queries, please send a mail to CommonPlatforms.ServiceDesk@kone.com --------------------------------------------------------- How to check the status of already raised Access request? After creation of access request and the mail is received, the user can also check the status of an already raised access request using below URL. https://knecigrp9800.konenet.com:44398/sap/bc/webdynpro/sap/zkone_search_request# As shown below, enter the GRC request number to get the Status of the request, approval stage and name of the approver with whom the access request is pending. Once the GRC request number is input, click on Enter button in order to get the details of the access request. Once approvals have been received at all approval stages, then access request will be processed automatically and user will get access in respective SAP system(s) for which access has been requested. Also, user will receive an automated notification email on completion of the request. Notes: 1> If the user is not responding to request to fill in "access request excel template" then follow the 3 strike rule and close the ticket. 2> If the user DO NOT want to fill in the "access request excel template" then forward / route such requests to Infosys support team. 3> Access requests submission for China users follow different process / approach. If AskIT team receives any China user related access request, please ask user to reach out to respective China unit's Key users to submit access request for ERP (Z0*) systems on behalf of user. 4> While raising the access requests, if there is any error message on request screen like roles not found / Manager not found... etc error message then please route such requests to Infosys support (SAP Security) team. 5> While submitting access requests if AskIT team see any of below pop-up message windows then AskIT team can route such requests to Infosys SAP Security support team.

short_description: Ask IT - Requesting Thinkcell licences through the MyAccess Portal
introduction: How to create request in MyAccess Portal for Thinkcell license.
instructions: Thinkcell has a user specific license fee and the application should be used only when needed for daily work. Application is targeted only for Finance or other KONE analyst roles. Request license to be assigned which happends via MyAccess Steps to raise the request are as below: Login to MyAccess portal with link https://myaccess.kone.com Click on Manage My Access Choose the role you are requesting for and then click on the Next button You will be prompted to next screen. click on Submit button. Your request is successfully submitted for Approval. Once the request is approved you'll get email as return that your request is approved After that the software gets automatically delivered to your computer - may take some time but within an hour anyway.

short_description: Ask IT - VDI Tickets assignment
introduction: Ask IT - How to assign VDI tickets ?
instructions: STEPS TO IDENTIFY GENPACT & NON-GENPACT(Standard, Developer, Power) USERS · There are 4 kinds of Users VDI are being assigned at present i.e. Genpact, Standard, Developer and Power · Genpact Users are part of “ VDI_SEC_GenPact ” Security Group, refer the screenshot · VDI Hostname starts with EUAZ1MSEUGIxxxx or EUAZ2MSEUGIxxxx e.g. · All Genpact user ticket should go to KONE.ITO.VDI.GENPACT Queue · Standard Users are part of “VDI_SEC_Standard” Security Group, refer the screenshot · Standard VDI Hostname starts with EUAZ1MSEUSIxxxx, EUAZ2MSEUSIxxxx e.g. · Developer Users are part of “VDI_SEC_Developer” Security Group, refer the screenshot · Developer VDI Hostname starts with EUAZ1MSEUDIxxxx, EUAZ2MSEUDIxxxx e.g. · Power Users are part of “VDI_SEC_Power” Security Group, refer the screenshot · Developer VDI Hostname starts with EUAZ1MSEUPIxxxx e.g. · All Non Genpact(Standard, Developer and Power) user ticket should go to KONE.ITO.VDI Queue

short_description: How to add a new user and remove an existing user access in Scheduler application?
introduction: How to add a new user and remove an existing user access in Scheduler application?
instructions: Steps to be followed are as below: 1. Check if the end user details like display name, Kone id, branch name and branch id are provided in the incoming ticket for the access. Also, check if the access is requested in which environment i.e. production or acceptance or both, if nothing is given it is production. If needed ask the user for the necessary details. 2. Open cloud hierarchy and select production server. If user requested access to acceptance then select acceptance server. Figure 3(A) 3. After connecting to the desired environment, select the application as Scheduler from the dropdown as shown in the screenshot below: 4. Now, check for the branch code for which access is requested in the ticket received. Find (Ctrl+F) and search branch code then click on the respective code and then click on Select button. The highlighted part in the image is the branch for which the access has to be provided. Whereas the rest in the image are work centers and must not provide access by selecting the work centers. 5.Click on add User. 6.Click on Browser / Import: While Clicking on Import: i. Enter the Last Name of the user and Click Find if you click on Import. Import will get values from active directory. If you choose to use the Import button and the name has too many entries, the application will crash and recommend to use a different way of adding user. Select the user from the results and click OK. While Clicking on Browse: ii. Enter the Name of the user and Click Find First if you click on Browser. Browser will get values from database. Select the user from the results and click OK. 7. Select the specified Access level which is provided by user. 8.If users need access to all the branches select root. And if user wants all of CKE or CKQ or MKO then select Company code and provide access which will provide access to all the branches inside that company. 9.Click File -> Refresh then you’ll be able to see the added end user’s id in the respective group or branch. 10. Finally, inform the ticket owner from teams/ personal mail id that the access is granted and ask for confirmation before marking the ticket as resolved. 11. To Remove access of user click on check box of previously provided access / user specified access. 12 . When the tick mark turns into a cross symbol it means it will reject access to that level and any levels subordinate to that level. Click again on the cross symbol, it will turn into a blank box. The blank removes authorization and if the entire row is blank the user will be removed from the list 13. Finally, inform the ticket owner from teams/ personal email id that the access is removed and ask for confirmation before marking the ticket as resolved. After copying the link below paste it in Windows Run. \\uskvmmssos005\clickonce$\KonectApplicationsProduction\Konect Applications Double Click on Loader Application. 3. Select the <Install> button. 4. Also, you may want to ‘Pin’ the new version to your Windows Taskbar. 5. The new version of Konect Applications will look like the image below: For updating KONECT to the newer version follow the below steps: Open Konect Applications Click on Help Click on Check Local Application Files It will update your computer with the recent version. short_description: AMS Archiving activity
introduction: Monthly archiving activity
instructions: Purpose of Archiving Archiving is the practice of safely preserving inactive information that is no longer use on a regular basis for long-term retention. Such information is still valuable to businesses and should be kept for future reference. Also helps the system to keep up and running with the relevant data in the system. Conditions · Archiving process can be started on 7 th of every month and has to be completed before 20 th of every month. · The archived data has to be updated only in China Automation sheet no other tabs in the sheet to be worked on. · Not to make changes directly in summary sheet (First make changes in another excel ) Archiving FI track Go to tcode: z_archive_dashboard Click on the download files 4 th radio button and provide the details as show in the below snapshot and click on execute button. Once you click on the execute button you will get the message as table cleared successfully as shown below snapshot. Now come out of the tcode and login again the same tcode. Now click on the first radio button and fill the details as highlighted in the below snap and execute in background IMMEDIATE CHECK AND SAVE Now click on the second radio button and proceed the same process. Now click on the third radio button and proceed the same process. Now go to SM37 and check the jobs status till It gets completed we can work on TAANA tcode. Go to tcode: TAANA BSEG: Create a variant and Schedule the job with the below parameters highlighted. ZZRESA: Create a variant and Schedule the job with the below parameters highlighted. VBRK: Create a variant and Schedule the job with the below parameters highlighted. Summary sheet updating Once the scheduled jobs in TAANA t-code is finished we have to update the values in a separate excel sheet by exporting in a separate sheet. 1. For BSEG – Once the job is successfully completed. Pull the values in our system and bifurcate the values in Finance Maintenance -KCC and Finance (Others)-KCC. Finance Maintenance -KCC : The values considered here should be of business are VA, VC and Blanks. A pivot table of this with Fiscal area and Business Area has to be pulled and maintained in separate excel. Finance (Others)- KCC : From the same BSEG table remaining Business Area (ESU, SBU, V1, V5, VB, VE) has to be selected and maintained in a pivot in a separate tab in excel. 2. VBRK – Once the VBRK job is successfully finished. We make a Pivot using fiscal year and number of entries archived and maintain this in a new tab of the excel. This can be named as sales order invoices 3. ZZRESA – Once this job is finished similarly we and update the same in an separate tab of the excel. His can be updated under special ledger. Generally we get values for last 3 years in ZZRESA. Once this values are pulled we can update it in the formula based tracker in After archived (TAANA) respectively under BSEG(Finance maintenance, Finance Other), VBRK (Sales order invoices), ZZRESA (Special ledger). Lastly, After updating the values in after archived (TAANA), we get the value under Archived. The values which we get under Archived can be copied in Final Summary sheet under tabs, BSEG -Finance Maintenance, Finance others, ZZRESA- Special ledger, VBRK – Sales order Invoice respectively. After checking the total count of jobs finished or cancelled, prepare the PPT files as discussed in the call check the recording link below. https://kone-my.sharepoint.com/:v:/p/prasad_jekte/ERmQT8Mx28pGppbBMUoZdvAB55715tpJ9BrHbj5_bnEX3g https://kone-my.sharepoint.com/:v:/p/vinodkumar_chakali/EUAfnAtxbl9HsQxQ1zrujBYBGDHUDRRQzYg34uOyIq9VwQ Get the access for the SharePoint links: https://kone.sharepoint.com/:x:/r/teams/Common%20Platforms/_layouts/15/doc2.aspx?sourcedoc=%7B4DC59014-B1B1-4CEF-A5AB-E94E661DEA00%7D&file=Summary.xlsx&action=default&mobileredirect=true&cid=d14279fd-85a4-46b1-b7be-5c9041995288 https://kone.sharepoint.com/sites/SAPPlatform/Shared%20Documents/Forms/AllItems.aspx?e=5%3Abd8383e5bc84408c9a85884e9da21cd2&at=9&FolderCTID=0x012000F438E43A1FCF244AA52FD70E67750C89&viewid=d4e44223%2D4feb%2D4ea7%2D8d88%2D8503802f9366&id=%2Fsites%2FSAPPlatform%2FShared%20Documents%2FGeneral%2FService%20Management%20Documentation%2FAdministration%20and%20Reporting%2F2021%2FGlobal%2FArchiving%20Reports

short_description: AskIT - Instructions to be followed before raising access request in GRC Form
introduction: What are the Instructions to be followed before raising access request in GRC Form by Ask IT Team?
instructions: Objective: This Knowledge article explains instructions to be followed / list of validations to be performed before raising access requests for SAP ERP (Z0*) systems in GRC access request form based on Clone ID / Transaction Code / Role. Note: If there are any requests related to user group changes ( i.e. like adding or removing T-codes from user group ) or If the ASKIT team see the keyword as “User Group” in the request details related to SAP systems then ASKIT team can route such tickets to Infosys team without asking for filled in template from user or requestor . This KB article also explains the instructions to check if user's ID exist in SAP ERP (Z0*) system before raising the access request. 1> How to check if user and Clone User already assigned with User Group Job Role(s) in SAP ERP (Z0*) systems? When user requests for access to SAP ERP (Z0*) systems related access, please check if user (existing user ID in SAP ERP) already assigned with User Group job roles in requested SAP ERP systems. If the user is already assigned with User Group job role(s) then please DO NOT raise access request using GRC Form. In such cases if user already assigned with User Group job role(s), please route the request to Infosys SAP support (SAP Security) team with comments: User already assigned with User Group job Role(s) access in SAP ERP (Z0*) system(s), please check and add required additional access into existing User Group job role(s) assigned to user and communicate to user accordingly. In case Clone User is assigned with User Group job role(s) then ask user / requestor to raise the request using User Group based access request form available in KONE Way portal (OR) if user has any challenges to raise request using User Group based access request form then route such requests to Infosys Support (SAP Security) team to help user to submit User Group Job role based access request and communicate to user accordingly. Note: User Group job role's naming convention starts with YECCC: Steps to check if user / Clone User is already assigned with User Group job role(s): Step 1> Log in to respective SAP ERP system (Z04 / Z03 / Z02 / Z01) where user requested for access. Step 2> Execute transaction code: SU01 and enter User's ID in User field and click on Display (Spects) symbol to check assigned User Group job roles of user. (For Clone ID based, Transaction Code based, role based access requests) Enter Clone User's ID to check if Clone user assigned with User Group role(s) (For Clone ID based access requests, check if both user and Clone User already assigned with User Group job role(s) ). Step 3> Go to Roles tab Step 4> Check if user assigned with role(s) naming convention starts with YECCC: as shown in below sample screen 2> How to check if user's ID existing in SAP ERP (Z0*) system before raising the access request. Note: This instruction need to followed for China user's access requests for SAP ERP systems. Step 1> Log in to respective SAP ERP system (Z04 / Z03 / Z02 / Z01) where user requested for access. Step 2> Execute transaction code: SU01 and enter User's ID in User field and click on Display (Spects) symbol to check if User's ID exists in respective SAP ERP (Z0*) system. If system opens respective user's profile / details means respective user's ID exist in respective SAP ERP system. When user's ID does not present in respective ERP system then system will show the message like "User does not exist " at the left-bottom side of the screen.

short_description: How to download the File from AL11 and Upload it in SAP AL11 directories by creating a Variant.
introduction: How to download & upload the file in SAP directories AL11?
instructions: Background Issue: Payment discrepancies in the files sent by Billtrust to SAP for credit card payments. Since the issue was only in Z04, so to replicate the scenario in lower environment business uploaded the file in Z03 AL11 directory and we downloaded that File and Uploaded it again in AL11 directory by creating a Variant for testing purpose. Steps to download file from AL11: Open T-code AL11. Open the file you want to download by following the path. For Example Open the line item where file is located Select the line item file which you want to upload and click on the icon Download file as text. File will be downloaded into the system. Steps to upload the file in AL11 directories by creating a variant: Go to T code CG3Z à Enter the source File (Where the file is currently located) and Target file on application server (Where you want to upload the file) The source file and target file are followed by file name as mentioned in the screenshot. Click on Upload à File will be successfully uploaded to the target location. Go to T code se38 à Give program name à Select Sub objects as Variants à Change Give Variant Name à Create Give Source location (here source location means Target location where you have uploaded the file previously) and Archive location means where the file should go for archival after work completion. As per point no. 6 our source location will be: /usr/sap/interfaces/Z03/OC/incoming/in/sap/test/matching/export_201_20211102141301.csv.asc For archival location you can mention any location where you want file to go for archiving after the work is completed. For Example: After this click on Enter Go one step back save values Save the Variant.

short_description: Document entries for archiving per year
introduction: How to get number of entries of document for specific year in transaction code TAANA
instructions: Table Entries in TAANA for Archiving 1) Open Transaction Code TAANA 2) Search for table (for Purchase Requisition – EBAN) 4) Select table entry and execute 5) Select and create variant 6) Select data fields such as all data will get fetched for KCC 7) Select Data Selection and enter all plants of Company Code KCC and Year 2020 (01.01.2020 to 31.12.2020) 8) Continue select immediate => check => Save 9) Check the scheduled job in Transaction code SM37 10) After finishing the job refresh Transaction code TAANA 11) Select the column No. Entr. And do summation. In data selection if you do not find field then select new field selection. 12) Open Transaction Code SARI to check archived data in 2020 13) Enter archiving object and select Archive Infostructure 14) Enter details as entered in transaction code TAANA plants and year and select Number of entries Add this number + summation result from TAANA EBAN => 0 + 5.457.415 = 5.457.415 => Purchase Requisition MSEG => 0 + 33.917.490 = 33.917.490 => Material Document REBEL => 0 + 243.742 = 243.742 => Invoice document EKKO => 0 + 1.995.358 = 1.995.358 => Purchase Order AUFK => 0 + 567.107 = 567.107 => Production order

short_description: How to check common issues faced during Production support for KONE Invoicing solution.
introduction: How to tackle common issues faced in KIS application production support and how to resolve them.
instructions: ZVEID Report - If you want to view status of IDOC's for any particular country code, please fetch ZVEID report from SAP. You can view the invoice and IDOC details in ZVEID reports. E-Invoice Reconcillation Report or the ZVEID report is created to give a complete visibility of the Invoice the steps and timing in the process from the time invoice is created and sent to vendor to processing of it by them. It gives us the information about the IDOC Number and its statuses. Transaction code : ZVEID ZVEID Reconciliation activities in short: Billing admin runs the report ZVEID on daily basis or at least on weekly basis Billing admin reviews and analyzes the outcome, and assigns actions to the responsible teams/owners. Examples below: If customer email address is missing, Master data team is informed to add/update the email address IT ticket is raised and Infosys supporting only on technical matters, e.g. segment missing, table body error, validation exception, cannot convert string Finally, Billing admin ensures that the non-delivered invoices are sent to customers - manually, if so required, with email or mail. IDOC Status and Actions from MC Finance team: If the status of the invoice is 39. Action from Infosys Finance team : P lease ask user to retrigger the output of the invoice. Once the output is retriggered, the invoice will get processed. If not, then the invoice will throw and error with status 40. If the status of the invoice is 40 with error. Action from Infosys Finance team: P lease provide error to the O2C team in below template: Invoice number ? IDOC number ? Country code ? Sales org ? Creation date of invoice ? Status of invoice with error ? Screenshot of invoice details if possible ? Once all the details are received from user, please raise a ticket with Anachron team . If the status of invoice is 41, it means invoice is successfully processed and sent to customer. Action from Infosys Finance team: To make sure, please login to Anachron Invoicing portal and search the invoice in invoice field. The search result will provide you the invoice with PDF. If the invoice is in 41 status but Discarded state. Action from Infosys Finance team : please raise a ticket with Anachron team. Provide them all the details regarding the invoice and ask them to check if the invoice was loaded and processed in previous batch. If not, then please request Anachron team to rollback the current batch and reprocess the invoices again. If yes, then please check if the invoice is present in O2C portal. If present, then ask O2C team to not to process it again. If the invoice is in 41 status but the pdf is not available in SAP Action from Infosys Finance team : Download the pdf from O2C portal and get the SAP PO team to attach the pdf in SAP. Important IDOC status and Actions from Business: IDOC Status 30: IDOC is generated but is ready to be sent to PI (middleware) Action : No Action Required from Business IDOC Status 03: IDOC sent to PI (middleware) Action : No Action Required from Business IDOC Status 39 : IDOC reached vendor application Action : No Action Required from Business IDOC Status 40: Error in processing the invoice at Vendor end Action : Action Required from Business. Raise a ticket to CPSD with the ZVEID screenshot for the error invoice number. IDOC Status 41 : Invoice PDF generated and sent to SAP/Discarded Action : Action Required from Business. Check if the PDF is available in Invoice or else raise a ticket to CPSD. Process with Anachron Detailed Process flow with Anachron When user create Invoice output – E-invoice triggered in invoice based on condition records maintained for Output. The current status of Output is in progress (not yet processed A batch job runs and process the output. When it is processed IDOC got created And when IDOC processed XML file generated and sent to PI System IDOC also carry any attachments if invoice having. PI will send XML to Anachron Anachron will create PDF invoice with global layout and sent to SAP via PI. The PDF invoice attached back to respective invoice. Anachronn also send PDF invoice to customer based on delivery channel Also there is a portal where customer can access and get invoice PDF. Print partner – Paragon/Chorus/Cegedium depending on the country. SFTP server SFTP server is used for transfer of outbound messages (XML + PDF) to O2C and to receive Inbound files (PDF Layout) from O2C to SAP PI. Make sure to check with Johan on quarterly basis if there are any pending files to be picked up from SFTP. If yes : Action - Schedule a call with SAP PI and O2C team. Request SAP PI team to check the files which are not picked up. If No files are present in Inbound folder, it means the process works just fine. Pagero process : Part 1: Billing Document creation and generation of layout. Adding associate attachments to the document Part 2: Outward flow of data from SAP ECC to middleware SAP BTP A new segment Z1ATTA02 is created in IDOC to send the filename and content repository of the print output attachment. This segment is marked as mandatory for Sales Org. maintained in ZCA_CONSTANTS table. IDOC is not created (output goes in error) if this new segment is missing in IDOC. Action from Infosys team : Inform user to fill in the mandatory Field. Part 3: Outward flow of data from SAP BTP to vendor Pagero When the EDI output is processed, it will trigger the IDoc to BTP system using an RFC connector. CPI will pick up the files (XML+PDF Attachments) from BTP and transfer it to SFTP. Action : Invoice should fail at BTP end if out of both files (xml+pdf) one is not present in BTP. Invoice should throw an error and should not be send to Pagero. This check works at SAP BTP end. If this check does not work due to any reason, please raise a ticket to SAP BTP team. Part 4: Outward flow of data from vendor Pagero to customer Pagero SFTP server unzips the files, and these files are sent to the customer electronically. We have following rule for determining the delivery channel. Pagero deliver the billing document via Email. E-Mail ID should be maintained in the Bill-to-party Master General Data. Pagero also upload the invoice data on the Pagero Online Portal. Invoice Monitoring Process: Invoices for all country codes are being monitored in SAP which are in status 39,40 and 41 with no attachment lists. These invoices are being monitored by Infosys OCC Monitoring team . Weekly email is being sent to MC_Finance_Support shared mailbox with the list of all invoices in error. A P3 ticket is then created by Monitoring team to AMSD team, AMSD team check the invoices with the respective teams. Once the invoices are processed successfully, ticket is marked resolved. Mass Attachment : This process can be used if any country want to send some common communication letter to all the customer for specific country along with the invoices being sent to customers. Business will raise SR for PDF upload for specific sales org with details of validity period, delivery channel for which PDF is valid for etc. SDO to approve SR for the AMS to take action AMS team will upload the PDF with details in SAP by using T-code created in this change. When AMS upload PDF, there will be archive link created for PDF when it get archived in repository (Image master) At the same back end Z- table is also updated with details of PDF and document id generated while archiving. When invoice generated for that particular sales org, then IDOC will get the document ID populated in IDOC attachment segment. When IDOC sent to PI system, Proxy interface will trigger to send PDF to Vendor FTP folder via PI system. Finally PDF will be sent to customer as per delivery channel selected along with invoice PDF.

short_description: Ask IT - Basic Issues in Apptio
introduction: Basic issues in Apptio
instructions: Browser Refer KB000A2775 Access 1. New user: knowledge article KB000A1712 in ServiceNOW 2. Change or delete user: knowledge article KB000A2702 in ServiceNOW a. User not able to access e.g. after a name and/or email address change -> knowledge article KB000A2702 in ServiceNOW Related articles in Apptio Help Center: User and role management . In case of ticket that user is not able to access Apptio, first check that the user exists in Apptio user list. If not, the access should be requested in MyAccess, and be processed like defined in KB000A1712 . For SSO, check the SSO related topic KB000A2777 . System not responding 1. Check is the problem only in certain environment (Dev, Staging or Prod), or all of them. 2. Check if some other users are able to access the same environment(s). Preferably someone with similar access than the user who has the problem, and one with different access (e.g. admin). 3. If other users are able to access normally, it could be a local caching issue. Ask user to delete the browsing history and re-open the browser to try again. 4. If step 3 doesn’t help, check if the user has had new access roles added recently and is trying to use that new role for the first time. a. If yes, try to impersonate the user, and change the role to the one he/she is trying to use. Check again if the user is able to access after that. 5. If the above steps don’t help, create a ticket in Apptio Support. a. It is possible that allocated resource limits are reached and system is therefore not responsive.

short_description: Old PR approval trigger during Network mass cleansing
introduction: How to stop old PR approval mail trigger during Network mass cleansing?
instructions: Issue - In Z04, when NW status is changed from TECO to REL, the PR release strategy is getting activated & mails are getting triggered to the recipient ( CXO level) . As the PR are very old, it is creating confusion. Analysis – This behavior is due to automatic removal of deletion flag from PR line items, due to change in the NW status (TECO --> REL). The release strategy is getting triggered from back end automatically and hence the mail is getting triggered to the person concern. Proposed Solution: When the NW volume is less for mass cleansing – Clean the individual NW manually in CJ20N. NW status should be changed from TECO --> REL, then control key should be changed & finally the status should be changed back to TECO, before saving the NW. When the NW volume is high for mass cleansing - Temporary deactivation of release strategy during non-working hours of business. Following steps to be performed. Steps : · Extract PR release strategies from AUSP table with input in the fields ATINN = C_KC_PRQ_PLANT, ATWRT = Specify the required Plant (Ex. SFA) · List all release strategy and release group combinations from OBJEK field Object (OBJEK) = Release group + Release strategy Ex. Object – 1E1A means Release group- 1E and Release Strategy- 1A · Remove existing C_KC_PRQ_PLANT characteristic value from CL20N to all the release strategy and release group combinations C_KC_PRQ_PLANT = blank This stops activation of old PR’s with release strategy and trigger mails to users · Perform Network mass cleansing activities by scheduling background jobs TECO à REL and finally to CLSD · After completion of NW mass cleansing, add the characteristic value C_KC_PRQ_PLANT to all the release group and release strategy combinations using CL20N

short_description: Ask IT - SharePoint Download issue solution steps for registered devices
introduction: Your organization doesn't allow you to download, print, or sync using this device | Download issue on SharePoint site fixed
instructions: Your organization doesn't allow you to download, print, or sync using this device | Download issue on SharePoint site fixed Issue: User is not able to download files from the SharePoint site and gets error below on the top of SharePoint site. Your organization doesn't allow you to download, print, or sync using this device. To use these actions, use a device that's joined to a domain or marked compliant by Intune. For help, contact your IT department. The below methods can be performed to resolve the issue . considering the user device is connected to Kone Network. 1. Verify if the user has required permission (Read/Edit/Full access,etc) in the SharePoint Site 2. Make sure the user device is registered on Azure Active Directory. Steps to check:- Browse AAD with the below link through Admin Account. https://portal.azure.com/#blade/Microsoft_AAD_Devices/DevicesMenuBlade/Devices/menuId/ or Devices - Microsoft Azure Search for the device and verify if it’s registered. The device should be Enabled: Yes Note: In case the device is not registered on Azure Active Directory then route the case back to AskIT to get the device registered on AAD. 3. Verify whether the User’s device is connected to KONE ACCOUNT as mentioned in the pictures below . If not , make it connected by clicking on Connect button and reboot the System. Reverify the same. · Navigate to check: Window Settings -> Accounts -> Access work or school-> Connect Kone account · Also, navigate to check: Window Settings -> Accounts -> Email & accounts -> Add an account - add Kone email ID Note: If you are facing some issues while trying to connect the device with Kone Account route the ticket to concerned team through AskIT. 4. If steps 1, Step 2, & Step 3 are fulfilled then clear the browser History of the Browser and Close it. Verify the same by reopening the site on the browser. Also, try with Microsoft Edge browser on both modes (Normal and Incognito).

instructions: Open Planview( https://kone.pvcloud.com/ ) --> Go to Work tab --> Search the Work ID --> Click on enter. Go to KONE - Roll out Gate Approvals(ADMIN) --> Click on Edit. short_description: Ask IT - Known Sync issue in One Drive
introduction: How to solve the "There is a sync issue in One drive"
instructions: If user gets the below error. This issue can be addressed by end-user without administrator support by a single click on the " Resolve the conflict files " option. This is a Conflict issue occurring due to sync issues. Let’s take example if there are 2 employees with the same Teams Channel (or) SPO (User A and User B) working on the same Word document User 1 is making changes offline (while User 1 is Away with no Wi-Fi connection) User 2 is working on the same document and making changes online while being connected to Wi-Fi ( The original document consists of lots of text (paragraphs) When User 1 makes changes locally on the computer (while offline ) parallelly (user 2) Making the Changes Online which will be synced SharePoint immediately (because User 2 is online ) When User 1 becomes available online, the changes that he\she made earlier are now synchronized to SharePoint, which will get the following message: One Drive needs your attention. There is a sync issue in One Drive. Click to learn more about this issue and how to resolve it

short_description: Instructions on opening a document protected by someone who left KONE
introduction: Process for opening a document protected by someone who left KONE
instructions: Basic principles All the documents and business critical information should be properly shared with manager during the handover process early enough before user’s last working day All the protected documents should have at least two owners (full control access) In case of any illegal or other suspicious activity contact always security@kone.com Ask IT process for opening a document protected by someone who left KONE This process is applied for opening encrypted documents owned and protected by someone who has left KONE and when there is a critical business need to get the document open. The process should not be used if the document has another owner still working for KONE. If the document is labelled as Confidential or Secret, it is very important to verify whether the document (ownership) can be shared as requested. Check that the document to be opened is protected (encrypted), cannot be opened and does not have a secondary owner who would be able to help with the issue. Check who the information asset owner is and document it in the ticket. Depending on the information in question, it can be a function lead, product owner, project manager or line manager. This person should get Full Control permissions to the content (to be implemented by the O365 team). Check if the document is in a person’s OneDrive or email account. If yes, follow the below process. If the document is stored in a shared SharePoint, assign the ticket to O365 team (O365 queue – KONE.M&C.support). Follow the standard Data Protection process for O365 access (KBA3610), to get the needed approvals to open the document (note: the access need to be requested for the information asset owner) Check that the required documents are in place: Account holder’s written consent by consent form/email/SMS and fully completed Legal Questionnaire (attached file: O365 Data Access Request - Initial Legal Questionnaire) If the attachments are not received, Ask IT agent asks for proof of written consent from the account holder and sends the attached Legal Questionnaire to the requester and tells the requester to return both documents to Ask IT Ticket to be in the respective Ask IT queue till the requested documents are returned to Ask IT If written consent from the account holder is received, Ask IT may grant access to the requester. If not, Ask IT agent sends the completed Legal Questionnaire for approval to Data Protection team (instructions in the Legal Questionnaire) Ticket to be in the respective Ask IT queue till the approval from the Data Protection team is received If the request is denied by the Data Protection team, Ask IT needs to communicate the same to the requester In case of exceptional urgent need due to a critical business, compliance, security, legal hold, or legal reason it is possible to request a supervised opening. In that case, Ask IT agent asks the requester to fill in the Legal Questionnaire and when received, sends it to the Data Protection team After having the needed data protection approvals in place (valid only if the document is is in a person’s OneDrive or email account), assign the ticket to O365 team (O365 queue – KONE.M&C.support) with the approved file or the written consent from the account holder. O365 team process for opening a document protected by someone who left KONE If the document is in a person’s OneDrive or email account, check that the Data Protection team or the account holder has approved the access as per the approval process above before proceeding with the request (not needed if the document is in a shared SharePoint). Request a business approval which is compulsory to open the document. By default, the approval is requested from the information asset owner (should be documented in the ticket). Depending on the information in question, it can be a function lead, product owner, project manager or line manager. The asset owner should get Full Control permissions to the content and can after that share the document further per need and content confidentiality. Document the business approval into the ticket. Secret documents can sometimes contain insider information. Therefore, if the document is labeled as Secret, the information asset owner and/or manager of the account holder should be asked whether there is a possibility that the document contains insider information. If this is the case, the information asset owner should not be granted with permission to the content and Insreg@kone.com will have to give their approval. Document this into the ticket. Once the business approval and possible insider information check is completed, contact sridhar.b@kone.com and s enthoor i.sivakumar@kone.com to assign the information asset owner Full Control permissions (ownership) to the document. Inform the requester and close the ticket upon proper confirmation.

short_description: SAP IMT Issue
introduction: How to handle 'TEDAT field missing' error in IMT tool
instructions: KBA: Error – “TEDAT Field Missing” Analysis: Every FL network in SAP and IMT is assigned to a specific supervisor. These networks have different fields like AUART(Order type), ERDAT(Created on), AEDAT(Changed on) etc. Similarly, if TEDAT field is missing in the network we will get “TEDAT Field Missing” in IMT tool while rescheduling or confirming MS 3C. We can check in which network TEDAT field is missing by specifying supervisor personal SAP no. in Function module - ZBAPI_IMT_SITEMANAGEMENT_MILST. Goto Tcode – SE37 and Enter FM name ZBAPI_IMT_SITEMANAGEMENT_MILST and click on Test/Execute Step 2 : Enter Supervisor no. in the IM_VERNR field and click on execute Step 3: After executing you will get results as shown in the below image. Double click on view details For those networks where TEDAT fields are missing, We need to remove them from below IDA table : 1.ZPS_IDA_HEADER 2.ZPS_IDA_ITEM 3.ZPS_IDA_ITEM_MS 4.ZPS_IDA_MS We can’t directly remove entries from these tables. We need active FFID to access t-code SE16 and /h (To switch on debugging mode) . Step 4: After getting FFID, Goto Tcode – SE16 and Enter the first table name i.e., ZPS_IDA_HEADER and Enter. Step 5: Enter networks in the order field where the TEDAT field is missing. Step 6 : After executing you will get a list of network details where TEDAT field missing. Click on the check box and double click on the network number. You will get the following results: This will Switch on Debugging mode Step 7: You will now enter ABAP editor.Goto to Desktop 3 tab and Variable tab as shown in below image Step 8: Click on Edit and type ‘DELE’ here. Then type fn+F8 keys on the keyboard Step 9: After clicking on fn+F8 keys, you will get below the option of ‘Delete Entry’ Entries will be deleted for this network. Similarly, do it for other entries also After Deleting all the entries check again for all the networks, you will get the following message.

short_description: Ask IT - How to provide access to users in Mileage Analysis Tool
introduction: How to provide access to users in Mileage Analysis Tool ?
instructions: Solution: Steps to be followed are as below: 1. Check if the end user details like display name, Kone id and username are provided in the incoming ticket along with the information about the desired company code or branch for the access. Also, check if the access is requested in which environment i.e. production or acceptance or both, if nothing is given it is production. If needed ask the user for the necessary details. 2. Open New Hierarchy Application. (Hierarchy Admin Tool) 3. After connecting to the desired environment, select the application as Mileage Analysis Tool from the dropdown as shown in the screenshot. 4. Now, check for the branch code for which access is requested in the ticket received. Find (Ctrl+F) and search branch code then click on the respective code and then further click on ‘Select’ button. The highlighted part in the image is the branch for which the access needs to be provided. Whereas the rest in the image are work centers and must not provide access by selecting the work centers. 5. Click on Add Users button located at the upper right side of the window seen here. 6. Click on Import/Browse: While Clicking on Import: i. Enter the Last Name of the user and Click Find if you click on Import. Import will get values from active directory. If you choose to use the Import button and the name has too many entries, the application will crash and recommend to use a different way of adding user. Select the user from the results and click OK. While Clicking on Browse: ii. Enter the Name of the user and Click Find Next if you click on Browse. Browse will get values from the database. Select the user from the results and click OK. 7. Select the specified Access level mentioned by the user, if there is no access mentioned then provide read and update access to the user Delete and Regional Admin access must be provided when it mentioned by the user. 8.If users need access to all the branches select root. And if user wants all of CKE or CKQ or MKO then select Company code and provide access which will provide access to all the branches inside that company. 9. Click File -> Refresh then you’ll be able to see the added end user’s id in the respective group or branch. 10. Finally, inform the ticket owner from teams/ personal email id that the access is provided and ask for confirmation before marking the ticket as resolved. 11. To Remove access of user click on check box of previously provided access / user specified access. 12 . When the tick mark turns into a red check mark means the authorization is not only removed but revoked at that level and any level subordinate to it. Removing the marks completely to an empty box removes that authorization only. Removing all marks in the row of a user will completely remove the user from that authorized area when the window (database) is refreshed. 13. Finally, inform the ticket owner from teams/ personal mail id that the access is removed and ask for confirmation before marking the ticket as resolved. Try these steps to install the KONECT Mileage Analysis Tool: 1. Assure you are connected to the KONE network (Pulse Secure “Global VPN ” if you are out of the office). When selecting the links (underlined text) below, use the Ctrl key and Mouse Click at the same time. This software installation requires that you are an administrator on your computer. You can grant yourself administrator access to your computer for 24hrs by following the instructions in the site: Temporary Administrative Rights (sharepoint.com) 2. copying the link below and paste it into your Internet Explorer browser URL bar Link: \\uskvmmssos005\ClickOnce\mileageanalysistool\Mileage Analysis Tool.application 3. Once you paste this URL in browser and press Enter button, you will get below message, once you received it then click on “Open” 4. Once you click on Open, you will receive below message window, in which you will get “Install” option, you need to click on it, and it will install the Mileage Analysis Tool in your system.

short_description: Steps to restart server and bring dataset online in IRMA Production in case of system maintenance mode
introduction: Steps to restart server and bring dataset online in IRMA Production (In case of Application is in system maintenance mode due to Dataset & Database not in sync)Need to check log
instructions:
1. Check QDBODBC log if there any error like the following: [Microsoft][ODBC Driver 17 for SQL Server]TCP Provider: An existing connection was forcibly closed by the remote host.

State = 08S01, ErrorCode = 10054, Message = [Microsoft][ODBC Driver 17 for SQL Server]Communication link failure 2. Check if QServer log if there is any error like the following: "QUINTIQ_DB: ErrorCode 1000000089: . . . . . . . . State = 08S01, ErrorCode = 10054, Message = [Microsoft][ODBC Driver 17 for SQL Server]TCP Provider: An existing connection was forcibly closed by the remote host.

State = 08S01, ErrorCode = 10054, Message = [Microsoft][ODBC Driver 17 for SQL Server]Communication link failure
","",1416063,[713756.0.42806679] 3. Check if the QServer logs contains the error which says Server state Running ==> SystemMaintenance, reason: The commitment status of the last database transaction is unknown. 4. If confirmed that the application is in system maintenance mode due to Database and Dataset not in sync. Proceed to Restart. 5. Stop the services as per the following sequence: Server Quintiq component Service name EUDVMAPPRABR102 Thin client engine Quintiq_TCE EUDVMAPPRABR103 Thin client engine Quintiq_TCE EUDVMAPPRABR104 Quintiq Remote Job Server Quintiq_RJS EUDVMAPPRABR101 Quintiq Integrator Quintiq_QIntegrator EUDVMAPPRABR101 Quintiq Server Quintiq_QServer EUDVMAPPRABR101 Quintiq DBODBC Archive Quintiq_QDBODBC_Archive EUDVMAPPRABR101 Quintiq DBODBC 1Plan Integration Quintiq_QDBODBC_1PlanIntegration EUDVMAPPRABR101 Quintiq DBODBC Quintiq_QDBODBC 6. Take backup of dataset files from Quintiq\Dataset folder. Copy the DSS folder "E:\Quintiq\Datasets\KoneIrmaInternal" and Paste it inside "E:\Quintiq\Datasets\Backup DSS". Rename the folder "E:\Quintiq\Datasets\Backup DSS\KoneIrmaInternal" to KoneIrmaInternal_YYYYMMDD. (Where YYYYMMDD is the date) 7. Start following Quintiq services Server Quintiq component Service name EUDVMAPPRABR101 Quintiq DBODBC Quintiq_QDBODBC EUDVMAPPRABR101 Quintiq DBODBC 1Plan Integration Quintiq_QDBODBC_1PlanIntegration EUDVMAPPRABR101 Quintiq DBODBC Archive Quintiq_QDBODBC_Archive EUDVMAPPRABR101 Quintiq Server Quintiq_QServer 8. Start Quintiq Thin Client Engine for Editor and Open Quintiq Thin Client Editor.(Make sure to use Quintiq version 6.1.3) 9. In the Thin Client Editor > Open the Datasets form. a. Select the Company from Define Datasets Form. b. Select root in the Dataset folders list c. Select the Company Dataset d. Click on the UP arrow. A dialog will open. e. Select “ Memory only ” from the dropdown list and click OK f. Once the Dataset is visible in the online dataset list. g. Select the online dataset Right click > Change storage state h. Select “ Hybrid” from the drop down and click OK. 10. Repeat the STEP 8 for all other Working Unit datasets. Follow this priority list to bring the datasets online. (Note: Search for Working Unit numbers in the Dataset folders list. Many working units will be in ‘LostAndFound’ folder) After bringing the frontline working units marked in Yellow in the Excel the following TCE and RJS services can be started ( Step 10 ). 11. Start the following Services: Server Quintiq component Service name EUDVMAPPRABR104 Quintiq Remote Job Server Quintiq_RJS EUDVMAPPRABR105 Quintiq Remote Job Server Quintiq_RJS EUDVMAPPRABR102 Quintiq Thin Client Engine Quintiq_TCE EUDVMAPPRABR103 Quintiq Thin Client Engine Quintiq_TCE After this step IRMA / IBR will be accessible for the datasets which are online. 12. Once all datasets are online as per the list start the QIntegrator Server Quintiq component Service name EUDVMAPPRABR101 Quintiq Integrator Quintiq_QIntegrator Risks: a) Starting of Quintiq Editor is a risk, as the business logic is defined using this tool and if any code or logic is accidentally changed, then identifying and reverting it will be time consuming and might lead to data loss or corruption. b) Loading datasets online have other risks such as loss of DB connectivity while loading. This may cause the data to get corrupted and restoring/reverting this potential corruption will need L3 support.

short_description: Ask IT - How to troubleshoot vulnerable applications in KONE
introduction: Introduction: As we are already aware that, the Log4j vulnerabilities has already represented a complex and high-risk situation for companies across the globe. Microsoft recommends customers to do additional review of devices where vulnerable installations are discovered. As a part of threat and vulnerability management, we aim to review our managed and unmanaged softwares in KONE, so they can be onboarded and kept secured. Starting April 2022, we would be aggressively tackling the vulnerabilities of KONE happening due to the outdated versions of multiple softwares. At present, we would be starting with a few applications and slowly this would be accumulating as per the risk. Most of the identified applications are not managed centrally and hence user either needs to update or uninstall the application to mitigate the vulnerability. These softwares are not managed centrally, so the responsibility to update the application is with the user who installed it and want to use in future. For managed applications, updated application will be available via SCCM.
instructions: Ask IT Scope: It has been observed that in some cases user might need admin rights to uninstall or upgrade an application. In this scenario, Ask IT would be the first point of contact. As agreed, Ask IT would not advertise ‘MakeMeAdmin’ tool to users and Ask IT support person would uninstall/upgrade the application using their own credentials over remote session. User notification to be sent to affected users and they would be requested to either upgrade the application to latest version or uninstall the old vulnerable version from their devices. A grace period of two weeks will be given to the users starting from the day, the communication will be sent to the targeted end users. If they fail to update the application within two weeks, then uninstallation will happen automatically from SCCM. For Ask IT reference: Vendor URLs to update the applications and do uninstallation are given below. In the absence of Vendor URLs, Ask IT needs to uninstall the application Manually from control panel. Below list to be kept updated by EUP team as and when required. SET 1 (April 2022): 1. Apple iTunes a. Download Latest Version: https://support.apple.com/en-us/HT210384 b. Uninstallation Guide: https://support.apple.com/en-us/HT204275 2. Apple iCloud a. Download Latest Version: https://support.apple.com/en-us/HT204283 Clicked the link marked in yellow. This installer also works with Win10: b. Uninstallation Guide: https://support.apple.com/guide/icloud-windows/sign-out-or-uninstall-icw4adfaaff0/icloud 3. Apple Software Update: a. Apple software update gets automatically installed after installing either iTune or iCloud. b. Uninstalling iCloud or iTunes from the device will uninstall Apple Software update also. 4. VLC Media Player a. Download Latest Version: https://www.videolan.org/vlc/download-windows.html b. Uninstallation Guide: https://wiki.videolan.org/Documentation:Uninstalling_VLC/ 5. Zoom a. Download Latest Version: https://zoom.us/download b. Uninstallation Tool: https://support.zoom.us/hc/en-us/article_attachments/360084068792/CleanZoom.zip 6 . Wireshark a. Download Latest Version: https://www.wireshark.org/download.html b. Installation & Uninstallation Guide: https://www.wireshark.org/docs/wsug_html_chunked/ChBuildInstallWinInstall.html 7. WinRar a. Download Latest Version: https://www.win-rar.com/download.html?&L=0 b. Uninstallation: Uninstall WinRar from Control Panel > Programs and Features 8. Putty a. Download Latest Version: https://www.chiark.greenend.org.uk/~sgtatham/putty/latest.html b. Uninstallation: Uninstall Putty from Control Panel > Programs and Features 9. IrfanView a. Download Latest Version: https://www.irfanview.com/main_download_engl.htm b. Uninstallation: Uninstall IrfanView from Control Panel > Programs and Features

short_description: Managing Windchill User Groups via Active Directory and MyAccess Roles
introduction: Managing Windchill User Groups via Active Directory and MyAccess Roles
instructions: This article describes the actions to enable the new Windchill User Group via Active Directory and MyAccess role. Creating the user group in Windchill itself is described in KB000A1549 . Windchill User Groups are preferably managed via MyAccess roles. In order to implement this, follow the below-listed actions: Request for a new MyAccess role from MyAccess team Raise the request via AskIT Portal - Search for " MyAccess IDM Roles " Role name: "Windchill: User Group: <user group name>", where <user group name> is the name of the group in Windchill, e.g. PLT_CRD_ECOMOD_DESIGNER_UG Role description: "This role grants membership to Windchill user group <user group name without underscores "_">", where <user group name without underscores "_"> is the name of the Windchill group with empty spaces in place of the underscores, e.g. PLT CRD ECOMOD DESIGNER UG (this will help to better find the roles in MyAccess) Role Owners: Service Manager + Windchill Group Owners Entitlement / AD group: WNC_UG_<user group name>, e.g. WNC_UG_PLT_CRD_ECOMOD_DESIGNER_UG AD group needs to be created?: Yes Group of Approvers: Windchill User Group Approvers Role Completion Notification text: "You have now been added to the Windchill User Group. Please note that it might take up to 1 hour before the change is synced to Windchill." Once the MyAccess role and AD group have been created, add the new AD group as member of the corresponding Windchill User Group For managing permissions of the user group to specific context, please follow KB000A1753 .

short_description: Planview - How to manage user accounts
introduction: How to manage user accounts in Planview.
instructions: Check License availability At first check that there is a license available for a new user: In case e.g. full licenses (user type: Portfolio manager) are not available to the requested Planview user role some inactive user needs to be downgraded as viewer (user type: Requestor). Ask Anja Airasvirta or Satu Hirvonen to provide usernames to be downgraded. Downgrade inactive users as Viewer in case lack of licenses In case Satu or Anja has requested you to downgrade users as viewers do the following. Users with full license (Portfolio manager, program manager, KW FL manager or Work manager) are in scope of the downgrade as viewer. Select the not active user based on either one who has never logged in and the account has been granted long time ago, e.g., over 11 months one who has logged in a very long time ago, e.g., over 11 months. Change the Planview Role for the selected user as Viewer, do not remove Lifecycle roles Update the current role to “Pager” field as: YYmm Ex <Role> . Example: “2209 Ex Portfolio Mgr.” Open the user for review and update: Note: MyAccess role will remain as it was and in case user needs to be reverted back to Full license user then user either contacts via AskIT ticket or requests a new role via MyAccess. Then the information in “Pager” field shall be cleared out. Planview user account creation MyAccess request has been approved and the AskIT ticket is created. a) Create account Administration/Add a user AskIT ticket field Example content Planview new user field Access Name Planview XYZ group - Portfolio manager Role based on the role in group name. User Name Jubair , Mohamed Full Name Type Employee KONE Role KTO Engineer WorkerID 64119750 User ID, Insight Analytics User ID User SamaccountName k64119750 User Email mohamed.jubair@kone.com Email User Username mohamed.jubair@kone.com Network Authentication: User Additional Information Planvew MyAccess group consists of two parts: Planview portfolio or area + Planview user Role . E.g. Planview- Service Equipment Business (SEB) Portfolio - Program Manager Lifecycle Roles: For Roles: Portfolio manager, Program manager, KW FL manager, Work manager select “Manager” . Viewer roles do not have Lifecycle role set. When account is created Key user who has approved the account creation can grant the needed grants for the user. b) User ID already exists error message User account is already A) created of it has been B) deactivated and needs to be activated again. Update current user role: User account is already created and it is active. In case new role is requiring higher role that the current one then please update the new role. In case the role is lower then do not change the role. Check if the user role needs to be upgraded. 1. Has the lowest permissions and 7. the highest. IT Viewer Viewer Work Manager Program Manager FL KW Manager Portfolio Manager Administrator c) Activate deactivated user In Administration/Users and Roles/Configure Users Toggle the Show Deactivated Users to view all users and then use the search You can now activate the user and fill in the new information according to the request. Inform requestee, requestor and related Key user about the account created/updated When the account is created or updated inform the requestee, requestor and key user of the role. You can find the Planview role specific key user from the key user page . Key user will then get the notification about the account creation, and they can then update needed grants for the user if needed.
