short_description: Ask IT Process & Workflow to handle Cybersecurity Issues reported from Cybersecurity Operations team/KONE users
introduction: What is the process and steps to be followed by ASKIT to handle Cybersecurity Incident?
instructions: Introduction: This KB article will help ASKIT to handle/assist/support KONE users/KONE business units if Security Incidents are reported to ASKIT. Also, this KBA will help ASKIT to handle and process Security Incidents when assigned by KONE Cyber Defense team ( KONE.KTI.CybersecurityOperations ). What is Security Issue? A security issue is any unmitigated risk or vulnerability in our system that hackers can use to do damage to systems or data. This includes vulnerabilities in the servers and software/applications connecting our business to customers, as well as our business processes and people What is Account compromise? Access to Microsoft O365 mailboxes, data and other services, is controlled through the use of credentials, for example a username and password. When someone other than the intended user steals those credentials, the stolen credentials are considered compromised. With them the attacker can sign in as the original user and perform illicit actions. Using the stolen credentials, the attacker can access the user's Microsoft 365 mailbox, SharePoint folders, or files in the user's OneDrive. One action commonly seen is the attacker sending emails as the original user to recipients both inside and outside of the organization. What is a Malware/Malicious activity? Malwares are infected files found on a host. Some of the source of malware infections could be but not limited to, user has downloaded something from suspicious sites; has clicked on phishing link mistakenly and provided his credentials; user has connected corrupted infected USB to system; network traffic etc. Common Malware Types: · Cryptominer · Wannacrypt · Double Pulsar · Trojan · Adware/Pub · DNS query for malware domain · Keygen · DDoS, Password stealing worm · Dealply · Ramsomware · Malicious files Overview of Cybersecurity Operations: Our SOC provider (Deloitte) is responsible for monitoring Security events and logs for IT, IoT and OT environment in centralized SIEM. They are responsible for performing initial triage and remote response actions wherever possible to remediate the issue. The Incidents for which there is a need for KONE Support/Intervention is required to remediate the issue, those Incidents will be assigned to our operations queue ( KONE.KTI.Cybersecurity Operations ) which is monitored 24/7 by Cyber Defense (L1) Team. Cyber Defense Team reviews the assigned Security Incidents and performs needed triage and contact end users for investigating and may assign the tickets to ASKIT either for contacting the China user to verify the legitimacy or to perform any remote actions or to assign to respective queues for actions expected to remediate the issue. General Instructions for ASKIT to handle Security Issues: 1. Security Incidents will be assigned from KONE.KTI.CybersecurityOperations queue to ASKIT queue with the actions required from ASKIT team. 2. Since Security Incidents should be processed and handled on time without delays, ASKIT should be strictly adhering to the acknowledgment time and resolution time as agreed with KONE. SLA for Security Incidents is 2 days 3. Since the Security Incident priorities will be based on Microsoft tool priority, the security Incidents will be assigned to ASKIT as P3 in most of the cases. 4. These Security P3 cases should be picked up and acted on time efficiently and should not be handled like a normal P3 IT Incident. 5. Wherever there is a need for raising an MI, Cybersecurity Operations team will be contacting ASKIT to increase the priority to P1 or P2. 6. If there are any issues in understanding the recommendations provided by Cybersecurity Operations team, please contact the Cyber Defense via Teams and ask your queries over ticket to get the clarifications. 7. Regular follow ups to get the needed actions completed on time should be made from ASKIT. 8. Please ensure that ASKIT is following all the escalation matrix required for support within 2 days. Any security Incident should not be aging more than 2 days unless until there is a proper reasoning behind the delay (Reimage, user out of office etc.). If any ticket is more than 2 days, then it should be brought to the notice of Cybersecurity Operations team in the Weekly calls that is organized with Cybersecurity Operations team. Process to be followed by ASKIT to handle security Incidents: 1. Check the Next actions provided in the activity feed (Notes section) 2. Go through the recommendations and actions expected from ASKIT and plan the response actions correctly. 3. If there is a need to contact the user for verifying the legitimacy of the Incident. Escalation matrix : User à User’s manager à Region SDM 4. Account compromise/Account related security issue à Contact the user over phone call to verify the legitimacy instead of chat or email since we might not be sure if the user/hacker is responding to us. 5. If the user is not responding/not reachable to call, call the users manager and explain the issue on his colleague system/account and its adverse impact to KONE. Get the needed support from user’s manager for confirming whether it is legitimate or not. · Calling the users/user’s manager and SDM should be done in their region Business hours and not in non-business hours. · If the Incident has been received at non-business hours, then an email should be sent explaining the issue to user’s manager. · On the next business day, an attempt should be taken and followed from Step 4 6. If the assigned security Incident is not account related or something related to malware/suspicious activity, then ASKIT can investigate with the user over email or Teams chat. Eg: Investigating with user related to inserting infected USB, Scheduling engineer visit for Reimage etc. 7. Update the Investigation results in the Incident Activity feed clearly with the actions taken and outcome. 8. If the user confirms it as a legitimate activity, update clearly in the Activity feed which can be Finetuned. If the user is not aware this can be Illegitimate (Turning out to be a real threat or security impact to KONE). 9. All the Incidents should be assigned back to KONE.KTI.CybersecurityOperations queue once the recommended actions are completed to ensure the mitigated actions are successful or not and so these Incidents should not be resolved in ASKIT queue or any other queues. To note: If the next steps contain instructions to assign the ticket to other supporting teams, assign it to their respective queue with actions expected from them and ask them to assign it to KONE.KTI.CybersecurityOperations once their actions are completed. If there is a need for investigation with KONE Stakeholders, then email them with the exact issue and update the findings of the investigation. Note: Do not follow 3 Strike policy and resolve the Incident if there is no response from the user /escalation points, rather assign the Incident to KONE.KTI.CybersecurityOperations. Please ensure if all the needed actions and effort to resolve the Security Incident are taken by ASKIT before assigning it to Cybersecurity Operations queue. Account compromise issues (Reported by KONE End - users): 1. If users contact ASKIT stating their account is compromised, received phishing email/suspicious email/email from unknown sender – Educate the user to report those emails via Hoxhunt ( Report this email ) option in Outlook. 2. If the user informs that there is issue in Hoxhunt Functionality in Outlook, please follow KBA1652. 3. If the user informs there is a security issue on his system, please ensure to check all information required for analysis and investigation are collected from the users and assign the ticket to KONE.KTI.CybersecurityOperations. The Cybersecurity Team will update on next actions to be taken Note: During Security Incidents, as an immediate remediation account level actions like password reset, Account Enable and Disable is performed by Security Operations team via MyAccess. The users manager will an automated Email notification with temporary password which can be communicated to user for reset his/her password. Please note, this works if the account is connected in KONE network. If the user is connected remotely, user will not be able to login back with the temporary password provided by MyAccess. In that case, below steps to be followed. https://passwordreset.microsoftonline.com/ - Password reset has to done by the user through this portal. Once password is reset, VPN needs to be connected. Once VPN connected, user has to lock the screen (Windows Key + L ) and then user need to unlock it with the newly changed password. Since VPN connection is in background, this will accept the newly changed password and that will sync with the end user host. ASKIT can assist the users to reset the password using new self-service password reset portal. The end users can reset their own password if their mobile number is updated in Workday or by using MFA (Multi Factor Authentication) by following the above steps. If Account is locked out, unlock the user account by following the troubleshooting steps. If users suspect that someone else has their password, they can use the portal to change it immediately. Self-Service password reset portal Link: https://passwordreset.microsoftonline.com/ List of Cybersecurity Operations Team members. Global Cybersecurity Operations Team: thomas.lakofski@kone.com niklas.sarokaari@kone.com joona.romppanen@kone.com Cybersecurity Operations team: v.sathishkumar@kone.com mohamedthameemansare.m@kone.com jeyakarlmarx.j@kone.com jawaharlal.c@kone.com Cybersecurity Operations L1 team: abishekp.sam@kone.com gunasekaran.d@kone.com narendran.r@kone.com nithyanantham.t@kone.com jeevaraj.s@kone.com hariraj.l@kone.com

short_description: 1PLAN - NON SAP Data load
introduction: How to upload Orderbook data for Non SAP countries ?
instructions: What is Non Sap Data load ? 1plan gets the source data from BW & Beacon ( SAP ), but still few countries are not added and in SAP . Here we are going to do data load for 2 countries 1, BOSNIA(KBO) 2, CROATIA We will get the excel file in zipped format from business in every WD1 & WD9 (Twice a month) rarely once a month as shown below. Step 1, Download the Zip file from the mail and extract the file . Step 2 , In the zip file we have files for above 4 counties , We need to load two files for each country I ) Project data II ) Financial data Project Data, File name should be always “ Project_Database “ Name should be same as above while uploading in 1plan, In Project Database , we should check only two sheets i) Project Data ii) Equipment Data Please check the below steps to upload the file without any issue i) Remove the data without any title or table ii) Unique values should be true so that we can avoid duplicate values Unique values Formula is =countif( Project number column ,Project number A1)=1 Similar it we should do it for all, here we are comparing project numbers with the list so that we can avoid duplicate , iii) If we find Fales just delete the entire row and continue iv) Check the project number column that we have any symbol or alphabet other than Number , we have to delete that one too v) We have to do the same process for both Project Data & Equipment data Financial Data File name should be “ ProjectDatabase_FinancialData “ In this Excel we will be looking only the Financial Data , just copy the sheet and make it as separate Excel , Sheet name should be same as below , follow the same steps we did for Project Database Make these two excel as a folder for Each country , totally we will be having 4 folder to upload to 1plan. Steps to upload Excel to 1plan , Uploading should be done in non-working hours like late evening of IST time above 8 PM i) Open 1plan prod , click on import Icon ii) Here you will be getting a Pop up for importing data , Select Excel – and choose the path by using browse – here select the folder for first country iii) Then select the OrderBook (Non – SAP Countries) Then upload one by one for each country iv) To see changes in Values you can go to Forms – Analysis - Analysis (DPConfig) Here you can see the no of count getting increased after uploading for each country We have do this process during WD1 & WD 9 , once completed mail to the key users .

short_description: Jira - Procedure to Create a New Jira Space for KONE IT(Tribe/ Portfolio)
introduction: Jira - Procedure to Create a New Jira Space for KONE IT(Tribe/ Portfolio)
instructions: This knowledge article gives instructions on how to create new Jira Space for KONE IT. Requests for new KONE IT Space creation shall only be accepted via Service Catalog form. Jira Space in KONE IT are classified as Tribe Space , Portfolio Space. Any IT Space creation needs approval from Reunanen Johanna ( johanna.reunanen@kone.com ) In this KBA you can find the instructions for creating all 2 types of Jira Project For Jira Scrum/Kanban board creation Kindly follow the steps given in the KBA8190 NOTE: 1) After Approved from Reunanen Johanna , only you should proceed to create the Project To create the Jira Space, follow these instructions: 1. Log onto Jira and ensure that you have administrative permissions 2. Select Spaces - Create Space. Under Software development , select " Scrum " or " Kanban " based on the user's request and continue with " Use Template ". On the next page, click on " Select a company-managed project ". 3. Add the project details as provided in the Service Request form: Name : Space Name , min. 2 characters, max. 80 characters, Department names must be added in brackets to the Space name. Kindly check with user for which Department this project is created. Based on that add the Department name in brackets to the Space name. For example Software Solutions is the Space name requested by user. And this Project belongs to IT Supply Chain & Product Solutions - SCPS . Then the Space should be names as Software Solutions (SCPS) Below are details of the Department and some examples of updated Jira Space names Key : Prefix , min. 2 characters, max. 10 characters Select " Share settings with an existing project " and based on the User's request select the template: Case 1 : Type of Project to be created is Tribe Project , select " KONE Tribe Template( KONETT ) " Case 2: Type of Project to be created is Portfolio Project , select " KONE Portfolio Template ( KPT ) " Case 3 : Type of Project to be created is Simple Team Project , select " xRD Vanilla Team Template(XRDVTT) " NOTE: For managing the team backlogs. Levels: Epic Sets -> Epic -> Story/Task/bug ->Sub-Tasks Approval Needed from Leena Kangas NOTE : Levels: Initiative ->Epic Sets. Approval needed from Pekka Marttila or Juha Koponen to create this type of project. NOTE : Levels: Epic Sets -> Epic -> Story/Task/bug -> Sub-Tasks Approval needed from Pekka Marttila or J uha Koponen to create this type of project. And then Click on Next a prompt will come, Click Continue , the Project is created 4. Now that the project has been created, continue to assign the permissions. Select " Space settings " -> On the " People " tab, select " Add people " 5. Add the person specified in the service request form as administrator with role " Administrators ". This will grant the Space administrator permissions to modify the Space, e.g. add additional administrators. Add " JIRA_EIT_DCOE_Contributor " with role " Agile Team Member ". This will assign modify permissions for all users with the MyAccess role JIRA: EIT & DCOE Contributor role. This applies only to "KONE IT" Space! Other teams' Space are utilizing individual groups. 6. The group JIRA_KTI_TEAM group will be added by default with Observer role. Change the role to Contributor. 9. Then go to Space Settings -> Details and change the Space Category as KONE IT and also change the Space lead to user's name. By default, name will be IT support team member name. Then click on Save details . After creating a new Jira Space, it must be added to the automation, updated in the SharePoint Excel file, and integrated into the Anton Power BI connector query using the links provided below: Automation rules: Rule builder - Automation - Jira & Rule builder - Automation - Jira After creating a new Jira Space, it must be added to both automations attached in below attachment SharePoint Excel file: Jira community - Jira Project to Department Mapping - All Documents Power BI connector query: Power BI Connector - Jira For Jira Scrum/Kanban board creation Kindly follow the steps given in the KBA8190

short_description: AskIT - How to raise SAP ERP(Z0*) systems access requests for China users using GRC Form
introduction: How to raise SAP ERP(Z0*) systems access requests for China users using GRC Form?
instructions: Objective : This KB article explains the process / steps to be followed while submitting SAP ERP (Z0*) systems related access requests for China Users using GRC form by AskIT team. Note: AskIT team has to handle only new user's access requests based on Clone ID for China users for SAP ERP (Z0*) systems access requests. Any other access requests (ServiceNow Tickets) for SAP ERP (Z0*) systems need to be routed to Infosys Support Team (Infosys China Security support Team) along with filled in template. (Please refer to respective China unit's approval process before routing the requests to Infosys support team.) We have 3 separate units in China region and the process to be followed is different for different China unit user's access requests for SAP ERP (Z0*) systems. a> China Front Line (FL) b> China Supply Line (SL) c> China Giant KONE (GK) When China users reach out to AskIT team for SAP ERP (Z0*) systems access request, then please ask to which China unit among above 3 units he / she belongs to. Based on user's unit, please follow corresponding unit's related process as explained below. a> Process / steps to be followed for "China Front Line (FL)" user's access requests in GRC form by AskIT team. Step 1> Provide blank template of "SAP ERP Access Form China" to respective user and ask to fill in the template with required access details and get the filled in template reviewed and approved by respective Change Manager. Once respective change manager has approved the filled in template, user has to send the respective filled in template and approval mail to AskIT team for creating access request using GRC form. Note: Please refer to blank template of "SAP_ERP_Access_Form_China" attached to "Notes & Attachments" section of this KB Article. Step 2> AskIT team has to check if the filled in access form template is reviewed and approved by corresponding change manager in mail or not. If the user belongs to "China FL(CHN) - NEB" OR "China FL(CHN) - FICO" as per template then filled in template has to be reviewed and approved by Change Manager : CHNAMPEN (Amy Jia Peng ) If the user belongs to China FL(CHN) - SEB as per template then filled in template has to be reviewed and approved by Change Manager : CHNJUZHU (Julia Zhu Li Min) If the corresponding change manager not reviewed / approved filled in template then send back the template to user and ask to get the approval from corresponding change manager mentioned above. Step 3> If the filled in template reviewed and approved by corresponding change manager then Ask IT team has to attach the approval mail and filled in template to corresponding ServiceNow ticket and can submit the access request in GRC Form for new users based on Clone ID. Please follow below steps to raise a GRC request for new user access provisioning in SAP systems based on the clone ID. You can navigate to the GRC SAP Access Request form by following the below instructions. 1. Open GRP Production (SSO) from SAP GUI log on pad. 2. Execute ZFORM t-code. 3. After executing the "ZFORM" t-code, an Access Request Form will be opened then please fill in the details as per the template. While filling the GRC access request form, AskIT team has to replace the "Manager ID" with corresponding "change manager's ID" in the GRC form manually for China Front Line users. If the request is not for "New user based on Clone ID" then route such requests to "Infosys China Security support Team" attaching filled in template and approval mail from corresponding change manager to respective Remedy ticket. short_description: Training Document for SAP GRC - 3
introduction: 1> How to raise User Group (Job Role) based access requests using SAP GRC standard access request form. 2> Instructions to be followed before raising SAP Access Requests by Security Administrator.
instructions: Objective: This KB Article explains on h ow to raise User Group (Job Role) based access requests using "SAP GRC standard access request form" and instructions to be followed / various checks / validations to be performed before raising access requests by SAP Security support team in SAP standard GRC Access Request form. 1> How to raise User Group (Job Role) based access requests using SAP GRC standard access request form? Note: User Group (Job Role) in SAP ERP / ECC (Z0*) systems starts with YECC: naming convention which is composite role. Step 1> Log in to SAP GRC Production system and execute NWBC t-code (OR) Open below GRC Production (GRP) system NWBL URL and log in with GRC Prod log in credentials. https://knecigrp9800.konenet.com:44398/nwbc/?sap-nwbc-node=root&sap-theme=sap_corbu Step 2> Once log in to GRC Prod NWBC, navigate to Access Management -->Access Request to open SAP standard access request form. Step 3> Once access request form is opened, mention the respective Remedy ticket / Incident number in "Description" field and fill in "Request Details" on the right side of the screen. Step 4> Add corresponding User Group(s) / Job Role(s) in bottom pane of the access request form screen. Note: SAP_ALL_USERS is the Active Directory (AD) / LDAP group (role) added to every new SAP user account access requests automatically (by default). Th is LDAP group / role should NOT be removed from request. Step 5> Check if basic user details are populated under User Details tab. If not populated, then put the cursor in User filed in the form and then click on enter button and check if user details are populated. Even after above step if still user’s details are not populated, then ask user to raise an ASKIT ticket to get the required user details ( First Name / Last Name / E-mail / Manager) mapped in Active Directory (AD) system. Step 6> If the request being raised for "Primary User Group (Job Role)" for user then navigate to "User System Details" tab and then add corresponding User Group of YECC: Role added in "User Access" tab. For Example: Primary User Group (Job Role) added in User Access tab is YECCC: FTR_TRM_GSSC then corresponding primary user group : FTR_TRM_GSSC should be added in User System Details tab. If the User Group (Job Role) access raised for multiple SAP ERP systems then corresponding Primary User Group should be added for each SAP system entry in "User System Details" tab. Note 1: LDAP_KNECIGRP9800 system entry can be left blank in "User System Details" tab as its related to Active Directory (AD) system added for default AD group / role in User Access tab. If the request being raised for "Secondary User Group (Job Role)" for user then navigate to "User Groups" tab and then add corresponding User Group(s) of YECC: Role(s) added in "User Access" tab. For example : Secondary User Group (Job Role) added in User Access tab is YECCC: FTR_TRM_GSSC then corresponding secondary user group : FTR_TRM_GSSC should be added in User Groups tab. If the Secondary User Group(s) (Job Role) access raised for multiple SAP ERP systems then corresponding User Group(s) should be added for each SAP system entry in "User Groups" tab. Note 2: If the request needs to be raised for multiple User Groups (Job Roles) for new SAP User account / ID access then check with respective user that which User Group is for Primary job responsibilities and which User Group(s) / Role( s) are for secondary job responsibilities. Then, raise two separate access requests for required User Group (Job Roles), one request for "Primary User Group (Job Role)" and second request for Secondary User Groups (Job Roles). Step 7> Fill in details in Custom Data tab like shown in below sample screen. Note: Request reason and Request Justification details need to filled in as why the access is needed. Request Justification should be filled in with the request details on why the access is needed. T-code(s) / Clone ID field should be filled in as "Not Applicable, Its User Group(s) based Access Request" for User Group (Job Role) based access requests. Step 8> Click on Submit button to create / submit GRC request. 2> Instructions to be followed before raising SAP Access Requests by Security Administrator a> If Group (Job Role) based access request needs to be raised for New SAP User ID then always raise the request as Primary User Group based access request. b> If new SAP new user require access to multiple User Groups (Job Roles) then raise one request with Primary User Group (Job Role) and second request with Secondary User Group(s) (Job Roles). c> Currently secondary User Groups based access requests should be raised only for GSSC and MAKE user groups for respective users. d> If additional t-codes / authorization access request needs to be raised for existing user who already has User Group (Job Roles) in SAP ERP production system then please DO NOT raise access requests for task roles except for task roles which are maintained in exception custom table : ZGRC_EXCEP_ROLES and ZGRC_PUG_EXP_RLS in GRC Prod (GRP) system. If additional t-codes / authorizations access required is not part of exception list of task roles then analyze and add required additional access into existing User Group Role which is already assigned to user following required User Group (Job Role) change approval process. e> If any new exception task roles to be added to above custom table: ZGRC_EXCEP_ROLES and ZGRC_PUG_EXP_RLS in GRC Prod (GRP) system then get approval from Kristiina Kantola (KONE Internal Controls Team) and Dinesh Mahajan (KONE GRC & Security Configuration Owner) before adding new task roles to above exception table. f> If the access request needs to be raised for existing user who already has ONLY task roles in SAP ERP production system then please DO NOT raise access requests based on User Group (Job Roles) instead raise requests based on only required task roles expect if user has only task roles which are part of above exception table. (OR) if such existing user who has task roles which are not part of exception custom table but still required User Group based access then existing task roles need to be removed from user (i.e. marked for removal in the access request by adding corresponding roles from "Existing Assignments" tab in the corresponding GRC request) and then add required User Group (Job Role) into same access request. Note: For this case, existing task roles need to be marked for removal in request ONLY after acceptance from respective user. g> If the existing user has Primary User Group (Job Role) already in SAP ERP Production system and then need access to different Primary User Group (Job Role) then existing Primary User Group (Job Role) should be marked for removal by adding corresponding User Group Role from "Existing Assignments" option of respective user's access request form and add new Primary User Group (Job Role) with assign action under "Provisioning Action" column of request. h> " User Group" field in Logon Data tab of SU01 screen is considered as "Primary User Group" and User Group field in "Groups" tab of SU01 screens is considered as Secondary User Group(s). i> If the access request needs to be raised based on Clone ID then check if Clone ID has User Group (Job Roles) then raise User Group based access request for user with corresponding User Group (Job Role) of Clone user instead of raising Clone ID based access request. Also, if such Clone user has any exception list of task roles then add those exception list of task roles also into current user’s access request. j> For access provisioning in Non-Production SAP ERP (Z0*) systems there is no restriction / validations required to assign / raise requests for task roles on top of User Group (Job Roles) and vice-versa.

short_description: Windchill - Find Corresponding MyAccess Role
introduction: How to find corresponding MyAccess role for granting access to Windchill content?
instructions: Windchill content is primarily managed via user groups, which in turn are managed via MyAccess roles. If you need access to a certain file in Windchill, you can use the Container Search functionality to find the corresponding user group. 1. In Windchill, open Container Search via the Quick Links dropdown menu 2. Search for the file 3. Click on the Context Name link to get the full list of User Groups, which manage this folder Note that there might be multiple user groups granting different types of access (guest vs. member). If you are unclear which is the correct group, please discuss with your team leader. 4. Copy the appropriate group name and request the corresponding MyAccess role via MyAccess

short_description: Avaya Contact Recorder - Unable to play the recordings
introduction: How do we support the Avaya contact Recorder access issue? How can we check the precise system settings in the user machine to make the system to play the records.
instructions: Please find the attached guide to troubleshoot the ACR issues. Download – Kone – Enable IE Mode Open Software Center Install “Kone – Enable IE Mode If you do not see this software in the Software Center, please open an AskIT ticket. Example of ticket: “Please push the following software to the following serial number: Kone – Enable Mode (Add serial numbers here) (Info can be found System under Device Name) Please send ticket to SCCM team.” Enable IE Mode in Edge In Edge go to the URL edge://settings/defaultBrowser and set Allow sites to be reloaded in Internet Explorer mode to Allow . Click restart. Adding site to “Internet Explorer mode page” Select Add and input URL for Avaya Recorder Reminder: Adding URL to IE Pages will expire in 30 days. The Avaya Recorder URL must be added every 30 days. Configuring Java Add Avaya Recorder URL in Exception Site List in Java Select Edit Site List Select Add and input URL for Avaya Recorder If you do not have Java installed, please open an AskIT ticket to have it pushed to your machine. Enable Java Add-Ons Search for Manage browser add-ons Select Manage add-ons Java(tm) Plug-Ins must be enabled Select each Java Plug-In and select enable After these steps are done, Avaya Recorder should work in Edge. If you have any questions on this, please contact Ashokkumar Paramasivam (Avaya Configuration Owner)

ProductionAdministrator – with this role you are allowed to do the same as with role Production plus to do all manual interferences described above and plus to change the setting in the application. ProductionSuperUser – it contains the same authorization as Production Administrator except of the possibility to change setting in the application. 7. Once all the previous steps are completed, Click on Save . 8. Share the user credentials to the user. 3. Providing Access at DB Level 1. Login to SQL Server Management Studio. 2. short_description: Special Fee - Configration steps
introduction: What are the steps to achieve the implementation of the special fee?
instructions: Whenever a new request comes from Front Line, we need to make that all the configuration elements are ready for the implementation; they are: 1. Create 3 new materials XX_SPECIAL_FEE - XX_SPECIALFEE_DOOR – XX_CONDITION_FEE where ‘XX’ will be replaced by the country ID (ie.: Italy = IT, France = FR, etc…; in order to achieve the material creation, the template below has to be filled in and sent to KBS_PDM ISC_PDM@KONE.com (currently we have M Thanesh thaneshm.muthuramalingamt@kone.com following this task up). (check the attachment in "Files"). In Column ‘A’, put the full material ID with the country ID of the requesting unit and in Column ‘K’ the same to be copied for the English (EN) language and make sure by asking the requestor if any additional language must be defined with the proper description. When sending the template to PDM team, put in the message and make sure that the reference materials FI_SPECIAL_FEE - FI_SPECIALFEE_DOOR – FI_CONDITION_FEE are mentioned and being used for copy and to which Plant & Sales Org. we are requesting the new materials to be created. Important things to check are that: o Material XX_SPECIAL_FEE has Division = 04 in tabs “Basic Data 1” and “Sales Org. 1” o Material XX_SPECIALFEE_DOOR has Division = 05 in tabs “Basic Data 1” and “Sales Org. 1” o Material XX_CONDITION_FEE has Division = ‘BLANK’ in tabs “Basic Data 1” and “Sales Org. 1” 2. After materials got created, let’s make sure that Configuration profile for materials XX_SPECIAL_FEE - XX_SPECIALFEE_DOOR is added (Tcode CU41); take the materials FI_SPECIAL_FEE - FI_SPECIALFEE_DOOR as reference to complete the Class type (300) and the Class assignment (KCSM_FEE_ADMIN - KCSM_FEE_INVOICE). 3. Make sure that characteristic KCSM_SPECIAL_FEE is only visible for the Sales Orgs requesting this process by maintaining dependency KCSM_SPECIAL_FEE_VISIBILITY with the new Sales Org. 4. Make also sure that characteristic KCSM_SPECIAL_FEE is included in the Variant Configurator layouts, wherever possible, under “Invoice” class (ie.: for material XX_KONE_NEMO). 5. Set up related pricing condition for material XX_CONDITION_FEE (could maybe be done directly by frontline) 6. Configure the trigger for material XX_CONDITION_FEE in DMR: o Check if any “Material Group from 1 to 5” is available at contact-item level (check HOI dependencies, ie: XXX_HOI_ASSIGN or XXX_NEMO_HOI_ASSIGN to see if any rule already in place) o After the Material Group available is identified, update the HOI dependency so that whenever characteristic KCSM_SPECIAL_FEE = 1 to put the value 1 for the identified Material Group In table ZDP90_01 add as many entries as per the existing Activity types already configured, flag the ‘X’ in Bill Flag parameter and put the value 1 in correspondence of identified Material Group.

short_description: How to enable Microsoft Teams Calling Feature
introduction: How can we enable teams calling for a user?
instructions: How to enable Microsoft Teams calling feature To enable teams calling follow the following steps with these set of commands: ONE-TIME STEP : Run these commands first time to build the session (Open Windows Powershell) · $PSVersionTable.PSVersion · Untrusted repository · Install-Module -Name PowerShellGet -Force -AllowClobber · Install-Module -Name MicrosoftTeams -Force -AllowClobber · Update-Module MicrosoftTeams STEP1: Connect MS Teams Online : 1. Connect-MicrosoftTeams on local machine Windows Powershell with your admin credentials. 2. Once connected then Run this command to check If person has teams calling enabled or not.It Checks Line URL and Telephone number Get-CsOnlineUser -Identity "frits.karssies@kone.com" | fl *voice*,*line*,*dial* There can be 2 scenarios : First, When user doesnot have teams calling enabled as seen in first screenshot Second, When user has teams calling already enabled as seen in second screenshot below 3. Now after checking for the user, If user doesnot have teams calling enabled then assign the appropriate teams license to the user “Microsoft Teams Phone Standard” 4. Wait for 5 minutes to get it synced, then assign the number as per users location, choose the number from the excel of free numbers by filtering the location. 5. Before assigning the number, check if the selected number is already assigned or not through this command. “Get-CsOnlineUser -Filter "LineURI -like '+4951164721162*'" | ft UserPrincipalName, LineUri,OnlineVoiceRoutingPolicy” When number is already assigned to someone else. When number is free to be assigned. 6. If number is free to be assigned then assign the number using this command “Set-CsPhoneNumberAssignment -Identity darja.krause@kone.com -PhoneNumber +4951164721162 -PhoneNumberType DirectRouting” (Change phone number and Identiy address as per the requirement) After successful assignment check with the first command again to be double sure. 7. Enable Routing policy through this command “Grant-CsOnlineVoiceRoutingPolicy -Identity 'frits.karssies@kone.com' -PolicyName 'NL-FullAccess' “ (change the policy name as per the location) Steps to DISABLE CONTACT (if you want to disable someone's contact and enable it for another one) “ Remove-CsPhoneNumberAssignment -Identity darja.krause@kone.com “ (change identity as per the users email address) 1. Check with the below command for the active details for the user Get-CsOnlineUser -Identity "darja.krause@kone.com" | fl *voice*,*line*,*dial* 2. Now run the above remove command and Supply values for the following parameters: PhoneNumber: + PhoneNumberType: DirectRouting 3. Now assign any number If required.

short_description: ASKIT KBA - How to handle EngCalc Installation Ticket
introduction: How to handle EngCalc Installation Ticket?
instructions: Audience: AskIT Team EngCalc is a KONE software for Order Engineers and R&D team members to perform technical calculations for KONE Elevators. EngCalc is installed in the user’s machine. EngCalc has the following user levels. · Standard ( AD group - ECT_Standard ) · Experienced ( AD group - ECT_Experienced ) · R&D ( AD group - ECT_RD ) · SOF Experienced ( AD group - ROLE_IDM_FI_GLB_ECTSOFEXPERIENCED ) o – This is a secondary access level and would be applicable only if the user is already member of one the other EngCalc groups To use EngCalc, the user must have the required authorization at the requested user level. The authorization to use EngCalc is arranged via MyAccess. MyAccess would automatically raise an AskIT ticket once the MyAccess request has been approved with the following details. · Client ID – “My Access Interface” · Description would contain the user details, user’s machine name and whether user requires EngCalc installation or Updated User rights. (or) Then ticket should be assigned as below and any one of the Scenario to be followed · Service – Engineering Calculation · Application – EngCalc · Queue (or Owner) – Incident Management There are 3 scenarios for EngCalc installation. Scenario 1 – KONE user needs EngCalc to be installed on his/her machine and it is the first time the user is requesting for EngCalc Scenario 2 – KONE user needs EngCalc to be installed (or reinstalled) on his/her new laptop. The user already has authorization to use EngCalc. Scenario 3 – KONE user has authorization to use EngCalc at a particular user level. The user is requesting for using EngCalc at a different user level Scenario 1 – KONE user needs EngCalc to be installed on his/her machine and it is the first time the user is requesting for EngCalc Authorization to use EngCalc is handled via MyAccess. If the AskIT ticket is raised by the user directly, then the AskIT agent should advise the user to raise MyAccess request for the same, and then close the ticket. o The AskIT agent should share with the user the Knowledge Article - KBA7801 - on how to raise the request via MyAccess. If the AskIT ticket is raised by MyAccess, then the AskIT agent should follow the instructions mentioned in the knowledge article – KBA7753 - to complete the EngCalc installation. Scenario 2 – KONE user needs EngCalc to be installed on his/her new laptop. The user already has authorization to use EngCalc. This scenario is for situations where an user has authorization to use EngCalc and where he needs the EngCalc software to be reinstalled on his laptop. This scenario would be applicable in cases of PC refresh. The user does not need to raise a MyAccess request for authorization since he already has authorization. The AskIT agent should double check with the user if he already has authorization to use EngCalc. If the user does not have authorization to use EngCalc, then the user would not able to use EngCalc even if EngCalc is installed on the user's machine. After confirming with the user that he already has authorization to use EngCalc and just needs EngCalc to be reinstalled on his machine, then the AskIT agent should follow the instructions mentioned in the knowledge article – KBA7753 - to complete the EngCalc installation. Scenario 3 – KONE user has authorization to use EngCalc for a particular user levels. The user is requesting for using EngCalc at a different user level This scenario would be applicable when the user has been authorized to use EngCalc at a particular level , for ex: Standard, and now wants to access EngCalc at a different level for ex: Experienced level. This requires a new authorization, and hence the user should raise a MyAccess request for the required user level. If the AskIT ticket is raised by the user directly, then the AskIT agent should advise the user to raise MyAccess request for the same, and then close the ticket. o The AskIT agent should share with the user the Knowledge Article - KBA7801 - on how to raise the request via MyAccess. If the AskIT ticket is raised by MyAccess, then the AskIT agent should use the KBA7754 to upgrade the user’s rights to requested level.

short_description: ASKIT KBA - How to install EngCalc for KONE domain users (New Installation AND PC Refresh scenarios)
introduction: How to install EngCalc for KONE domain users? (New Installation AND PC Refresh scenarios)
instructions: Audience: Ask IT Team This knowledge article would replace the KBA1551. EngCalc is a KONE software for Order Engineers and R&D team members to perform technical calculations for KONE Elevators. EngCalc is installed in the user’s machine. (New Installation AND PC Refresh scenarios) EngCalc installation is done in 2 parts – · Part 1 - Install EngCalc Pre-Req ( New Installation or PC refresh) · Part 2 – Install EngCalc Application (This should be done after Part 1 is completed) Part 1 - Install EngCalc Pre-Req - New Installation EngCalc dependencies are installed by the SCCM package - KONE_EngCalc_PreReq_EN_06 Once the MyAccess request is approved, the user is added to the respective EngCalc AD group. Then the pre-req will be installed in user’s machine automatically . You can check if the dependencies are installed by checking if the Status of the EngCalc SCCM package in Software Center is “Installed”. When EngCalc Pre-Req is installed successfully the Desktop shortcut is created. If Desktop shortcut is visible, then move to Part 2. If you are not able to find the Desktop Shortcut, check the below points: Check if the MyAccess roles are assigned. Check if AD groups are assigned. Check the status in software Center if the pre-Req is installed If yes, then Shortcut will be created. If No, reach SCCM team to check, why Package is not installed or Failed User connected to KONE Network or VPN Whether user have restarted the machine in last 24 hours - Below are the AD group details: MyAccess Role AD Group EngCalc: Standard User ECT_Standard EngCalc: Experienced User ECT_Experienced EngCalc: R&D User ECT_RD EngCalc: SOF Experienced User ROLE_IDM_FI_GLB_ECTSOFEXPERIENCED Part 1 - Install EngCalc Pre-Req - PC Refresh If the scenario is PC Refresh, Engcalc pre req is installed automatically to new machine. NOTE : Latest machine must be added as Primary machine for user. As stated above, check if Pre-Req is installed in Software Center and the Desktop shortcut is installed and proceed to Part 2 .If you are not able to find the Desktop shortcut or the EngCalc Pre-req is not installed, check with SCCM team if User’s machine ID is added as Primary machine, User connected to KONE Network or VPN, Whether user have restarted the machine in last 24 hours Request user to Login and logout 7 times to detect the new machine is added as Primary machine Part 2 - Install EngCalc Please reach out to the user via Teams and connect to this machine. Please ensure that the user is connected to KONE network either directly or via KONE VPN. If the EngCalc dependencies SCCM package is correctly installed, then the package would have placed a shortcut on the user’s machine named “EngCalc Installation”. This shortcut points to the path - http://scengtools.konenet.com/KONE_EngCalc/ If the shortcut is not visible (or have been moved), then you can directly navigate to the above path in a browser window on the user’s machine. NOTE : The below step must be done by the End User with the guidance of AskIT. AskIT must not take remote and do this. This will work only if the End User installs it. Please click on “Install” in the browser window. This would download a file named “Setup.exe” and save it to the user’s Downloads folder. Please run the downloaded Setup.exe file. This does not require Admin Rights. There might be a warning that the Published cannot be verified. You may ignore this warning message and click on “Install”. The above action would download the EngCalc installation file, and then automatically start the installation. This might take several minutes to complete (around 5 minutes but could vary depending on the network connection). Then the installation file would automatically validate the user. This step might take several minutes to complete. Once the validation is completed, the EngCalc application would be installed and launched.

short_description: ASKIT KBA - How to Upgrade or Elevate EngCalc User Access
introduction: How to Upgrade or Elevate EngCalc User Access ?
instructions: Audience: Ask IT Team This knowledge article would replace the KBA3170. EngCalc is a KONE software for FL users, Order Engineers and R&D team members to perform technical calculations for KONE Elevators. Authorization to use EngCalc is handled via MyAccess. If the ticket to upgrade or elevate EngCalc user access is from MyAccess, then it means that the user has raised the access request via MyAccess and that the MyAccess request has been approved. Please check if the user belongs to the below AD group based on the Access Name mentioned in the ticket. * Please note that the user levels - "Light R&D" and "Light Experienced" which are used earlier are deprecated and no longer in use. This step does not require reinstallation of EngCalc since the user already has EngCalc and simply wants his access rights to be modified. This can be achieved in the below manner. 1. Reach out to the user via Teams and connect to the user’s machine. 2. Ensure that the machine is connected to KONE network either directly or via KONE VPN. 3. Open EngCalc by typing EngCalc in the Search Box and clicking on EngCalc. 4. Click on Tools à Update User Rights on the EngCalc application, Click on Yes to continue the user's rights upgrade. 5. This might take several minutes to complete. This will close the EngCalc, and then the user can open EngCalc with the updated rights.

short_description: Out of scope of Sharepoint online for Office 365 L2 Support

instructions: The following items are out of scope of knowledge capture of sharepoint Online: 1. Any kind of setup/configuration activity at tenant level 2. No scope of sharepoint on-premises. 3. short_description: Add users/ Remove users/ Modify user's access in a Jira Spaces
introduction: How to Add users/ Remove users/ Modify user access in a Jira Spaces If a user is not having access to Jira Space, the user may face below errors 1) If any user gets the below error while accessing a Spaces in Jira 2) If any user gets the below error while adding filter to Issues in a Jira Space
instructions: If the user faces the above errors 1) Check if the user is having basic Jira access in Active Directory(AD) . Open AD , search for the user's name and open the account. Click on Member Of tab. Check if the user is part of Jira_All_Users. This means the user is having basic access to Jira. 2) If Yes, then check which Jira group he is added for example: JIRA_EIT_DCOE_Contributor/ JIRA_RD_TEAM_WRITE/ etc., and if this group is added to the Jira Spaces for which the user is requesting access a) IF User is part of the group and the group is added to the Jira Space THEN user will have access to that Space. b) IF User is part of the group and the group is not added to the Jira Space THEN user will not have access to the Space, we have to add the user individually to the Space. c) IF User is not part of the group THEN user will not have access to the Space, we have to add the user individually to the Space. For Scenarios b) and c), Follow the below instruction to add the user to the Space Individually. Note : -> Before adding any person to the Jira Space, Kindly get an approval from the respective Jira Space Administrators and enquire what level of access has to be provided to the requestor. To check who is the admins, navigate to Space Settings in a Space -> People and see who are the admins -> If the user states, he is able to view the project but not able to create story or do other activities in Jira project, then check what level of access the group is having to the project. You can compare this with the Project permissions by navigating to Space Settings -> Permissions . Here you can check the corresponding level of permission needed for doing an activity in the Space and add the user individually to the Space with that permission with approval ADD USER TO JIRA Space Kindly follow the below steps to Add users to a Jira Project individually. Step 1 : Go to the Jira Space in which you need to add the user and click on Space Settings -> People and click on Add People Step 2 : The Add People pop up menu appears. Search for the user's name and select the name. Select the Role from the Drop Down list and click on Add 1 person button NOTE : You can select one or more users at a time, if you decide to give same role for all of them. For example, if you selected 3 users then the button will be Add 3 persons The User is added to the Jira Space. MODIFY USER'S ROLE IN JIRA SPACE Go to the Jira Space in which you need to modify the user's role and click on Space Settings -> People Select the role from Role drop down list respective to the user. And the role is changed. You can select multiple roles also. REMOVE USER FROM JIRA Space Go to the Jira Space in which you need to remove the user and click on Space Settings -> People Select the Remove option corresponding to user's name and confirm by clicking Remove button You will get the below confirmation.

short_description: ASK IT - SharePoint Download issues due to Device Compliance
introduction: What to do when we are getting a Sharepoint Download Restriction/ Error?
instructions: Sharepoint Download issues due to Device Compliance: 1. Pleas check if the user is getting an error like “ Your organization doesn't allow you to download, print, or sync using this device. To use these actions, use a device that's joined to a domain or marked compliant by Intune. For help, contact your IT department. ” 2. If any user is facing this error please check if his device is Compliant. Go to Azure Active Directory -> Users ->Provide the user’s mail id in Search bar and find the user. Then go to Sign-in logs of the user and check from a sign-in log of any application for the user if his device Join Type is “ Hybrid Azure AD joined ” or not. 3. If not, please send the ticket to Compliance Team to make his device Compliance, Based on the updated device enrollment status in the work notes, the ASKIT team must route the case appropriately. If multiple mobile devices are detected, the MDM team should handle them, whereas cases involving multiple registered Windows devices should be managed by the HAM team. 4. If his device Join Type is “Hybrid Azure AD joined” then please request the user to use another browser(clear the cache and browsing history before it). If this step works then this is a browser issue and out of scope for O365 Team. 5. If the above step does not work please go to his Registered Devices and check if multiple devices are registered under his account. If this is the case please route to Compliance Team(MDM/HAM Team) asking for removal of unused devices and to just keep the device under use. 6. After they have removed the devices please check with user if the issue persists or not. 7. If the issue persists please grant yourself the same access as the user and check from VDI if the issue occurs for you or not and forward the findings to O365 L2 Team for further investigation in the issue.

short_description: ASKIT KBA - How to request EngCalc access level via MyAccess?
introduction: How to request EngCalc access level via MyAccess?
instructions: Guide to requesting for EngCalc authorization via MyAccess Please discuss with your manager and colleagues to identify the correct access level to request in MyAccess.There are the below 4 access levels available. · EngCalc Standard User o This is recommended for Front Line users. o This requires 2 level of approvals. § Line Manager § EngCalc Business Owner · EngCalc Experienced User o This can be applied for the Supply Line users who might need to use advanced functionalities in EngCalc. Experienced Users would also be able to use components from other supply units and special components. o This requires 2 level of approvals. § Line Manager § EngCalc Business Owner · EngCalc R&D User o This should be requested only by the members of the KTI R&D teams – QRD and SRD. o This requires 2 level of approvals. § Line Manager § EngCalc Business Owner · EngCalc SOF Experienced User o This should be requested only if you are already member of one of the above 3 groups ie.. engCalc Standard or EngCalc Experienced or EngCalc R&D. This role should be requested only by the members of the Supply Operations Finland (SOF) team. o This requires 3 level of approvals. § Line Manager § EngCalc Business Owner § SOF Team Leader 1. Navigate to MyAccess portal by clicking the below link - https://myaccess.kone.com/ Please provide your KONE email address and password. You might need to also provide second level of authentication using your phone, if you have Multi Factor Authentication activated. 3. Once logged into MyAccess, you would see a screen like below, Click on “Manage My Access” If you have the permission to raise requests for other users too, then you would need click on the button “Manage User Access”, and then select your username ( which would be the first item in the list ), Type “EngCalc” in the “Search Access”.Select the correct access level, and click on the button to the left of the access level or role, 8. Click on the “Next” button. Click on “Submit” button to raise the access request. 10. MyAccess would send a notification via mail with the request details.The request would go to the line manager and EngCalc business owner (and SOF team if the requested role is SOF Experienced ) 12. After the approval cycle is completed, the user would be added to the appropriate user groups. 13. MyAccess would automatically raise a ticket to AskIT requesting for EngCalc installation or upgrade of user rights in EngCalc. the AskIT agent would contact you to guide through the remaining EngCalc installation steps. Please contact AskIT ( AskIT@kone.com ) if you do not get a contact request from AskIT after a week of getting the MyAccess approved.

short_description: Create Roles in MyAccess
introduction: KBA - Create Roles in MyAccess How to create Role in MyAccess? What details should be provided for role creation? How Active Directory Security Group should be selected? How Azure Active Directory Security Group should be selected? How should Ticket Based permission be added to the role? How should Email Based permission be added to the role? How are role owners and approvers notified about successful role creation? How the logged in user is notified about the failure of role creation? Answer This form should be used for role creation for applications that are already onboarded to MyAccess. Note AD based application are onboarded automatically and are visible in MyAccess create roles! For new ticket based and email based application onboarding, please submit request via ServiceNow: Application onboarding request Create Role functionality allows (only) Role Owners to create new Roles in MyAccess After submitting the role creation form, role owners and approvers are notified via email for successful creation of role. In case of failure, the role owner who submitted the form will be notified .
instructions: KBA - Create Roles in MyAccess How to create Role in MyAccess? What details should be provided for role creation? How Active Directory Security Group should be selected? How should Ticket Based permission be added to the role? How should Email Based permission be added to the role? How are role owners and approvers notified about successful role creation? How the logged in user is notified about the failure of role creation? Answer This form should be used for role creation for Deploy,Design, Ideation,Inventory,Operational life_cycle_stage of CMDB Business Applications that are already onboarded to MyAccess. And used for role creation for new applications of ’Role Type’ as ’Azure Active Directory’ and ’Active Directory Security Group’. For ‘Email Based’ or ‘Ticket Based’ ’Role Type’ new application onboarding and related role creation, please create an AskIT ticket. Create Role functionality allows (only) Role Owners to create new Roles in MyAccess After submitting the role creation form, role owners and approvers are notified via email for successful creation of role. In case of failure, the role owner who submitted the form will be notified . In this case please contact AskIt and ask ticket to be routed to KONE.MYACCESS.SUPPORT for analysis. What details should be provided for role creation? CMDB Business Application: The CMDB Business Application for which permission belongs should be selected from the list. If other than available list, can select ‘Business Role’. And select ‘Distribution List’ for Distribution List creation. Environment: Display Production, Acceptance, Development, Sandbox in dropdown. This is not required for ‘Distribution List’ creation. Role Type: Select one value from the list. The value's will be ‘Azure Active Directory’ for Entra ID Security group–related application, ‘Active Directory Security Group’ for AD Security group–related application , ‘Email Based’ for email-based application and ‘Ticket Based’ if Ticket based application. If the Business Application belongs to both email based and ticket based, the option to select either ‘Email Based’ or ‘Ticket Based’. This will be hidden for ‘Distribution List’ creation. If new CMDB Business Application with Role Type as ‘Email Based’ or ‘Ticket Based’ selected, an error will be displayed “Email or Ticket based Self onboarding is still not enabled for this application, Please contact MyAccess Support” Role Name: The role name should be provided. It will allow you to enter the alphanumeric and : ( . , @ # -) characters. Role Name should be unique. If the Role Name entered is already existing in MyAccess, an error will be displayed “Role Name already exists in the System, please use a different Name.” Role Display Name : This is Read only. It is auto populated in the format of CMDB Business Application: Role Name_Environment. For ‘Business Role’, it is in the format of Role Name_Environment. For ‘Distribution List’, it is in the format of Distribution List: Role Name. Description: The brief description of the role which describes clear information of the access rights in the application. Role Owners: Atleast two role owners should be selected. Atleast one of the owners should be a KONE employee. It populates the logged in user by default. Comments Required: Comments can be set as mandatory for the Role request by selecting this box. Attachment Required.: Attachment can be set as mandatory for the Role request by selecting this box. Attachment Text: The Attachment text box will appear, and owner needs to add text to explain what kind of attachment is needed. Text box can contain a link to the needed attachment template of form. Role Completion Notification: Add additional information that helps end users to follow after the access is granted. For example: Possible software installation steps. This text will be included to the Access Request completion email notification mail from MyAccess to end users. Non Requestable: Select this box to hide the role temporarily in MyAccess. Approval Flow: Approval flow can be added for Role Requests and for Role Removal Requests. Different Approval flow types are visible in the Approval Type drop down menu. Different approval flow options are explained in the table below. Role End Date: The role owner can select the date on which they want the role to be deleted from MyAccess. Also, the associated entitlement/group will be deleted from the application. Role Assignment Validity in days: Value should be entered in days. Role will be deprovisioned after the number of days entered. If any values other than numbers are entered validation error is thrown “Minimum Role Validity period is 1 day, only Numbers are allowed. MyAccess does not accepts partial day validity”. How to Navigate to Create Role: Select menu from the top left corner in MyAccess. Select from the drop down menu Manage Roles à Create Roles. The Create Role form will open, and you can provide the required details and save them by clicking Confirm. Before you confirm the form add correct approvers: Note: More than one approver should be selected under “First Level Approvers” and “Second Level Approvers”. Approval Type Approval Flow No approval needed Requested Role will be provisioned without any approval. Manager An approval request is sent to the Manager of the person to whom the role is requested (requestee). Role will be assigned after Manager’s approval is received Named Approvers Approval request is sent to the approvers listed under “First Level Approval” Role will be assigned as soon as one of the approvers has approved Manager, Named Approvers Approval request is sent to Manager and after Manger’s approval to the approvers listed under “First Level Approval”. Role will be assigned as soon as Manager and one of the “First Level approvers” has approved. Named Approvers, Named Approvers Approval request is sent to the approvers listed under “First Level Approval”. As soon as some of the “First Level Approvers” has approved, request will be sent to the “Second Level Approvers” Role will be assigned as soon as one of the First level approvers and one of the Second level approvers have approved. Manager, Named Approvers, Named Approvers Approval request is sent first to Manager, after Manager’s approval to the First Level Approvers. As soon as some of the First Level Approvers has approved, request will be sent to the Second Level Approvers. Role will be assigned as soon as, Manager and one of the First and one of the Second level approvers have approved. Once click on “Conform” button on Read only form, it will display “Role creation request is submitted successfully.” in Dashboard. New role will be created and notification will be received in 10 minutes. How Azure Active Directory should be selected? When the Role Type ‘Azure Active Directory’ is selected, and the option to create new ‘Entra ID Permission’ will be provided as shown below. It will allow to enter the alphanumeric characters to create the Entra ID Permission Name. If the provided value is already present, an error will be displayed “Entra ID Permission is already used, Please use a different Name.” When the Role Type ‘Active Directory Security Group’ is selected and all the existing AD groups CN from its value in MyAccess are listed under ‘Select the existing AD group name from the list’ field as shown below. We can select the existing AD group available in the list and create a role. If the required AD Group Name is not available in the list, the field Create New AD Group Should be selected and the option to create new AD group will be provided as shown below. It will allow to enter the alphanumeric and # _ - characters to create the AD Group Name. How should Ticket Based permission be added to the role? When the CMDB Business Application of type Ticket Based is selected, The Role Type ‘Ticket Based’ to be selected and the permission is created with the same name as role technical name and added under role. How should Email Based permission be added to the role? When the CMDB Business Application of type Email Based is selected, The Role Type ‘Email Based’ to be selected and the permission is created with the same name as role technical name and added under role. After completion of role creation, role owners and approvers are notified about successful creation of role and the user who is creating role is notified about failure of role creation. Following are details - Email Subject Description Information: Role <roleName> has been successfully created in MyAccess. Success email template used to provide information to role owners and approvers about successful creation of role. This email has a role display name and Business Application. Information: Role <roleName> creation failed in MyAccess. Failure email template used to provide information to the user creating the role. This email has role display name and information about who has performed role creation.
