short_description: How to reset a password for Windows or Laptop or Desktop or AD or mobilephone?
introduction: How can end users do a self service password reset / reset a Windows or Laptop or Desktop or AD Password?
instructions: There are two ways for resetting the password :- First Method - If an end user is connected to KONE Network (KOffice) For security purposes, KONE users are prompted to change their Windows login password at specific intervals. Users get a 'password expiry' message or email before the current password expires. Sometimes, the users may themselves decide to change the password when they are connected to the KONE Network. Please follow the steps below to change the Windows login password:- Login to your computer with your existing username and password. After logging in, press the Ctrl , Alt and Delete keys on your keyboard together on your computer. This step will take you to the login page, so read the instructions below before pressing the keys. On the login window, click on the Change a password option. This will direct you to the Change password Enter your existing password in the first box and the new password in the next 2 boxes. Please note that the system will not accept your old passwords so type a new password. If the password does not meet the password requirements specified by KONE, you will be prompted to type a new password. Press the Enter key on the keyboard or click the right arrow key next to the Confirm password box. This will change your windows login password. Second Method - When an end user is working remotely and connected to home network/hotspot or has forgotten the password Password reset should be done with using "Microsoft Online Password Reset" at https://passwordreset.kone.com/ . Once password is reset, VPN (GlobalProtect) needs to be connected. Once VPN (GlobalProtect) connected, user has to lock the screen (Windows Key + L) and then the user need to unlock it with the newly changed password. N.B : Since VPN connection is in background, this will accept the newly changed password and that will sync with the end user host. IMPORTANT NOTE: The prerequisite for the service to work, is that the user has saved his Work mobile phone number in Workday in an international format (+ country area subscriber) . This way the password reset can send out the necessary text message containing a verification code, after which the user can change his/her AD-password. Example of international format of phone numbers: +32 499 123456 General Note regarding the examples below: “The page uses international language automatically based on device and/or browser setting. On an Android phone, it uses the language configured in Settings – General Management – Language and Input – Language. The screenshots were taken on a phone configured for English language.” 1. To reset your password, go to https://passwordreset.kone.com/ . 2. Type in your User ID email and the requested characters for verification from the generated image: 3. Select available authentication methods, preferably the authenticator app. If it is not available, use the text or callback: 4. Type in the one-time verification code press Next: 5. Type in your new AD-password and press Finish: 6. Your password has been reset: *** END OF ARTICLE ***

short_description: Access Review for Campaign Owner
instructions: Access Review KBA for Campaign Owner What is Access Review The access review part of a certification is that when someone who is an authority of some sort- such as a Role Owner, or application owner reviews the access that other users have, to verify whether it is correct and appropriate and makes any necessary corrections to revoke inappropriate access. Actors in Access Review There are 3 actors in Access Review process. They are Campaign Owner Certifier User Campaign Owner : Campaign owner is that person who initiates the access review process. Please note that only Role owners can initiate the access review process. Certifier : Certifier is one who reviews the access of users. Certifier may be a role owner, a role approver or any other person. Campaign owner needs to select a certifier at the time of creating an access review. User : Users are those whose accesses are examined throughout in the mentioned process. In case of self-review process, users first need to review their access. Then the certifier may approve/reject the decision. Otherwise, users may get involved, only if the certifier rejects the access. This KBA contains the instructions to be followed by the Campaign Owner. Please note that only Role owners can see “Create Access Reviews” option. How to navigate to the access review page. Go to MyAccess ( KONE MyAccess - Home ) and click on the quick-link panel in the top left corner of the page. Then click on the “Manage Access” and Role Owner can see “Create Access Review” option. On clicking the “Create Access Review” link, Access Review Form will be opened. Access Review Form elements and validation Filter Roles or Application: Role owner can start the Access Review process using any of the options below: “All Roles I Own” to start access review for all the requestable roles owned. “Select Roles/Applications using filter” to start access review for selected roles On selecting the “Select Roles/Applications using filter”, a new field named “Filter based on Role/Application” is populated. This is a dropdown field, and the owner can choose “Role Name” or “Business Application” value. If Role Owner selects the “Role Name” option, then it populates “Search Roles” dropdown with all the requestable roles owned. Note: If you own multiple roles, the role list might take some time to appear. Role Owner can select one or more than one role from the “Search Roles” dropdown. Selected roles will be shown below the dropdown control. If Role Owner selects the “Business Application” option, then it populates “Search Applications” dropdown with all the applications owned by the Role Owner. Role Owner can select one or more than one application from the “Search Application” dropdown. Selected applications will be shown below the dropdown control. All the requestable roles mapped with the selected applications will be considered for the access review. Please make sure to select at least one role or application and proceed with the next step. Select Certifier Role Owner can select certifier through this dropdown list. There are three pre-populated values for this dropdown. They are “Role Owner”, “Approvers of the Roles” and “Other Person”. If role owner selects “Role Owner” or “Approvers of the Roles”, then the Role owners or the approvers of the selected role become the certifier for the access review. If the role owner selects the “Other Person” option, then it populates a new field named “Select the Certifier Name”. There the role owner can type the name of the desired certifier and select it. Please make sure to select a certifier and proceed with the next step. Schedule Role owner can select the “Start Date” and “End Date” from the Schedule section of Access Review form. Start date indicates the date when the access review starts. Similarly, End date indicates the date when the access review process ends. Both start date and end date fields are mandatory to create an access review. Start date should be current date or future date. There should be at least a gap of 7 days between the start date and the end date. Access Review Option There are two options available in the access review process- “Enable Self-Review by End-User” and “Enable Challenge Period for Reviews”. If the role owner selects the “Enable Self-Review by End-User” option, then the Access Reviews first go to the users to take a decision by themselves. Once the users have taken a decision, certifier can check and approve/reject the user's decision. If the role owner selects the “Enable Challenge Period for Reviews” option, then certifier can first approve/revoke the access. If the certifier chooses to revoke the access, the challenge period will then start, and the user will get a notification and option to explain why he/she would still need that access. Upon selecting “Enable Challenge Period for Reviews” option, a new textbox is populated to provide the challenge duration value. If the users do not react to access revocation within the challenge duration, certifier’s decision will be final, and the user’s access will be revoked. Access Reviews Campaign Name Role Owner needs to provide a name for the access review in the “Access Reviews Campaign Name” text field. At the time of access review creation, Start Date is added at the end of the provided campaign name. Campaign Owner Campaign owner is a dropdown field and populated with the role owner name by default. Once the form is complete, click on the Confirm button to create an access review. The access review will then be assigned to the Certifier for next steps. Click Cancel to cancel the access review and exit the form.

short_description: Access review for certifier
instructions: What is Access Review The access review part of a certification is that when someone who is an authority of some sort – such as a Role Owner, or application owner – reviews the access that other users have, to verify whether it is correct and appropriate, and to make any necessary corrections to revoke inappropriate access. Actors in Access Review There are total 3 actors in Access Review process. They are 1. Certifier 2. Campaign Owner 3. User 1. Campaign Owner : Campaign owner is that person who initiates the access review process. 2. Certifier : Certifier is one who reviews the access of user. Certifier may be a role owner, a role approver, or any other person. Campaign owner needs to select a certifier at the time of creating access review. 3. User : Users are those whose access are examined through out in the mentioned process. In case of self-review process, users first need to review their access. Then the certifier may approve/reject the decision. Otherwise, users may get involved, only if the certifier rejects the access. This KBA contains the instructions to be followed by the Certifier . How Does the Certifier Knows When They Have an Access Review to Do? Certifier will get an email notification. Regardless of the means of notification, certifier can always find any access reviews that are awaiting their attention. · Go to MyAccess home page ( KONE MyAccess - Home ). You have a tile on your home page for Access Reviews. · Click on Access Reviews. 1 Access Review means you have one access review campaign to complete. · You can also find your access reviews under the My Work menu, or from the Quicklink menu at the left. 1. List of Access Reviews This is the main Access Review listing for you, the reviewer. All the access reviews you need to do are listed here. For each review, this front page shows you some essential information. 1. The percentage progress wheel shows how much of this review has been done so far. 2. The Username of the certifier. 3. When the review is due. 4. How many individual review items you have, and how many are completed. 5. The person who set up the access review (Campaign owner). 6. The present phase of access review. 7. This button allows you to forward this access review to others and allow them to certify. Sorting Options In the upper right, you have some options to sort your reviews. The Sort By drop down lets you choose criteria for sorting, and the ordering button beside it lets you toggle between ascending and descending order. Click on start to start the access review. 2. Reviewing the User’s Access The items for you to review are presented in a tabbed interface. Depending on what you must review, you will see either 2 or 3 tabs: 1. The Important tab lists the access review items that require immediate attention. This includes policy violations, challenge items, and returned items. This tab only appears if any of your review items fall into the “important” category. If you don’t have any review items in this category, this tab won’t show up. That means that some of your access reviews may give you an Important tab, and others may not. 2. The Open tab shows access review items awaiting your attention, excluding any items listed on the Important tab. 3. The Review tab shows the access review items that you have completed for this access review campaign. You can change decisions from this tab, up until the point the access review has been completed and signed off. Organizing How the Review Items Are Displayed There are several options for you to control how Access Review items are displayed. · Use the Columns option to choose what information is shown for the review, and in what order. Note that there is a button for adding columns. · Use Group By to organize your review items by several things – for example you might want to group them by application rather than by user. When you choose an option for grouping the items, the Group By button turns green and adds the group criteria, as an indication that grouping has been applied. · Use Filter to display some subset of your items. Some standard filtering fields are provided, and you can also add your own options for filtering. · Use the List option in the upper left for organizing items. This is useful if you want to focus on one user at a time and see just that user’s items. · The List option also lets you view specifics about an identity. Click on the name tab to see more information about this person and their access. To go back to viewing items for all users, click the List icon again and select All Identities. · In the upper right there are a few more options – you can click the "Info" icon to view some general information about the review like due date, owner, Phase of the access review etc. and you can also use the Download button to download the review items to CSV file. Reviewer can view the access review details by clicking the “Info” icon. 3. Making Access Decisions These are the decisions you make directly on an access review item: · You can approve the access. When you approve access, you are indicating that it’s OK for this user to have this access. That means no action will be taken, and the user’s access will remain the same as it is now. · You can revoke the access. When the reviewer revokes access, MyAccess will remove the access. Note: In KONE account, nothing is revoked until the entire access review campaign is complete and signed off. Individual vs Bulk Approval You can take your decisions either by each item individually or through bulk processing. When you’re making your decisions, you can make them one at a time or you can do them in bulk. This is another option that’s configurable: bulk decision-making can be turned off or on, both per certification, and globally. If bulk processing is enabled for your review, you’ll see the Bulk Decisions button. · Once you’ve chosen the items whose access you want to approve or reject, then click Bulk Decisions like approve or revoke. 4. Saving Your Decisions · Once the decisions are made, please click on save to save the decisions. · As soon as the decisions are saved items will be moved from Active tab(Important or Open) to Review tab. 5. Changing Decisions · Once a decision has been saved, you can still go to the Review tab and make changes there. · Click the 3-line menu beside the item, then choose Undo Decision from the flyout menu. Note Until you’ve signed off on the full access review campaign, you have the option to change the decisions you’ve saved. · Once the necessary changes are made, please click on Save. Comments, History, and Details · Comments : You can enter comments at any time on an item. · History : History shows the history of the review item, including previous owner history. · Details : Details shows details about the access this item applies to, such as role name, type, and hierarchy for roles or application name, type, and owner for entitlements. 6. Passing Access Reviews to Other Users You can also assign the access review (single or bulk items, entire access review) to others in three ways which involves different levels of ownership or responsibility both for you and for the person you pass it to: · Delegating Reviews When you delegate a review item to someone else, you are saying they can make the decisions and return the review to you, so that you can review it, accept or change their decision, and then sign off. With delegation, the original reviewer still retains control of the decision and the sign-off. You can delegate the items one-by-one or in bulk. Please check the below images to understand both processes. When you delegate something, you choose whom to delegate it to, and you can enter comments to explain why you’ve delegated or provide instructions. You can keep track of delegated items in your own access review view. Delegated items are shown in your Open tab and are labelled as delegated. The 3-line menu gives you options for viewing any decisions made by the person you delegated to, as well as history, details, and comments. · Reassigning Reviews You can also reassign items – either the whole identity, a single line item, or a specific set of items in bulk . Reassigning is different from delegating, which means that reassigned item is no longer the responsibility of the original certifier. The items to whom they are reassigned to, own complete responsibility for all decisions, and must sign off. However, the original certifier (that is, the person who has reassigned the access review) still retains responsibility for making sure that the person (who is making decisions) completes the review. Reassigning is done through the Bulk Decisions menu, even if you only want to reassign one item. When you reassign items, they will no longer appear in your own access review view. The default behavior for a reassignment is that the person who reassigns the items cannot sign off on their own certification until the reassigned items are completed and signed off by the person you reassigned them to. This means that although you are no longer responsible for the decisions in the certification, you still retain responsibility for making sure the new reviewer completes their review. · Forwarding Reviews Forwarding means you handover all responsibility for the access review to others. You can’t withdraw it, or even see what’s going on with it – you pass all responsibility to the new owner, including the ability to change any decisions you may have already made. Forwarding is done from the main access review listing. That is, you can forward an entire review, but you can't forward individual line items or identities within a review. 7. What Reviewers can do with Delegated, Reassigned, or Forwarded Reviews · Delegated Reviews When you delegate a review item, the user you delegate it to can make decisions about access in the very same way as you (the original owner). Note: Access review will show up for the new reviewer as a work item (under the My Work > Work Items menu) rather than as an access review. In the Manage Work Items listing, click a view to open the access review. The user can make the similar kind of review decisions in the work items view as a reviewer might make in the Access Reviews view - they can Approve, Revoke, Revoke Account, Allow exceptions. The reviewer can opt to decide on only some of the items you’ve delegated. If the reviewer left any items undecided, those will come back to you, on your Open tab, for you to process. The items they’ve decided on will show up in your Complete tab. The person you delegate to also has the option to reject the access reviews that have been delegated to him or her. If someone rejects items you have delegated to them, those items will come back to you for review, and will appear in your Important tab. The person you delegate can also forward the items to someone else for review. If this happens, you as the original owner will see an update in your review – you will see the name of the new owner as the delegate, not the name of the first person you delegated it to. · Reassigned Reviews Reassigned items appear to the new reviewer as new Access Reviews (unlike Delegated items, which are accessed as work items via the My Work menu). The new reviewer will take actions on the review items in the same way as the original owner would have done, including delegating, forwarding, or reassigning the items to someone else. Note though that certifications can be configured to limit the number of times each item can be reassigned. The default behavior for reassignments is that the original owner (that is, the person who reassigned this item to the new reviewer) can't complete the sign-off of the main access review until the person whom (new reviewer) the items were reassigned to has completed, saved, and signed off on their decisions. · Forwarded Reviews Forwarded reviews or review items become the full responsibility of the user they were forwarded to; the new owner processes these reviews in the same way as an original owner would, with all the same options. Remember that when a review or review items are forwarded, they can no longer be recalled or acted on in any way by the person whom (original reviewer) forwarded them. 8. Undoing Delegation Sometimes when you pass a review item or an entire review to someone else, you may need to recall it for some reason. You can recall the delegation and reassignments that you have done to someone else. Please follow below steps: · For individual delegated items, simply click the decision menu for an item and choose Undo Decision. Note: · When you undo a delegation, any decisions made by the person you delegated to are undone. · You can only undo individual line-item delegation if the items were delegated individually. In other words, if you delegated an entire identity, you can't recall items one by one; you will have to undo the delegation of the entire identity. 9. Undoing Reassignment If the person you reassigned something to has decided, you can still undo or change the decision, until the reassignment owner has completely signed off on his or her review. You can also recall a reassigned review. This takes ownership back from the person you reassigned to, returning ownership to you. Please follow below steps: · Click on the info icon at the top and then click Additional Reviews. This will show you all the items that have been reassigned. · Click on the Return button and confirm that you want to return the item to yourself. You can also use the Email icon to send an email message to the new reviewer. 10. Undoing Forwarded items Forwarded items can’t be recalled. Once you forward something, you no longer have access to it, and can’t recall it or edit it in any way. 11. Challenge Period When users’ access is about to be revoked, they can challenge on those decisions which are made by the certifier. Users will provide justification in this case if they want to retain their access. For example, if you have revoked a user’s access to the financial application, during the challenge phase, that user will get an email saying that access has been revoked, and they can respond with comments on the items if they still need that access. You would be notified if that user has challenged it - the user might remind you that they are normally not supposed to have Financial Reporting access but are working on a special project right now that requires the access. Then you have the option to go back to the item and change the decision to approve their access. NOTE: Challenge period is the number of days within which the user should provide a justification if the access is still needed. Challenge period is decided by the campaign owner. If the users do not react to access revocation within the challenge duration, certifier’s decision will be final, and the user’s access will be revoked. 12. Signing Off Decision When decisions have been made for everything in your review, you can sign off. Sign off can’t occur until all items in certification have been decided, including any delegated or reassigned items. Also, if a challenge period is enabled, sign off can’t happen until that is over. A red Sign-Off button means that you have finished the items that you are responsible for, but reassigned reviews haven't been completed yet. A green Sign-Off button means that all individual reviews for this overall access review are completed, and the entire review can now be marked as complete.

short_description: How to Disable “replyall” on Outlook
introduction: How can we disable "replyall" option in outlook client
instructions: How to Disable “replyall” on Outlook Steps to be followed : 1.Ensure you have enabled the “Developer” option on the ribbon under “File” > “Options” > “Customize Ribbon“. 2.Now you can select the “Developer” tab and “Design a Form“ 3.Select “Message“, then “Open“ 4.Select the “Actions” tab, then select the line with “Reply to All” and click “Properties“ 5.Uncheck the “Enabled” box then select “OK“ 6.Select the “Properties” tab, then check the “Send form definition with item” box. Choose “OK” on the dialog box that appears. 7.Click “Publish” > “Publish Form As..." 8.Select the “Look In” drop-down menu you wish to add the form to, then provide the form with a name. In this example we named it “noreply“. Choose “Publish“. 9.Close the developer windows, and you’re done. sNow whenever you’d like to send an email message with “Reply to All” disabled, select “Developer” > “Choose Form” > “Look In: Personal Forms Library” > “noreply” > “Open“.

short_description: How to Provide One drive Access for a user’s mailbox

short_description: Convert SharePoint Site to a Microsoft 365 group
introduction: Convert SharePoint Site to a Microsoft 365 group?
instructions: Convert SharePoint Site to a Microsoft 365 group Steps to convert a sharepoint site to M365 Group : 1. Open the Settings menu in the top suite navigation header, and click Connect to a new Microsoft 365 Group . 2. The group creation experience appears. Optionally, update the group name and email alias, and select the group's privacy and classification (if enabled in your tenant). Then click Connect Group . For more information about group creation and settings, see Learn about Microsoft 365 Groups . 3. The Add and assign people panel appears. This is a proposed list of owners and members, based on an inspection of your existing site. You can add new owners and members, or remove people from the proposed lists. Click Finish when you're done. 4. The New site Home Page appears. Your new modern home page includes the following: § A link to the previous home page in the left navigation bar under 'Home' § A link to 'Conversations' in left navigation bar which navigates to the group's Outlook mailbox § A site activity web part that will show real, current usage in the site § A modern page layout that can easily be modified to suit needs § Group information in the header, including privacy, classification (if set), and member photos You can manage your new group's membership at any time.

short_description: Access Review for User
introduction: What is Access Review The access review part of a certification is that when someone who is an authority of some sort- such as a Role Owner, or application owner reviews the access that other users have, to verify whether it is correct and appropriate and makes any necessary corrections to revoke inappropriate access.
instructions: Access Review KBA for User Actors in Access Review There are 3 actors in Access Review process. They are 1. Campaign Owner 2. Certifier 3. User · Campaign Owner : Campaign owner is that person who initiates the access review process. Please note that only Role owners can initiate the access review process. · Certifier : Certifier is one who reviews the access of users. Certifier may be a role owner, a role approver or any other person. Campaign owner needs to select a certifier at the time of creating an access review. · User : Users are those whose accesses are examined throughout in the mentioned process. In case of self-review process, users first need to review their access. Then the certifier may approve/reject the decision. Otherwise, users may get involved, only if the certifier rejects the access. This KBA contains the instructions to be followed by the User. 3. Access Review Options for Campaign Owner There are two options available for the Campaign Owner in the access review process- “Enable Self-Review by End-User” and “Enable Challenge Period for Reviews”. Based on the option selected by the campaign owner, users will receive an email from MyAccess if any action needs to be taken. 4. Self-Review by End-User In this case, the Access Reviews first go to the users to take a decision by themselves. Users will get below notification from MyAccess to self-review their access: · Click on MyAccess link given in the email- https://myaccess.kone.com · Once you are in the homepage, Click on notification (bell) icon à Others · You should be able to see a work item marked as Delegation · Click on “i” to see the details of the work item · Click on “forward arrow” to forward the item to someone who can review the access on your behalf. In this case, the work item will be assigned to the person that you choose, and it will no longer be visible to you. · Click on “View” to view the entire details and review your access. Make sure to review the access before the ‘Expiration’ date shown in the work item · Click on “Add comment” to provide your comments/justification to the certifier who will take the final decision to approve/reject your access · You can then check the roles assigned to you and choose “Approve All” if you want access to all the roles or “Revoke All” if you do not need the roles anymore · You can also take individual action against each role if you have multiple roles/accesses to review. Once done, make sure to save your changes or cancel if you want to discard the changes. · If you want to undo the decisions, please click “Clear Decision/Clear Decisions” · Once you have finalized your decision, Click on o Complete- To complete the work item- Certifier will be able to see your decision(approve/revoke) and comments o Reject- To send the work item back to the certifier to approve or revoke the access o Forward- To forward the work item to someone who can review your access on your behalf · The certifier can check and approve or reject the user's decision based on the justification provided by the user · If the certifier approves, you will continue to have access to the role · If the certifier rejects, role will be removed, and you will lose the access that was being granted by that role 5. Enable Challenge Period for Reviews If the role owner selects the “Enable Challenge Period for Reviews” option, then certifier can first approve/revoke the access. If the certifier approves the access, no action is needed from the user. If the certifier chooses to revoke the access, the challenge period will start, and the user will get below notification and option to explain why he/she would still need that access. Challenge period is the number of days within which the user should provide a justification if the access is still needed. Challenge period is decided by the campaign owner. If the users do not react to access revocation within the challenge duration, certifier’s decision will be final, and the user’s access will be revoked. · Click on the link in the email to go to MyAccess · You should be able to see a work item marked as Challenge · Click on “i” to see the details of the work item · Click on “forward arrow” to forward the item to someone who can review the access on your behalf and challenge the access revocation. In this case, the work item will be assigned to the person that you choose, and it will no longer be visible to you. · Click on “View” to view the entire details and challenge the access revocation · In this form you can see the Role name and the reason given by the certifier to revoke your access · If you would like to retain the role, enter the “Reason for Challenge” and click “Challenge” · The certifier can check and approve or reject the user's decision based on the justification provided by the user · If the certifier approves, you will continue to have access to the role · If the certifier rejects, role will be removed, and you will lose the access that was being granted by that role

short_description: PrivX Secret Vault-how to add Secrets in PrivX
introduction: This document is for end-user and AskIt
instructions: PrivX Secret Vault You can use PrivX to securely store and delegate access to secret data. PrivX secrets are under the Secrets page. On this page, you can view and edit secrets based on the roles assigned to you. You can also search secrets by their name. "Secret" tab is also added to server connection UI along with the existing Display, Files, Settings tabs. You can use vaulted secret more easily in browser-based connections. Adding Secrets Create a ticket to KONE.PAM.Support with the information below: o Secret Name - The name serves as the unique ID of the secret and cannot be changed Example: o Secret Type - JSON or Credentials - once selected, this cannot be changed o Role Names - that shall have Read access and Write access to the secret. The role which has Write access is also given read access to view the secret. One secret may have multiple roles with read/write permissions Example: o Role description o Role owner o Role approver(s) Once the roles and groups are created by PAM Support Team, you can request the roles from MyAccess by following the instructions provided in the KBA link - Request access in MyAccess Once the role request is approved, login to PrivX, go to Secrets page and search with Secret Name Choose the Edit option to add the Secret Data . There are two types of secret data a) JSON: JSON format is the secret type where users can add secret data manually. Users can add multiple secrets data in one secret and include comments inside the secret data. b) CREDENTIALS: Credentials format where users can add username and only one password per secret. a) Adding secret in JSON format We can add multiple secrets to secret data in JSON format as shown in the below example b) Adding secret as Credentials - username and password (Only One password per secret) Click Save once you have added the Secret Data . Save option will be disabled if the format of the Secret Data entered is incorrect. Accessing and Modifying Secrets You can access the secrets based on the role that has been granted to you. 1. With Read permission, you will be able to view the secrets. 2. With Read and Write permission, you will be able to view the secrets and edit the secret data. 3. If you want to change/update roles that are assigned to a Secret, please contact PAM Support 4. If you want to delete/ remove a secret, please contact PAM Support 5. Secret names and secret types cannot be edited/changed once they are created. If you need to change the secret name or secret type, a new secret must be created and the old one should be deleted. Additional information The secrets are static and it's the Owners (Editors) responsibility to manage the Secrets, Changing the secret (Password) in AD will not be updated directly in the vault. PAM Support team will be able to see the secret names, edit the read/write roles and delete secrets. But they will not be able to see the secret itself. Auditing is supported for the following actions - secret created, accessed, changed, removed. If there are any issues/queries, please contact PAM support team via mail PAMSupportTeam@KONE.com or raise a ticket to KONE.PAM.Support.

short_description: OneNote Sync Issue
introduction: How to fix OneNote sync issue
instructions: Sign in to the Right OneNote Account The OneNote desktop app works with multiple accounts. For example, you can sign into the OneNote desktop app with a free Microsoft account for your personal notebooks and an Microsoft 365 work account for your business-related notes. Here’s how to determine which account is signed into OneNote: 1. Open the OneNote desktop app. 2. Go to the Settings and More menu (the three-dot icon). 3. Select Settings . 4. Select Accounts . 5. Turn on the Sync notebooks automatically toggle switch. 6. Turn on the Sync down all files and images toggle switch 7. Close the Options pane Troubleshoot Sync Problems When a Notebook Won’t Sync If a notebook will not sync, determine if the sync problem is with the OneNote desktop app or with the Microsoft server. 1. Open the OneNote desktop app. 2. Right-click the notebook and select Copy Link to Notebook . The link is copied to the Clipboard 3. Open a web browser. 4. Go to the Address bar , paste the link to the notebook, and press Enter . 5. If prompted, sign into your Microsoft, work, or school account. If the Notebook Opens Successfully If the notebook opens successfully in OneNote Online, the problem may be with the OneNote desktop app. Usually closing and reopening the notebook will fix the sync problem. To close and reopen a OneNote notebook: 1. In the OneNote desktop app, right-click the notebook and select Close This Notebook 2. In OneNote Online, select Edit Notebook > Edit in Microsoft OneNote . 3. Wait while the notebook syncs and opens in the OneNote desktop app. If the Notebook Does Not Open If the notebook does not open in OneNote Online, the problem may be with the server. Check the Microsoft Service Status page. If OneNote is good to go, check with your ISP to see if your service is working properly. Fix Sync Issues When a Section of a Notebook Won’t Sync If you’re working with others on a shared notebook and sections of the notebook are not updating, manually sync the notebook. If that doesn’t work, copy the section that won’t sync to a new section. Share and Collaborate in OneNote with 18 Tips and Tricks Manually Sync a Notebook To manually sync a notebook: 1. Right-click the notebook that won’t sync. 2. Select Sync . 3. Select Sync This Notebook . 4. Select the Page sync status icon. If the Section Does Not Sync If the section did not sync, create a new section and copy the pages from the section that does not sync to the new section. Then, press Shift+F9 to manually sync the notebook

How to change the report options for a user in e-Sign. 1 . Login adobe sign https://konesign.eu2.adobesign.com/account/homeJS . 2 . Go to Account --> Users --> Select user --> Report Options. 3 . Change the report options as requested and click on Save.

short_description: Reset password for Test/Service/Robot/Shared accounts in MyAccess
introduction: How to Reset Password in MyAccess This article gives instructions resetting password for the Test/Service/Robot/Shared accounts by the respective Owner/Deputy owner.
instructions: KBA – Reset Password in MyAccess Question How to reset password in MyAccess? What details should be provided for resetting Password? What is the Password Policy referenced while generating new password? What are the details of audit events? How is user notified about the success or failure of password reset? How to reset password in MyAccess? Functionality is available in MyAccess to reset password. NOTE: min length is 24 characters, recommended 30 characters. How to Navigate to Reset Password: Select menu from the top left corner in MyAccess. Select from the drop-down menu Password Management then Reset Password. . Then Reset Password form will open, and you can provide the required details and click on Reset Password. What details should be provided for resetting password? Username: It is drop down and consists of list of Test/Service/Robot/Shared accounts of logged in user. Need to select any one account from the dropdown. Note : On clicking Reset Password button, password will be changed and cannot be reversed. What is the Password Policy referenced while generating new password ? KONE-PasswordPolicy-NPIM is used while generating the new password. What are the details of audit events? For reset password success or failure audit events are also logged. Audit Description Create This audit event provides information about reset password success or failure. It displays the target as identity name and source as selected account name . For failure cases, the error message is displayed along the above details. How is a logged in user notified about the success or failure of password reset? If the Password reset is successful, the User is displayed with the new password: If there is any error while resetting the Password, the user is displayed with the following message: Additionally, Email is also sent in both Success/Failure scenario to the Logged In user. Email Subject Description MyAccess Notification: Password reset successfully for account . Success email template is used to provide information about successful reset of password. This email has Display Name of the Test/Service/Robot/Shared Account . MyAccess Notification: Password reset failed for account . Failure email template is used to provide information about the failure of reset password. /

short_description: Change Password in MyAccess for the Tier 2 and 3 accounts
instructions: KBA – Change Password in MyAccess NOTE: This applies only if you have T2 or T3 workstation admin credentials! Question How to change password in MyAccess? What details should be provided for changing Password? What are the mandatory guidelines to be followed for the new password? What is the message displayed after submission for failure case? What are the details of audit events? How is a logged in user notified about the success or failure of password change? Answer This form should be used for changing his own password for the Tier 2 and Tier 3 account. How to change password in MyAccess? Functionality is available in MyAccess to change password. How to Navigate to Change Password: Select menu from the top left corner in MyAccess. Select from the drop-down menu Password Management then Change Password. Then Change Password form will open, and you can provide the required details and click on Change Password. What details should be provided for changing password? Username: It is drop down and consists of list of UPN of Tier 2 and Tier 3 account of logged in user. Need to select any one account from the dropdown. Enter New password: New password should be provided based on the password policy. Confirm New password : Need to re-enter the same password, if it is not same validation is thrown. Note : On clicking change password button, password will be changed and cannot be reversed. What are the mandatory guidelines to be followed for the new password? MyAccess uses Active Directory Password guidelines. Following are the Password Guidelines: 1. Minimum length supported is 10 characters. P lease note-if you are changing the password NOT according to policy it wont go through in the system!. T2 password min lenght is 16 characters. 2.min length 10, at least one small letter & one capital letter, special chars allowed only [@#$%^&+=.] , at least one number, Note:your or company names not allowed 3. Use phrases which are easy to remember, yet strong. 4. Avoid using people, places, events, or pet names. What is the message displayed after submission for failure case? NOTE: Password change wont go through, if your cursor is in confirm password box. Come out to box before submitting the request . Change password successfully completed: What are the details of audit events? For change password success or failure audit events are also logged. Audit Description Create This audit event provides information about changing password success or failure. It displays the target as identity name and source as selected account name . For failure cases, the error message is displayed along the above details. How is a logged in user notified about the success or failure of password change? Email Subject Description MyAccess Notification: Password changed successfully for your account . This is to inform you that password changed successfully for your account. Associated UPN: Workstation Admin account UPN This is an automated message from MyAccess. Please do not reply to this email. If you have questions related to this email, please contact AskIT . MyAccess Notification: Password change failed for your account . This is to inform you that password change failed for your account. Associated UPN: Workstation Admin account UPN Retry changing password after sometime , if still issue persists please contact MyAccess Support via AskIT . This is an automated message from MyAccess. Please do not reply to this email. If you have questions related to this email, please contact AskIT .

Step 2 : Click on “ Create a Scrum board ”. or " Create a Kanban board " based on user's requirement. Select the option “ All issues from your project ” and click on Next . After the Scrum/Kanban board is created, Ensure that The below 3 steps are followed: Set the Issues columns right(workflow statuses) Add User as the Admin to the Scrum/Kanban Board Provide Edit Filter rights to the user STEP 1: To set the Issues columns right(this applies for both Scrum & Kanban boards) - follow the below steps: Step 1 : Go to and click on Board Settings . Select Columns tab. Step 2 : Ensure that the below workflow is executed, by drag and drop and also use Add column(+) option STEP 2: To add User as the Admin to the Scrum/Kanban Board - follow the below steps: Step 1 : Go to and click on Board settings and click on Details tab and in the Administrators provide the user's name STEP 3: To provide Edit Filter rights to the user - follow the below steps: Step 1 : Go to and click on Board Settings. Click on details Tab, Then select Edit Filter Query. Select Edit Permissions. NOTE : The person who created the board will only have the Edit Permission for the Filter query Step 2 : In the Editors field dropdown select the option User , Search for the user's name and select the respective account. After selecting the user's account click on Add. The username is added. Click on Save Again, go the Configure Board we can see that the user name is added with the Edit Filter rights.

short_description: GitHub - Error in Git Bash access & Generate Access Token
introduction: How to generate access token in GitHub and rectify error in Git Bash
instructions: Error 1 : While opening GitHub via Git Bash if we get the below error The error is more than likely connected with Git Bash process - usually means that too many process are opened in the system. Manually stopping them should do the trick. In case any issues with accessing resources appeared as a result of enforcing SSO, that means you aren't using SSH (which is recommended). You should create personal access keys and use them for git operations. In case you really don't want it and prefer to continue with HTTPS, you needed to generate access token. Find the below steps to generate Access Token. Note : This process may change. So please refer the URL for the updated procedure: https://eur02.safelinks.protection.outlook.com/?url=https%3A%2F%2Fdocs.github.com%2Fen%2Fenterprise-cloud%40latest%2Fauthentication%2Fauthenticating-with-saml-single-sign-on%2Fauthorizing-a-personal-access-token-for-use-with-saml-single-sign-on&data=05%7C01%7Cmohammed.abdulraheem%40kone.com%7C71dedc0668f7459c583308dabd900ed2%7C2bb82c642eb143f78862fdc1d2333b50%7C0%7C0%7C638030723669603389%7CUnknown%7CTWFpbGZsb3d8eyJWIjoiMC4wLjAwMDAiLCJQIjoiV2luMzIiLCJBTiI6Ik1haWwiLCJXVCI6Mn0%3D%7C3000%7C%7C%7C&sdata=KBkuO5U%2BnTbnevkmvQWlN7kjBfAg9EreQcI0xypO%2Fjo%3D&reserved=0 Step 1 : In the upper-right corner of any page, click your profile photo, then click Settings . Step 2 : In the left sidebar, click Developer settings . Step 3 : In the left sidebar, click Personal access tokens -> Tokens(Classic) Step 4 : Next to the token you'd like to authorize, click Configure SSO . If you don't see Configure SSO, ensure that you have authenticated at least once through your SAML IdP to access resources on GitHub.com Step 5 : Drop down the Configure SSO and click Authorize . Error 2 : After enabling SSO in Github, if you get the below authentication errors when downloading the artifacts from Github Repository in Jenkins Build The above error can be fixed by Authorizing the Personal Access Token using SSO Note : The Personal Access Token Can be configured under Developer Settings in GitHub User Settings.

short_description: Planview - How to add the missing schedule, template or gates (Gantt)
introduction: How to add the missing schedule template or gates (Gantt) in Planview. 1 . Missing gates, workflow not run till the end 2 . Missing all gates. User suppose that the lifecycle is run till the end 3 . Missing one gate
instructions: 1 . Missing gates, workflow not run till the end There is a work, but it does not have any of the gates i.e., schedule template in place. Solution: Ask end user to check that the Lifecyle is run till the end by the creator of the work. Open the “Active Lifecycle Steps” tile on Work tab. In case you can find the work from this list then ask the “User Responsible” to run the lifecycle till the end. Open the item by clicking the Lifecycle step column item and run the lifecycle by updating the missing data and “Save and Complete” In the last phase of the lifecycle run you will be guided to open the view of Work and Assignment with all the necessary gates relate to the work type in focus. 2 . Missing all gates. User suppose that the lifecycle is run till the end In case you are not able to see any row related to the work in scope in “Active Lifecycle Steps” tile then the Planview support admin needs to copy the gates from template to the work in focus. Solution: Admin can check from the Work view of full work portfolio all the active lifecycle steps and check if it is still the case of just open lifecycle steps. Then just ask the “User Responsible” to run the lifecycle till the end. In case it is sure that gates have been deleted then check the following. Open the Templates virtual portfolio: (Use the “See All Work Portfolios” view in Work tab) In case you do not have the work portfolio “Templates” available then just create one by selecting Templates from the property picker. List of templates. Check the work type you are going to update: Open the “Work and Assignments” view from corresponding template Select all rows for Milestones And then Paste under in target work in case the whole Milestones section is missing. Use default setup all the selections and leave Prefix empty 3 . Missing one gate One of the gates related to the work type is missing. E.g., one is by mistake deleted. Solution: Planview support administrators can add the missing gate. Add the missing gate same way as above from template but now just select the missing gate. Example: K5 is missing. Deleted by mistake. Verify that the gates are OK after the update Finally check the column set in view KONE – 2. Advanced schedule and check that each milestone has the corresponding identifier

short_description: Ask IT - Process to be followed by ASKIT for Host Isolation during Security Incidents
introduction: What is the process to be followed by ASKIT for Host isolation in KONE during Security Issue?
instructions: Introduction KONE Cybersecurity Monitoring service 24/7 currently provided by SOC provider Deloitte. KONE Cyber Defense team is responsible for overall Security Operations and Security issues concerning to KONE IT, OT and IoT environment. PC Security team is responsible for AV Incidents on KONE workstations This KBA article will help ASKIT to understand the process that is followed in KONE Cyber Defense and PC Security team and to be followed by ASKIT when the host is isolated from KONE network during Security Incident. Process: The following remediation action will be taken by KONE Cyber Defense team/PC Security team to minimize the disruption caused due to critical cyber threats in KONE. The host will be isolated from KONE network through the Defender console as containment action to ensure no lateral movements in the network. Cyber Defense team will proceed with deep investigation to find the root cause of the alert and provide next counter measures to remediate the Security issue. Isolation will also be initiated by KONE PC Security team if the devices are non-complaint or if they suspect malicious behavior on the workstations. When the host is isolated by either Cyber Defense team or PC Security team, this alert notification will be sent automatically. Once the Host is isolated from the KONE network, automated Email Notification is sent to User’s manager, respective country SDM, ASKIT and OSS to keep them informed about the situation and next actions. It is also informed to respective SDM’s in the email notification to provide a spare laptop to the impacted users until the host isolation is released, and the needed remediation actions are taken. Since the host is isolated until the Security Investigation is completed, user will not be able to access KONE network (Unable to connect to network), Unable to access internal KONE Applications, Outlook and MS Teams etc. Steps to be taken care by ASKIT: Since ASKIT is also informed about the Host isolation via email notification to askit@kone.com , an Incident will be created to ASKIT. This notification is also shared to OSS team These notifications will contain the impacted Host and affected user so that ASKIT can refer these Incidents when these users reach ASKIT for the below mentioned issues 1. Outlook not working. 2. Unable to connect to network. 3. Unable to access KONE internal application like SAP, etc. 4. Teams not working. These Incidents can be resolved in ASKIT queue and can be referred if any users contacts ASKIT for the above-mentioned issues. Note to ASKIT: ASKIT should guide the user and communicate clearly that the host is isolated due to Security reasons and will be released from isolation once the investigation is completed and the needed remediation measures are taken. Do not perform any troubleshooting steps and do not recommend reinstalling the OS. Sample Host isolation template: From: KONE Azure Sentinel < sentinel@kone.com > Sent: Wednesday, October 26, 2022 1:45 PM To: Impacted User’s SDM and User’s Manager Cc: Security < security@KONE.COM >; AskIT < AskIT@kone.com >;kone.PCsecurity@KONE.com ; KONE_QueueManagement@hcl.com Subject: [KONE Cybersecurity Operations] Machine Isolation Notification Importance: Low Hello, We are contacting you from KONE Security Operations Team. A security alert was detected on device xxxxxxxx.konenet.com, which is associated with User name, and you have been identified as the user’s manager and associated regional IT SDM. Information about the alert: Device name: xxxxxxxx.konenet.com Username: xxxxx Description: The reason for isolation is "xxxxxx" and Isolation initiated by "xxxxx". Please reach out to "xxxx" if you require further details about this isolation. After initial investigation, the affected device was decided to be isolated from KONE corporate network. This is a mitigative action to minimize the impact of the threat detected on the affected device. To avoid disruption to business, please provide spare laptop to the affected user until the investigation is completed and issue is remediated. Note to ASKIT and OSS team: This Incident is for your information and can be resolved. When this impacted user contacts ASKIT, please refer this Incident description and communicate to the user that the host is isolated due to Security reasons, and it will be released from isolation once the investigation is completed and the needed remediation actions are taken. Best Regards, KONE Security Operations Team

short_description: Ask IT - MFA Reset Process
introduction: This KBA explains the process and steps to be followed by Ask IT to reset Azure MFA
instructions: Introduction: Contacting service desk for getting MFA reset or Phone number changed are commonly used way for cyber criminals to gain access to targeted users or company’s information. Therefore, it is crucial to validate callers identity without any chance to mistakes ! - If the request is concerning MFA reset for server admin access via PAM (PrivX), the request should be done via MyAccess role: ‘ PAM: MFA Reset’. 1. What user is requesting, which could relate to MFA? If it requires MFA reset, proceed with next steps. a. Known scenarios: i. Old Mobile phone with authenticator app is not accessible anymore as the phone has been broken, lost, or wiped and valid phone number has not been used for the authentication. 2. Confirm user’s current authentication methods (SMS or authentication App). a. Login to https://entra.microsoft.com with your Admin account (If required activate the Authenticator Admin Role in PIM). b. Navigate to Identity -> Users -> All Users c. Search for the user account and open the account which the MFA is concerned. d. Check the current authentication methods – is SMS configured? If SMS is configured, instruct the user to setup the MFA. If only authentication app is in use, but not accessible anymore, then proceed with MFA reset in next steps: 3. Call back the user to the individual phone number in Entra and confirm the name of the user and the user’s manager name -> Ask IT to reset the MFA during the call back. Do not accept the call back number if it is different from the number updated in Entra. a. Reset the MFA by selecting “Require re-register Multifactor authentication” b. Or, update the phone Authentication phone number c. Advise the user to re-register the MFA by following the KB0011527 (Set up 2-step verification and Register Multi Factor Authentication 4. If user’s phone number is not listed in Entra or the user cannot be validated via phone call (like language barrier), validate the user by contacting the user’s line manager preferably via Teams-chat or phone call (if manager can’t be contacted or validated get help from the one above line manager or SDM (preferably via Teams-chat) to point the contact who can validate the user. If user’s or manager’s phone number is missing from AD, please advise manager to get the missing Mobile phone number updated and visibility set as ‘public’ in Workday – refer to KB000A2794 (Ask IT - Two step verification issues in Workday, Ask HR) More references: MFA setup: KB0011527 | Register for Multi-Factor Authentication (MFA) Phone number in Workday: KB000A2794 | Ask IT - Two step verification issues in Workday, Ask HR

short_description: TIP - How to publish unstructured content from PDM to TIP?
introduction: How to publish unstructured content from PDM to TIP?
instructions: 1 . KTD creates a service request for PDM-PDF publishing to TIP in AskIT portal. KTD defines the PDM link and target folder in AEM. We may also receive tickets from outside KTD such as Field Trainer / Maintenance Operations Excellence Specialist. Note: Get approval from Petri.Ahola@kone.com and proceed further. 2 . Open PDM site and find the document number which is to be published in TIP. e.g. http://pdm.konenet.com/document/DL1-02.36.004/en/P.1 3 . XDOC support downloads the PDF document from PDM. 4 . S ave it in local ( e.g. AM-81.10.001.pdf ). Login AEM ( https://kone-aem.adobecqms.net/assets.html/content/dam ) and U pload the PDF to Unstructured document folder in AEM. 5 . XDOC support checks if a DITAMAP is available in the target folder. If not, support creates a new DITAMAP (Create à DITA Map à Map (TIP unstructured document) à Next à Title à Create à Open Map. The procedure to create the new DITAMAP is as defined below. - DITAMAP title: document name in PDM. - copyryear: copyright year in the PDF - created date: date in the PDF - category: document category in the PDF if available (in the header of the pdf) - KTD_Documentnumber: document number available in PDM - KONE Type: document issue available in PDM 6 . XDOC support adds the pdf topicref to the DITAMAP (topicref à Link URL à Search à Choose the PDF file à Select). 7 . XDOC support adds the metadata from PDM to the DITAMAP properties (Select the DITAMAP à Properties). The properties page will be displayed. Navigate to Technical tab and add all the required metadata available in PDM, then click on Save & Close. 8 . XDOC support releases the DITAMAP and the PDF with the bulk release tool. The release state according to the state in PDM. To release the DITAMAP, open Map Console view à Release Assets à Select All à Set the state as in PDM à Release à Ok). 9 . XDOC support creates the baseline. Baseline naming: document number (issue) release. For example: - DL1-06.28.006-SOC (A) for marketing release - DL1-07.22.007 (-) for sales release - DL1-01.06.227 (H) for release To create the Baseline, open Map Console view à Baselines à Create à Enter the baseline name à Browse all topics à Select all à Save. 10 . XDOC support exports the publication to the TIP. Open the Map Console view à Output presets à TIP UD à Edit à Use Baseline à Select the Baseline à Done. 11 . Then select TIP UD à Generate. 12 . Check the status of the output in OUTPUTS tab.

short_description: Ask IT and users - User account is disabled in Myaccess / Enable and Disable Requests
instructions: How to enable or disable user account User accounts are disabled based on the LAST_DAY_OF_WORK in Workday. Manager should be advised to update end date/extend contract in Workday to enable the account. How to check for end date in MyAccess? 1. Login to MyAccess by clicking on following link. https://myaccess.kone.com/identityiq/home.jsf 2. Open the user’s identity in MyAccess (Identities -> Identity Warehouse) 3. Search the user by entering WorkerID and click on the Application accounts 4. Expand the drop-down option of the workday 5. Check for the LAST_DAY_OF_WORK attribute of the user, it will be the past date due to which the user has been disabled in Workday and he cannot login If the user is disabled in Workday and Active directory, please share below content to the requestor. Hi ( User Name), We have verified in MyAccess that the user’s (email id) end date is in the past in Workday , therefore account has been automatically disabled in Workday. Could you please ask user's manager to update the LAST_DAY_OF_WORK to future date in Workday by going through link " https://kone.portal.dovetailnow.com/ " via "Links to HR Tools" header. Post that, user will be activated. If user’s manager is facing any issues in updating LAST_DAY_OF_WORK in Workday, please contact AskHR@kone.com to update the attribute. Note: Incase user is active in Workday and disabled in AD only, route the ticket to MyAccess team for checking the reason for disabled AD account. It is possible that AD Account is disabled by CyberSecurity on purpose. MyAccess team will validate the reason for AD account disabling and enable the AD account if it is not disabled on purpose. Disable user/ User deactivation If the user is active in Workday and Active directory and needs to be disabled, please share below content to the requestor. Hi (User Name), We have verified in MyAccess that the user is still active in Workday. If the user needs to be disabled, please ask user's manager to update the LAST_DAY_OF_WORK to current/today’s date in Workday by going through link " https://kone.portal.dovetailnow.com/ " via "Links to HR Tools" header. Post that, user will be deactivated. If user’s manager is facing any issues in updating LAST_DAY_OF_WORK in Workday, please contact AskHR@kone.com to update the attribute.

short_description: Create new shared Account/Edit shared account
introduction: Managing existing shared account in MyAccess
instructions: Create new shared Account/Edit shared account It is against KONE Information Security Policy to create Shared accounts unless systems or applications cannot operate without them. To create Shared account, user should have “MyAccess: Shared Account Manager” access in MyAccess. The Access will be approved only for Service and Delivery managers. Before you can start creating shared account, you need request role: MyAccess: Shared Account Manager This role allows to create and manage shared accounts in MyAccess. All role requests will go for IAM team's approval. Please fill in Business reason in comments field. Access will be granted only to Service managers and Delivery managers. Create Shared Account For Creating new shared account follow below process: 1. Go to https://myaccess.kone.com and click on the navigation icon in the upper left corner. 2. Click Manage Identity and Create AD Account 3. Options are shown to select as shown below: 4. Select option Shared Account and click create. 5. Form opens with all below fields 6. If the user has the role, the user is presented with an account creation form with fields to be filled out such as – Type: Read only field, has Shared account as default value. Account Name Please note that the account name will be generated as per “Name of system/Application” and “Name Extension” field values entered like SHR_appname_nameext. This account name given in field. It should be unique across existing samaccountnames. Name of the System/Application (required field) Please note that the account name will be generated with “SHR_” + the name of the system/application. It does not accept _T1, _T2, _T3 or _ADM. Name Extension If “SHR_” + name of the system/application already exist. Please add some identifier like running number, letter or extension word. Account Usage (required field) drop down to be selected CMDB Business Application Name (required field) refers to the CMDB Business application name which is to be selected from the available dropdown list. Valid from (required field) refers to date since when account is created. Valid to (required field) refers to the date until when the account will be active. Shared account will expire and get disabled after this date if no actions are taken by the account owners to extend the validity period. You can select a date maximum 13 months in future. Description of the Account (required field) Please describe what the account is used for. Owner (required field) is the primary owner of the account. You can optionally provide more than one owner for the shared account. If you do so, at least one of the owners must be a KONE employee. In this case, the password will be delivered to all the owners. Deputy owner (required field) is the other owner of the account who will also be able to update the account during its life cycle. You can optionally provide more than one deputy owner for the shared account. If you do so, at least one of the deputy owners must be a KONE employee. Allowed Logon Computers is a mandatory field. Please specify the allowed logon computer(s)(Servers) for the account. If the account is used only in the cloud environment, kindly update the Account Usage to Cloud and add "DummyComputer" as the allowed logon computer. If "DummyComputer" is added as allowed logon computer, then the account cannot login to any domain joined servers. If the account needs to login to domain joined server(s), please add those server(s) as allowed logon computers. 7. On filling up the fields of form, user need to click ‘Next’ button on right corner. This will present a read only form with entered details for different fields. To make any changes to field values, user need to click ‘Back’ button on left corner. If entered details are correct, then user need to click ‘Submit’ button on right corner of UI page. 8. On successful submission of the request, a message will be shown on the screen as below and the account will be created within 5 minutes time. 9. Account Owner will next receive an email notification “New Shared Account Created “ with account details and instructions for password reset. Edit Shared Account For existing Shared account updates, owner/deputy owner of the shared account need to follow below steps: - Go to https://myaccess.kone.com and click on the navigation icon in the upper left corner. - Click Manage Identity and Edit AD Account - Form will appear requesting you to select the account that you need to update/edit. The drop down will show you only the accounts that you are owner or deputy owner of. - After selecting the account, click Edit - A new form will appear with the account details, and you will be able to update the account description, CMDB Business Application Name (refers to the CMDB Business application name which is to be selected from the available dropdown list), Valid to -date and the owner/deputy owner of the account. You can optionally provide more than one owner or deputy owner for the shared account. If you do so, at least one of the owners/deputy owners must be a KONE employee. - After you are ready with the changes, click Confirm. - On successful submission of the request, a message will be shown on the screen as below and the account will be updated within 30 minutes time. NOTE: Warning messages before Shared account will be disabled MyAccess will send multiple warnings to both Owner and Deputy Owner before account is disabled so that the owners will have enough time to go extend the validity if needed. The warnings are being sent 60, 30, 14, 7 and 1 day(s) before the validity of the account ends. If the account is not extended 30 days before expiration, in addition to the account owner and deputy owners, corresponding CMDB application owners also will receive the notification to ensure the accounts are extended on time. The notification will look as below.

short_description: Teams Calling dial pad missing
introduction: I am a Teams Calling user but cannot see the dial pad in my Teams application.
instructions: If you are a Teams Calling user but cannot see your Teams Calling dial pad in your Teams desktop or mobile app (Calls section), please follow the below guidelines to fix the issue. If the issue persists, please contact Ask IT. Despite the issue, you should be able to use Teams Calling through Teams web application (browser) available on teams.microsoft.com. A. Go to the Microsoft Remote Connectivity Analyzer on https://testconnectivity.microsoft.com/tests/TeamsDialpadMissing/input and perform the Dial Pad test by filling in the needed information Sign in with your KONE credentials Write the verification characters and select “Verify” Acknowledge and select “Perform Test” B. If the test results tell that you should see dial pad in your Teams app (like in the below picture), please proceed with the next steps. If you receive an error in the test, please contact Ask IT for help C. If your Teams desktop is still running, right-click the Teams icon in the taskbar and select Quit D. Open the Run dialogue box by pressing the Windows logo key + R. In the Run dialog box, enter %appdata%\Microsoft\Teams, and then select OK E. Delete all files and folders in the %appdata%\Microsoft\Teams directory F. Restart your Teams application and check the dial pad is visible. If the issue persists, please contact Ask IT for help

instructions: It’s only applicable to US and Canada Suggested Fix: Solution: Steps to be followed are as below: 1. Check if the end user details like display name, Kone id and username are provided in the incoming ticket along with the information about the desired company code or branch for the access. Also, check if the access is requested in which environment i.e. production or acceptance or both, if nothing is given it is production. If needed ask the user for the necessary details. 2. Open Cloud hierarchy (Hierarchy Admin Tool - 1) 3. After connecting to the desired environment, select the application as Mobile D-Board from the dropdown as shown in the screenshot below: 4. Now, check for the branch code for which access is requested in the ticket received. Find (Ctrl+F) and search branch code then click on the respective code and then further click on ‘Select’ button. The highlighted part in the image is the branch for which the access has to be provided. Whereas the rest in the image are work centers and must not provide access by selecting the work centers. 5. Click on Add Users button located on the upper right side of the window seen here. 6. Click on Browse / Import: While clicking on Import: i. Enter the Last Name of the user and Click Find if you click on Import. Import will get values from active directory. If you choose to use the Import button and the name has too many entries, the application will crash and recommend to use a different way of adding user. Select the user from the results and click OK. While clicking on Browse: ii. Enter the Name of the user and Click Find First if you click on Browse. B rowse will get values from the database. Select the user from the results and click OK. 7. Select the specified Access level which is provided by user. B ranch user access is provided if it is mentioned by the user. 8. If users need access to all the branches select root. And if user wants all of CKE or CKQ or MKO then select CKE and provide access which will provide access to all the CKE branches and same for MKO and CKQ. 9. Click File -> Refresh then you’ll be able to see the added end user’s id in the respective group or branch . 10. Finally, inform the ticket owner from teams/ personal mail id that the access is granted and ask for confirmation before marking the ticket as resolved. 11. To Remove access of user click on check box of previously provided access / user specified access. 12. When the tick mark turns into a red check mark means the authorization is not only removed but revoked at that level and any level subordinate of it. Removing the marks completely to an empty box removes that authorization only. Removing all marks in the row of a user will completely remove the user from that authorized area when the window (database) is refreshed. 13. Finally, inform the ticket owner from teams/ personal mail id that the access is removed and ask for confirmation before marking the ticket as resolved.

short_description: Shared Mailbox access Delegate by Mailbox Owner
introduction: How to Delegate Shared mailbox access by Mailbox Owner? How to Delegate Calendar Permission? How to Access Shared Mailbox Access in Outlook? How to Access Shared Mailbox Access to OWA?
instructions: How to delegate permission on shared mailbox \User mailbox by Mailbox Owner. Outlook for Microsoft 365 Outlook 2021 Outlook 2019 Outlook 2016 Outlook 2013 Mailbox Owners can add the permission by following these steps and members can add a secondary mailbox if you have been granted the appropriate permissions to their mailbox or shared mailbox. Consider these scenarios: A manager needs an assistant to monitor email on his or her behalf. In this scenario, an assistant that has been granted the appropriate permissions to a manager’s mailbox can view the manager’s mailbox. A manager needs an assistant to monitor a specific folder on his or her mailbox. In this scenario, an assistant that has been granted the appropriate permissions to a folder in a manager’s mailbox can view only the contents of that folder of the manager’s mailbox. The steps in this article assume that you have already been granted the appropriate permissions to the other person’s mailbox. A delegate user in Outlook is a user that is granted permission by another Outlook user to act on his/her behalf. A delegate user is one that can send and reply to messages on behalf of another Outlook user. A delegate automatically receives Send on Behalf permissions. For Outlook Calendar By default, the delegate can read only your meeting requests and responses. The delegate isn’t granted permission to read other messages in your Inbox. Messages sent with Send on Behalf \ Send as permissions include both the delegate's and your name next to from on the email header. Steps to delegate Send on Behalf \ Sent As Permission to manager outlook (Email, Calendar) Open Outlook on your PC Click the File tab Click Account Settings Click Delegate Access Click Add - If Add is greyed out, an active connection might not exist between Outlook and Exchange (see the Outlook Status Bar for Connection Status) Type the name of the person whom you want to designate as your delegate, or search for and then click the name in the search result list. Select the delegate name and click Add , and then click OK Note: The delegate must be a person in your organization's Exchange Global Address List (GAL) In the Delegate Permissions dialog box, accept the default permission settings or select custom access levels for Calendar, Tasks, Inbox, Contacts, and Notes. Note : If a delegate needs permission to work only with meeting requests and responses, the default permission settings, such as Delegate receiving copies of meeting-related messages sent to me , are sufficient. You can leave the Inbox permission setting at None . Meeting requests and responses will go directly to the delegate's inbox. Click OK Joe Blogs has successfully been granted Delegate Access. The Delegate user will then receive the Mailbox will be automatically added to the outlook profile. Note : if the mailbox is not added, Please follow the manual process to add the secondary mailbox. How to add a secondary mailbox to your outlook profile Navigate to the File­ tab in Outlook. In the Info tab press Account settings->Account settings. Highlight the account with access to the shared mailbox and press Change. Press More settings Go to the Advanced tab and press Add Enter the name or address of the shared mailbox and press OK. Then press Apply->OK->Next->Finish and go back to your main Outlook window. You should now see the shared mailbox in the overview to the left of the Outlook window. Note: When the user wants to access the secondary mailbox only Web version, Please follow the below steps: How to add secondary mailbox in Outlook Web Application (OWA) If you do not want to add the other person’s mailbox to your folder list in Outlook Web App, you can open the other person’s mailbox in a separate browser window. Log in to your mailbox using Outlook Web App by using https://outlook.office.com/mail/ In the Navigation bar on the top of the Outlook Web App screen, click on your name. A drop-down list will appear. Click Open another mailbox . Type the email address of the other mailbox that you want to open, and click Open . The other person’s mailbox opens in a separate Outlook Web App browser window. Note: If you have only been provided access to specific folders in the other user’s mailbox, you will only see the folders to which you have been granted access.
