short_description: Redirect URL with Azure Application gateway - QPR
introduction: How to redirect URL with Azure Application from QPR legacy environment to SaaS environment
instructions: Background of this arrangement: QPR was running in KONE cloud from the address ( http://kwprocesses.konenet.com/QPR/Portal ). We migrated QPR On-Prem to the SaaS environment, Our old legacy documents which are in PDM, sharepoints, etc., are referring to the old URL. This is indeed needed to redirect the links to the new external SaaS URL https://kone.onqpr.com/ Application Gateway in Azure which listens in IP address 10.130.31.4. In addition there’s a redirection rule for external site, https://kone.onqpr.com in this case. You can simulate this locally on your workstation by either adding a record to your host’s file. Or by simply using http://10.130.31.4 instead of http://kwprocesses.konenet.com . For example, Teemu’s example below -> http://10.130.31.4/QPR/Portal/document/ID344 -> you can see it’s neatly redirected to https://kone.onqpr.com (although the resource itself doesn’t exist anymore). Please test this yourself and the permanent solution is to change the current kwprocesses.konenet.com DNS record to point to the Azure Application Gateway which does the redirect. Please also note that we found references directly to the server’s A record in DNS, like in the SharePoint quick links. This can be tackled by also changing the server’s A record to point to Azure Application Gateway for redirecting.

short_description: Work Order completed in FSM but only Partially Confirmed (PCNF) in SAP.
introduction: How to restore service order status consistency between FSM and SAP in scenario where a Work Order is Completed in FSM but it’s only Partially Confirmed (PCNF) in SAP, instead being Technically Completed (TECO)?
instructions: Details of the scenario: FSM side: Work Order header status is Completed, but there is at least one Work Order Line Item (Operation) which is in any other status than Completed, Canceled SAP side: Corresponding Service order is NOT in TECO status, only PCNF. There are no SAP side processing errors for this order in SAP backreporting monitoring cockpit. Reason why the order is only PCNF and not TECO: When SAP processes the backreporting request, it detects that not all Work Order Line Items (Operations) are Completed (or Canceled). Thus, SAP treats this as so called Partial Backreporting. This is correct and expected behavior from SAP. There are two known scenraios why this can occur. User of the FSM Web UI has manually updated the Work Order header to Completed, without checking that all line items are Completed or Canceled. We have an occurrence of a technical issue where the SAP to Salesforce interface user (KONE Interface) has re-opened a line item which has already once been Completed by a user. The root cause for this technical issue is being investigated in a problem ticket PR00014860 at the time this knowledge article is written. Procedure to get these orders updated to TECO status in SAP Business representative must first make the confirmation to judge if all work has been completed in the real work. If this is the case, the business user must also update all Work Order Line Items (operations) to Completed or Canceled status. To re-trigger a new backreporting processing request to SAP: Erase the current value from the Work Order field “Notify SAP” and save the Work Order. Manually populate a value BACKREP in the Work Order field “Notify SAP” and save the Work Order. This triggers a new backreporting processing request to SAP. SAP will process the newly sent backreporting processing request the next time the scheduled backreporting job is executed. Because all Work Order Line Items are now in Completed / Canceled status in FSM, the corresponding Service Order will now be updated to TECO status in SAP.

short_description: How to use a shared mailbox in Outlook on the web and Client application
introduction: How to use a shared mailbox in Outlook on the web and Client application?
instructions: Pre-requisites · To access Shared mailbox user requires Full access to the particular Shared Mailbox Note: AskIT team can assist users to follow the below steps to access Shared Mailbox. In case of any issues ticket can be routed to CUST.KONE.INT.SD queue. The below steps can be suggested to users who already have access to the shared mailbox. A. Add the shared mailbox so it displays under your primary mailbox If you want to monitor the email from your primary mailbox and the shared mailbox at the same time, use this method. After you complete this task, the shared mailbox and its folders are displayed in the left navigation pane each time you open Outlook on the web. OUTLOOK Web App (OWA) Sign in to your account in Outlook on the web (https://outlook.office.com/mail ). For Exchange Online mailboxes, right-click Folders in the left navigation pane, and then choose Add shared folder . For Exchange on-premises mailboxes, right-click the name of your primary mailbox (such as Molly Dempsey) in the left navigation pane, and then choose Add shared folder . 3. In the Add shared folder dialog box, type the name or email address of someone who has shared a mailbox with you, and then click Add . Or, type the name of the shared mailbox you are a member of,(Example: such as info@contoso.com.) The shared mailbox displays in your Folder list in Outlook on the web. You can expand or collapse the shared mailbox folders as you can with your primary mailbox. You also can remove the shared mailbox from your Folder list. To remove it, right-click the shared mailbox, and then click Remove shared folder . Open the shared mailbox in a separate browser tab Use this method if you want to view and manage the email for the shared mailbox in its own browser window. This method also lets you receive notifications of new items in the Inbox of the shared mailbox. Sign in to your account in Outlook on the web. On the Outlook on the web navigation bar, click your name. A list appears. 3. Click Open another mailbox . 4. Type the email address of the other mailbox that you want to open, and then click Open . Another Outlook on the web session opens in a separate window, allowing access to the other mailbox. Tip: If you mistype the email address of the mailbox, the result is "No match was found". Try typing the email address again. Send email from the shared mailbox Choose New mail above the folder list. A new message form opens in the reading pane. At the top of the message, choose > Show From . This option shows you that the people who receive the email message will see it comes from the shared mailbox and not from your own email account. 3. The first time you use the shared mailbox, you need to add the address to the options available in the From drop-down list: a. Right-click the From address and choose Remove . b. Type the shared address. The next time you reply from the shared mailbox, its address will appear as an option in the From drop-down list. To add recipients, type the name of anyone who is in your contacts folder or organization’s address list, or type the email address in the To or Cc box. The first time you enter a person’s name, Outlook on the web searches for that person’s address. In the future, the name is cached so it resolves quickly. Type a subject. Type the message you want to send. When your message is ready to go, click Send . Reply to mail sent to the shared mailbox The first time you reply to a message sent to the shared mailbox, you need to add the shared address to the From drop-down box. Here's how to do that: Open Outlook on the web and go to your shared mailbox. Open a message that was sent to the shared address. Click Reply . At the top of the message, choose > Show From . 5. Right-click the From address and choose Remove . 6. Type the shared address and send the message. The next time you reply from the shared mailbox, its address will appear as an option in the From drop-down list. Use the shared calendar One of the great things about using a shared mailbox is that anyone who has access to the shared mailbox also has full access to the calendar. You can create, edit, and delete events in the calendar. Use the shared calendar just as you would use your own calendar. Here's a short list of things you can do with the shared mailbox calendar: Let your team know when you're going on vacation or when you’ll be out of the office. Create a team meeting. Edit a calendar event that someone else created. Cancel a meeting that someone else created. To access the shared calendar, do the following: Sign in to your mailbox using Outlook on the web, and then select Calendar . Right-click Other Calendars , and then click Open calendar . 3. In From directory , search for the shared calendar you want to open. Select the shared mailbox you want to open, and then click Open . 4. The shared calendar displays in your Calendar folder list. B. Accessing Shared mailbox on Outlook Client application Please note: After your admin has added you as a member of a shared mailbox, close and then restart Outlook. The shared mailbox should automatically display in your Folder pane in Outlook. Folder pane in Outlook. What if it didn't work? § If your admin only recently added you to a shared mailbox, it may take a few minutes before the shared mailbox appears. Wait a bit and then close and restart Outlook again. § If that didn't work, then manually add the shared mailbox to Outlook: a. Open Outlook. b. Choose the File tab in the ribbon. c. Choose Account Settings , then select Account Settings from the menu. d. Select the Email tab. e. Make sure the correct account is highlighted, then choose Change . f. Choose More Settings > Advanced > Add . g. Type the shared email address, such as info@contoso.com. h. Choose OK > OK . i. Choose Next > Finish > Close . Send mail from the shared mailbox 1. Open Outlook. 2. Choose New Email . 3. If you don't see the From field at the top of your message, choose Options > From . 4. Click From in the message, and change to the shared email address. If you don't see your shared email address, choose Other email address and then type in the shared email address. 5. Choose OK . 6. Finish typing your message and then choose Send . From now on when you want to send a message from your shared mailbox, the address will be available in your From drop down list. Whenever you send a message from your shared mailbox, your recipients will only see the shared email address in the message. Reply to mail sent to the shared mailbox 1. Open Outlook. 2. Open the email message you want to reply to. 3. From field at the top of your message, you should see the name of your shared mailbox. If you don't, choose the From dropdown box and choose your shared mailbox. 4. Finish typing your message and then choose Send . When you want to send a message from your shared mailbox, the address will be available in your From drop down list. Whenever you send a message from your shared mailbox, your recipients will only see the shared email address in the message. Use the shared calendar After your admin has added you as a member of shared mailbox, close and then restart Outlook. The shared calendar associated with the shared mailbox is automatically added to your Calendars list. 1. In Outlook go to calendar view, and choose the shared mailbox. 2. When you enter appointments, everyone who is a member of the shared mailbox will be able to see them. 3. Any member of the shared mailbox can create, view, and manage appointments on the calendar, just like they would their personal appointments. Everyone who is a member of shared mailbox can see their changes to the shared calendar.

short_description: ASK IT - Enabling Teams Recording Option
introduction: How to check and assist user in enabling Teams recording option?
instructions: Step 1: Teams recording feature will be enabled by default for all E5 users. Step2: Conditions under which a user with Teams Recording feature enabled can record a session: 1> User should be the host of the meeting and it should be a scheduled meeting. 2>User is in a scheduled meeting where the host of the meeting is also having his/her teams recording option enabled. Step 3: How to start Teams recording 1. Start or join the meeting. 2.In your meeting controls, select More actions > Record and transcribe > Start recording . Step 4: How to stop Teams recoding 1.In the meeting controls, select More actions > Record and transcribe . 2.Choose one of the following: (i) Stop recording to stop the recording and live transcription. short_description: Create new configuration version issue - TACO
introduction: How to troubleshoot the taco plan for network states Failed and a new version cannot be created issue?
instructions: Step 1 : Search the network in the TACO and click "configuration history" Try to click "create new configuration verison" option. and If the network state "Failed" showed And if it shows below Error Message : This is one of those double value cases issue. NOTE : General issue should be fixed when next SAP release. Workaround : Step 1 : Go to trace.kone.com Step 2 : Search Network number ->components ->characteristics -> WGT_RATED_LOAD_Q -> Check the correct value Example correct value " 680" Step 4: Go to varipdm and check if you can remove the false value. Remove the wrong value "800" in WGT_RATED_LOAD-Q order has this in "parameters" Step 5 : Update correct value and save it Then, it should try to generate new version . If that is not happening, reconfiguration needs to be started manually and you need to check that order creates new configuration and is in state "Done" might fail again => rinse and repeat Now network state "Done" Note : The user to release the order as quick as possible ! Next SAP import will overwrite the changes and order will be failing again. Disclaimer : Parameters are "bussiness data" so, one really needs to know what to do when manipulating it. in trace portal I know that data is most current and correct, that is why it is important to check correct value before removing another value.

short_description: How to fill out the mandate information required for "Application packaging request Form"
introduction: How to fill out the mandate information required for "Application packaging request Form"
instructions: Note: - Please ensure that application details, including the product owner and version number, are updated in the CMDB before submitting the Application Request Form. The MECM/Intune team does not have access to ServiceNow CMDB to update application details. In ServiceNow, we have a SR form named as " Application packaging request Form " that must be completed by the appropriate application owner if you need to package KONE-approved software and publish it in Intune. Below is a quick overview of some mandatory information required fields: Application Packaging Information: Requester Name and Email id: Requester Department: Application Name: Application Version: Request (Upgrade / New): Application Priority (Urgent / Normal): License Information If Any: Dependencies and Pre-Requisites Information: Installation Media \ Setup Files Location: Installation Instructions with screen shots if possible: Any specific customizations are required please provide details: Application Manufacturer Name: Business Group Phone Number and Email Address of Application owner: Desktop model (Win 10 or Win 11): Deployment type (Available or Required): Any specific User Defined Tests needed: Note to the key information required: 1) Upgrade Information: Please let us know whether we need to handle the upgrade of the older versions already exist in the machine from the packaging side or through AD group. 2) Install Instructions: Please provide us with customizations details that needs to handle in the package along with the screen shots. 3) Unless specific co-existence problems have been reported, it will not be possible to test co-existence between new packages and other Customer applications. 4) Installation Media \ Setup Files Location: It is the location from where the team can get the installation media or files to create the package 5) Packaging will only start once all the required information for package and Installation Media \ Setup files is provided to us. 6) After analyzing the application, it will be decided whether it is SIMPLE, MEDIUM or COMPLEX category. Note: To understand how application requests work, refer to the Application Request Guide (Check Attachment).

short_description: Cybersecurity Major Incident Management Process
introduction: This KBA purpose is to understand the Cybersecurity Major Incident Management process which is aligned together with KONE SIAM team and ASKIT.
instructions: The purpose of the document is to understand Cybersecurity Major Incident Management Process with MIM team Process followed to initiate MIM for Cybersecurity Incidents: Approach that can be taken by Cybersecurity Operations team (Deloitte/CyberOps L1 /KONE CyberOps L2/KONE Cybersecurity Operations) to trigger a MIM process. The Major Incidents of all the tribes/portfolios are managed by KONE SIAM team. If the issue is deemed to be a Major incident, please propose an incident in the ServiceNow tool and contact KONE SIAM/ASKIT over call and ask them to invoke MIM process. Refer to KBA KB000A7127 for the detailed MIM process. Please note: Any ITIL user who has access to the ServiceNow tool can report a Major Incident. It’s NOT mand atory that incidents should be routed via ASK IT to reach MI team. https://kone.sharepoint.com/sites/ITCommunications2/Shared%20Documents/Forms/AllItems.aspx?id=%2Fsites%2FITCommunications2%2FShared%20Documents%2FGet%20support%2FAsk%20IT%20country%20phone%20numbers%2Epdf&parent=%2Fsites%2FITCommunications2%2FShared%20Documents%2FGet%20support&p=true&ga=1 Note to ASKIT: Since the Incident is reported from Security team, MIM Template details could be collected on the call which shouldn’t cause further delay. MIM Team Contact Details: - Name (KONE MIM) Email Address Work Number Sravan Kumar Garjala garjalaa.sravankumar@kone.com +91 7824865551 Sankar M sankaranarayanan.m@kone.com +91 7824865552 Vivek M vivek.matheswaran@kone.com +91 7824865553 Caroline S caroline.sylviya@kone.com +91 7824865554 Steps to be followed when the Security Incident is reported from End user or KONE Stakeholder: AskIT: - End user / KONE Stakeholder can report Security Incident to AskIT directly. If ASKIT requires support to confirm the reported issue is security Incident or not, ASKIT can reach out to security@kone.com for confirming if the reported security issue will quality to raise MIM or to justify this the reported issue is Security related or IT related. If qualified for MIM, AskIT will reach out to MIM team by phone ( even its single user reported ) followed by Email with MIM Template attached to check whether incident should be treated as P1/P2. Multiple users reporting Security Incident to AskIT will be considered as P1/P2 without any delay and MIM process will be followed by AskIT ITIL user. MIM Team: - Here are the contacts from Cybersecurity Operations team. MIM team can reach out to any of these three contacts for the support from Cybersecurity team for major incidents where security team intervention is needed. Email address Role Contact Number jeyakarlmarx.j@kone.com Cybersecurity Operations - Analyst +917358197947 mohamedthameemansare.m@kone.com Cybersecurity Operations – Senior Analyst +918939490450 v.sathishkumar@kone .com Associate Lead Security Engineer - Cybersecurity Operations +918056950375 vishnu.mageswaransubhadra@kone.com Cybersecurity Operations - Analyst +919363011563 Cybersecurity L2 Operations team will reach out to Global team for any support or invention required to confirm whether reported Incident qualifies as Priority Incident (P1/P2) Joona Romppanen Särökaari Niklas Thomas Lakofski MIM Team will initiate MIM bridge and will involve all the stakeholders to drive the incident towards closure to mitigate the risk at hand. Impacted Service\Impacted Application to be mapped with appropriate Service and Application impacted and the queue should be KONE.KTI.Cybersecurity.Operations MIM team will only send Security incident communication via Email Notification, Global Outage notification and X-matters . No security incident communication to be done on Yammer and Remedy force broadcast until further instructions are received from KONE Communications team and Cybersecurity team. For MIM Notification purpose - Cybersecurity team will review and approve the information which must be shared by MIM team The Recipients list should be common which includes the Business IT team and impacted Infra or Application specific stakeholders for a normal Security Incident which has no major impact. If the Incident is more critical which might cause reputational damage or financial loss, the recipient list will be reviewed by KONE Communications team and KONE Cybersecurity team. KONE Cybersecurity Operations team: - The business stakeholders should receive a MI initial bridge invite(assessment) email from "kone.mimanager@kone.com” if does not happen within 10 minutes, take the actions listed below. CyberOps team will reach out to below contacts for first level escalation. KONE MIM Team (To be used only for Escalation) Poornima Krishnamoorthy poornima.krishnamoorthy@kone.com +91 9791042088 MIM related communications and updates should be sent every 30 mins to the agreed recipient list. Activities and Responsibilities of different roles during MIM: ACTIVITY RESPONSIBLE ENTITY OUTCOME Malicious activity/Security threat is detected or indicated within KONE infrastructure. CYBEROPS Activity is identified as Security Incident causing business impact. Declaration of threat level based on the severity of the situation CYBEROPS Threat level is declared. Immediate response activities to prevent spread of the malicious activity. CYBEROPS ISOLATE, BLOCK, REMOVE, RESET Heads up for ITOPS CYBEROPS CYBEROPS gives heads up for ITOPS. Further activities and escalations are based on the threat level. CYBEROPS Per needs: Establish war room, communicate with key stakeholders R&D, ITOps, ITLead, BizLead, Legal, Global Comms. HIGH/CRITICAL level of threat. CYBEROPS War room protocol is initiated to ensure rapid reactions and situational awareness. BizLead and ITLead are expected to participate immediately for decision making based on operational picture CyberOps and ITOps working closely to contain situation and to deliver regular updates to operational picture Heads up for other key stakeholders. Start preparation of Incident Brief. CYBEROPS Incident Brief is used to communicate situation with various stakeholders. It is a living document and, in the end, (when incident is over and autopsy is being performed) shall contain overview of the incident. FIRST HOUR PROCEDURES (RESPONSE + ANALYSIS) PARALLEL ACTIVITIES PER STAKEHOLDER BODY ACTIVITY RESPONSIBLE ENTITY OUTCOME Assign ‘hands on’ deck to work with CYBEROPS when requested by CYBEROPS. Legal, BizLead & Global Comms., are given heads up. ITOPS SOLUTIONS LEGAL BIZLEAD GLOBAL COMMS SME’s are assigned to assist/work with CYBEROPS to contain on-going attack. Heads up status for other key stakeholders. Hands on work.

short_description: Ask IT - Guide to requesting for MathCad authorization via MyAccess
introduction: Guide to requesting for MathCad authorization via MyAccess
instructions: Guide to requesting for MathCad authorization via MyAccess Mathcad installation and authorization to use license is arranged via MyAccess . There are the below 4 Mathcad roles available in MyAccess. · Mathcad: 15 Global · Mathcad: 15 Finland · Mathcad: Prime Global · Mathcad: Prime Finland Please note that Mathcad 15 is already discontinued by PTC, and will no longer be available in KONE. The PTC announcement is available in the below link - https://www.mathcad.com/-/media/Files/PDFs/Mathcad/End-of-Sale-Mathcad-FAQ-2.pdf Currently, Mathcad 15 licenses are provided for a limited time period so that teams can transition from Mathcad 15 to Mathcad Prime in a phased manner. The guidance for new Mathcad users would be to use Mathcad Prime as much as possible, and request for Mathcad 15 license only after due consideration. There is also a geographical criteria to decide on which MyAccess role to opt for. If you are located in Finland, then please opt for one of the below 2 Finland MyAccess roles. · Mathcad: 15 Finland · Mathcad: Prime Finland If you are located outside Finland, then please opt for one of the below 2 Global MyAccess roles. · Mathcad: 15 Global · Mathcad: Prime Global The instructions on how to request for the role in MyAccess is given below. 1. Navigate to MyAccess portal by clicking the below link - https://myaccess.kone.com/ 2. This portal is accessible from the Public Internet and uses the Single Sign-on for authentication. a. If you are not already authenticated, then this portal would ask you to provide authentication details. b. Please provide your KONE email address and password. You might need to also provide second level of authentication using your phone, if you have Multi Factor Authentication activated. 3. Click on “Manage My Access” a. If you have the permission to raise requests for other users too, then you would need click on the button “Manage User Access”, and then select your username ( which would be the first item in the list ). 5. Type “MathCad” in the “Search Access” textbox, and click the button 6. You would see the below search results. 7. Select the needed role, and click on the button to the left of the access level or role 8. Click on the “Next” button. 9. MyAccess would show the requested access level. Click on “Submit” button to raise the access request. 10. MyAccess would send a notification via mail with the request details. 11. The request would go to the line manager 12. After the approval cycle is completed, the user would be added to the appropriate user groups. 13. MyAccess would automatically initiate the download of the corresponding application package via Software Center. ( It is expected that the target machine is in KONE network for the installation package download to be completed ) 14. Please contact AskIT ( AskIT@kone.com ) if you do not get the Mathcad software installed after a week of getting the MyAccess approved. Note: The Software Center has been offering both Mathcad 7.0 and 9.0 versions. It is important to install the version that meets your specific needs. In order to use both versions, you must first install the lower version of Mathcad 7.0 and then higher version of Mathcad 9.0 15. Please contact AskIT ( AskIT@kone.com ) if you do not get the Mathcad software installed after a week of getting the MyAccess approved.

short_description: Access shared mailbox in Outlook Web Application and outlook application
introduction: How to access shared mailbox in Outlook Web App and outlook application?
instructions: Introduction This KBA guides end user on How to access shared mailbox in Outlook Web Application and outlook application Prerequisites You must be granted access to the shared mailbox (Full Access permission). For Outlook Desktop, the mailbox may take up to 60 minutes to appear automatically after access is granted. Procedure: 1. Accessing a Shared Mailbox via Outlook Web App (OWA) Option A: Open Shared Mailbox in the Same Browser Session Go to https://outlook.office.com Sign in with your KONE email account In the top‑right corner, select your profile picture or initials Click “Open another mailbox” Enter the shared mailbox email address (for example: sharedmailbox@kone.com) Click Open ✅ The shared mailbox opens in a new browser tab 2. Accessing a Shared Mailbox via Outlook Desktop (Windows) Option A: Automatic Mapping (Default Behavior) If you have Full Access permission: Close Outlook completely Reopen Outlook after 30–60 minutes The shared mailbox should appear automatically in the left‑hand folder pane ✅ No manual steps required Option B: Manually Adding the Shared Mailbox in Classic Outlook If the shared mailbox does not appear automatically: Open Outlook Go to File → Account Settings → Account Settings Select your email account and click Change Click More Settings Go to the Advanced tab Under Open these additional mailboxes , click Add Enter the shared mailbox email address Click OK → Next → Finish Restart Outlook ✅ The shared mailbox will now appear in Outlook Option B: Manually Adding the Shared Mailbox in New Outlook Open New Outlook In the left navigation pane , scroll down to Folders Right‑click Folders Select Add shared folder or mailbox Enter the shared mailbox email address Example: sharedmailbox@kone.com Click Add ✅ The shared mailbox will appear in the left folder pane

short_description: How to install and use Password Manager (KeePass)
introduction: How to store passwords in a safe and secured manner ? Follow the KBA on how to install Password Manager (Keepass) to save passwords in a secured manner.
instructions: Introduction: KeePass is a password manager, which helps you to manage your passwords in a secure way. You can store all your passwords in one database, which is locked with a master key. So, you only have to remember one single master key to unlock the whole database. Database files are encrypted using the best and most secure encryption algorithms. How to get KeePass Installed: KeePass is made available to all Computers and Laptops. To install click on Start -> Type – Software Center and Open it. Search for “KeePass” as shown in the below screenshot. Click to open and install the KeePass On Successful installation, Open the KeePass and create a new Database as shown in the below diagram. Click on Ok Select your OneDrive and Create a New Folder and provide a name for the KeePass Database. Click on Save to finish. Enter a Master Password and confirm the Password. Click OK to finish. Provide a Name for Example: KeePass-DB and Click OK Click Print if you want to keep a copy of the KeePass emergency Sheet which has the path of the Database or Skip it if not required. This will finish the KeePass Database setup. If you lose the database file or any of the master key components (or forget the composition), all data stored in the database is lost. KeePass does not have any built in file backup functionality. There is no backdoor and no universal key that can open your database. To create and save a password either click on the marked icon in the below screen shot or right click on the empty pane. Select Add Entry. Enter the Title, Name, Password and Re-enter Password. Provide a Notes about the credential. Click Ok to save. Example 1 : In this example we will see how to save the credential for a T2 account To Retrieve and use the Password, Right click on the Account Name, select copy Username or Copy Password as shown below and use it for authentication. Example 2 : In this example we will see how to save the credential for a Gmail account Enter the Title, Name, Password and Re-enter Password, Enter URL if any. Provide a Notes about the credential. Click Ok to save. To Retrieve and use the Password, Right click on the Account Name, select copy Username or Copy Password as shown below and use it for authentication. Example 3: In this example we will see how to save the credential for a Facebook account Enter the Title, Name, Password and Re-enter Password, Enter URL if any. Provide a Notes about the credential. Click Ok to save. To Retrieve and use the Password, Right click on the Account Name, select copy Username or Copy Password as shown below and use it for authentication.

short_description: Create or Modify Jira "Release" in Spaces
introduction: How to Create or Modify Jira "Release" in Spaces
instructions: Procedure to Create or Modify Jira "Release" in Projects : 1. Create an API token in Jira : NOTE : Once the API Token in Jira created, after using it for the Release action, Revoke API Token ( delete it). Click on Revoke option In the confirmation pop-up menu, Click on Revoke You will get a message like this Next time when doing similar action you can create a new one by following the below steps 2. Get Admin rights at the Space level : NOTE : Once created or modified the Release in Jira, Kindly remove yourselves from the Admin rights at the Project level in Jira. Next time when doing Release action you can add yourself Admin rights on Project level in Jira by following the below steps NOTE : When we click on the Space Settings >> issues >> Types and drop down Action then touch Edit issue types , we can see the pid = project id 3. Precheck the Input File : The input file as to be submitted by the User via Service Catalog form - Login to Service Now ESC( https://koneprod.service-now.com/esc ) -> search for Jira -> In the Service Catalog form select Create or Modify Jira "Release" in Projects The Excel Template for Input file is provide in the Service Catalog form. For more details you can go the link Teams Channel - Jira Community's team channel 4. Run the script : Recommended Python version for this Jira release activity is Python 3.9 For easy UI download PyCharm 3.9 version In Kone Software Center we only have 2. version of Python, hence download 3.9 version Python and Pycharm 3. version from google. Kindly follow the below steps to perform Jira Release: In the script there is a line - " import requests ". For this line to work proper we have to install the "requests" module. NOTE : If the "requests" module is not installed we will get the below error: Error Image : Kindly follow the below steps to install the "requests" module: Search Python in Windows search and click on Run as Administrator Run the command " python -m pip install requests " Note : You can check the Python version your having by running the command py --version After the request module is executed the script will run properly CREATE AN API TOKEN IN JIRA Kindly follow the below steps to create an API Token in Jira Step 1 : Click on your Profile icon on your top right corner and select Manage account . You will be directed to a new window. Select Security tab, scroll down and select the option Create and manage API tokens Step 2 : Click Manage your account . Step 3 : Select Security Tab and click on Create and manage API tokens option. Step 4 : Under API token , click on Create API token button Step 5 : Give a label/ short name for the API token to remember and click on Create . Step 6 : Click on Copy to copy the token and save it, once closed, this token will not be visible to us Step 7 : You will receive a mail like this after creating the API token Step 8: You can find the API token here ADMIN RIGHTS ON PROJECT LEVEL IN JIRA Kindly follow the below steps to add yourself Admin rights on Project level in Jira: Step 1 : Login to Jira , and Click on Settings icon and Select Issues option Step 2 : Search for Permissions , and click on Permission Schemes Step 3 : Under Default software scheme select Permissions Step 4 : Under Administer Projects select Update Step 5 : Select Single user and search your name. Step 6 : Select your name and click on Update Step 7 : Similarly add yourself as Single User for Browse Projects also. Step 8 : Similarly, Under KONE Permission Scheme Template select Permissions Step 9 : Similarly add yourself as Single User for Administer Projects and Browse Projects under KONE Permission Scheme Template INPUT FILE Input file should be shared by the user and should be .csv extension. If user requests for the input file sample or example, Support team has to provide the Jira Release template to the user. The template file needs to filled in with the data that is needed to be uploaded. Template only contains examples for guidance to fill in the information. Filling in the data to template is responsibility of the requestor/user. If the file is not filled in with proper information, the upload will fail. The documentation, script and templates for upload are available in Confluence: https://kone.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/TOOLS/pages/92636814/Version+Release+upload+tool Jira Release template is found here Jira community - Release Management - All Documents (sharepoint.com) Note : .xlsx – file needs to be converted to .csv prior on using the utility Follow the below steps to ensure the input file is all set up for using. 1. The Python script is built to use semicolon ( ; ) as the delimiter. We will usually have .csv with comma ( , ) as the delimiter. To change to semicolon(;) delimiter follow the below steps: Step 1 : Open the Excel file(.xlsx) and save it as .csv Step 2: Click on File Step 3: Select More -> Options Step 4: Select Advanced -> Uncheck Use system separators check box . Set Decimal separator to comma ( , ) and Set Thousands separator to period ( . ) and click on OK . 2. Date Format should be yyyy-mm-dd . Excel automatically seems to convert the date format to system local, even if data is originally stored as text format. The date format has to be set correctly. NOTE : Even if we follow the below steps and change the data format to yyyy-mm-dd, if we close the file and open it again the date format changes. Hence double check the format, once you change the date format then directly run the script. If you change the format, close and then open it the format changes. So if you run the script, error is thrown Kindly follow the below steps to set the dat format correctly : Step 1 : Select all the date data and click on Number Format drop down and select More Number Formats... Step 2 : Under Number tab select Date tab and select the formal yyyy-mm-dd and click on OK Now you can see the date is changed to the correct format yyyy-mm-dd RUN THE PYTHON SCRIPT Recommended Python version for this Jira release activity is Python 3.9 For easy UI download PyCharm 3.9 version In Kone Software Center we only have 2. version of Python, hence download 3.9 version Python and Pycharm 3. version from google. Step 1 : Create a folder in C Drive(the place where the Python software is there) and move the Input File, Python Script folder to the same Folder. Step 2 : Open the PyCharm -> File -> Open Step 3 : Select the respective .py(Python script) and click OK Step 4 : Give the following details as shown in the below image Step 5 : By clicking the below highlighted icon, RUN the program Step 6 : After running the program, the output will be as below After running the script, an output file Versions_Output.csv will be generated in the folder where the script is located If we open the Versions_Output.csv file, we can see a column " Action" is added. These actions will let us know if there are any errors occurred in the Release update NOTE : Attach the Versions_Output.csv file to the AskIT request once the release is completed and checked by the user ERRORS IN THE OUTPUT SCREEN Errors can be identified directly in the output screen of the script or else we can check it in the Versions_Output.csv file Errors can be identified directly in the output screen Error 1 : If project is not found in Jira, we will get the below error(This error is shown in the output screen of the script) Error 2 : If the input file is not properly set as stated above, the below error is thrown Actions in Versions_Output.csv file Action 1 : If we get , " No need to update, values to be update same as in Jira. Output file values as they are in Jira " - It means the release is already present in the Jira application in the respective projects Action 2 : If we get , " Error creating version. A version with the same name might already exist in the project " - It means this is the duplicate entry in the Input file so the update was not done Action 3 : If we get , " Updated. Output file values as they are in Jira. " - It means the previous version is update Action 4 : If we get , " Created. Output file values as they are in Jira. " - It means the version is created Action 5 : If we get , " ProjectID not found, or user does not have Browse project permissions. " - It means that the project is not found in Jira Action 6 : If we get , " Project not found or user does not have the required permissions " - It means that the project is not found in Jira or the support person might not have required permissions(Project permissions) Action 7 : If we get , " Error updating, user might not have required permissions or request is invalid. "- It means that the support person might not have required permissions(Project permissions) or request is invalid.

short_description: Create and maintain Service account in MyAccess
introduction: Answers to most common questions on Service accounts: MyAccess is the master source for Service accounts. MyAccess is used to create new Service accounts and maintain the life cycle of Service accounts. Service account is to be created only for system, application and services usage and it is never used by a human to login to resources. Each Service account must be mapped to a system in CMDB. Service accounts cannot be used for Console or RDP login. Each Service account must have two owners. If you need to add access to the service account and the access cannot be found from MyAccess, please raise an AskIT ticket to MyAccess support team. In order to update service account, you need to be either the Owner or Deputy Owner of the account: if you need to become the owner, please contact the existing Owners and request them to add you as an owner in MyAccess. If your account is not yet in MyAccess Service Account Management, please reach out to MyAccess Support via AskIT and the team will immediately help to move your account to MyAccess
instructions: Content of the attachment document: Service account creation and management from MyAccess 1.What is Service account? Service account is a non-person identity that has an AD account. Service account is created only for system, application and services usage and it is never used by a human to login to resources . Service account is often used to run a script or software on a server. Main features of service accounts are that they are not internet facing, they are not allowed for Console or RDP login and their passwords are more complex than normally and the password is set not to expire in AD. 2.How do I create a new Service account? Go to https://myaccess.kone.com and click on the navigation icon in the upper left corner. Click Manage Identity and Create AD Account Form will appear. Select Service account and click Create New form will appear for you to provide the details for account creation. Please fill in below details and click Next. Name of the System/Application should be as in CMDB. Please note that the account name will be generated with “SVC_”+ the name of the system/application. Name Extension will become mandatory field if “SVC_” + name of the system/application already exist. Please add some identifier like running number, letter or extension word. Account Usage Account will be used in On Perm AD, Azure AD and Hybrid (OnPrem/Cloud/Both) CMDB Business Application Name refers to the CMDB Business application name which is to be selected from the available dropdown list Country refers to the region where service runs. This is requested merely for technical reasons. Valid from refers to date since when account is created. Valid to refers to the date until when the account will be active. Service account will expire and get disabled after this date if no actions are taken by the account owners to extend the validity period. You can select a date maximum 13 months in future. Description of the Account is mandatory field. Please describe what is the account used for. Owner is the primary owner of the account who will receive the initial service account password. Deputy Owner is the other owner of the account who will be able to also update the account during its life cycle. Allowed Logon Computers is a mandatory field. Please specify the allowed logon computer(s)(Servers) for the account. If the account is used only in the cloud environment, kindly update the Account Usage to Cloud and add "DummyComputer" as the allowed logon computer. If "DummyComputer" is added as allowed logon computer, then the account cannot login to any domain joined servers. If the account needs to login to domain joined server(s), please add those server(s) as allowed logon computers. NOTE: depend on your configuration-if Service account need value LAST_NAME, please raise ticket to MyAccess support. Currently MyAccess user interface is not supporting this and MyAccess support will add the value manually to the AD and exclude the service account from the certain automated tasks in MyAccess. Confirmation page opens for you to review the data before sending. NOTE: When the field (Account Usage) is selected as “Azure AD”, field (Allowed Logon Computers ) should have DummyComputer$ and should not allow the user to select more values NOTE: When the field (Account Usage) is selected other than “Azure AD”, field (Allowed Logon Computers) should allow user to select more values and remove if required If you want to still modify the data, click Back. If not, click Submit. Account Owner will next receive an email notification “New Service Account Created….” with the initial password, account details and instructions for password reset and storage. 3.How do I update my Service account? In order to update service account you need to be either Owner or Deputy Owner of the account. Go to https://myaccess.kone.com and click on the navigation icon in the upper left corner. Click Manage Identity and Edit AD Account Form will appear requesting you to select the account that you need to update/edit. The drop down will show you only the accounts that you are owner or deputy owner of. After selecting the account, click Edit A new form will appear with the account details and you will be able to update the account description, CMDB Business Application Name, Valid to –date, Allowed Logon Computers and the owners of the account. After you are ready with the changes, click Confirm. Changes will be submitted and you will receive notification email “Account changes were processed…” 4.Warning messages before Service account will be disabled MyAccess will send multiple warnings to both Owner and Deputy Owner before account is disabled so that the owners will have enough time to go extend the validity if needed. The warnings are being sent 60, 30, 14, 7 and 1 day(s) before the validity of the account ends. If the account is not extended 30 days before expiration, in addition to the account owner and deputy owners, corresponding CMDB application owners also will receive the notification to ensure the accounts are extended on time. The notification will look as below. The warning message also contains instructions on how to navigate in MyAccess to update the account validity. 5.Disable/Enable/Delete Service account MyAccess will disable Service account whenever it has reached its validity period. Owner and Deputy Owner will receive notification when service account has been disabled including the instructions on how to re-enable the account again. Service account can be re-enabled within 60 days from disablement. After that, Service account will be permanently deleted. 6.Requesting accesses for Service account If you need to request any access to service account, you can do so via MyAccess à Manage User Access. Please refer to Knowledge Article# KBA2858 for detailed instructions. If you can not find the correct AD group/access in MyAccess, please contact MyAccess Support via AskIT for help. 7. One of the account owners go inactive If one of the service account owners will go inactive, the remaining owner of the account will receive a notification, and a work item will be pending in MyAccess expecting for the remaining owner to update second owner.

short_description: How to get the Local Admin Rights for your workstation?
introduction: 1. What is Local Admin Rights for Workstations? 2. How to request for Local Admin Rights for Workstations using MyAccess Portal? 3. What happens after your access request is approved in MyAccess Portal? 4. How to use the Local Admin Rights credentials for administrative task on the Workstations?
instructions: What is local admin rights for workstations? It is a separate personal admin account used along with the normal user account. This admin account is only used for administration of personal workstations. N.B: Ask IT can help with any application/software installation (which is centrally managed by KONE) and you can also perform a self-service installation of many applications via the Company Portal which does not require any admin privileges . If you feel that the application is missing from the offering, please login in to Ask IT portal and submit the request for application onboarding. How to request for local admin rights for workstations using MyAccess portal? NOTE : If you already have permanent workstation admin rights for PC and you need admin rights for second PC, you need to remove initial role (MyAccess -> Manage user Access -> Your ID -> Choose Tab "Remove Access" -> select "Permanent Workstation Rights" -> next -> provide justification -> submit) and re-request the workstation admin role again and select both PC's in the form when requesting the role. Also note that T3 admin credentials cannot be requested for non-person accounts. Please follow these instructions: 1. Login to MyAccess Portal and select "Manage User Access" 2. Search for the user for which the local admin rights is required and select the account as shown below - 3. Type and search for "Workstation', you will find two options available: Workstation Admin Rights for 72 Hours (This is a temporary access valid for 72 hours) Permanent Workstation Admin Rights (This is a permanent access valid for 365 days) Requesting for Temporary Admin Access for 72 Hrs : The below screenshots will explain the steps for requesting Temporary Admin Access for 72 Hrs . a. It is requested to select "Workstation Admin Rights" which is valid for 72 hours and then click on the "Next" button. b. Click on the "Comment *" tab and provide the required business justification explaining why you would need admin access on your workstation then and click on "Save". Note : If you have multiple workstations, you will get an option to select the workstation name on which you require Admin access to. In case your workstation is not tagged under you name, please create an Ask IT ticket to tag the asset under your name. c. On the next screen, please click on "Submit" d. After you click on "Submit", for users having more than one workstation , you will see a display coming up which says "More Info Needed" and you will need to click on "Complete Form" to select the device where you would like to have the admin privileges. e. After you click on "Complete Form" button, the next screen pops up where you should now see a drop down under "Tier 3 Active Directory", " Workstation Name" for the list of workstations to which the user is tagged to and he can select one workstation from it. f. You will receive "The ID for this request" for the access request which you have raised g. The submitted request will be sent for your Line Manager's approval. You can view the status of your request by clicking on "Request Pending" as shown below - Requesting for Permanent Admin Access for 365 days : 1. In case Permanent Admin Access is required, which is valid for 365 days, please follow Step 1 to Step 3 and then, select "Permanent Workstation Admin Rights" or " Permanent Workstation Admin rights - R&D/Field/Maintenance " as shown below - 2. Click on the "Comment *" tab and provide business justification including the technical requirements and example of use cases for the request. What software are you trying to install or use? click on "Save". Note : If you have multiple workstations, you will get an option to select the workstation name on which you require Admin access to. In case your workstation is not tagged under you name, please create an Ask IT ticket to tag the asset under your name. 3. On the next screen, please click on "Submit" 4. After you click on "Submit", for users having more than one workstation , you will see a display coming up which says "More Info Needed" and you will need to click on "Complete Form" to select the device where you would like to have the admin privileges. 5. After you click on "Complete Form" button, the next screen pops up where you should now see a drop down under " T3 Active Directory", " Workstation Name" for the list of workstations to which the user is tagged to and he can select one workstation from it. 6. You will receive "The ID for this request" for the access request which you have raised 7. The submitted request will be sent for your Line Manager's approval. You can view the status of your request by clicking on "Request Pending" as shown below- What happens after your access request is approved in MyAccess portal? short_description: ECM - How to verify if the equipment/contract number is available in the respective document vault in ECM
introduction: How to verify if the equipment/contract number is available in the respective document vault in ECM
instructions: We many receive requests from end users that the equipment/contract number is not available in ECM. In such cases, we need to follow below steps. 1 . Check if the equipment/contract number is a valid one and it is available in Production Master vault. 2 . If it is available in master vault and from there we can be able to find the respective regional vault to which it belongs to. Then search for the same in respective regional vaults. If it is not available in the regional vaults then it may take some time to replicate. 3 . If the provided equipment/contract number is not available in the master vault then we need to validate it either in trace PDM or in SAP data. If it is available in trace PDM then we can export the details and import it in master server. 4 . In some cases, the equipment/contract number will not be available in trace PDM or in SAP we need to check if it is available in Salesforce. To check in Salesforce please contact ranjith.m@kone.com.

short_description: Network not visible in IES
introduction: Reason for Network not visible in IES
instructions: For the Network to appear in IES below things to happen. #1 Milestone 3 has to have been confirmed on SAP side (and data should have been sent from SAP to IES via Mulesoft integration) & #2 The Taco plan has to be released in TACO (and data should have been sent by TACO to IES via Mulesoft integration), if not then the Network will not be accessible. If the Data from TACO is corrupted, then it is advised to reconfigure create a new configuration & release it for Network from TACO Portal end. Note: Creating a new TACO configuration and releasing can either be done by the end user or the support team. #3 Checking IES Data in AWS Tables Table: ies-app-prod-installations-persistent-installations-table This table should have records for NET, INS, ASS and QUE. If any category of records are missing, the Network will not be displayed in IES. Table: kone-ies-prod-networks-koneiesnetworksprodtable and kone-ies-prod-installationtasks-koneiesinstallationtasksprodtable - The Network records should be available in the tables. #4 Checking AWS Lambda CloudWatch Logs: SAP Networks ingest data lambda, InstallationTasks ingest data lambda and Assignment ingestion - The logs should be available for the Network Even after the above analysis are good, if the Network is not visible in IES, then it may be due to error in data ingestion into IES from Mulesoft. Perform below steps to Check if there is any issue in Data ingestion from Mulesoft. Open the Taco Order in the Variclient application. Go to Special=>Task Configurator=>Send Order to Mulesoft, to send the Order again to Mulesoft, Even after resending the orders to Mulesoft, if the Network is not visible in IES, then we can reach out to Mulesoft_AMS (mulesoft_support@kone.com)

short_description: ASK IT - How to give calendar permission to a mailbox
introduction: How to give calendar permission to a mailbox?
instructions: 1. Open Outlook and go to the "Calendar" tab on the left side bottom corner of the outlook 2. Right-click on your calendar and select "Properties". 3. Click on the "Permissions" tab. 4. Click the "Add" button to select the team mate that you want to give access to. 5. In the "Add Users" dialog box, enter the name or email address of the desired mailbox (example: PlanningtoolNL) and click "Add" & "OK" 6. Set the desired permission level for the mailbox. You can choose from the following permission levels: • Can view when I am busy - This permission allows the user to view your free/busy information. They will not be able to see any other details about your calendar appointments or events. • Can view titles and locations - This permission allows the user to view the titles and locations of your calendar appointments or events, but not the details or attendees. • Can view all details - This permission allows the user to view all the details of your calendar appointments or events including attendees, notes, attachments etc. • Can edit - This permission allows the user to read, add, remove and edit all details of your calendar appointments or events, including attendees. • Delegate - Delegation is a special permission that allows another user to manage your calendar on your behalf. With delegate access, the user can create, edit, and delete appointments on your calendar, as well as respond to meeting invitations and view private appointments. 7. Click "Apply" and "OK" to save the changes.

short_description: Teams Calling troubleshooting steps
introduction: What to do if there is a user who If there is a user who says they do not receive calls to their Teams Calling number?
instructions: This guideline is mostly targeted for O365 L2 support team to help with Teams Calling users. If there is a user who says they do not receive calls to their Teams Calling number: Check the number is routed to Teams: log into the GSIP tool ( https://gsipdb.konenet.com/ ) and ensure the number is routed to Teams / O365 L2 support team If the number has been recently assigned to the user and it is not routed to Teams, assign a new number which is confirmed to be routed to Teams. Please confirm the number change with the user before. If the number has been working previously and is not routed to Teams for the moment, confirm with the user if the number should be routed to Teams. If yes, contact GSIP service manager nagarajan.ramamoorthy@kone.com to understand how to proceed with the case. (Frontline approval on routing changes and cost is needed unless something is done wrong by the GSIP service which needs to be fixed.) If the number is part of an IVR flow , call the number from Teams to see if you can hear the announcement If no announcement can be heard, re-assign the IVR number by first changing the number to a dummy number and then back to the original number If the announcement is heard, try to select the option for the user to see if the call is going through, check the Teams Calling phone settings to ensure e.g. no call forwarding is turned on and try to reset the Teams status of the user. If still not working, ask the user to re-sign into the Teams app and try if calls work through Teams web app or mobile app (clearing cache might work) If the number is not part of an IVR flow and if the user is unable to make or receive calls, check the Teams Calling settings to ensure call forwarding is not enabled, reset the Teams status for the user, and ask the user to re-sign into the Teams app. If the issue persists, try clearing the cache and try if calls work through the Teams web app or mobile app. To clean the cache: If Teams is still running, right-click the Teams icon in the taskbar, and then select Quit. Open the Run dialog box by pressing the Windows logo key +R. In the Run dialog box, enter %appdata%\Microsoft\Teams, and then select OK. Delete all files and folders in the %appdata%\Microsoft\Teams directory. Restart Teams. If a user receives calls in their Teams desktop and / or web app but not in their Teams mobile app (concerns both normal Teams calls and phone calls to their Teams Calling phone number): Ensure the Teams notifications are turned on in mobile settings (both phone and app settings) Reinstall Teams mobile app if calls are still not coming through

short_description: Teams Calling - Group voicemail instructions
introduction: How to create and use a group VM for Teams Calling users?
instructions: This guideline is mostly targeted for O365 L2 support team to help with Teams Calling users. Group voicemail (VM) is a shared voicemail feature for Teams Calling users. The group VM is used with Teams Calling auto-attendants where needed: a call is redirected to the VM if nobody within the call queue answers it. All the VM Group members can access the voicemails through their Outlook (Groups section). Group voicemails are not available through Teams application. How to create a group VM Open the Microsoft Admin Center Select the "O365Groups" option from the left-hand menu Select the Active teams and groups, under Teams and groups On the group page, select the Add Microsoft365 group Under Choose a group type select "Microsoft 365" Enter the required information, such as the name of the Voice Mail, and a description of what it is for. You can now start adding members and owners to Voice Mail. You can add members by either their name or email address Once all members have been added, select the "Save" button to save the changes Voice Mail is now created and ready for use Note! The members of a VM Group do not necessarily align with the participants of the associated call queue. Thus, adding someone to the related call queue does not automatically provide them access to the VM Group. However, in most cases, it is necessary to allow all call queue participants access to the related group VM. Therefore, if a call queue has a linked group VM and a new member is added to the call queue, please confirm with the requester if they should also be granted access to the voicemails. Group VM has owner(s) and member(s) Group owner can add and remove members from the group. All members can access the VMs. By default, users manage the memberships by themselves. The best practice is to have at least two owners within a group. When someone is added as a member into a VM group, the person gets added into the respective O365 Group, a mail notification is sent about that to the person and a respective folder is created in Outlook under Groups. Transcript feature for group VMs This feature can be turned on within the call queue settings if needed to get the VMs with transcript. For the moment the transcript feature works only for longer (20 seconds or mode) voicemails.

short_description: Teams Calling - guidelines to activate Teams Calling for a US user
introduction: How to activate Teams Calling for a US KONE person?
instructions: This guideline is mostly targeted for O365 L2 support team to activate Teams Calling for a US user. Note! For the main site users (Moline, Lisle and Allen), do not assign Teams calling manually but request the related MyAccess role (e.g. Teams Phone Calling feature - United States - Moline IL ) To create a US Teams Calling user one needs to Assign a number (see details in the Number assignment section) Assign a routing policy (see details in the Routing Policy section) Provision a dial plan (see details in the Dial Plan section) Assign a masking policy (only applies for branch users, see details in the Masking Policy section) Assign an emergency policy (see details in the Emergency policy section) – Note! This is a must and bases on US regulations! Assign a calling policy (see details in the Calling policy section) Number assignment For Moline, Allen, and Lisle office users, we are using individual DIDs. For each US branch user (not working for Moline, Allen, or Lisle office), we use Moline DID with the masking policy of the individual branch main numbers. You may find the below the US number ranges. Moline (used for Moline office and US branch users + IVRs) +1309743[5000-6499] Allen (used for Allen office users + IVRs) +146985480[00-99] +146985482[00-99] +14698548[700-899] Lisle (used for Lisle office users + IVRs) +163095531[50-99] +163095542[00-49] +163095544[01-50] +163095545[00-49] +163095549[00-99] Power shell command: “Set-CsPhoneNumberAssignment -identity 'Venkata.subramanian@kone.com' -PhoneNumber +16305264251 -PhoneNumberType DirectRouting” Routing Policy If the user is based in Moline, you need to use US_Moline_Full_Access routing Policy. If the user is based in any other US location, you must use the US_ Downers_Grove_Full_Access routing policy. The reason behind this is that for Moline users, we use Moline SBC as the primary one and Downers SBC as the secondary one and for all other users it is vice versa. Power shell command: Grant-CsOnlineVoiceRoutingPolicy -Identity 'Venkata.subramanian@kone.com' -PolicyName 'US_ Downers_Grove_Full_Access' Dial Plan Every US user must be provisioned with Dial Plan “US Dial Plan”. The dial plan is for Digit manipulation. If the user dials 9988 it will convert to 988. Power shell command: Grant-CsTenantDialPlan -Identity 'Venkata.subramanian@kone.com' -PolicyName 'US Dial Plan' Masking Policy Allen, Lisle, and Moline users will display the individual DID number with no masking policy. Whereas all for all branch users we will assign Moline DID but the actual number to be displayed is the site main number for which the correct masking policy needs to be assigned for all the branch users. The masking policy is selected based on branch user location / branch. Examples of masking policies for US branch users (site ID and name included helping to select the correct policy) US_Masking_US048_Albany US_Masking_US030_Indianapolis US_Masking_US040_KansasCity US_Masking_US096_QuadCities US_Masking_US050_Cleveland US_Masking_US073_Portland Power shell command: Grant-CsCallingLineIdentity -Identity 'Venkata.subramanian@kone.com' -PolicyName 'US_Masking_US048_Albany' Emergency Policy All US and Canadian users should be able to dial emergency calls as per the regulatory requirement. For this reason, Emergency call routing policy ( US911) and Emergency Calling Policy need to be updated. It is mandatory to have an emergency policy for all US and Canadian users. Emergency Call Routing Policy determines whether enhanced emergency services are enabled for users who are assigned the policy, the numbers used to call emergency services (for example, 911 in the United States), and how calls to emergency services are routed Emergency Calling Policy is used to determine who are notified and how they are notified when a user calls the emergency number. The correct policy for the user is selected based on the user location (US: Allen / Lisle / Moline and branches). Power shell command: Grant-CsTeamsEmergencyCallRoutingPolicy -Identity 'Venkata.subramanian@kone.com' -PolicyName 'US 911' Grant-CsTeamsEmergencyCallingPolicy -identity 'Venkata.subramanian@kone.com' -PolicyName 'Lisle Emergency Calling' Calling Policy In US, Canada, and Mexico, Teams Calling users receive call notifications (and are able to pick up them if needed) also when they are in another call or a meeting and no busy tone is played for callers. This is different from the default setting applied for most of the other countries. Powershell command: Grant-CsTeamsCallingPolicy -identity 'venkata.subramanian@kone.com' -PolicyName 'Global plus receiving call on Busy'

short_description: How to reset your Macadmin login password
instructions: How to reset your Macadmin login password : 1. Reset your login password using a recovery key This method of password recovery for Mac Local Admin account. If you turned on FileVault encryption and created a recovery key, you can use the recovery key to reset your login password. Prerequisite: 1. Device should be enrolled in Microsoft Intune through Corporate Enrollment method 2. File Vault enabled and recovery available in Intune 3. Contact AskIT and get the recovery key before password change 1.1 Reset password 1. If you forgot password and unable to login to local account, make sure that you are at the login window, enter any password up to three times until you see password-reset options below. 2. You will be prompted with below screen and message 3. Select the Arrow , from the pop up message 4. After clicking Arrow , Device will be rebooted and prompted with Reset Password Screen 5. Type the recovery Key for your mac device , Then Click Next 6. Select the Mac local account which require password reset 7. Click Next 8. You will prompt to type new password , Type the password and Password hint 9. Ensure that new password is meet with password KONE-Intune complexity 10. Then click next 11. Then click Restart 12. Making sure that you're at the login window, then Select the mac account name and type the new password 2. Password reset from macOS Recovery If the standard and FileVault steps to reset the password of your Mac user account don't succeed, try below steps to reset Admin password from recovery 2.1 Start up from macOS Recovery Determine whether you're using a Mac with Apple silicon , then follow the appropriate steps to start up from macOS Recovery: · Apple silicon : Turn on your Mac and continue to press and hold the power button until you see the startup options window. Select the gear icon labeled Options, then click Continue. · Intel processor : Turn on your Mac and immediately press and hold Command (⌘)-R until you see an Apple logo or other image. 1.1 If you're asked to select an admin user If you're asked to select an admin user you know the password for, click “Forgot all passwords?” and proceed as described below. 2.2 Use the Reset Password assistant You should now see the utilities window, which shows options such as restoring from Time Machine, reinstalling macOS, and using Disk Utility. 1. From the Utilities menu in the menu bar, choose Terminal. 2. In the Terminal window, type reset password , then press Return to open the Reset Password assistant. 3. If you're asked to select an admin user you know the password for, click “Forgot all passwords?”. 4. At the Reset Password window, click Deactivate Mac, then click Deactivate to confirm. 5. If you see an Activation Lock window, enter your Apple ID email and password, then click Next. 6. At the Reset Password window, enter your new password information, then click Next. If this window shows multiple user accounts, click the Set Password button next to each account name, then enter the new password information for each account. 7. When the password reset is completed, click Exit. 8. Choose Apple menu  > Restart, then log in with your new password.

short_description: Ask IT - How to get the Local Admin Rights on workstation
introduction: What is local admin rights for workstations? It is a separate personal admin account used along with the normal user account. This admin account is only used for administration of personal workstations, like installing and maintaining development applications.
instructions: Ask IT - Please consider and go through the following KBA link before guiding the end user for Permanent or Temporary admin rights access : KB000A8494 End User - How to install “MakeMe-NetworkConfigOperator_01” from Software Center? Points to remember: Ask IT not to promote the local admin rights solution except the situation where Ask IT cannot take a remote of the end user’s device, when Ask IT can guide the user about temporary admin rights (for 72hrs). When the admin access is required regularly like in application development or Field support / Technical Helpdesk for installing non-KONE applications, Ask IT can guide the user about permanent admin rights. Ask IT to inform the user (whatever be the mode of communication – chat, phone, email) that after raising the request, it needs to be approved by the line manager ************************************************************************************************************************************ What is local admin rights for workstations? It is a separate personal admin account used along with the normal user account. This admin account is only used for administration of personal workstations. How to request for local admin rights for workstations using MyAccess portal? Please follow these instructions: 1. Login to MyAccess Portal and select "Manage User Access" 2. Search for the user for which the local admin rights is required and select the account as shown below - 3. Type and search for "Workstation Admin Rights', you will find two options available - Temporary Workstation Admin Rights (This is a temporary access valid for 72 hours) Permanent Workstation Admin Rights (This is a permanent access valid for 365 days) Requesting for Temporary Workstation Admin Rights : The below screenshots will explain the steps for requesting Temporary Workstation Admin Rights (for 72 Hrs) . a. It is requested to select "Temporary Workstation Admin Rights" which is valid for 72 hours and then click on the "Next" button. b. Click on the "Comment *" tab and provide the required business justification explaining why you would need admin access on your workstation then and click on "Save". Note : If you have multiple workstations, you will get an option to select the workstation name on which you require Admin access to. In case your workstation is not tagged under you name, please create an Ask IT ticket to tag the asset under your name. c. On the next screen, please click on "Submit" d. After you click on "Submit", for users having more than one workstation , you will see a display coming up which says "More Info Needed" and you will need to click on "Complete Form" to select the device where you would like to have the admin privileges. e. After you click on "Complete Form" button, the next screen pops up where you should now see a drop down under " T3 Active Directory", " Workstation Name" for the list of workstations to which the user is tagged to and he can select one workstation from it. f. You will receive "The ID for this request" for the access request which you have raised g. The submitted request will be sent for your Line Manager's approval. You can view the status of your request by clicking on "Request Pending" as shown below - Requesting for Permanent Workstation Admin Rights ( for 365 days) : Special Note : If there's requirement to run development application or hardware, which requires permanent admin access you can request it. Please provide a detailed justification and note since the approval flow will go to your line manager and to the IT. 1. In case Permanent Workstation Admin Rights is required, which is valid for 365 days, please follow Step 1 to Step 3 and then, select "Permanent Workstation Admin Rights" as shown below - 2. Click on the "Comment *" tab and provide the required business justification explaining why you would need admin access on your workstation then and click on "Save". Note : If you have multiple workstations, you will get an option to select the workstation name on which you require Admin access to. In case your workstation is not tagged under you name, please create an Ask IT ticket to tag the asset under your name. 3. On the next screen, please click on "Submit" 4. After you click on "Submit", for users having more than one workstation , you will see a display coming up which says "More Info Needed" and you will need to click on "Complete Form" to select the device where you would like to have the admin privileges. 5. After you click on "Complete Form" button, the next screen pops up where you should now see a drop down under " T3 Active Directory", " Workstation Name" for the list of workstations to which the user is tagged to and he can select one workstation from it. 6. You will receive "The ID for this request" for the access request which you have raised 7. The submitted request will be sent for your Line Manager's approval. You can view the status of your request by clicking on "Request Pending" as shown below- What happens after your access request is approved in MyAccess portal? Once the request is approved, you will receive an email as per the sample screenshot below. Please setup a new password in MyAccess, in order to use your new admin account. HOW TO USE THE LOCAL ADMIN RIGHTS CREDENTIALS FOR ADMINISTRATIVE TASK? We can use the local admin rights credentials in multiple ways. The best way to use the credentials is to run the application directly and enter the local admin rights account credentials when prompted. Example 1: Installing VLC application a. Double click the VLC Application b. Once you see the "User Account Control" dialog box, enter the admin user name and password to continue with the installation. Example 2: Using Run as administrator in command promp a. Click on Start Menu, type "CMD" in the search bar. Then right click on the "Command Prompt" and Select "Run as Administrator" as shown below. b. Enter the admin user name and password in the "User Account Control" dialog box to open "Command Prompt" as admin. c. The Command Prompt will now open with administrator access, you can now proceed with the commands as required. Example 2: Logging into Workstation using the local admin rights credentials a. In this method, logoff or sign-out from the current session and login with the local admin rights credentials. Press CTRL + DEL key and choose "Other user". Now, enter the Credentials as shown below b. Once logged in with the administrator access, perform the tasks as required and logoff from the session. N.B: Ask IT can help with any application/software installation (which is centrally managed by KONE) and you can also perform a self-service installation of many applications via the Software Center which does not require any admin privileges . If you feel that the application is missing from the offering, please login in to Ask IT portal and submit the request for application onboarding.
