short_description: SAP to Salesforce service order integration: error handling instructions
introduction: How to fix most common SAP to Salesforce Service Order integration errors
instructions: This knowledge article contains error handling instructions for most common data quality related integration errors in the SAP to Salesforce service order integration. 1. Invalid reference to a contract line item ERROR MESSAGE: Foreign key external ID: 12345678-10-87654321 not found for field Line_Item_SAP_Key__c in entity ContractLineItem WHAT DOES IT MEAN: The SAP service order has a reference to contract-item-equipment combination which is not found in Salesforce. This error appears most commonly when somebody has changed the equipment of the order in SAP but has forgotten to update the sales document (contract) number. HOW TO FIX: Open the service order in SAP and check the equipment and sales document (contract) references. If the sales document or item are incorrect on the service order, fix them. If the sales document (contract) and item references are correct on the SAP service order, it means that there is a problem with the contract integration that affects this contract. Check the errors for the contract integration to see if this contract is on that error list or raise an incident to SAP AMS to investigate why this contract is missing from Salesforce. 2. Invalid reference to an equipment ERROR MESSAGE: Foreign key external ID: 12345678 not found for field Equipment_SAP_Number__c in entity Asset WHAT DOES IT MEAN: The SAP service order has a reference to an equipment which is not found in Salesforce. The equipment error appear most commonly when there is an issue with customer master data, but there can also be other reasons. HOW TO FIX: Check the errors for equipment integration to see if this equipment is on that error list or raise an incident to SAP AMS to investigate why this equipment is missing from Salesforce. 3. Invalid reference to any other object ERROR MESSAGE: Foreign key external ID: 12345678 not found for field A in entity B WHAT DOES IT MEAN: SAP service order has a reference to a record which is not found in Salesforce. The fields A and B will tell you what object is missing (for example work center or service territory). HOW TO FIX: Raise an incident to SAP AMS to investigate why those objects are missing from Salesforce. 4. Component line item with invalid quantity ERROR MESSAGE: Enter a Quantity Consumed greater than zero. WHAT DOES IT MEAN: The SAP service order has a component line item with empty or zero quantity. Salesforce does not allow component line items without a quantity. HOW TO FIX: Open the service order in SAP and check the component line items. Ensure all component line items have a valid quantity.

short_description: Change Password for Workstation Admin account in MyAccess and T2 account
instructions: KBA – Change Password in MyAccess Question How to change password in MyAccess? What details should be provided for changing Password? What are the mandatory guidelines to be followed for the new password? What is the message displayed after submission for failure case? What are the details of audit events? How is a logged in user notified about the success or failure of password change? Answer This form should be used for changing his own password for the Workstation Admin account and T2 account. How to change password in MyAccess? Functionality is available in MyAccess to change password. How to Navigate to Change Password: Select menu from the top left corner in MyAccess. Select from the drop-down menu Password Management then Change Password. Then Change Password form will open, and you can provide the required details and click on Change Password. What details should be provided for changing password? Username: It is drop down and consists of list of UPN of Workstation Admin account of logged in user. Need to select any one account from the dropdown. Enter New password: New password should be provided based on the password policy. Confirm New password : Need to re-enter the same password, if it is not same validation is thrown. Note : On clicking change password button, password will be changed and cannot be reversed. What are the mandatory guidelines to be followed for the new password? MyAccess uses Active Directory Password guidelines. Following are the Password Guidelines: 1. Minimum length supported is 10 but it is suggested to use at least 14 characters T2 account at least 16 characters. 2. Minimum 3-character types from the following options should be used lowercase, uppercase, numbers, and symbols. 3. Use phrases which are easy to remember, yet strong. 4. Avoid using people, places, events, or pet names. What is the message displayed after submission for failure case? For failure case when password does not meet password guidelines: What are the details of audit events? For change password success or failure audit events are also logged. Audit Description Create This audit event provides information about changing password success or failure. It displays the target as identity name and source as selected account name . For failure cases, the error message is displayed along the above details. How is a logged in user notified about the success or failure of password change? Email Subject Description MyAccess Notification: Password changed successfully for your account . This is to inform you that password changed successfully for your account. Associated UPN: Workstation Admin account UPN This is an automated message from MyAccess. Please do not reply to this email. If you have questions related to this email, please contact AskIT. MyAccess Notification: Password change failed for your account . This is to inform you that password change failed for your account. Associated UPN: Workstation Admin account UPN Retry changing password after sometime , if still issue persists please contact MyAccess Support via AskIT. This is an automated message from MyAccess. Please do not reply to this email. If you have questions related to this email, please contact AskIT.

short_description: How to harden service account in MyAccess
introduction: This document provide you guidance how to harden Service accounts as a self-service.
instructions: HOW TO HARDEN A SERVICE ACCOUNTS IN MYACCESS There's a couple of option to harden your service account. You can find all hardening information from this document below. Note: If you have hybrid account, we recommend to change account usage as below (see picture). IAM team have created Dummy Computer as a value for Allowed Logon Computer. It means if the account is on cloud, and doesn't have any login computers, owners can select DummyComputer to Map it against. Harden your service account: There are two main hardening settings for the service/ shared accounts: Allowed logon computers Usage of the account should be restricted only to the needed computers. Please specify the allowed logon computer(s)(Servers) for the account in MyAccess by editing the account. If the account is used only in the cloud environment, kindly update the Account Usage to Cloud and add "DummyComputer" as the allowed logon computer in MyAccess. During Service account edit, the allowed logon computer field is mandatory under following conditions: - When allowed logon computer is set for the first time during Create/Edit, there will be a grace period of 14 days, after which the Allowed logon computer field will be mandatory in the service account edit form. For example, if Allowed logon computer is added on 11/01/2024 for any service account, if you try to edit the same account on 11/15/2024 or later, the Allowed logon computer field will be mandatory. Note: The field is not mandatory for the service accounts present in the exception list. Note: If "DummyComputer" is added as allowed logon computer, then the account cannot login to any domain joined servers. If the account needs to login to domain joined server(s), please add those server(s) as allowed logon computers in MyAccess. Please note that there's a Dummy Computer as a value for Allowed Logon Computer , if the account is on cloud, and doesn't have any login computers, owners can select DummyComputer to Map it against. "Allowed logon computers" can be checked from MyAccess > Account > Attributes or from AD for those accounts that are already migrated to MyAccess. 2. RDP and interactive login block (only for service accounts) Service accounts RDP block applies automatically once an account is in MyAccess. This means that it is not possible to login by giving account ID and password (console logon / RDP block) at all. 3. RDP login Block (Only for Shared accounts) Shared accounts RDP block applies automatically once an account is in MyAccess. This means that it is not possible to login to any Servers/Workstations through Remote login. If there is a business requirement to do RDP using a service/shared account, it needs to be agreed with IAM team and based on approvals, MyAccess Support team can enable the RDP Usage for the corresponding account. To initiate approval process, request should be sent over email to IAMAPPROVAL <IAMAPPROVAL@KONE.COM>. Special case: hardening service accounts that use LDAP as the connection mechanism: Please add all of the Active Directory Domain Controllers (AD DCs) to the “Allowed logon computers”. In June 2023, the AD DCs to be added are: APAZ1MSADS120,APAZ1MSADS121,CHNVMMSADS119,CNAL1MSADS401,EUAW1MSADS118,EUAZ1MSADS116,EUAZ1MSADS117,EUHCIMSADS111,EUHCIMSADS112,EUHCIMSADS113, EUAZ1MSADS122, EUAZ1MSADS123 How to navigate MyAccess: Go to https://myaccess.kone.com/identityiq/home.jsf 1.Click the 3 lines for a drop-down list 2. Select Identities -> edit AD account 3. Type your service account and click edit 4.Add the server ID to the “allowed logon computers” section and click confirm. Iterating the hardening of service account (case: something broke when doing the hardening) Adding the “allowed logon computers” means that the service account only works in the PCs/servers that were listed. After the adding those, and saving the changes in MyAccess, it could mean that the system stops working in some context/scope. In that case, the first thing to check is: Which server did the system stop working on? Was that server added to “Allowed logon computers”? If not, then add the server If the service account has an email box, and it is sending emails: if email sending stops working entirely in some scenarios, then it might be that the service account hardening doesn’t work for your current account case. Consider using Azure Active Directory service principal (service account) for sending emails from the service. See “Second best option: stop using AD for the application service accounts, move to Azure active directory usage” in the Background section. If service account is used only for email sending and possible receiving, and AAD service principal is not a feasible option, then special care should be given to other hardening: e.g. ensure that the password is really complex and really long. Also, consider if the account password needs to be stored at all to any safe, instead if you ever again need to change the service account password, it can always be changed by the owner from the MyAccess UI, and the old password doesn’t need to be known at all. If service account is used wider and in many servers, and then additionally for email sending as well: consider separating the logic to 2 different service accounts One for normal operations, where the service account is properly hardened and “allowed logon computers” is defined Another one for purely email sending If email sending service account cannot be hardened, please write a note of that to the “description”, e.g. “Allowed logon computers cannot be set due to email sending. Planned to be fixed on MM/YYYY by [connecting to.”. Add to your application roadmap steps to remove the AD service account dependency e.g. by moving to AAD to use email sending/receiving.

short_description: SAP to Salesforce contract integration: error handling instructions
introduction: How to fix most common SAP to Salesforce Contract integration errors?
instructions: This knowledge article contains error handling instructions for most common data quality related integration errors in the SAP to Salesforce Contract integration. 1. INVALID REFERENCE TO A CUSTOMER ACCOUNT ERROR MESSAGE: Foreign key external ID: 12345678 not found for field Customer_SAP_Number__c in entity Account WHAT DOES IT MEAN: The SAP service contract has a reference to a customer account which is not found in Salesforce. One of the most common reasons for this is an incorrectly executed account merge process where the Leading Customer partner has not been added to a duplicate customer account in SAP. HOW TO FIX: a) Check the contract number and customer account number from the error message. b) Search for the account number in Salesforce. Most probably you will not find the account from Salesforce. c) Open the contract in SAP and check the Sold-to and Decided-by partners of the contract. d) Check these accounts in SAP. If one of these accounts is blocked as a duplicate in SAP but there is no LC (leading customer) partner function, then this is the root cause. Ask KBS to fix the missing LC. If the steps a) - d) did not reveal the root cause, open an incident to SAP AMS and ask them to investigate why the account is not found in Salesforce. 2. INVALID REFERENCE TO AN EQUIPMENT ERROR MESSAGE: Foreign key external ID: 12345678 not found for field Equipment_SAP_Number__c in entity Asset WHAT DOES IT MEAN: The SAP contract has a reference to an equipment which is not found in Salesforce. The equipment error appear most commonly when there is an issue with customer master data, but there can also be other reasons. HOW TO FIX: Check the errors for equipment integration to see if this equipment is on that error list or raise an incident to SAP AMS to investigate why this equipment is missing from Salesforce. 3. INVALID REFERENCE TO ANY OTHER OBJECT ERROR MESSAGE: Foreign key external ID: 12345678 not found for field A in entity B WHAT DOES IT MEAN: SAP service order has a reference to a record which is not found in Salesforce. The fields A and B will tell you what object is missing. HOW TO FIX: Raise an incident to SAP AMS to investigate why those objects are missing from Salesforce.

short_description: SAP to Salesforce equipment integration: error handling instructions
introduction: How to fix most common SAP to Salesforce Equipment integration errors?
instructions: This knowledge article contains error handling instructions for most common data quality related integration errors in the SAP to Salesforce Equipment integration. 1. INVALID REFERENCE TO A CUSTOMER ACCOUNT ERROR MESSAGE: Foreign key external ID: 12345678 not found for field Customer_SAP_Number__c in entity Account. WHAT DOES IT MEAN: SAP is indicating a reference to a customer account which is not found in Salesforce. If this equipment has an active contract in SAP, the customer reference is coming from the Contract’s Sold-to or Decided-by partners. If this equipment does not have an active contract in SAP, the customer reference is coming from the Equipment sold-to partner. One of the most common reasons for this is an incorrectly executed account merge process where the Leading Customer partner has not been added to a duplicate customer account in SAP. HOW TO FIX: 1. Check the equipment number and customer account number from the error message. 2. Search for the account number in Salesforce. Most probably you will not find the account from Salesforce. 3. Check if this equipment has an active contract in SAP If yes, open the contract in SAP and check the Sold-to and Decided-by partners of the contract. Check these accounts in SAP. If one of these accounts is blocked as a duplicate in SAP but there is no LC (leading customer) partner function, then this is the root cause. Ask KBS to fix the missing LC. If not, open equipment master record in SAP and check the Sold-to of the Equipment. Check this account in SAP. If this account is blocked as a duplicate in SAP but there is no LC (leading customer) partner function, then this is the root cause. Ask KBS to fix the missing LC. If the 1-3 did not reveal the root cause, open an incident to SAP AMS and ask them to investigate why the account is not found in Salesforce. Once the account has been created to Salesforce, the next automatic nightly re-processing of the equipment interface will re-send the equipment from SAP to Salesforce. 2. INVALID REFERENCE TO ANY OTHER OBJECT ERROR MESSAGE: Foreign key external ID: 12345678 not found for field A in entity B WHAT DOES IT MEAN: SAP equipment has a reference to a record which is not found in Salesforce. The fields A and B will tell you what object is missing (this might be for example Functional Location, Service Territory or Work Center) HOW TO FIX: Raise an incident to SAP AMS to investigate why those objects are missing from Salesforce.

short_description: KEO - FileImportLog_Failed_DataLoad in Kone Easy Order tool
instructions: Step 1: First Check the Error Message, Initial Filename, Types for Import, FK_Unit in Incident description. Step 2: Then go to production database server and connect in SSMS and get the actual file path using FK_Unit and InitialFilename. Note down [Type] and [PersonalId] from the query result. Database server - EUDVMMSSQL206\SEKO080P,51001 Database – EasyOrder Table – FileImportLog Query- Step 3: Go to application server where this Excel files are stored. - If [Type] is 'Material’ or ‘Storage Location’ in the database, then Excel file will be in ‘Import’ folder. - If [Type] is ‘HR’ and [PersonalId] in the database is having any specific user id then it is import using UI and the file will be in ‘Import’ folder. - If [Type] is ‘HR’ and [PersonalId] in the database is ‘#LoadHRFileWorker’ then it is import using #LoadHRService and the file will be in ‘HR Files’ folder under ‘Import’. In HR folder there will be two other folder named ‘Archive’ and ‘Failed’. If #LoadHRService is success, then file will be in ‘Archive’ folder and if failed then file will be in ‘Failed’ folder. Step 4: Copy file from the server and download for data verification as per error message. - Mandatory field is empty - More than one role is selected Step 5: After checking the excel file with data based on error message, we have to mention the appropriate resolution in incident. Step 6: After mentioning the Resolution steps we can change the status to Resolved/Closed in this incident. Common types of error we get in File Import 1. IMPORT_Error_MandatoryColumn – In Excel Mandatory column data is missing at any of the row. 2. IMPORT_Error_MoreThanOneRole – In HR Excel Multiple Roles are selected. 3. IMPORT_Error_DuplicationOfEmployeeEmailAccrossAllUnits – In HR Excel User has been already assigned with another unit. 4. File could not be imported. Following articles are duplicated for the same unit: KM51110976, KM51111010, KM51171468, KM51108159 – Due to duplicate articles in Same Unit. 5. An error has occurred. If the error persists, please contact your key user – File Format different or may be problem in merge in excel sheet. 6. You have one or several column(s) different from the one provided in the template (row 1), or the file uploaded is different from the template. Please find the first error retrieved, fix it and upload it again: System detected column (A) header name – Due to mismatch on Import Template and User uploaded file.

short_description: KEO - Manual User Creation in Kone Easy Order tool
instructions: Step 1: First check into database whether user is available or not in [EasyOrder].[HR].[User] table using User email address as shown below. SELECT TOP (1000) [PK_PersonalId] ,[FK_Unit] ,[FirstName] ,[LastName] ,[Location] ,[KoneNetworkId] ,[CostCenter] ,[JobFamily] ,[Email] ,[FirstManagerEmployeeId] ,[FirstManagerName] ,[FirstManagerEmail] ,[SecondManagerEmployeeId] ,[SecondManagerName] ,[SecondManagerEmail] ,[Plant] ,[IsUnitAdmin] ,[IsSuperAdmin] ,[CanOrder] ,[IsPersistent] ,[FK_Role] ,[IsCostCenterAdmin] FROM [EasyOrder].[HR].[User] where Email = 'nishant.shah@kone.com' Step 2: If user is not available, then Contact Key User and get the approval for manual user creation. Step 3: After approval get the user details from Outlook properties. - Get First Name, Last Name, Kone Network Id (Alias in the above screenshot), Location etc. from General tab. - Get First Manager Employee Id, Name, Email from Organization Tab. - Double Click on First Manager’s name. New tab will open with First Manager’s details. - Same way double clicks on Second Manager’s name. New tab will open with Second Manager’s details. - Get [PK_PersonalId] from Pager present in Phone/Notes tab. Step 4: After getting all the details you can prepare SQL query as per below and execute: USE [EasyOrder] GO INSERT INTO [HR].[User] ([PK_PersonalId] ,[FK_Unit] ,[FirstName] ,[LastName] ,[Location] ,[KoneNetworkId] ,[CostCenter] ,[JobFamily] ,[Email] ,[FirstManagerEmployeeId] ,[FirstManagerName] ,[FirstManagerEmail] ,[SecondManagerEmployeeId] ,[SecondManagerName] ,[SecondManagerEmail] ,[Plant] ,[IsUnitAdmin] ,[IsSuperAdmin] ,[CanOrder] ,[IsPersistent] ,[FK_Role] ,[IsCostCenterAdmin]) VALUES ('01562183' ,6 ,'Ramona' ,'Sieben' ,'Hattingen' ,'RSIEBEN' ,'2490001' ,'Office Services Administrator' ,'ramona.sieben@kone.com' ,'01003428' ,'Matthias Osthoff' ,'matthias.osthoff@kone.com' ,'28000848' ,'Guojing Fei' ,'guojing.fei@kone.com' ,'220' ,0 ,0 ,1 ,1 ,1 ,0) GO Step 5: After executing query, get confirmation from the user and resolve the incident.

introduction: How to create Employee Profile in ECMS?
instructions: I. Add the user to ECMS 1. Visit https://kone.cobblestonesystems.com/core/EmployeeList.aspx and click on Employees -. Add Employees: 2. As per MyAccess request Select Standard/ Super license in the Add Employee for License Type field and then click on Continue Employee add. 3. short_description: Adding/Removing User from Teams Channel/group
introduction: How to Add/Remove User from Teams Channel
instructions: This KBA is created only for O365 AMSD To modify user access on Teams channel please follow the below steps. Requirements from Requester: Channel/Group Name or email address Please get approval from Owner of the teams channel/group. If there is no owner get approval from User’s line manager. Steps to Add user: Step 1: Make sure that your admin roles for Teams is Active. Link: https://portal.azure.com/#view/Microsoft_Azure_PIMCommon/ActivationMenuBlade/~/aadmigratedroles Step 2: Go to Admin Center and open Microsoft Teams admin center. Team admin center Link : https://admin.teams.microsoft.com/teams/manage Step 3: Click on Teams -> Manage Teams-> Search for the teams channel/group. Step 4: Click on Add member/Owner as per the ticket description and search for the user and select now click on apply. Step 5: You will be getting the confirmation notification as below. Steps to Remove user: Note: If the request raised by the user itself no approval needed. If it was other person get approval from owner/line manager and proceed. Follow the step 1 to 3 from above. Select the member who has to be removed and click on Remove. Once the user removed you will get the confirmation notification. Now please drop an access confirmation mail to requester and follow the three-strike policy and proceed for ticket closure.

short_description: KBP User creation steps
introduction: How to create and map user for KBP in Appian
instructions: Create the user in Appian Designer by following below steps. 1. Navigate to Users tab in Appian designer and create user by entering the user details Username, Email id, First Name, Last Name and Temporary password. 2. KBP Financial Contacts c. KBP Goal Owners d. KBP Goal Contacts e. KBP Viewers f. KBP Business Administrators

short_description: KSMP - Trigger invoices from SAP to KSMP
instructions: Conditions: A, Only one year data with one company code can be sent with multiple invoices. B, Max we should not send more than 18k to 20k invoice per day C, We should also crosscheck the FTP server which should not exceed more than 1GB of invoices data per day. Step 1: The invoices which need to be triggered from SAP to KSMP will be provided by the Key User/Business Step 2: In SAP Z04 open the t-code ZMM_SRMIDOC . Step 3: Now click on variants and type *SYN* and add * in created by field and select execute. Step 4: Now double click on SYN DAILY and check the job parameters & IDOC parameters to be maintained same as below. Step 5: We have to trigger invoices from SAP to KSMP by filtering the only one Invoice Posting Year and only one company code. Step 6: Now in SAP, enter the start date, end date, Invoice Posting Date Year and Invoice Company Code from the Invoices list as shown below. Step 7: Copy the Invoices from the excel and paste it in the Document Number tab as shown below. Step 8: Now click on Program and click on Execute in Background follow the steps as below. Step 9: Once clicked on Save you would receive a pop up as below. Step 10: Now open new tab in SAP and enter the t-code SM37 (if the invoice is triggered by you then don’t need to change anything in this screen) and click on Execute . Step 11: Open FileZilla and enter the below credentials. Host : sftp://213.95.223.25 Username : kone Password : Please contact the KSMP support team for password Follow the path from the below screenshot.

short_description: User Creation Steps For Refactored EWA
introduction: How to create and map users for refactored EWA.
instructions: Create the user in Appian Designer by following below steps. 1.Navigate to Users tab in Appian designer and create user by entering the user details Username, Email id, First Name, Last Name and Temporary password. 2.Open the user in designer tab and add “AZURE” in Custom Field 2 then click on “SAVE” button. So, User will be only part of 'KEA Azure Group' not in 'KEA SAML Group'. 3.Add Users to 3 group: a. Azure Group b. DER All Users c. If the user manager/ pool manager, need to add the DER Super User Group. 4. In Cloud Database, search with “DER USERS” click on Enter in keyboard. Click on “DER USERS” table name. 5.Search with user’s mail id in Email Address field and click on GO. If user is not found, then follow the below steps. 6.Click on “Insert” and enter the user details as per excel sheet attached in the ticket. For EWA user/FL user insert USER_TYPE_ID as 1. 7.For providing user’s a pool access, user needs be added in DER_USERS_POOLS table. 8. In Cloud Database under table search with DER_USERS_POOLS Table. Click on insert tab to insert the values like USER_ID, POOL_ID, IS_POOL_MANAGER, ACTIVE 9.Now user’s manager details need to be update so for this need to insert values in DER_USER_MANAGERS table. Based on manager’s details provided in the user creation forms insert the values. 10.After inserting the values in respective database tables in cloud database, next step is to sync the record type. For that go to Appian designer and search for the below record types and sync the records. DER User DER Users Pool DER User Manager

short_description: User Deactivation Steps for Refactored EWA
introduction: How to deactivate user's in Refactored EWA
instructions: Check whether the user is present in Appian designer's Users tab. If user is present in Users tab, then select the user and click on the button deactivate user. After deactivating in users tab go to database and check in “DER_USERS” table with the email id/username of the user and make the active column as ‘0’. In the next step open “DER_USERS_POOLS” table and take the id of user from “DER_USERS” table and search that id as “USER_ID” in “DER_USERS_POOLS” table and deactivate all the active rows of that “USER_ID”. In "DER_USERS_MANAGERS" check whether user is manager or not. If user manager then deactivate all the active rows of that “MANAGER_USER_ID”. Go to Appian designer à Objects tab and select the “Record Type” object and sync the below records. DER User DER Users Pool DER Users Manager

short_description: How to save Mapkey and Config.pro settings in Creo
introduction: How to save config.pro settings for mapkey and configuration editor?
instructions: Problem: C reo toolkit startup error as config.pro is a duplicate. Solution: Please launch Creo using the shortcut defined below. C:\KONE_PTC-local\UpdateTool\Startup_Icons How can I save the Mapkey? Step 1: Search for "Mapkey" settings in Creo Step 2: Select "New" and then create "Keyboard Shortcut" & "Name" in appeared dialog box Step 3: Start "Record" and do the required operations Step 4: Select "Pause & Stop" and then click Ok Step 5: Select "Save changed" and then click Close Step 6: Save changes in location C:\PROE_WORK\config.pro Launch Creo Step 1: Files>>Options>>Configuration Editor Step 2: Select "Add Option" and find the option name and value & then made the changes as required Step 3: Select "Import/Export" options and then click on "Export per current filter" Step 4: Save changes in location C:\PROE_WORK\config.pro

short_description: ePil - Login issue with correct username and password in EPIL
introduction: How to guide the user who is facing problem with logging in of EPIL with correct username and password
instructions: Step 1: Please check with user if their password contains special characters apart from the below mentioned one: !@#$%^&*()- Step 2: If user confirmed that, please guide the user to reset the password as ePil supports only the below list of Special characters. !@#$%^&*()- Step 3: If user does not know the steps to reset the password. Please provide the below URL to reset the password. https://passwordreset.microsoftonline.com/passwordreset#!/ Step 4: After resetting the password user will be able to login in ePil.

Go to settings and scroll to the end and check the application version. Check the language in IRMA workday in the below highlighted for checking the language assigned in Workday. short_description: Steps to provide TOORMA access
introduction: How to provide access to TOORMA?
instructions: Steps to provide access for TOORMA: Types of access: Key user Tool Master Key user access: Step1: Open and launch IRMA WP system maintenance. Step2: Check if the user has been imported from workday to IRMA in the highlighted form. Step3: If user exist in workday If user doesn’t exist in workday If user exist in Workday: Right click on users tab --> click create new user and fill the required details. Note: please check if the user is existing in users tab before creating new user After creating new user drag and drop the working unit and role access as required. Note: Only two types of access 1. Standard User (give supervisor role), 2. Admin User (Give supervisor and special access--> right click on the working unit assigned and enable assign key user). Open User admin panel in IRMA application --> Search for the user and assign supervisor role for both Admin and standard User. Click Apply --> Ok If user doesn’t exist in workday Please send email to below mentioned ask HR team and wait for one day to reflect the same in IRMA. AskHR@kone.com Tool Master access: Tool master access is the access where the tool master can allocate Toorma Tiraks. Access required tool masters is they need extra additional access with key user access. Step1: Follow the key user access steps as follows in the above “ Key user access” steps. short_description: DOS - PF - Request to restoring the deleted files.
introduction: How to restore the deleted files in Project Folders ?
instructions: In Project Folder Sharepoint the deleted files will be present in Recycle Bin. Note : The deleted files will be present in the recycle bin up to 90 days from the date of deletion. After 90 days the files will be deleted permanently and we cannot restore them. Step 1: Open the respective project folder site in which we need to restore the deleted files. Open the Recycle bin present in the home page . Step 2 : Select the file/document which need to be restored and click on restore button in the top left.

short_description: Alert for server restart EUDVMMSAPPGST02
instructions: Step 1: Open the server ‘ EUDVMMSAPPGST02’. Step 2: Open the Event Viewer console ( eventvwr.msc ) and go to Windows Logs -> System ; Step 3: Use the Event Log filter by clicking Filter Current Log in the context menu; Step 4: In the filter box, enter the EventID 1074 and click OK; Step 5: Only shutdown (reboot) events will be left in the log list. Open the last event; Step 6: The event with User32 as a source shows a user who initiated a Windows restart. Step 7: If the user is mentioned as 'NT Authority\System' then the server restart has happened because of patching activity. Confirm with T-systems or check with the team member if they have received any information about the patching activity. Step 8: Once we get confirmation about patching activity we can resolve the ticket.

short_description: Install the IMT3 app from Intune company portal
introduction: How to install the IMT3 app from the Intune company portal ?
instructions: The IMT3 app will be accessible only through Intune (the official KONE MDM solution) or through the .apk file from Sharepoint to install the IMT3 application. For Intune users: Whenever we release a new IMT3 version, The new version will be available in the Intune Play Store and the users can update to the latest version of the IMT app from the Intune Play Store. Please find the below steps to install the IMT3 app from the Intune Company Portal app in the Android tablet. Note : If the Intune company portal is already installed in your device please skip step 1 to 3 and proceed from Step 4 . Step 1: Search and Install the application ‘Intune Company Portal’ from the Google Play Store in your Android Tablet . Step 2 : Sign-In to the Company Portal application using KONE credentials, Step 3 : Once after successful sign-in and new Work profile creation, User can see below apps in the ‘Work’ profile of the device. Note: The location of ‘Work’ profile be will be different for different devices. Step 4 : Open Company Portal app from the ‘Work’ profile, and select ‘Get Apps’ from the options menu, Step 5 : User will be navigated to Play Store of ‘Work’ profile, where all the apps related to KONE will be listed. From which user can search and select ‘KONE IMT3 ’ app. Step 6 : Select and Install the ‘ KONE IMT3 ’ app . Once after successful installation, the app KONE IMT3 will be listed in the ‘Work’ profile section. Step 7 : Open the IMT3 app and Login with KONE credentials. If the user needs the Pre-prod (Z03) version of IMT3 app. Below is the Pre-Requisite for displaying the KONE IMT3 (Z03) app in Intune Play Store. Pre-Requisite : User must have the Intune roles in MyAccess portal. short_description: AskIT- How to handle the tickets for URL/websites blocking by ZSCALAR
introduction: How to handle the tickets for URL/websites blocking by ZSCALAR
instructions: When you try to access the URL in Kone Environment which is not trusted by Kone corporation, you will get the following error “Your organization has selected Zscaler to protect you from internet threats.” Please follow the instructions below. Error screenshot will be like Why you get this error: If a URL is identified as untrusted based on this evaluation, Zscaler will block any attempts to access it, thereby ensuring the protection of users and their systems from potentially malicious or harmful content. Solution: Do following steps below In order to Access the URL. Step1:- Raise a Service Request to open the URL. Go to--> askit.kone.com--> All--> Employee center--> Network security Services- URL Whitelisting The SR form will be like shown below Fill the below details and submit the request; Requestor name, Email ID, URL website(to be whitelisted), Justification and attach error screenshot Once you submitted, ticket will be assigned to “KONE.ITO.FIREWALL” queue. If the Zscalar blocks the URL/Website, Kone Firewall team will validate the category(except Kone-Blacklist category), and whitelist the URL with proper justification provided in the ticket. Please find the screenshot below If the category is under Kone-Blacklist the ticket will be transferred to cyber security team( KONE.KTI.CYBERSECURITY.OPERATIONS) and they will validate the URL requested for approval. Please find the screenshot below. When the Request(URL) is not approved by cyber security team, they will update the justification(for rejection) and send the ticket back to firewall queue( KONE.ITO.FIREWALL) . Firewall team will share the information to the requestor and close the ticket.

short_description: M365 Support Team - How to fix Email Archival related issue
introduction: How to fix Email Archival related issue
instructions: M365 Support Team - How to fix Email Archival related issue This KBA is allowed only for O365 Support team on how to fix Email Archival related issue Scenario 1 : When the Mailbox was automated the default MRM policy is as below: 1. Connect to Exchange Online and go to New Exchange admin center. 2. Go to Recipients > Mailboxes . 3. Select the mailbox of the user and go to Mailbox icon and click on it. 4. Now check the Manage Mailbox policies, if it is default Office User 2 Year archive, the mailbox is automated for default MRM policy. Scenario 2 : Enable Archive from On-prem server Connect to On-prem and open exchange admin center and follow the steps 1. Go to Recipients > Mailboxes . 2. Select a mailbox. In the details pane, under In-Place Archive , click Enable 3. Now connect to Exchange Online and go to New Exchange admin center 4. In the new EAC, navigate to Recipients > Mailboxes . 5. Select the mailbox of the user and go to Mailbox icon and click on it 6. Now click on Manage Mailbox policies . 7. Now enable the Retention policy to default 2-3 years based on user’s mailbox size. 8. Now select Others , and under Mailbox archive , select Manage mailbox archive : 9. On the Manage mailbox archive pane, turn on Mailbox archive , and then Save . 10. Ensure to run MRM using this command: Start-ManagedFolderAssistant javed.akhtar@kone.com -FullCrawl 11. To check the Retention Policy, use the below command. Get-mailbox "javed.akhtar@kone.com" |fl *policy* 12. To check if any personal Tag use the below command Get-MailboxFolderStatistics -Identity "javed.akhtar@kone.com" -FolderScope inbox |fl *archive* Note: Please ensure the same steps of scenario 2 for the shared mailbox to enable archive manually.

short_description: Ask IT - How to change language in Adobe Acrobat Pro\Standard ?
introduction: How to change language in Adobe Acrobat Pro\Standard ?
instructions: Here's a step-by-step guide on how to change the language in Adobe Acrobat Pro/Standard: Open Adobe Acrobat Pro/Standard: Launch the Adobe Acrobat Pro/Standard application on your computer. Access Preferences: On Windows: Click on "Edit" in the top menu, and then select "Preferences." Language Preferences: In the Preferences dialog box, find and click on the "Language" category on the left-hand side. Choose Application Language: Look for the "Application Language" or similar option. This might be under a subcategory like "International" or "Language Settings." From the drop-down menu, select your desired language for Adobe Acrobat Pro/Standard. Confirmation and Restart: Some versions of Acrobat might prompt you to confirm the language change. If prompted, confirm the change. You might need to restart Adobe Acrobat Pro/Standard for the language change to take effect. If prompted, close and reopen the application. Language Change Complete: Once you reopen Adobe Acrobat Pro/Standard, the interface should now be in the language you selected. Note: For Adobe products, only best-effort support will be provided. At KONE, license management for Adobe is handled internally, but there is no dedicated support team available for Adobe-related technical issues. The AskIT team will assist based on available KBA articles, and no further escalation or in-depth troubleshooting is possible beyond that.

short_description: Installation and Uninstallation of ClearCase Application - Old Method
introduction: How to install and uninstall ClearCase application
instructions: Pre-requisite : 1. User should be added to the AD group adClearUsers ( If not added, guide the user to raise a request in MyAccess for the role ClearCase : User by following the steps given in the URL: https://koneprod.service-now.com/now/nav/ui/classic/params/target/kb_view.do%3Fsys_kb_id%3D7cb0e4b1877fb5945b23400e8bbb35a3%26sysparm_rank%3D1%26sysparm_tsqueryId%3D895e47d4872cce10d143a9330cbb3597 2. User's machine should have admin rights(T2 rights), If user has T3 admin account or does not have admin rights, we have add them temporary rights and then have to remove it after installation is completed CASE 1: Procedure for Installation of ClearCase To install ClearCase, first we have to install IBM Installation Manager Kindly follow the below steps to install IBM Installation Manager Step 1 : Open Run in your system and paste \\keimssos061\CC9x and click OK Step 2: Copy the ZIP file agent.installer.win32.win32.x86_64_1.8.7001.20170920_1141.zip from \\keimssos061\CC9x and paste it on your local Drive. Step 3 : Extract it and run “ install.exe ” files as Administrator (Right click and Run as administrator ) Step 4 : After process we will get installation window, Click “ Next ” Step 5 : Accept the license agreement and click “ Next ” Step 6 : Click “ Next ” Step 7 : Click on “ Install ” The below image shows the installation is happening Step 8 : Click on Restart Installation Manager After this the system will restart. To verify that IBM Installation Manager is installed navigate to Control Panel -> All Control Panel Items -> Programs and Features you will IBM installation here CASE 2: Procedure to Install ClearCase Client Step 1 : Open Run in your system and paste \\keimssos061\scripts\ClearcaseAutomationScript and click OK Step 2 : Copy the .bat file Install_CC_9.0.0.3_x64.bat and paste in your local machine NOTE : Delete the Batch file once Installation is completed successfully. Step 3 : Right click the .bat file and click on Edit Step 4 : Run the following commands in the Command Prompt as Administrator . Command 1: NET USE X: /delete /yes Command 2: NET USE X: "\\keimssos061\scripts" /yes Command 3: "C:\Program Files\IBM\Installation Manager\eclipse\tools\imcl.exe" -input X:\ClearcaseAutomationScript\responseFile_CC_9.0.0.3_x64-May22.xml -svP -log %TEMP%\CC_inst_log.xml -acceptLicense > %TEMP%\CC_inst_progress.log 2>&1 Once the 3 rd command is executed successfully Restart the machine , the system will restart automatically. NOTE : If no changes are happened and the command keeps on running or skips the execution and jumps to a new command line it means that the command is not executing successfully. Terminate by clicking esc button. Check if all the applications are closed and check for the error in the %temp% (in Run) and take necessary action. Open the File CC_inst_progress.log , find the error and take the necessary action For example find the below error. After the restart is completed. Open the ClearCase Control Panel by navigating ClearCase Home Base(as Administrator) -> Control Panel or by using the command “ cc.cpl ” in Command prompt as Administrator In the Control Panel do the below Configurations : Note : Before making the changes Stop the Services in ClearCase We can see that the services are running here, Under Services Startup click on Stop Services 1. Under Registry tab Select the option Use registry server on host and set the value as keimssos092 2. Under Licensing tab select the option Use ClearCase (Atria) Licensing on host and set the value as keimssos092 3. Under Option Tab do the below instruction: The below 3 highlighted check points must be checked. The SMTP host must be set to the value edusmtp.konenet.com Check the Use view profiles for creating views and set the View Profile Tree value to \\KEIMSSOS092\ccstg_d\CCViewProfiles and click on Apply In Services Startup, start the Start Services again and click on OK Add below User Variable in Environment Variable’s Variable Name: CLEARCASE_PRIMARY_GROUP Variable: adclearusers Step 5 : Delete the Batch file once Installation is completed successfully. CASE 3: Procedure to Uninstall ClearCase Step 1 : Open the IBM Installation Manager as Administrator and Click Uninstall You will get the below window. Step 2 : Check if the check boxes are checked and Click Next Step 3 : If you get the below error as Blocking Processes , note down the Process ID. Open Task Manager and under Details tab, search for the respective Process ID Right Click on the Task and select End Task Click on Refresh and then Uninstall option will be enabled click on it. Select the Restart Now radio button and click on Finish After the system is restarted, navigate to the path C:\Program Files (x86) and Shift +Delete (permanent Delete) the IBM Folder. NOTE : If you get any error while deleting the IBM files in File folder contact m.babu@kone.com And again, Restart the machine. CASE 4: Procedure to Reinstallation ClearCase application Before starting the re-installation process close all the applications (especially related to ClearCase ) and reboot the system For reinstallation, first we have to uninstall the present application To uninstall ClearCase from user's machine follow the steps provided in CASE 3 NOTE : If you get any error while deleting the IBM files in File folder contact m.babu@kone.com If needed, uninstall IBM Installation manager also. To uninstall IBM Installation Manager navigate to Control Panel -> All Control Panel Items -> Programs and Features and Uninstall IBM Installation manager. After uninstalling, navigate to the path C:\Program Files and Shift +Delete (permanent Delete) the IBM Folder. NOTE : If you get any error while deleting the IBM files in File folder contact m.babu@kone.com Restart the machine. Follow the steps provided in the CASE 1 to install IBM Installation Manager (if IBM installation Manager is not present in user's machine) After IBM Installation Manager is installed (or if already IBM Installation Manager is present in user's machine), then follow the steps provided in the CASE 2

short_description: How to - Printer Configuration details for Scan to email and Scan to folder (Only Ricoh)
introduction: Ricoh Printer Configuration details for Scan to email and Scan to folder
instructions: Note - This is purely supported as best effort from Ask IT and configuration will be done by local vendor specific to each country. Currently we haven’t implemented this solution through VPSX, and discussion is in progress for licensing to manage it via VPSX. This document is prepared based on the testing performed only on Ricoh Printer Models . It is also to ensure that, if Ask IT and local vendor fails to resolve the issue, no further involvement to be entertained. Steps to be followed by AskIT (If local vendor support is available) Please provide the following details to users who want to set up Scan to Email for the first time so that they may setup it with the help of a local vendor, no matter which country they are in. If user contact AskIT directly for assistance , p lease follow the step by step instructions ( If local vendor support is not available ). 1. Get the IP address and open in any web browser using http:// 10.10.10.10 (example). 2. Printer IP address will be displayed on the printer screen. 3. Printer web interface showing as below. 4. Click on Login 5. Enter Login Username - admin. Click on login, leave the Login password blank or try the login password as system@rse or system@emea. If the above credential doesn't work, then need to check with SDM’s who will have the privilege credential for login to the web portal 6.Device management /configuration 7. Email Section Administrator Email Address is optional. If the SMTP server name is mentioned as eudsmtp.konenet.com please leave as-is. Because, Since 4th September 2023, old Server eudsmtp.konenet.com is configured to point to the new servers (eudlxsmtp.konenet.com) automatically. Once the information is updated then need to add the email address on the Address book section. Note :- The maximum file size that can be attached to an email using Scan is 29MB. Scan to Folder Most of SDMs would already be familiar with the Scan to folder functionality and use it frequently. However, this document is created to do the basic checks on the configuration. We don't have a support team, therefore kindly advise end users and SDMs to use the scan to email / OneDrive functionality rather than the scan to folder. ü Parent Share Name - \\KONENET.COM\GFS ( This is the path which is used globally by all SDM's ) ü Account details for shares – Konenet\ SVC (Country Specific Service account) ; SDM’s should have this account details If user contact AskIT directly, please follow the below screenshots to do the SMB configuration. 1. Get the IP address and open in any web browser using http:// 10.10.10.10 (example). 2. Printer IP address will be displayed on the printer screen. 3. Printer web interface showing as below. 4. If the above credential doesn't work, then need to check with SDM’s who will have the privileged credential for login to the web portal . 6. Click on device management /configuration 7. Go to SMB Ensure that Port number 445 is selected and SMBv2/SMBv3 is active for this configuration. Please ignore the workgroup name and computer name. Need to add the folder path on the Address book as below and complete the setup. Name can be updated as " Server" . It could be anything as its just a display name. Please check with SDM's to get the shared path and use the Konenet\SVC (Country Specific Service account) for authentications to complete the configuration. short_description: POC&GR - Job Failure Notification
instructions: Team is getting Job failure mails from SAP side on daily basis. for the below jobs 1. P2P_000_FL_POC_GR_1D 2. P2P_262_FL_POC_GR_1D 3. P2P_221_FL_POC_GR_1D 4. P2P_XXX_FL_POC_GR_1D Step 1: First, we have to check with MM team (priya.peela@kone.com) about the error which they are getting for the above jobs. Step 2: For getting the parent and child SAP jobs we have to connect with vinay.kondapalli@kone.com . Step 3: The error details on the above-mentioned jobs are given below. 3.1. P2P_000_FL_POC_GR_1D – This is the parent SAP job. As child job is getting failed we were getting the mail on this job failure. 3.2. P2P_262_FL_POC_GR_1D – This job is getting failed as there is no write permission provided for the folder. 3.3. P2P_221_FL_POC_GR_1D – This job is getting failed as there is no folder itself. 3.4. P2P_KAI_FL_POC_GR_1D – This job is getting failed because of failure in the job ‘ P2P_KAI_POC_GR_1D_DELVADD ’ Step 4: To rectify that the below steps have been taken. 4.1. short_description: M365 L2 Support - How to troubleshoot OneDrive Issues , SharePoint Issues and Basic Excel issues
introduction: How to troubleshoot OneDrive Issues , SharePoint Issues and Basic Excel issues
instructions: If a user can't access a SharePoint site or another user's OneDrive site 1. Determine the permission level that the user should have on the site. 2. Verify the permission by using the Check Permissions feature: a) For a SharePoint site, select Settings > Site permissions. For a OneDrive site, select Settings > Site Settings > Site permissions. b) In the ribbon, select Check Permissions. c) In the User/Group field, enter the user's name, and then select Check Now. d) Review the permissions that the user has on the site, and also check the related security group (if applicable). 3. If the user doesn't have appropriate permissions, grant the user permissions to the file or site. 4. If the user continues to receive an error message, remove the user from the site. Then, grant to the user permissions to the file or site. Note: If errors continue to affect a guest account, we recommend that you remove the guest account from the Microsoft 365 admin center completely. Make sure that the user is removed from the site collection. Then, grant to the user permissions to the file or site. If a user can't access their own OneDrive site This issue is usually caused by a site user ID mismatch. To fix the issue, check whether a new UPN exists. If it does, delete the new UPN, and then restore the original UPN. If a user can't access a shared link Remove the affected user from the site, and then share the file or folder again. How to Change a site address in the new SharePoint admin center 1. Go to Active sites in the new SharePoint admin center and sign in with an account that has admin permissions for your organization. 2. To open the details panel, select the site name. 3. On the General tab, under Site address, select Edit. 4. Enter the new site address, and then select Save. How to Troubleshoot OneDrive Sync issues Clear office cache: Navigate to C:\users\username\AppData\Local\Microsoft\Office\16.0\OfficeFileCache Delete all files/folders inside Reset OneDrive Press the Windows key + R to open "RUN" box where you can type the commands below: Enter the command: %localappdata%\Microsoft\OneDrive\onedrive.exe \reset Confirm > The OneDrive system tray icon should disappear and re-appear after two minutes. If the OneDrive icon doesn’t re-appear, run the following command: %localappdata%\Microsoft\OneDrive\onedrive.exe Go to Control Panel > User Accounts > Credential Manager > Click on "Windows Credentials" > Select the OneDrive credential > Remove Also, on the Control Panel > User Accounts > Credential Manager > Click on "Web Credentials" > Select the OneDrive credential > Remove Registry Editor>HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE>Software>Microsoft>Office>15/16>Export>Delete Unlink The OneDrive account Right-click the OneDrive icon in the taskbar>Go to Settings> Head over to the Account tab > Now, go to Unlink this PC to disconnect your OneDrive account from your Windows PC Uninstall OneDrive and install latest version - Try to completely uninstall OneDrive: Windows Key+X > Windows PowerShell (Admin). Copy the below line, paste into Powershell and Enter taskkill /f /im OneDrive.exe This will terminate any OneDrive process Copy the below, paste into Powershell and Enter if you’re using 32-bit Windows 10 %SystemRoot%\System32\OneDriveSetup.exe /uninstall if you’re using 64-bit Windows 10 %SystemRoot%\SysWOW64\OneDriveSetup.exe /uninstall (Note - It is recommended that you create a backup of your registry before modifying registry as suggested below so that if you commit some mistake, you will be able to restore - https://support.microsoft.com/en-us/help/322756 ) Now Windows Key+R > Type Regedit and Enter Go to this path HKEY_CURRENT_USER\Software\Microsoft\OneDrive You can remove all folders that include OneDrive numeric version (example - 18.172.0826.0010 folder) and from the Accounts folder, remove all the folders inside After that restart computer and install One Drive latest version from this link: https://go.microsoft.com/fwlink/?linkid=860984 How to troubleshoot basic excel issues open Excel in safe mode. Do one of the following: § If you are on Windows 11 or Windows 10, choose Start > All apps > Windows System > Run . Type Excel /safe in the Run box, and then click OK . § If you are on Windows 8 or Windows 8.1, click Run in the Apps menu, type Excel /safe in the Run box, and then click OK . If Excel starts in safe mode, continue with the steps in the COM Add-ins Use the following steps to determine if a COM add-in is causing the problem: 1. Click File > Options > Add-ins . 2. Select COM Add-ins and click Go . 3. Clear all the check boxes in the list and click OK . 4. Close and restart Excel in normal mode (not in safe mode). If the issue does not occur when you restart Excel in normal mode, start enabling your add-ins one at a time until it does. This will allow you to figure out which add-in is causing the problem. Be sure to restart Excel each time you enable an add-in. If disabling COM add-ins did not resolve your issue, see if the issue is due to an Excel add-in . Excel add-ins 1. Start Registry editor. To do this, press the Windows key + R to open a Run dialog box. Type regedit.exe and then press OK . 2. Locate and then select the following registry key: HKEY_CURRENT_USER\Software\Microsoft\Office\<1x.0>\Excel\Options 3. In the Name column, look for any REG_SZ value equal to OPEN. If you don't find a REG_SZ value whose name is OPEN, continue with the Investigate possible issues with files in Startup folders section. 4. Right-click OPEN, and then select Rename . 5. Rename OPEN to RenamedOPEN, and press Enter. 6. Repeat steps 4 and 5 by adding "Renamed" to the beginning of any REG_SZ value that starts with OPEN. For example, rename OPEN1 to RenamedOPEN1. 7. Start Excel in normal mode. 8. If the issue does not occur when you restart Excel in normal mode, start re-enabling your Excel add-ins one-by-one by removing "Renamed" from the name. You'll be able to identify the add-in that's causing the problem. Remember to restart Excel each time you re-enable an add-in in the registry. Try starting Excel without opening workbooks in this folder: 1. Click File > Options > Advanced . 2. Under General , in the At Startup, open all files in box, note the path of the folder configured as the alternate startup folder. 3. Move any files found in this folder to another folder location, and then start Excel in normal mode. 4. If the issue does not occur, start moving any workbook you moved in step 3 back into the alternate startup folder one-by-one, restarting Excel in normal mode each time. You'll be able to identify the workbook that's causing the problem. To find the path of the XLStart folder and move workbooks out of it, do the following: 1. Click File > Options . 2. Click Trust Center , and then under Microsoft Office Excel Trust Center , click Trust Center Settings . 3. Click Trusted Locations , and then note the path to the XLStart folder in the list of trusted locations. 4. Move any files found in this folder to another folder location, and then start Excel in normal mode.

short_description: PAM Tool - SAP Security
introduction: Process document for SAP Security team to store non-dialog users’ credentials in PAM tool. OBJECTIVE: This document outlines the process for updating and maintaining credentials for non-dialog user IDs in SAP systems using the PAM tool. The PAM tool serves as a secure credentials vault, storing privileged user credentials along with the associated system and owner details.
instructions: Process Steps for user creation: Requests to create non-dialog users are submitted either via email or through a SNOW ticket to the KONE SAP Security team. Upon receiving the request, the KONE SAP Security team seeks approval from SAP Security TDE. Once approval is granted, the non-dialog user ID is created in the respective SAP system following the defined naming convention. The approval is documented in the corresponding SNOW ticket. The SAP standard transaction code SU01 is used to maintain passwords for System, Service, and RFC users in the SAP GUI. The user type should be defined as System or Communication, depending on the requirement. Passwords must be generated and maintained in accordance with the following principles: Enforce long, complex passwords for system users in line with KONE’s password policies Include at least one uppercase letter, one lowercase letter, one numeric digit, and one special character Ensure a minimum password length of 8 characters Store credentials only in secure repositories (e.g., PAM) During user creation, the information regarding the owner of the non-dialog user ID must be obtained from either the requestor or approver. This information should then be recorded in the Address tab of transaction SU01. Note: Owner can only be someone from Kone IT team. Kindly contact Platform Owner or TDE, SAP Security for any clarifications on the same. Process steps for storing credentials on PAM: PrivX PAM access and permission models are based on role-based access controls (RBAC). A role is created in MyAccess and associated with users. Roles can either be explicitly granted to specific users by PAM administrators or requested directly by users through workflow requests. Depending on the job role or team of the PAM user, different roles are assigned. These PAM roles can be requested via the KONE MyAccess Portal ( KONE MyAccess - Home ). The roles assigned to users determine which targets—such as hosts, applications, networks, and in this case, secrets—they are authorized to access. The roles to be requested for the SAP Security Team are as follows: – When adding a new secret (System/Service/Communication user credentials), you must define the users associated with specific roles who will have the following permissions: Read Permission – to view the secret Write Permission – to modify the existing secret Reference: MFA Setup and Reset for PAM – Knowledge Article #KB000A5577 End User - MFA Setup and reset for PAM | ServiceNow Important Note: Any activity such as new user ID creation, updates (including password resets), or sharing credentials with other teams must be approved via email by the designated KONE owner of the user. New user ID/secret creation on PAM tool The same password, owner, and system information must now be updated in the PAM (Privileged Access Management) tool, which is used for the secure storage of System/Service/RFC (communication) user credentials. Login to PAM tool and select “Secrets” tab -> select “Add Secret” button. Link to access the PAM tool is: https://pam.konenet.com/auth/login Provide the following inputs: User ID Scope -> Global Add roles for Read and Write access to the secret Secret Data (add details like credentials, owner, system and specific client number) Note: If the JSON format for password is not correct, the “Save” button will not be enabled and you will see the error to correct the format. JSON script password needs to be maintained in below format: { "data": "******** // SID1-Client1, Owner- XXXXX, Purpose - XXXX", "data2": "******** // SID1-client2, Owner- XXXXX, Purpose - XXXX", "data3": "******** // SID2-Client1, Owner- XXXXX, Purpose - XXXX " } Once the Secret is saved, you can see the overview: Secret can be viewed in the format below: To update an existing user on PAM, search for the user and select Edit to either update the password or add credentials for another system under the same user ID.

short_description: M365 L2 Support - How to check Microsoft 365 service health for Service Degradation.
introduction: How to check Microsoft 365 service health for Service Degradation.
instructions: 1. Go to the Microsoft 365 admin center at https://admin.microsoft.com and sign in with an admin account. 2. To view service health, in the left-hand navigation of the admin center, go to Health > Service health, or select the Service health card on the Home dashboard. The dashboard card indicates whether there is an active service issue and links to the detailed Service health page. 3. On the Service health page, the health state of each cloud service is shown in a table format. 4. The All services tab (the default view) shows all services, their current health state, and any active incidents or advisories. An icon and status in the Health column indicate the state of each service. 5. If there is an active incident or advisory for a service they will be listed directly under the service name in a nested table. You can collapse the nested table to hide the incidents or advisories in this view by clicking on the chevron icon to the left of the service name. 6. To filter your view to only show all the active incidents, select the Incidents tab at the top of the page. Selecting the Advisories tab will only show all the active advisories posted. 7. The History tab shows all incidents and advisories that have been resolved within the last seven or 30 days. 8. If you're experiencing an issue with a Microsoft 365 service and you don't see it listed on the Service health page, tell us about it by selecting Report an issue, and completing the short form. We'll look at related data and reports from other organizations to see how widespread the issue is, and if it originated with our service. If it did, we'll add it as a new incident or advisory on the Service health page, where you can track its resolution. The Reported Issues page will show all issues your tenant has reported from this form and the status. 9. To customize your view of which services show up on the dashboard, select Preferences > Custom view, and clear the checkboxes for the services you want to filter out of your Service health dashboard view. Make sure that the checkbox is selected for each service that you want to monitor. 10. For example: Some users' new meetings created in the Outlook desktop client may automatically include Microsoft Teams meetings ID: TM670454 Status: Service Degradation Impacted services: Microsoft Teams Details Title: Some users' new meetings created in the Outlook desktop client may automatically include Microsoft Teams meetings User impact: Users' new meetings created in the Outlook desktop client may automatically include Microsoft Teams meetings. More info: Impact is specific to the Microsoft Teams meeting add-in for the Outlook desktop client build version 1.0.23125. Impact occurs despite users unchecking "Add online meeting to all meetings" in Outlook Calendar settings. Users can also disable the add-in to avoid this issue. While we're working to remediate this impact, affected users can create non-Microsoft Teams meetings using the following steps: 1. Access the New Meeting dropdown in the Calendar ribbon of Outlook. 2. Select the New meeting request option in the menu by right clicking on Outlook calendar. Current status: We've determined the fix previously identified in TM649896 isn't fully resolving the impact for users. We've developed and are applying an additional targeted fix within our next service update, which we expect will be available for all users by mid-September. We'll continue to monitor as the deployment of this service update progresses, and we'll provide a more specific completion timeline as soon as it becomes available. Scope of impact: Users leveraging the Microsoft Teams meeting add-in for the Outlook desktop client build version 1.0.23125 and attempting to create new meetings in the Outlook desktop client may be affected. Start time: Thursday, June 15, 2023, at 4:34 PM UTC 11.Solution: a. Go to the Microsoft 365 admin center at https://admin.microsoft.com and sign in with an admin account. b. To view service health, in the left-hand navigation of the admin center, go to Health > Service health, or select the Service health card on the Home dashboard. The dashboard card indicates whether there is an active service issue and links to the detailed Service health page. Status Definition Investigating: We're aware of a potential issue and are gathering more information about what's going on and the scope of impact. Service degradation: We've confirmed that there is an issue that may affect use of a service or feature. You might see this status if a service is performing more slowly than usual, there are intermittent interruptions, or if a feature isn't working, for example. Restoring service: The cause of the issue has been identified, we know what corrective action to take, and are in the process of bringing the service back to a healthy state. Extended recovery: This status indicates that corrective action is in progress to restore service to most users but will take some time to reach all the affected systems. You might also see this status if we've made a temporary fix to reduce impact while we wait to apply a permanent fix. Investigation suspended: If our detailed investigation of a potential issue results in a request for additional information from customers to allow us to investigate further, you'll see this status. If we need you to act, we'll let you know what data or logs we need. Service restored: We've confirmed that corrective action has resolved the underlying problem and the service has been restored to a healthy state. To find out what went wrong, view the issue details.

short_description: How to remediate the azure sync error.

instructions: To remediate the azure sync error, follow the below steps: Possible Sync issues: Groups : · This error comes under the “other” category in the AADC sync error portal · Security enabled group can have the group email address in the email attribute. · Owner / contact of group should not be in the mail attribute. · Add mail to Notes instead If managed by SVC_SAILPOINT_IIQ –> MyAccess team If not managed by SVC_SAILPOINT_IIQ -> M365 team to remove mail attribute and add it to notes as backup. Proxy addresses / mail collision : This normally falls under the “Duplicate Attribute error” section in the AADC sync error: · Validate the attributes of the object in local AD and Azure AD · Infosys to contact owner of the object and figure out which one will keep the mail attribute, and which one should be remediated. · Remove the attribute that’s causing Conflict and wait for sync. Data validation failure: Remove invalid character from UPN / Email / Proxy Occurs When the object in on premises is not equal to Object attributes in Cloud Predominant for the room mailboxes. · To fix the issue, Copy the UPN from the cloud account and update it in the local AD(for now ) · Remove the special characters, post contacting the owner of the Object · KONE to figure out how these are created in the first place MyAccess · Users -> MyAccess · Meeting rooms –> M365 Team (Infosys to share the creation steps to validate to avoid upcoming error) Example: External accounts / guest SMTP duplicate: · Synced external accounts that has external email in SMTP should get the clearmail attribute in extensionattribute5. This can be solved with a script. Check with MyAccess team. · Over time – Decide new attribute to store external email. Msexchextensionattribute for example? MyAccess team discussion. MyAccess team to update script / fix Pager: This error comes under the “Large attribute” category in the AADC sync error portal · Should be employee ID – Looks like security team is storing data about actions on the user object. · Jørund to review process with legal changing pager to another attribute (Jørund Dahl)

short_description: How to troubleshoot basic excel issues
introduction: How to troubleshoot basic excel issues
instructions: Open Excel in safe mode. Do one of the following: § If you are on Windows 11 or Windows 10, choose Start > All apps > Windows System > Run . Type Excel /safe in the Run box, and then click OK . § If you are on Windows 8 or Windows 8.1, click Run in the Apps menu, type Excel /safe in the Run box, and then click OK . If Excel starts in safe mode, continue with the steps in the COM Add-ins Use the following steps to determine if a COM add-in is causing the problem: 1. Click File > Options > Add-ins . 2. Select COM Add-ins and click Go . 3. Clear all the check boxes in the list and click OK . 4. Close and restart Excel in normal mode (not in safe mode). If the issue does not occur when you restart Excel in normal mode, start enabling your add-ins one at a time until it does. This will allow you to figure out which add-in is causing the problem. Be sure to restart Excel each time you enable an add-in. If disabling COM add-ins did not resolve your issue, see if the issue is due to an Excel add-in . Excel add-ins 1. Start Registry editor. To do this, press the Windows key + R to open a Run dialog box. Type regedit.exe and then press OK . 2. Locate and then select the following registry key: HKEY_CURRENT_USER\Software\Microsoft\Office\<1x.0>\Excel\Options 3. In the Name column, look for any REG_SZ value equal to OPEN. If you don't find a REG_SZ value whose name is OPEN, continue with the Investigate possible issues with files in Startup folders section. 4. Right-click OPEN, and then select Rename . 5. Rename OPEN to RenamedOPEN, and press Enter. 6. Repeat steps 4 and 5 by adding "Renamed" to the beginning of any REG_SZ value that starts with OPEN. For example, rename OPEN1 to RenamedOPEN1. 7. Start Excel in normal mode. 8. If the issue does not occur when you restart Excel in normal mode, start re-enabling your Excel add-ins one-by-one by removing "Renamed" from the name. You'll be able to identify the add-in that's causing the problem. Remember to restart Excel each time you re-enable an add-in in the registry. Try starting Excel without opening workbooks in this folder: 1. Click File > Options > Advanced . 2. Under General , in the At Startup, open all files in box, note the path of the folder configured as the alternate startup folder. 3. Move any files found in this folder to another folder location, and then start Excel in normal mode. 4. If the issue does not occur, start moving any workbook you moved in step 3 back into the alternate startup folder one-by-one, restarting Excel in normal mode each time. You'll be able to identify the workbook that's causing the problem. To find the path of the XLStart folder and move workbooks out of it, do the following: 1. Click File > Options . 2. Click Trust Center , and then under Microsoft Office Excel Trust Center , click Trust Center Settings . 3. Click Trusted Locations , and then note the path to the XLStart folder in the list of trusted locations. 4. Move any files found in this folder to another folder location, and then start Excel in normal mode.

short_description: Self-Help Guide for Citrix VDI Users - Slowness Issue
introduction: Self-Help Guide for Citrix VDI Users - How to troubleshoot common issues and optimize your Citrix VDI experience for better performance, leading to improved productivity.
instructions: Introduction: Citrix Virtual Desktop Infrastructure (VDI) offers remote access to your desktop and applications. This guide will help you troubleshoot common issues and optimize your GenPact Citrix VDI experience for better performance, leading to improved productivity. Step 1: Ensure a Stable Network Connection: 1. Make sure you have a stable and high-speed internet connection. 2. If possible, use a wired Ethernet connection for a more reliable and faster connection. Step 2: Test Your Internet Speed: Use a reputable internet speed testing tool (e.g., Ookla Speedtest) to check your current internet speed. Step 3: Update Citrix Workspace (Receiver): 1. Ensure you have the latest version of Citrix Workspace (formerly Citrix Receiver) installed on your local device (laptop or desktop, not within your VDI). 2. Check for any available updates for Citrix Workspace and install them on your laptop or desktop from https://www.citrix.com/downloads/workspace-app/windows/workspace-app-for-windows-latest.html Step 4: Troubleshoot Common Issues: If you encounter common issues like slow performance, here are some steps to troubleshoot: - Slowness: Close unnecessary applications and browser tabs within your virtual desktop. This can free up system resources and improve performance. Open Task Manager from your VDI and identify the top CPU and Memory consumed processes. - Application Issues: If specific applications are slow, try closing and reopening them. - MS Teams: If you are experiencing slowness issues in Microsoft Teams, please try using the Teams web version inside the VDI (https://teams.microsoft.com/) - Outlook: If you are experiencing slowness issues in Microsoft Outlook, please try using the Outlook web version inside the VDI (https://outlook.office365.com/mail/) - Web-Based Applications: If you are experiencing slowness issues while using any web-based applications like CORA, CSD, etc., try accessing the other web portals like Kone intranet and observe the speed inside the VDI - Audio/Video Problems: If you experience choppy audio or video, check your network speed and latency as described below. Close any bandwidth-intensive applications running on your local device(laptop or desktop). Step 5: Use the Citrix Connection Quality Indicator (CQI) Tool: Citrix Connection Quality Indicator is a tool deployed in your GenPact VDI for all users. This provides real-time metrics about your Citrix session, including your network latency and ICA RTT. This CQI can be accessed by clicking the up-arrow symbol in the system tray. Double-click the CQI icon in the system tray to view the notification regarding your connection strength. Additionally, if you click on the small triangle symbol, you can access real-time information about the Round-Trip Time (RTT) and latency of your network connection between your laptop or desktop and the VDI. High latency can lead to slowness. If you see high latency, consider moving closer to your router, or use a wired connection, or change your Wi-Fi connection. The other notifications from the CQI tool are below for your reference: Collecting Data state: Network data is being collected after you log in to the VDI. Poor and Weak states: Network quality to the VDI from your laptop or desktop is not sufficient. You can expect slowness in the VDI. Busy states: The user session is busy due to activity on the user's side. Excellent state : The user session has a good network, and as a result, the user can experience a high-quality VDI session. Idle state: The user session is idle. Step 6: Save Your Work Regularly: Citrix VDI sessions can occasionally disconnect. To prevent data loss, save your work frequently, especially in critical applications. Step 7: Logoff Regularly: Please close all the opened applications within your VDI session and log off the VDI while you are leaving for the day. This will ensure no unnecessary apps, browsers, etc. are consuming the VDI resources. Step 8: Contact VDI Support (if needed): If none of the above suggestions resolve your issue, please consider submitting a support ticket to the VDI team by answering the following queries. Queries: 1. **When Did You First Notice the Slowness?** 2. **Is the Slowness Consistent or Intermittent?** 3. **Have You Experienced Slowness in Specific Applications or Tasks, Including Web-Based Applications? Please Mention the Names of the Applications.** 4. **Are Other Users in Your Organization Experiencing Similar Issues?** 5. **What Type of Tasks Are You Performing When You Notice Slowness?** 6. **Have You Tested Your Internet Connection Speed? What Is Your Bandwidth Speed, ICA RTT (Round-Trip Time), and Latency?** 7. **Are You Connecting from Home?** 8. **What Is Your Geographic Location?** 9. **Are There Any Error Messages or Pop-Ups During the Slowness?** 10. **Have You Tried Closing Unnecessary Applications or Browser Tabs?** 11. **Have You Restarted Your Local Device Recently?** 12. **Do You Have Any Background Downloads or Updates Running on Your Local Device?** 13. **Have You Recently Made Changes to Your Network or Wi-Fi Settings?** 14. **Are You Using a Wired or Wireless Connection?** 15. **Have You Tried Accessing the VDI Session from a Different Location or Device?** 16. **Is Your VDI Client (e.g., Citrix Workspace) Updated to the Latest Version? What is the Version of Your Citrix Workspace? ** 17. **If You Are Using GenPact VPN During the Slowness, Can You Disconnect the VPN and See If the VDI Performance Improves?**
