short_description: How to troubleshoot SharePoint Issues.
introduction: How to troubleshoot SharePoint Issues
instructions: If a user can't access a SharePoint site or another user's OneDrive site 1. Determine the permission level that the user should have on the site. 2. Verify the permission by using the Check Permissions feature: a) For a SharePoint site, select Settings > Site permissions. For a OneDrive site, select Settings > Site Settings > Site permissions. b) In the ribbon, select Check Permissions. c) In the User/Group field, enter the user's name, and then select Check Now. d) Review the permissions that the user has on the site, and also check the related security group (if applicable). 3. If the user doesn't have appropriate permissions, grant the user permissions to the file or site. 4. If the user continues to receive an error message, remove the user from the site. Then, grant to the user permissions to the file or site. Note: If errors continue to affect a guest account, we recommend that you remove the guest account from the Microsoft 365 admin center completely. Make sure that the user is removed from the site collection. Then, grant to the user permissions to the file or site. If a user can't access their own OneDrive site This issue is usually caused by a site user ID mismatch. To fix the issue, check whether a new UPN exists. If it does, delete the new UPN, and then restore the original UPN. If a user can't access a shared link Remove the affected user from the site, and then share the file or folder again. How to Change a site address in the new SharePoint admin center 1. Go to Active sites in the new SharePoint admin center and sign in with an account that has admin permissions for your organization. 2. To open the details panel, select the site name. 3. On the General tab, under Site address, select Edit. 4. Enter the new site address, and then select Save. "Your organization's policies do not allow you to share with these users" error when sharing an item or site in SharePoint Online or OneDrive for Business Check the external sharing setting for your organization, 1. Sign in to https://admin.microsoft.com as a global or SharePoint admin. 2. In the left pane, under Admin centers, select SharePoint. (You might need to select Show all to see the list of admin centers.) 3. In the left pane of the new SharePoint admin center, under Policies, select Sharing. 4. Under External sharing, specify your sharing level for SharePoint and OneDrive. Check the external sharing settings for the site that you want to share from If you're sharing from a SharePoint site: 1. In the SharePoint admin center, select Try it now to open the new SharePoint admin center. 2. In the left pane of the new SharePoint admin center, select Active sites. 3. Select the site that you want to share from, and then under External sharing select Change. 4. Make sure that either New and existing guests or Anyone is selected, and then select Save if you made change 5. Try sharing again. If you're sharing from OneDrive: 1. In the Microsoft 365 admin center, in the left pane, under Admin centers, select OneDrive. (You might need to select Show all to see the list of admin centers.) 2. Under External sharing, select either New and existing external users or Anyone for OneDrive. 3. Select Save. 4. Try sharing again. Sharing options are greyed out when sharing from SharePoint Online or OneDrive This issue can occur if you do not have sharing settings enabled properly for the site or organization. This issue may also occur if the site collection feature Limited-access user permission lockdown mode is activated. For example: 1. Select the file or folder you want to share, and then select Share. 2. Select the dropdown list to change the type of link. In the Link settings screen, you can change who is able to access the link and edit the item you're sharing. 3. If one of the sharing options is grayed out, your organization's administrators may have restricted it. Check the external sharing settings for your organization 1. Sign in to https://admin.microsoft.com as a global or SharePoint admin. (If you see a message that you don't have permission to access the page, you don't have Microsoft 365 administrator permissions in your organization.) 2. In the left pane, under Admin centers, select SharePoint. (You might need to select Show all to see the list of admin centers.) 3. In the left pane of the new SharePoint admin center, under Policies, select Sharing. 4. Under External sharing, specify your sharing level for SharePoint and OneDrive. Check the external sharing settings for the site that you want to share from If you're sharing from a SharePoint site: 1) In the SharePoint admin center, in the left pane, select Sites > Active sites. 2) Select the site that you want to share from, and then select Sharing. 3) Make sure that either New and existing guests or Anyone is selected, and if you made changes, select Save. 4) Try sharing again. If you're sharing from OneDrive: 1) In the Microsoft 365 admin center, in the left pane under Users, select Active users. 2) Select the user, and then select the OneDrive tab. 3) Select Manage external sharing. 4) Make sure Let people outside your organization access your site is turned on, and Allow sharing with anonymous guest links and authenticated users or Allow sharing to authenticated guest users with invitations is selected. 5) Try sharing again. Limited-access user permission lockdown mode If the site collection feature Limited-access user permission lockdown mode is activated, the end user will see both Anyone and People in Microsoft grayed out. To resolve this issue, follow the steps below: 1) Go to Site administration > Site settings. 2) Select Site collection features. 3) Deactivate Limited-access user permission lockdown mode.

short_description: Create & manage a rule on a message in outlook
introduction: How to created & Mange rules on a message in outlook
instructions: 1. Log in to your Outlook account . 2. Right-click an existing message and select Rules > Create Rule. 3. Select a condition, and what to do with the message based on the condition. For example, to move messages with a certain title to a specific folder, select the Subject contains condition, select Move the item to folder, select or create a new folder, and then select OK. 4. When you're done creating the rule, select OK. 5. To use the rule right away, select the Run this new rule now on messages already in the current folder checkbox, and then select OK. The message now appears in that folder. How to manage rules in Outlook settings To manage an existing rule in Outlook: 1. Log in to your Outlook account. 2. Click the “...” icon on the right side, then select “Rules” and “Manage rules.” 3. From here, you have the following options: Disable: Deselect the toggle box to the left of the rule. (You can select this toggle box to enable a rule that you previously disabled.) Run manually: Select the play icon to run the rule manually. Edit: Select the pencil icon to open the “Rules” menu to edit the rule. Delete: Select the trashcan icon to delete the rule.

short_description: KEO - How to change the unit for an user in KEO
introduction: How to change the unit for an user in KEO
instructions: 1. Connect the KEO SQL DB, based on test or production environment. KEO Test: EUDVMMSSQL103\SEKO55D KEO Production: EUDVMMSSQL206\SEKO080P 2. short_description: Employee Creation in Kone Compliance Screening (Dowjones) Application
introduction: Employee Creation in Kone Compliance Screening (Dowjones) Application
instructions: Environment and Accesses KCS has 3 environments KCS Production environment which can be accesses via https://kybp.cericosolutions.com/account/ssocompany/902e3285-f551-429f-8d39-2674957acc0a by KONE credentials (SSO). KCS UAT environment which can be accesses via https://auth.cericosolutions.com/Account/UserLogin . Users have to use their email address having +uat as suffix and user have to set their own password. (Example: firstname.lastname+uat@kone.com) KCS SIT environment which can be accesses vi https://auth.cericosolutions.com/Account/UserLogin Users have to use their email address having +sit as suffix and user have to set their own password. (Example: firstname.lastname+sit@kone.com ). User Management User accesses are requested and approved in MyAccess solution. Once approval is completed, MyAccess will send details for user with needed profile to KCS_Support@kone.onmicrosoft.com . A new ticket will be created in AskIT Remedy Force once the request is approved in MyAccess. User profile creation To add employee, please follow below steps Login to KCS application, Click on Employee tab. Click on Add button and fill the required information received from the request. First Name Surname Email Address: Only UPN ID If Access request if for production, we give UPN email address only. SSO is implemented for KCS production, user need to required to set their password. If Access request is for UAT, we add +uat suffix and user must set their own password. (Example: firstname.lastname+uat@kone.com) Business Unit: By default, we give all the employees ALL THIRD PARTIES only unless we get specific request to provide GLOBAL level. Groups & Roles: We have 5 Types of Groups can be assigned to user based on the request. Compliance Compliance Approver Notification Receiver Standard (No Notifications) ZZ-DOWJONESTEAM-NOEMAILS We have 6 types of roles can be assigned to users based on the request. Admin Business User Compliance Super User Dow Jones Admin Reviewer Risk Search User NOTE : We assign Groups & Roles based on the request and combinations below PROD - Legal/Compliance: Groups= Compliance + Role=Reviewer PROD - Business Users: Groups= Standard (no notification) + Role=Business User PROD – Super User: Groups= Compliance, Compliance Approver+ Role=Compliance Super User PROD-Admin: Groups= Standard (no notification) + Role= Admin Employee Tags: We have 7 employee tags available in the system. While creating new user, provide the tag based on the role requested by user. Once all the details are provided, click on Submit button. When the user account is submitted, the user will receive registration email. Until the user is registered, the account will be marked as “Not Registered” and the admins can resend the registration email again if needed. If user is registered, admins can see the information as Registered under Is Registered. Once the employee profile is created, we have EDIT & DELETE option available to modify the profile and to delete the profile based on the support request created in remedy force. To Remove Employee in KCS From Employee Profile Page Click on Edit and then make Active to Non Active by clicking on the below sown button and click on Save.

short_description: Access to KFF SOPs
introduction: How to provide access in KFF?
instructions: The Access provider should have the access to the KFF application with at least Supervisor admin privileges. Step 1: Access to KFF should be requested via MyAccess, and not granted directly on the application. If the user has requested KFF access through MyAccess, we can go to Step 2. Note : In case the user requests access through AskIT/Mail then we inform the user to raise the access request from MyAccess. Here’s the URL: https://myaccess.kone.com/identityiq/home.jsf Step 2: We should know the name of the user, userId of the user, role for which the user wants the access to and information about which country/company/Branch/Planner Group the user wants the access to. Step 3: Login to KFF Application through the following URL depending upon which environment we have to give the access: These URLs works through open internet also. (It does not works when connected to VPN) https://kff.kone.com (Production) https://qa-kff.kone.com/ (Acceptance) Use your Kone Credentials to Login into KFF. Step 4: Navigate through the menu and select the “User Administration” tab. *User will ask for one of the following Roles: IT-Objects, Kone, Supervisor Admin, BackOffice, Supervisor, Global Admin, Service Manager, Supervisor Responsibility Usually user asks for Supervisor Access but if any other access is required, take the following approvals. IT-Objects - From the Configuration Owner of KFF : "Mastromatteo Cesare" Cesare.Mastromatteo@kone.com KONE - From Configuration Owner of KFF Global Admin - Configuration owner of KFF Supervisor Admin - Usually assigned to KFF Key user - Needs confirmation from previous KFF Key user OR Global Manager ("Paolo Bonfiglio" Paolo.Bonfiglio@kone.com.) BackOffice - Key user of that country Supervisors - Key user of that country, usually included in the ticket. Service Manager - Key user of that country (it can be that the Service manager/key user are the same person) Supervisor Responsibility - Key user of the country If there is any doubts in approvals, we can contact "Ayrookuzhy Joe Kuriakose" joekuriakose.ayrookuzhy@kone.com . Step 5: Select the tab (Role), to which we have to give the access to (in this case supervisor access example is taken). Step 6: Click on Add user Icon, at the top right corner to add new user. We will see a new “Edit User” box loaded on the screen Step 7: Search for the name of the user in the text box, it will search through the active directory of the Kone. Use the format of search as “Last name” SPACE ” First name”, as it will not search through incomplete names, userId or email Id as of now. Select the correct user from the searched list, to give the access. Step 8: Information about the user’s permissions will be loaded on the same dialog box. In the below screenshot we can see that, Sharma Mitanshu, has Access to County: Denmark. For a new User, the permissions will be blank, and nothing will be there. The 4 add buttons at the bottom, is used to add the access of Country/Company/Branch/Planner Group to that user respectively. It is a tree structure, so If we will give access to the Country , the user will automatically get access to all Company, Branches and Planner groups for that company. Similarly, if we give access to company , user will have access to all Branches and Planner groups for that company and so on. Planner group access is the narrowest access we can give to the user. Step 9: According to the user’s requirement, we can provide access to whole country, Company, Branch or Planner Group. Adding The country: Adding the Company: Adding the Branch: Adding the Planner Group:

short_description: AutoCAD Installations
introduction: AutoCAD Installations
instructions: AutoCAD is a licensed 2D CAD software from Autodesk. KONE has so-called token-based licenses, which means that license costs for this tool is based on usage. Due to the high cost of AutoCAD, KONE also offers a more cost-effective software very similar to AutoCAD, called progeCAD . This software usually suits the majority of 2D CAD users and shall be chosen primarily. You can get the software installed via MyAccess role progeCAD: English. For Chinese or German language versions, please request progeCAD: Chinese or progeCAD: German role. If progeCAD does for some reason not suit your purpose and you absolutely require AutoCAD, please request MyAccess role AutoCAD: English . We also offer language versions for German and French, in that case request for AutoCAD: German or AutoCAD: French. Please note that the AutoCAD roles require approval not only from the line manger, but also from the Autodesk team. To evaluate, if AutoCAD is indeed required, a business justification is required. Follow KBA6126 for further details. Once the role request has been approved, the software will be pushed out via Software Center. It might take quite some time before the installation has been completed. In case it doesn't get installed to your computer, please contact AskIT. KONE also offers AutoCAD Mechanical. This version has additional features specifically for Mechanical Design. Keep in mind that KONE preferably uses 3D CAD tool Creo for 3D design, so AutoCAD Mechanical shall be avoided. If you indeed require this software, also in this case a business justification is required when requesting this role.

short_description: Creating KSS/Intelex Inspection Checklist - KONE Safety Solution (KSS, Intelex)
introduction: Creating KSS/Intelex Inspection Type & Checklist Questions
instructions: How to Create an Inspection Checklist 1. Creating Inspection Type & Checklist · Login to Intelex and change your location in top right to that respective country to create a checklist. · Search for Inspection in Search Apps Box and select Inspections. · Then click on More tab and select Inspection type. · Click on Add Entry. Then provide Code & Name of the checklist (get from requester). Description is optional. Then click on Save. · Then you will be re directed to the page as shown below · Now, click on Add Entry under Checklist and the fill the Code, Title and Description (opt) and Click on Save · Once you click on Save button, you will see the a new page. 2. Create Questions (get from requester) · Click on Create & Attach a New Questions under Questions section. Then you will see a new window for creating first Question. · Now provide the details I. Checklist Type – Default II. Question Code – Will be default and you can differentiate by giving 1.1, 1.2,1.3 ending. III. Order – provide the order number (1,2,3) to visible the questions in order in under user view. IV. Question – Fill up the Question provided by the requester V. Guideline – NA VI. Section – Provide the Section to see the questions in category wise. VII. Answer Type – Select the Answer Type from existing list. VIII. Choice List – Select the Choice List from existing list. · Now Click on Save & Add Entry to create next question. · Once you create all the questions, click on Save & Exit and you will see the created questions. Verify the questions and the details once before Publishing. · Now, from the same screen, click on Publish on top of the page. Then click Ok. Then you will the success message. We can verify this by creating a new Inspection from the same location or sub locations that checklist created. Now by going to Inspections, then Add Entry, select Type of Inspection, in new window you will see the Inspection Type you created and select that Inspection Type. Then you will be asked to select Inspection Checklist from the dropdown list and select the checklist you created.

short_description: People Flow Analyzer(PFA) User Account Creation
introduction: How To Provide Silver / Gold / Platinum Access to People Flow Analyzer (PFA)
instructions: PFA access is managed via MyAccess. Only MyAccess requests are accepted to provide PFA access as per the respective role. If you receive any Ask IT ticket for PFA access, check if the user is having the respective MyAccess role for PFA Silver/Gold/Platinum level. If present, then check the respective certificate is there and then provide the access. Without MyAccess role and Course completion certificate the access must not be provided. If not present, guide them to raise a request in MyAccess for the respective level. If you receive any MyAccess request to provide PFA access, check if PFA(Silver/Gold/Platinum) Course Completion Certificate is attached in RITM. Below is the example of PFA Course Completion. NOTE : 1) The user must complete the PFA Silver/Gold/Platinum course in Workday 2) If user provides a different image as not below, validate the certificate with amarthya.chaterjii@kone.com . Follow the steps given below to give PFA access: Login to People Flow Analyzer (PFA) and click on Users . There are 2 scenarios here, CASE 1 : EXISTING USER Search for the user in search box if the user already has access the name will be visible in the below. Click on the respective user name. Here check the below: 1) Check If the user is Active: If he is Active check the next step If Not Active, Check his Active Directory (AD) account status and Course completion certificate. Then provide the respective level of access by selecting Role and changing the status as Active and click Save 2) Check If the Role PFA(Silver/Gold/Platinum) requested by the user is already provided to user: If yes, then inform the user that the user already has access to the requested role in PFA. Share the PFA URL and guide user to access. If user still faces error forward the issue to L3 queue If No, provide the requested Role Silver/Gold/Platinum by checking if the MyAccess role is assigned and Course completion certificate is present. 3) Check if the respective Country is assigned to the User: Check the Active directory -> Search for the name -> Address -> Check the Country of the user. See if the same is mapped in PFA. If not change accordingly. CASE 2: NEW USER You can find if a user is new user by searching the username in the search field. The search result will be empty as below. Follow the below steps to provide PFA access to New User. NOTE : Check if the Respective PFA(Silver/Gold/Platinum) MyAccess roles are assigned and the Course Completion certificate is present Step 1: Click on New User . Search for the username, the respective name will be visible below, click on it. short_description: 1Plan - Beacon history data load
introduction: How to load the beacon history data into 1Plan application?
instructions: Step 1: Get the start month and year from the business key user (generally 2 years history) Step 2: Login to AWS Production and open the "AUT_P0016B1_Archive/" folder from one-plan-data-prod S3 bucket. Step 3: Move the VOLUMES & FINANCE ACTUAL files of one month alone to the "AUT_P0016B1/" folder Step 4: Monitor the respective Gluejob and wait until the run is successful Step 5: Repeat step 3 and 4 for all month files. Step 6: Check in RDS if there are any duplicates in finance and volume tables by executing the below queries. Result should be 0. SELECT COUNT(*) FROM (SELECT business,DataGroup , Category , Entity , TechPlatform , TimeStampMonth FROM beaconfinancial_cumulative group by business ,DataGroup , Category , TimeStampMonth, Entity , TechPlatform HAVING COUNT(*)>1)foo; SELECT COUNT(*) FROM (SELECT Business_out, Quantity , Category , datagroup , TimeStampMonth , TechPlatform , Entity , [type] FROM beaconvolume_classification group by Business_out ,Quantity , Category , datagroup , TimeStampMonth , TechPlatform, Entity , [type] HAVING COUNT(*)>1)foo; Data will be automatically loaded along with regular data load schedule (WD1-WD6 and WD8)

short_description: 1Plan - Update Beacon PC Mapping file in AWS
introduction: How to update Beacon PC Mapping file in AWS
instructions: 1PLAN Beacon PC Mapping file processing PC Mapping file is received form the beacon team every month and should be processed before being used in the AWS. This preprocessing is done to modify the raw file accordingly so that it is acceptable to AWS Glue jobs processing. There is a python script to preprocess the Beacon PC Mapping files. Step 1: Make sure you have python installed in the KONE VDI. Type ‘python’ and hit enter in the command prompt. If python is installed, you will see the version of it like 3.10.8 in the above picture. Step 2: Enter this command in the command prompt to install pandas. ‘pip install pandas’ – to install pandas package. Step 3: Download the python script from the 1PLAN 10151 share point in KONE VDI. Step 4: Download the current month & previous month raw Beacon PC mapping files. Make sure you put the date in the raw file names to differentiate both files. Current month file: Previous month file: Guideline to select Previous month file: Check when the PC Mapping file was uploaded in AWS. You need to check for the mail received right before that date (In short, you should be selecting the previously processed file) Step 5: Download the current Beacon PC Mapping file from the AWS. Step 6: Upload a copy of the current Beacon PC Mapping file in the share point. Step 7: Open the python scrip with IDLE, and then run the script. Wait for the files selection dialog to open. Make sure you have both raw files, current PC mapping file, and the python script in the same folder. Step 8: Once the file selection dialog is open, select the files correctly, the previous month raw mapping file, current month raw mapping file, and the current AWS PC mapping file. After selecting the necessary files, click on process files. Check if Outlook is automatically opening and allow if any pop up is shown Step 9: Once processing is completed, you will get a feedback popup, click on ok, and close the dialogs and IDLE. Step 10: You will find the below folder structure, in the archive folder, you will have the raw files that you selected, in the timestamp folder, you will find the difference files and the latest PC mapping file which needs to be uploaded in the AWS. Step 11: Go to AWS Mapping folder, delete the old PC mapping file and upload the latest PC mapping file. Step 12: If any alert mail is triggered, check with Business team if those entities are needed in 1PLAN or not and proceed to add necessary entities in 1PLAN.

short_description: Process to be followed by ASKIT team if KONE users reported Incidents stating the emails have been quarantined and not delivered to their inbox.
introduction: What is the process to be followed by ASKIT when KONE users reaches them when the emails are not delivered to their Inbox from suppliers/customers or external partners or when the email was Quarantined by M365 Defender?
instructions: Introduction: Microsoft Defender will help protect users and systems from malicious emails that could harm computers and infrastructure or compromise system data. This KBA article will explain the process that can be followed by ASKIT Team if the user states that they haven't received email from suppliers/customers or other parties to their inbox or when they receive any Email/Incident from user related to Email Quarantined by M365 Defender. Process : ASKIT will gather information about the context of email (business related or non-business related) along with sender details from the reported user. List of information that can gathered from the End user Timestamp Sender Email address Subject of the email (Optional) If the user mentions that the email is beyond 30 days, ASKIT can inform the user that those email could not be tracked in Defender and assign the case to KONE.M&C.Support. Once the above information are gathered, the Incident will be assigned to O365 queue KONE.M&C.Support. O365 Team will perform basic analysis by navigating to M365 Defender>Review>Quarantine and search for the Quarantined email with sender/subject or time. Please note, the Quarantined email can be searched for last 30 days timeframe only. O365 Team can perform checks to validate the Quarantine reason and policy type. After checking the email records, and if everything seems normal, the Incident can be assigned to KONE.KTI.CYBERSECURITY.OPERATIONS to perform Security investigation in order to determine the reason why the email has been quarantined. Some of the validation will include analyzing the malicious content (Attachment, sender domain, IP, URL’s) etc. Post investigation, if there are no security risks in the email, Security Operations team will release the email from Quarantine and can resolve the Incident. If there are security risks, then inform the same to the user and suggest further remediation steps and close the Incident. If this case is turning out to be false positive, SOC/CyberOps team will have to raise a case with Microsoft and finetune the same.

short_description: How to handle Verizon network proactive tickets
introduction: How to handle Verizon network proactive tickets?
instructions: What is Proactive ticket? Verizon is proactively monitoring all network components in KONE Office Network, and the Network Event Mgt. monitoring detects and automatically reports incidents - Verizon refers to this as ‘Proactive’. These incidents are sent as Proactive tickets also to KONE Remedyforce. Step 1: When Verizon find any issues in Kone devices and requires information from Kone for further troubleshooting, Verizon will assign the ticket to “ASK IT India Queue”.. Please make a note that proactive ticket always starts with subject “ Proactive ”, but sometime, they will send ticket to Kone to get the site access hours, site contact etc with normal description”. You can also verify the tickets “ created by” parameter filled by “Mulesoft Interface ” Ticket View’s in RF: Step 2: ASK IT agent will open proactive RF ticket and go to the “details” section and verify the details updated by Verizon NOC in Verizon ticket under Vendor reference number . Step 3: ASKIT agent open the Verizon ticket under “Verizon Reference Ticket number” and see if Verizon ask ASKIT team to collect any information from Kone end. See below example tickets and see the latest ask from Verizon….. Step 4: Please copy the information from “comments from vendor “section and send email to relevant stakeholders. If Site Tier type is Tier1 to Tier 3, First, second and third communication sends to Local POC, SDM , RSM and CCed to officeconnectvitysupport@kone.com For Tier 4 to Tier 6 sites , first communication sends to Local POC & officeconnectivitysupport@kone.com , second and third communication to SDM and RSM. Make sure the subject will be standard for all the Proactive tickets. Subject: Network Issue_Local action required_ Site ID EG002_VZ ticket 2023082404079_RF ticket IN02209448 Message template from ASK IT to LCON: Hi Sir/Madam, As per our master site data, you are the local point of contact for the site “XXXXX”. We have received an incident ticket #xxxxxx from the Verizon network vendor, and below are the details of the incident. Kindly request that you check and respond back with details to be shared with Verizon for further troubleshooting. Message template from ASK IT to SDM : Hi SDM, We have received a network proactive alarm for the site “XXXXX” from the Verizon vendor and contacted LCON for the site, but we didn’t get a response. Kindly see the below incident details (RF ticket #xxxxxxxx) from Verizon for the reported incident. They requested KONE to check and respond back to the Verizon ticket. We will update the Verizon ticket for further action or troubleshooting as soon as we receive an update from your end. Important information: Master site list: To check the site Tier type, LCON details etc See article KBA3222 at link https://konewayflow.lightning.force.com/lightning/r/a2j1r00000DD2TxAAL/view See below SharePoint link for Region SDM contacts. IT Service Delivery Managers (sharepoint.com) See below Contac details for Region Connectivity RSM APM- Venkatesh R (Systems) <r.venkatesh@kone.com> GCN- Gavin Shi Gavin.Shi@KONE.com Europe- Boris Tomas <tomas.boris@kone.com ENA- Valmores Jesus jesus.valmores@kone.com Make sure the subject will be standard for all the Proactive tickets. Subject: Network Issue_Local action required_ Site ID EG002_VZ ticket 2023082404079_RF ticket IN02209448 Also ASK IT reaches out to LCON and SDM via Microsoft teams during local Business hours and update about the Proactive incident. If ASK IT receive an update from LCON or SDM, update in the Verizon ticket. If no response is received, please reach out to RSM of the region via Teams and update on the ticket. Step 5: Once ASK IT receive an update from LCON or SDM, they will update in the Verizon ticket under “comments to Vendor” section in the ticket. Once save it, please verify at the end of the page that information’s are updated… Important Note: 1. Response SLA: 1. once Ticket Assigned to ASK IT India Queue, ASK IT will send an email to require stakeholders 2. ASKIT update the Verizon ticket once LCON/SDM updated Tier 1--30 mins Tier 2--30 mins Tier 3--1 hour Tier 4, 5 6- -2 hours 2.If the local POC is not updated in Master sheet data, please reach out to Officeconnectivitysupport@kone.com 3. If the local POC email id is not valid, please contact region SDM to get the new POC. You can send email to SDM and CCed to officeconnectivitysupport@kone.com 4.ASK IT always login to Verizon ticket and then see the recent updates from “Comments from vendor” and then send an email to LCON and SDM 5.Similarly, once they receive an update from LCON or SDM, they will update at the “Comments to vendor” tab to pass the information to Verizon NOC. 5. ASK IT reach out to LCON & SDM via Microsoft teams (one time) during local Business hours and update them about the Proactive incident. If they respond back to Teams with the details, update back to the Verizon ticket. (If ASK IT did not get response from SDM, then reach out to RSM for further checking)

short_description: Update/Modify record details (Inspections, Incidents & Near Misses) - Kone Safety Solution (KSS, Intelex)
introduction: Update/Modify record details (Inspections, Incidents & Near Misses) - Kone Safety Solution (KSS, Intelex)
instructions: End users will request KSS Support Team to edit Inspections, Incident & Near Misses records, as they are restricted to modify the records once it is submitted. Edit access is only given to respective location Full Access Users & System Administrators only. The record can be edited while it is in initial stage only. If the record is in mid of workflow, inform user and bring the workflow to starting stage. Then the workflow process need to be followed again by person responsible only. Support team is not responsible to move forward the workflow or closure of the record. Support team can only Delete (Archive) the record from Intelex with proper approval in place. Edit Inspections: 1. Login to Intelex https://global.intelex.com/Login3/Kone and click on Search Apps field and search for Inspections. 2. From Inspections page, search for the Inspection under record no. field and click on the number. 3. From Inspection Record page, click on Edit and then Edit Read-Only. 4. From here, we can edit the information like Business Unit, Name, Inspection Date, Person Responsible, Action Owner, Project Manager, Supervisor, City, Job Site Name, Company/ Person Name under Party Involved, Equipment Details, Action Plan Summary (Delete only) 5. We cannot edit Inspection Record No., Location name, Type of Inspection, Inspection Checklist, Party Involved, questions. NOTE : For editing any information in Inspections we need approval from safety manager or key user- or the person who reported the inspection. Edit Incidents & Near Misses: 1. Login to Intelex https://global.intelex.com/Login3/Kone and click on Search Apps field and search for Incident Management. 2. Search for the Incident number under Incidents page or Near Miss under Near Miss page 3. Click on the record Number and the Click on Edit and then Edit Read-Only. 4. From this page, we can edit Location, Business Unit, Incident or Near Miss date & time, Severity, Risk Potential, reported by, Date & Time reported, Equipment Details. 5. We should not edit any workflow related fields like under Analysis, Investigation, Review, Approval stages fields. NOTE : For editing any information in Inspections we need approval from safety manager or key user- or the person who reported the inspection. How to Re assign Incidents & Near Misses: 1. Open Intelex and click on My Tasks Symbol from left screen options, then click on All Tasks option. 2. Then search for the Incident or Near Miss under Name field and check the box beside the record which you want to re assign. 3. Then click on Re assign button from the options above and the search for the employee to whom you want to re assign the record 4. Once you click on Select the record will be assigned to that person. NOTE : For Re assigning any record, we need approval from safety manager or key user.

short_description: How to open ticket to FLCAD, KONE Palette and EPT
introduction: How to open ticket to FLCAD KONE Palette and EPT
instructions: 1. Go to Ask IT Portal 2. Select Something does not work Opened by and Opened for are filled automatically, change the Opened for only if you are opening the ticket behalf of someone else. 3. Select the correct service: FLCAD, KONE Palette or EPT Dialog 4. Fill the Description Try to properly describe the problem, “It does not work” or “There is an error” are not sufficient. In FLCAD case, which was requested from KTOC, it is recommended to include the KTOC CAMOS ID in the long description to prepare in case the ticket needs to be forwarded to KTOC team. 5. Add the Attachment(s) In FLCAD case original XML-file, DWG or PDF file(s) with the issue marked clearly to the file(s). In FLCAD case the ticket must contain the case file(s). Tickets without files are rejected. In EPT case XML-file, error message content or image of it. In KONE Palette case block name, error message or image of it. 6. Submit the ticket 7. Email containing the ticket number is sent to the ticket opener. Ticket progress can be followed in Ask IT Portal by selecting View in My Activity . NOTE! It is not recommended to use Chat or Virtual Agent to open tickets for Layout Engineering. In both cases the tickets are usually lacking information or are other vice inadequate.

short_description: South Africa Microsoft Teams calling with BT support

short_description: Guidance on Sideloading restrictions
introduction: install mobile applications. untrusted locations, Google Play. Apple store, sideloading, application blocking
instructions: Sideloading restriction • With KONE managed devices it will not be possible to download or install mobile applications from untrusted locations, meaning you’re only allowed to download the apps from the trusted locations such as Google Play (on your personal profile), KONE managed Google Play on your Work profile or from the Apple Store. • We will enforce this policy again, due to a mistake while applying new policies, back on starting from 1st Nov'2023 . Your current sideloaded apps won’t be affected, but you’re not allowed to sideload these anymore. • If you have a strong business reason and have a need to be excluded, please be in touch directly to sangilipandy Arumugam, Platform Owner for Mobile Device Management - Workplace & Platform Services, Technology & Services, for further discussion. For more information, please see the Intune FAQ : (Q) I'm trying to install a KONE application but it's not possible. I need this application to do my work. What to do? • Intune is configured so that it blocks sideloading of untrusted applications. (Sideloading means that the application is downloaded from somewhere else than the official application stores.) Sometimes even KONE applications are being blocked. The goal is to onboard all KONE applications in Intune which would enable the installation and easy use of the application in the KONE environment. • KONE has aimed to onboard the most critical applications. If you have a specific application in mind, please contact sangilipandy Arumugam, the MDM platform owner, for onboarding. We need more information about users, application owner name, business criticality and impact if the application cannot be used on an enrolled device when you contact us for onboarding an application. (Q) Are there any restricted apps, or the possibility of IT blocking an app without discussion? • No applications will be blocked if they can be installed from the official app store. However, sideloading of apps (e.g. downloading from a link) is blocked. Cybersecurity basics course and guidance:

short_description: 1PLAN - TRB data load and post data load activities
introduction: How to load TRB input data into 1plan application?
instructions: Step1- First, we need to check the input excel received from business, whether we have a correct data or not, if we have the correct data in excel, then we should upload this excel into PAM or in VDI system, for importing the data into Quintiq. Below are the key checklists we must validate in the excel. a. Rename the excel file as “ TRB input data” b. Then open that Excel and unprotect the sheet ACT TRB 3A, ACT TRB 3A SERVICE, SL ACT 3B, ACT TRB SL OR, TRB SL OB 3A password is “kone” if it is already unprotected then leave this step. c. Go to ACT TRB 3A sheet and delete unwanted data outside the actual data received if any, then do the same for SL ACT 3B, ACT TRB 3A SERVICE, ACT TRB SL OR, TRB SL OB 3A sheets also. d. Then open the Final Check sheet and right click on macros of Check list for ACT TRB 3A and then press Refresh button, do the same for Check list for SL ACT 3B, ACT TRB 3A SERVICE, TRB SL OB 3A, ACT TRB SL OR (go towards right ). Then check the Grand total row for both check lists, it should be empty as mentioned in the image below. The grand total row must be empty for doing the remaining steps, if it is empty then only start step 2. If the Grand total row is NOT empty, then hold the TRB import activity and inform the L3 team immediately. Step2- Once data is ready then login 1 plan application and click on “ Import data” button, as shown in the below image. Step3- After clicking the import button one dialogue will open like below, in this dialogue we must select the “ Path” for input excel and need to check “ TRB import” checkbox, then press OK. It will take some time to upload the data into system, wait for some time. Step4- Once data is uploaded you can check the data in the TRB Data Form (Form->Transactional Data->TRB Data) Step5- Once we can see the data in “ TRB Data ” Form then we need to calculate the corresponding fact types manually, like below. Step6- Open “ TRB Data Import ” view, once it is opened, we must calculate the data for the following fact types in the matrix 1. ACT TRB 3A MOD, 2. SL ACT 3B, 3. ACT TRB 3A SERVICE, 4. ACT TRB SL OR, 5. TRB SL OB 3A. Go to the root level first then right click on the cell which is in past time (sop -1) and then press “Instant Calculation”. And check other listed fact types are updating or not. Step7- Select the two fact types, 1. TRB MLO 3A UN MOD, 2. TRB MLO 3A UN SERVICE , and calculate these two fact types for last month alone. Do not click on Instant calculate , click on Calculate , and select the from (last month start date) & to (last month end date) dates.

short_description: Country Specific Account – Scan to Folder (Sysricoh)
introduction: Country Specific Account – Scan to Folder (Sysricoh)
instructions: Country Specific Account – Scan to Folder This document outlines the need for improved security by implementing country-specific service accounts for printer functions and focuses on the below points. 1. The process for creating and securing these accounts and requesting the necessary access. 2. It provides recommendations for preferred methods of document management. 3. It explains the limited support scope for Scan to email & Scan to OneDrive until licensing and setup are completed. Current Situation: There is one global Sysricoh account in use for all printers, but it has no designated owner, and the password hasn't been reset for over eight years, which is a security concern. New Approach: This process requires creating country-specific service accounts for Scan to folder functionality, and these accounts should be owned by Service Delivery Managers (SDMs) of the respective countries. Country-Specific Service Account Creation: SDM’s are responsible for Scan to folder usage in their respective countries and thus need to create a new service account. They should request access through MyAccess and use a specific naming format for the service account (e.g., SVC_PRINT_SERVICE_FR). SVC – Service account, PRINT_SERVICE – Printing Service, FR – Country code The information to be provided includes the account name, usage (On-Prem), a description, the owner IT Manager name), and deputy owner (Chattopadhyay Sayak) this is Mandatory. To create a service account please follow this article - KB000A8407 Account Security: The document emphasizes that account details should not be shared and SDM’s managing multiple countries should request separate service accounts for each country. Getting Access for Service Account: SDM’s should log a ticket with the MyAccess team to request the addition of required roles for the service account to access the file share (Scan to folder) 1. MyAccess team will add the roles manually if the number of groups are less or 2. MyAccess team will create a business role if any country has multiple groups to be added to the roles. Recommendation: We (Workplace & Platform Services) recommend using either Scan to email or scan to OneDrive (discussion in progress with T-system). Scanning feature is currently outside of the support scope, and local vendors should be consulted for assistance. Maybe best effort can be provided by AskIT for assistance, For more information please refer the following KBA - KB000A8721 We (Workplace & Platform Services) will provide assistance for scanning feature once the necessary licenses are purchased and services are enabled through the VPSX application.
